Perhaps they shouldn’t be called werewolves but monsters would be more accurate."

Listening to Donald’s speculation made me somewhat scared, "Are there really such monsters existing? That’s terrifying, like something out of a Resident Evil scenario!"

Seeming to find my reaction somewhat cute, Donald chuckled deeply, lowered his head to kiss my lips gently, and comforted me in a soft voice, "These are just our speculations at the moment, not necessarily accurate. Don’t worry, dear, the Moon goddess wouldn’t allow such a nature-defying, humanity-extinguishing scenario to happen, that’s too incredible."

"All right, let’s not talk about this now. Didn’t you say you wanted to ask Elizabeth about that matter yesterday? Let’s go now," said Donald as he got up, and he kept hold of my hand from start to finish.

I nodded, agreeing with his suggestion.

But as Donald and I had just left the meeting room, we saw Elizabeth in the hospital corridor talking to Benjamin with a very serious expression on her face.

Donald and I exchanged glances and slowly walked over.

"Elizabeth? What are you doing here so early? What are you talking about with Benjamin?" I asked curiously.

Elizabeth turned to look at me, her serious expression suddenly turning a bit despondent, "I also wanted to help take care of the hospital’s Lycans, but I seem to be a bit too clumsy; even just changing dressings, I couldn’t do it properly."

Her slight despondency seemed somewhat cute to me. I smiled gently and said, "The mere fact that you have this initiative to help others is already commendable. Take it slow, I was also all thumbs yesterday."

Standing nearby, Benjamin, a very witty and humorous Lycan, looked at Elizabeth, who seemed somewhat defeated, and made a jest, "You’re the most beautiful and capable Luna of the Silver Moon Pack, the Lycans who had their wounds dressed by your hands are probably going to heal faster."

"Come on, don’t be discouraged. After all, not many people are as intelligent and exceptionally talented as your sister, who can learn anything quickly. I was actually quite surprised by her yesterday!"

Seeing more gloom in Elizabeth’s expression, I decided to retract my previous praise of Benjamin.

He was indeed a very capable Lycan, but when it came to speaking and cheering people up, he was sorely lacking. I guessed he definitely didn’t have a girlfriend.

Donald seemed to have noticed something too, and he coughed lightly twice, giving Benjamin, who was still rambling, a look that signaled him to stop.

I reached out and pulled Elizabeth over, looked up at Donald to signal him to follow, "We can learn these things later, come with me now; Donald and I have some things we want to ask you."

Elizabeth, sensing something serious from my expression, followed us obediently into a nearby doctor’s office.

"What do you want to ask?" Elizabeth asked, puzzled, sitting beside me on the sofa.

"It’s about ’MASTER’. Donald wants to know more about it; could you recount everything you heard that day? Don’t be nervous, Elizabeth, we just want to get more information," I said, beginning our inquiry with a gentle pace, not to make Elizabeth too tense since she always gets a bit nervous in front of Donald.

Elizabeth first looked at me, then turned to look at Donald sitting opposite, slowly furrowing her brow as she seriously recalled the events.

"We inhaled that damned sedative and then fell unconscious. They must have bound me first and then found another opportunity to abduct Margaret, as there was some time difference between our exposure to the sedative, allowing me to wake up in the vehicle first."

As she spoke, she became slightly excited as if remembering something, "That’s right, we were transported to that cabin in a horse-drawn carriage, they hid me and Margaret under the seats of the carriage!"

chapters.title: Chapter 219: Misty Forest
chapters.content:
Chapter 219: Misty Forest

[Margaret’s POV]

"I heard that long-faced, narrow-eyed man tell Angel that after they successfully take down the Silver Moon Pack, they plan to transfer me and Margaret to a place called... Misty Forest! Yes, Misty Forest, and they mentioned ’MASTER’ will be holding a banquet there. It sounds like a celebration banquet."

Elizabeth’s effort to recall was worthwhile, as she managed to remember another important piece of information: the place called Misty Forest.

As for ’MASTER’ wanting to hold that damned celebration banquet, let him dream on! They were going to be defeated this time, they would be eating our fists and claws instead of a banquet.

I mockingly thought about this enemy I had yet to meet, tightening my grip on Elizabeth’s hand.

"So where is this Misty Forest? Have you heard about it before, Donald?" I asked a bit excitedly, hoping that the information Elizabeth provided could be of some use to Donald.

Donald had been quietly listening to Elizabeth and me talk. When this seemingly mysterious place was mentioned, a glint of interest flashed in his eyes.

He looked up at me, snapped his fingers handsomely, and replied, "More than heard, we even speculated about this location when we were making the surprise attack plan last night!"

"However, it’s quite a distance from several enemy strongholds we’ve identified, and the terrain is complex and changeable—difficult territory to navigate, truly a hard place to attack and easy to defend."

Donald spoke and then fell into contemplative silence, looking particularly enchanting while deep in thought.

I realized I had inappropriately drifted off again, quickly shook my head, trying to clear my thoughts. Losing my senses because of my mate was definitely not acceptable.

Elizabeth noticed my distraction and asked in a low voice, "What’s the matter, Margaret?"

I shook my head and signaled that it was nothing. I couldn’t very well say that I was a bit struck by Donald’s excessive handsomeness, could I?

"This is very important information. Thank you, Elizabeth, you might have really helped us out a lot."

Donald’s tone was somewhat excited. He suddenly stood up, came over, bent down to kiss my forehead, and without any pause, continued, "I need to discuss some things with Christian. You be careful here, dear. I’ll leave now; see you tonight."

After he said this, he turned and left immediately, leaving me to exchange knowing glances with Elizabeth, who had a teasing look in her eyes.

"Margaret, your relationship with the Lycan King is really good. I’m a bit envious of you," Elizabeth said with a smile, her expression tinged with sadness.

I patted her head encouragingly, "As long as you’re clear about what you want and firm in your decisions, you can be the same. Cheer up, we have a lot to do today. Although Benjamin isn’t very eloquent, he really needs our help."

At my words, Elizabeth shrugged resignedly, attempting to tease me with ease, "You’re always like this, and he didn’t say anything wrong. Sometimes I really want to swap brains with you; you always seem to be smarter than me."

I raised an eyebrow and looked at her with a mischievous glance, "Alright, I admit, I am indeed an excellent girl. And it’s not just that I seem smarter than you, I really am smarter."

Elizabeth rolled her eyes at me wordlessly, as if she didn’t wish to retort.

I laughed aloud and pinched her cheek, continuing, "Come on, no more eye-rolling. You have a lot of shining qualities too! Like your makeup skills, and your understanding of fashion."

"That doesn’t sound like a genuine compliment."

"My dear sister! I truly think you’re amazing, and very pretty and cute! Let’s head out now; mornings are always the busiest times at the hospital."

With my joking and teasing, Elizabeth’s mood seemed to brighten a bit. We had never teased and ’mocked’ each other like this before, and it seemed like a novel, fun way to interact. I believed Elizabeth felt the same, since she had genuinely grown.

In the afternoon, Elizabeth and I left the hospital.

Donald and Armstrong had an important surprise action to carry out tonight, so we needed to head over early to check on them.

Although helping out at the hospital was hard work, receiving various kinds of gratitude from everyone made both Elizabeth and me feel that our efforts were not in vain.

Perhaps inspired by us, Benjamin’s medical team saw the arrival of many girls offering help at noon. Most of them were girls from the pack, and I was delighted by their awareness and commitment.

The lovely and beautiful girls could also make their contributions to defending the pack.

chapters.title: Chapter 220: Lead the assault
chapters.content:
Chapter 220: Lead the assault

[Donald’s POV]

When I led the team away from the Silver Moon Pack, Margaret was watching me worriedly from behind. I knew she was concerned about my safety, but there were things I had to do.

I was the Lycan King, the guiding light and direction for all Lycans of the Wolf royal family. The more challenging the task, the more I needed to lead by example!

"Everyone must come back safe!"

Margaret called out to us loudly from behind; I looked back at her, smiled, raised my hand, and gently waved, signaling for them to hurry back.

Armstrong still stayed behind in the pack, and I had turned down his request to lead the team. His steady and cautious nature was clearly more suited for our support at the rear.

His Beta Anthony, however, was a very alert warrior, and I had brought him along on this trip.

We estimated that this raid would be completed before dawn tomorrow. If all went well, I would lead the team back to the Silver Moon Pack by noon, and then we’d start preparing to return to the Palace.

Margaret just hoped wholeheartedly for my safe return, even though I had great confidence in this combat plan.

Sometimes I felt that Angel was right; after having Margaret, I seemed to become not so invincible. I began to have some weaknesses, as Margaret could always easily stir all my emotions.

But upon thinking it over carefully, I didn’t quite agree; love does not make people weak. It is actually the supreme tool that warriors use to forge their armor! With someone to protect, we become braver and more decisive.

Angel would never understand this; she always lived in a world of her own, stubborn and autocratic.

The night was heavy and deep, even more so in the forest that blotted out the sky. Fortunately, our night vision wasn’t limited like ordinary Lycans; everyone could still move freely in such an environment.

We crouched low in the bushes, focusing intently on the forest ahead, shrouded in mist. The Misty Forest truly lived up to its name; this thick miasma was a very reliable natural barrier!

"Your Majesty, the miasma might even be poisonous. Have the warriors put on masks and night vision goggles!" Christian whispered from my right side, suggesting softly.

I nodded in agreement with the suggestion and raised my hand to take the anti-poison masks and night vision goggles handed over by him, specially made by Benjamin.

Although we weren’t disturbed by the darkness, we couldn’t guarantee that this unknown miasma wouldn’t harm our eyes. To prevent any accidents, we had to be fully prepared.

Anthony crouched at my left, his wolf eyes fixedly observing the situation inside. As the Beta of the Silver Moon Pack, he knew this area a bit more than all of us.

"Noticed anything?" I asked him softly.

Upon hearing this, Anthony came to his senses, respectfully glanced at me, and answered, "We should travel through the trees. This miasma is heavier than air; walking normally might result in inhaling it. We need to be cautious and avoid detection before encountering any enemies. I can take the lead."

Anthony’s strategy was smart. I raised my hand and patted his shoulder, responding, "Let’s do as you say. I’ll take the lead with you at the front."

We decided not to travel in wolf form; human form would make it easier to conceal our traces and sounds, making climbing trees even more suitable. We only needed to expose our wolf claws to easily manage such actions.

The trees in the Misty Forest were tall and dense, like a natural route for our travel.

I was intensely aware of everything unfolding before me and did not notice any signs of Lycan activity. I thought the enemy would likely be in the center of the Misty Forest; after all, if it were me, I would choose to do so.

The landing spots I chose on each tree were highly secretive, and if not for the occasional sound of wind rustling the leaves, our tracks would not have been discovered by anyone.

When I saw the first wooden house appear before me, I realized we had finally reached our destination.

This must be the enemy’s patrol point; there were no more Lycans around.

I initiated a mind-link with Christian and Anthony: <Anthony and I one each, Christian keep an eye on sudden arrivals from their rear, move!>

At my command, the three of us landed quietly and approached the two Lycans at the cabin door.

My actions were extremely fast; the Lycan’s neck was snapped before he could even get a clear look at my face.

I casually tossed the body into Mark’s arms behind me, bowed my waist, and continued to move forward. After landing, I noticed the miasma’s color had lightened somewhat; it seemed not as severe here as outside!

This was a peculiar discovery, and the first person to find this place was nothing short of a genius. I was even looking forward to meeting my opponent now, a very clever and brave person.

chapters.title: Chapter 221: Good to see you after such a long time
chapters.content:
Chapter 221: Good to see you after such a long time

[Donald’s POV]

Anthony had also dealt with the watchful Lycan, while Christian was carefully advancing among us, step by step.

The Lycan troops I led had all safely touched down, and I had a premonition that this was going to be a night of exceptionally smooth operations.

Because a cluster of houses quickly came into our view, and the one that caught our eyes the most was the most magnificent dwelling perched at the very top.

I gave Christian a hand signal to move forward and rushed towards that direction, lowering my breaths. I thought that there would probably be someone I was anticipating to meet inside, such as the unmasked ’MASTER’!

As I approached the exquisite two-story house, I noticed the light from a window on the first floor flicker for a moment, giving me an uneasy feeling.

I pressed myself against the stair’s side panel, using the cover of night to hide my presence, and once again lowered the sound of my breathing.

<Your Majesty! Someone is coming out from inside!> That was the message Christian conveyed to me from the rooftop across from me.

I strained to listen to the footsteps overhead, suddenly feeling that the sound was somewhat familiar.

"Who’s there?"

Realizing our presence had been exposed, I made a split-second decision, stepping on a box by my leg, using the leverage to leap onto the wooden stairs, and completed the shift just before the enemy could make out my figure!

"Night raid! We’re under a night raid! Where’s the alarm, sound the alarm!"

"What? Who could have gotten past our guard to come here? Who is it?"

"Attack! Kill these intruders!"

"No! MASTER is still in the house, brothers, charge and protect MASTER!"

"Angel..."

Hearing the alarms and shouts coming from all directions, I wasn’t the least bit worried about these people’s counterattack, because those who could appear here were the elite of the Wolf royal family’s Lycans, and naturally, they could easily handle this motley crew.

The only surprise to me was the enemy who had first sensed something wrong, she happened to be an acquaintance of the assault team and personal guard force, but she was not the MASTER I wanted to see.

"Long time no see, Donald, did you miss me?"

I pressed Angel to the ground with my wolf claw and said sternly, "Shut your mouth, Angel, I didn’t want to see you here at all. He’s inside, isn’t he?"

"Who? Are you talking about MASTER?" Angel countered with feigned confusion, her eyes full of craftiness.

Not intending to waste any more words on Angel, I pushed her aside, and my massive, silver-white body burst through the wooden door in an instant.

"He might have been a moment ago, but now, I wouldn’t know," Angel’s voice rang out behind me. I didn’t want to engage with her, and seeing the Black Wolf running up to the second floor, I didn’t hesitate to chase after him!

I had a strong feeling that the so-called ’MASTER’ had just overheard my conversation with Angel through that wooden door.

"Stop!" I yelled sternly, but the other party was obviously not the sort to comply obediently.

I quickly climbed to the second floor in pursuit of his figure, watching helplessly as he leaped out from a very concealed window, a window I wouldn’t have thought of opening had I not seen MASTER opening it himself.

"We’ll meet again soon, Donald. Hahaha!"

I followed his voice to the window, without any hesitation to jump out after him, but I was immediately repelled by a colossal force, like a powerful, inviolable Energy Shield!

My body, thrown by the repulsion, shattered the wooden furniture behind me, and when I got up from the ground, shaking off the dizziness in my head, MASTER had already vanished, blending into the forest like the favored child of the night, leaving no trace to be followed.

I couldn’t help but swear, and had it not been for Anthony and Christian firmly holding me back, I would have torn the entire second floor apart in my rage!

"Are you all right, Your Majesty? When we came up, you were just bounced back by that power wall; are you hurt?"

The pain spreading across my back did little to quell my fury. I took a few deep breaths before turning to Christian, "I’m alright. Did you catch Angel? MASTER has already fled; she must not escape again!"

"Yes, Your Majesty! Mark is leading a team to encircle her below. Those Lycans saw MASTER fleeing without looking back, and they also abandoned her, each running for their lives!" Christian replied.

I looked towards the wide-open balcony doors on the second floor, leaped with ease, and landed firmly on the ground below, my greenish-grey wolf eyes fixed intently on Angel not far away.

As long as we catch her today, we have a chance of finding out about MASTER’s whereabouts later.

"Capture her; I want all the information on MASTER. Don’t let her get away again," I ordered everyone in a calm voice.

chapters.title: Chapter 222: Love is wrong?
chapters.content:
Chapter 222: Love is wrong?

[Donald’s POV]

Angel’s pure white wolf form was extremely conspicuous against the night sky, if it weren’t for the bloody wounds on her hind legs, I would have thought she was entirely at ease in this duel.

This stronghold, to my surprise, had not gathered as many enemies as I expected, and most of them were just very ordinary werewolves.

If I hadn’t run into Angel and the fleeing MASTER right as I entered, I would have started to doubt whether this was really their secret base.

The MASTER’s escape from the fight seemed to have had an ’excellent’ leading effect on these werewolves, as they scattered and fled into the forest, looking to follow his example. My led Lycan assault team, on the other hand, had taken advantage of the chaos to capture many, making tonight’s haul quite good.

I looked at Angel, who was being surrounded by Mark and Christian, with an unkind expression. After scanning the area for a moment, a sense of strangeness began to creep into my heart.

I glanced at Anthony and gestured for him to bring his ear closer.

After confiding my suspicions to Anthony, I turned my head back to the ’hunt’ taking place not too far away.

That’s right, Angel was the ’prey’ we were hunting!

"Angel! You have always been a very smart person, you should know that the only thing you can do now is to surrender obediently. Give up, you can’t escape no matter what today," I said.

"It’s such a waste that both his Majesty and Elliot placed so much trust in you. His Majesty even entrusted you with managing such an important team as the assault team. Is this how you repay him? You’re a shameless traitor; you deserve death!"

"Surrender now, come back with us, and then accept his Majesty’s judgement. Only then can you make amends for all the crimes you’ve committed!"

There were quite a few members of the assault team among the Lycan forces tonight, and their feelings towards Angel, their former commander, were incredibly complex.

They wanted to kill this traitor directly to assuage the resentment in their hearts, but they were also reluctant to make things too absolute, as if there were no other options besides a deadly confrontation with Angel.

They are gallant warriors of the Wolf royal family, and also comrades who grew up and trained hard together with Angel. I think no one hates what Angel has become at this moment more than they do!

It feels as if a ’flesh and blood’ companion, someone close as kin, betrayed them, and in doing so, negated all of their past feelings.

Regarding the current situation, I actually feel very heartbroken and helpless.

It was my fault for not realizing earlier that Angel had ulterior motives, causing my warriors to face such a cruel reality on the battlefield. Their leader had not even seen a real battle before she turned traitor and defected to the enemy.

"Trust? Hahaha, you mean Donald’s trust in me?" As if hearing something utterly ludicrous, Angel exploded like dynamite at the question posed by Mark, her rage blazing as she loudly retorted.

"That’s the funniest joke under the sun! You can turn around and seriously ask him if he’s ever had a shred of trust in me, his hardworking former commander of the assault team," she continued.

With the manic laughter receding, Angel, her eyes filled with resentment and unwillingness, looked through the crowd at me, "I liked him, I admired him, but my love wasn’t some kind of poisonous reward that demanded blood. How could he treat me like that? Did I do anything wrong in loving him?"

"Or is it that only that good-for-nothing, lowly wench can occupy Donald’s heart? What gives her the right to so easily win your love?"

"Wasn’t it us who grew up together and trained together? Why should she appear halfway through and steal you away from me? Just because she is your absurd fated mate? How is the Moon goddess this unfair? I won’t accept this!"

As Angel ranted, her green wolf eyes burned blood-red with jealousy and bitterness. Watching her, I felt she had truly gone mad.

I instantly shifted from my wolf form to human, frowning and shaking my head, "Enough, Angel! Do you think I will still trust you? I see you have completely lost your mind. You dare to question and defy the will of the Moon goddess, what wouldn’t you dare to do?"

"What colossal benefits did MASTER offer you to make you completely betray your own family? I’m really curious. You say you love me, hmm?" I chuckled mockingly, my gaze on Angel filled with coldness.

"The one you love, isn’t it the one who tries to control everything, who even dreams of manipulating everyone? You don’t love me, you just love the identity and glory I represent as the Lycan King!"

"In the end, you’re nothing but someone who craves power and is arrogant and vain. If something is even slightly against your wishes, you would furiously try to destroy it..."

chapters.title: Chapter 223: Hard-mouthed
chapters.content:
Chapter 223: Hard-mouthed

[Donald’s POV]

"So, you want everything back under your control again, am I right?"

I asked Angel directly, looking into her eyes. If it were in the past, I might have left her some dignity, but seeing her stubborn and unrepentant manner, I abandoned that thought.

The depth of Angel’s resentment toward Margaret was too great. I couldn’t let her continue this way; I had to find a way to control her.

"I don’t want to hear your nonsense anymore, but if you surrender willingly, I might consider leniency in your trial as long as you cooperate fully with us in all actions against MASTER."

I thought my last two sentences might have stirred something in Angel’s heart. Her expression changed briefly, then she replied with forced toughness, "Donald, do you think I’m still the girl who would trust you wholeheartedly? You’re wrong!"

"Not to mention Margaret has poisoned me against her, she will never let me go. Even if I do what you say, collaborate with you in tracking down and encircling MASTER, would you really be lenient with me?"

"Impossible! The Royal Family’s punishment for Lycan deserters is extremely severe and cruel. The elders won’t listen to you entirely, so I never had any way out from the start. Don’t think you can deceive me again!"

"I can’t turn back now..."

"Then who do you think caused you to become what you are today? Is it the assault team? His Majesty? Elliot?" Christian, who had been silent all along, suddenly interrupted Angel with an indignant and agitated tone.

He sadly and helplessly lowered his head as he continued, "In the end, it’s nothing but your own greed at play. Why do you still shamelessly blame others? You say Miss Margaret hates you, but that’s because you did many outrageous things to her. You brought this upon yourself!"

Angel turned her gaze from me to Christian, and a dark light gleamed in her eyes.

Seeing this, Christian was unfazed; he calmed down and then continued, "Fine! I’ll not talk about your grudges with Miss Margaret right now. I only want to ask you one thing, did Elliot ever try to persuade you to give up cooperating with MASTER that day?"

"He is your cousin who has cared for you since childhood, the kindest person to you in the assault squad. And what happened? Do you realize how deeply you’ve hurt him? He’s nearly dead! You really don’t deserve to be his sister."

At the mention of Elliot, the fierceness in Angel’s expression seemed to dissolve a bit. I guessed that deep down, she still cared about Elliot.

"Ha! So what? He was stubborn that day, wouldn’t listen to anything I said. His martial skills aren’t great, he couldn’t beat me, so he got hurt by his own fault. He shouldn’t have run away on his own, after all, with me there, MASTER’s people wouldn’t really mistreat him."

Angel remained silent for a while and then stubbornly refused to repent.

I actually hoped that her argument would convince her own self, and it would be best if she kept her defiance.

"There’s nothing to discuss with you about betraying your own for selfish desires. Elliot would never condone such actions. Since you remain unrepentant, don’t blame us for being unkind to you."

With that, I signaled Christian and Mark with a gesture that it was no longer worthwhile talking to Angel; it was crucial to take action and capture her now.

Of course, they quickly understood my intent, and without another word, they pounced on her directly.

I stood not far off, squinting as I watched the battle where one faced ten, still pondering why certain individuals who should have appeared by now hadn’t appeared yet; they were truly masters at biding their time.

Angel, once a commander of the assault team, was highly skilled. Even though she had sustained a significant injury in her leg by this time, she still managed to struggle against these Lycans for a long time with all her strength.

After about 3 minutes had passed, Angel began to weaken from the relentless assaults organized by Christian, and just as she was about to fall powerless, a sudden, awkward shift happened in the battle.

The person I had been waiting for since earlier finally showed up!

"Angel! Are you alright? Can you still stand? Come over to us quickly!"

A few black-clad Lycans suddenly emerged from the forest, immediately creating a standoff with Christian’s group, which didn’t surprise me.

Angel wasn’t the only one who had deserted; she had her supporters, including Chris and his group who had also betrayed the Wolf royal family.

I had previously encountered this Chris in the assault team; he was one of Angel’s confidants.

chapters.title: Chapter 224: Bad hunch
chapters.content:
Chapter 224: Bad hunch

[Margaret’s POV]

Today was the second day Donald led the assault team into the Misty Forest.

I woke up early, or, to be more precise, I had not truly closed my eyes all night. Insomnia had taken hold of me during the night spent apart from Donald.

Even though Donald had obtained new information from Elizabeth and explained to me that there was a high chance of success for the mission, I still felt uneasy inside—a very bad premonition lingered in my mind.

And as it turned out, my worries were not unfounded, or rather, the specific people and events I worried about were not quite accurate, but my sense of foreboding for the situation was indeed extremely precise!

When Benjamin informed me that Elliot’s injuries had suddenly deteriorated, I was a bit stunned; things had developed into the most complex and urgent situation possible.

At this point, Elliot could no longer wait for Donald, not even for a minute. He had to leave the Silver Moon Pack immediately to seek a good doctor and better medication at the Palace of the Wolf royal family.

"Are you saying we must leave right now? But Donald hasn’t returned yet, and we don’t have a Lycan capable of taking control of the entire situation. How are we supposed to coordinate this?"

I said, my brow furrowed as I looked into Benjamin’s eyes, full of confusion and anxiety.

Benjamin was, of course, very aware of the severity and suddenness of the current situation. He ran his hands through his disheveled black hair, looking utterly exhausted and guilty!

"I’m sorry, Miss Margaret! I have failed the trust His Majesty placed in me; I couldn’t keep Elliot’s condition from worsening. I am so sorry to put such pressure on you, but I can’t just watch Elliot die! I want to save my friend, to seek one last chance at life for him!"

As Benjamin spoke, his eyes hardened with determination.

I could actually empathize with the complexity and guilt Benjamin was feeling at the moment. The words I had just been brewing in my mind to persuade him and myself now seemed impossible to say.

I looked at the pale-faced Elliot lying in the sickbed, took a deep breath, and said, "I understand. I’ll take full responsibility for this matter from now on."

After saying this, I pondered for a moment, then lifted my gaze to Benjamin, my mind already made up, "There’s still a portion of the assault team left in the pack, not many, but enough to form a Guard Team to protect both me and Elliot on our return to the city. There’s no other option now!"

"Inform them at once, leave the Lycan patrol on the outermost perimeter of the forest, then have the rest of the Lycans stationed in the pack assemble in half an hour. We need to march back to the city urgently! We can’t stay passive waiting for Donald, we must leave for the city immediately. I will discuss the rest with Armstrong. Go, make it happen."

Benjamin seemed stunned by my decisiveness and efficiency, giving me a blank look before suddenly springing into action to deliver the orders.

I was aware that to these Lycans of the Wolf royal family, I was not the absolute and legitimate Lycan Queen.

Since being with Donald, a lot had really happened, without any time for ceremonies. This made me worry whether these Lycans would truly heed my command, whether my authority over them was enough?

The situation had developed so suddenly and urgently that I didn’t have much time to ponder these things; I could only do what needed to be done, adopting a firm and resolute stance.

Armstrong received the news that I was to lead the team taking Elliot back to the Palace and immediately came to find me at Donald’s residence, with Elizabeth following closely behind.

"Margaret, listen to me! I advise against such a risky action. There are too few Lycans in the pack now; they cannot ensure your and Elliot’s safety. Departing now is gambling with your own security!"

"The Lycan King would never allow you to act so impulsively and dangerously. You must stay, wait for the Lycan King to return safely with his team, and then make other plans."

I, of course, heard Armstrong’s words clearly and understood his disapproval and concerns about the situation.

He was always a steadfast and practical worker, never one for taking risks or acting without substantial certainty.

"Ruth, go check on the vehicles at the entrance and see how the preparations are going, then help me move these important items into the car. Leave the rest here; we’re pressed for time right now."

After instructing the maid to take care of mine and Donald’s luggage, I turned back to face Armstrong and Elizabeth, who was looking at me with a face full of concern but not speaking a word.

Seeing that I wasn’t responding, Armstrong, somewhat agitated, shook his head and urgently said, "I’m talking to you, Margaret! Did you hear me? You can’t be so willful!"

chapters.title: Chapter 225: Worthwhile adventure
chapters.content:
Chapter 225: Worthwhile adventure

[Margaret’s POV]

"Do you think so too, Elizabeth?" I looked up at the anxious Armstrong before turning my gaze to Elizabeth and asked softly.

Upon hearing this, Elizabeth looked up and met my eyes, and after a few breaths of silence, she finally responded, "Benjamin is not an impulsive man. Since he has made such a request at this time, he must have had no other choice left."

"If Margaret does not act now, it would mean letting Elliot’s life slowly drain away, and that would be far too cruel..."

"Elliot was injured trying to save us, we cannot give up on him now! That’s why I support your decision to head to the Palace now, even though it’s truly dangerous and frightening. It’s all worth it for Elliot. He was our former tutor and our friend."

Elizabeth’s thoughts were similar to mine, and I was certain that the same blood flows within both of us, ultimately making us the same kind of people.

Armstrong seemed quite shocked by Elizabeth’s speech, his eyes widened in disbelief as he stared at her.

I knew very well what kind of person my sister was; although she might be delicate and capricious, her inherent goodness and sense of right and wrong were indeed very strong.

"Just like Elizabeth said, to save Elliot’s life, it is worth taking this risk."

I looked at Elizabeth with a gentle smile, then raised my head to meet Armstrong’s gaze, "This is my thoughts on the matter and my answer to your question, Alpha. Is there anything else you’d like to know?"

Armstrong, with a stern face, glanced at me and Elizabeth. His lips parted as if he wanted to say something more, but he ended up swallowing his words!

He was not someone who could completely disregard a companion’s life; I believed his opposition was genuinely well-intended.

"Margaret... all I can say is you are incredibly bold. If the Lycan King comes back and does not see you, he will be furious, and I am afraid I can’t withstand his towering rage!"

"That won’t happen, Armstrong. I think Donald will understand my decision; he isn’t a ruler who sticks rigidly to the rules,"

I confidently rebutted Armstrong and halfway through I realized my statement might bear some ambiguity; I paused briefly, then explained, "I didn’t mean to imply anything about you, do not misunderstand."

Armstrong took a deep breath, his expression not showing any anger, "So you think I am inferior to him? Well, I don’t really need that answer."

"I will do my best to support your action. The pack has been relatively stable and safe lately. So I’ll personally lead the team and accompany you and a group back to the Palace."

"If the Lycan King’s raid goes smoothly, they should be able to return to the Silver Moon Pack by noon. With our departure, we’ll only be half a day’s journey behind them, they can surely catch up!"

"What did you say?" I interrupted Armstrong, surprised by his extensive planning for my matter.

I glanced sidelong at Elizabeth beside me and coldly said to Armstrong, "You are going to personally lead a team to escort Elliot back to the Palace? What about the pack? Are you going to leave it unattended? Do you even realize what you’re saying?"

Armstrong fell silent for a while, looking at me wanting to argue further.

I raised my hand to signal him to stop, my tone leaving no room for objection, "If you think I haven’t considered this option, then aren’t you underestimating me too much? I didn’t mention it because it’s truly unfeasible, and I dismissed it right away. Is the pack really stable now? I hardly think so!"

"So I can never allow you to do this. Elliot is important, but the lives and safety of so many in the pack are equally important! Go cool off for a while, then you’ll understand why I say so."

As I spoke, I shifted my gaze away from Armstrong and turned to instruct the silent Elizabeth, "Come help me; it’s really not working with just Ruth alone."

Elizabeth snapped back to reality, came over, and started to help, leaving Armstrong out in the cold.

"Did the Lycan King also agree to this?" Elizabeth quietly inquired.

At her question, I frowned and explained without turning my head, "Actually, I’ve lost contact with Donald. Not just me, Benjamin too. I’ve been trying to get in touch with him in the past 10 or so minutes, but still, there’s been no response."

"Why?"

Elizabeth’s face held the exact query as mine a moment ago, and that was something even Benjamin couldn’t answer; he speculated it might be because of the special miasma in the Misty Forest and its defensible terrain, which blocked all external mind-links.

That was also one of the reasons my mind was uneasy, I was somewhat worried about Donald’s safety.

chapters.title: Chapter 226: Now just leave
chapters.content:
Chapter 226: Now just leave

[Margaret’s POV]

I slowly shook my head, my tone calm as I replied, "I also don’t know why. But Benjamin guesses it might be related to the particular nature of the Misty Forest. Now I have no choice but to believe that Donald will definitely succeed."

Perhaps seeing the unease in my heart, Elizabeth sighed softly, took my hand, and said soothingly, "Someone as brave and smart as the Lycan King will surely be fine, so you can relax, Margaret."

I don’t want to be so anxious and uneasy, yet my feelings always seem beyond my control.

I guess I’m still not strong enough to face adversity, and that’s not good. I must grow and become more stable; only then will I truly be worthy of my mate—Donald.

I took a deep breath and put on a somewhat forced smile, "Of course I know he’s a very capable person, but that worry is always unavoidable. Perhaps you can relate, since Anthony is also on this mission."

Turning to look at Elizabeth, I thought for a moment before asking, "So how have you and Armstrong been these past days? Have you figured out what you want?"

"You know, if I can successfully escort Elliot back to the Palace this time, I might not return to the pack for a long time. Then you will have to face many things and make decisions on your own."

"But Father should be coming back soon; he might be able to help you," I added, thinking of our father who was out on a mission.

We always manage to give each other sensible advice or heartfelt comfort when discussing each other’s matters, but when it comes to ourselves, it’s rare to be absolutely rational and confident.

"What good advice can Father give me? He’d probably just be thrilled if I stuck close to Armstrong; after all, in his heart, there is nothing that pleases him more than honor and power!" Elizabeth said helplessly, her eyes filled with reluctance as she looked at me.

"I used to always dislike you, bully you, and now I can’t bear to see you leave; I think I must be crazy."

Who could have known that in such a short time, so many important things would happen? In the end, Elizabeth and I have grown up, we have changed.

I smiled at Elizabeth and teased, "After all, I am your sister born a few minutes before you. Even though we’ve had some misunderstandings and unpleasant things happen before, the bond of flesh and blood is not easily severed. Aren’t we always family? Didn’t you just call me ’sister’ in front of Armstrong, why have you changed back now?"

Elizabeth, not wanting to engage with my teasing, continued to busily work with Ruth.

Armstrong stood alone in the living room, his expression somber, his mind seemingly preoccupied with thoughts.

But none of these concerned me any longer; he couldn’t stop me from doing this, not from the beginning.

Soon, half an hour passed, and I met up with Benjamin at the entrance of the hospital. This was what we had agreed on earlier; the Guard Team was to assemble here and set off.

"Is Elliot settled in properly? We must protect him on this journey and not allow the slightest external factor to affect his injuries," I said, looking at the solid and understated carriage at the hospital entrance while talking to Benjamin.

Benjamin nodded, his tired, reddened eyes also glanced at the carriage, "Yes, Miss Margaret, Elliot has been properly arranged by me."

"This carriage was previously used by His Majesty, its safety and stability are absolutely reliable. It is very suitable for you and Elliot to travel in. I will also follow along with the Guard Team the whole way to promptly check on Elliot’s condition."

Benjamin was extremely capable in his work. I bowed slightly to him and sincerely said with a smile, "Thank you for your hard work, let’s set off now."

At my command, the Guard Team’s caravan, following the black, discrete stretch carriage, left the pack. They were well-organized and impressively stable, their presence alone being both daunting and reassuring.

I don’t know how the people in the pack found out about this, but by the time my caravan passed through the western gate of the pack, it was already crowded with many people, and among them were many faces I was very familiar with.

Of course, Armstrong and Elizabeth were there to see me off, but Selena standing behind them was evidently not there for that purpose. She had always gotten along poorly with Elizabeth, often taking it out verbally on me. Now that Elizabeth and I have mended our relationship, it must be quite uncomfortable for her.

If it had been before, I think I would have been pleased by this situation, but now I cannot feel that simple joy, because I carry a heavy burden.

chapters.title: Chapter 227: Werewolf Squad
chapters.content:
Chapter 227: Werewolf Squad

[Margaret’s POV]

Selena’s malice used to be one of my worries, but times have changed, and her annoyance has become less significant, at least I didn’t care at that moment.

Ruth opened the car door for me from the outside, I bent over to get out and looked at Elizabeth and Armstrong, "No need to see us off, we need to keep a low profile on this trip, Alpha, please take everyone back! Elizabeth, come here, I have a few words for you."

I beckoned Elizabeth over as I spoke.

Armstrong glanced at Elizabeth and took the initiative to say, "I know you won’t agree to my proposal, but your trip is fraught with danger. I just formed a squad on the spot; they will join the Guard Team in protecting you and the patient."

"You don’t need to say anything to refuse, this is just my sincere dedication to His Majesty, and the only thing I can do for Elliot. Be careful along the way, and let us know if anything happens. The Silver Moon Pack is loyal to the Wolf Royal Family."

Seeing the serious expression on Armstrong’s face, I knew that if I refused his kindness then, it would seem rather excessive, so I sincerely bowed slightly and quietly said, "Thank you."

Armstrong didn’t say anything, just looked at me gently, then walked up to the captain of the Guard Team, that is, Pack Louis who led the team to stay, and quietly talked with him. Given their serious expressions, they were probably discussing the Werewolf Squad joining the Guard Team.

I shifted my gaze back to Elizabeth, but she was still staring fixedly at Armstrong. Noticing my gaze, she sighed softly and said directly, "Do you think he’s really just trying to express his sincerity to His Majesty, or does he have other motives?"

"I really can’t see through him. What should I do, Margaret? I really don’t want you to leave. How about you let me go with you?"

After hearing Elizabeth’s words, I did not respond immediately but just looked at her tenderly.

I knew that wasn’t what she truly thought because she had prepared herself to face these issues alone. She was merely venting, "Come on, you know that’s not possible. I’m only doing this because I have no other choice. What would you do if you went with me? The journey is perilous and arduous, and you wouldn’t be able to endure it."

I said, glanced at someone behind us who was straining their ears to eavesdrop, and frowned slightly.

Elizabeth noticed Selena’s intentions at that moment too. She turned back somewhat angrily to Selena and scolded in a deep voice, "If you’re so eager to know what Margaret and I are discussing, why don’t you just come and stand here, Selena? You still sneak around in your dealings, completely lacking integrity!"

Elizabeth’s voice as she scolded Selena was quite loud, and her last words were downright harsh, attracting immediate attention from the surrounding crowd. Curious gazes inadvertently lingered on Selena and us.

Selena, feeling guilty as if a thief, was scrutinized by the crowd and immediately became flustered and angry, glaring with wide eyes and confronting Elizabeth fiercely, "Who is eavesdropping on your conversation? Don’t falsely accuse others!"

"Just because you became the pack’s luna, you think you can bully anyone you want. I’m not afraid of you two sisters. Don’t think you can bully me in front of all these people!"

She said, focusing her gaze intently on me, her eyes filled with envy and dissatisfaction.

She might think she had hidden her feelings well enough that no one would notice, but she didn’t realize all her little schemes were transparent before me. I saw through all her thoughts at a glance.

"That’s your own admission of ’eavesdropping’; we said nothing, so you’re merely incriminating yourself!" Elizabeth picked up on the flaw in Selena’s words, stood up straight, and pointed directly at her nose as she spoke.

Caught by Elizabeth’s words, Selena’s expression was quite spectacular, looking like a complex paint palette. Watching this, I found it somewhat amusing, turned my head back, and exhaled helplessly, not wanting to waste time with Selena here.

But perhaps Selena felt that I was mocking and provoking her, she immediately flared up and took a couple of steps forward, pointing at me and accusing loudly, "What are you laughing at? Are you mocking me? Margaret, do you really think that just because you partnered with the Lycan King, you’ve ascended to unreachable heights like a phoenix?"

"You really are shameless! After all, you’re not even as smart as Elizabeth. What makes you think the Lycan King would really fancy you?"

"His Majesty is just intrigued by the novelty of being with you, and once he gets bored of you, he’ll naturally cast you aside! Don’t come crying back from the Palace then, huh! Stop being so smug in front of me..."

chapters.title: Chapter 228: Lycan Queen’s Dignity
chapters.content:
Chapter 228: Lycan Queen’s Dignity

[Margaret’s POV]

"Ah!"

Before Selena could finish her last sentence, Elizabeth’s raised hand had already slapped her face.

The exclamation and the crisp sound of the slap rose together, drawing exceptionally noticeable attention; even Armstrong and Louis, who were behind the convoy, heard it.

Benjamin poked his head out of the car and asked with some worry, "What’s wrong, Miss Margaret? Was someone deliberately making things difficult for you?"

I gently grasped Elizabeth’s palm which had just struck someone. Her hand was slightly warm, and I thought it might even swell later on.

I shook my head and said, "It’s nothing serious, it’s just that someone was rude to me, and Luna Elizabeth gave them a lesson. It’s already been dealt with."

The slap Elizabeth had given Selena must have been with all her strength, otherwise her own hand wouldn’t be so warm.

I looked at Elizabeth’s frosty face, gently rubbed her hand, and gestured for her to calm down—for such a small matter, it wasn’t worth getting upset over.

"Someone was rude to you, Miss Margaret? They indeed deserved a good lesson! You are the mate recognized by His Majesty and the future Lycan Queen of the entire Wolf royal family; your dignity and reputation certainly shouldn’t be slandered by others!"

Benjamin was an extremely clever man; he quickly grasped the situation and declared very seriously, seemingly convinced I had been greatly wronged.

As Armstrong and Louis came over, he had just finished this speech, effectively setting the charge against Selena!

Armstrong looked sternly at Selena, his eyes seemingly brewing some anger.

Louis gestured to a Lycan behind him and ordered in a calm, deep voice, "Capture this she-wolf who offended the Lycan Queen, and hand her over to Alpha Armstrong. I trust her Alpha will handle this matter seriously."

"The honor and dignity of our Wolf royal family must not be compromised or slandered by anyone. If anyone else dares to offend the Lycan Queen in the future, she will be the first warning to all! Did everyone hear that?"

I glimpsed Louis, who spoke in a toned-down voice, and a different kind of thought flashed through my eyes.

Louis’s move to take advantage of the situation and make an example was executed flawlessly.

At least from my perspective, it was impeccable, scaring certain people in the Silver Moon Pack who coveted Elizabeth’s position, and the effect was naturally splendid.

It also opportunistically made the Guard Team’s Lycans understand my importance in Donald’s heart, thus inspiring deference towards me and ensuring their dedication to their escort mission.

This was a perfect strategy killing two birds with one stone. I had some admiration for Benjamin and Louis’ instant, tacit cooperation—they truly were battle-tested comrades over many years.

And all I needed to do at this moment was to remain composed and calm, subtly aligning with them.

Such a simple task, I naturally executed it perfectly; even Elizabeth seemed taken aback by my demeanor.

She looked at me and, lowering her voice, asked, "Aren’t you angry at all? Selena was insulting you just now!"

"Why should I be angry? Just because of her baseless allegations and rumors? I simply took her barking as meaningless noise. Would you take offense at the harsh barking of a dog?"

As I spoke, I touched Elizabeth’s face, the person I worried about most leaving behind in the Silver Moon Pack, "Remember, not everyone’s anger is worthwhile. Haggling with these people only lowers your own status and is meaningless."

"All we need to do is our own part, striving to stay calm and avoiding mistakes. As for the rest, we’ll leave that to others!"

"The status of my relationship with Donald is a matter between the two of us, and doesn’t allow for others to meddle or criticize. Should anyone be foolish and insolent enough to do so, it’s their own fault for the consequences they face. Am I right, Luna Elizabeth?"

My final words were intended for Selena and Armstrong to hear, of course, but also for the Guard Team’s Lycans who harbored disrespect towards me. I needed them to hold me in esteem out of respect for Donald’s position, even if just for my sister’s sake, and the gravely wounded Elliot.

"Time is pressing, so I will write the matters I wanted to tell you about in a message for you. Look it over when you have time. Goodbye, Elizabeth. Keep in touch when you can; I’ll miss you!"

After saying that last sentence, I turned and got into the car, commanding Louis with composure, "Let’s depart."

"Yes, my Queen."

Louis’s address of "my Queen" made my heart flutter, and my fingers involuntarily tightened around the hem of my dress. Fortunately, aside from the grievously injured and unconscious Elliot and the accompanying Benjamin in the car, there were no others. Neither of them could discern my nervousness and unusual behavior.

chapters.title: Chapter 229: Difficult decisions
chapters.content:
Chapter 229: Difficult decisions

[Donald’s POV]

The Misty Forest wasn’t any less dead just because it was daytime, the light and visibility slightly improved; it still exuded an oppressive dullness that inevitably agitated the mind.

Anthony looked up at the canopy above us and after pondering for a moment, said to me, "Your Majesty, simply chasing after her like this isn’t going to work. We definitely aren’t as familiar with the terrain here as Angel is, and she and those Lycans might just be lying in wait to ambush us; we can’t keep blundering deeper!"

"What are you suggesting? That we just let Angel get away? Do you have any idea how difficult it is to catch her? Her counter-tracking record at school was the best in the entire institute. If we miss this chance, we won’t be able to find her again!"

Mark’s impatience was evident in his eyes. Christian turned his head towards him, signaling him not to vent the fury of Angel slipping through our fingers again at his own people!

Indeed, yesterday’s operation to encircle Angel had ultimately failed, and she had once again escaped from my grasp.

We hadn’t expected Chris and another Lycan to be resolved to die last night in their attempt to save Angel!

Taking advantage of their familiarity with the layout of our stronghold and a disregard for their own lives, they had managed to create a diversion for Angel in their desperate fight and subsequent self-destruction, buying her a chance and the time to escape.

Angel had successfully fled, and two Lycans from my assault team had been injured during the enemy’s self-destruction. We had chased Angel deep into the Misty Forest, intent on completely taking down this ruthless, unrepentant traitor.

But as time rapidly slipped away, I started to accept the fact that Angel had managed to escape from us once more.

Anthony had been incredibly rational all along, what he said made a lot of sense; persisting in this fruitless chase was pointless. We didn’t have the time or the forces to play ’hide and seek’ with Angel in the forest—Margaret and Elliot were waiting for me to return!

For some reason, our contact with the outside world had been completely blocked without us knowing when it had started.

Margaret must be extremely worried not being able to reach me, I couldn’t continue this struggle with Angel. And the gravely injured Elliot didn’t have much time to spare for me.

"Call off the pursuit of Angel, turn around immediately, and signal the team in the stronghold. We’re heading back to the Silver Moon Pack," I said, squinting my eyes as I issued the military order.

"Your Majesty? Are we really giving up on this rare opportunity? Angel, she..."

"Choose between keeping Elliot alive or having Angel die; right now, you can only pick one of them," I said harshly, cutting off Mark’s anxious rebuttal and leaving him with two options, while I looked at him with cold calm eyes and furrowed brow.

Mark seemed to be frozen in place by the two difficult choices, staring blankly at me, speechless for a long time, his breathing heavy behind the gas mask.

I looked up at the silent people around me and silently sighed, "Once you’ve decided, let’s go, while we still have time."

Turning around, I walked at the front of the team, not worried that these Lycans, who valued loyalty, wouldn’t follow; they were surely well aware that Elliot was far more important than Angel.

No matter how difficult Angel was to capture, there would still be another chance to act; but if Elliot were to die from his injuries, there would truly be no possibility of his return. The weight of the two issues was clear to anyone who was lucid.

The rustling of footsteps sounded behind me—Christian and Mark had followed, along with the other Lycans who had been part of the pursuit of Angel.

When we arrived back at MASTER’s stronghold in the forest, it was already 7 o’clock in the morning. I realized we had to move quickly, or we might not make it back to the Silver Moon Pack before noon!

I didn’t want such a scenario to unfold; oftentimes during these moments of urgency and crisis, unexpected events are more likely to occur, and I worried that my beloved mate might not be able to handle such a situation.

When we left the Misty Forest with our prisoners, the sky outside was already bright. Today’s weather was poor, with clouds hanging in the sky, looking like it could rain at any moment.

My unease grew more pronounced; I had this nagging feeling that something bad had happened!

Following me, we moved swiftly through diverse woods and fields, the members of the assault team nearly struggling to keep up as I constantly urged and reminded them to follow, without any spare time for rest.

The rain ultimately fell, drenching us all like drowned wolves, bodies splattered with rainwater and mud, each one of us disheveled.

But at that moment, nobody cared about such things; we all knew that time was of the essence for Elliot’s life.

Anthony and Christian stuck close behind me.

chapters.title: Chapter 230: Act Separately
chapters.content:
Chapter 230: Act Separately

[Donald’s POV]

While we were crossing a swamp, Anthony suddenly said he had received a mind-link from Armstrong!

The moment I heard this information, I tried to contact Margaret, thinking the barrier between us had disappeared, but I didn’t expect Margaret to still not respond. My heart sank, and that unsettling feeling surged back.

I stopped and turned to look at Anthony, who was in contact with Armstrong.

About ten seconds later, clarity returned to Anthony’s eyes. I looked at him and urgently asked, "What did Armstrong say? Has something happened to Margaret?"

Anthony looked at me strangely, as if wondering why I didn’t contact Margaret myself, but ultimately, as an extremely intelligent and measured warrior, he quickly suppressed his doubts and began answering my questions.

He said, "Yes, Your Majesty, it was our Alpha who initiated contact with me. But it’s not Miss Margaret who’s in trouble, it’s Elliot!"

He paused, and his gaze darkened, "Elliot’s injuries suddenly worsened this morning, and Benjamin is at a loss. They had no choice but to report the matter to Miss Margaret, they..."

"And then what? What happened to them? Keep talking!" I pressed, irritated by Anthony’s hesitancy.

Anthony swallowed, seemingly to ease his own nerves, "The Alpha said they set out from the pack early in the morning. It was Miss Margaret and Louis who led the team, with the healer Benjamin accompanying them. They’ve been on the move for nearly six hours!"

This sudden news stunned me for a second, but instead of pressing further, I fell into a contemplation.

Why can’t I establish a mind-link with Margaret now that she has only left the Silver Moon Pack? Is it a problem just between her and me?

What about the other Lycans who are with her; have they received any mind-links from the Lycans back at the pack? Why haven’t Louis and Benjamin proactively contacted me?

If Anthony can now contact Armstrong, why can’t I?

Many questions flashed through my mind, and I found it to be a particularly strange point in the situation. First, I turned to Christian and Mark at my side and asked, "Have Benjamin and Louis not contacted you? I mean, since we left the Misty Forest?"

Upon hearing this, they quickly shook their heads, clearly bewildered, "Your Majesty, has something happened? Why do you look so grim? Is there something wrong with Miss Margaret?" Christian asked first.

I shook my head, thinking to myself that it was all very ghostly! If only I knew what had happened to Margaret, then at least I wouldn’t be as clueless as I am now, relying on wild guesses.

"I can’t reach her right now. You both try to contact Benjamin and Louis, see if anyone responds." I thought for a moment and then continued to instruct, looking at them with some expectation.

Watching Christian and Mark nodding silently one after the other, I swallowed nervously and turned to Anthony.

"You have to follow me for now. I need you to keep contacting Armstrong so I can stay informed about Margaret’s group. Christian, you stay behind; we need to act separately now. Make sure you keep an eye on these prisoners and bring them safely back to the Palace."

As I talked, I looked back at the other Lycans, slowed my somewhat anxious breathing, and continued to give orders, "None of us will go back to the Silver Moon Pack now. We need to find a way to contact Louis’s group, who set out before us, and catch up as fast as we can to the six hours we’re behind them."

"I know this will be very tough, but I can’t let Margaret and the others continue like this. They have too few guards with them, and the road back to the Palace is far too dangerous. We need to meet up with them quickly..." before anything irreversible happens!

I didn’t voice the last part of the sentence. I just looked with a heavy expression at the somewhat tired and bedraggled group of Lycan warriors.

I absentmindedly grasped a handful of my wet hair, looking at the dreary road ahead and said gravely, "Let’s move out."

Christian stood behind me, seemingly wanting to say something, but in the end, he remained silent. He respectfully nodded to me, said ’yes’, and then turned to urgently command the Lycan warriors.

We had to allocate all the hands we had so that both sides of our mission could proceed smoothly.

chapters.title: Chapter 231: Margaret’s Letter
chapters.content:
Chapter 231: Margaret’s Letter

[Donald’s POV]

After splitting into two groups with Christian, I led the Lycan Guard along with the other Lycan warriors, nearly 40 people in all, and continued marching forward.

Our marching speed was extremely fast, but still, it couldn’t compare with a fast-moving convoy of vehicles. I realized that this wouldn’t do; I needed to find a way to acquire some vehicles. Only then would we have a chance to catch up with the group that Margaret was in.

Fortunately, between Silver Moon Pack and the Palace, there were several relay stations, and I could have the next one arrange some for me.

Without delay, having made up my mind, I tasked Mark with contacting the Lycans at the relay station. Now, I had no other capable subordinates by my side; they had all been dispersed.

Eric had followed my orders and stayed with Silver Moon Pack, primarily responsible for communication between Silver Moon Pack and the Palace. Christian, now burdened with a dozen werewolf prisoners, had also fallen behind us.

Elliot was seriously injured and unconscious, while Benjamin had only been a medic in the assault team before this. For him to step up during a crisis like Louis did and willingly take on the full responsibility and task of the entire Guard Team was already quite remarkable.

I’m gratified that my subordinates are all competent Lycans capable of standing on their own.

Of course, the most important person is Margaret! My mate is such a beautiful and kind girl, delicate and in need of protection, yet now she also had to shouldering her responsibilities as a Lycan Queen!

I’m not angry with Margaret for acting on her own, because she simply wanted to ensure Elliot’s survival.

Ever since she’s been with me, she seems to have become braver and more resolute, which is a good thing, although it always makes me feel heartache for her.

Actually, when I first heard the news, my initial reaction wasn’t anger or outrage, but rather a touch of surprise, followed by a full chest of worry and urgency!

Having such an empathetic and capable mate, I truly consider myself an incredibly fortunate man.

Margaret, of course, couldn’t know what I was thinking at that moment, but I believe she could sense it, just as she knows I wouldn’t really get angry with her over this matter.

Our understanding of and trust in each other had gradually been building up over this period of time.

After marching through the torrential rain for nearly 2 hours, we finally got the vehicles needed for traveling at the relay station we had contacted in advance.

Jerris stood not far behind us, handling a simple handover with the people from the relay station’s vehicle team, while Mark and I pulled aside the person in charge of this station, asking him some other very important questions.

The person in charge was a very calm-looking Lycan who stood by my side with utmost respect.

I raised my hand to point to the main surveillance at the entrance of their station and asked in a deep voice, "Can that monitor capture all the vehicles that passed by here today? I need all the footage it captured this morning. I’m giving you 5 minutes. Send it directly to my phone, understand?"

The Lycan, seeing the serious expression on my face, didn’t dare to ask anything extra but nodded his head, quickly instructing someone nearby to copy the recordings.

After giving the instructions, I took a closer look at him and suddenly felt he looked familiar, "Have I seen you before? The last time we passed through this relay station, were you the one who received us?"

As if he had not expected at all that I would remember him, the Lycan’s expression became excited, and he frantically nodded, "Yes, Your Majesty! We only met briefly, and I’m so happy that you still remember me!"

I wasn’t too concerned with his excitement, but an idea sparked in my mind, and I continued to ask, "Then you should recognize the black limousine that I rode in last time we came by, right? It should have passed through this entrance this morning. Do you remember it?"

I had only asked casually, with a probing tone, but unexpectedly, I received a response from the person in charge. When I saw him nodding and bowing again, I was even a bit surprised!

"Yes, Your Majesty. I remember the vehicle you mentioned and the convoy that accompanied it."

He took an envelope from another Lycan behind him, extended it towards me with both hands and continued to explain seriously, "In fact, when the Lycan Queen and Lord Benjamin passed by here, they left a letter for you and also instructed us to proactively contact you to inform you of their following route."

"It’s strange to say, I don’t know how the Lycan Queen guessed that you’d come here to look for news afterward. She really is a very smart person."

As I listened to his report, I quickly tore open the envelope.

The handwriting inside was undoubtedly Margaret’s, which I recognized at a glance.

chapters.title: Chapter 232: Starry Ravine
chapters.content:
Chapter 232: Starry Ravine

[Donald’s POV]

Margaret’s handwriting was exceptionally graceful and beautiful, with a hint of pride hidden within that others simply couldn’t replicate!

I was certain it was Margaret who had written this—as I skimmed through the information, I looked up at the responsible party with furrowed brows and asked with some alarm, "They encountered a heavy downpour right after leaving the Silver Moon Pack, and after receiving the message that the road ahead had collapsed, they had no choice but to reroute to Starry Ravine?"

"It was only when they passed here on their way back that they left this message for us. So, is this why we couldn’t get through to their phones? Your signal tower was damaged by the storm, correct? When will it be back up and running?"

As if caught off guard by the question, he hesitated before nodding and replied, "Yes, Your Majesty, we are currently unable to reach the Queen’s convoy as well. If you wish to proceed despite the rain, I do have a satellite phone you can use."

I patted his shoulder and after a few seconds of silence, I said, "You’ve done well. We need this information... and that satellite phone. But it would have been better if you had told me this the moment I arrived."

After I finished speaking, I turned to Mark and snapped my fingers, loudly commanding, "Take all the equipment, destination Starry Ravine, let’s move."

Upon hearing my command, Mark nodded and solemnly responded, ’Yes,’ then he gave the stationary responsible party a deep look before turning to set the group into motion.

The responsible party seemed shaken by my last word, and Mark’s particularly sharp gaze, taking quite a while to recover.

I gestured for the Lycan warrior in the driver’s seat to get out, then I took his place.

The vehicle was a high-chassis black off-road, perfectly suited for the challenging and variable terrain of Starry Ravine. Mark quickly followed, taking the seat beside me, clutching the invaluable satellite phone, and muttered, "This guy really talks too damn much! If he had reported to us as soon as we arrived, we wouldn’t have wasted these few minutes. His brain must be defective."

Mark was voicing exactly what I was thinking, but I couldn’t afford to dwell on it given our urgent timeline.

I glanced at Mark and expertly started the car, "Keep everyone close, we are racing against time."

After speaking, I glanced at the rearview mirror. The Lycans had already loaded all the prepared supplies on the vehicles, with just a few stragglers yet to board.

I pressed the accelerator, and the sleek black off-road charged into the curtain of rain like a beast that had long been in hiding.

With my lead vehicle setting the pace, the black convoy behind us surged forth in succession, disappearing into the heavy rain. It was only then that the responsible party slowly came to his senses, but all we left him with was a string of our red taillights.

I certainly didn’t care about his fear; I was still pondering why.

If it was just a signal tower malfunction, why were we completely unable to contact Margaret and her group? Could there be something else that I’m unaware of?

Why do Margaret and my mind-link always encounter various strange problems? Even Benjamin and I have never previously been unable to reach each other using mind-link. It was really bizarre.

I thought that after catching up with them later, I would definitely have Benjamin think this situation through and figure out how to clear up and solve this issue!

I absolutely cannot stand the feeling of being cut off from my mate. If not for the pressing situation now, I think I would genuinely lose my mind.

Speaking of our next destination—Starry Ravine, Devil’s Valley inevitably comes to mind.

If it hadn’t been for the terrible weather and the extremely poor road conditions on the highway to the Palace, I think Margaret and her group wouldn’t have considered passing through Starry Ravine.

The name Devil’s Valley might sound a bit terrifying, but the canyon at the very end of this valley, Starry Ravine, is truly very dangerous!

Because of natural geographical reasons, the canyon walls on both sides of Starry Ravine are very narrow and meandering. Apart from some fierce wild animals prowling around, there are also unpredictable rockfalls or mudslides.

If you’re not careful, you might just become a feast for the wild animals in the valley!

Although I finally received information about Margaret, my worries actually deepened instead of easing.

Given today’s terrible weather, the safety index for the convoy traveling through Starry Ravine isn’t much better than taking the collapsed highway.

This is indeed a very dangerous and challenging task! Thinking about Margaret and all these bothersome issues, my handsome brows deeply furrowed as if etching a rugged mountain range on my forehead.

chapters.title: Chapter 233: Several troublesome matters
chapters.content:
Chapter 233: Several troublesome matters

[Benjamin’s POV]

If I had the ability to see into the future, I think I would definitely not have suggested that our convey take a detour through Starry Ravine!

Even though the highway had collapsed due to the torrential rain, I was sure it could have been quickly repaired, and we might even have been able to wait for His Majesty and the others on the spot. After all, since we hit the road, Elliot’s condition had stabilized somewhat, and he was no longer as critical and worrying as he had been within the Silver Moon Pack.

I think it was probably because I had administered to him the new medicine I had successfully developed.

Back to the matter of detouring through Starry Ravine, it truly was a terribly bad idea. Although the Lycan Queen and the members of the Guard Team didn’t blame me, staring at the predicament in front of us, I plunged into endless self-reproach and annoyance once again.

The downpour still went on, and the sky had also slowly darkened. It was hardly possible for us to stay here for too long, we had to quickly resolve the several troublesome matters in front of us!

First were the assorted rocks and mud that had tumbled onto the road surface from the mountain due to the rain. Second were the unevolved, gaping-mawed wild beasts that had been drawn to our halted convoys by the lights and noise.

The third matter was the most bizarre and baffling one, which was that all of us had completely lost contact with the outside world!

After entering the valley, our cell phones all lost their signal, and even the mind-link between werewolves, the closest and most reliable form, stopped working.

This really was like seeing a ghost. I cursed silently in my heart, rolled up my sleeves, and got out of the car with the Lycans of the Guard Team to clear the scattered rocks from the road.

Of course, we weren’t foolish enough to focus all our attention and manpower on clearing the rocks. The growls of wild animals coming from the woods on both sides of the road made us very wary too.

Margaret had been driving the motorhome behind us with a worried frown the whole time.

Yes, even she had taken initiative and started driving the motorhome. Now, our usual motorhome driver was standing with me, walking along the road in the pouring rain, occasionally needing the combined strength of several people just to push aside large rocks that were lying across the road.

Louis had taken a few Lycans to be responsible for the security of the entire convoy. They directly killed any foolish beasts that rushed towards us seeking death by drawing their swords.

In fact, if it wasn’t for this ravaging rain and the boulders on the road, we could have crossed Starry Ravine quickly and reached the next station to rest and wait. But now the situation is incredibly frustrating and speechless, because we were in a dilemma with no good way forward or back.

"Benjamin! Stop worrying about those rocks for now and come back to check Elliott’s condition. His temperature seems to have risen again,"

It was the Lycan Queen calling my name from the driver’s seat, poking her head out. I instinctively looked back and took 2 seconds to register what the Queen was saying.

I patted the shoulder of the Lycan beside me to signal that I was going to go check and to be careful when he was working alone.

Seeing his nod of agreement, I turned and walked back into the motorhome. I was soaked through by the rain, so I could only hurriedly take off my raincoat and jacket and quickly sit beside Elliot’s sickbed, reaching out to check on him carefully.

Elliot’s body temperature had indeed risen a bit, and his abdominal wound had seeped blood due to the unavoidable jolts of the journey, making it stand out starkly against the white gauze.

"The temperature is a little high, but it’s not a big problem. But his abdominal wound needs to be redressed. I’m going to wash my hands and be right back. Ruth, could you prepare the things for me?"

I informed the Queen in a soft voice, then turned to look at the other person in the motorhome, the maidservant the Queen had brought from the pack named Ruth.

Ruth had very nimble hands for everyday tasks, but seemed somewhat averse to the smell of blood. With only me and her in the motorhome besides the Queen, it wasn’t really possible for me to task the Queen with such things, right?

Although the Queen truly had no airs about her and was very kind and friendly when she was with us, I still needed to be cautious about certain things, such as showing her respect.

But the next second, I found myself reluctantly putting that thought on hold, as the Queen still took on the task I had given to Ruth.

I had said before that she was a very smart woman, and she had a talent for medicine and treatment. If my professors at medical school could meet her, they would surely be extremely excited and happy, because such a talented student like her is actually quite rare.

The Queen simply said ’Let me do it’ to Ruth in a soft voice, then quickly took out everything I needed from the medicine kit.

chapters.title: Chapter 234: Attacked
chapters.content:
Chapter 234: Attacked

[Benjamin’s POV]

"What’s the road ahead like? If it’s still this impassable, we might have to spend the night in this ravine, which isn’t good news for us."

Margaret, delivering a bandage to me, spoke with a worried tone.

I was well aware of our grim situation, and after a heavy sigh, I replied, "The road behind us might be even worse! We haven’t even completed half of Starry Ravine yet, and the rain doesn’t seem to want to stop. It looks like we will have to spend the night in the ravine."

As I spoke, I skillfully applied medicine to Elliot and, looking at his unconsciously furrowed brow, I sighed again and continued, "I’m really sorry, Lycan Queen. I had no idea there would be so many issues in this ravine. If I had..."

"You don’t need to apologize to me; we all know you did it for Elliot’s sake. It’s everyone’s wish, we all hope that Elliot stays well. So, please, stop blaming yourself. It wasn’t just your decision to take this route, and if anyone should be saying sorry, it should be me, considering I’m mainly responsible for this situation."

The Lycan Queen interrupted me tenderly; she was still that understanding and gentle girl.

Relieved by her words, I smiled lightly, the guilt and anxiety in my heart slightly fading away, and my hands moved faster wrapping Elliot’s wounds.

Just as I threw away the blood-stained bandage and turned to signal to the Lycan Queen that I was going to hop off to continue working, a sudden shout, followed by an enraged roar of a beast, burst from outside the vehicle!

"Ah!... Help!"

"Roar!"

[Margaret’s POV]

Benjamin and I almost simultaneously pushed open the door and ran out. The shout came from directly in front of the vehicle. Due to the dense vegetation all around our heads and the dim twilight, that spot was pitch-black; even the headlights couldn’t illuminate it!

"Who’s out there? Jack? Is anyone there?" I gripped the dagger previously tied around my waist and asked in a lowered voice.

My voice seemed to fall into an infinite black hole, receiving no response, but Jack, who had been clearing rocks with Benjamin, should have been right there.

I exchanged a glance with Benjamin, pulled out the sharp dagger at my waist, and crouched slightly, advancing steps with Benjamin.

Following us stealthily were Louis and several Lycans. I made a gesture to them to spread out and approach from both sides.

Before we had taken a few steps forward, a sticky scent of blood mixed with rain assaulted us!

By the time I saw what was attacking us, my heartbeat almost stopped. Benjamin and Louis’s piercing warnings rang in my ears; I instinctively retreated, and a sound of air being sliced was less than 10 centimeters from my face followed by a chilling roar.

"Roar!"
