"It’s a tiger! Everyone, step back!"

"Quick, retreat, Lycan Queen! Move to the left!"

"God! Is that Jack’s hand? That beast attacked Jack! It even ate his hand! Fuck! Brothers, kill it, avenge Jack!"

I actually couldn’t hear the noisy chaos around me clearly; all my attention was on this giant tiger with a bloodied maw and a fierce gaze.

Survival instincts made me instinctively lean to the left, and with a neat roll, I successfully moved out of the tiger’s attack range. Suppressing my heart that almost jumped to my throat, my right hand holding the dagger was slightly trembling.

This was almost an innate fear deep in my bones. Even though I was a werewolf endowed with intelligence, and the other was just an unevolved tiger, I was still afraid of it.

It really looked strong, well-fed, clearly accustomed to dominating the ravine.

I didn’t know why it came out to hunt on a rainy day and even attacked humans, which seemed quite unusual and suspicious. I speculated baselessly as I looked into its terrifying, bloodshot eyes.

As we got a clear look at what it had just done and managed to maintain some distance, the situation on the field began to change slowly!

Surrounded by dozens of us, with the headlights of the vehicles not far off lighting up, we could almost see every move it made.

Louis, leading several Lycans, was the first to complete their shift and began to circle the tiger slowly. Those sharp, gleaming wolf eyes looked as if they would tear it apart!

chapters.title: Chapter 235: Anesthesia gun
chapters.content:
Chapter 235: Anesthesia gun

[Margaret’s POV]

The raindrops, as large as beans, fell from the sky for free, causing some pain when they struck the body. The rain, accompanied by a chilly wind blowing through the canyon from who knows where, uncontrollably started to make everyone shiver with cold.

I looked at the fierce beast encircled by Louis and several Lycans in the middle, and whispered to Benjamin beside me, "You should go back to the car first. If you see anything wrong, just drive off without worrying about us."

At that moment, Benjamin’s nerves must have been extremely tense because after I finished speaking, he just stared at me blankly for a moment before finally snapping back to reality, "What did you say? No! I would never leave any member of the Guard Team behind, especially not the Lycan Queen!"

As he spoke, he turned his attention back to the nearby battle, but continued to add, "Louis and his team are very experienced in combat, it’s not certain they will lose to this beast. I still have some tranquilizer guns that can be used here to help them."

"Rather, the Lycan Queen should get back to the vehicle quickly. The situation is too dangerous now. You can’t stay outside any longer; you should be like Elliot and stay inside the RV without coming out! Please go back, we’ve got this!"

Benjamin had already pulled something out from the bag on his body after he finished speaking. It must be the tranquilizer gun he mentioned.

The battle ahead of us had officially begun. Louis charged directly at the beast, letting out a chilling howl from his throat, trying to overpower the beast with his presence and make the beast wary of us.

Benjamin and I looked over at the same time as Louis’s sharp wolf claws fiercely slashed across the beast’s neck. The beast let out a low painful cry, but showed no intention of retreating. The light in my eyes dimmed as I realized this was going to be a tough battle.

"Not good! Its skin is too thick and tough. Louis and the others can hardly inflict any major damage," Benjamin analyzed in a low growl through the rain.

Watching Louis smoothly dodge the beast’s claw, I let out a slight sigh of relief, looked back at Benjamin, and pointed at the object in his hand urgently said, "It looks like we’ll have to use that! We don’t have much time to wrestle with it, and, our people can’t afford to fall here either."

We probably can’t kill this tiger in a short time, but using a tranquilizer to bring it down is still a good strategy.

As I spoke, I wiped the rainwater off my face with my hand and made up my mind, "I have a plan. These beasts have strong combat and defense abilities, so we need to find a way to inject the tranquilizer where its skin is softest! But that seems like a difficult task..."

Benjamin should have understood the unfinished implication in my words by now.

Tigers are generally fierce and good at fighting. When they fight animals of the same size, there are few that can compete. But there is no truly flawless creature in the world—these beasts have their weaknesses too!

They are huge, with agile limbs, sharp claws, and teeth, but their bellies are extremely soft, and this is one of their innate flaws.

Of course, it’s also one of our weaknesses as werewolves. The reason Elliot was injured this badly this time is precisely because of this flaw!

"Roar!"

"Be careful! Get behind it!"

"Damn it! Why is its skin so thick?"

Louis and his team had been sparring with the tiger for several rounds, but still couldn’t injure it, which inevitably made everyone a bit anxious.

Hearing their conversation, Benjamin knew this wasn’t the time to hesitate. He clenched his teeth and urgently said to me, "I understand! You must take this tranquilizer gun. Louis, the rest of us, and I will draw its complete attention! This gun has three arrows loaded with tranquilizers, and you only have three chances."

After succinctly conveying his message, Benjamin handed me the tranquilizer gun. I had the same thoughts, so without any hesitation, I took it straight from his hand.

I couldn’t just abandon the Guard Team still fighting in the storm and run back to the vehicle to hide; something in my blood was calling me to join the fight!

Moreover, looking at the current situation, it’s not necessarily absolutely safe inside the RV.

Because while we were engaged in a fierce struggle with the tiger, the last car of the Guard Team’s convoy was suddenly hit by a giant rock falling from above the canyon, immediately wrecking the vehicle!

Fortunately, everyone was working in the storm at the time and no one was sitting in the vehicle, thus avoiding any casualties.

chapters.title: Chapter 236: Fight
chapters.content:
Chapter 236: Fight

[Margaret’s POV]

Now, I had no other thoughts besides wanting everyone to survive and walk out of this gorge alive—that was all that mattered.

But even such a small wish, it seemed that the heavens did not intend for me to fulfill it.

Our losses had already occurred without us realizing!

A Lycan beside Louis was sent flying by a tiger, and before he could even cry out in pain, he had heavily crashed against the rock wall and then fell to the ground motionless.

My eyes widened instantly, and I had the urge to go over and check on him, but Benjamin and Louis’ shouts instantly woke me from that moment’s clarity.

They almost simultaneously yelled out a reminder to shoot:

"Lycan Queen! Get down! Raise your gun and shoot!"

"Now! Shoot its belly!"

In the recent times, my combat skills and response ability in battle had significantly improved. Apart from my intensive training, being marked by Donald was certainly a reason for my rapid progress.

I was no longer my former self; I could now ensure my own safety and even contribute in some simple battles, like this moment!

I crouched down decisively, the action of firing my gun even faster than my brain’s neural commands.

The tiger, cornered by Louis and Benjamin on either side, had its huge eyes fixed on the Lycan in front of it. It slightly lowered its body and its sturdy hind legs firmly gripped the ground—a signal that it was about to attack.

After a short growl, its entire body suddenly lunged towards the Lycan, its gaping mouth aimed at the Lycan’s neck!

I guessed this beast was somewhat clever, as it had chosen the smallest in size among the Lycans and the closest to it as its target.

The moment Benjamin and Louis shouted their warnings, I had already rolled into the mud on the ground, squinting as I pulled the trigger of my tranquilizer gun.

The sound of the arrow tearing through the air vanished in the rain, and I stared unblinkingly as the tiger roared in pain upon being shot!

I hit the beast with my first shot, but it still pounced on the Lycan. I swallowed hard and fired again; since the distance between me and the Lycan was very close, this shot even struck the tiger’s eye after it had knocked down the Lycan and hit the ground!

It cried out in pain, its gaping mouth no longer paying attention to the Lycan beneath it, and with an arrow-struck head, it lunged at me again.

My heart stopped in fright, and driven by my brain’s survival instinct, my body finished shifting in an instant, and I rolled away from harm!

The nauseating smell of its bloody breath had already sprayed onto my cheek, brushing close to the hair on my face.

This was indeed a narrow escape! I had practically performed a miraculous act of survival. Watching this scene unfold, Louis and Benjamin were almost scared to death.

I think I must have been terrified too, because after Betty had rolled away, her hind legs trembled slightly and she nearly didn’t manage to stand firm!

<That scared me to death! I thought it had bitten me. It really charged directly at you—that thing sure holds a grudge!> Betty murmured in immense shock.

Of course, the beast held a grudge. I had shot it twice, each shot more severe than the last! Even abandoning the Lycan that was close at hand, it tried to bite through my neck.

The tranquilizer took effect quickly within the tiger’s body, and seeing its staggering steps, I knew our plan had succeeded!

I glanced sideways at Benjamin and Louis and whispered softly, "The drug’s taking effect. Let’s all go at once and try to kill it right here!"

After saying this, I was the first to crouch down and charge forward, but a shout from behind suddenly made my mind reel, and I involuntarily turned back to look!

I think this silvery figure will leave an indelible mark in the river of my life going forward.

The shocking shout that had dazed me came from Donald; when I saw the concern and surprise in his beautiful, deep wolf eyes, he had already reached in front of me, likely terrified.

"Margaret?! Get out of the way!"

Donald’s sudden appearance froze me in place, completely unaware of the beast staggering two meters away, refusing to fall!

As it slowly approached my direction, my eyes held only Donald.

I was harshly knocked aside by Donald, while he was struck on the shoulder by the tiger’s paw!

I cried out his name desperately, my feet instantly gripping the ground, and my body curved like an arrow sprung from a bow, fiercely counterattacking.

Accompanying my movement were Louis and Benjamin, and somewhat behind them was Anthony.

chapters.title: Chapter 237: God-like Arrival
chapters.content:
Chapter 237: God-like Arrival

[Margaret’s POV]

"Donald!"

"Your Majesty!"

I felt as though I had thrown myself onto a robust beast, but all that occupied my mind was the sight of Donald’s shoulder, slightly bleeding.

As if telepathically connected, I nearly transformed into my human form together with Donald.

I knelt down, drained of strength on the muddy road, and just as I was about to lift my head and check his shoulder, I was pulled into a warm, broad embrace the next second.

"I was really going crazy, Margaret! Can you please not leave my side again?"

Donald’s warm breath caressed my ear, and I trembled slightly, barely noticeable, feeling my eyes grow hot and wet as something scalding began to flow.

My tears mixed with the rain and blood soaking Donald’s neck. He held me tightly, as if trying to crush and meld me into his flesh to ensure we could never be separated again!

I missed him too, my mate had descended from the heavens like a God, saving me from the brink of death once more.

This almost cruel reality sent a chill through me, for without Donald’s intervention, I suspected that my encounter with the beast might have ended with more than just a shattered shoulder...

Even though its nerves were numbed by the tranquilizer, its combat prowess was not something a wolf of my stature could challenge, and at that moment I had foolishly thought about taking it down directly. I must have been slightly mad.

As for the fact that the tiger was ultimately subdued and pinned to the ground by me and a host of Lycans, I suppose that can only be explained by my desperate concern and sudden burst of strength in battle.

As Donald held me and we slowly walked back to the caravan, I looked back at the beast lying on the ground, gasping for breath.

The lying beast looked much larger than when it stood, and a surge of fear overwhelmed me, causing me to clutch the corner of Donald’s shirt that was touching me.

"Are you shivering, Margaret? Are you scared, or are you ill?" Donald asked softly as he led me into the caravan.

I shook my head and looked up at him, seeing my haggard reflection in his gray-green eyes, my eyes grew hot again, "I’m fine, Donald. I just missed you so much!"

Donald’s eyes filled with pain as he gazed at me. He bowed his head and gently kissed my forehead, then my slightly reddened eyes, and finally licked the tears off my face.

This tender and affectionate gesture made my heart soften, and clutching his neck, I started to weep softly, "Why did it take you so long to catch up? Don’t you know how worried I was when I couldn’t reach you?"

"Elliot’s injuries suddenly worsened, and the situation was too critical at that moment. Benjamin and I were utterly helpless! Donald, I didn’t know what to do, I couldn’t just let Elliot die before my eyes!"

"I was so afraid something would happen to you in the Misty Forest, but I had to force myself to trust you, to believe in our rapport. Today has been so exhausting, Donald. Did you go through days like this before? I never want to be apart from you again!"

I cried out all the unease and fear I harbored within me that day, letting my emotions flood out in Donald’s arms, as if this God-like man was the sole pillar of my spirit, without whom I couldn’t survive on my own.

And indeed, it seemed to be the case. All my pent-up emotions had collapsed the moment I saw Donald, reasonlessly and utterly. This was even more thrilling and grievous than the last time I had escaped from Angel’s clutches.

Donald held me tight, and words of comfort and love never ceased from his lips. After about three minutes, I regained a bit of my composure, organizing my shattered emotions and looking up at Donald’s somewhat pale face.

Thinking of his injury, I restrained myself and sat up straight from his embrace, turning my head to look at his shoulder, "Sorry, I lost control of my emotions for a bit. How is your shoulder? I’ll go and call Benjamin to take a look at it!"

Just as I was about to rise, Donald grasped my wrist, "It’s nothing, we can look at it later. Just sit with me for a while. They’ll come over after handling things outside, there’s no need to remind them specially."

The warm temperature of Donald’s palm heated my wrist, and I couldn’t resist his request, especially when those deep, beautiful eyes looked only at me.

I grasped his hand back, my eyes red as I obediently nodded my head, agreeing to his suggestion, while my peripheral vision only now noticed the inside of the caravan, where Ruth, silent and head down, had watched my breakdown in front of the small infirmary where Elliot had lain all day.

I realized she had seen me collapse in tears and momentarily felt a flush of heat and embarrassment.

chapters.title: Chapter 238: Guard Team casualties
chapters.content:
Chapter 238: Guard Team casualties

[Margaret’s POV]

I had just been unreasonably venting my pent-up emotions at Donald, and while I could bear to see that out-of-control side of myself when facing my mate, I still felt somewhat embarrassed if a stranger were to see it.

The plan was as childish as a three-year-old’s.

Donald, keenly aware of my thoughts, glanced at Ruth, whose presence was almost negligible, and a tender smile slowly spread across his eyes, "Feeling shy now? You weren’t embarrassed when you were crying with me earlier."

As he spoke, he leaned in to kiss me and continued in a hushed voice, "Don’t be shy or embarrassed; I like it when you vent your emotions to me. It means you recognize me as your closest, most trusted person. I like that. Margaret, I love you."

Donald seldom opened up like this, and I was almost surprised as I opened my eyes to look at him, but I quickly ’drowned’ in his gaze which held only me.

I tilted my chin up to kiss him back, not caring anymore whether Ruth was watching. She hadn’t made a sound from the beginning, but eventually, it was Benjamin and his companions, concerned about Donald’s injuries, who disrupted the hard-earned peace and intimacy between Donald and me.

"Your Majesty! Are you... are you alright? I apologize for interrupting you and the Lycan Queen, but we need to tend to your wound first," Benjamin said.

Perhaps it was seeing Donald and me together as he opened the car door that caused Benjamin to stutter a bit before he continued his speech.

I slightly pulled away from Donald, not looking at the Lycan King’s somewhat teasing gaze, and said directly to Benjamin, "Let’s do it, Benjamin. I can be your assistant."

As I spoke, I got up and brought the medical kit from inside the motorhome, watching as Benjamin began treating Donald’s wounds.

Donald’s shoulder was smeared with blood, looking quite gruesome. I frowned deeply, feeling incredibly distressed as it seemed Donald always got hurt saving me, just like the time when Angel got hurt, and now again.

"How is it? Is the injury severe? Just tell me if there’s anything I need to do," I asked Benjamin.

Benjamin’s expression was somber, but not overly worried or grave, which probably meant it was not too serious.

But at that moment, I had lost most of my rationality to ponder over this question, and I was just full of worry and sympathy for Donald.

"His Majesty’s injury isn’t severe; the Lycan Queen doesn’t need to worry too much. The hit didn’t damage his muscles or bones; he just needs to take his medicine and rest well," Benjamin responded.

Upon hearing Benjamin’s reply, some of my unease dissolved. I handed over the antiseptic and pain relief medications. As I looked at Donald’s somewhat pale face, I murmured, "That’s good, I will make sure he takes his medicine and rests well."

After helping Donald change into clean clothes, I remembered to ask Benjamin about the situation outside, "How are the Guard Team’s casualties? Louis isn’t hurt is he? And that Lycan who was pounced by the tiger? Oh right..."

I paused briefly, then looked at Benjamin and continued gravely, "What about Jack? Is he still alive? I only saw him lying in a pool of blood, his hand..." His hand had been eaten by that beast.

I didn’t finish the last sentence, just tightened my hold on Donald’s hand. Jack was the Lycan driving us today and had been the first one attacked by the beast.

Actually, I am very afraid of seeing people around me get hurt and bleed. A young Lycan I’ve spent just half a day with made me this upset; I can hardly imagine how I would react if something similar happened to Donald! I might simply go mad.

"The Guard Team lost three Lycans: Jack, Leon, and Tony, who was unfortunately hit on the head by a falling rock. Everyone else also sustained various degrees of minor injuries. Fortunately, His Majesty has now joined us, his brought Lycans should suffice to protect us for the remainder of our mission," Benjamin explained.

Thinking of that Lycan who had been hit by the beast right in front of us, my mood became heavier. I closed my eyes and observed a half-minute of silence, then holding Donald’s hand, I said solemnly, "Let’s take them home. The weather in Starry Ravine is too harsh all year round. I’m afraid they won’t see the sun rise here."

Donald quietly listened to Benjamin’s report, and a flash of anguish passed through his eyes. He looked at me and nodded, agreeing with my suggestion.

He gripped my hand a bit tightly, which calmed my heart some. I glanced sideways at Benjamin and at Louis and Anthony waiting outside the motorhome, then spoke in a gentle voice, "Let’s get ready to leave. We can’t afford to stay overnight in Starry Ravine; it’s simply too dangerous."

If I can help it, I would never return to this place.

chapters.title: Chapter 239: Where have you seen it before?
chapters.content:
Chapter 239: Where have you seen it before?

[Margaret’s POV]

With the large troop of Lycans Donald brought with him, our journey behind Starry Ravine became somewhat easier. Our small caravan had grown into a large one, stretching out for dozens of meters when seen from a distance, imposing and luxurious to a terrifying degree.

Donald naturally would not part from me. The extended RV had astonishing space, and even with his addition, along with Anthony and a few familiar Lycans, it didn’t feel cramped.

I talked a lot with Donald, and to my questions and curiosity, he always had an answer ready. He also did not intentionally hide from me the fact that he had encountered Angel in the Misty Forest but that she had escaped again.

I was alarmed by how much had happened during their raid, looking at his face filled with worry and heartache.

"I didn’t expect Chris to be so devoted to Angel, even willing to risk his own life to help her escape..."

I realized that this was not a good topic to delve into and paused before speaking again, "MASTER managed to escape on his own, leaving so many werewolves behind at his stronghold. It seems he’s an extremely ruthless and treacherous person."

"Since we’ve been able to locate them once, we can do it a second time. Capturing them is just a matter of time. Don’t be too anxious, we have so many people with you!"

Donald was somewhat weary, hugging me as we leaned on the comfortable and spacious car seat, and he gently kissed my forehead, continuing languidly, "When I saw MASTER’s retreating figure, I always felt like I recognized him. That pitch-black fur, as dark as a moonless night, I feel like I’ve seen it before, but when I try to remember, I can’t recall anything."

"It would have been nice to capture Angel, then we wouldn’t have to guess, we would know directly. Angel..." Donald said, his voice heavy as he uttered the woman’s name, as if only by devouring her could he quench the towering hatred in his heart.

I wasn’t jealous just because of his casual mention of her name, but I still disliked the idea of Angel appearing in Donald’s words, or in any of his emotions, including hatred.

Because of this little bit of selfishness, I didn’t pick up the topic Donald had dropped. Instead, I took his uninjured hand and gently urged him to close his eyes and rest for a bit. Even if he couldn’t sleep in the car, a little feigned rest could still be beneficial.

These past couple of days, I had been distressed by a variety of unexpected events, and Donald had been even more so, never having a moment’s rest. He was utterly exhausted.

While I was talking with Donald, our caravan had completely exited Starry Ravine, marking the end of this memorable leg of the journey, and the metaphorical stone in my heart finally dropped somewhat.

As we traveled, night fell, and without any delay because of Elliot’s condition, we didn’t stop for any adjustments, instead speeding towards the next station, intent on making a night journey.

Donald’s return had the effect of a powerful boost of morale, and we all managed to breathe a small sigh of relief.

Perhaps truly exhausted, Donald slowly fell asleep in his seat. I touched his not-so-well-looking face, pulled up the blanket prepared for him, and got up to walk to the other end of the RV where Elliot’s small infirmary was.

"How is he doing? Has his temperature come down?" I asked, glancing at Benjamin who was tending to Elliot, lowering my voice.

Benjamin had thick dark circles under his eyes, looking frighteningly worn out. I figured he wasn’t having an easier time than Donald. As the most reliable therapist at the Lycan King’s side, his exceptional work ability came with great pressure.

"It’s relatively stable; he should last until we get back to the Palace. Assuming there are no further complications."

I nodded in response and sighed as I looked at Elliot on the bed, "He will be alright. We need to trust Donald and Elliot too! You’ve been tired for a long while now. Why don’t you go down and rest a bit? Ruth and I will watch over him here. If anything happens, I’ll call you immediately."

Benjamin looked at me gratefully, bowed slightly, and departed, while Ruth and I spent the first half of the night guarding Elliot. The more challenging latter half of the night was persevered through by Mark’s insistence.

The next day, as dawn broke, I woke up nestled against Donald, my entire body feeling as if it had been crushed, devoid of any sense of rest.

"You’re awake? How does your shoulder feel? If it hurts a lot, I can get you some painkillers. Also, what would you like for breakfast? We have sandwiches and compressed biscuits!" I asked Donald softly.

chapters.title: Chapter 240: The bed is about to fall apart
chapters.content:
Chapter 240: The bed is about to fall apart

[Margaret’s POV]

"If possible, I’d like to ’eat’ you first."

Perhaps because he had just woken from sleep, Donald’s voice was somewhat husky, and even carried a certain irresistibly sexy allure.

I glanced at him but did not respond immediately.

Seeing me only giggle without speaking, Donald reached out and gently tugged, and I tumbled into his arms. His gaze was far more explicit than his words, and I felt my heart race wildly.

"Benjamin and the others are just in the carriage in front, they could see us if they pushed the door open, so don’t you dare mess around," I reminded him in a soft voice as Donald’s warm large hand wandered around my waist.

"What ’mess around’? Haven’t you missed me?" asked the Lycan King with a smile.

Of course, I missed Donald. I loved the warmth and intimacy of skin against skin with him, as well as the top-notch excitement and thrill when we made love!

Scenes from the past floated into my mind, and my ears began to burn. I swallowed unnoticed, still uttering a warning, "Not now, Donald, they could come in at any time... mm!"

Before I could finish my sentence, an eager Donald had already held me tightly, and with a quick and agile maneuver, he had flipped me under him, sealing my lips firmly!

Donald kissed me passionately, and before I could say another word, the clothing on my chest had been rudely torn open. He fondled my breasts in his palm, and his torso, clad only in a thin undershirt, pressed tightly against me.

I felt my body tingle as if with a delicate electric current, becoming numb and overwhelmed with pleasure as my sexual desire was directly provoked. I moaned, keeping my voice down, and occasionally, words calling out Donald’s name slipped from my lips!

"Donald! Mmhm... slower, take it a bit slower!"

But apparently, the impatient Lycan King had no intention of heeding my request. His hands grew more forceful, and the huge bulge pressing at my inner thigh throbbed vigorously, as if ready to charge at any moment.

It had been several days since Donald and I had been together, and we couldn’t withstand the stimulation of each other’s desires. We clung together, exchanging several passionate, fiery kisses!

I sensed something unusual about Donald today. He seemed incredibly eager, as if he would go insane or die the next moment if he could not enter me.

I lifted one hand to embrace his neck, calming my breath and caressing his ear, "Slow down, Donald! Don’t rush, I’m right here! You... you need to calm down; if we make too much noise, they’ll notice."

Seemingly annoyed that I was still concerned about other things at such a moment, Donald forcefully parted my legs with one hand. My delicate and pretty skirt was instantly pushed up to my lower abdomen and the bed. Donald’s burning, huge member pressed against me in an instant, startling me so much that my whole body trembled, my attention drawn to my lower half.

He arched his strong back slightly and with a gentle thrust, his member rubbed against my vagina and penetrated inside, but stopped again after a few seconds!

Given Donald’s astonishing size, the sexual urge this time came abruptly and swiftly; there was no prior preparation to stretch a bit, so it was a bit too much for me. A mix of shock and haste, I blurted out words like ’slower’, ’too big’, and ’it hurts’.

Donald finally seemed to hear my ’dissatisfaction’ and slowed down his movements, lowering his neck to kiss me all over tenderly.

The bedroom in the RV was quite small, and naturally, the bed was even smaller. We could sleep side by side, but if we wanted to do anything more, it felt a bit cramped, especially given Donald’s extraordinary and imposing build!

Realizing the small bed was on the brink of collapse, Donald quickly lifted me by the waist and knelt on the bed.

I wrapped my arms instinctively around his neck, the sweat from our skin making things slick, and I almost couldn’t hold onto his lower back. Narrowing my eyes, I looked at him, uttering a questioning sound, "Hmm?"

Donald of course knew what I was wondering about. As he stood up, his feet on the floor and still thrusting, he replied in a husky voice, "The bed’s about to break; let’s do it against the wall."

Blushing at the nape of my neck as much as on my face when hearing Donald’s incredibly sexy voice, I wasn’t sure if it was because of his words that I felt shy or if it was the stimulation from the intense lovemaking!

About two hours later, when Donald pulled me out of the room, everyone in the front carriage had their heads buried, their mouths carrying a somewhat suspicious smirk.

Naturally, I wouldn’t ask too much; I just picked up the medicine kit nearby and with nimble and steady movements, helped Donald with the dressing on his shoulder...

chapters.title: Chapter 241: Magnetic field
chapters.content:
Chapter 241: Magnetic field

[Margaret’s POV]

The thoroughly satisfying sexual encounter I just had with Donald left us both a bit too excited.

The marks on Donald’s upper body were both ambiguous and obvious, and I didn’t want anyone else to see them, not even the physical therapists!

Although werewolves have strong natural healing abilities, it still takes some time. Before that, I need to do something to cover up, after all, my skin is quite thin, not as thick as a certain Lycan King’s, like city walls.

Donald didn’t care about the covert glances from everyone else. After glancing at the paper file passed by Anthony, he asked Benjamin in a calm voice, "How are the Lycans who were injured yesterday? We still have a 2-hour journey before the next rest stop where we can take a breather."

"Also, I’ve contacted the Minister of Palace Patrols, and he will bring his people to meet with us later. The healers of the Holy Medical Pavilion are also prepared to receive Elliot. We just need to make it there within the agreed time."

By the time Donald had finished this statement, he had also finished reading the document in his hand, and he raised his eyes to look at the few people standing in front of him, waiting for their response.

"Your Majesty, everyone sustained only minor injuries which have all been taken care of," Benjamin was the first to speak up and answer. "About the task you entrusted me with last night, I already have some theories, but to confirm them, we still need the support of some research data."

Regarding what Donald had instructed him to do last night, which Benjamin mentioned, I started feeling curious. Sitting next to Donald, I asked him in a low voice, "What was it? Why so secretive?"

After hearing my question, the expression on Donald’s face changed a bit, showing some annoyance and confusion, "Speaking of this, it’s time I had a good talk with Benjamin. Haven’t you been researching those potions for several days?"

"Now, tell me, this time Margaret, the assault team, and Guard Team’s Mind-link got blocked midway again. What exactly is the reason for this?"

"Or is it still because of those special potions? But this time, we did not encounter werewolves who used the powder, and apart from the animal attacks on the way, you didn’t run into any other suspicious individuals, did you?"

After listening to Donald’s queries, I clasped his hand, understanding that he was still fixated on the fact that we had lost contact once again.

Among closely-linked werewolves, the Mind-link should be the most stable and reliable way of communication, but ever since Donald and his group arrived at the Silver Moon Pack, this theory seemed to have been challenged many times.

Apart from the ’Mist’ drug occasionally used by the military, which has a similar effect, there are also special potions from MASTER and even peculiar environmental influences that could block our Mind-link.

It seems to be becoming a common phenomenon.

Everyone began to frown slowly, knowing this was abnormal.

"I previously hypothesized that the blocking of Mind-link was due to Your Majesty and the assault team entering the Misty Forest. The environment and terrain of that forest are very peculiar, and the magnetic field is also very different from the outside, so it seems possible to explain the disruption of Mind-link in this way," Benjamin said.

"I think perhaps that’s also one of the reasons why MASTER chose that place as their stronghold. He indeed is a very talented individual," Benjamin said, shaking his head, seemingly at a loss for words at the enemy’s overly commendable abilities.

After giving it some thought, I still found it strange. Glancing at Benjamin, I asked softly, "When they were in the Misty Forest, it could indeed be explained as you said, but what about after that?"

"After they left the forest, our Mind-link was still ineffective. Could it be that Starry Ravine is also like the Misty Forest, having some kind of magnetic field that affects our ability to contact each other?"

The situation was indeed incredibly bizarre, almost impossible to fully explain.

After hearing my doubts and analysis, Benjamin also fell into deep thought, and the RV quieted down once again.

"It seems this matter is complicated, and we won’t be able to clear it up in a short time. Let’s put it aside for now," Donald sighed lightly after half a minute of silence, summarizing in such a manner.

With that, he looked toward Anthony, who had been silent throughout, with a look of appreciation in his eyes, "You’ve been a great help to us this time. I will surely reward you generously when we return to the Palace. Think about what you want, and just tell me directly then."

Seemingly not expecting Donald to say that, a look of surprise appeared on Anthony’s face. He paused, then suddenly knelt on one knee, raised his fist to his heart, and said gravely, "It is my honor to serve Your Majesty. Anthony dares not claim a reward for his contribution."

chapters.title: Chapter 242: My selfishness
chapters.content:
Chapter 242: My selfishness

[Margaret’s POV]

Watching Anthony’s meticulous and fiercely loyal demeanor, I smiled gently and glanced at Donald beside me before speaking, "I heard from Donald about what you all went through yesterday, and you really did complete your task very well."

"After leaving the Misty Forest, you continued to help with various things, which was a great help to us. Therefore, it’s only right that Donald wants to reward you; there’s no need for such modesty and politeness. I believe Armstrong would be happy for you as well."

While saying this, I looked around at the others and after a moment, I continued with a smile toward Donald, "Actually everyone has worked hard on this matter, and there’s no rush to reward anyone at this moment. How about we wait until we reach the next stop at the Windstorm Pack, and then you can properly recognize and reward everyone’s efforts?"

"That way, everyone will be happy, and Anthony can take your reward back to the Silver Moon Pack from Windstorm Pack. He’s Armstrong’s Beta; we couldn’t really have him come back to the Palace with us, could we? He needs to go home."

I actually saw Donald’s reluctance to part with Anthony, but the vast Werewolf Palace wouldn’t lack a brave and resourceful Beta like him. Outstanding Lycans like a dime a dozen surround Donald, and Anthony might not necessarily receive the recognition he truly deserves.

I know this is a somewhat cruel reality, but there is indeed a gap between Anthony and the likes of Chris and Benjamin.

A friend in times, I hope Anthony can do as he wishes and have a more relaxed life; he will surely encounter two different fates in the Silver Moon Pack and the Werewolf Palace.

Besides, I have a bit of selfishness as well; I hope a fine Beta like him stays in the pack to protect my family and kin.

And not to have my sister, Elizabeth, separated from this man who has quietly stood by her at such a critical moment of love choices.

That would be unfair to both Elizabeth and Anthony.

So, a multitude of thoughts flashed through my mind in these few minutes, before I finally turned my attention back to Donald with some apology.

After hearing my words, Anthony looked up at me with gratitude in his eyes; I knew these were his thoughts.

The broad prospects of going to the Werewolf Palace could not outweigh his obsession to stay silently by Elizabeth’s side. Anthony was also a very persistent and passionate person.

Donald, an extremely intelligent man, quickly caught on after hearing all this.

He looked at Anthony, who was half-kneeling on the ground, then nodded his head and agreed to my suggestion.

Elizabeth, far away in the Silver Moon Pack, had no idea that Anthony almost couldn’t return.

In fact, while I was considering Anthony’s alternate path, I also had my own uncertainties. With innate differences between him and the Lycans around Donald, wasn’t I the same?

I think in such a vast Werewolf Palace, there must be countless she-wolves like Angel who are outstanding and in love with Donald.

To gain everyone’s acceptance and stand by Donald’s side, to become his true and rightful Lycan Queen, is indeed a daunting goal!

I’m not as naive as Elizabeth, not even a bit unaware of what will happen when I return to the Werewolf Palace with Donald.

I love Donald and want to stay by his side forever; this is certainly a most difficult task, but I won’t be discouraged or give up easily.

My inner turmoil runs much deeper than what’s apparent on the surface.

Of course, Donald has no idea how much mental preparation I have to do within myself to agree to return to the Werewolf Palace with him.

I don’t want to appear overly sentimental and timid in front of him; I want to grow up and become someone who truly deserves him as soon as possible.

The two-hour journey passed in a flash, and our Guard convoy safely arrived at the Windstorm Pack. It’s actually quite close to the Werewolf Palace now, probably about a day and a half’s journey left.

Donald stood on the steps below the mobile home, raising his hand to lead me off the vehicle.

But this ordinary scene fell into the eyes of the Alpha of the Windstorm Pack—Austin, which made him slightly frown.

"Oh my revered sovereign, you’re finally back! You should know, I’ve been waiting here for you for 3 hours, and I think the kitchen’s delicacies must all be ready by now. Let’s hurry on over," he said.

While speaking, he bowed slightly to Donald with great respect, ardently using some welcoming words, and when he raised his head to look at Donald and me, his gaze suddenly fell on our clasped hands.

"Um... May I ask who this lady is?"

Donald must have known Austin for quite a long time since they interacted with each other in a very familiar way, and their conversation showed relaxed and cheerful expressions.

"Her name is Margaret, she’s my mate," this was Donald introducing me to Austin.

chapters.title: Chapter 243: They did it on purpose?
chapters.content:
Chapter 243: They did it on purpose?

[Margaret’s POV]

"Austin, Alpha of the Windstorm Pack and an old friend of mine," Donald said with a smile brimming in his eyes, clearly delighted that I could meet his friend.

At times, he was genuinely adorable, much like a lively, sunny boy. Of course, this side of him only surfaced around people he was very close and affectionate with.

I smiled and nodded at Austin, considering that a proper greeting.

Although Donald seemed quite familiar with him, from the very first moment I saw him, I felt that there was a complex aura about this man, and my instinct told me he was no simple character.

He seemed to harbor some hostility towards me! This intuition was so bizarre that I found it somewhat absurd.

"What? Your mate? Your Majesty, how did you suddenly stumble upon your mate? This is indeed a great event! Have you informed the elders in the Palace? They must be thrilled, right? And... where is Angel? This must have been quite a shock for her too, right?"

Perhaps noticing a change in Donald’s expression, Austin suddenly quieted down while speaking.

If not for the genuine look of jest on his face, I would have suspected that he was deliberately saying these things to anger Donald.

As the Alpha coordinating with Donald and the entire Guard Team, how could he not know about Elliot and Angel’s situation?

Could it be that Donald’s security measures for escorting Elliot back to the Palace were so stringent? Was Austin doing this on purpose?

Donald unconsciously tightened his grip on my hand, aware of his many reservations about Angel. I subtly squeezed his hand in return and then suggested with a light laugh, "Alpha Austin, hello, could we perhaps move this conversation to the lobby first? It’s quite crowded here, not really a suitable place for discussions."

He seemed to realize Donald was displeased and chuckled awkwardly, nodding in agreement, "Yes, yes, let’s go inside and sit down to talk. I was too hasty, it’s been such a long time since I’ve seen His Majesty."

"The last time His Majesty’s convoy passed through Windstorm Pack, I missed meeting with His Majesty due to other duties, and I still regret it, haha! Miss Margaret, this way please..."

Smiling at Austin’s response and small talk, I took Donald’s hand and started walking inside, but he remained still, fixedly gazing at Austin.

I turned back, somewhat puzzled, and before I could ask ’what’s wrong,’ I heard Donald seriously tell Austin, "You can address her directly as the Lycan Queen, that’s what Christian and the others do."

"..."

Out of the corner of my eye, I noticed a slight pause in Austin’s expression, after which he chuckled lightly and nodded repeatedly in agreement, "Yes, yes, I misspoke! Anyone whom Your Majesty deems as your mate is naturally the Queen of the Wolf royal family. I apologize for my mistake, please don’t take offense, Your Majesty and the Queen."

He bowed slightly, appearing quite apologetic.

"But you work fast, it’s only been two months since we last met, and now you already have a mate and there’s a new lady in the Wolf royal family, haha! I was really taken aback by this news; you can’t blame me for that, can you?"

Austin said, his face showing more jest and tease.

Donald looked relaxed and gradually started to smile as if he quite agreed with what Austin said.

He chuckled and playfully punched Austin on the shoulder. He didn’t respond verbally but kept holding my hand.

A few minutes later, we all settled in the reception hall of Windstorm Pack, and the Lycan guards began their meal and break.

Because in another hour, we would be back on the road again.

While Donald was talking and chatting with Austin, I sat silently beside him, sipping the drink in my cup and occasionally topping up Donald’s as well.

When a sudden and abrupt sound of high heels echoed from outside the reception hall door, like the Lycans below, I instinctively raised my eyes and looked over.

"Donald, brother! You come to our pack, and you don’t even tell me in advance? If it weren’t for my cleverness in noticing my brother secretly holding a welcoming party for you, were you planning to sneak away directly from the pack again? Hmph! You really are too mean."

Watching the bright, beautiful girl at the entrance, and hearing her soft, adorable ’complaint,’ I finally understood why, from the very first moment, there was a clash in the aura between my friend Donald, Austin, and me!

chapters.title: Chapter 244: Lillian
chapters.content:
Chapter 244: Lillian

[Margaret’s POV]

Because the moment Austin saw Donald extending his hand to me gently, he had already categorized me into the rival camp of his sister’s foes!

He was extremely familiar with it, as if he had handled countless similar situations before.

"Little fool Lillian, I have told you over and over again, you can’t address His Majesty as ’brother’ like that!"

"Even though you two could be considered companions who have grown up together since childhood, you ought to be sovereign and subject now, at the very least. And besides, I am your real brother!"

In a pretended disgruntled manner, Austin reminded Lillian, and by the end of his speech, he even cast a ’jealous’ glance at Donald, seemingly uncomfortable with the Lycan King stealing his sister’s attention.

From the concise words he previously uttered, I could tell that his sister, Lillian, seemed to care quite a bit about Donald.

It turned out that Angel wasn’t the only beautiful girl who had grown up with Donald; we hadn’t even truly arrived at the Werewolf Palace yet, and we had already bumped into another of his close female friends!

I felt slightly uncomfortable, but not enough to be overtly jealous on the spot. It was merely an ambiguous use of ’brother,’ which indicated that Donald probably didn’t have any feelings for this Lillian.

"You are my real brother, and of course, that’s not comparable to Donald! Humph, I won’t talk to you anymore. Donald himself doesn’t mind, so why are you scolding me like this? Be careful, or I’ll complain to mom!"

Lillian, in a spoiled tone, stepped forward, her fair, slender hands gently crossed in front of her chest, tilting her chin up and coquettishly speaking to Austin, yet her stunning eyes remained fixed on Donald.

She seemed to have not noticed me sitting next to Donald the entire time.

"When did I ever scold you? Am I not just reminding you, you little rascal? Come on, please don’t be so willful, alright?"

Austin sighed lightly and then, helplessly rising to his feet, went over to greet Lillian, "Behave yourself, come here and pay respects to His Majesty... and the Lycan Queen. Don’t let everyone think you have been so spoiled by me that you lack the most basic etiquette!"

Austin paused for a moment after speaking, as if the word ’Lycan Queen’ was very difficult for him to utter.

I watched the siblings with a detached expression, yet my mind was on another person. Lillian’s personality indeed resembled Elizabeth’s, except she wasn’t as beautiful as Elizabeth.

It appeared that my sister, who besides her beauty had no redeeming qualities, wasn’t someone just anyone could surpass.

However, promptly, Lillian’s sharp questioning shattered my momentary trance, and Donald held my hand under the table, his eyes filled with a bit of worried questioning.

"What? You are saying this woman is Donald’s Lycan Queen?" Lillian asked in a high-pitched voice, her expression changing in an instant, with impolite scrutiny filling her wide eyes and then swiftly followed by a flicker of sarcasm and disdain.

"Are you joking, brother? Donald never had a girlfriend before, so how could he suddenly have a queen? Your joke isn’t funny at all," she said.

Lillian looked towards Donald and me behind the main table, mainly at Donald, as if she was waiting for a precise response from him.

"This matter has not been formally announced to everyone because our return to the Palace needs to remain confidential for now. It’s normal that you are not aware of it."

Donald answered Lillian and Austin’s questions but never looked up at them from beginning to end.

As he spoke, he squeezed my hand and slowly stood up, his body leaning towards mine, "I would like to officially introduce to the Windstorm Pack my mate Margaret, the highly esteemed Lycan Queen of the Wolf royal family."

As he spoke, he glanced sideways at Austin and Lillian standing below, his countenance filled with pleasure and joy, and yet, more than anything, with respect and certainty.

I knew Donald was affirming my status as the ’Lycan Queen’, and my heart felt immeasurably warm and soft, as I looked up at him with eyes full of smiling affection!

This is my God-like mate, who would be the first to stand up when others doubted me, attempting to look down and provoke me, utterly reliable.

Taking a deep breath, I gently squeezed his hand in return, and when I turned my gaze back to Austin and his sister below, the entire aura about me had completely shifted.

I didn’t know when it began, but the aura about me was becoming more and more like Donald’s. The majestic and authoritative presence inherent in his bloodline seemed to have also infused some of its essence onto me.

Looking down at the girl with a face full of shock and surprise, I slightly curved my lips and said somewhat distantly, "Hello Lillian, my name is Margaret."

Lillian’s dumbfoundedness broke with my words, and as she looked up to meet my gaze, she let out a heavy breath as if she found it quite hard to accept, "You say you are His Majesty’s mate? Ha! A she-wolf who popped up from nowhere dares shamelessly claim to be Donald’s queen? I think you’re just mad for glory and wealth!"

chapters.title: Chapter 245: My apologies to the Lycan Queen
chapters.content:
Chapter 245: My apologies to the Lycan Queen

[Margaret’s POV]

I slightly furrowed my brows, finding it amusing and absurd as I watched Lillian, who suddenly had an emotional breakdown and began cursing at me.

"I don’t think there were any particularly obscure words in Donald’s introduction of me before, were they hard to understand, or does Miss Lillian have a problem with her comprehension ability?"

I’m not usually the type to confront people like this, but Lillian was one of the rare exceptions who could elicit such distaste from me at first sight!

Being straightforward and speaking without restraint are sometimes two different things. The indulgence and pampering of her parents and older brother do not justify her arrogant and impolite behavior. She seems even more detestable than Elizabeth was before.

At least our Elizabeth wouldn’t be so foolish as to publicly provoke the Lycan Queen and offend the Lycan King.

"What do you mean by that? Are you mocking me?" Lillian glared at me with her big eyes, as if she couldn’t believe what she was hearing and seemed ready to devour me alive if I dared to nod in agreement!

I couldn’t quite grasp why she was so angry, so I casually raised an eyebrow and watched her with an exceeding calm.

I noticed that the gazes of everyone in the hall were fixed on us, feeling somewhat caught off guard and irked.

I was about to ask Austin, who had stood by her side in silence, whether their family always allowed such arrogant and rash behavior from their girls.

But before I could voice my questions, Donald, next to me, and Austin, who had been feigning deafness, spoke up in unison:

"Lillian! What are you planning to do to my queen?"

"Shut your mouth, Lillian, have you gone mad? You have no right to speak here, get down!"

"I..." Suddenly chastised by the two most prestigious men in the hall, Lillian, no matter how spoiled she had been before, was startled into silence, her mouth opening as if she wanted to say something, but Austin obviously didn’t give her the chance.

"Go down!" Austin commanded her in a cold voice.

Seeing this, I gave a glance to Alpha Austin without showing any reaction and thought he was quite clever and adept in dealing with people.

He didn’t speak when Lillian was rude to me at first, but as he saw Donald’s expression growing colder and colder, he hurried to play the role of a stern parent.

Despite Austin scolding Lillian, in reality, he was still protecting her.

Setting other things aside, it seemed that Austin was quite a good brother who doted on his sister.

He must still hold considerable weight in Lillian’s heart. Having been sternly rebuked by him twice, Lillian seemed to have got the message to restrain herself.

She glanced at me resentfully a couple of times before turning to leave, but Donald wasn’t someone easily fooled.

He wouldn’t allow this brother-sister pair to challenge and bully me in front of him like this, "Stop, did I allow you to leave?"

Lillian halted her steps and looked up at Donald beside me, surprised.

"Earlier, you didn’t know that Margaret was my queen, and you were rude to her. I can overlook that, but you clearly heard how I introduced her just now, and yet you continued to speak to her disrespectfully. That’s a bit too much."

"Your Majesty! Lillian, she..."

Seeming to realize that Donald had no intention of letting this slide, Austin’s expression changed, and he hastily attempted to intercede on Lillian’s behalf, but was immediately silenced by a cold look from Donald.

Austin closed his mouth and said no more, and Donald then withdrew his gaze from him, "I know you’ve been spoiled by Austin without discipline, but that was in the past. You’re grown up now, and you can’t always think of yourself as a child, can you? You need to be responsible for your own actions."

Donald then looked at me apologetically, his eyes full of tenderness and concern.

"Now, apologize to my queen. If she forgives you, I will let you leave today! Looking at your brother is useless, apologize!" Donald’s voice was very cold, and his gaze upon Lillian was filled with chill.

Observing Donald like this warmed my heart, thinking how wonderful he was!

I’ve encountered situations like this more than once. Not to speak of these people from the irrelevant Wild Wind Pack, even within my own pack, many doubted and scorned.

It seems that an ordinary person like me is fated not to be with the highly esteemed Donald! Even if we are fated mates granted by the Moon Goddess, it seems it’s not that simple to be accepted by everyone.

I knew such doubts wouldn’t end with today, but ultimately, Donald has always stood by me, and that’s more than enough for me.

I’m a person who is easily contented; as long as Donald loves me, that’s all I need.

I chuckled lightly and met Lillian’s indignant gaze, waiting for her to apologize to me.

chapters.title: Chapter 246: Gambling lost
chapters.content:
Chapter 246: Gambling lost

[Margaret’s POV]

The atmosphere in the reception hall was very solemn. Other than a single Austin, no one from the Windstorm Pack dared to speak much in front of Donald like this.

"When a person makes a mistake or misspeaks, it’s their responsibility to own up to it! Miss Lillian, do you find it difficult to offer a sincere apology to our Lycan Queen? Heh."

Benjamin rose during the quiet, his mouth emitting a few ambiguous chuckles, and he continued without pause, looking towards Lillian whose face had turned red: "You might not know, but the last one who publicly defied our Lycan Queen like this wasn’t let off easily with just an apology or two. She was thrown directly into prison by their pack’s Alpha!"

"Oh dear! I don’t even know if that person has been released yet. Her Majesty is now merely asking for an apology, and that is actually considering the many years of acquaintance between you and your brother, otherwise, it would not be this simple."

As Benjamin spoke, he glanced subtly at Anthony beside him.

After exchanging a knowing look with him, Anthony continued in a deep voice, "According to the rules, those who show great disrespect to His Majesty or the Queen need to be punished with ten strokes of the cane. Alpha Austin, Miss Lillian, you surely do not wish for His Majesty to administer the punishment in public, do you?"

Donald did not deny what Anthony had said, and I looked up slightly either without saying a word.

At that moment, Lillian, stimulated by Donald’s ruthless demeanor, suddenly became very red in the eyes. If not for the fact that crying was somewhat embarrassing and disgraceful, I guessed she would have directly wept for Donald to see.

I squeezed Donald’s hand forcefully, trying to dissipate some of the jealousy in my heart! Indeed, one must keep a close watch on a man as outstanding as one’s own, or everyone would want to covet a glance.

Donald, sensing my mood, quietly stroked the back of my hand as a form of comfort.

Austin then called out Lillian’s name sternly, including a reminder in his voice.

He knew that apologizing now was unavoidable; Donald was not a king of changing his mind suddenly—his words were always followed by actions.

Lillian kept her eyes on Donald and did not respond to Austin’s reminder. Only after a long time did she reluctantly and quietly say to me, "Sorry."

Seeing her very stifled and resentful expression, I suddenly felt very unimpressed, and returned lightly with a hum, "Let there not be a next time. In the future, you’d better bring your brain when you talk and act."

"Don’t wear a face full of indignation as if you’ve suffered a great injustice. From start to finish, I never bullied you, it was you who came looking for trouble. Donald and I are not to blame."

Saying this, I no longer spared Lillian another glance and turned to Donald, "I am done eating, I’ll go wait in the car for you."

Hearing this, Donald nodded, kissed the back of my hand, and replied, "Alright, I’ll have Louis take you back first, I won’t be long here. We’ll leave in precisely half an hour."

Knowing that Donald still had matters to attend to here, I smiled lightly and turned to leave, not giving Lillian and Austin any more of my attention.

Lillian might truly be naive and spoiled. Her admiration for Donald was genuine, but Austin’s involvement wasn’t so simple...

He was using Lillian to test my standing in Donald’s heart!

He also wanted to use Lillian’s admiration for Donald to make Donald hesitate between taking sides with her and her sister. Even if the outcome of this test turned out poorly, it wouldn’t prevent them from escaping unscathed.

He was gambling on Donald’s regard for me. But, in the end, he lost completely!

Donald’s approach to love was neither vague nor wishy-washy. He was resolute and clear-headed, knowing exactly what he wanted, so from the start, Austin had been betting on the wrong horse.

These were instincts that sprang up within me about this situation. I felt that Austin was not as simple as he appeared on the surface; he gave me a very insincere, dishonest impression!

Back in the RV, I was still pondering over the recent events, with Louis and Ruth following behind me, occasionally interjecting a few words as if trying to cheer me up.

"Actually, Lycan Queen, you shouldn’t feel upset. At least our Guard Team and the medical squad’s Lycans all hold you in high regard."

"You are kind-hearted, brave, and straightforward. I think you are much better than that contrived Lillian! Just based on the fact that you personally led the team to bring the gravely injured Elliot back to the Palace for treatment, I, Louis, will stand by your side from now on. I support you!"

As Louis said these words, his expression was very sincere and straightforward. I believed he was telling the truth; that look didn’t seem like a deceiving one.

chapters.title: Chapter 247: Heart Where?
chapters.content:
Chapter 247: Heart Where?

[Margaret’s POV]

"Thank you, Louis, I’m glad you said that." I breathed out lightly, smiling at Louis.

The young Lycan looked at me, slightly embarrassed as he touched the back of his head, his expression sincere and adorable.

The atmosphere in the car relaxed considerably, Ruth prepared some tea and snacks, mentioning that I hadn’t eaten much earlier and now was a good time to fill my stomach.

minutes later, someone knocked on the RV’s door from the outside, and Ruth stepped forward to open it.

Looking at the silent Anthony, I nodded toward a chair, signaling him to sit down and talk, "Be careful on the way back, and remember to speak to Elizabeth first when you reach the pack, so she won’t worry about you incessantly."

Upon hearing my words, Anthony bowed his head and replied respectfully, "I understand, Lycan Queen, I will be careful. And... thank you for your words this morning!"

"I know His Majesty values talent and wants to elevate me, but my heart is not in the Palace, I only want to return and care for Elizabeth. I can’t respond to His Majesty’s appreciation of talent. I am truly grateful to you for helping me out, thank you very much!"

After listening to Anthony’s words, I just smiled softly, not feeling that I had done anything remarkable, as letting Anthony return was also a personal wish of mine.

"Alright, no need for so many thanks. We are friends, after all, there’s no need for such formality and distance. I did it for Elizabeth’s sake, you should be well aware of that," I said with another sigh, thinking of my dear sister; it seemed like my worries for her were never-ending.

As I spoke, something came to mind, and I looked at Anthony to continue, "Right, I also owe you thanks for standing up for me at the banquet. You and Benjamin seem to have a good understanding of each other."

"You are the Lycan King’s Queen, it’s our duty as subjects to act as such. Or as you just said, because we are friends, friends should help and support each other!"

Anthony’s words were well spoken, I nodded and smiled, "If only you were Elizabeth’s mate, I think you are far better than Armstrong."

I was not joking about that; I truly thought Anthony was more suitable for Elizabeth than Armstrong, as Anthony is a man who knows how to love.

After exchanging a few more words with Anthony, the Guard Team Lycans started getting back into their cars, and Donald approached from not too far away, Austin tagging along obediently behind him.

I waved my hand at Anthony, bidding him goodbye.

Anthony climbed into the car with remarkable ease, nodded to the people outside, and without a farewell, he drove off.

Mark and Benjamin were a bit melancholy, commenting that Anthony was too honest; going to the Palace is something many werewolves would beg for, and yet he didn’t care for it.

"Everyone has their own aspirations. His heart is with Silver Moon Pack; you can’t keep someone against their will." I chuckled lightly, watching Anthony’s car drive away.

"And what about yours?"

Upon hearing the familiar and pleasant voice behind me, I arched my eyebrows and turned to look.

Donald was standing there with a teasing look in his eyes and a lower tone, continuing to ask, "Where is your heart?"

Benjamin turned his head with some tact, pretending not to have witnessed the scene unfolding before him, while Lycan Mark seemed to have a much lower EQ.

He stared at the joking Lycan King, as if his worldview was a bit shaken!

I could not miss such a gaze; I coughed lightly, my ears reddening slightly, but there was a stubborn refusal on my face to be flustered, "My heart, naturally, lies with my pack; I love my home."

Upon my response, Donald’s gaze darkened a bit, murmuring a low ’really?’, then he grabbed my hand tightly, ignoring the stares of the others around us, wrapped his arm around my waist, and led me straight onto the RV!

As we walked, he didn’t forget to issue his command, "Everyone get on board; let’s go."

In my peripheral vision, I saw Austin’s displeased face. Donald didn’t look at him again after getting on the car, and I keenly sensed something odd but didn’t ask Donald directly in front of so many people. If it was something that could be discussed, Donald definitely wouldn’t hide it from me.

Our convoy stayed at the Windstorm Pack for just over an hour, and some unpleasant incidents happened because of a Lillian.

Of course, I had no time to care about their sibling embarrassment or discomfort; a certain Lycan King, quite particular about certain matters, was pressing on my lips, administering his ’severe interrogation’!

"Mm! Donald..."

"Where is your heart? If I’m not satisfied with your answer, I’ll keep kissing you!"

"You’re so childish!" I said, somewhat exasperated.

chapters.title: Chapter 248: You are jealous!
chapters.content:
Chapter 248: You are jealous!

[Margaret’s POV]

In response to my slight ’accusation,’ Donald did not utter a word, simply hooking the corners of his lips, firmly pressing over me without any intention to let go.

He slowly bowed his head, and the hot breath from between his nostrils ran wild like electricity along my neck, prompting me to twist uncomfortably and raise my hand to lightly resist his pressing body, hoarsely reminding him, "My waist is still sore, can you let me off just this once?"

In the same place and posture, with the same person, just a few hours ago, I had just concluded a passionate and exhausting session of lovemaking with Donald. Indeed, I was a bit more than I could handle at the moment.

"I have something to ask you. Can you get off me first, please?" I said softly to Donald, gently caressing his cheek with my hand.

Donald looked into my eyes tranquilly, as if he was pondering whether or not to listen to me and give me a break.

After a few seconds, he caught my hand and kissed it, then rolled onto his side, leaning against the wall behind him, but his hand that was wrapped around my waist did not loosen at all, "What do you want to ask? Is it about Lillian?"

At his words, I snorted softly and looked up at him with a twisted mouth, "So you knew I wanted to ask about that? Tell me honestly, just how many ’close female friends’ have you had before? Other than Lillian and Angel, are there many others who like you this much?"

"Hmm, I only realized today, after meeting Lillian, that there really, really are a lot of girls who like you and admire you. With no fuss, you’ve set up so many rivals in love for me!"

Thinking about this, I became somewhat exhausted and leaned into Donald’s embrace, pretending to sigh with weariness as I felt all my jealousy pouring out.

Yet, the ’culprit’ responsible for all this did not rush to explain or clarify after hearing my interrogation. Instead, Donald just looked at me with amusement in his eyes, as if the expression on my face was something incredibly entertaining.

I glared at him with some displeasure, slowly sitting upright from his embrace, and pursued relentlessly with my words, "I’m asking you something, didn’t you hear me? What exactly is your relationship with that Lillian?"

"Why did Austin say you and her grew up together since childhood? Didn’t you grow up in the Palace? Why then would you be involved with them, brother and sister?"

"Speaking of which, you have quite a few childhood friends! Who else is there? You better come clean with me! What are you laughing at... stop laughing!"

Perhaps finding my behavior extremely amusing, Donald’s smile grew bigger as he listened, as if he was about to burst into laughter at any second.

The very serious me: "..."

"Donald, if you keep laughing, I’m going to get angry!"

"I’m sorry, baby, don’t be angry, I really didn’t mean to laugh at you! It’s just that you’re so adorable—I’ve never seen you act so coquettishly before. Are you jealous? You are just too cute, how can you be this cute?"

Donald wrapped his arms around me tightly, his handsome face bursting with an open smile.

I looked at him somewhat speechlessly, my eyes filled with protest and dissatisfaction, "I’m discussing serious matters with you, and you actually think I’m acting coquettishly? Where am I acting coquettishly? I’m being very serious, okay?"

"Alright, alright, you’re not acting coquettishly, is that okay? You’re jealous!" Donald looked at me with a helpless face, his eyes, like precious stones, brimming with indulgence and joy. He seemed to particularly enjoy such simple happiness, just because I was jealous over his and Lillian’s interaction.

I narrowed my eyes at him in a decidedly dissatisfied manner and remained silent, but my demeanor was resolutely demanding honesty from him.

Donald observed me for several seconds, apparently completely defenseless against this side of me, and immediately surrendered, chuckling while nodding in response, "Alright, baby, whatever you want to know, I’ll tell you everything. Don’t be angry, okay?"

He said while kissing my forehead, exhaling softly, then continued, "Actually, I’m not very close to Lillian. It’s not like Austin said, growing up together with the siblings from childhood."

"At most, Lillian was a playmate for half a month during our childhood, and that was many years ago. To me, she is simply Austin’s sister, nothing more, nothing less."

"You are the first person I have truly liked and loved, no one can compare to the place you hold in my heart, you are unique! The one I love now is you, and it will only be you in the future as well. Does that make you feel a bit more assured?"

It turned out Donald had always been aware of the immense pressure in my heart. He was not the type of man to constantly spout sweet nothings. Instead, he offered me comfort and support more through his actions.

chapters.title: Chapter 249: Donald’s Past
chapters.content:
Chapter 249: Donald’s Past

[Margaret’s POV]

Just like earlier at the welcome banquet held by Windstorm Pack, when faced with the queries and tests from Lillian and Austin, he didn’t hesitate to stand by my side. That is what I call practical support through actions.

During the time Donald was speaking softly, I was actually silently watching him. This man rarely explains anything with such patience, and I really like the way he is right now.

I opened my arms and wrapped them around Donald’s neck, burying myself in his embrace.

Smelling his scent, I mumbled, "I feel safe with you by my side. I never really thought there was anything between you and Lillian. I know you’re not that kind of person, I... I just feel regretful, regretful for missing out on your past life, for not meeting you sooner."

If only I could have met Donald earlier, before Lillian and Angel knew him.

Donald seemed to chuckle softly, his arms tightening around me, and he kissed my neck tenderly, consoling me, "It’s not really about missing out, I’m very satisfied with meeting you in the Silver Moon Pack. I’m content with everything I have now. I love you, Margaret, and I will always be by your side."

As he spoke, he seemed to suddenly remember something, snorted in amusement, and continued, "Besides, I never thought there was anything good about my former self. The way things are now is just fine."

I didn’t quite understand what Donald meant by that last sentence, so I straightened up slowly from his embrace, looking at him with confusion in my eyes.

Donald let out a light sigh, and this time he decided not to ignore my questioning. He gazed at me and asked softly, "Do you know why I’m acquainted with Austin?"

Donald rarely volunteered information about himself, and of course, I couldn’t possibly know, so I shook my head.

"When my father was young, he had a very powerful Beta named Joseph, the most outstanding Lycan in the entire Werewolf Palace."

"He was invincible and had amassed glorious combat achievements at a young age, the brightest and most dazzling figure in the Werewolf Palace at the time. It can be said that without his full support and unwavering loyalty, my father never could have won the struggle for the throne against Uncle Dan!"

Hearing Donald mention several names that sounded very familiar to me, I looked up at him and joined the conversation warmly, "The Uncle you’re referring to, is he the one known as King Lington, Dan?"

"I heard from my father that many years ago, he was decisively defeated by the former Lycan King, your father Alex, outside the Demon Domain Forest, and no one has seen him since. People say he died, but who knows if that’s true!"

As I continued with some confusion, I asked, "But what does this have to do with your connection to Austin? Weren’t you just talking about your father’s Beta, Joseph? How is he related to all of this?"

Donald held my hand, looked down into my eyes, and his gaze became somewhat nostalgic, "King Lington doesn’t have much to do with this. By the time Austin, Angel, Henry, and I were learning combat from Joseph, he had already disappeared for a long time!"

"Speaking of which, I don’t know if he actually died because my father never mentioned him in front of us."

"I think my father is still saddened by the betrayal he faced. After all, they were biological brothers who grew up together. The fact that they became rivals for the position of Lycan King and fought each other is an everlasting pain between them."

When Donald talked about the past, his expression turned solemn, and his handsome brows furrowed deeply, like millennium-old ice that couldn’t melt in the depths of the Antarctic Sea.

I raised my hand to caress his face, softening my voice with affectionate reassurance, "These are your father’s stories, they have nothing to do with you, who was just a little brat back then. You don’t have to feel so sad and regretful."

Continuing in a gentle voice, I kissed Donald’s deeply furrowed brow and said, "In the end, a king stands and a defeated man falls, and the events that cause pain and regret will pass. All we should do is look forward, and as long as we have a clear conscience, that’s all that matters."

"That said, I knew that you and Angel shared the same school and teacher. I didn’t expect that Austin was the same. He seems to have gotten quite close to your Royal Family, huh!"

Observing Donald’s eyes brighten, I began trying to shift the topic away from the heaviness, "By the way, besides those two, who is this Henry you mentioned? Is he also a Lycan of the Wolf Royal Family? Or is he an Alpha of some pack? You never mentioned him before today!"

I thought I was deftly navigating away from the heavy subject, but I unwittingly stumbled upon yet another sensitive topic.

After the struggle for the throne ended in defeat, King Lington mysteriously vanished from the Demon Domain Forest, leaving behind just a very young son—Henry!

chapters.title: Chapter 250: Henry
chapters.content:
Chapter 250: Henry

[Margaret’s POV]

Actually, Donald was only 12 years old at the time and did not understand the treacherous subtleties and changes in the royal environment.

He did not know when it started, but his once close cousin had changed, becoming no longer as cheerful and sunny as before, like someone completely different from the marrow of his bones.

"Although Uncle Dan’s sins were heavy, my father was clear about right and wrong; he did not implicate a child in all of this. Henry was abandoned by his father but still lived in the palace,"

"My parents treated him the same as they always had, and from age 12 to 14, Henry lived very discreetly and in peace."

"However, what no one expected was that these two years of calm were only an illusion. Dan was Henry’s biological father, a demon he could never shake off in his lifetime..."

Donald took a long, deep breath as he spoke; once the door to the past was opened, those sad and painful things could no longer be hidden, "Henry was seriously injured during an outdoor training session and has never been able to stand again since..."

"After waking from his terrible injuries, he said he had been set up and fell off a cliff! My father was furious at the time and ordered a full investigation into the matter, but in the end, no one was found and no traces were uncovered, so it had to be ruled an accident."

Having heard the story up to here, I too began to frown slowly, my emotions complex, not knowing whether to lament Henry’s harsh fate and misfortune or to feel resigned to the whims of fate!

The sins once committed by King Lington, how could Henry, his only son, possibly escape them?

Even if the former Lycan King Alex could let go, what about the others? You should know, to seize the throne, King Lington had done some utterly inhumane things!

Listening to the older generation talk about this matter, they would shake their heads and sigh, full of regret and sorrow for those who died in the turmoil.

I think Donald, being a person of profound feelings and loyalty, must have been deeply pained by Henry’s misfortune.

Just like now, merely bringing up those past events makes him profoundly melancholic.

"So what happened later? Is Henry still in the palace, how is he now?" I looked at Donald anxiously and asked.

At my words, Donald gave a bitter smile, sighed, and continued, "After that, Henry completely changed, no longer gloomy as before but became incredibly irritable and angry!"

"He was a bright and cheerful young man as a child with excellent grades and a breezy disposition. But that mysterious ’accident’ shattered his life; he could never stand again, and he became like lifeless deadwood, brooding and desolate."

"At first, I often visited him, trying every way to encourage him to ’stand’ up again, but his personality had changed, he refused to listen to kind words, and no matter how hard outsiders tried to help him, it was futile. This situation lasted until we both turned 18 and began to improve a bit."

"I still remember at my coming-of-age banquet, the words he said to me! He said, ’No matter what happened before, we will always be brothers!’ Later, when I took over the responsibilities from my father and became the leader of the entire Wolf royal family, he has always silently supported me."

"We would occasionally gather together, he, Angel, and Austin, to drink and chat! But I always feel it’s not the same as before, especially after Angel betrayed the Wolf royal family, that sunny childhood, we can never return to it."

For now, the story’s ending isn’t too bad—at least Donald can still see this brother who is very important to him; he, Henry, and Austin can still occasionally sit together to talk and chat.

Aside from the absence of Angel.

I looked at Donald’s profile, curved my lips slightly, and continued, "That’s already very good, he managed to survive such a difficult phase on his own, that’s really remarkable, we should sincerely wish him well, right?"

At my words, Donald’s eyes seemed to drift off a bit, as if reminiscing or perhaps forcing himself to accept reality.

At that moment, I didn’t quite realize how deeply this matter had impacted Donald, but I could see that he was very worried; Henry’s future seemed like it was covered in a thick veil, making one incredibly anxious and sorrowful.

"Perhaps. But you’re right, we should wish him well, and once we return to the palace and sort everything out, I’ll take you to see him, you two should get along well."

As he spoke, Donald’s eyes brightened a bit, and he continued while playing with my hair, "You and he are both very gentle and kind people."

I nodded, showing my anticipation.

It was the first time Donald had spoken so openly and patiently about his past to me.

I really enjoy our slow, steady conversations about each other, feeling as if in this way, we can get closer to each other’s hearts.
