chapters.title: Chapter 251: Materials and weapons
chapters.content:
Chapter 251: Materials and weapons

[Donald’s POV]

It had been a very long time since I had spoken of these past events to anyone.

It was the matter involving Austin and Lillian that started the conversation today, and since Margaret was so eager to know about my past, I took the time to slowly tell her everything.

Margaret’s final words of comfort were quite effective for me. The gloom in my heart was somewhat dispersed, and I bent down to kiss Margaret on the forehead.

Looking at her gentle and tender expression, as she lay completely obedient in my arms, I couldn’t help but smile. Margaret was my ’Stabilizer’.

It had been nearly an hour since we set off from the Windstorm Pack, and Margaret seemed a bit tired. She leaned against me, closed her eyes, and fell asleep, while at this moment, a knock came from the door of our room.

Quietly closing the door in front of me, I turned my head to glance at Mark and whispered, "Speak in the front carriage."

Standing behind me, Mark respectfully nodded his head, uncertain if my careful actions were appropriate or not.

It was now 2 o’clock in the afternoon. I looked at the overcast sky outside the RV and asked the subordinates standing before me, "Two months ago, Austin began stockpiling supplies and weapons in secret. Have you found anything new about it?"

"Don’t tell me you still know nothing. Without Captain Christian around, do you all just not feel like working anymore?"

As I spoke, I turned my head, my gaze shifting between Mark and Louis and finally resting on Benjamin.

I knew that this matter wasn’t within his area of responsibility, and I had not intended to involve him, but the space in the RV was limited. He had been doing his research in a corner of the front carriage for the past few days, and the dark circles under his eyes were nearly as black as the bottom of a pot.

"We admit our mistake, Your Majesty! It’s just that Austin has been incredibly secretive, and we can’t investigate him openly, which is why progress has been so slow," Mark reported to me, his head bowed in sincere anxiety, his face filled with remorse.

At this time, Louis also spoke up. He first glanced at Mark and then said, "I’ve already communicated with Captain Christian who’s following us. He said he had found some clues, but the opponent’s vigilance is so high that we dare not spook them by acting rashly!"

"We now only know that Austin has stockpiled about 200 submachine guns in his pack’s warehouse! Whether they’re for his own use or for someone else, we still do not know."

"200 guns? That number is indeed ambiguous. He could say they were for his own use, and it wouldn’t be implausible. What about the batch of supplies we noticed before? Do we know where he transported them?" I asked Mark and Louis in a somewhat sarcastic tone before pressing on.

In the territory of the Wolf royal family, werewolf packs need to report in advance to the Palace if they want to stockpile weapons or supplies. Austin’s trip to purchase supplies two months ago should, according to custom, have been first known to me, but that was not the case.

Austin chose to conceal this from the Palace. I learned about it from the reports of the personal guard force, with Christian being the main person in charge at that time.

However, before Christian could figure out what was happening, there was a report of an unidentified attack on the Red Sun pack which was located outside Devil’s Valley, near Margaret’s pack.

According to the intel from the Alpha of the Red Sun pack, we suspected that some kind of rebel force was at work, which led to the series of events which followed.

Investigating Austin was temporarily handed over to others, and our unavoidable stay at the Windstorm Pack was actually to make contact with the investigation team.

I continued to be as ’familiar’ and ’intimate’ with Austin and Lillian as usual, all of it to avoid arousing Austin’s suspicion.

He likely still had no idea I was aware of these matters, perhaps thinking I was simply displeased with Lillian’s disrespect towards Margaret.

"The destination of the supplies is Lilo Sea. Our investigation team tried to follow for a while, but ultimately lost track of them. We only know that the supplies were delivered by a shipping company called ’Voyager,’ and the owner is a young werewolf named Karl," Louis said as he opened his file bag and handed me a photo with the shipping company’s name on it.

I took the photo from him and said gravely, "Find someone to make contact with this Karl. I want to know who he works for, who he’s dealing with!"

Pointing at the cargo ship in the photo, I thought for a moment and then continued, "Inform Eric, who’s still in the Silver Moon Pack, to go directly to Lilo Sea. Leave this matter to him."

chapters.title: Chapter 252: Benjamin’s New Discovery
chapters.content:
Chapter 252: Benjamin’s New Discovery

[Donald’s POV]

Of course, Louis and Mark didn’t dare to defy my orders.

They quickly accepted the task and went on their way, holding the satellite phone as they headed back to the front passenger seat of the RV, where their work wouldn’t disturb Benjamin or me in the main cabin.

Gazing at their retreating figures, I leaned back in the seat and tiredly pinched the dry corners of my eyes.

The past few days had been too chaotic, too full, wearing me down, especially when I thought of the things related to Austin. An air of gloom and confusion filled my heart.

I couldn’t understand why Austin would do such things, what was going through his mind as he kept those things hidden from me. Was he, like Angel, harboring a lot of dissatisfaction towards me?

When exactly did we, friends who had grown up together, start to change?

No one could answer these questions for me; I could only bury them deep within my heart, hoping perhaps that no answers would ever surface...

"Your Majesty!"

Benjamin’s sudden outburst from inside the cabin interrupted my contemplation, and I instinctively looked up at him, curiosity and inquiry in my eyes.

Benjamin’s expression was very excited, one hand holding a dazzling notebook, the other pinching a vial of green liquid, as he exclaimed enthusiastically, "I’ve finally figured out the ingredients of the serum that allows weak werewolves to complete a special shift!"

Upon hearing it was related to ’MASTER’s’ serum, my interest was instantly piqued, and I sat up straight, gesturing with my hand for Benjamin to continue.

"You would never guess how the serum that turns ordinary werewolves into ’killing machines’ was created! This ’MASTER’ is truly a genius."

I had heard endless praises of ’MASTER’ before and didn’t find it surprising anymore, so I stayed silent, looking at Benjamin.

Realizing his excessive excitement, he swallowed before finally getting to the point, "The special ingredient that can alter the physical strength of a werewolf post-shift might be unheard of by us, but that doesn’t mean it doesn’t exist!"

"I just found an old notebook from when I was studying abroad, which recorded a herb that can instantly strengthen bones and muscles. It’s called—Blood Crystal Grass!"

"This herb is very rare and precious, and for the past decade, few pharmacists have been able to find it! I also heard about it from a teacher during my studies and saw its picture once. We also identified similar traces of this herb in the serum bottles we obtained from the enemy..."

Listening to Benjamin suppress his excitement, I pondered for a moment before quietly asking, "If you were able to analyze that serum before, why did you only remember the name of this herb now?"

"Your Majesty, that’s an excellent question! That’s what I’m about to explain to you..."

Benjamin wasn’t frightened or flustered by my inquiry; he flipped through his notebook, pointing at a pencil sketch of a herb, just about to continue when the sudden opening of the rear room door cut him off!

"What are you and Benjamin discussing? He looks like he’s won the lottery."

Margaret and I exchanged glances at the door. She looked at Benjamin’s excited face and asked with some confusion.

I gestured for her to come over, quickly explained the reasons for Benjamin’s excitement, kissed her cheek, then whispered, "Why did you wake up so soon? Are you hungry, should we get something to eat first?"

Margaret gave me a light glare, seemingly dissatisfied that at this moment, I was still only concerned about getting her to eat.

"Benjamin, continue, I want to hear what this is all about," Margaret said to Benjamin, setting aside my suggestion without hesitation.

I looked at her with a slight sense of helplessness and nodded at Benjamin, indicating that he could continue.

Benjamin, having been abruptly interrupted mid-sentence, almost choked on his own words. Waiting for Margaret and me to finish talking, he took a deep breath and continued, "Actually, the issue I’m about to explain to you is what I just mentioned!"

"Although I was successful at analyzing that serum before, I just couldn’t figure out the proportions of the various substances and didn’t know the primary ingredients!"

"I just flipped through this notebook and saw the entry about Blood Crystal Grass, and suddenly recalled its medicinal properties and uses. Then I linked it to the similar effects of that serum, and everything made sense!"

"The enemy’s injection-type serum, which is used to alter body size and enhance combat ability, is made from a large amount of Blood Crystal Grass extract, along with some special ingredients to stabilize its medicinal properties, thus it was directly developed successfully!"

chapters.title: Chapter 253: MASTER’s new features
chapters.content:
Chapter 253: MASTER’s new features

[Donald’s POV]

"This is actually a very simple pharmaceutical method! At first, I had no idea that it was Blood Crystal Grass, nor could I imagine the enemy could get their hands on so much of the rare and precious Blood Crystal Grass."

"We were led into a cognitive trap by the enemy from the very beginning. They might have gathered a large number of weak or disabled Lycans. The investment in this potion is massive, and the consumption is incredibly astonishing..."

Benjamin sucked in a deep breath, as if he had discovered some extremely shocking truth, paused for a moment, and then continued, "If the enemy isn’t some top technical talent in the field of growing medicinal herbs, then they must be a powerful figure with both money and influence. The vast amount of these medicinal ingredients, along with the complete manufacturing process for the potion, is not something an ordinary person could handle!"

When Benjamin finished this passage, his expression had become very grave.

This unexpected discovery made us even more aware of the complexity of the enemy; this ’MASTER’ not only has many tricks up their sleeve and incredible intelligence but now also has the characteristic of immense wealth and influence!

And with just that point alone, I could already lock down quite a few suspects.

My mood gradually darkened with Benjamin’s analysis, and the light in my eyes dimmed. Margaret was shocked by such a situation, but she quickly noticed my discomfort.

She held my hand with some worry, her eyes full of concern as she looked into mine.

"What are you thinking about?" Margaret asked me.

I took a light breath and, looking into her eyes, said gently, "Don’t worry, darling, I’m just thinking that the complexity of this matter has far exceeded our initial expectations. After we return to the Palace tomorrow, I need to consult with the elders about this."

If all of Benjamin’s analysis is correct, then the real reason behind ’MASTER’s actions is even more worth my in-depth investigation!

I have an intuition that ’MASTER’ is plotting something big, but I can’t determine what it specifically is just now. The malice of the other party has been quite evident all along, and I think I should make some more comprehensive preventive measures.

I planned in my mind what to do after returning to the Palace, but I didn’t continue to discuss much in front of Margaret.

My mate is a very attentive and intelligent person. I worry that if she knows about all these concerns in my heart, she will always be scared for me.

That is something I don’t want to see happen.

I love her deeply, and I only want her to stay by my side, carefree; that’s all that matters.

But it is often the case that the more you hope things will go well, the more they seem to go against you. Simply put, man proposes, God disposes.

My good intentions at the moment were shattered in a second. I had not expected that on the border between the Werewolf Palace and the Windstorm Pack, someone would dare to launch an attack on our convoy!

Margaret initially led a Guard Team of four vehicles, with one scrapped at Starry Ravine. Adding the six vehicles I brought over, our speeding convoy actually consisted of nine.

Just the three leading military SUVs at the front of the convoy already carried an unapproachable weight and authority; alongside that, our elongated custom-built RV was even more dignified, not something people would dare to take a second glance at. I didn’t think any ill-advised person would dare to approach!

So, when the entire convoy suddenly came under attack, my first instinct told me that this must be a new action directed by ’MASTER’.

The first to notice the abnormal situation were the two cars traveling before and after us.

Almost at the same moment, I received their urgent mind-link!

"Your Majesty! There seems to be an emergency!"

The Lycan driving reported the situation, followed by the Lycan in the lead car who noticed that the road ahead was blocked by huge rocks, "Your Majesty! Our path is blocked by a boulder, and these people are obviously well-prepared!"

I glanced out of the car window at the enemy convoy. As our path was blocked, our own vehicles had been forced to slow down, "These opponents are more than well-prepared, huh? They are intent on a do-or-die battle. Everyone, ready your weapons and prepare to combat at any time!"

My command was relayed instantly, and Margaret clutched my hand, her expression tense and worried.

"Who are these people? Why have they suddenly appeared here? How did they know which way we were taking back to the Palace? This is too weird, Donald. Be extra careful!"

To Margaret’s questions, I would say that I also want to know. I gave my tense mate a reassuring smile and responded, "Don’t worry, these small fries won’t be able to break through the personal guard force’s defenses, and the Lycans of our assault team are no pushovers."

With that, I patted Margaret’s hand and stood up, grabbing the submachine gun that had not seen any use up to that point...

chapters.title: Chapter 254: Don’t Get Hurt
chapters.content:
Chapter 254: Don’t Get Hurt

[Donald’s POV]

Gazing into Margaret’s incomparable eyes, I smiled comfortingly and continued, "You and Benjamin stay in the car, and Louis and Mark will stay close to protect you. I will deal with those bastards quickly, they and whoever is behind them need to know what happens when they provoke me, Donald, time and again!"

Upon hearing this, a look of worry instantly filled Margaret’s eyes. She seemed to have so much to say but ended up swallowing her words.

She obediently nodded and said to me in a serious tone, "I understand, be careful and don’t get hurt!"

I touched her cheek and replied, "I promise you."

During the few seconds Margaret and I were speaking, the lead vehicles in our caravan had already been forced to stop. Several off-road vehicles that were following closely behind our RV also preemptively circled around, tightly protecting the RV that carried Margaret and Elliot!

[Margaret’s POV]

When the first gunshot rang out outside of our RV, I instinctively turned to look through the window, and it was at that moment Donald got out of the car and walked out.

I hurriedly tried to reach for the hem of his clothes, but I wasn’t successful. Louis and Mark quickly came over, swiftly locking the car door and clutching their weapons, ready for action.

I didn’t want to be a nuisance at such a critical moment and become that unreasonable, careless person, so I forced myself to calm down, knelt down on the ground and through the bulletproof one-way glass in front of me, I started observing the situation outside.

’Bang’!

The enemy’s vehicle had collided head-on with the outermost off-road vehicle, and it was at that moment I saw Donald’s retreating figure.

Bending over, he quickly jumped into another car, the corner of his mouth moving slightly, as if arranging for the assault team members to counterattack.

Given the current situation, even I, a novice in battle, knew that we couldn’t sit idly by!

The enemy’s intentions had become blatantly obvious when they rammed into the off-road vehicle, they wanted to keep us here at all costs.

The vehicles that were speeding toward us without tapping the brakes were their suicide squad.

Because the RV was taller than all the other cars on the scene, I could see all of the enemy’s offensive moves. I spoke to Louis and Mark, "The cars ramming into us now are only their vanguard. We need to have the Lycans from the personal guard force take them out first. This way, the assault team can also use our own vehicles as cover to counterattack!"

No sooner had my words fallen than several gun barrels protruding from the windows of our caravan had already started the vanguard battle!

"Lycan Queen! You and His Majesty really are in sync, he just said the same thing through our mind-link system! Whatever you can think of, he can definitely think of too, so you don’t have to worry!"

It appeared Mark received some information, his gaze froze for a second, then quickly became clear again, likely having just heard Donald speak in the mind-link.

It was then that I realized I had been too nervous to tune into Donald’s mind-link, and after taking a deep breath, I began to seriously listen as Donald used the mind-link to command the Lycans to fight!

<Right side! At 3 o’clock, there’s another bomber vehicle, snipers take it out!>

<Yes, Your Majesty!>

<Watch out for the Lycans stealthily approaching from the sides! Kill them, let none escape!>

<As you command, Your Majesty!>

<Send a few more people to clear the rocks blocking the main road; if that fails, just smash through with the vehicles. I refuse to believe we can’t get through! Everyone else, follow my lead; any within range, open fire and eliminate!>

<No survivors, Your Majesty?>

<They are merely fodder for their human wave tactics, and the real puppeteers may not even be present on the battlefield. If you pity them, then you will be the next one to die!>

The Lycan warrior who heard Donald’s response seemed to have no more questions. Donald stopped talking and all I could hear were the chilling gunshots and the frenzied enemy’s charge.

I silently swallowed and clenched my fist as I watched the off-road vehicle where Donald was.

"These people don’t seem to be ordinary Lycans..."

Benjamin suddenly murmured beside me. I glanced at him and asked aloud, "Did you notice something?"

Benjamin pointed to a Lycan that had just been shot dead by one of our Lycan warriors and continued with a frown, "Did you notice the branding of torture on their faces, and the black clothes they’re wearing, Your Majesty? Those are the punishments for rogues who have made serious errors, and we’ve seen those black garments quite a few times before!"

"These might not be ordinary Lycans at all, but rogue ones that have been banished from the pack!"

chapters.title: Chapter 255: Massive Attack by Rogue
chapters.content:
Chapter 255: Massive Attack by Rogue

[Margaret’s POV]

"They’ve always been brutally evil, lacking any organizational obedience. What kind of person or organization could possibly gather them together, willing to dedicate their lives to it?"

Benjamin’s puzzled murmur woke me up. I widened my eyes, looked at him, swallowed my saliva, and asked, "You also think this is very strange, don’t you? They seemed to have planned in advance, setting a trap for us to fall into! How did they know about our secret mission? Why were they sure we would take this route?"

"This area is the junction between the Palace and the Windstorm Pack. Their large-scale ambush here, why didn’t it attract any attention from either side? This attack is too unusual! Or to put it more directly, aren’t they being too bold?"

As I spoke, I began to doubt the real purpose of these people again!

If their only goal was to stop us from escorting Elliot back to the Palace for medical attention, then why didn’t they act before we met up with Donald?

Isn’t Starry Ravine an excellent ambush site?

Or is their real target not Elliot at all, but rather any one of us, including Donald and me?

Or perhaps they are here to provoke the entire Wolf royal family!

When this thought rose from the depths of my heart, I was startled.

I shook my head, trying to dismiss this outrageous idea, but another intuition deep in my mind told me this might be the ultimate answer!

Because apart from this, there was no other reason to explain the current situation. Our Guard Team might have been targeted by the enemies from the very beginning.

Thinking this, I suddenly remembered the beast that attacked the Guard Team in Starry Ravine. Its eyes, glowing with a strange red, wasn’t it just like these rogues attacking us now?

They all seemed like battle monsters that had lost their self-consciousness, knowing only to rush forward and devour the flesh of their enemies!

"Do you think these people, like those ambushers before, were lured by an enemy with potions, determined not to give up until their goal is achieved?" I asked Benjamin.

Hearing my speculation, Benjamin’s expression slowly froze.

He pondered for a while, gently shook his head, and said, "It doesn’t seem likely. These rogues are different from those ambushers. They are much more impulsive and reckless, and they don’t have the kind of potions that could rapidly enhance their combat abilities. They probably don’t belong to the same person..."

Benjamin spoke and then immediately contradicted himself, as if he couldn’t figure out some key point, "That doesn’t seem entirely accurate, either. We need to capture a few more prisoners and interrogate them thoroughly! It would be best to capture their leader to extract more useful information."

I looked at Benjamin, his eyebrows furrowed, and decided not to trouble him further with this problem. As a researcher and thinker like him, it must be painful not to be able to unravel the puzzle right in front of him, right?

My attention returned to Donald, who then issued a few commands, all of which were precise and perfect military orders. He was like a born military strategist, a beacon of faith firmly believed in by all soldiers when standing on the battlefield!

As time gradually passed, the attack-defense battle centered around the RV heated up. Donald’s defensive orders were absolutely right, and it was impossible for the enemy to break through the perimeter in a short time, making the area where Elliot and I were the safest on the field.

The enemy’s battle commander seemed to have gradually realized this. They stopped pursuing the tactic of a mass charge and began to shift forms, trying to utilize the agile and fluid wolf form to dodge the gunfire on the field and break through to our location!

Our convoy, including Ruth and me, had only 58 people. Compared to the endless stream of rogues, we seemed to have no advantage. Our ammunition was limited, and the battle wouldn’t end before we ran out.

So, as time trickled away, this modern medieval warfare soon returned to its primitive state. The fight among the Lycans was based on claws and teeth, which was no different now!

With a command from Donald, the Lycans surrounding the outermost perimeter of the convoy instantly completed their shift, and soon tangled with the enemy’s rogues.

They were Lycans fighting one against ten, unafraid of such bloody combat, even more, excited and thrilled by it.

Thus, with Lycans in wolf form leading the combat and those still armed covering them with gunfire,

this well-coordinated battle mode soon severely weakened the enemy. The ground was covered with the bodies and blood of rogues, and the air was filled with a nauseating smell of blood...

chapters.title: Chapter 256: Living Target
chapters.content:
Chapter 256: Living Target

[Margaret’s POV]

Even facing such frightening casualties, the commander of the enemy organization attacking us seemed to have no intention of sparing our RV; there were dozens of rogues I saw in my line of sight charging toward the RV!

Most of their attacks were intercepted by our Lycan team members, with only a few rogues breaking through the layers of defense to get close and attack our RV.

Louis and Mark’s nerves were incredibly tense, veins in their hands bulging as they clutched their guns.

When the glass in front of me was struck by a bullet, leaving cracks, I knew we could no longer keep hiding; we had to break through the encirclement and get out as soon as possible. We couldn’t just sit and wait for death.

"They have guns designed specifically for armored vehicles; remember not to expose your position! Louis, open the right door. We need to go out and face them; we can’t let them focus all their attention on this RV," I said, lying flat on the ground with an undeniable determination in my eyes.

"Lycan Queen! How can this be? His Majesty said you cannot leave this RV. If anything happens to you, Mark and I would be inexcusably negligent! No, I cannot agree with you!" Louis protested, his eyes widening in alarm.

Mark, who stood beside him by the door, wore the same expression of refusal on his face.

Benjamin looked at Louis, who was speaking, then turned his head to look at me. "Louis is right, Lycan Queen. We can’t let you take the risk of getting out! They won’t break through this RV any time soon. Let’s wait for His Majesty to coordinate the defense. Please don’t make it hard for Louis and the others!"

"I’m not trying to make it hard for any of you! Haven’t you realized? The enemy doesn’t have many sniper rifles that can counter bulletproof equipment, and their only target is this RV; it’s literally a live target right now!"

"They must know I am in here, which is why they’re targeting us so specifically. Getting out and changing positions at this time is also for my safety!"

I continued, picking up a handgun from the table without pausing, "Elliot is still in the medical room inside; I can move, but he can’t. The two of you better think about it!"

After finishing my piece, I didn’t bother with them any further and glanced at the maid, Ruth, who was crouching not far away.

I slid the handgun across the floor to her and softened my tone before continuing, "Just in case, take this gun and hide in Elliot’s medical room! You keep an eye on him; we won’t let the enemy have a chance to break in. Go now."

In the few seconds while I was speaking to Ruth, our RV’s left side driver’s seat and rear compartment glass were attacked by enemy bullets!

These gunshots were like warnings, as well as some sort of declaration.

Having gained some courage from the ordeal at Starry Ravine, Ruth looked at me with moist eyes before firmly nodding, picking up the pistol, and crawling towards Elliot’s medical room.

I then turned my attention back to Benjamin and, after a moment’s thought, reassured him, "Don’t underestimate me too much; I’m someone who has walked away unscathed from Angel’s clutches! I’m aware of my limits and will protect myself."

"And you are Elliot’s last line of defense! Of course, I believe Donald won’t let me or you be of any use."

Benjamin, being the Lycans’ medic, would probably be about as good in combat as I was, but his intelligence was worth much more than mine.

I didn’t spell out this last part directly but just gave him a look before gesturing for Louis to open the door.

After wrestling with himself, Louis finally clenched his teeth and handed me another submachine gun from his shoulder, then forcefully yanked open the door and jumped out first.

At this point, Mark didn’t say much either, just followed closely behind Louis, his expression quite grim.

They must have felt extremely reluctant, but at that moment, I couldn’t deal with so much; I was certain that my choice was the best one. I leapt out of the RV with agile movements, guarded by the two of them, one in front, one behind.

As the outside cold air hit me, my brain instantly became more alert. I subconsciously tensed every nerve and muscle in my body, my excellent vision scanning every inch of the surrounding space.

’Roar!’ An earsplitting wolf howl came from behind us. I was startled and immediately turned around, but Mark had already raised his gun and started shooting first!

After several cries of pain, I smelled the blood in the air...

But the rogue that had been shot wasn’t immediately felled; instead, it charged at us even more furiously.

Mark and I realized the gravity of the situation almost at the same time; the rogue’s fur was able to withstand the shots from the submachine gun!

chapters.title: Chapter 257: Unkillable Rogue?
chapters.content:
Chapter 257: Unkillable Rogue?

[Margaret’s POV]

The bizarre scene seemed to stun Mark. He froze for a second, and just as I caught a glimpse of the rogue’s claw about to tear through his chest, I reached out and grabbed the sleeve of his shoulder...

In that critical moment, I rolled to the side with Mark, narrowly avoiding the sharp claw!

Meanwhile, Louis seized the opportunity. With his submachine gun directly against the rogue’s head, he didn’t hesitate to pull the trigger and spray bullets, instantly obliterating the rogue’s face, including its unprotected wolf eyes.

A gruesome howl erupted, and I smelled the nauseating scent of blood in the air. Holding my breath in discomfort, my stomach churned repeatedly.

I let go of Mark’s clothes and stood up with the submachine gun in hand. Suddenly, a whooshing sound strikingly close reached me from behind.

"Look out! Behind you!"

Louis’s warning came almost in sync with my actions.

I turned and fired. The bullets struck the attacking rogue, but they only lodged in its skin! Its hide seemed as impenetrable as a wall of bronze and iron. From such a close range, I couldn’t penetrate its body.

"The face and abdomen are its weak points! Lycan Queen, aim for their eyes!" Mark was shouting this reminder with his high-pitched voice.

Of course, I knew their vulnerabilities. It was just that everything happened so suddenly, I didn’t have time to adjust the aim of my gun.

Luckily, the bullets still inflicted some damage. The rogue howled in pain and leaped at me with its gaping jaws. But in the next second, Louis, who had completed his shift, sent it flying with a crash against the side of a nearby car!

"This isn’t right, Lycan Queen. These rogues are not like the ones His Majesty and the others encountered before! Dammit, what kind of monsters are these that can’t be killed even with bullets to the face?"

Of course, I didn’t know what was going on either. I quickly used my mind-link to inform Donald of the situation and sought his help.

This was not the time to pretend to be strong. These monstrous rogues were beyond our ability to handle; of that I was well aware.

"Gradually move closer to the front, Donald won’t leave us hanging!"

As I spoke, I completed my shift in an instant, gracefully and deftly avoiding another rogue’s attack, and in the process, I hopped onto the roof of the RV. I glanced up to assess Donald and the others’ situation.

Donald responded to my mind-link almost immediately. His silver figure quickly appeared atop the SUV to my front left.

But I only had time to exchange a glance with him when the bullets shooting at my feet the very next second drew my attention!

Leveraging the keen fighting instincts of a werewolf, I dodged several sniper shots by a hair’s breadth. Not until I took cover behind a larger vehicle did I dare peek in the direction of the gunfire!

When I clearly saw the familiar figure hidden in the lush tree canopy, my mouth dropped open in shock, and I subconsciously denied to myself, "No! This can’t be! She shouldn’t be here!"

At that moment, the enemy seemed to have discovered where I was hiding as they ’responded’ with several viciously accurate bullets!

I quickly withdrew my head, trying hard to stay calm. Now was not the time for expressions of confusion and incomprehension, and no one was going to answer the questions in my mind.

The only thing I could do then was to report this bizarre situation to Donald.

Angel had been targeting me from the start; her sniper rifle was not directed at anyone else, always at the RV where I had been. After I moved out of the RV, she followed with her rifle!

Thinking back to the shots I narrowly avoided just moments ago, a shiver ran through me. I snorted to myself, "Angel, Angel, your determination to see me dead is truly persistent!"

"But I won’t let you get your way easily. We’ll see. You can’t always be so lucky to escape from our hands."

This was Donald communicating with me through mind-link. Instinctively, I wanted to turn and look at him, but the gunfire above my head stopped me in my tracks.

I bit my lip in frustration, really wanting to rush out and have another fight with Angel. She was like an evil spirit, always so persistently haunting me!

I quickly replied to Donald and lay still.

Angel’s surprise appearance was alarming to both me and Donald, but even more bizarre and inexplicable was that she seemed lively and as combat-ready as ever!

chapters.title: Chapter 258: Angel again
chapters.content:
Chapter 258: Angel again

[Margaret’s POV]

It had only been a few days since Donald encountered Angel in the Misty Forest, where he ordered his assault team members to surround and severely injure her.

Shouldn’t she be hiding somewhere, secretly healing? Or, like Elliot, lying in a hospital bed unable to move—she definitely shouldn’t be disgustingly clutching a sniper rifle, appearing on the battlefield, and taking cold shots at me from a hidden position!

Had her wounds healed so quickly?

Or was it possible that her being gravely injured by Donald in the Misty Forest was just a deception?

Was her feigned injury and escape merely a ruse to deceive Donald, with the purpose of letting all the Lycans lower their guard, then waiting for an opportunity to strike out of the blue, in hopes of catching us off guard?

Just like now?

In that instant, countless doubts flooded my mind, I couldn’t fathom why Angel was appearing here at this time; there had to be some strange secret behind it.

While I puzzled over these things, the gunfire and battle around me still raged.

Thanks to Donald’s meticulous care, those peculiar rogues could no longer approach me; they were directly taken down by the Lycans working in pairs. Though the battle was tough, ultimate victory was undoubtedly ours.

Angel seemed to be infuriated by the evasive tactics I continuously employed, and began frantically shooting at the car in front of me!

The sound of gunfire made my heart contract in fright, but it was followed by a surging tide of rage inside me! This woman was so arrogant as to be utterly lawless, as if I not only stole Donald from her but also killed her parents.

I never quite understood why Angel harbored such intense hatred towards me; I just thought this woman must have some major psychological issues—or to put it simply, she was mad!

I was patiently waiting for Donald to give me the next instruction, knowing that I couldn’t just stay hidden here forever.

"Margaret! Come over to me, move quickly, and don’t lift your head."

It was Donald’s voice coming from behind me; I immediately turned my head to look and then darted towards him, keeping my body low.

"Where is Angel? Have you seen her on this side?" I asked.

Donald quickly used his large and superior body to shield me, his wolf head gently nuzzling my head as if to confirm I was unharmed, "I’ve sent Mark and his team after her. She won’t escape!"

"From now on, you must stay close behind me at all times. I worry that besides this large-scale attack by the rogues, the enemy might have more tricks up their sleeve. I’m not comfortable having anyone else protect you!"

Hearing Donald speak like this, naturally, I had no objections. In fact, I yearned to be by his side at all times because only by knowing he was within my line of sight could my heart settle a bit.

He wanted to protect me well, and I was no different!

As night slowly fell, the battle between us and our enemies began to shift. Perhaps due to the length of the fight, the rogues, initially ferocious and aggressive, started to show signs of fatigue.

This was enough to reveal how rigorous the regular training of Donald’s Lycans was, which accounted for their superior endurance on the battlefield.

I followed behind Donald, scanning the surroundings, guessing that if the enemy had no more moves, the battle would probably end within half an hour.

Donald seemed to think the same. He swatted away an overconfident rogue with a sweep of his paw and looked towards Louis, not far from us, and said in a deep voice, "Don’t give them any chance to breathe. Everyone, attack! Notify Mark to find a way to draw Angel here."

"Yes, Your Majesty!" Louis replied respectfully, taking the lead with his Lycans into the enemy ranks.

Yet what none of us expected was a strange turn of events that happened just then!

The rogues, engaged in a bloody battle, suddenly all stopped and turned to retreat towards a birch forest. And Angel, whom Donald had ordered to be pursued, unexpectedly walked out from the woods at that moment.

In her hand, she held a silver pistol, pressing the barrel against the head of a blood-covered Lycan, mockingly looking at Donald and me.

"Dear Donald, we meet again! Have you missed me these past few days? Hahaha, it’s okay if you haven’t. I came to bring you a gift this time, you’ll like it, won’t you?"

With an arrogant tone, Angel spoke, and without warning, she suddenly pulled the trigger, blowing the Lycan’s head apart!

The blood-red brain matter sprayed on the ground, and Louis, who had not run far, witnessed this scene and seemed to recall something suddenly.

He sharply turned his head toward Donald, his voice trembling slightly as he reminded, "That’s one of the personal guard force..."

chapters.title: Chapter 259: Gift
chapters.content:
Chapter 259: Gift

[Margaret’s POV]

"During their time with the Silver Moon Pack, they participated in the operation to rescue the Lycan Queen and Luna Elizabeth, and Elliot and Eric were the main people in charge that day!"

"Eric reported after the battle that they disappeared near that cabin in the forest. I originally thought they had all fallen in battle, but now..."

As Louis spoke, he took a breath that was hard to believe, then continued in a hoarse voice, "I didn’t expect that they weren’t dead, just captured by the enemy and subjected to such inhuman torture! Your Majesty, the Queen, we must find a way to rescue them!"

Louis’s grievous words echoed in my ears as I coldly glanced at Angel across from me before shifting my gaze to their feet—to those pitiful, blood-soaked members of the personal guard force Lycans!

Beneath the grim sky, there was a silence as still as death! Donald and I didn’t speak immediately but watched Angel, who was smug and triumphant, brewing a storm of rage within us.

"Angel, what exactly do you want to do? You can’t defeat us, so you resort to such vile and despicable methods? They are Lycans of the Wolf royal family, true warriors capable of taking on ten at a time!"

"You were their comrade until not long ago, and now you treat them with such heartlessness? I can understand your hatred for me, but to do this to them as well... I absolutely cannot condone it!"

In an instant, I completed my transformation back into human form, and spoke fiercely to Angel. If I didn’t vent, I felt I might die from the anger her actions caused!

The rage that Donald was feeling at that moment must have been similar to mine. He transformed back into his human form, his eyes deep and icily cold as he looked at Angel, "Release them. I won’t say it a second time."

The majesty of the Lycan king was still utterly chilling in that moment. I stood next to Donald, trembling involuntarily, while Angel on the other side must have felt it even more deeply.

She laughed inexplicably first, then looked at Donald full of sarcasm.

"I told you, I’m here today to bring you a gift! As for whether the gift arrives dead or alive, that depends on your performance," she said, toying with the silver pistol in her hand, her eyes filled with defiance.

I was certain Angel had an ulterior motive. I reached for Donald’s hand, trying to calm him down.

Interrupted by my gesture, Donald seemed to slowly come back to reason. He gave a slight mocking smile to Angel, full of ridicule, "Do you really think you and your pathetic rogue entourage here can escape from me today? You won’t always be so lucky."

"MASTER sent you today for more than just to provoke me, right? What’s your purpose? Where is he?"

Angel seemed unfazed by Donald’s retort, instead pulling another severely injured Lycan towards her and continuing with a smile, "You’re so defiant. It seems you really don’t care about the lives of these Lycans, do you?"

"Then I’ll just kill them all. Their blood would make a lovely red carpet for your beloved Queen. What do you say, Donald, isn’t that a good idea?"

"What do you mean?"

"You are mad! Angel, you’re a lunatic!"

Donald and I spoke almost at the same time.

He asked in a somber tone what Angel meant while I outright cursed at her—this woman was a complete psychopath!

"These Lycans were supposed to be dead already. It was only out of kindness that I begged MASTER to spare these pitiful lives!"

"After all, to sacrifice their lives for a worthless Queen is sadness enough, and I think it’s not worth it for them. Dying outside the Werewolf Palace is far more valuable than dying for that wretch in the Silver Moon Pack, right, Leon?"

As Angel spoke, the gun in her hand was already pressed against the brow of the Lycan named Leon. Her gaze shifted from Donald to me, turning venomous in an instant.

I knew Angel was pressuring me, wanting to get something from me. I could very well pretend not to understand. I don’t know Leon, so why should I put myself in such a passive position for him?

As long as Donald is here, Angel wouldn’t be able to touch me at all!

But could I really turn a blind eye? These members of the personal guard force had fallen into danger for Elizabeth and me in the first place.

I couldn’t just do nothing, even though I fully understood Angel’s malicious intentions!

"What do you want to do, just say it," I said to Angel.

Donald seemed to have anticipated my concession. His large hand gripped mine tightly, and he continued in my stead, concluding with a bite at the end, "I don’t care what you want to do, but you’re destined not to leave here today. If you escape again, I might as well step down as the Lycan King and let MASTER have it!"

chapters.title: Chapter 260: Negotiation
chapters.content:
Chapter 260: Negotiation

[Margaret’s POV]

As Donald’s forceful words hit the ground, I even saw some subtle, but very eerie, muscle twitching on Angel’s face!

She was silent for a few seconds, then suddenly burst into hysterical laughter, looking as though she had gone completely mad. "Hahaha! Donald, you truly are the man I took an interest in. Facing death, yet still so arrogant and confident."

"You don’t think I dragged these disabled Lycans all the way from the Silver Moon Pack back here just to prepare a surprise for you, do you?"

"When did you become so naive? Could it be that you were infected by that whore by your side? Hahaha, I told you she would drag you down, and yet you chose to believe that whore you’ve only known for less than three months over my sincere advice!"

Angel snorted coldly, tilted her chin up, stared at Donald, and scornfully continued, "Well, it doesn’t matter anymore. We’ve come this far, and talking more about it is pointless! However, on account of these Lycans who had once trained with me, I’m going to ensure they die enlightened and I will plan a way out for them..."

This woman’s way of speaking is so disorganized now, it’s truly difficult to guess what she really wants to do. I furrowed my brows and coldly watched her, waiting for her to reveal her ultimate goal!

Just as Angel said, they wouldn’t have gone to such lengths just to ambush our convoy and provoke the Wolf royal family—it’s not that simple.

Our palace convoy was tightly guarded and protected by many exceptional Lycan warriors. Despite the savagery of their rogue army, they are but a ragtag bunch. Indeed, breaking through the defenses of the army under Donald’s command was no simple feat.

This development in their attack is exactly why MASTER and Angel couldn’t afford to return without accomplishing anything, thus resulting in the current scenario!

They are scheming, and their wicked plans are interlinked.

The idle talk she pulled with me and Donald was merely to gain some leverage in the upcoming negotiations, whether for tangible benefits or for her personal demeanor!

While discussing, Angel suddenly paused. The scorn on her face disappeared at that moment, and her previously beautiful face turned dreadfully pale, completely devoid of color. She continued to glare into my eyes, filled with malice, "To trade Margaret’s worthless life for the lives of so many Lycan warriors, Donald, today you have struck a good deal!"

As she said these words, her eyes were fixed on me, but her words were directed at Donald!

Angel’s demand was not far off from what I had braced myself a second earlier. I listened to her state their negotiation terms with utmost calmness.

However, Donald, standing beside me, was not as composed and rational!

"You know that’s impossible, I will not agree to your demands! And, what makes you think you have the right to negotiate with me?" Donald firmly rejected Angel’s terms, his expression chilling enough to freeze someone solid.

He smirked slightly as he spoke, a smirk filled with sarcasm and disdain, "If MASTER himself were here today, perhaps there would be a need for discussion. But you, Angel? You’re not worthy of negotiating with me."

"A traitor who forsakes her own family and beliefs, what right do you have to stand before me and speak so grandly? The traitors are what we in the Wolf royal family despise the most."

"That you can still bounce around here today just shows your former good luck, and MASTER really isn’t choosy, keeping any trash by his side!"

As Donald’s judgmental words slipped from his mouth, the expression of the judged could be described as richly varied.

I hadn’t realized before how adept Donald was at verbal attack; he was practically grilling Angel over the coals in front of all these Lycans!

Although what he said was the truth, clearly Angel didn’t see it that way.

Angel probably thought Donald had wronged her, and she became what she is today because of being backed into a corner by me and Donald. She perceived herself as the most pitiable victim in these events.

"No! That’s not how it really happened. I did all of this to be with you, Donald. Why are you so heartless? You should know why I did what I did!" As Angel’s emotions spiraled out of control, she took two steps forward as if trying to get closer to Donald.

Still motionless, I watched Angel across from me, feeling infinitely tense and a momentary complexity.

Yet, Donald remained the same skilled and rational war strategist, knowing that this was a good opportunity...

chapters.title: Chapter 261: Psycho Attack
chapters.content:
Chapter 261: Psycho Attack

[Margaret’s POV]

As long as she could completely enrage Angel, causing her to lose her ability to think rationally, Mark and his people ambushed in the birch forest would seize the opportunity to capture Angel and a few rogue leaders!

Indeed, the reason Donald was striking up so much conversation with Angel was merely to create a favorable time and opportunity for Mark and his team.

Just a few minutes ago, Mark, dispatched by Donald, had suddenly informed us of his plan through a mind-link...

Initially, he had not found an opportunity to strike at Angel in the forest, until there was a sudden change in the stand-off on the battlefield—that was when Angel and those rogue leaders, escorting our incarcerated Lycans, walked out. That’s when Mark, on the spur of the moment, stealthily surrounded them with the intention of capturing Angel directly!

Of course, the prerequisite was to ensure the safety of all our fellow Lycans.

"Yes! I have betrayed the Wolf royal family and my own clan. Anyone can call me a traitor, but you cannot! Donald, you cannot call me a traitor! I’m doing all this for you!"

Angel continued to ramble on, stubbornly insisting that none of this was her fault and that she was innocent!

"Really? Was it I who made you severely injure Elliot, was it I who made you betray your own trust, was it I pressing down on your neck forcing you to cooperate with MASTER?" Donald asked back calmly, his intense glare seeming as though it was about to materialize into something tangible the next second.

And these continuous counter-questions instantly became the last straw that broke Angel’s spirit!

At that moment, Angel seemed to realize her inability to refute and, collapsing mentally, she raised her hand high, pointing her gun directly at Donald.

My heart tightened suddenly, and my reflexes acted faster than my brain could control my body!

I stepped forward and shielded Donald, and the bullet that should have hit Donald directly in the chest unexplainably missed by a lot...

"Hmm!"

"Margaret! You..."

The bullet grazed the flesh on my shoulder, and I let out an involuntary grunt from the intense pain that nearly shattered me.

Donald’s cry of alarm sounded almost simultaneously above my head, and as I instinctively looked up at him, I could only make out his eyes full of surprise and fear before my lids slowly closed in exhaustion!

I felt such pain all over, and I was utterly exhausted...

[Donald’s POV]

The vivid red blossom of pain on Margaret’s shoulder shocked my eyes!

Instinctively, I stretched out my arms and urgently embraced Margaret, who was collapsing into my arms. I had not realized how panicked and fearful I was in that second.

I was kneeling on the ground, holding Margaret, my fingers trembling as I dared not touch her face, "Margaret? Baby? How are you, don’t scare me! Please wake up, talk to me, okay?"

With her eyes closed, Margaret did not respond to any of my questions. I watched as her face lost color instantly, and I was dumbstruck!

It wasn’t until I heard other people around me that I suddenly came to my senses, turned around, and hysterically shouted: "Bring Benjamin here, Margaret is injured! Benjamin? Where is Benjamin?"

"Your Majesty! Please don’t panic, Benjamin is right behind; he’ll be here any moment!"

Louis also had immediately rushed over when Angel fired the shot, and now he was helplessly looking at me and the unconscious Margaret.

"Your Majesty, please don’t worry, the Lycan Queen was only shot in the shoulder!" a Lycan squatting at my left desperately said.

His reassurance was ineffective for me. I gave him a cold side glance, wanting to ask him what he meant by ’only shot in the shoulder?’ Did he not realize who Margaret was? I wouldn’t allow Margaret to suffer any injury by my side!

Yet the irony was, being by my side, Margaret was always bound to get hurt.

Intimidated by my look, that Lycan froze on the spot, while Benjamin at that moment rushed over swiftly.

I didn’t bother with that Lycan anymore, focusing back on Margaret and Benjamin, "She took a bullet for me and then passed out. Quick, check how she is!"

Benjamin, having been immersed in battlefield medics for many years, was naturally adept at emergency care. He nodded respectfully at me and then started examining Margaret’s wound.

Staring at the bloody gunshot wound, my hands holding Margaret trembled slightly, and Benjamin probably noticed but said nothing.

"The bullet is lodged in the bone! We need to perform surgery on the Lycan Queen immediately to remove the bullet! Your Majesty, we don’t have adequate medical equipment in the vehicle, we need to head back to the hospital in Royal City now!"

Benjamin just needed a few glances to determine Margaret’s condition, his tone extremely grave. I naturally understood the urgency of the situation.

chapters.title: Chapter 262: Do not try to stop me
chapters.content:
Chapter 262: Do not try to stop me

[Donald’s POV]

"Then go back! Have someone clear the way ahead! What use are the clean-up crews if they can’t move a rock after all this time?" I asked in a deep voice.

Seemingly intimidated by my commanding presence, no one dared to respond for a moment, until Benjamin finally swallowed and respectfully replied, "They have only managed to open a gap, our RV is too wide, it can’t get through completely!"

Upon hearing this, I took several deep breaths trying to calm myself, fearful that I might completely lose my composure otherwise.

"You come with me, everyone else stays and follows Louis’s orders, thoroughly clean up the battlefield, and capture all the rogue leaders alive!" I said as I hugged Margaret tighter, nimbly stood up from the ground, and turned to walk towards the RV.

While Benjamin and I were busy tending to Margaret’s injuries, good news finally came from Mark who had been lying in wait to ambush Angel!

<Your Majesty! We have successfully captured Angel, but the rogues have started to retaliate on a full scale. I need Louis’s assistance right now!>

<Understood, Louis is on his way. Keep a close watch on her! If she escapes again, don’t bother coming back to the Palace.> I replied to Mark’s mind-link, gently placing Margaret on the couch with my hands.

After glancing at Benjamin, who was holding a bandage ready to treat Margaret’s bleeding, I issued a stern order: "Stabilize her bleeding first, we’re heading back to the Royal City right now."

Benjamin nodded heavily at me, clearly not about to defy my orders.

I spun around and sat in the driver’s seat of the RV, without paying much attention to the Lycan driver’s hesitation and surprise upon moving aside. The only thought in my mind was to get Margaret home.

I drove the RV out from the encircled guard convoy, heading straight towards the gap between the huge boulder and the mountain side of the highway, slamming the accelerator hard!

’Bang!’

’Screech!’

A severe collision noise was heard as the front of the vehicle slammed into the sides of the gap, the body of the RV screeching painfully as it scraped against the boulder and mountain wall. The Lycan beside me uncomfortably covered his ears, while I was as if deaf to it all, only Margaret’s blood-stained upper body fuelling my tense nerves!

"Your Majesty! It’s impossible to get through like this, the vehicle will get stuck!"

"Who said it’s impossible to get through? Not even the Moon goddess can stop me today!" I gripped the steering wheel tightly, flooring the accelerator.

The RV in my hands became an indomitable steel beast, plunging forward fearlessly even if the road ahead was filled with knife edges and ravines.

When the vehicle’s body squeezed its head through the gap, I considered retracting the harsh words I had previously directed at my subordinates.

They weren’t completely useless, after all; carving out such a path in such a short time was no simple task.

The specially reinforced RV was ultimately ruined in my hands, emerging from the gap scraped and scarred, we still burst through!

"My God, Your Majesty! We actually made it through..."

"You drive for the remainder of the journey, I want you to get us back to the Royal City at top speed! Do you understand what I mean?" Ignoring the Lycan driver’s gasp, I began unbuckling my seatbelt as I issued the command.

He swallowed nervously as I stepped out of the driver’s seat, nodding and taking his place at the wheel.

As I returned to Margaret’s side, the RV surged forward on the road, and by that time Benjamin had roughly managed to stop her bleeding.

Gently cradling Margaret back into my arms, I looked at Benjamin and asked, "It’ll take at least half a day to get to the Palace from here, can she hold out that long? Tell me anything you need me to do, just say it!"

Benjamin took a sharp intake of breath, stood up, and grabbed the satellite phone from a nearby desk, looking me in the eye he said directly, "Half a day is just the driving time. If Your Majesty notifies the Holy Medical Pavilion at the Palace and asks them to send a helicopter for us, it would only take two hours!"

"We have already entered the Royal City’s territory, using military helicopters is not out of line, Windstorm Pack has its own mess to deal with, and they would hardly dare make an issue out of this! This way, we can save the time the Lycan Queen would lose on the road, and Elliot could receive treatment sooner!"

Benjamin handed me the satellite phone and continued, "Although I’ve managed to stop Her Majesty’s bleeding, she can’t sustain this condition for long. To prevent further complications, we have to race against time, Your Majesty!"

chapters.title: Chapter 263: Subvert the old order
chapters.content:
Chapter 263: Subvert the old order

[Donald’s POV]

Margaret being shot and wounded shattered much of my sanity, and I hadn’t even thought of such a solution beforehand!

I took the satellite phone from Benjamin’s hand, without the time to blame myself for the oversight, and started contacting the Palace’s military arsenal manager, also the officer in charge of military supplies, Charlie, while also issuing an urgent command to the Holy Medical Pavilion.

After I had finished all that, I breathed a slight sigh of relief and looked down at Margaret in my arms.

"The Lycan Queen and Elliot’s injuries are severe, warranting exceptional actions. I believe the council won’t trouble us over this, Your Majesty. You needn’t worry too much,"

Perhaps seeing my despair, Benjamin spoke up, attempting to console me.

I knew what Benjamin was worried about, but those things didn’t really concern me. All I was thinking about was Margaret’s injuries.

Leading the werewolf royal family, we stood as the most distinguished entity within the werewolf world, the guardians of every independent werewolf pack, enjoying the love and trust of all werewolves.

But this does not mean we can casually interfere with the internal order of their packs or conduct military activities within their territories without cause, unless it pertains to their own safety!

Of course, situations like Austin’s, which had already exceeded our normal procedures, were another matter entirely. As the Lycan King of the Royal Family, it was only right for me to investigate him.

In short, werewolf packs must obey the Royal Family, but the royal personnel must not abuse their power for personal gains. Our relationship isn’t strictly hierarchical but one of guardianship and fidelity.

This is precisely why Benjamin was so concerned about me openly using military equipment from the Palace at the border of the Windstorm Pack; it was a different matter from a defensive counterattack when faced with danger.

The council always had the duty to supervise me, so their reaction to such actions was perfectly normal!

However, I didn’t care about that at all; rules are rigid, people are not. Often, we need to be flexible in our actions.

Benjamin’s concerns were reasonable. I took a deep breath and said softly, "I’m not worried about such things. I’m just reflecting on the situation we encountered on the battlefield. Those rogues who had completed the shift, their fur and muscles could even block bullets. What do you think about that?"

"And Angel, she was severely injured by us in the Misty Forest just a few days ago. How could she have recovered so quickly? There must be something else going on that we don’t know about,"

I suddenly realized that all these things could be discovered through interrogating Angel, and temporarily put that matter aside in my mind.

Benjamin didn’t know what I was thinking and after a moment of contemplation, he replied, "Your Majesty, I witnessed all the situations you mentioned, and they are indeed very strange. Yet, if we link them to the previous research involving Blood Crystal Grass, wouldn’t it be a bit easier to understand?"

"Rogue wolves, in our usual understanding, are nothing more than vermin, impossible for anyone to recruit or use, unless there’s a reason compelling them to act accordingly..."

I thought of what Benjamin said, but observing his thoughtful expression, I followed his train of thought and asked, "Like what? What sort of lure would satisfy their greed and desires?"

"The answer is quite clear already. A robust physique that fears no bullets, and the overturning of the entire werewolf royal family, or rather, all the orders of the ’Old World’ that are adverse to them!"

"Considering MASTER’s usual way of doing things, the answer is self-evident. They are targeting you, Your Majesty. Could it be that they want to establish their own order?"

This shocking statement flowed from Benjamin’s mouth, startling even him!

I looked at him calmly and did not immediately endorse his words. I had thought through these things long before, and the situation was far more severe than Benjamin imagined. For instance, the sequential betrayals and changes of Angel and Austin, all attributable to MASTER’s great feats.

What I needed was not a guess but a theoretical basis supporting the inference, like Benjamin’s research on the unusually strong transformations of enemy werewolves, the discovery of the effects of Blood Crystal Grass!

"Your Majesty, don’t you find their methods incredibly bizarre?"

Seeing that I had yet to speak for a long while, Benjamin thought for a moment and then asked me again.

"I have already thought of what you mentioned before; I need more concrete evidence. Their biochemical research on werewolves is so advanced; shouldn’t we learn from it too?"

chapters.title: Chapter 264: Special case helicopter
chapters.content:
Chapter 264: Special case helicopter

[Donald’s POV]

I looked across at Benjamin and responded in a deep voice.

"After returning to the Palace today, I will hand over full responsibility for this research to the researchers of the Lycan Medical School. You have experienced all that has happened here, so you should assist them from the sidelines. I hope you can come to a result sooner rather than later."

This was a plan I had devised earlier but never had the chance to inform Benjamin about. Now, it naturally came out in conversation.

Upon hearing my words, Benjamin nodded respectfully at me without refusal.

I gave him a look and after a moment, added, "Having you handle so many tasks at once is indeed asking too much of you. But with Elliot gravely injured and both Christian and Eric not by my side, I have to rely on you even more. You are the only one I truly trust to do these jobs for me."

Benjamin clearly did not fully understand what I meant by my last comment, but he did not say another word. Instead, he knelt down respectfully and saluted, saying, "I am willing to give my heart for the Wolf royal family and Your Majesty."

"Get up, there’s no need for such formalities," I said warmly, and then turned my attention back to Margaret.

In the time it took for me to rush towards the Royal City with Margaret in tow, the rear forces led by Louis and Mark, from the Lycan Army, had swiftly cleaned up the battlefield, taking prisoners and quickly catching up to us.

The ’clean up the battlefield’ command I had given them was not as simple as it sounded. It was an order to kill all the rogues that had not yet fled, sparing only the lesser leaders and Angel, who were still useful for further interrogation, leaving no survivors!

I wanted the masterminds behind these people to know that the dignity of me and the Wolf royal family must not be challenged; those who crossed the line had only one fate – death.

Louis notified me the moment they set out to chase after us. I only ordered them to keep a close watch on Angel and return to the Palace as quickly as possible.

After about half an hour of travel, we encountered the belatedly arriving Royal Palace Patrol Team. They had received my secret order early in the morning to come and support our Guard Team, but they only managed to arrive after our confrontation with MASTER was already over.

Of course, I knew it was no one’s fault for this sudden development - if anyone was to blame, it was MASTER for his unpredictability and dubious intentions!

Apart from the patrolling team who had made a futile trip, a helicopter from the armory arrived soon after.

I disregarded the surprised looks from everyone present and quickly boarded the helicopter with Margaret in my arms. After Elliot was also brought on board with assistance, we set off to return directly!

The Lycans from the patrol team I had just met were left behind by my command. I was worried that the escorting of Angel might face some unforeseen changes, so I left them for Louis and Mark to deploy as needed.

About two hours into the flight, Benjamin and I arrived at the Palace successfully.

When the familiar scenery came into view, my gaze fell on several Lycans in white lab coats not far off; they were the doctors from the Holy Medical Pavilion.

At this point, I couldn’t be concerned with the other patient on the plane, Elliot, and had to leave him entirely in Benjamin’s hands. Meanwhile, I only focused on carrying Margaret in my arms, hastening with every step towards the doctors of the Holy Medical Pavilion!

"She’s been shot in the right shoulder, the bullet is lodged inside and can’t be removed. Bleeding has been stopped, but she requires immediate surgical intervention!"

As I spoke, I placed Margaret on the emergency bed they had prepared in advance, my expression tense as I watched them, as if I was about to etch the command to save Margaret’s life onto their foreheads!

Once they saw the wound on Margaret’s shoulder, they instantly understood the urgency of the situation. While pushing the emergency bed towards the indoors, they began asking me for details about Margaret’s condition.

I followed them step by step, giving a detailed account of everything concerning Margaret.

After Margaret was successfully rushed into the operating room, a familiar and kind voice behind me called me back to some sense. "Don’t worry too much, Donald. They are the best doctors from the Holy Medical Pavilion. They will find a way to save this girl!"

Licia was standing behind me, perhaps she had been since the beginning, her worried eyes looking at me by the operating room door, her kind and gentle face full of motherly love.

Snapped back to reality by this voice, I turned to look at the beautiful Licia, my nerves, which had been taut for hours, suddenly relaxed quite a bit, "I... I’m sorry, Mother, I didn’t want to worry you. I’m just too scared of losing Margaret!"

chapters.title: Chapter 265: Licia
chapters.content:
Chapter 265: Licia

[Donald’s POV]

"What did you say? The girl who was just rushed into the operating room is Margaret? The mate you met in the Silver Moon Pack?" Licia exclaimed, covering her mouth in utter astonishment as she looked at me.

I took a deep breath, trying to appear a bit more composed at this moment.

Knowing I couldn’t avoid Licia’s questioning, I looked up, met her gaze for a moment, and then nodded honestly.

"Donald! What on earth happened to you on your way back to the city? She... How did Margaret get so seriously injured? Are you hurt?" Licia had already taken a step forward and was carefully examining me.

The blood stains on the white shirt hidden under my coat all belonged to Margaret; I had no injuries on my body.

Licia noticed this too, and when she looked up at me again, her clear green eyes were already brimming with tears.

"All this blood is Margaret’s. She took a bullet for me on the battlefield to protect me," I explained, my voice filled with guilt and concern.

I rubbed my face, feeling a prickling in the corners of my eyes, "I didn’t anticipate Angel would suddenly go mad and shoot at us directly. Only when Margaret collapsed in my arms did I realize what had happened before!"

"I think Angel has truly lost her mind. That shot was clearly aimed at my heart! Mom, you don’t think anything will happen to Margaret, do you? If she really... If something bad happens to her, I don’t think I could ever forgive myself for the rest of my life! And I won’t let Angel get away with it."

Licia wasn’t actually aware of what we had been through today, but as she listened to my oral account, her expression had already become tense.

She looked at me for a while, turned her head to glance at the operating room, and finally gently patted the back of my hand, consoling me gently, "It will be alright. Didn’t you say Margaret was only injured in the shoulder? With so many excellent doctors in the Palace, there’s no doubt she will be fine. You should trust her."

As Licia spoke, she took a shallow breath, her gaze towards me deepening and clearing significantly. At that moment, my thoughts were consumed with Margaret, and I wasn’t quick to grasp the deeper meaning behind her gaze.

My mother, the Empress Dowager of the Wolf royal family—Licia—is a very gentle and kind person, but beyond that, her strong and broad character often comforts and empowers me.

She and my father, Alex, have loved each other dearly all their lives, serving as a rare model of mates within the entire Wolf royal family. I love them deeply.

My father, Alex, is a man of great loyalty. From him, I learned not only the wisdom and strategy a king should possess but also to always remain gentle and patient with the mate you love.

Licia used to say I resemble my father a lot, sometimes looking at me as if seeing through me to my father. I know she misses her beloved and of course, she loves me dearly.

After my father passed away due to illness, she became the person closest to me in this world. In her presence, I would involuntarily soften and relax a lot; she’s the woman who has seen me at my most vulnerable.

However, after I met my mate Margaret, there was another individual before whom I could truly let down my tough ’armor’.

They are both the most important people to me.

I had imagined Margaret’s first meeting with Licia would be more formal and warm—not as hasty and perilous as it has been now!

In fact, if it could have prevented Margaret from being injured this time, I would have given up the thought of catching Angel at all costs!

All I want is for Margaret to be safe.

But now that things have developed to this state, all my frustrations and regrets are futile. I can only pray that Margaret returns to me unharmed.

Time slowly passed as Licia and I anxiously waited. When Benjamin returned to the door of the operating room after having handled all the matters concerning Elliot, an hour had already passed.

"Your Majesty, the head of the Holy Medical Pavilion, Andrew, has taken over the follow-up treatment for Elliot. All our medical resources will be fully directed to Elliot, so you need not worry about him now," he reported.

Benjamin arrived with several people behind him. He first bowed respectfully to Licia, who was next to me, and then he reported the situation regarding Elliot to me.

I glanced at the few people with him, without acknowledging them, and simply responded, "I understand. Keep a close eye on Elliot’s situation, and inform me immediately of any developments or additional needs, understood?"

"Yes, Your Majesty," Benjamin replied respectfully.

chapters.title: Chapter 266: Pointing and gesturing
chapters.content:
Chapter 266: Pointing and gesturing

[Donald’s POV]

There was an antique clock hanging on the wall by the surgery room door. I glanced at it, then asked with a furrowed brow in a muted voice, "How are Louis and Mark doing? Has David’s patrol team met up with them yet?"

Hearing my question, a Lycan holding a folder beside Benjamin stepped forward, bowed his head, and replied gravely, "Your Majesty, Captain David has already met up with Louis and the others. It’s estimated that it will take another four hours to reach the Palace."

"Also, we just received a satellite call from Captain Christian. He said they are about to enter the territory of the Windstorm Pack, and if all goes well, they’ll be able to bring the prisoners back to the Palace by this time tomorrow."

After listening to the Lycan’s report, I nodded and responded, "The prisoners Christian is bringing back are of great value for interrogation and research. Once they arrive at the Palace tomorrow, you’ll be responsible for coordinating with the personal guard force to take the prisoners to the Lycan Medical School, where others will take over from you."

I paused, then looked at him and continued, "Why are the elders so quiet today? Usually, when I return to the Palace, aren’t they already swarming at me with all sorts of official business that needs approval? Where did you just come from, Clark?"

Upon my mention of the council of elders, a flicker of light flashed in Clark’s eyes, then he began to speak, "Your Majesty, during the time you were out investigating the attack, a lot of things actually happened in the Palace."

"Although the elders are all revered and respected, and have the duty to supervise and remind the Royal Family, they do not have the right to exercise royal powers. Many matters are beyond their authority."

"So..." Clark hesitated, seemingly deliberating how to report the next piece of information to me in a more appropriate and gentle manner.

Seeing Clark’s indecisive expression and frowning deeply, I said harshly, "So what?"

Startled by the sternness in my gaze, Clark’s body shook. He didn’t dare delay any longer, "So the elders approached Prince Henry and asked him to temporarily handle some important matters on your behalf, such as the recent collective petition from the ministers demanding that you promptly select a Lycan Queen!"

"What did you say?"

My eyes narrowed slightly, dangerously fixating on my Minister of Internal Affairs—Clark, "Are that bunch of old codgers still not giving up on meddling with the selection of the queen? Do they eat too much, living too leisurely?"

No one dared answer my rhetorical question delivered with an unexpected tone, not even Licia by my side, who just looked at me with worried eyes and didn’t say a word.

I let out a light snort, thought for a moment, then continued to question Clark, "So how did Henry handle them? It’s just like those old fools in the council to dump such petty and annoying matters on him."

Those old coots from the council, who were practically sly as foxes, knew all too well about my profound bond with Henry, so it seemed reasonable for Henry to step up and temporarily fend off these trivial issues for me.

Those old geezers had one point right, and so did Clark, my Minister of Internal Affairs. By sending him to ’open the way’, they intended to test my tolerance for the situation.

"Prince Henry initially handled it beautifully, but those ministers are more troublesome than the last! Pressed by necessity, in the end, Prince Henry had no choice but to place the young ladies recommended by them into the Star Moon Pavilion next to the Eastern Shine Palace. Today marks the third day!"

The Eastern Shine Palace is my main residence within the Palace complex, while the Star Moon Pavilion is a small loft next to it. It isn’t large, but it’s very close to the Eastern Shine Palace.

Henry must have been pushed into a corner, hence he came up with this solution that offended no one, leaving the matter for me to handle myself upon my return to the Palace.

After quickly understanding all the ins and outs, I was almost amused by my good brother’s antics. This wasn’t help—it was essentially throwing fuel on the fire!

I had already informed the council of elders about Margaret’s situation, and many of the Court’s ministers also knew. So Henry definitely knew about it—it was just that those people had annoyed him to the point of recklessness.

Just picturing him sitting in his wheelchair, miserably wrangling with the ministers, I could hardly keep from laughing. It was really hard on him.

With a resigned chuckle, I turned to Clark and retorted, "Where is he now? When you go down later, send him a message for me. I’ll wait for him at the Eastern Shine Palace tomorrow, tell him I have matters to discuss with him."

Clark nodded, indicating that he understood.

As I spoke, the expression in my eyes darkened slightly, and I looked at Benjamin, who had been rather quiet all along.

chapters.title: Chapter 267: The will of the king
chapters.content:
Chapter 267: The will of the king

[Donald’s POV]

"Additionally, you two will head to the Star Moon Pavilion with my decree right now! Command those people to leave immediately. My choice for the Lycan Queen is already quite certain, and I do not require others to worry about it for me. When Margaret fully recovers to a healthy state, that is when I will hold the coronation ceremony for her as Queen."

In the midst of my speaking, the door to the operating room was suddenly pulled open from the inside. My eyes brightened, and I stepped forward, continuing to speak without a pause, "If anyone dares to defy this decree, have them come to me directly."

"Yes, Your Majesty!"

"Yes, Your Majesty!"

As the voices of Benjamin and Clark echoed in unison, I had already begun to converse with the doctor standing at the door of the operating room, no longer continuing my conversation with them.

"How is Margaret doing? She’s not in any trouble, is she?"

The doctor, who had clearly just stepped away from the operating table, glanced at me respectfully before responding, "Your Majesty, the surgery went very smoothly! The emergency measures you took and the patient’s expedited transport to the hospital were all very timely. The surgery was a success, and as long as the recovery treatment proceeds normally, the patient should be able to be discharged within a month."

The doctor’s words allowed the heavy stone in my heart to gradually settle. I took a deep breath, only then realizing my legs had grown somewhat weak.

It turns out that from the moment I carried Margaret off the helicopter until now, I had been standing with tense nerves the entire time, no wonder my legs were weak.

"That’s good! Very good! It’s a relief she’s alright. I appreciate all your hard work, and I entrust the Queen’s subsequent recovery treatment to you as well." As I spoke, I exhaled deeply once more, joy of surviving a close call still apparent in the depths of my eyes.
