Benjamin and Clark also stood in place and listened to the news before departing. I paid them no attention, instead continuing to question the doctor about when Margaret could wake up and when I could go see her.

Licia had been standing by my side all along. Seeing the weight and gloom fade from my demeanor with just a few words from the doctor, she too couldn’t help but let out a sigh.

After observing me, she waved over several maidservants and quietly issued them a few instructions before turning to me with a gentle voice, "Now that there are temporarily no other concerns with Margaret, shouldn’t you also relax a little? Donald, you too have just been through a battle and need to rest properly."

"Let Dr. Ace take care of Margaret for now, and you go back to the Eastern Shine Palace to wash up and regroup," Licia said as she turned her head toward the doctor named Ace, her facial expression still warm and kind, "Notify His Majesty immediately of any signs of Margaret waking up, will you?"

Dr. Ace naturally wouldn’t refuse and promised respectfully.

I looked at my mother, who had already arranged everything, and after pondering for a moment, I nodded in agreement.

Dr. Ace had mentioned earlier that Margaret might not truly wake up until tomorrow. I was in a disheveled state, and there were several other orders I needed to give to my subordinates, so retreating to the palace to recuperate was indeed necessary.

But before returning, I needed to check on Margaret’s condition first!

After pondering for a moment, Dr. Ace nodded in agreement to my request and led me to the intensive careward to see Margaret.

...

I suppose the girl lying on the hospital bed, with her incredibly pale face and eyes closed as if she were asleep, had never suffered such severe injuries!

She did all this to protect me.

I reached out to gently straighten Margaret’s disheveled hair on her face, exhaled softly, and a tender smile played on my lips, "Promise me you’ll wake up soon, okay? Mother is still waiting outside to meet you, you two haven’t met before!"

As I spoke, I bent down to kiss Margaret’s forehead and continued in a soft tone, "If you don’t speak, I’ll take it as your agreement. I’m going back to the palace to attend to some matters and will come to see you later. I promise, when you open your eyes, I’ll be by your side."

The bright and tidy hospital room was very quiet, except for my muttering and the sounds of the machines monitoring Margaret’s vital signs. Margaret did not respond to me in any way.

I smiled, stood up, and left the hospital room.

Now that Margaret’s condition had stabilized, it was time to settle the score with certain individuals!

After all, I am not someone who can be deceived and bullied by just anyone. For those harboring malicious intentions, it was time to pay a price.

At a quarter past eight that evening, I summoned several executive ministers to the Eastern Shine Pavilion and issued several royal decrees that shocked them.

chapters.title: Chapter 268: Wake Up
chapters.content:
Chapter 268: Wake Up

[Margaret’s POV]

When I became aware of the sudden brightness in front of me, a surge of intense pain from my right shoulder and half of my body came crashing over me. I let out a stifled groan that was unbearable, but the sound and my breath only bumped against the oxygen mask on my face, failing to make much noise.

The fact that Donald could hear such a faint sound at all was actually quite remarkable.

My head felt heavy and foggy, and I was extremely sleepy, yet there was a voice above my head calling to me incessantly!

I was incredibly familiar with this voice; it was like an excellent painkiller or tranquilizer, inexplicably comforting me.

"Margaret? Baby, you’re finally awake! Don’t rush to move, the effect of the anesthesia in your body has just worn off, and your shoulder must be hurting a lot, right? I’ll call Ace over right away to give you a painkiller injection, just bear with it a bit longer."

Donald’s voice was filled with a mix of emotions—happiness, distress, and urgency—all of which rushed into my ears at once!

I squinted, trying to see him, but the morning light was just as piercing to my eyes at that moment. It took a good while before I could clearly make out the Lycan King in front of me.

"Are you... alright? Are you hurt anywhere?"

My voice must have been very weak, but I was sure Donald heard every word.

His hot breath and kisses landed first on my forehead and then gently on my eyelashes, as if I were the most precious treasure of his life. "I’m fine, baby, not injured. You... you little fool, I didn’t need you to take that bullet for me. You really scared me to death, baby!"

Donald uttered a heavy sigh, as if a massive stone in his heart had finally and truly hit the ground.

At that moment, my sluggish brain couldn’t support me in analyzing the love and tenderness behind Donald’s words.

Just as I was about to say something else to him, several doctors in white coats suddenly swarmed around my hospital bed. I figured they must have been the ones who snatched me back from death.

So, I really wasn’t dead. Angel had aimed that shot at Donald’s life, and I only suffered a minor injury. Thinking about it, it seemed that Donald and I had come out ahead.

Having come to this conclusion somewhat unexpectedly, I was hit by a wave of sleepiness welling up inside me, and my eyes slowly closed again.

I don’t know how much time had passed, but when I woke up again, Donald was still by my bedside, with a very beautiful and kind-looking lady sitting next to him.

Donald’s eyes, very similar to hers, were deep and bright, and looked incredibly beautiful. However, Donald had a more intimidating presence and depth, not quite as gentle as her.

"It looks like she’s awake, Donald," I heard her say softly.

Donald immediately turned his head toward me, his eyes twinkling with joy as he hurriedly asked, "Baby, are you awake? Do you want some water? How are you feeling now, is there any discomfort anywhere? Take your time, there’s no need to rush!"

Donald’s barrage of questions came at me like machine gun fire, leaving me somewhat overwhelmed. I stared at him blankly, not knowing which question to answer first!

Fortunately, the beautiful lady beside us couldn’t bear to see me confused for long. She seemed to pat the excited Donald, then turned to me with a gentle voice asking, "Your shoulder shouldn’t hurt as much now, right? The doctor just gave you pain medication. Do you want to drink some water or go to the bathroom?"

I looked at the lovely lady, and after a moment, responded with a hoarse voice, "I’d like some water, thank you."

At my words, she smiled slightly and said, "No need to thank me. Whatever you want to do or need, just tell me directly. Donald is a bit rough and might not be the best at taking care of the sick. This is his first time."

I could hear the teasing tone she had towards Donald, and while guessing her identity in my mind, I returned a light smile and turned to look at Donald again.

The description of him as ’rough’ was accurate; he seemed to have forgotten to introduce me to this lady!

Donald raised the hospital bed, fed me a bit of warm water, and asked me with tender eyes if I wanted more.

I shook my head, somewhat frustrated with his obtuseness at this moment; he didn’t seem to notice the curiosity and questions in my eyes. Indeed, it often seemed I had to fend for myself!

Glancing at the beautiful lady behind him, I smiled gently and said warmly, "Thank you for visiting me, Empress Dowager! I really didn’t expect our first meeting to be like this, I’m so sorry."

Seemingly not expecting a patient to be the first to break the ice, both Licia and Donald looked at me in surprise.

chapters.title: Chapter 269: Mother
chapters.content:
Chapter 269: Mother

[Margaret’s POV]

Donald seemed just as surprised as I was that I guessed correctly, a hint of astonishment in his eyes, before it turned into a look of happiness.

Licia’s eyes were brimming with laughter when she looked at me, but I didn’t miss the fleeting scrutiny deep within them. When it came to matters related to Donald, I was exceptionally sensitive, even in my current state of serious injury.

"How do you know who I am when you’ve only just woken up?" she asked.

With Donald’s help, I shifted my numb body and raised my eyes to meet Licia’s upon hearing her question.

It seemed she didn’t necessarily need my answer. After asking her question, she slowly curved her lips into a smile and continued, "Did Donald tell you about me? He rarely speaks of me in front of others. This child has always been so steady and mature since he was young."

"That’s not true! Donald holds a lot of love and respect for you in his heart."

I took the conversation from Licia with a gentle smile and looked toward Donald, "He often talks about you to me, saying you were the gentlest and most beautiful lady in the whole world. Now that I see you, he definitely wasn’t lying to me."

"Ha ha ha, you are so sweet with your words, I like you!" Licia seemed very satisfied with my response, her light, pleasant laughter filling the hospital room.

She glanced at Donald and, without pausing, added, "No wonder Donald has been so full of praise for you previously. It seems you truly are a likable girl."

"Alright, let me formally introduce myself! Hello, Margaret, my name is Licia, I’m Donald’s mother, and the Empress Dowager of the Wolf royal family."

Seeing the look in Licia’s eyes become formal, I also collected myself from my relaxed state and responded politely, "Hello, madam, I am Margaret. It’s a pleasure to meet you for the first time."

When I finished speaking, both Licia and Donald could hardly contain their smiles, particularly Donald, who had been silent but smiling the whole time.

"Baby, how can you be so adorable! Why are you still calling Licia ’madam’? You should call her ’Mom’ like I do," Donald said with a laugh.

At his words, I instinctively looked up at him and saw his teasing, mischievous eyes, slowly feeling a bit of playful indignation rising in my heart.

I feigned annoyance and glared at him lightly.

Seeing my reaction, Donald quickly surrendered, hastily apologizing, "Alright, alright, we won’t tease you anymore. You are just too cute! Once your injuries heal a bit, I’ll take you to the Eastern Shine Palace. It’s very close to where I usually work and rest, so I can see you all the time."

He paused for a moment, glanced at the smiling and silent Licia, then continued, "As for how you want to address Licia before the ceremony, that’s up to you; I respect your wishes. But after our ceremony is over, you can’t do that anymore!"

Suddenly hearing Donald mention some ceremony, my mind was hit with a huge question mark.

Of course, I didn’t intend to hide my confusion and directly asked, "What ceremony? What are you talking about, Donald?"

As if struck by the puzzled expression on my face, Donald simply looked at me with an indulgent smile, yet didn’t respond immediately.

Instead, it was Licia who had been quiet until now, playing the commentator, "Silly child, the ceremony Donald is talking about is, of course, your Lycan Queen coronation ceremony! That is, your wedding ceremony with him."

"We plan to bring over your relatives from the Silver Moon Pack while you’re recovering. Firstly, to let me and them meet, and secondly, to discuss all matters concerning your wedding with Donald."

"So, just focus on healing during this time. Donald and I will take care of these things. Of course, if you have any requests or suggestions, make sure to tell me directly and immediately; I’ll try to fulfill your needs!" Licia said, her smiling face uncovered, as she tenderly reassured me, "Also, I hope you start calling me ’Mom’ from now on. I’m looking forward to it!"

"Look, you first called me ’Empress Dowager’ and now ’madam’; why make things so distant and formal? That’s not good, hm? Just call me ’Mom’ like Donald, okay?"

Persuaded by Licia’s blend of gentle urging and firm insistence, how could I refuse to speak up!

I could only send a plea for help with my eyes to Donald beside me, but then I crashed into his encouraging, smiling gaze.

I: "..."

"I understand your thoughts, and I will contact my father and mother, as well as my sister and brother-in-law. Before they all arrive at the Palace, I’ll have to trouble you with all these arrangements, mother!" I looked into Licia’s eyes and obediently said.

chapters.title: Chapter 270: Prince
chapters.content:
Chapter 270: Prince

[Margaret’s POV]

Licia and Donald were both very satisfied with my amiable change in addressing Licia!

Especially Licia, the laughter in her eyes became exceedingly genuine as she nodded at me in approval, then turned around and rummaged through her purse to pull out an exquisitely delicate gift box, extending it towards me.

"This is a meeting gift I prepared for you. I hope you like it," she said with a smile.

Surprised, I slightly widened my eyes and, after a pause, quickly responded with thanks, "Thank you! Thank you so much, you are really thoughtful! I didn’t manage to prepare anything for you, I’m so sorry."

"My dear child, don’t ever say that, you have already done so much for Donald!" Licia shook her head, looking at me disapprovingly as she countered.

As she spoke, she glanced at my shoulder as if she wanted to see the wound through my patient gown.

"All I wish now is for Donald and you to always be safe and sound. These past few months you’ve been together have been so terrifying, I was extremely worried. But, thankfully, now that you both are back in the Palace, my heart has settled down."

After listening to Licia’s remarks, I looked at her apologetically, as we had indeed caused quite a few people who care deeply for us to be frightened for a long time.

But, just as Licia said, now that we were back in the Palace, things had truly become somewhat stable.

After chatting with Licia for a while longer, I couldn’t resist the exhaustion of my body and once again lay down.

Although our werewolf bodies are exceptionally strong compared to humans, modern cold weaponry still required a fair amount of time for us to recover.

As Donald and I dined in the evening, Licia wasn’t in the hospital room, but there was another mysterious and noble visitor who I had often heard mentioned by Donald.

"Everyone outside says you brought back a uniquely beautiful woman from the Silver Moon pack this time, making you send away all the nobles’ and ministers’ daughters from the Star Moon Pavilion..."

The handsome man wheeled in by the nurse spoke to Donald teasingly and with a smile.

Hearing someone talk, Donald and I both subconsciously turned our heads to look.

The young Lycan in the wheelchair seemed quite out of place to me, and I felt a growing sense of strangeness and curiosity.

The hospital staff in this private noble hospital all knew this was my room alone, and Donald had been with me continuously, so no one else would be allowed to barge in here. For him to be wheeled in by the nurse without being stopped or questioned by the guards at the door, his identity had to be either rich or noble!

In the Wolf royal family, the only Lycan who had suffered serious injuries and still couldn’t stand was indeed the Werewolf Prince—Henry!

I quickly confirmed his identity and then looked up at Donald, who was giving me water to drink, signaling him that he could put down the straw, as I could manage by myself.

Donald didn’t agree with my suggestion, his gentle eyes still on me, but he spoke to the person by the door, "How dare you bring up that matter? Don’t you see how much trouble you’ve caused me!"

"The threshold of the Eastern Shine Palace was nearly flattened yesterday and today by that bunch of old men! I don’t know why they have so much energy to spare every day."

Donald finally put down the things in his hand and turned his head towards Henry.

Between these distinguished cousins with great authority, they had their own unspoken ways of communicating and associating.

Donald was complaining about how a few days ago, Henry had crammed all those distinguished young ladies into his Star Moon Pavilion. Donald now seriously suspected Henry did it on purpose, possibly enjoying stirring up trouble!

About these incredibly frustrating matters, Donald naturally wouldn’t keep them from me; our relationship was open and secret-free.

And if he didn’t clarify these things to me in advance, I might indeed overthink them, or even get angry! Donald wasn’t so foolish, so he had mitigated the risk in advance.

As Donald spoke these words, Henry across from him lightly raised an eyebrow, seemingly unconcerned by this kind of biting comeback.

From the looks of it, Prince Henry had apparently taken command in crisis, shielding the Lycan king from this somewhat tricky situation, yet the Lycan king turned around and laid all the blame on the Prince...

Of course, these public bickerings were just one of the pleasures of jesting and arguing between the cousins.

At that moment, I didn’t understand all this; both Donald and Henry seemed a bit odd to me, neither appearing truly to be quarreling nor as if nothing had happened.

chapters.title: Chapter 271: Cousin?
chapters.content:
Chapter 271: Cousin?

[Margaret’s POV]

"Who knows? Haven’t that group of old folks always been like this?"

Henry agreed with Donald’s opinion, his expression calm and even carrying a somewhat cynical smile.

As he spoke, he shifted his gaze from Donald and turned his head to look at me.

I saw a flash of light in his eyes, but before I could comprehend what it signified, he had already begun to speak again.

"Is this beautiful young lady our future Lycan Queen? Your eyes are truly lovely! My name is Henry, Donald’s older cousin by half a month."

Hearing Henry’s compliment and his introduction, I smiled lightly and replied, "Hello, Prince Henry, I am Margaret."

As if unsatisfied with my introduction, Henry raised an eyebrow ever so slightly, looking at me with a teasing tone as he continued, "You can just call me by name, or cousin if you like; there’s no need to be so polite and restrained. I consider myself to be very easygoing, and you can ask Donald about that."

At his words, I nodded gently without saying anything, merely smiling gracefully at him.

After all, this was Henry’s and my first meeting, and in the presence of Donald, there was no need to be overly familiar or intimate—it would be rather strange.

Donald, perhaps not as sensitive and cautious as I am, first glanced at Henry and then humphed lightly, saying, "Margaret is simply being very polite. Do you think everyone is like you, always so overly familiar?"

As he spoke, the laughter in Donald’s eyes was becoming harder to contain, and he seemed to enjoy the relaxed banter with Henry.

"Also, this habit of yours, introducing yourself as my cousin to everyone we meet, when will you truly correct it? I was only born half a month later than you; that doesn’t make you my brother, and I won’t agree to Margaret calling you brother, ha!"

Sitting in his wheelchair, Henry’s face showed extreme helplessness, as if he were already very accustomed to someone’s petulant demeanor!

He smiled lightly, with a calm voice he continued, "Even if I were only older by one minute than you, I would still be your cousin, let alone half a month! That is an indisputable fact, and as a member of the exceedingly distinguished Royal Family and Lycan King, you can’t keep being stubborn and not acknowledging that."

With a smile, Henry looked at me, somewhat helplessly continuing his complaints, "He has been stubborn like this since he was a child. Getting him to call me brother is harder than reaching the heavens! Hahaha."

The Werewolf Prince’s hearty laughter echoed through my hospital room. I gave him a mild smile and then turned to look at Donald without saying much.

"Who’s being stubborn? It’s clearly you who’s always been unreasonable since our childhood, always wanting others to call you brother. What bizarre requests! Utterly childish!" Donald countered Henry’s statement with disapproval and stood up.

He walked over to Henry’s side, pushed him along in his wheelchair, and continued to chatter, "Today is your first meeting with Margaret. In the future, she and I might have many occasions to trouble you, so just make sure not to think about slacking off!"

With the last remark added, Donald then sat down next to Henry, appearing relaxed and pleased.

Upon hearing this, Henry sighed helplessly, glanced at Donald, and responded, "Look at what you’re saying! When have I ever been lazy? Don’t smear my image in front of our sister-in-law; I’m meeting her for the first time today. Leave me with some dignity, will you!"

"Also, with so many capable and astute subordinates by your side, why must you always focus on me? I’m just a leisurely and ineffective prince; can’t you truly let me be free? Right now, I truly don’t want to meddle in any affairs... except for eating, drinking, and having fun!"

Henry said this with a helpless look on his face, adding the last part suddenly.

It seemed Henry was hinting at something, and even I quickly realized what it was, let alone the always sharp and smart Donald.

Donald looked at Henry, the laughter in his eyes fading slightly, and after a long silence, he finally spoke, "Who has been filling your head with nonsense again? I’ve said it long ago, the grievances of the older generation have nothing to do with us! I don’t care what happened in the past; what matters is the bond we’ve had growing up together..."

"That’s always been my belief, and I hope you feel the same. Don’t let the opinions of others affect you. You will always be you, understand?"

chapters.title: Chapter 272: The real purpose
chapters.content:
Chapter 272: The real purpose

[Margaret’s POV]

The young and majestic Lycan King had a light of steadfast belief in his eyes that was truly piercing; I was deeply moved by such a scene and even more so for Henry as the other party involved.

He exchanged a glance with Donald, and there was something in his eyes that I couldn’t quite understand.

After a pause, Henry finally asked hoarsely, "What will you do with Angel next? She grew up with you, will you kill her directly to quell the anger of all the Lycan warriors in the Royal Family?"

I slightly widened my eyes, looking towards the startling Prince Henry.

From the moment Henry entered, he had been steering the conversation with Donald, and at this moment, his real purpose for this visit became truly clear!

His trip to the hospital today wasn’t to meet me, the future Lycan Queen, nor was it to reminisce with his old friend, Donald, but was aimed at Angel, who had been imprisoned in the royal dungeon since last night!

Henry bringing up Angel at this point must be intending to plead with Donald on her behalf.

I’m not quite clear on the stories between Donald, him, and Angel from a long time ago; I only know that some people might have changed over time, and clinging to old loyalties can sometimes be a very foolish act.

Perhaps Angel doesn’t deserve Henry and Donald going through all this trouble for her...

Of course. sentiments like these aren’t suitable for me to express at this time. Angel holds some very special feelings for Donald, and as her romantic rival, it isn’t appropriate to speak ill of her in front of her friend, Henry!

Even if she committed heinous crimes, a disgrace and a criminal to the entire Wolf royal family.

Henry cares deeply about his past relationship with Angel, and Donald cherishes Henry as a brother and good friend. Now that Prince Henry has come forward to intercede, it seems there might still be a slight chance for leniency.

I imagine the Werewolf clan behind Angel must have been very busy these last few days since the crime Angel committed was too grave to be easily dismissed.

Or perhaps, it could no longer be dismissed at all.

Now, it mainly depends on what Donald is thinking.

Considering all this, I gently looked towards the Lycan King, whose shoulder wound seemed to flare up in pain again at this moment!

The gun Angel had pointed at Donald and me was chillingly sinister.

After Henry finished his questions, the hospital room fell into a deathly silence for a moment. I licked my somewhat dry lips, took a few deep breaths, trying to ease the pain of the wound on my shoulder.

"So this is your real purpose for coming here today?" Donald furrowed his brows, looking at the equally solemn Henry, and asked in a stern tone.

Seeing that Henry remained silent and did not answer, Donald scoffed mockingly, then slowly shifted his gaze away, and continued, "I know what you’re thinking, but the severity of this matter isn’t as simple as you think! I have to give everyone a fair explanation and cannot possibly be lenient on her..."

"I wasn’t planning on letting her off either! She can’t expect to get away with all the evil she has done; I will make sure she pays the proper price," Donald said.

As he spoke, the mocking expression on his face had completely faded, leaving only seriousness, "So you can go back and directly inform Angel’s family that I have handed over this matter entirely to the council of elders, who will be responsible for judging all of Angel’s crimes, with no room for negotiation or personal favors!"

Hearing this, Henry seemed to want to say something more, but seeing the uncompromising expression on Donald’s face, he ultimately fell silent, aware that he had undertaken a thankless task!

After speaking, Donald turned to look at him again, the corners of his mouth slightly curled, but without any genuine smile, "In fact, they should feel fortunate that I don’t currently intend to settle scores with Angel’s family power behind her..."

"So before I find it necessary to settle this matter, let them all behave and stop any scheming, understood?"

Henry raised his eyes to meet Donald’s, his expression looking almost calm, but it was impossible to discern what he was really thinking.

I silently watched all of this unfold, wondering whether to speak up at this moment to ease the tension in the atmosphere; after all, the atmosphere between Donald and Henry was incredibly stiff, quite intimidating to witness.

But before I could decide whether to speak or not, the Werewolf Prince had already taken a step back, bowing to the Lycan King!

chapters.title: Chapter 273: Protect the short.
chapters.content:
Chapter 273: Protect the short.

[Margaret’s POV]

"I told you earlier that you wouldn’t agree, but Aunt Judy just knelt on the ground and wouldn’t get up, forcing me to make this trip to plead with you on Angel’s behalf..."

Henry said with a self-mocking laugh, his eyes on Donald conveying helplessness and guilt. He continued, "I’m sorry, Donald, I perhaps shouldn’t have come! I should have considered this matter from your perspective... Don’t be mad, just pretend I wasn’t here tonight, I’ll leave now."

The hospital room was very quiet, Henry’s words falling to the ground like dead weight.

I glanced at Donald’s somewhat indifferent expression, and when I turned away, I couldn’t help but quietly say, "But you are already here..."

He paused for a moment, then turned his face to look at me.

"You didn’t go to the Eastern Shine Palace during the day to find Donald, which means you know very well that this matter should not be addressed publicly as official business, but rather in a more intimate, private capacity! You and Angel have had a close relationship since you were children, but what about you and Donald, don’t you share a bond?"

I spoke softly and sighed lightly, feeling sorry for my mate who valued emotion so deeply, "You only see the plight of Angel in this situation, but have you thought about the pain of Donald being betrayed by his close friend? Clearly, he is the most helpless and innocent party here; why do you always think he is being unreasonable and ungrateful?"

Henry’s lips twitched slightly as if he wanted to add something.

I glanced at him and cut him off directly, "Don’t tell me how you couldn’t bear to refuse the eager requests of the elders! This matter isn’t supposed to be discussed like this."

"If betraying the Lycan King and the pack can be easily forgiven, then how will the dignity and beliefs of the Lycans be reestablished in the future? Would this country not become completely disordered?"

"Sure, profound friendship is very important, but aren’t national justice and tribal faith even more crucial? As a Prince of the Wolf Royal Family, you should be clearer on this point!"

"I’m not afraid to offend you, Your Highness, but I’ll speak bluntly—you really shouldn’t have come today! By doing so, you’ve been quite unfair to Donald, and somewhat disappointing..."

"I can see that Donald truly cherishes having you as a brother and a friend! You shouldn’t judge him from the perspective of an outsider. There’s no need for more explanations; you being here today already shows that your inner balance has silently shifted towards favoring Angel!"

The things I said were indeed some harsh realities.

I could have chosen to sugarcoat things and say something soothing to ease the tension between the brothers.

But that would be unfair to Donald. I didn’t want him to keep feeling upset while still having to excuse Henry’s appearance today and those biased and unfair emotions!"

So I chose to strike directly, shattering Henry’s seemingly helpless apology, and laid the bare facts clearly in front of all of us.

Only in this way could I truly honor Donald’s sincerity towards him.

"I..."

Henry probably didn’t expect me to be so direct and harsh, and several times he tried to say something but I bluntly pushed it back, eventually leaving him stuttering a few ’I’s.

Donald seemed quite pleased with my fiercely defensive attitude, a hint of a smile and comfort in his eyes, yet his face remained utterly impassive, looking very cold.

"Sorry, Donald, I..."

"You better go back."

About 2 minutes later, Henry’s apology and Donald’s dismissive words collided, and the atmosphere in the hospital room became tense again.

Listening to Donald’s cold voice, Henry’s expression stiffened, and he took a sharp breath before finding his voice again, "Maybe Margaret is right, I was too one-sided about today’s matter."

"I need to apologize to you, Donald! You can be upset about this; I took things for granted and didn’t really consider your feelings, I’m sorry..."

"I’ll go back now, and once you’ve cooled down and decided to forgive today’s brash mistake, I’ll gradually show my face to you again." Henry said, deeply looking at Donald, an expression of deep remorse on his face.

Gazing at his sincerely remorseful eyes, along with this proactive attitude of admitting his error, I suddenly thought this Lycan wasn’t so bad after all, definitely not arrogant or disrespectful!

I gave a soft sigh, stretched out a finger to scratch the palm of Donald’s hand, signaling him to let it slide, to not make the situation too stiff to handle later.

Donald was naturally not one to sweat the small stuff; he first grasped my mischievous fingers, then exhaled and spoke in a deep voice, "I’ll pretend you weren’t here tonight, and don’t bring up Angel in front of me again, or watch out—I might lose my temper!"

chapters.title: Chapter 274: Perfect Monarch
chapters.content:
Chapter 274: Perfect Monarch

[Margaret’s POV]

Although Donald’s tone wasn’t particularly polite when he said these words, I just knew that he had no intention of holding a grudge over Henry’s matter anymore.

Donald wasn’t a petty person; the unpleasantness of the night had been resolved, thanks to Henry’s timely apology.

When Henry left the hospital alone, he seemed in relatively good spirits.

I watched the hospital room door gently close and looked up at Donald standing beside me.

The young and handsome Lycan King didn’t seem to be affected by the recent events. After adjusting the blanket over me, he slowly sat down in a nearby chair.

After a moment of thought, I asked, "Are you okay, my dear? Don’t think about those things that upset you anymore, okay? Let’s talk about our own matters!"

Upon hearing my words, Donald raised his beautiful and clear eyes, looking at me tenderly and slyly, "Do I look that unhappy to you?"

I was slightly startled and didn’t immediately grasp what he meant by his question.

However, Donald didn’t really need my answer; he looked at me and let out a soft chuckle, a lot of warmth and sweetness appearing on his face, and his voice grew warmer, "The feelings and matters between Henry and myself are quite complicated, not something that can be explained in a short time. So you don’t have to worry about them; I will take care of everything."

"Angel will no longer obstruct anything between us, I can swear to you right now! The Elders may be annoying and noisy, but they will still handle things fairly and give everyone a just resolution. No one can interfere! Neither Angel’s family behind her nor Henry can manage to..."

As Donald spoke, he gently took hold of my hand, and a look of guilt and compassion filled his eyes. He paused before continuing, "In the matter of punishing Angel, the only one with the power to decide her fate is me! I have the authority to give Angel a chance at life, but I don’t wish to do so."

"In fact, I can’t wait to kill Angel immediately! Only by doing so can I truly alleviate some of the hatred in my heart for her and the guilt towards you. My dearest Margaret, you were always the most innocent in all of these matters, Angel should not have treated you that way!"

Donald’s emotion was slightly agitated as he said these words. His grip on my hand was strong, causing my fingers to start hurting, but I ignored these details, just gently sighing as I looked into his eyes.

I was certainly aware of my innocence in these matters, but I didn’t feel that taking a bullet for Donald was an injustice that required him to kill Angel recklessly to repay or avenge me!

I was just doing my utmost to protect the mate I loved. Beyond that, there was nothing more.

I deeply loved Donald and wished for his lifelong safety and joy. I hoped to be with him forever, and beyond that, I desired nothing more.

Thinking of these things, my eyes began to moisten. Leaning against the pillow, I looked up at him with a smile and reassured him, "The crime Angel committed isn’t just about trying to kill me. In this matter, what you need more is to provide a just resolution to Elliot, as well as to those Lycans who lost their precious lives in the war due to Angel’s last-minute betrayal."

"Avenge me is just incidental to your duties. Please don’t let hatred blind your judgment, okay? I know you are a flawless ruler, and you can easily do this, right?"

Perhaps it was my too-gentle tone of speech at the moment, but Donald’s previously indignant mood quickly calmed down.

He let out a soft sigh, his grip that restrained my hand unconsciously loosened, and he bowed his head to tenderly kiss the palm of my hand, yet he remained silent.

After a while, he finally spoke, "I know what to do now, I am, of course, your impeccable mate!"

With his lips curving into a smile that spread across his face, he looked at me and continued, "What would I become without you by my side? Would I go insane and kill everyone who ever hurt you? That would be too terrible, so please don’t ever leave me, Margaret. And don’t get hurt anymore!"

Seeing Donald calmed down, I let out a sigh of relief, nodded, and said, "I won’t leave you, Donald. We will be together forever, living a happy and perfect life."

chapters.title: Chapter 275: Brother and sister affection is deep
chapters.content:
Chapter 275: Brother and sister affection is deep

[Donald’s POV]

I was very satisfied with Margaret’s answer, and I bent down to kiss her somewhat pale cheek, smiling softly as I cooed, "I love you, Margaret. Get some sleep, baby, you need plenty of rest right now."

Margaret had no objections to my suggestion and nodded her head slightly before slowly closing her eyes.

I think she must have been feeling tired already, only she didn’t show any signs of it earlier because Henry had been here the whole time.

Actually, seeing Margaret just now arguing her point with Henry made me very happy!

As the most prestigious king of the Wolf royal family, I am burdened with too many responsibilities and obligations, and although the people around me respect and fear me, very few love me with such attentive detail!

Even Licia rarely protects me unconditionally and childishly.

In Margaret’s eyes, I seem to be a child who needs a lot of protection. It’s a strange and rare feeling, but unexpectedly not displeasing, and I even like this feeling of being protected unconditionally!

It appears this is how Margaret shows her love, and I believe I have discovered a tremendous treasure for myself.

In the quiet and warm environment of the hospital room, Margaret soon fell asleep. Listening to her long, gentle breathing, I bent down again to kiss her beautiful brow, and then I got up and left the room.

I gently closed the door behind me and glancing to the side, I saw several Lycan guards poised in the hallway. I said in a deep, slow voice, "Apart from Dr. Ace and myself, no one is allowed to enter this room alone. If anything happens, notify me immediately, and I will hurry back."

"Yes, Your Majesty!"

"Yes, Your Majesty!"

Hearing the response of the Lycan guards, I nodded my head and again repeated the instruction ’Keep a good watch here,’ before striding out of the hospital.

At 9:15 pm, Werewolf Palace, Eastern Shine Hall.

Although it was already quite late, the hall was still brightly lit, which indirectly indicated that tonight would not be peaceful.

Clark, dressed in a cerulean uniform, opened the doors of the hall for me, and I walked in with large steps, listening to Clark’s brief report while scanning with my eyes the Lycan standing in the center of the hall.

"Austin was brought to the Royal City at noon today and has been clamoring to see you in the hospital but both Christian and I stopped him. Since then, he has been standing in the hall, refusing to go downstairs or speak with anyone. This caused the Empress Dowager and the council of elders to come over!"

Hearing the mention of a person who should not be present in such situations, I looked down at Clark and asked in a quiet voice, "Why is the Empress Dowager here? Where is she?"

Clark visibly inhaled sharply at my question, his expression difficult and unpleasant as he quickly reported, "Because Alpha Austin was not brought here by the city patrol team alone, his sister—Lillian—was with him!"

"After arriving at the Royal City, Miss Lillian first tried to find out the news about you and the Lycan Queen through many people. Failing that, she then turned to the Empress Dowager... Your Majesty, you know the Empress Dowager has always been quite fond of Miss Lillian."

"So, that’s why the Empress Dowager is also present here now, is it?" I cut off Clark’s next words and looked towards the people in the hall.

Clark fell silent then, merely bowing his head and leading the way up front.

Although my conversation with Clark was not loud, it immediately drew the attention of everyone in the hall.

"Donald! You’ve finally shown up! Do you know how long I’ve been looking for you today? Why did you let David bring my brother back to the palace? Do you know what people in the Royal City and our pack are saying about my brother? If you can’t give me a reasonable explanation today, I won’t easily forgive you!"

The first to attempt to blame me was none other than Lillian, whose expressions were extremely anxious and her tone unusually agitated.

Her emotions were complex, and when her gaze fell on me, it seemed as though she had gone through a lot of struggle and deliberation.

I didn’t particularly care what Lillian thought, I didn’t even deign to look at her directly, but rather looked at the center of the hall where Alpha Austin stood, silent and meeting my gaze.

"Your Majesty!"

"Good evening, Your Majesty!"

Voices gradually rose around the hall as others greeted me, and I nodded slightly, indicating that everyone need not be overly formal.

Licia, being highly esteemed, was the only one in the hall who could remain seated to greet me.

Simpson then looked at me with a serious face before bowing slightly as a sign of respect.

I was somewhat helpless toward this old fellow who loved to join in the hustle and bustle, but considering his special status as an elder statesman from two generations, I decided to give him another chance...

chapters.title: Chapter 276: When questioning in court
chapters.content:
Chapter 276: When questioning in court

[Donald’s POV]

Let today’s special interrogation be graced by those old timers representing the elders.

Having made such a decision lightly in my heart, I chuckled softly, ignoring the gazes of everyone as I turned and took my seat on the throne at the top of the hall, my eyes casting a darkened look over all present, including Austin.

"I thought you should at least answer Lillian’s question first instead of looking at us so coldly and distantly," Austin said in a calm tone as he received my top-down gaze.

Upon hearing Austin’s words, Lillian glanced at him, then turned her gaze back to me, "That’s right, Donald, you should at least answer my question. Have I offended you in any way?"

"Or is it the matter with Margaret before? Have you still not forgiven me? I did apologize to you already, what gives you the right to be so relentless? Say something!"

Lillian’s tone was verging on collapse when she spoke. I glanced at her sidelong, slightly knitted my brows, and coldly said, "I believe I’ve reminded you more than once, do not address me by my name, Lillian. You should call me Your Majesty or Lycan King!"

Resting back into the throne, I looked toward Lillian and Austin and continued, "Start off by demanding I answer your questions? Do you two think you’re mistaken because it’s I who am interrogating you siblings! What gives you such entitlement?"

Interrogating an Alpha of the Wolf royal family in court is a grave matter, so the expressions of those below had all grown solemn.

Especially so for the sole person involved in the affair, the expression on Alpha Austin’s face was truly a sight to behold!

"’Interrogation’? Are you implying you wish to interrogate me, Your Majesty?" Austin asked, seemingly perplexed, after a slight cold chuckle, he questioned me in return.

At his words, I just furrowed my brows and looked at him, remaining silent.

Austin didn’t seem the least embarrassed, instead, he continued to ask with a puzzled look, "May I ask why, Your Majesty? What heinous crime have I committed that would lead you to interrogate me in court without any prior investigation or questioning?"

"After all the years of our acquaintance, is this to be my end? I refuse to accept this treatment of a Wolf royal family Alpha by you; my pack won’t allow it either! I demand a reasonable explanation!"

Listening to Austin’s high-sounding sophistry, the corners of my mouth slowly formed a sarcastic smile.

Looking straight into Austin’s eyes, I chuckled and counter-questioned, "So in your eyes, Austin, I, Donald, am one who disregards all factual evidence and wields royal authority only to satisfy my own desires? Is that the sort of person I am?"

"Or do you believe that I could never uncover those filthy acts you’ve committed in secret? Could you be overconfident?"

When I finished speaking, the expression on Austin’s face hadn’t changed, only a subtle dimness appeared in his eyes, hardly noticeable.

Lillian was completely bewildered at this time, seeming not to understand any of the riddles spoken between me and Austin. She looked at Austin beside her, then raised her eyes to me on the throne, and asked in utter confusion, "What are you two talking about? Why don’t I understand a word?"

"Donald... Ah, no, Your Majesty, is there perhaps some misunderstanding between you and my brother? Didn’t we all sit together in harmony just yesterday? Less than a day has passed, so why has it come to this? What on earth has happened, can’t one of you tell me?"

Lillian was fraught with impatience in her tone. I glanced at her sideways, with little interest in explaining to her.

She would do best to always remain uninvolved and better still, forever ignorant; otherwise, I cannot guarantee my continued patience with her.

Austin was unlike me, unable to maintain his composure; he was always incredibly protective of his beautiful and delicate sister, a true brother-obsessed maniac. So, in the end, he couldn’t help but interrupt Lillian, attempting to alleviate her anxiety and prevent her collapse!

"You shouldn’t have come with me to the Royal City today; this is no longer your concern! Now, leave this place and wait outside!"

As he spoke, he seemed to survey all those present, his gaze ultimately settling on Licia, the woman of highest status in the room apart from me, the Lycan King.

"The Empress Dowager has always been fond of you; she won’t allow His Majesty to mess with a clueless girl like you. Just wait outside, we should be finished shortly!"

chapters.title: Chapter 277: Ironclad Proof
chapters.content:
Chapter 277: Ironclad Proof

[Donald’s POV]

Austin’s eyes were on Licia, but his words were directed at Lillian.

Lillian hadn’t expected Austin to still be trying to drive her away and sharply refused, "I won’t! Why do you still want to push me out now? I’m your only sister, and I won’t let anyone hurt you on purpose!"

"Even if he is the most revered being in the entire werewolf world, that’s not acceptable! We need a reasonable and lawful explanation and investigation, not just his personal say-so!"

Lillian, growing increasingly defiant as she spoke, took a step forward in her high heels, tilting her head up to challenge me, "What evidence do you have that my brother did something wrong? Bring out your evidence now, and we can confront it right here on the spot!"

"You and my brother grew up together; I want to see if the friendship you’ve shared over so many years is going to be completely exhausted today. Is it really worth it for some gossip?"

I looked at Lillian’s stubborn face with a light smile, my fingers caressing the beautiful gems on the throne, and said, "It seems there are quite a few people who want to talk about personal ties today... But I’m not fond of watching soppy dramas about deep sibling bonds. Austin is right about one thing, you can leave and wait outside."

"After all, this matter doesn’t concern you that much, and if you insist on interfering, it will only harm your brother. Whether you take my advice or not is up to you! But if you insist on staying, then you’d better keep your mouth shut. Today’s matter is between Austin and me, understand?"

After I finished speaking, I paid no more attention to the indignant Lillian, but snapped my fingers at Clark, who had been silent, signaling that the final act could officially start.

Clark bowed respectfully to me and walked down to the people holding some papers, distributing these documents while explaining in detail, "These documents are a summary of the weapons, equipment, and various daily necessities that Austin purchased privately a few months ago. The lords may examine them closely; if there are any questions, you can come directly to me. I’ll be responsible for providing explanations, as well as any doubts and misunderstandings everyone may have regarding His Majesty’s on-the-spot interrogation of the Alpha of the Windstorm Pack."

"Our arrest and interrogation of Austin are in line with the rules and regulations of the Wolf royal family, and there is no such thing as what Miss Lillian and Austin mentioned earlier—mistreating them without any verification or inquiry. This is the official warrant for Austin’s arrest and the Royal Family’s detention order!"

Licia and Simpson were slightly stunned at first, then raised their hands to take the papers from Clark and began to read with frowns.

Lillian almost snatched the document in front of her, skimming it rapidly, then looked at Austin with eyes full of shock and confusion, "No! This is impossible! My brother wouldn’t do such defiant acts; there must be a mistake, it can’t be!" she retorted aggressively, her fingers clenching the documents until they nearly turned white.

I glanced at her, stood up from the throne, and gesturing toward Austin, suggested softly, "Your brother is right there, why don’t you ask him first? Whether he’s done what’s mentioned here and why he would do such things—is he dissatisfied with the Wolf royal family or with me?"

With my words, Lillian’s entire body seemed to freeze, her head turning instinctively to look at Austin, her eyes rapidly reddening, filled with many complex emotions.

’Why? Is this true?’ Lillian’s eyes asked Austin this.

Clearly, even someone as unsophisticated as Lillian knew that the actions Austin had taken were extremely unusual and could easily be deemed as rebellion and defiance!

For me to simply present such incriminating evidence and interrogate him quietly in the dead of night was already exceedingly merciful and considerate.

According to the traditions of the old Lycans, for a Lycan with malicious intentions like Austin, dying thousands of times over would have been perfectly normal.

The status and dignity of the Wolf royal family do not permit any threats or provocations.

"Brother... you! Speak up, refute them! You didn’t do these things, right?" Lillian, hardly able to believe what was unfolding, pleaded with Austin.

Licia, unable to bear Lillian’s hysterics and distress, casually closed the document in front of her and, furrowing her brow, looked toward Lillian...

chapters.title: Chapter 278: Donald VS Austin
chapters.content:
Chapter 278: Donald VS Austin

[Donald’s POV]

"Enough, Lillian! Today’s affairs are clearly beyond our involvement. Leave with me now. His Majesty and the ministers will handle everything."

Though Licia spoke with a hint of reluctance, she left no room for negotiation for Lillian.

As she stood up, she looked at Lillian, whose face was wet with tears, "If Austin were truly innocent, he wouldn’t have been so eager to push you towards me just now. He must be well aware that if these matters came to light, I would be the only one able to save you from Donald’s hands..."

"You should appreciate his well-intentioned efforts," Licia said emotionlessly.

She seemed to despise Austin, who had remained silent the whole time in the hall, and after casting a contemptuous glance at him, she then stepped out of the Eastern Shine Palace.

Her maids followed, seizing Lillian, and despite Lillian’s distraught struggles, took her directly out of this place of dispute.

I bet Licia must be infuriated with Austin by then!

Not only had Austin deceived and betrayed the entire Wolf royal family, but he had also taken advantage of the Empress Dowager Licia’s final sliver of mercy for Lillian to save his most important sister in the nick of time.

Austin’s plan was clever indeed, utilizing all of Licia’s reactions to the utmost.

I narrowed my eyes, casting a dangerous look at Austin below. I accepted Licia’s decision to take Lillian away, after all, Lillian was indeed unaware of all her brother’s schemes.

In this regard, Austin truly hadn’t failed to consider Lillian’s safety.

In some cases, not knowing the details is far safer than knowing!

"You’ve achieved your final goal, so isn’t it time to come clean about everything to me?" I asked Austin.

Upon hearing this, Austin slowly lifted his head to look at me, his face taking on a somewhat morose and ominous smile, as he gravely said, "I thought it would be at least another two months before you’d discover all that I have done, but unexpectedly..."

He suddenly paused, the smile on his mouth fading slightly, and continued in an unfriendly tone, "I underestimated your strength. Had you not been so obstinate, I believe we could have continued to be friends, at least for the last two months, just like we were yesterday in the Windstorm Pack."

I knew what Austin swallowed after ’unexpectedly’: he hadn’t anticipated that I would suspect him so soon, nor that I would act so swiftly in searching for evidence and arresting him, and he hadn’t anticipated my ruthless change of face!

"Enough talk! You know that’s not what I want to hear."

Seeing Austin so talkative sparked my anger, but my face remained as cold as the ice of the Arctic, "Two months? Besides collecting firearms and materials with your accomplices, what other schemes have you planned? You better tell me everything now, or I can’t guarantee Lillian’s continued safety in the Royal City!"

I knew what Austin cherished most, and I had no intention of being a pointless good guy.

"You... You just promised the Empress Dowager that you would spare Lillian, why?" Stabbed where it hurt by my sharp words, Austin couldn’t maintain his composure or his sullen demeanor, looking anxiously at me.

I stepped forward, standing in front of him, and retorted with a sneer, "What promise? I merely accepted that Licia took Lillian away but never said she’s completely out of the woods! I am no saint of moral perfection; you’ve always known that, haven’t you?"

As I questioned, I pretended to sigh lightly, watching Austin’s changing expressions with amusement, and continued, "So, drop any unrealistic hopes. The moment you stepped into the Royal City today, you should have known there’s no turning back! Unless..."

I paused, patted Austin on the shoulder mockingly, and added, "Unless your accomplices have the gift of foresight, because today you’re totally in my hands."

Yes! I met with many ministers overnight in the Eastern Shine Palace just so I could smoothly take down Austin today and prevent leaks of his arrest from sabotaging my long-term plans.

The reality proved that all my planning was meticulous; the only thing I overlooked was Austin using Licia to save Lillian.

But that wasn’t an issue hard to solve; after all, Licia would ultimately stand by me.

Following my words, Austin didn’t respond immediately; he seemed entirely stunned.

chapters.title: Chapter 279: Attack, poison
chapters.content:
Chapter 279: Attack, poison

[Donald’s POV]

It had been a long time before the deadly silence in the hall had started to dissipate somewhat.

Having grilled Austin into silence, he finally spoke in resignation, "Donald, you’re still as clever as we were when we were children."

As he spoke, the expression in his eyes suddenly changed, and I was certain that was a very fierce intent to kill!

’Austin wants to kill me.’ This thought swept through my mind, and somewhat incredulously, I glanced at him and instinctively turned sideways, narrowly dodging his sharp claws.

"Your Majesty, be careful!"

"Your Majesty!"

"Someone, protect His Majesty!"

As Austin’s attack motion startled everyone, several cries for help and shouts of alarm burst out in the hall. Clark and Simpson, who were closest to me, immediately moved forward to shield me.

I snapped back to my senses and disdainfully looked at Austin, who had shown a fierce color, pushed the two in front of me aside, and grappled with Austin!

"You could have been faster and more ruthless. Why didn’t you just crush my throat?" I swung my fist and knocked off Austin’s head aside, coldly asking.

Austin swallowed the blood in his mouth, looked up at me with a fierce gaze, and mockingly responded, "Do you think I didn’t want to? I just couldn’t..."

"What does that mean?" I asked reflexively, grabbing Austin by the neck, with a bloodthirsty emotion flashing in my eyes.

Naturally, Austin would not kindly answer my question.

He just looked at me with a defiant and hateful expression, then bit into a small pill he had previously hidden in his mouth, revealing his bloodied wolf teeth and said resentfully and angrily, "Donald! Your arrogant and foolish reign will be over soon. I will always be waiting for you below... I’ll wait for you to join us, you won’t be smug forever! Ah..."

As Austin spoke, black blood sprayed from his mouth and nostrils. Clark, horrified, quickly grabbed my hand and urgently warned, "He’s poisoned, Your Majesty! Let him go quickly, be careful the poison enters your skin!"

I didn’t really heed Clark’s warnings, just staring intensely at the miserable Austin before me.

I never expected Austin, to protect that mysterious and sinister ally behind him, would even disregard his and his sister’s lives!

What vast conspiracy lies behind all this? This question lingered in my mind, and I refused to let go of Austin’s neck for a long time.

Until he slowly closed his eyes and stopped all breathing.

Seeing the mocking and disdainful smile on Austin’s lips, my heart shook tremendously, and beyond that was an unrelenting fury!

"Austin!" I roared his name as if I wanted to devour him alive.

"Your Majesty! Please calm down!"

"Your Majesty... he’s already dead! Can you let go of him now?"

"Austin suddenly died by poisoning in the Eastern Shine Palace. How are we going to explain this to the Windstorm Pack wolves? Wouldn’t they revolt immediately? Where are all the weapons and supplies they collected earlier? Your Majesty, you need to make plans and decisions quickly!"

"Yes, Your Majesty! We can’t keep being passive, we need to strike first!"

Although I found Clark and the ministers’ voices incredibly noisy, they were somewhat useful!

I calmed down amid their exchanges, letting go of Austin’s neck, and after a few heavy breaths, I turned to Simpson, "You are entirely right, the esteemed Inspector Simpson! But that’s not the most important issue right now."

Pointing at Austin’s body on the floor, I instructed Clark, "Notify Benjamin and the people from the Holy Medical Pavilion, I want them to confirm that all signs of life in Austin have indeed ceased completely!"

"Also, declare to all wolves of the Windstorm Pack that Austin died because he failed to manage the border territories effectively, which led to the Guard Team being ambushed by unknown forces, and that he was brought back to the Royal City for questioning by the patrol team. Put the incident of Angel’s surprise attack on the Guard Team temporarily on his head!"

After entrusting this task, I scanned all the people around me and continued to order coldly, "Everyone present today, listen to the order, I do not wish for anyone else to know about this! Violators will be killed! Do you all understand?"

As if intimidated by the imposing presence I projected, the dozen ministers stiffened, and then, as if suddenly realizing, they knelt on one knee in unison, performing a grand salute and respectfully replied, "Yes, Your Majesty! We are willing to offer our hearts to the Wolf royal family."

chapters.title: Chapter 280: Andrew, the old dean
chapters.content:
Chapter 280: Andrew, the old dean

[Donald’s POV]

I nodded lightly upon hearing this, glanced down at Austin on the ground, and silently returned to the throne.

It was only after all the Lycans in the great hall had dispersed that Clark, along with Benjamin and the dean of the Holy Medical Pavilion—Andrew, returned to the hall, accompanied by Christian who had just rushed back to the Palace not long before.

During their journey, they must have heard Clark’s brief account of the events, so they immediately began their work on examining the body!

Benjamin was well aware of why I had done this. Along the way, we had been frightened by the unceasing appearance of strange potions from ’MASTER.’ Who could guarantee that Austin’s act today wasn’t schemed by ’MASTER’ well in advance?

I couldn’t risk the possibility that it wasn’t, so I had to be cautious at all times.

About 5 minutes later, Andrew was the first to lift his head. He first looked at Benjamin next to him, who was still focused, and then turned to respectfully report to me, "Your Majesty, I am certain that this man is dead, showing no signs of life."

Listening to Andrew’s report, I let out a soft sigh, raised my hand slightly, and replied calmly, "Rise, Mr. Andrew, I understand. Thank you for making the trip so late."

As I spoke, I glanced at Benjamin on the ground but paid no heed to the confusion in Andrew’s eyes.

Soon, Benjamin also slowly rose from the ground, his expression extremely solemn as he responded, "Your Majesty, my conclusion is the same as Teacher Andrew’s."

I nodded, looked at Christian on the side, and instructed, "Take the body away discreetly, just keep it at... keep it at the Holy Medical Pavilion for now, and remember, do not let anyone see it."

Christian, who would not question any of my decisions, respectfully responded with a ’yes’ and then led two people to carry away Austin’s body!

I glanced at their retreating figures, my gaze particularly falling on the white sheet covering Austin, furrowing my brows heavily, as if they couldn’t be smoothed out...

In the end, it was Andrew’s calm inquiry that brought back my senses. He asked, "Your Majesty, since you had already determined that this person was deceased, why call me and Benjamin here on such short notice? Do you think he was feigning death?"

As he spoke, he seemed to recall something, a flicker of complexity flashing in his eyes as he continued, "Could Austin also be involved with those bizarre werewolves? Are you suspecting that he is involved with that organization? Commander Angel... no, is Angel herself also like this?"

Seemingly struggling to accept such a fact, Andrew looked at me with a pained expression, tinged with sadness.

Regarding this highly respected elder, I held great respect and trust. I began to explain, "This matter has not been fully investigated yet. I will inform you first when there are results. I know you have always liked Angel, but she has committed many crimes, and I will not be lenient. I hope you can understand that."

Upon hearing this, Andrew heaved a deep sigh and shook his head to explain, "Your Majesty, you have misunderstood my intention; I am not trying to plead for anyone. I am just worried about you!"

"Angel and Austin were both very close companions and friends by your side, and now all are involved in this major case of treason and collusion with the enemy. The enemy is clearly targeting you! You must be particularly careful! If there is anything else that the Holy Medical Pavilion can assist with in the future, just speak up. Even if it costs me my life, I will continue to support you.

"You still have so many capable subordinates by your side, you won’t be left unguarded."

The dean’s words were timely and struck a chord with me.

Just moments ago, I indeed doubted myself. Why would my friends around me betray me like this? In Austin’s eyes, was I never truly his friend? And why did he harbor so much hatred towards me?

In those corners I hadn’t noticed, when had he and Angel started to change?

Such ridiculous self-doubt of course would not be expressed to outsiders, but Andrew, an astute physician, noticed my anomaly. With a few simple words, he soothed my fleeting vulnerability.

"Thank you, I understand." I responded warmly, a trace of a smile sliding across my eyes as I gladly accepted the old dean’s comfort.

Meanwhile, Benjamin, who had been silent until now, and Clark, whose facial expression was somewhat indiscernible, both also visibly relaxed a bit.

chapters.title: Chapter 281: I am your way out.
chapters.content:
Chapter 281: I am your way out.

[Margaret’s POV]

Today marks my third day in this private ward. My shoulder wound from the gunshot has healed quite a bit, thanks to Dr. Ace and the meticulous care of the nurses.

Donald is always by my side in the hospital room. Although sometimes he leaves for a little while due to his busy official duties, he always rushes back to my side as if he would lose something when he can’t see me for a moment.

This morning, Licia came by and talked to me about some things concerning the Austin siblings. I was surprised at how the situation had developed and listened with trepidation as Licia orally recounted the events of that day!

Actually, she had heard about these situations from Donald, and after much contemplation, had decided to tell me everything.

Donald is a person who values friendship and loyalty deeply. Although he often doesn’t show it, he truly cares about his friends. Naturally, his mood has not been the best after being betrayed and sold out by Angel and Austin one after the other.

He chose to keep it all to himself, but there were always people who cared about him, like Licia, like his mate.

The afternoon sunlight was brilliant, and as it shone through the window of the hospital room, it bathed everything in a warm glow. I leaned against the pillows on my bed, gently turned my head, and looked tenderly at Donald sitting by the bed, "Dr. Ace says my wound is healing very well, and in a couple of days, I should be able to leave the hospital and go home."

"Speaking of which, I’ve never been to the place where you usually work and rest. You’ll have to show me around when we go," I said.

Donald, of course, agreed to all my requests.

He nodded, took a glass of water from the table, and handed it to me. I chuckled as I looked at him, finding it somewhat helpless that he took Dr. Ace’s words as ’divine decree’.

"I don’t want to drink water, I want you to accompany me outside to enjoy the sun. Can you?" I asked with a smile in my eyes.

"Enjoy the sun?" Donald asked, surprised, after a second.

I raised my hand and pointed at the wheelchair leaning against the wall corner, signaling that I was all prepared and just waiting for my mate to come and be my free labor.

minutes later, on the spacious lawn beneath the hospital.

"How is Elliot doing now? I haven’t seen Benjamin or Louis come over to see me these past few days. Christian has been following us around, so he must have arrived in Royal City by now, right?" I asked Donald with a calm and gentle tone, smiling up at him.

Donald bent over and kissed my lips, a smile curling up as he replied, "Asking about other men in front of me, you’re getting bolder by the day! Isn’t worrying about me enough for you?"

Looking at Donald, who was needlessly jealous, I laughed helplessly, "Why are you even jealous of your own subordinate? Isn’t that childish?"

As I spoke, I reached out and tugged at Donald’s tie, signaling for him to lower his head a bit more.

Donald looked at me indulgently, letting me pull his tie as I kissed the corner of his lip, "One question for one kiss, it’s a fair trade," I suggested.

Donald was, of course, delighted with my proposal. Supporting my wheelchair’s armrest with his hand, he tilted his handsome face and exchanged an enthusiastic kiss with me until I was nearly out of breath. Only then did he mercifully release me!

"Your body hasn’t fully recovered yet, don’t tempt me like that."

The man’s deep, sexy voice arose by my ear, as if a seductive demon was whispering, causing my heart to flutter and making me look up at him, my emotions stirred.

Perhaps struck by my emotionally moved expression and gaze, the light in Donald’s eyes dimmed, and he was about to bend down to kiss me again when I stopped him with my hand on his chin.

I looked into his eyes, smiling as I gently asked, "Why haven’t you told me about your bad mood these past days? We’re mates, Licia is worried about you too."

As I spoke, I caressed Donald’s cheek with my fingertips, my eyes filled with concern, "The world was never meant to be absolutely fair. When we gain something, we are destined to lose something else. We have to learn to reconcile, to find our own ’outlets’ for our emotions."

I looked up and kissed Donald’s lips, whispering, "And I will always be your ’outlet’."

Something turbulent in Donald’s eyes, and his body seemed to stiffen for a moment before relaxing completely.

He knelt down and hugged me tightly, his arms strong and powerful.

After a long moment, he answered me hoarsely, "I’m not as fragile as you all think I am, but if you want to comfort me, then hurry up and get better. You know, I’ve been waiting for a long time."

Although I was somewhat surprised by Donald’s reaction, I understood him because I missed him just as much...

chapters.title: Chapter 282: Affairs
chapters.content:
Chapter 282: Affairs

[Margaret’s POV]

The afternoon stroll had hastily come to an end because Donald was too eager, seemingly in desperate need of my comfort!

"Ha! Hmm... Donald!"

After the door of the hospital room slammed shut, Donald’s long, sturdy arms encircled my waist, and he bent down to seal the lips that I had wanted to continue speaking with.

Donald kissed me with great emotion, yet his touches on me were exceptionally gentle. He usually preferred more wild and direct sexual encounters, so the tenderness he exhibited today was a rare occurrence.

Because he had to be careful of the wound on my shoulder, Donald wasn’t able to take off all of my top. Instead, in a fit of passion, he undid my bra and firmly grasped my breasts, gently yet powerfully massaging them!

His other hand wasn’t idle either, holding my waist and quickly positioning me on the hospital bed.

His actions were completed very swiftly, and by the time I regained some of my senses, my entire body was pinned down on the bed, unable to move, and the area near my thighs, left unprotected by the hospital gown, was already harshly confronted by his vigorous member!

Donald had no intention of relieving himself. His trim waist tense, he breathed hotly and urgently through his flared nostrils, as if the next second he would lose control and penetrate me, completely claiming me.

I was somewhat shocked by his urgency. I lowered my head to look at his sizable member against me but was quickly stopped by Donald gripping my chin, forcing me to lift my face passively to endure his unbearable and excited desire!

His tongue was forceful and passionate, as if it intended to draw my soul directly into his stomach.

I responded to him with equal fervor, but this action, like a final fuse, instantly ignited his aggressive advances!

"I can’t hold back any longer, baby! I’ll be gentle...."

"Hmm? You... Donald, slow down! I’ll help you!"

Donald didn’t plan on accepting my gradual assistance; he only wanted to occupy all of me immediately, to have my warmth envelop his firm and intense desire.

When Donald’s member thrust in, I was still stunned by its terrifying size, always feeling an absurd sensation that he might tear me apart!

But it seemed I was uniquely gifted, as I smoothly accommodated all of Donald. He sighed deeply, and his hot breath ignited next to my ear.

When Donald and I made love, there was always an incredible synergy and comfort, a familiarity earned through adjustment, understanding exactly which angles and positions brought true pleasure to each other!

The skin on Donald’s back was very firm to touch, a pleasure to hold, and my nails, neatly trimmed during my recovery, did not damage him, allowing only a slight and feeble curling and spreading, fully welcoming the man on top of me.

My entire body, except for the bandaged wound on my shoulder, had been stripped bare by Donald, my skin beginning to flush with the intensity of the act, blooming beautifully in the daylight like a tender, radiant bud!

I was taken to the heights of ecstasy, completely oblivious to the pain from the wound on my shoulder. When the wound slightly tore open and the smell of blood brought back a trace of Donald’s senses, our closely intertwined, erotically tangled movements gradually slowed.

I looked up to kiss Donald’s neck, lightly humming through my nostrils, questioning why he had suddenly stopped.

Donald leaned down to kiss me back, his voice hoarse as he explained, "Your wound has opened up; doesn’t it hurt?"

I looked up at him in confusion but suddenly crashed into his eyes filled with teasing and intense frustration, "Hmm? What, what happened?"

Seeing me like this seemed to frustrate Donald greatly. He silently observed me for several seconds, then bit his lip, as if making a certain decision.

Seeing Donald lean into me, I thought he was going to kiss me and lifted my chin to meet him, but instead of his lips, the next second I felt his hot breath on my neck!

"Ah! Donald... why are you biting me?" I suddenly felt pain and looked at him with slightly furrowed brows.

Donald smirked, explaining as he raised an eyebrow, "Let’s put this one on your tab for now, and you can pay me back all together when you’re better."

After saying that, he rolled off me and sat on the edge of the bed, his member still lively and erect. I glanced at it, questioning him with my eyes.

Donald didn’t speak but quickly walked into the nearby bathroom, his additional explanation trailing behind him, "Although I’ve been extremely careful, the surgical wound on your shoulder still opened up. Just wait a bit for me, I’ll be right out to call the doctor for you!"

chapters.title: Chapter 283: Copy it?
chapters.content:
Chapter 283: Copy it?

[Margaret’s POV]

As Donald’s voice faded, my delayed reasoning violently snapped back into place, and the pain from my wound became sharp. I instinctively turned my head to see that the bandage had started to bleed...

In that moment, my emotions were incredibly complex. On one hand, I felt that my pain tolerance had seemingly increased, on the other, I was annoyed with myself for being so captivated by Donald that he noticed something like this before I did.

Embarrassed by my own dullness and lust, I pulled the blanket over my head, my ears blushing as if they were boiled!

By the time Dr. Ace was called in by Donald using the pager bell, Donald had already dressed me. However, my rosy complexion was in stark contrast to the slightly reopened wound on my shoulder.

"It’s nothing serious, just some cosmetic stitches that have come apart. Lycan Queen, you should try to move your arm with smaller and slower motions from now on, that should prevent any further issues."

Dr. Ace quickly treated my wound, and while he was tidying up the dressing kit, he reminded me with patience and warmth in his voice.

He did not suspect how my wound was forced open, and the culprit standing by my sickbed merely smiled at me, showing no intention of offering an explanation.

Of course, I didn’t want to hear the true facts of the matter from Donald either – too embarrassing! I would rather admit that it was an accident caused by my own carelessness.

I nodded and feigned seriousness, saying, "I understand, Dr. Ace. Thank you for your hard work."

Dr. Ace smiled, shook his head slightly, and after giving a slight bow to Donald, he left, leaving just me and a certain villain in the sickroom.

Donald glanced at me, his eyes glinting with mischief, "Have you now learned to tell lies? You’ve turned naughty, dear."

I glared at him silently, not really wanting to talk to the childish ghost, but thankfully, a sudden knock on the door quickly drew Donald’s attention away.

The people entering were Benjamin and Christian. They seemed to have planned to come together.

"Is there something important? Margaret hasn’t had her afternoon tea yet, would you like to wait a bit before discussing?"

Although Donald’s words were phrased as a question, his tone did not suggest he was inquiring about the two Lycans’ intentions. I reached out and tugged at the hem of Donald’s shirt, signaling him to proceed with his official matters first—afternoon tea could wait.

Donald gave me a resigned look and, after thinking it over, accepted my suggestion.

"Then speak quickly," he urged the two Lycans who exchanged glances.

Benjamin and Christian shared a somewhat gossipy look and then respectfully replied, "Your Majesty, Andrew and I have made some new discoveries while conducting pathological research on the captives. I felt it was necessary to bring it to your attention immediately."

As he spoke, Benjamin handed a glass vial to Donald, and Christian opened a video player on his tablet.

It was a video from their experiments in the laboratory, where occasional chilling screams could be heard!

This was not Benjamin conducting any inhumane experiments on these captives; he had simply segregated those infected with the Mysterious Potion, maintaining their vital signs while observing their subsequent physiological changes.

"With the collective efforts of myself and several heads from the Lycan Medical School, we’ve finally identified the combination and proportion of the compounds within the Mysterious Potion. If Your Majesty wishes, we are even capable of replicating it!"

Benjamin finished, looking up at Donald, as if to gauge his interest.

Donald watched the subjects in the video without speaking for a long time, prompting Benjamin to continue reporting, "Thanks to Mark and others who captured Angel alive, our research on the ’MASTER’ potion has made such significant progress over the past few days!"

"Weren’t you wondering why Angel’s wounds in the Misty Forest healed so quickly, Your Majesty? After days of research and experimentation, we finally found the key to it!"

What Benjamin mentioned next was indeed an issue Donald and I had been puzzling and caring about. My curiosity was piqued, and my eyes searching as I looked at Benjamin.

"Stop beating around the bush and tell us," Christian urged Benjamin, who seemed fed up with the medical officer’s inexplicable desire to perform.

Christian’s words caught Benjamin off-guard. Curbing his desire to tell the story, he continued to report to Donald and me, "’MASTER’ seems to have injected Angel with an exceptionally potent drug, the effects of which are to rapidly restore her depleted combat strength and heal all the physical injuries on her body!"

chapters.title: Chapter 284: Hide the Truth
chapters.content:
Chapter 284: Hide the Truth

[Margaret’s POV]

Even though we had long been witnesses to the ’MASTER’s bizarre and extraordinary potion-making abilities, the shock and amazement inside me couldn’t be stilled upon hearing Benjamin talk about such a thing!

Because this had transcended the realm of what ordinary people could comprehend, it seemed to have become something of a fantasy.

I widened my eyes slightly and looked at Benjamin, who stood before me with a serious expression, fear evident in his eyes, "A rapid recovery from all physical injuries? Is this a potion or is it magic? This is too inconceivable, right?"

I shook my head, feeling that such a thing was excessively bizarre. After a brief ponder, I continued to ask, "Then, wouldn’t such an illogical potion have some side effects when they use it on themselves? I mean, can it really be completely harmless?"

"How is she... How is Angel doing now? Why didn’t I see any surveillance videos monitoring her on the computer?"

After voicing the doubts in my heart, my gaze turned to Benjamin and Christian.

Upon hearing my question, Benjamin first glanced at me before shifting his gaze towards Donald, who stood beside me, as if seeking his consent.

Seeing Benjamin react this way, I involuntarily turned my head towards Donald.

It seemed my mate had concealed something from me.

"Are these questions that difficult to answer? Why do you need to ask for Donald’s opinion first? Can’t you tell me?" I asked.
