Upon hearing my question, Benjamin immediately shook his head vehemently and hurriedly explained, "Of course not, Your Majesty. You are our Lycan Queen, and you have the right to be informed about all matters concerning the werewolf race!"

"I did not answer your questions immediately because His Majesty had specifically instructed that your well-being is paramount as you are still recovering from serious injuries. Regarding the traitor Angel and her cohorts, there’s no need to burden you with every detail unless necessary."

Benjamin’s explanation was somewhat plausible. I looked at Donald doubtfully and softly asked, "Is that all? Why do I feel like you’re hiding something from me?"

Throughout my conversation with Benjamin, Donald had been watching me calmly. Upon hearing my words, he just smiled lightly, his eyes gleaming with shrewdness, "Honestly, I hadn’t expected you to be so perceptive now, darling. Alright, I did indeed keep a few things from you these past days."

He said, taking my hand gently and continued with a smile, "I intended to wait until you were a little better before telling you... Austin committed suicide by poisoning himself, and Angel has gone mad."

I hadn’t expected such a sharp turn in Donald’s words, revealing two pieces of extremely heavy news so directly.

I stared at him with wide eyes, unconsciously repeating his words, "What? Austin committed suicide? Angel... Angel went mad? Why? How did this happen so suddenly?"

As if he had anticipated my shock, Donald’s expression did not change much, but he held my hand a little tighter, "Three days ago, Austin was summoned to the Royal City and was interrogated on the spot by me and several ministers at the Eastern Shine Palace. At first, he refused to admit his deeds, but after I threatened him with Lillian, he finally broke down and confessed."

Seeing Donald pause suddenly, I prompted, "And then? What happened next?"

Donald sighed deeply, finding some comfort in my tender gaze, "He suddenly lashed out, attempting to attack me in the palace, but I stopped him. He bit into the poison capsule he’d hidden in his mouth and committed suicide. It was a fast-acting poison, and no one could save him."

The emotion in Donald’s eyes dipped significantly as he spoke, something only I, who was closest to him, noticed.

I took his hand in mine, looking into his eyes with a gentle tone, "Austin, having harbored malice from the start and wishing harm upon you and the Royal Family, is no longer your friend."

"You need not feel the slightest guilt over his suicide; it was a price he had to pay himself. Don’t be sad, you still have me, and so many companions surrounding you!"

Donald seemed to crave my comfort, yet not quite so.

He smiled, leaned down to plant a doting kiss on my forehead, his voice incredibly tender, "I know all that you’re saying, and I am neither sad nor guilty. I just wonder why they felt the need to do this."

As his warm kiss lingered on my forehead, I slowly looked up at him, knowing that among the ’they’ he referred to, there was also Angel.

chapters.title: Chapter 285: Crazy
chapters.content:
Chapter 285: Crazy

[Margaret’s POV]

"According to our latest research on Angel’s body and blood, we found that her internal organs show varying degrees of failure, and her blood concentration is higher than that of an ordinary Lycan. Her mind seems to have remained at the stage when she was six years old,"

Benjamin took over the topic from Donald and began to report Angel’s recent condition to me with emphasis.

His expression was grave as he looked at me, seemingly sighing unconsciously, "The Lycans from the medical academy and I have determined that this is likely a severe side effect of that recovery potion. It can overdraw the potential power of the body’s organs for short-term injury recovery."

"The medicinal effect is naturally astonishing, but the side effects are equally terrifying! Your initial thoughts, Lycan Queen, were correct."

What Benjamin is suggesting here is that I had initially questioned whether this ’magic’ potion had any drawbacks for its users.

As it turns out, I was right. Angel went mad, which was a deserved outcome.

I let out a shallow breath and turned my full attention back to Donald, "The power behind Angel, ’MASTER,’ is indeed too twisted and terrifying. We cannot let him continue to act recklessly! We need to find a way to stop him, or capture him directly!"

"Otherwise, our lives will never be peaceful. Even the Lycans of the Royal Family can’t fully resist ’MASTER’s various seductions and temptations, let alone ordinary werewolf packs, whose situation is far more dangerous than ours."

Angel’s lesson has made me take ’MASTER’s threat more seriously. They are like some evil, rebellious religious force, seducing various people to their cause while using their sophisticated and ruthless pharmaceutical skills to create powerful war machines!

And the ultimate goal of all this is to satisfy their perilous and ugly desires! They regard the Werewolf Royal Family, led by Donald, as their greatest enemy.

I think a fierce battle of the century between us is inevitable.

Donald must be able to think more thoroughly and in detail about all these things that I have already considered. I am always willing to believe in my mate, for after all, he is the most impeccable Lycan in the whole world.

"You’re absolutely right, baby. This is also what I’ve been pondering these past few days," Donald said with a smile as he looked into my eyes.

He let out a light sigh and continued without much pause, "But this isn’t something that can be easily achieved by just talking about it. We need more information about ’MASTER,’ and time to plan more thoroughly. So, we can’t be hasty, we need to take it step by step."

"What’s the next step?" I took over Donald’s unfinished thought and asked.

Donald’s lips curved into a sly smile, and his expression turned cunning, "The next step, of course, is our wedding and your grand coronation ceremony as the Lycan Queen."

I was taken aback when Donald mentioned this, not expecting him to be thinking about such matters at this time.

But quickly, I caught on and my look towards Donald became inquisitive and probing, ’Already have a new plan?’

Without answering my probing gaze, Donald just smiled and started talking about something else, "Father and Mother should arrive at the Royal City in three days. Elizabeth and Armstrong have been delayed a few days due to issues with the Silver Moon Pack and will probably arrive at the Royal City later."

"In at most another week, you will be able to see your family in the Werewolf Palace, and our wedding will also officially take place then. You don’t need to do anything, just take good care of yourself, and perhaps look forward to our grand wedding ceremony."

Listening to Donald finish these two subjects, my eyes widened in astonishment. I looked at him in surprise, regaining my voice, "Are you serious? Are Father and Mother really on their way here? Why didn’t you tell me sooner? Did you arrange this, or was it the Empress Dowager?"

Seemingly dissatisfied with my questioning, Donald feigned a pinch on my cheek and retorted, "Would I lie to you about something so important? When have I ever lied to you? Regardless of who arranges these events, all you need to do is peacefully wait to be the bride, and I will give you my very best."

As Donald finished saying these words, my heart felt like it was filled with intoxicating syrup; I was utterly overwhelmed by sweetness!

Of course, I am looking forward to the wedding with Donald. Although the time we’ve known and loved each other is relatively short, we’ve been through so much together, the length of time doesn’t measure the depth of our feelings.

chapters.title: Chapter 286: Occasional encounter?
chapters.content:
Chapter 286: Occasional encounter?

[Margaret’s POV]

I was deeply in love with Donald and would have given my life for him.

I believed Donald felt the same way.

"Thank you, Donald," I said to my lover, a slight warmth in the corners of my eyes.

Donald kissed my eyes and answered with a smile, "I love you too."

I had once eagerly anticipated spending my life with Armstrong, but after meeting Donald, I deeply realized that such thoughts were mistaken. Armstrong was not meant for me; everything I had belonged to Donald, including my soul!

That afternoon, I asked Ruth to accompany me for a walk in the garden outside the Eastern Shine Palace.

Thanks to the relentless effort of Dr. Ace and Benjamin over the past few days, the injury on my shoulder had almost completely healed.

Benjamin had gained some inspiration from the ’MASTER’ recovery potion and had created a new, extremely rare healing medicine, which was highly effective and had almost no side effects!

This event had become a milestone for the Lycan Medical School and the Holy Medical Pavilion these past few days, propelling Benjamin to become one of the most celebrated figures in Royal City. He truly was riding high.

I was very fortunate to be the user of this rare medicine, and my injuries quickly improved.

But even so, Donald always seemed to want to keep his eyes on me all the time! I always felt that he was being overly anxious about me. This was his royal chamber after all; no one could breach the layers of protection and harm me here.

"Lycan Queen! Let’s not walk too far. If His Majesty finds out that you are not resting properly in the palace, he might get angry, don’t you think? Besides, your wound isn’t completely healed; don’t catch a cold and fall sick again!" Ruth remarked worriedly, looking at the increasingly unfamiliar surroundings.

Donald had kept me under tight constraints while I was recovering, and I indeed felt overly excited about breathing this fresh air.

Looking around at the buildings, I recognized they indeed seemed unfamiliar. I slowly nodded and said, "It’s getting dark; let’s go back."

Ruth feigned a exaggerated sigh of relief upon hearing this. I smiled at her, turned around to head back, but before we could really leave the area, cries for help and shouts came from a simple building behind us!

"Catch her! Don’t let her get away!"

"This way, this way, block her! If she really escapes today, the Empress Dowager won’t spare us!"

"Ah ah ah! How could this bitch still be biting people?"

"Shut up! Keep it down! Don’t let others hear you, do you want to die?"

My hearing was much better than Ruth’s, and when they mentioned the Empress Dowager, I involuntarily stopped.

I looked back quickly and noticed that the group was heading in my direction, and the woman being chased by the maids looked familiar!

"Margaret? It’s actually you! Why are you here, to gloat at my disgrace?"

The woman sprinted to a halt right in front of me and Ruth, as if realizing there was no escaping now.

Listening to the woman’s rather impolite tone, I suddenly recognized her as Lillian.

The Lillian in front of me was starkly different from the last time I saw her; if not for her typical haughty demeanor and condescending gaze, I would not have recognized her at a glance!

Her dress looked like it hadn’t been changed in many days, her complexion and hair were as lifeless as her dark-circled eyes, which almost covered the lower half of her face, making her look utterly disheveled and dirty. She wasn’t wrong in her self-assessment.

Thinking about everything she had been through these days, I felt no interest in ridiculing her, merely glanced at her indifferently, twirled the fan in my hand, and was about to leave.

But it seemed that this glance ignited all her nerves, and she began screaming hysterically, her voice as if she wanted to devour me alive!

"You bitch Margaret! Stop right there! Who are you to mock me? What right do you have to laugh at me? Do you think you’ve defeated me? Don’t even think about it! I will not let you off easily even in death! You will pay with your life! Return my brother’s life to me! I’ll kill you, you bitch!"

Lillian’s hysterical shrieks did not pose any threat in my eyes; let alone her current weak and mad state, even at her prime, she was hardly a match for me.

Although I rarely show my martial prowess in public, that doesn’t mean I am weak. I am someone who can fight a few rounds with Angel.

chapters.title: Chapter 287: Mysterious Hostility
chapters.content:
Chapter 287: Mysterious Hostility

[Margaret’s POV]

I easily sidestepped Lillian’s sharp claws, my splendid skirt even rippling in the air.

Lillian, whose strike had failed, was not as graceful or relaxed as I was.

She couldn’t control her momentum, almost losing her balance and nearly crashing onto the garden’s marble tiles. "Ah! Margaret, how dare you dodge? You won’t escape my grasp today!"

I found her threat, unable to grasp the situation at hand, utterly amusing.

Glancing at her sideways, my face bore an expression of extreme sarcasm, "Why wouldn’t I dodge? Did you think, with your mediocre fighting skills, you could actually get an advantage over me? Who gave you such confidence?"

Turning to face her, the sarcastic smile in my eyes faded away to be replaced by a cold scrutiny, "I don’t know if you’re genuinely insane or just venting your anger on me, but your brother’s death was entirely his own doing—it’s unrelated to anyone else."

"You want me to pay with my life for him? You really are good at playing the fool!" I added coldly, a sneer forming on my lips, my gaze toward Lillian nothing but aloof.

After hearing my words, Lillian’s eyes reddened as she glared directly at me, her voice filled with venom.

"Isn’t it all because of you, bitch, stirring up trouble? My brother and Donald were such good friends, why did everything change so drastically after you arrived in Royal City? Isn’t it you who instigated Donald to kill my brother? Do you think I would believe a word you say?"

"Ha! Do you think you will always be so pleased with yourself? Donald won’t love you forever. I’ll wait for the day you fall hard from the highest peak! Hahaha!"

As she spoke, Lillian seemed to completely lose her sanity, entirely disregarding the servants rushing up to restrain her, desperately trying to provoke me once more!

I frowned and observed her, taking in her utterly desolate state.

Lillian’s pretty face was deathly pale, her eyes bloodshot as she stared fixedly at me. I couldn’t quite understand her thought process at this moment; it just seemed utterly absurd to me!

Austin’s death was in no way my fault, and Lillian’s irrational hatred toward me was truly speechless.

Gazing at Lillian restrained on the ground, I asked tentatively after some thought, "Why do you believe your brother’s death is related to me? Did someone tell you something? Who have you met in these past few days?"

Lillian, now thoroughly hating me, naturally refused to answer my questions and kept glaring at me, her eyes boiling with a murderous intent.

A silence spread between us; I smirked softly, knowing it unlikely to extract any useful information from Lillian, and continued, "It’s okay if you don’t want to talk. I’ll go ask Mother about it."

As I spoke, I shifted my gaze from Lillian to the modest building behind, continuing, "Three days ago, Mother rescued you from Eastern Shine Palace, and she has been keeping you protected in this house ever since, hasn’t she? Mother really does take good care of you."

As if provoked by the mention of ’Mother,’ Lillian suddenly began to struggle violently, cursing incessantly...

Had everyone not known I was the most innocent in this affair, this scene would have looked as if I were the evil supporting character, always relying on influence to bully others.

"Bitch! Don’t you feel too smug! I...ah!"

Before Lillian could finish her curse, she was slapped across the face by a maid!

She cried out in pain, her expression filled with humiliation and resentment, just about to continue cursing, but the maid interrupted her abruptly.

"Shut your mouth! Our respected Lycan Queen is not someone you can insult at will. Keep your mouth clean!"

The maid paused, glanced up at me respectfully, then continued to address Lillian, "Empress Dowager saved your life out of her kindness and mercy, don’t take this as an opportunity to ask for more!"

"Before it’s known by Her Highness that you again attempted to escape, you better go back with us quickly to avoid disappointing and saddening the Empress Dowager!"

My expression unreadable, I observed the speaking maid and the kneeling, disheveled Lillian, remaining silent.

The maid seemed older, likely a high-ranking servant in the Palace, bold in actions but surprisingly considerate in speech.

chapters.title: Chapter 288: Arrived at Royal City
chapters.content:
Chapter 288: Arrived at Royal City

[Margaret’s POV]

Lillian, standing before me now, naturally had no significant status, but after all, she was a junior whom Licia had liked and valued. How could an ordinary servant dare to hit her outright?

Thus, this maid must be one of Licia’s more capable attendants. As for why she appeared here first, this indeed was a question worth my deep contemplation.

With Alpha Austin dead and the Windstorm Pack weakened, Lillian also promptly lost all the backing she could rely on. Licia still valued Lillian so greatly, surely there was something beyond my knowledge going on here.

Perhaps, Licia wasn’t completely satisfied with me, the definitive Lycan Queen. I pondered over these thoughts in my heart, feeling inexplicably gloomy.

However, I quickly adjusted myself, and after a light sigh, I asked the maid, "Are you saying this isn’t the first time Lillian has tried to run away? Among those mother sent to guard her, isn’t there anyone more capable?"

I said this with a gentle smile, then continued suggesting, "Her status is rather special now. Until mother figures out what to do with her, you must keep a good watch over her and not let her escape."

"If you need any help, I can arrange for some Lycans to come over. This would ease your burden, and mother wouldn’t have to worry about this anymore either, right?"

After hearing my words, the maid pondered deeply for a moment before nodding and responding, "Thank you, Your Highness, for your consideration for us. We do need such help. My name is Martha, and I hope to relieve Your Highness of worries in the future."

Seeing this maid named Martha getting on track, the smile on my face slowly widened.

I nodded and did not continue speaking, but turned to look at the ground where Lillian lay, speaking indifferently, "Considering how pitiful you’ve been lately, I’ll overlook your recent insult. But make no mistake, I am not to be trifled with."

"My earlier words stand as they are, your brother’s death has nothing to do with me and Donald. Don’t be rashly incited by others and misplace your hatred."

Lillian quietly listened to me till the end, her expression slowly turning odd.

She bowed her head as if to hide something and after a long while, she bit her teeth stubbornly and said, "Do you think I’ll let you go just because you said this? Think again! The suffering I endured today, I will return to you sooner or later. We shall see."

I looked at this woman as if I were looking at a madman, sighed silently, and then said, "It seems you’ve been severely poisoned by the other side. Oh well, trying to discuss things with an idiot like you is truly a waste of my time."

Having said this, I shifted my gaze away from Lillian and slowly walked away from the place.

Lillian, unwilling to accept my assessment, kept staring at my back, mumbling some meaningless accusations.

I didn’t care about her reaction; my mind was heavy as I made my way back to the Eastern Shine Palace, where Donald also arrived at that time, followed by a group of weary-looking people!

It was Frantz and Nora, who after hustling along, finally reached the Royal City.

"Oh, my dear Margaret! Daddy has missed you terribly these past few months! Are you alright, my child? You seem to have lost quite some weight. Are your injuries healed? Mom and I were so worried as we traveled here!"

The middle-aged werewolf man who spoke had a handsome and upright appearance, but his steady gaze held complexities that I couldn’t quite interpret. He was my father—Frantz, who always favored my sister.

Donald, who seemed to have already seen my father’s ’overwhelmingly warm’ personality, simply stepped forward and held my hand, his eyes smiling at me.

It had indeed been a long time since I saw my parents, and the gloominess I felt a while ago cleared up at this moment.

I smiled at my dad and replied, "I missed you and mom too! It must have been tough traveling all this way, wasn’t it?"

At this, Frantz nodded vigorously, just about to tell me in detail about the hardships along the way, when Nora, my beautiful and gentle mother, quickly caught his hand to pause him.

Nora looked carefully at me as if I were someone she barely knew. I looked back at her, my eyes questioning.

"Your complexion still looks somewhat pale; let me check your wounds later."

She said this with a gentle smile, stepped forward, and grabbed my somewhat cold hand, continuing, "I have brought back some very good medicine from outside, which can help you heal faster."

chapters.title: Chapter 289: Nora’s Blessing
chapters.content:
Chapter 289: Nora’s Blessing

[Margaret’s POV]

Listening to Nora’s words full of care and affection, all the doubts in my heart dispersed in an instant, leaving only the warmth of being deeply loved by my mother.

Although I hadn’t received as much love from them as Elizabeth did growing up, they were still my only parents, and I didn’t truly resent them—I still loved them.

Especially my mother, the gentlest and kindest therapist of the Silver Moon Pack. Of course, she was also the most outstanding therapist in the pack!

I nodded obediently, looking at her with a smile, "Thank you, Mom, but I’m really fine now! The doctors in the Royal City are very skilled, I am truly all healed."

Although Nora’s suggestion was declined by me like this, she wasn’t the least bit angry; my mother was always so understanding.

She looked at me and sighed softly, saying tenderly, "Well, as long as you’re fine, that’s the best news. Your dad and I can truly relax now."

Nora let go of her hand and looked up at Donald standing beside me, continuing, "You’ve taken very good care of Margaret, thank you. But I hope she won’t get hurt again! You might not know, but Margaret has been particularly sensitive to pain since she was young; she feels pain more acutely than most people."

Listening to Nora’s words, I blinked my big eyes and subconsciously looked up at Donald.

Donald tightened his grip on my hand a bit, his expression becoming more serious and solemn. He nodded, vowing solemnly, "I understand, Mom! From this moment, until the end of my true life, I will do everything in my power to protect Margaret and prevent her from any harm!"

"If I fail, I’m willing to suffer a hundred, a thousand times over, the same pain as she would endure..."

"Donald! That’s enough, let it be, don’t say anymore!" I quickly intervened to stop Donald’s intent to continue, looking into his eyes, shaking my head to indicate enough, no more words were necessary.

Donald highly valued promises being a Lycan King of his word, I knew he would fulfill everything he said, but that was not the situation I wanted to see!

Life is unpredictable; who knows if we will experience other accidents in the future?

But if that day really does come, I definitely don’t want Donald punishing himself the way he vowed! I don’t wish to see that happen.

I love him, and I’m willing to do a lot for him, without needing him to "repay" me by punishing himself. I don’t want such a repayment.

Donald should know that’s how I feel, but he wasn’t planning to give up on making this vow to my mother!

He looked at me, his face breaking into a gentle smile, "It’s okay, baby, I am willing to make this promise to Mom, to use my life to guard and protect you!"

"If I fail, I’m willing to endure a hundred, a thousand times the pain as you do. To live and die with you, united in heart and never parting! If I break this vow, may the Moon Goddess deliver the harshest punishment upon me."

I was stunned that Donald would make such a solemn vow and just stared at him for a long while until Nora spoke in a gentle tone, bringing me back to my senses.

"Although this wasn’t my ultimate intention, you’ve already made your vow, and I’m willing to believe that you will take good care of my daughter."

"Margaret is a good child, and you are very fine as well. I hope that from now on, you will support each other, understand each other, and always live in happiness and safety," said Nora.

I turned to look at my mother, feeling the corners of my eyes grow slightly moist and my heart unbearably soft and warm.

Though Frantz didn’t speak much, he continuously looked at me with a smiling gaze, filled entirely with blessings.

In this moment, I felt like the happiest person in the world, having such loving parents and the love of my life, Donald, by my side.

"I understand, Mom!"

"Thank you, Mom."

Donald and I almost said in unison.

At our words, Nora smiled, looking very pleased as she nodded at the sight of me and Donald standing together, and suddenly, some incredibly hot liquid slid from her eyes!

They must have been tears of joy and relief.

I hadn’t expected the events of this evening to unfold like this, but the arrival of Nora and Frantz seemed to further solidify the reality of mine and Donald’s upcoming wedding.

Yes, I knew the wedding plans were underway, but my sense of participation wasn’t strong. I had gambled everything on returning to the Royal City with Donald, and besides him, there wasn’t anyone or anything else familiar to me.

My heart had always been somewhat floating, but in this moment, it truly settled down to earth.

I never realized how important the blessings of Nora and Frantz were to me!

chapters.title: Chapter 290: Transformation
chapters.content:
Chapter 290: Transformation

[Margaret’s POV]

These past few days, 7:30 PM had been the time Benjamin set for my daily medication.

I composed my somewhat excited emotions and smiled, gently patting Nora’s hand, signaling that I needed to step away for a bit.

Nora glanced at me and softly suggested, "Shall I come with you? I’d like to meet this impressive therapist, his name is Benjamin, right?"

Upon hearing Nora’s suggestion, I hesitated for a second but agreed without much delay. It wasn’t a big deal, so I responded, "Yes, mother, let’s go together. Taking the medicine won’t take long."

As I spoke, I turned to look at Donald, giving him a look that said we would be right back.

Nora glanced at Frantz, said something to him, and then stood up to follow me out.

Eastern Shine Palace, the place where Benjamin usually worked.

Unexpectedly, when I had finished and came out of the inner room, I encountered Licia and her servants in the Eastern Shine Palace.

Licia was speaking quietly with Benjamin. Seeing Nora and me emerge from the medication room, the somber expression on her face quickly cleared, leaving only a warm, gentle smile.

She looked at me and Nora, greeting us softly, "I had already prepared a welcoming feast for Lady Nora and Mr. Frantz in the dining room. I just came to see if you were done here."

Licia said, smiling kindly at Nora and then turned her gaze to me, "How are you? Your shoulder should be much better now, right? Benjamin told me you’re almost fully recovered, aren’t you?"

At her words, I nodded and replied with a light laugh, "Yes, mother, it’s almost healed."

"So, you were just discussing my situation with Benjamin. I’m truly embarrassed that you’ve been so worried about me! You must have been quite busy these days?" I continued without pause, my gaze falling on Benjamin.

Their conversation clearly hadn’t revolved around me, a sudden intuition telling me so.

I was actually quite curious about what Licia and Benjamin had been discussing, but obviously, they had no intention of telling anyone else.

I’m not sure if I was being overly sensitive, but, after the arguments with Lillian earlier that afternoon, I felt that Licia seemed somewhat different to me...

"Not very busy, I rather like this feeling," Licia replied to my question, stepping forward to gently pull my hand, though her eyes were looking at Nora.

With a smile, she added, "Now that Lady Nora and Mr. Frantz are in the Royal City to lend a hand, there are more and more people to help me, and I certainly don’t feel tired anymore. I’m really looking forward to what comes next!"

At Licia’s words, Nora smiled gently, but she didn’t reply.

I nodded very obediently and replied, "If there’s anything you need help with, mother, you just have to tell me, and I will assist you. My mom might not be familiar with all the Royal Family’s protocols, so she might not handle such matters well."

"Of course, I also need you to teach me many things to avoid making mistakes during the grand ceremony."

Having added that last remark, I looked at the two ladies beside me with a beaming smile.

Nora neither agreed nor disagreed with my words, while Licia never directly denied my requests. She replied, "These matters are trivial; a bright girl like you is sure to learn them quickly. Well, let’s not just stand around talking here, we should hurry back and take our seats. Donald must be getting anxious waiting!"

As Licia spoke, she turned to lead Nora and me out, but I suddenly spoke up, interrupting her, "Oops! Where’s my phone?"

"Did you lose your phone? Could it be back in the medication room?" Nora suggested aloud.

I thought briefly and nodded, "It must be in the medication room. I should go back and get it. Mom, you go ahead with her, I will be right there."

Hearing this, Nora looked at me a bit reproachfully, as if frustrated by my forgetfulness, "Alright, make it quick. Don’t keep everyone waiting too long."

As Nora led Licia away, I turned and headed back to the room.

My phone was indeed left inside, and I hadn’t lied to Licia and Nora about it, but walking behind by choice wasn’t just for this reason.

I wanted to ask Benjamin some questions.

..."You’re wondering who now resides in the building on the far west of the Eastern Shine Palace? I’m really not sure, I’m sorry Your Majesty, I can’t help you with this," Benjamin said, clutching a book in his hands, trying hard to remember, but upon realizing he really didn’t know, he turned to me with a somewhat apologetic expression.

chapters.title: Chapter 291: Ask Around
chapters.content:
Chapter 291: Ask Around

[Margaret’s POV]

I just shook my head slightly upon hearing that, not particularly concerned with Benjamin’s response. After musing silently for a bit, I posed another question, "Does Her Highness the Empress Dowager have any other relatives by her side? I mean, besides Donald and Prince Henry."

It seemed Benjamin hadn’t expected me to change the topic so abruptly, and a slow confusion crept onto his face. However, he’s always been an extremely intelligent man and wouldn’t overtly display curiosity without measure, especially when it comes to dealing with Donald and me.

Benjamin looked up at me respectfully, pondered for a moment, and replied, "Most of the Empress Dowager’s relatives perished in the wars that occurred over a decade ago; there aren’t many others close to her. If I really had to mention someone, there is Mr. Levi from the Spirit Dragon pack, who is a distant nephew of Her Highness the Empress Dowager."

"However, the Spirit Dragon pack is far too distant from the Werewolf Royal City. I guess the Empress Dowager hasn’t had any contact with Levi for many years."

As Benjamin spoke, I kept my gaze firmly on his eyes. Besides our relation as sovereign and subject, Benjamin and I had been comrades through many life-and-death crises, and I trusted that he wouldn’t deceive me with these matters.

Behind Benjamin stood a family of considerable strength, so this seemingly secret information about the Royal Family wasn’t much of a secret to him. This was one of the reasons why I had taken the initiative to stay behind today; I needed him to provide me with some useful information.

After listening to Benjamin’s response, I furrowed my brows, pondered for a moment, then voiced a follow-up question, "If she has no relatives by her side, what about close friends? Does she have any here in the Royal City?"

Given the extent of my concern for this issue, Benjamin shook his head almost instinctively and couldn’t help but ask in a soft voice, "Your Majesty, why do you inquire about these matters? Wouldn’t it be faster and more accurate to ask His Majesty directly about such things?"

Seeing the clear question in Benjamin’s eyes, I knew I couldn’t learn much from him and let out a light sigh in response, "I don’t want Donald to get involved in certain matters, so I had to avoid him and come to ask you instead."

I rubbed the phone in my hand as I spoke and, when I raised my eyes back to Benjamin, I emphasized in a grave tone, "Keep today’s matters a secret for now. When the opportunity arises, I’ll explain things clearly to Donald."

Upon hearing my words, Benjamin nodded, and his body relaxed imperceptibly, as if burdened by an incredibly grave matter!

I glanced at him, slightly amused, and with a more relaxed tone, I teased, "Look how scared you are. I only came to you because I genuinely couldn’t find anyone else to help. I thought since we’ve been comrades through life and death together, you would be willing to help me."

With another slow sigh, I turned my gaze away and continued helplessly, "This afternoon, we were nearly attacked by Lillian in the gardens. I thought, since she is someone the Empress Dowager wants to protect, it wouldn’t be good for me to confront her directly. Yet clearly, she doesn’t think the same way..."

"What? Lillian actually tried to ambush you! No...that’s not right. How would she know when you’d be in the garden? After Austin’s suicide by poisoning, she was taken away by the Empress Dowager and confined. Why is she still so obstinate? Your Majesty, did she injure you during the ambush?"

At that moment, Benjamin was like a walking "’Why’ Encyclopedia." I simply shook my head and looked at him without speaking, equally curious to know the answers to these questions.

Seeing that I only shook my head and didn’t talk, something seemed to click for Benjamin, and he widened his eyes slightly as he continued, "So the Empress Dowager just now took some medicinal remedies from me, and it was all actually for Lillian! But...why would she do that?"

Benjamin now realized something was amiss and looked at me strangely, pressing on with his question, "Lillian’s attempt to ambush you failed, and instead, she got injured, yet the Empress Dowager quietly covered it up and even specifically came to me to request medicine for her rapid recovery...why would that be?"

I looked at Benjamin, who had finally come to understand, and said in a calm voice, "You don’t need to analyze so clearly; it’s a bit upsetting."

Benjamin: "..."

Not dwelling much on the apologetic look etched across Benjamin’s face, I averted my gaze and continued, "So the Empress Dowager wasn’t actually discussing my injuries earlier but was getting medicine for Lillian, right?"

Benjamin, not daring to leave my question unanswered, nodded and then looked at me with a slight concern.

My mouth curved into a faint smile as the whole affair began to seem more interesting to me. While there was some discomfort in my heart, it wasn’t going to affect my rationale.

Licia’s steadfast defense of Lillian might really be due to some other important reasons and not because she dislikes me...

chapters.title: Chapter 292: I don’t want to make it difficult for him
chapters.content:
Chapter 292: I don’t want to make it difficult for him

[Margaret’s POV]

"Lycan Queen, are you alright?" Benjamin asked cautiously.

I turned back to look at him, inaudibly gasping for breath, and answered, "I’m fine. Thank you for telling me so much today; I’ll be going now."

Upon hearing this, Benjamin merely nodded, without adding anything further.

With a respectful bow, Benjamin retreated, while I, followed by a silent Ruth, turned and left the side hall.

The journey back through several corridors to rejoin the banquet felt slightly heavy with each step I took...

For I had realized that Licia’s treatment of me was not as cordial as it appeared; perhaps it was only due to Donald’s firm and persistent stance that she had reluctantly accepted me as the sudden choice for queen!

In her heart, the real future queen might actually be Lillian.

With such thoughts, a strange sense of relief suddenly washed over me; beyond kinship and love, there really are no spontaneous kindnesses in this world!

"Are you still thinking about what just happened, Lycan Queen?" Ruth asked me.

I suddenly snapped back to reality, realizing that I had been lost in thought, and we had already returned to the entrance of the dining room.

"You’re right to feel angry! Why should the Empress Dowager favor the sister of a criminal? You are the queen confirmed by His Majesty himself, you and she are supposed to be one family in the future. She shouldn’t treat you this way!"

Upon hearing this, I stopped walking and turned to look at Ruth behind me, "I don’t want to hear such words from you again. Remember to mind your words and be prudent wherever you are, understood?"

The tone in which I spoke carried some severity, Ruth, rarely seeing this side of me, swallowed and quietly said with pursed lips: "Understood, Your Highness!"

Seeing that Ruth quickly realized her presumption, I exhaled gently, slowing down my speech and kindly said, "I don’t care how others view this matter; you are my companion, and I hope you will pay attention to your attitude and behavior, and not be used by others!"

"That’s not the situation I want to see, and I’m not really angry... I just feel I was a bit too naive," I said.

I said with a light laugh, looking at Ruth I continued, "Let’s pretend we don’t know about this, let’s hurry up and go inside, Donald must be worried by now."

I didn’t want Donald to worry about these trivial matters while coping with so many others, making him feel uncomfortable between me and his mother! So, unless things reached the worst case, I didn’t plan to actively antagonize Licia; it wasn’t worth it.

After speaking, I stepped forward and walked in, with Ruth following slowly behind me.

She is a frank and adorable girl, aware that if she wants to stay by my side, she has to be smart.

Donald naturally worried about my tardiness, and sometimes I doubt he suffers from separation anxiety towards me!

"Why have you only just returned, darling? Nothing happened, right?"

The moment he saw me, Donald strode towards me, and wrapping his arms around my waist, he asked softly.

I smiled at him, responding softly, "I just went back to get my phone, what could have happened? Let’s sit down; we shouldn’t keep mother and the others waiting too long."

Donald looked deeply into my eyes as though searching for something, I let him scrutinize me closely, appearing entirely unflustered on the outside.

"Good, as long as you’re fine, let’s eat," he said.

I nodded, compliantly followed him to our seats, and as I wiped my hands with a towel to prepare for the meal, I even exchanged a usual glance with Licia seated across from me.

As long as Frantz was present at the meal, there could never be a dull moment; my father might not be very trustworthy in other aspects, but he was quite skilled in conversation and social matters in these settings, very unlike me and Nora.

As for who in the family is more like him, it would have to be my beautiful, yet not so clever sister, Elizabeth.

Speaking of Elizabeth, I really do miss her a bit. I have been recuperating all this time, and besides Donald and Ruth, I haven’t had any close people around; I truly miss the incessant chatter when she was by my side.

After the dinner ended, I took it upon myself to accompany Nora and Frantz back to their rooms to rest, Donald having no official duties tonight, also came along.

"This palace, after all, is a palace, everywhere so magnificent and beautiful! Building such a grand structure must have cost a lot of money, maybe more than I could earn in several lifetimes! Our Margaret is really accomplished to have made it possible for me to live in such a luxurious house in my lifetime."

chapters.title: Chapter 293: Huge Change Coming
chapters.content:
Chapter 293: Huge Change Coming

[Margaret’s POV]

Nora and I were walking slowly ahead when I heard the words that Frantz was rattling off to Donald, and my heart sank a little, prompting me to turn my head to look over.

Before I could open my mouth to ease the slightly awkward atmosphere, Nora and Donald had already taken the initiative to speak and gently moved past the moment.

"Frantz, have you been drinking too much? You should get some rest when we go back inside, and I’ll take care of sending Margaret and Donald off."

"If you like it, dad, then why not stay a bit longer? Margaret and I would be very glad to have you and mom stay in the Royal City,"

Donald finished speaking and looked up at me, a tender smile on his face.

I took a faint breath of relief, dispelling some of the gloom that had lingered in my mind. Donald’s love for me meant naturally he wouldn’t disdain anyone or anything associated with me, including my somewhat mercenary and biased, though not bad, father.

Nora was intelligent and kind-hearted; she always knew how to behave without making me feel embarrassed.

Frantz too seemed to realize something at that moment, and his alcohol-stimulated brain sobered up, as he laughed and started chatting with Donald again.

As long as Frantz wanted to make a good impression, there really wasn’t a scene he couldn’t handle.

It was after 10 p.m. before Donald and I returned to our sleeping quarters.

Having been injured and after going through so much today, I was really feeling tired after taking a bath.

"Our wedding ceremony is the day after tomorrow; have you been swamped these last few days? Is there anything I can help with?"

Wearing my slippers, I walked toward Donald, who was sitting on the couch, and let my body slump into his warm embrace.

Donald wrapped his arms around me, his movements practiced and natural, as if we had spent many such nights together.

"I’ve finished all the work on my hands, and all the details of the ceremony are being overseen by my mother, so there shouldn’t be any issues, don’t worry,"

As he spoke, he bent down to gently kiss the top of my head and rubbed his face against mine before continuing, "I was looking at the reports sent back by Eric. Although Austin is now dead, we still don’t know where the firearms and supplies he secretly prepared have gone."

"While there’s no concrete evidence linking him to ’MASTER,’ I have this nagging feeling that these two matters are subtly interconnected. There’s definitely something we’re not aware of lurking in the background..."

Hearing Donald mention ’MASTER’, I slowly sat upright from his embrace and looked at what he was holding in his hands, "So what did Eric say? Are Austin and ’MASTER’ really in cahoots?" I asked softly.

At my words, Donald picked up his phone from the desk in front of him, turned it on to the message from Eric, kissed my temple, and said, "I haven’t had the chance to read what he said yet; join me, will you?"

"Why send the message via an electronic device? Can’t you use your mind-link with Eric anymore?" I asked.

Donald paused while holding his phone, looking down at me. It seemed like he finally realized there was something odd about this situation.

While Donald and his Lycan subordinates occasionally used modern electronic devices during their work, they wouldn’t normally use phones over the more confidential and secure mind-link for something as critical as frontline reports. This was unlike the Lycans’ usual meticulousness and quite unusual.

I had actually asked without much thought, but seeing Donald’s expression become serious, I quickly realized something was amiss, "Has something happened to Eric at Lilo Sea?"

My intuition was clearly not wrong as Eric’s electronic message to Donald laid out several major events, brief but concise.

The sky was about to change.

’Respected Lycan King, following your command, I left Silver Moon Pack with a 10-man Lycan squad, secretly heading to Lilo Sea to investigate Voyage Company, which was transporting firearms and supplies for Austin. We arrived four days ago, and during our investigation, we had contact with the person in charge of the company—Karl.’

’My intention was to report back to you immediately, but halfway through, we were ambushed three times! Our 10-man Lycan squad suffered heavy losses, and we were forced to hide and seek refuge wherever possible.’

’The situation in the waters of Lilo Sea is very strange, all of our mind-links are failing, and right now, this is the only way I can report to you. Please excuse this method! Also, I am requesting immediate reinforcements!’

chapters.title: Chapter 294: Levi
chapters.content:
Chapter 294: Levi

[Margaret’s POV]

I slowly sat up straight, and the look in my eyes as I gazed at Donald’s phone screen gradually became more serious.

It seemed that the trouble Eric had encountered at the Lilo Sea was far more severe than Donald and I had previously estimated. The person in charge of the Voyage Company—Karl—didn’t look simple at all!

The email Eric sent was particularly lengthy, and the information he reported later was the most surprising and baffling for Donald and me:

’I suspect that Alpha Levi of the Spirit Dragon Pack at the Lilo Sea has had some dealings with the MASTER! Because the first attack we suffered was led by their pack’s Beta!’

’In the latest intelligence I obtained, Austin had secretly met with this Levi several times six months ago! The content of their conversations during those meetings is still unknown to me, but I can be certain that such secretive and cautious assemblies suggest that there must be a major event unfolding behind the scenes.’

’Your Majesty, due to Alpha Levi’s special status, we dare not directly confront him. We need more military support, or, rather, for you to come to the Lilo Sea in person to oversee this investigation! Eric appended.’

My gaze remained on the name in the letter, a not-too-unfamiliar one, Alpha Levi of the Spirit Dragon Pack!

What a coincidence this was! I had just heard about this person from Benjamin in the afternoon, and now I was hearing his name again from Donald in the evening. How can this not be considered coincidental and strange?

I slightly hooked the corner of my mouth, shifted my eyes away from the phone screen, and looked toward Donald, who was similarly silent.

"What do you think about this matter?" I asked Donald.

At my words, Donald slowly came back to his senses, his gaze resting on my face, "This number was given to Eric when the Silver Moon Pack set out. To be prepared for emergencies, I had someone secretly pass it to him, so it’s unlikely to be misused by enemies."

After hearing this addition from Donald, I slightly raised my eyebrows, but a weight seemed to sink in my heart.

Putting aside Donald’s caution and thoroughness, the information that Eric had sent back from the Lilo Sea was indeed true and reliable; only Eric himself was able to send emails to Donald using this account!

Eric’s investigative trip to the Lilo Sea had indeed run into trouble.

"Then how are we going to support them next..."

"Baby, why aren’t you curious about who this ’Levi’ is? Why don’t Eric and his team dare to confront him directly?"

Donald and I spoke almost simultaneously, and I looked up into his eyes as I heard the questions and probing tone in his voice.

It must be said that Donald’s intuition is sometimes so precise that it’s a bit frightening—he could tell something was off with me!

"So you really know who this person is? Why? I don’t think I’ve mentioned him to you before, and it’s even less likely that mother would have brought him up. So who told you about him?"

Seeing that I did not start a conversation, the perplexed look on Donald’s face deepened.

He asked and then fixed his downward gaze on me as if he wouldn’t let go until I answered.

I looked back at Donald somewhat helplessly, thinking to myself, King Lycan, aren’t you focusing on the wrong things? Isn’t the most important thing right now to discuss how to deal with Eric’s matter? Why suddenly start worrying about whether I know an Alpha who’s miles away?

But Donald’s curiosity wasn’t easily quelled, and gradually, I found Donald’s profound and distant gaze too much to withstand!

After a slight sigh, I began to voluntarily narrate all the events of the afternoon, including Lillian’s attempt to attack me, and Licia’s visit to Benjamin to get medicine for Lillian...

"So you went to ask Benjamin about mother’s kin just because you wanted to know the real relationship between mother and the Austin siblings? Why didn’t you ask me? Mother did handle it unfairly, I will go and have a good talk with her. As for Lillian..."

After I finished recounting the events of the afternoon, Donald was silent for a while before asking back. He began to speak then dropped his head slightly, a small frown on his brow, looking somewhat morose.

This was one of the reasons I didn’t want Donald to know about it—he would be caught between a rock and a hard place with me and Licia.

I exhaled and looked at him with a light chuckle, saying warmly, "Everyone’s heart has its biases, just like I don’t want you to be troubled by these matters. Maybe mother was just trying to protect the helpless Lillian."

"So let’s just let it go. I wasn’t hurt by Lillian, and for mother’s sake, let’s pretend it never happened. She won’t be able to escape Lycan guards and cause trouble again."

As I spoke, I stretched my neck up to kiss the corner of Donald’s mouth, then added, "It’s my fault for not telling you these things right away. Please don’t be mad at me, okay?"

chapters.title: Chapter 295: Nomination
chapters.content:
Chapter 295: Nomination

[Margaret’s POV]

"I know you love your mother very much and don’t want to upset the balance between us three because of some insignificant people, so..."

Before I could finish speaking, Donald suddenly interrupted me.

It seemed he pondered a lot before he started speaking again, "So, this won’t set a precedence. If you ever feel aggrieved like this again, you must tell me right away, no matter who it is."

The meaning of ’no matter who it is’ here implied that no matter who made me feel aggrieved in the future, even if it’s Licia, Donald would stand by my side.

I understood what he meant, and I felt even sweeter inside, but I wasn’t surprised at all.

Donald has always been a ruler who acts with integrity and propriety. He wouldn’t blindly take sides just because the person who did wrong was his mother; he has his own standards and thoughts for judging situations.

I nodded gently, knowing that the matter would be set aside for the time being.

Now that I had successfully solved the mystery of who ’Levi’ was, it was time to discuss the real business at hand!

"Then whom do you plan to send to support Eric? Christian is indeed very reliable, but if he goes to Lilo Sea, won’t you lack someone by your side to manage affairs and speak for you? He’s just returned to Royal City not long ago, isn’t it too tight a schedule to send him out on a mission now?" I asked Donald.

Donald surely had a better idea of how to plan these things. He looked at me, smiled lightly, and replied, "I have someone better than Christian in mind. Want to take a guess?"

At his words, I raised my eyebrows slightly and looked at him with curiosity in my eyes, "Who are you talking about? It’s not Elliot, is it? He’s just recovered from his injuries. How can you be so cruel? In that case, it’d be better to have Christian endure a bit more!"

Seemingly unsatisfied with the ’accusations’ in my words, Donald smiled mischievously and suddenly pressed his lips harshly against mine!

"Hmm? Donald! You..."

After a moment, Donald gazed at my slightly swollen lips and smiled happily, his eyes curving up, "You’ve been caring about this or that all day long; do you even have room for your husband in your heart? This is your punishment! Let’s see if you dare to talk about other men all the time after this!"

Disoriented by that passionate kiss and somewhat baffled, "..."

"Alright, let me tell you who it is! I actually just thought of him, sending him will definitely be unexpected for ’MASTER’, even Eric might not guess." Donald spoke with a generous tone, moved his fingers, and a new phone number appeared on the screen of his phone.

It was Anthony!

The Beta of the Silver Moon Pack that came to Werewolf Royal City with Armstrong and Elizabeth to attend mine and Donald’s wedding — Anthony.

"Why send him?" I asked Donald, not quite understanding.

"I’ve said it before, he’s a talent not to be missed, and I admire him. Plus, I have an intuition that he won’t reject my offer this time. Coming to Royal City is a very wise choice," he explained.

Donald answered in this manner, but my heart was filled with more doubts and confusion.

The next day at lunchtime in the refined and exquisite dining room of Eastern Shine Palace, the person who walked in behind Clark, who had opened the door, was a figure I was very familiar with.

At first, I thought it might be Elizabeth and Armstrong who had arrived, and I was quite delighted, but to my surprise, it was Prince Henry.

"If I’d known, I would’ve come a little later, not to disturb the intimate lunch of you newlyweds," Henry said, rolling closer in his wheelchair with a beaming smile.

I set down the cup in my hand, turned my head towards him, and nodded politely with a smile as a form of greeting.

Donald glanced at him and responded indifferently, "Knowing that is enough; make sure to have Clark announce you next time before you come in."

Henry’s eyes widened in surprise at Donald’s attitude and he responded with a lack of words, "Ah, you’re right. I’ll definitely pay attention next time."

I listened to the bickering of the two brothers, feeling resigned, then smiled and intervened, "Enough now, do you have to start arguing the moment you meet? Are you still three-year-old kids? Have you had lunch yet, Your Highness? Would you care to join us?"

Although our last encounter with Henry had not ended well, today, we all tacitly avoided mentioning the past, as if it had never happened.

"Really, sister-in-law? That’s wonderful! I’ve been running errands for this guy all morning and indeed haven’t eaten anything yet!" Henry exclaimed with joy.

chapters.title: Chapter 296: Test
chapters.content:
Chapter 296: Test

[Margaret’s POV]

After hearing Henry’s response, I said no more, merely tilting my head up slightly toward Ruth, who stood behind me, nodding at her to summon someone to prepare cutlery for Henry.

Soon enough, Henry was seated next to Donald, agreeably taking his place as they conversed about some matters concerning the wedding the following day.

"From the guest list you had drafted earlier, aside from a few elder Lycans, only those from Lilo Sea and the Alpha from the Demon Domain Forest weren’t present," said Henry.

As he spoke, he glanced sideways at Donald’s expression but, finding nothing out of order, he continued with his report, "Apart from the absence of attendees, no responses came back after the invitations were sent. I think..."

Before Henry could finish, Donald abruptly put down his utensils, wiped the corner of his mouth gently with a napkin, and interjected, "The werewolf packs from the Demon Domain Forest have never been too obedient to the Royal Family’s commands; it’s quite normal for them not to attend. But what about Levi, the Alpha of the Spirit Dragon Pack? He is my mother’s only living nephew. Why didn’t he come either?"

Listening to Donald’s question, I too turned to look at Henry, who was in charge of the wedding banquet guests, a shadow of deep color slowly emerging from the depths of my eyes.

Donald and I were fully aware of why Levi hadn’t personally arrived in the Royal City to attend the banquet; Levi’s political allegiances were unclear, and not long ago, he had even attacked the Lycan squad Donald dispatched to investigate Voyage Company. He surely wouldn’t dare accept a wedding invitation from Donald—unless he wished for death!

To end one’s life by poisoning, like Austin did, was the fate of those who betrayed the Wolf royal family.

As for why Donald didn’t reveal these facts to Henry but personally pressed him for information instead, I wasn’t quite certain, but I wasn’t foolish enough to expose Donald’s tactics either.

"The Spirit Dragon Pack resides far to the west, by the Lilo Sea. Could it be that the long distance is the reason Levi didn’t come voluntarily?" Henry seemed to have wracked his brains for a plausible explanation.

He suddenly paused as if another idea had struck him, and attempted an analysis, "However, tomorrow is your and Margaret’s grand wedding ceremony. Perhaps Levi is simply still on the road, unable to respond to us in time, right? It’s not that he doesn’t want to come."

"You, cousin, may not have been in much contact with him, but Aunt Licia has always been kind to us youngsters. Levi certainly wouldn’t overlook Aunt Licia’s face, would he?"

Upon hearing this, Donald slowly nodded, seemingly accepting Henry’s analysis, and replied faintly, "Let’s hope so. Speaking of which, I kind of miss him. The last time we saw him was at my Lycan King coronation, wasn’t it, brother?"

As I observed the exchange between Donald and Henry with an impassive face, a sense of oddity gradually surfaced within me. If Donald was testing Henry, wasn’t he being overly obvious?

With someone as perceptive and meticulous as Henry, there was no way he wouldn’t notice Donald’s probing! As to why Donald insisted on doing so, I was equally keen to know the answer.

But clearly, Henry and I were both in the dark about his reasons.

Henry seemed to freeze for a moment, locking gazes with Donald, and only after a long while did he manage a forced smile, saying, "It must be so. It’s truly been many years since we last saw Levi, how time flies!"

As he spoke, a strange look began to flicker in his eyes as he peered at Donald, filled with puzzled inquiry, "But why do you suddenly call me ’brother’? It sounds so ominous. Are you not feeling well? Tomorrow is your wedding, neither you nor Margaret can afford any problems!"

Thinking that Henry had finally caught onto something and might reveal something else, I remained silent, "..."

Donald, looking at Henry with a face of sincerity, stayed motionless but subtly arched his elegant eyebrows, "..."

"What’s wrong? Is there something on my face?"

Donald and I looked at Henry so intently and obviously that he touched his face and asked in return.

I believed Henry was likely without fault, for if he had truly done something to betray Donald, a reaction would surely have been triggered just now! Anyway, I didn’t see any signs of guilt or secrecy in him.

But if he had been acting just now, deceiving both me and Donald, it would be terrifying indeed...

Donald valued him above many others; I hoped he would not turn out to be like Austin or Angel.

"It’s fine now; once you’re done eating, you’d better leave quickly, you still have many things to take care of this afternoon, don’t you?" Donald’s skill in dismissing people was always profound.

Henry looked astonished as he glanced at Donald and then hesitantly pointed to the steak on the table that he hadn’t finished, stammering, "How can you be so cruel as a host? I’ve been toiling for a week over your affairs, and yet to be so heartlessly dismissed after just one meal?"

chapters.title: Chapter 297: Lost contact with people
chapters.content:
Chapter 297: Lost contact with people

[Margaret’s POV]

Although Henry verbally criticized Donald for his lack of compassion, he still ended up doing a lot of things in the afternoon.

By around 5 p.m., as I looked at the chat interface on my phone, chatting with Elizabeth, and the text I had sent two hours ago asking where they had arrived, which seemed to have disappeared into the void, a sense of unease slowly began to surface in my heart.

I raised my hand and pressed against my suddenly twitching and tightening heart, then casually dialed Elizabeth’s number...

But even after the ringing tone in the phone had finished playing, Elizabeth still did not answer my call. I frowned and pressed the end call button, trying to use the mind-link to contact Elizabeth and Armstrong!

[Elizabeth, where are you right now? Why aren’t you returning my messages and calls?]

[Armstrong, are you with Elizabeth right now? Where are you?]

Just like when I tried to contact them on the phone half a minute ago, there was still no response from Elizabeth and Armstrong.

I clenched my phone, wanting to go to the palace next door and discuss this matter with Donald, but I had barely reached the doorway when Nora and Fran stormed in so suddenly that I nearly got knocked to the ground!

"Oh my goodness! Margaret, are you okay? Your dad and I didn’t hit you, did we?" Nora had just stepped into the room when she saw me almost falling backward, so she asked anxiously.

Frantz initially looked irritated, but after seeing me, he gave me a surprised glance, his eyes also conveying a worried inquiry.

I was actually just a bit startled, my instinctive aversion reflex hadn’t caused me any harm.

I gently shook my head and calmly answered Nora, "I’m fine, Mom! Shouldn’t you and Dad be resting in your room, preparing for dinner later? Why are you suddenly here, looking so frantic?"

As I spoke, I also glanced sideways at Frantz, a flicker of different emotions crossing my mind.

"Your sister and Armstrong have been out of contact for the whole afternoon! It’s one thing for you not to notice, but you’re still thinking about dinner later? How can you be such a cold and heartless sister? Margaret, you’ve truly disappointed me so much!"

Before Nora could say anything to me, Frantz, who was beside her, already snapped at me furiously, turning on me in record time!

I turned to look at Frantz, my brows tightly knitted together.

I took a deep breath, holding back my helplessness and disappointment, and asked Nora, "What time did you last get in touch with Elizabeth? Let me see if it matches up with the time on my end."

Nora shot Frantz an irritated look but didn’t speak up for me; instead, she turned to me with a hint of apology, "It was at 10 o’clock this morning, I asked when she and Armstrong would arrive in Royal City."

"She said they should arrive by 4 p.m., but now it’s already 5 o’clock, and we still haven’t seen them. We can’t reach them through mind-link or phones!"

"What on Earth is going on? Elizabeth never fails to return my messages; could they have had an accident?"

Listening to Nora’s frantic and worried questions, I quickly reviewed the chat history with Elizabeth.

The last message from Elizabeth was at 12:04 p.m.!

That means when Nora called Elizabeth, the people of the Silver Moon Pack must have still been on their way normally, and they lost contact after noon, after Elizabeth sent me that text.

But that heavy feeling in my heart didn’t ease at all. I took a deep breath, turned to Nora and Frantz, and explained, "Before you came in, I had just confirmed the fact that Elizabeth and Armstrong’s group had lost contact. I was just going to find Donald to discuss this matter. Now that you’re here, let’s go together to talk to him."

Nora, her expression one of urgent concern, listened to my words as if she had finally found a solid pillar to lean on, taking a very subtle deep breath, and then she seemed to realize something...

She looked up at me, her eyes filled with a mixture of questions and guilt, "So you were about to leave this room because you’d also found out about this?"

Hearing this, I couldn’t help but look at Nora with a touch of resignation, and then at Frantz, whose complexion was now flushed with embarrassment, finally realizing that he had lashed out at me without understanding the situation.

"Elizabeth is my sister, too. My concern for her is no less than that of you and Dad. So there’s really no need for you to accuse me so hastily..."

chapters.title: Chapter 298: Bad news
chapters.content:
Chapter 298: Bad news

[Margaret’s POV]

"I would also like to know what happened between her and Armstrong this afternoon."

I said these two sentences very calmly, lifted my foot to step around Nora and Frand, and headed straight out the door.

The sound of Nora and Frand’s footsteps following me did not come until several seconds later, and I didn’t have to look to know what their expressions were—nothing more than impatience mixed with a superficial sense of guilt, no doubt.

The impatience was naturally for my beautiful sister Elizabeth, while the shallow guilt was compensation for having just misunderstood me and given me an earful.

Scenes like this I had experienced countless times before, but still somehow couldn’t get used to.

To admit that one’s own parents love someone else more than they love you is apparently a very difficult thing, even though that ’someone else’ is not a stranger, but my own sister whom I deeply love.

The situation was very urgent, and I did not have the spare time or energy to dwell on these grievances; I had to forcefully suppress them at the bottom of my heart. At this moment, connecting with or finding Elizabeth and their group was the most important thing!

Walking at the forefront with agile and quick steps, Nora and Frand trailed behind me, seeming to want to say something, but I had neither the time to entertain them nor the desire to respond much.

"Margaret? I was just about to go to your room to find you, how come you are here at this time?"

Donald’s voice came from the corridor ahead, which brought my attention back, and as I looked up, my footsteps didn’t stop as I continued towards him.

"What’s wrong? Has something happened?" Donald, with just one glance, saw the tension and gloom in my mood, and, holding my hand gently, continued to ask in a warm voice.

At his words, I let out a light sigh, looked up into Donald’s eyes, and said gravely, "We haven’t been able to contact Elizabeth for nearly five hours now, and I am worried they have encountered some kind of accident on their way here!"

"What? You’re saying that Armstrong and his party haven’t arrived at the Royal City yet? That’s impossible, didn’t he tell me he would be here by four o’clock?" Donald, upon hearing this, immediately voiced his surprise in a questioning tone.

He looked over at the Minister of Internal Affairs Clark who was behind him, with a questioning and puzzled look that seemed like it would spill out.

Clark, as if intimidated by Donald’s gaze, trembled slightly and replied urgently, "Yes, Your Majesty, the Alpha and luna of the Silver Moon Pack indeed did not arrive at the scheduled time. This is the latest news from the city’s patrol team outside the Royal City. They were just about to report to you!"

"Read it," Donald said, not wanting to hear more of Clark’s explanation, his face cold as he uttered this single word.

Clark didn’t dare delay, and immediately proclaimed a terrible piece of news, "The patrol team received a distress signal on the outskirts of the city at noon. When they arrived at the source of the signal, they found the scene in complete chaos. After several hours of investigation and inquiry, they confirmed that the ones attacked were the Silver Moon Pack coming to attend the banquet..."

"The attackers seemed to have killed many of their pack members and took away all the bodies from the scene along with captives!"

As Clark conveyed this shocking news, the atmosphere in the room instantly froze to an icy standstill. I stared wide-eyed, nearly forgetting to breathe as I looked at Clark in astonishment!

’Killed many people’—what does that mean? Does it include my sister Elizabeth as well? Armstrong and Anthony are such brave and skilled werewolves, how could they let something like this happen?

No! This couldn’t be true! Elizabeth and Armstrong could not be dead so easily!

My mind was swirling with many doubts about the attack, yet at the same time, I was forcing myself to stay calm and use my reason to analyze everything.

Perhaps it was after being with Donald and always being influenced by his calm and perfect approach to things that I have now also become someone who can keep their cool. But standing behind me, Nora and Frand, who had been silently absorbing the information, would not be as rational and calm!

"What do you mean by that? Are you saying Elizabeth and the others were attacked? How is that possible? Weren’t they already within the jurisdiction of the Royal City? Why would an accident still occur? Who attacked them? And why? Why?"

"Aren’t you the Lycan Minister of the Wolf Royal Family, aren’t you supposed to protect our pack? How come you are only finding out about it now? Are you all useless? And what about your so-called patrol team? Why did they take so long to confirm who was attacked?"

"Is this what you call the supposedly superior genes of the Lycan of the Wolf Royal Family? Hah! You’re simply too disappointing!"

chapters.title: Chapter 299: Out of control
chapters.content:
Chapter 299: Out of control

[Margaret’s POV]

Upon suddenly hearing such devastating news, Frantz had completely lost his mind. He began attacking everyone present indiscriminately, including his own Lycan King son-in-law whom he had always held in high regard!

"Donald, what kind of monarch are you? Your Werewolf Royal City has been infiltrated by the enemy. Aren’t you embarrassed? How are you going to explain this to me? With what will you compensate me for the pain of losing my precious daughter and son-in-law?"

Like a madman, Frantz accused everyone in sight, especially Donald, standing amidst the crowd. A surge of bizarre thoughts crossed my mind as I turned and regarded him with a cold stare.

Nora seemed much calmer than Frantz, but by now, her eyes were bloodshot. She reached out to grip the somewhat hysterical Frantz, her voice hoarse as she urged him to calm down.

"No... perhaps things aren’t as serious as you think, Mr. Frantz! Could you please calm down for a moment? We will handle this situation as swiftly as possible. Luna Elizabeth and Alpha Armstrong might not have encountered disaster; they could still be alive!"

Clark’s less than convincing attempts to calm the situation quickly broke the oppressively tense atmosphere.

I took a shallow breath, suppressing all the doubts in my heart, and squeezed Donald’s hand, frowning as I looked toward Frantz, his face flushed with rage, and I said in a stern voice, "Losing your mind won’t save Elizabeth. If you still hope for your sister to come back alive, cool down right now! This is not the place for you to vent your madness."

"Elizabeth and Armstrong have been attacked; is that something Donald did on purpose? Instead of cursing those treacherous and despicable attackers, or thinking of ways to find Elizabeth and support them, you just throw a fit in front of Donald and me?"

"Are you the only one anxious here? While everyone respects you, that doesn’t mean you can act recklessly!"

My tone was frigid and fast as I spoke, leaving Frantz a few steps away from me looking as if he’d been struck dumb, just staring at me.

Frantz hadn’t expected such a forceful response from me, and it seemed that Nora hadn’t either, "Margaret! I... Your father is just overly worried about your sister, please don’t be so angry with him. He’s lost his senses in his rage, but we still need to calm down and figure out how to rescue Elizabeth and the others!"

Turning to Nora, who was still making excuses for Frantz, a struggle flickered inside me. Eventually, I couldn’t help but respond dryly, "He has indeed lost his senses, but you’re the one who’s been maliciously enabling him your whole life! Why are you so weak in front of him?"

"You’re such an intelligent and kind person, why have you never stood up to him? Not for my sake, but for your own, have you ever seriously communicated with him?"

"Sometimes, standing by idly can make one an accomplice, Mother, do you realize that?"

As I uttered these somewhat sarcastic words, a light laugh escaped me, unable to fully restrain the bitterness within. If Frantz’s open and unapologetic favoritism hurt me and Elizabeth, then Nora played a role in exacerbating it.

I was saddened by this favoritism and grieved by it.

I had never thought to compete for Elizabeth’s belongings; all I wanted was a basic sense of fairness.

Frantz and Nora might overlook me, but they shouldn’t be so biased and selfish when it comes to our sons-in-law!

Donald is my chosen mate and the monarch of the entire Wolf royal family. Why should he have to suffer such a nauseating injustice?

They should treat Donald as they did Armstrong before, that is my final line in the sand.

This was, in fact, the first time I had expressed my rebellion so forcefully. I was fed up with their self-righteousness, and I wouldn’t allow anyone to slander or insult my Lycan King!

"Margaret..."

That was Donald tenderly calling my name.

I turned and looked up at him, trying to muster a faint smile, and spoke in a gentle voice, "Sorry, I also lost control today. These matters shouldn’t have been brought up in front of so many people, but I just couldn’t hold it in."

"Don’t feel pressured, this isn’t the fault of the Lycan King alone. Nobody could have predicted this; if anyone is to blame, it’s the enemy for being so cunning and sinister!"

As I spoke, Donald just quietly watched me, his eyes deep and calm as if they could soothe all my bad moods.

"I know, and I don’t think this is my fault," he said softly, as though comforting me.

He sighed gently, a smile and a look of indulgence appearing in his eyes.

chapters.title: Chapter 300: Wedding Eve
chapters.content:
Chapter 300: Wedding Eve

[Margaret’s POV]

"But I really like the feeling of you protecting me, my brave baby! Thank you," Donald said, the tenderness in his wolfish eyes almost enough to drown me.

My tense and on-the-verge emotions were soothed, and after taking a deep breath, I replied wearily, "My dad was being too much just now; he should have curbed his bad temper a long time ago. Today, he certainly got a lesson."

Ignoring the changing expressions on Frantz and Nora’s faces, I frowned and turned my thoughts to other matters.

Donald gently held my hand without saying anything to interrupt my train of thought, but he stood unwaveringly by my side all along.

"There’s a lot about this situation that’s still very strange. I think Elizabeth and Armstrong, who we’ve lost contact with, are probably not in any real danger," I said in a grave tone.

It seemed Donald had thought of something too; he simply looked at me with a soft smile and softly said, "Oh? Why do you say that? What’s on your mind?"

Just then, the sound of the wind came from outside the hallway. It looked like there was going to be a heavy downpour tonight. Donald and I stood in the middle of the corridor, surrounded by many people waiting for us to address how to deal with this sudden attack.

"Tomorrow is the wedding ceremony between Donald and me, and the patrols of the Royal City will only be tighter, not slacker! Therefore, bypassing all these strict patrols and striking the people of the Silver Moon Pack with precision is not something ordinary rebel organizations can achieve. They struck viciously with meticulous planning, and there must be further moves at play!" I voiced my analysis. When I looked up and met Donald’s gaze again, I saw his eyes filled with agreement—he obviously thought so, too.

The enemy chose this moment to covertly attack the usually inconspicuous Silver Moon Pack and taunt the Royal City’s patrol guards like a mockery. It was apparent that their underlying motives were quite clear!

They were either targeting me or the wedding ceremony tomorrow, or perhaps, it was both!

"Our Silver Moon Pack rarely makes enemies, let alone big ones. To carry out such an act without giving themselves away at all, their goals must be significant! And until they can benefit from me, Elizabeth and Armstrong are sure to be safe."

"There is no such thing as unwarranted malice in this world. Since they’ve done this, sooner or later, they will come forward to bargain with us!"

As I spoke these words, I slowly exhaled the stale breath from my lungs and with a cold glance at Frantz reminded him in a firm voice, "Until then, all of us must remain calm. We don’t want to end up without any information and, in the confusion, actually end up helping the enemy!"

Donald, sensitive to my incipient irritability, tightened his grip on my hand a little while continuing to speak calmly, "This is no simple sneak attack, but rather a provocation aimed at Donald and me. The wedding ceremony is imminent, and all of us must keep our composure, not be led by the nose by the enemy."

Donald looked to Chris and Clark with a glance, then after a moment of silence, continued, "David’s patrol guards won’t be this passive and incompetent. He’s definitely leading his men in investigating this matter right now. Here’s what we’ll do: before the wedding ceremony tomorrow, I promise I’ll give everyone an account of this."

"I will also do my utmost to protect the lives of every guest who comes to our Royal City for the feast, ensuring that such an incident doesn’t happen again. And you, Dad, can rest assured of this! I know how deep the bond between Elizabeth and Margaret is. I’ll make sure she arrives unharmed to attend her sister’s and my wedding; every person of the Silver Moon Pack is my guest."

The words of the Lycan King always carried weight, at least more convincing than I, the Lycan Queen.

While I didn’t quite know what Donald was planning next, I had unwavering faith in my mate. He was never one for making empty promises or deceiving others.

If he said he would do something, then he would certainly achieve it.

As for those calculating, despicable characters lurking in the crowd, plotting with malice, you’re doomed to fail! Evil never prevails over good, a truth that holds in this world.

Donald then issued several royal decrees to Chris and Clark, his strikingly handsome features exuding an unquestionable aura of authority and dignity from beginning to end!

Even though Frantz and Nora were convinced by his words, still harboring some concerns and unease, they finally stopped causing a nuisance, and I gradually let out a sigh of relief.

Half an hour later, the heavy rain descended upon the Royal City at night, fierce as if it were cleansing something away.

chapters.title: Chapter 301: The Silver Moon Pack that was attacked on March 1st
chapters.content:
Chapter 301: The Silver Moon Pack that was attacked on March 1st

[Elizabeth’s Perspective]

As my blurry consciousness gradually returned to my body, so came an unbearable agony!
