I think my arm must have been snapped off; the people who attacked us yesterday afternoon were ruthless, showing no mercy whatsoever.

I slowly opened my eyes, and the first thing I saw was Armstrong’s bloodied face. He was still unconscious, his breath through his nostrils very faint, but thankfully, I was certain he was alive.

I lifted my gaze toward the only source of light in the cabin, a three-meter-high iron window. Sounds of branches swaying were barely detectable from outside the window, and in addition, I could smell the earthy scent of soil, freshly washed by a downpour.

Had Armstrong and I been taken by the attackers to some secluded forest?

The excruciating pain in my arm made me wish for death. I suppressed the urge to cry out and gingerly kicked Armstrong, who was lying across from me, "Armstrong? Wake up! Can you hear me, can you wake up, please? I’m so scared Armstrong..."

When I called out Armstrong’s name, I realized just how hoarse and unpleasant my voice sounded, but at a time like this, I couldn’t be bothered by that. I needed to wake him up first; otherwise, alone, I would have no chance of managing our current predicament!

"Armstrong..."

"Bang!"

But before I could really wake Armstrong, the sound of footsteps came from outside the cabin, followed by the loud noise of the door being kicked open!

I was scared stiff for a second by the noise, instinctively closing my eyes, pretending I hadn’t woken from unconsciousness.

But our enemies were clearly not so easily fooled. A few seconds later, I heard a mocking sneer and a chilling voice said, "Since you’re awake, we can sit down and have a good ’talk,’ now can’t we? The respected Alpha of the Silver Moon Pack and Luna!"

Right as the other side’s words ended, I thought I heard Armstrong snort softly.

"Cough, cough, what would you like to talk about, oh exalted leader of the despicable Rebels?" Armstrong’s response was in a low, graveled voice as if scraped forcibly from his throat.

I sharply looked up at Armstrong across from me, glad that he had woken up, "You’re awake Armstrong! Are your injuries okay? Can you sit up now?"

Armstrong’s eyes glowed a blood-red, but his gaze upon me was gentle, I could even read a firm reassurance in them; he was trying to give me a sense of safety.

Looking at Armstrong, covered in blood, I could hardly control the tears that started to fall.

Seeing this, Armstrong just gave me a comforting smile before he continued to speak to our captor, "You dare provoke the entire Wolf royal family. The Lycan King and Queen will not let you go unpunished. You’d better brace yourself, for the wrath of the Lycan King is not something trash like you can bear!"

While speaking, Armstrong tried to shift his body a little to speak more directly to our captor. Being so close to him, I could almost immediately smell the blood on him!

Indeed, in the cruel battle yesterday afternoon, he and Anthony fought the enemy for hours to protect us. His wounds were all from that time, and Anthony...

’Is Anthony already dead?’ I asked myself.

But it seemed there was no answer to that question, as the last moment I was conscious yesterday, I seemed to see Anthony collapsed in a pool of blood; he had fallen mere steps away from me in the muddy ground!

"Really? You think that with such an impotent warning I would be frightened enough to release you? Oh my respected Alpha, you are just as ’naive’ and ’adorable’ as your luna, hahaha!"

As the leader spoke, he suddenly burst into a maniacal laugh, as if he had heard some incredibly amusing joke.

"I’m not afraid to tell you that the so-called Wolf royal family you rely on will soon no longer exist! And the Lycan faction led by Donald is about to be wiped out! This corrupt and filthy world is about to welcome its new overlord—the ’MASTER’—and we will establish a new order and future. ’MASTER’ will surely replace Donald!"

Armstrong and I listened quietly to the enemy’s blasphemous words, but inside, a tidal wave of emotions stirred fiercely.

So the enemy’s goal was not only to disrupt the Queen’s coronation ceremony today, but they intended to use this opportunity to officially declare war on the entire Wolf royal family!

Looking at the leader’s maniacal behavior, a sense of foreboding suddenly surged in my heart. They had ambushed the Silver Moon Pack yesterday and captured Armstrong and me, undoubtedly planning to use us as leverage against Margaret and the Lycan King!

chapters.title: Chapter 302 Sleepless Nights
chapters.content:
Chapter 302: 302 Sleepless Nights

[Margaret’s Perspective]

The torrential rain had poured down all night, and both Donald and I hadn’t slept at all—or to put it plainly, it was a sleepless night in the Royal City.

Because of the rainstorm, many werewolf residences on the outskirts of the Royal City had collapsed, as well as localized flood disasters occurring; Donald had barely closed his eyes as he busied himself handling all sorts of official matters and remotely directing ministers to rescue the werewolves.

I watched the faint red blood vessels in the whites of his eyes with a tinge of heartache, but there wasn’t much I could do to help him besides brewing coffee, staying by his side, not going anywhere.

"I understand, stay there and continue to pacify the people, don’t give opportunists any chances, understand? I will ask the Lycan Medical School to send assistance as soon as possible, you all keep a close watch on the situation there."

"What do you mean ’whether today’s royal wedding can still be held’ or ’how to explain things to the press and media’? If you ceremonial ministers can’t even handle a little emergency like this, then step down early and let someone else take your place, alright?"

"All of you need to think more clearly; I don’t want to hear such idiotic questions again."

"Notify David to report the latest investigation progress to me in ten minutes!"

"..."

Donald’s phone had been ringing non-stop, and ministers constantly surrounded him. I placed the steaming cup of coffee by his side and with a sideways glance, signaled to Clark, who was standing there dazed, to step out first.

Clark exhaled heavily, as if pardoned, casting me a thankful look before he turned and left; he had just been scolded bloodied by Donald over the disaster relief matter and now couldn’t wait to get away from this place!

Donald, who had just finished a mind-link with someone, was about to ask Clark about something when he realized the man had already left. "Where’s Clark? I had other things to talk to him about; why did he leave first?"

I gave Donald a tender smile, gently smoothed the furrow between his brows with my hand, and said softly, "Clark already has several tasks underway; let him off this time. Mark and Louis are outside the door, aren’t they? I’ll have Ruth call them in. You can have them do whatever you need."

As I spoke, I beckoned Ruth over, gesturing for her to call the others.

Donald naturally wouldn’t object to my kindness. After heaving a sigh, he grabbed my hand and kissed it, saying gently, "I never expected last night’s downpour would cause so many problems. You’ve been up with me all night; go get some sleep now; your wounds haven’t completely healed."

At his words, I simply shook my head softly, looked at Donald, and said quietly, "I’m fine, not even sleepy. But you haven’t taken a proper break in hours. Take this chance while there’s no one around to drink something and soothe your throat."

Donald wrapped an arm around my waist, looked up at me tenderly, "Having you here with me, I don’t feel tired at all. In a couple of hours, it’ll be our wedding ceremony. You should go rest for a while, get ready, and dress up. I can hardly wait to see you in your wedding gown in the hall!"

"Today, you are my bride alone; it feels so good."

While speaking, Donald added this sentence, his handsome face suddenly blooming with a silly warmth, which made my heart tender. I leaned down and kissed his beautiful eyes, nodding to show I understood.

But before Donald could release the arm hugging me, his phone on the desk rang again. I glanced to the side; the caller was David.

David was the captain of the Royal City Patrol Team. He was in charge of the investigation into the sudden attack on Elizabeth and Armstrong yesterday. Seeing him calling Donald now, my steps suddenly felt heavy and unable to move.

No one could be more concerned for Elizabeth’s safety than I was. My beautiful and pitiful sister, she mustn’t have any mishaps!

Donald, of course, understood what was on my mind. He looked at me, his eyes full of reassuring tenderness, then slid open his phone:

"Your Majesty, it’s David! We’ve just found a survivor from the Silver Moon Pack in the oak forest southwest of the Royal City. He says his name is Anthony, and he now requests to speak directly with our Lycan Queen. What do you say?"

Upon hearing David mention the name Anthony, Donald and I both paused briefly. I exchanged a knowing look with my mate, then said in a stern voice, "Give him the phone now, let him speak."

"Yes, Lycan Queen!" David did not hesitate at all in the face of my command.

Then, I heard the familiar voice of Anthony!

chapters.title: Chapter 303: Anthony faked his death to escape 303
chapters.content:
Chapter 303: Anthony faked his death to escape 303

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"Margaret? Is that you, Margaret? You must find a way to save Elizabeth and Alpha, they were injured and kidnapped by a group of werewolf mercenaries..."

"Cough, cough, cough, I feigned death to escape from their grasp, and you and Lycan King must find a way to save them! These mercenaries are strong, unlike any we or Alpha have ever seen before, and they also call themselves the Rebels!"

"I hope to provide you with some useful information. You must save Elizabeth; she was injured in the battle! Alpha as well..."

The voice of Anthony was intermittent, and his tone was very weak and powerless. I think if not for his strong will that kept him going until our rescue arrived, he should be unconscious and lying on the ground right then.

Upon hearing Anthony mention that Elizabeth was injured in the battle, my heart felt as if it were fiercely grasped in an instant. I urgently asked Anthony, "Do you know where they took Elizabeth and Armstrong eventually? In which direction did they possibly go? Are Elizabeth’s injuries severe? Where was she hurt?"

While I anxiously questioned Anthony, Donald had already tightly held my hand. He could always keenly detect when my emotions were disturbed, "My love, calm down. Now that we have found Anthony, we will follow this lead and surely find Elizabeth and the others. Don’t worry, I’m here for everything!"

After considering briefly, Donald then instructed David, "Now, ensure Anthony’s safety and get him back to the Palace first, then deploy someone to continue tracking the whereabouts of the Silver Moon Pack. Inform me immediately if there is any news."

"Moreover, the royal wedding ceremony is about to start soon, and the Royal City Patrol Team you lead cannot be negligent at this time! I have a premonition that today’s ceremony will not go smoothly."

Donald said this and looked up at me, his beautiful eyes flashing something inscrutable. I opened my mouth to say something to him but was quickly interrupted by David over the phone.

"Understood, Your Majesty! Anthony just passed out, and I’m bringing him back right now," David responded aloud.

The words I wanted to say were choked in my chest. Looking at Donald, my eyes were somewhat tearful. At that moment, I was completely clueless about what to do, but thankfully, Donald was still as rational and calm as ever, my only pillar of support!

I dared not think about the pain of losing Elizabeth, so I forced myself to stay strong and believe in my God-like mate.

At that time, Donald’s thoughts were the same as mine; he rose and embraced me, his tone tender yet indisputable, "Trust me, everyone is going to be safe today. Our wedding will proceed perfectly and smoothly."

I buried myself entirely in Donald’s embrace, nodding tearfully in agreement.

And it was only after everything that day came to an end that I truly understood the immense pressure my mate had been under! Of course, all of this was still to come.

Donald did not stay and cuddle with me for long, for he had many things to handle, and at that moment, all I could do was take care of myself, not distract him or hold him back, and at least be his beautiful and dignified bride, which was comparatively a simpler task.

When the first bell of the afternoon rang in the great hall, I knew the moment had finally arrived—the grand and solemn wedding of the century between me and Donald!

Ruth was very anxious behind me, as if she were the one entering the ceremony hall, and through the huge mirror in front of me, I looked at her and gently reassured, "Don’t be so nervous, everything will be fine."

Upon hearing this, Ruth looked at me with a worried gaze, biting her lip and anxiously responding, "How could I not be nervous, Lycan Queen? I am both happy for you and worried about what might happen next..."

"Miss and Alpha have not had any news up to now, and I’ve also heard from ministers outside that many guests have not arrived for today’s wedding ceremony; they do not endorse your wedding with His Majesty and are making a final collective protest!"

"Also, the werewolf packs in the suburbs seem unsatisfied with the Royal Family’s post-disaster handling—they’re causing quite a stir now! I have a very unsettling feeling, constantly feeling that today..."

I raised a hand to lightly touch the beautiful and pure white veil on my shoulder, calmly and irrefutably stating, "Don’t worry, no one can stop my and Donald’s wedding. This is a moment I have long awaited, and I will not allow anyone to ruin it."

chapters.title: Chapter 304 wedding first challenge
chapters.content:
Chapter 304: 304 wedding first challenge

[Margaret’s Perspective]

As if intimidated by the powerful aura emanating from me, Ruth’s mouth hung open slightly, and it took her a good while to slowly find her voice back. She looked at me with determination and nodded, then finally replied, "Yes, Lycan Queen Your Highness, no one will stop you and His Majesty from walking together side by side. We will all certainly protect you, and I will too."

Upon hearing this, I smiled gently, looked back at her, and said in a soft voice, "Go get the handgun I placed in the drawer. It’s time for us to go out."

"Hand... handgun? Your Highness, what do you need a handgun for now?" Ruth asked with some surprise.

At that moment, I just looked at her gently and firmly, without offering any further explanation.

Ruth saw that I didn’t answer, and quickly realized the reason behind it. Given the tense and perilous atmosphere of today, it was understandable for me, as one of the main characters of the event, to make such a precaution.

I counted the bullets in the handgun and quickly hid it within the heavy and luxurious folds of my gown. I think I should perhaps thank my dress designer—for designing my gown so beautifully and lavishly that, even if I hid a few handguns in it, outsiders wouldn’t notice at all and would only see its sacred and solemn appearance!

At 2 p.m. in the afternoon, the chimes of the Royal City Hall rang out once more. I emerged from the flower and ribbon-laden hall corridor, holding in my hand a blindingly white bouquet, and step by step, I approached Donald on the opposite side.

My mate today was particularly radiant, even though he had stayed up all night and was rushed off his feet up until the last second.

Donald was dressed in the white bespoke suit I had chosen for him earlier. With his tall figure, broad shoulders, and narrow waist, he wore the white suit exquisitely, standing out as the most dazzling presence in the crowd.

The sunlight was also gently shining on him at this time; he truly was the God-like mate of my dreams.

For a moment, I felt as if my tears were going to fall straight down, but at this very moment, both my eyes and face clearly showed a happy and beautiful smile. No matter how many setbacks and hardships we had encountered along the way, and no matter how many difficulties and dangers awaited us to face together in the future, at least in this moment, in our hearts and eyes, there was only each other.

My dear Lycan King Donald, I could finally marry him as I had wished, become his rightfully destined mate, and the one and only Lycan Queen of his life!

The MC who was hosting this grand wedding was still warming up the crowd when Frantz, who had been standing by my side, came directly towards me at that moment.

I came back to my senses and glanced at him indifferently.

"Didn’t you and Donald say you would give us an explanation before the wedding? Where is Elizabeth now? And where is Armstrong? You haven’t just remembered to complete your wedding and completely disregarded your sister and brother-in-law’s safety, have you?"

Regarding Frantz’s initial accusations and questions, I felt no surprise at all in my heart; I already knew what he was going to say as soon as he opened his mouth. After all, besides me and Anthony, only he and my mother cared the most about Elizabeth’s safety.

Nora was just looking towards us with a worried expression. Out of respect for Royal City etiquette, she wouldn’t come over like Frantz and accuse me directly, but I could see the emotions in her eyes very clearly.

I quickly averted my gaze from Nora and tilted my head slightly, looking at Frantz and softly responded, "I know you are very worried about Elizabeth right now, but clearly, this is not the time to talk about it. Can you please cooperate with me to finish what comes next? As for Elizabeth’s matter, I..." ’will definitely not forget it’!

"Now is not the time to talk about this? Then when are you going to save your sister?"

I hadn’t finished my words when Frantz loudly interrupted me. Looking at him, I felt a tinge of sadness in my heart.

This father of mine has never considered anything from my point of view, not even now when it is one of the most important moments of my life, not even when we are surrounded by thousands of guests, not even when I am at this moment the most noble woman in the kingdom!

"I trusted you before, thinking that you would definitely find a way to find your sister. But look at you now! You don’t even have the slightest awareness of being a sister; is it that you only want to be the glorious Lycan Queen? I tell you, if you don’t bring Elizabeth to me immediately, you can forget me cooperating with your wedding today!"

"I want to see how you’re going to explain this in front of so many people! Hmph!"

Frantz’s face was clearly filled with anxiety and anger, as if he would devour me, his own daughter, if I didn’t agree to his demand in the next moment!

I just stood there coldly, looking at him, not wanting to explain anything further.

chapters.title: Chapter 305 ’I’ll take you with me
chapters.content:
Chapter 305: 305 ’I’ll take you with me

[Margaret’s Perspective]

My father had never truly understood me, nor had he ever loved me seriously, not even once. The things he was doing right now were extremely real and concrete evidence of this.

I hadn’t expected that the first "difficulty" of today’s wedding would actually come from my own kin, which was a terribly ironic turn of events.

"If both Elizabeth and I were in danger, you would choose to protect her directly, wouldn’t you? I really didn’t want to ask you this question directly, but now..."

I said, followed by a somewhat mocking laugh, then shifted my gaze away from Frantz and looked distantly towards Donald, who had been watching this direction the whole time, "It seems I don’t need an answer at all. Elizabeth is my only younger sister, and even if it costs me my life, I won’t let her come to any harm. You can rest assured about that."

Frantz seemed to be stunned by the question I raised earlier, and only after a long while did he inexplicably furrow his brows—he seemed unable to understand my disappointment in him.

But at this moment I didn’t care about any of that. Amidst the romantic background music that was playing, I paid no attention to Frantz’s threats and warnings. Instead, I looked resolutely at Donald and began taking steps, drawing closer to him little by little!

The guests at the scene seemed quite surprised by my actions, for in conventional societal weddings, it is the father’s role to be the last escort for his daughter, personally leading the bride to the groom.

I didn’t pay attention to the murmurs around me and walked determinedly towards Donald.

"Margaret! Have you gone mad? What are you doing?"

"Margaret?"

The exclamation of Frantz and Nora rose behind me, which I completely ignored, resolutely heading towards Donald, as if he were my ultimate, most certain choice.

Donald naturally didn’t miss the commotion and clamor from my direction. I watched as his handsome features gradually furrowed into a frown, and then he took long strides to meet me!

It turns out that in the eyes of the one who loves you, he can take the initiative to walk towards you.

Donald came closer and took hold of my hand, his eyes filled with concern, "What’s wrong? Did you argue with your father?"

I shook my head slightly while looking at him, my eyes misty, "It’s all unimportant, let’s go."

Donald glanced back at the two people behind me, but quickly returned his gaze and grasped my hand tightly, as if to embed me into his very bones, "Alright, I’ll take you away."

I felt at that moment that, aside from "I love you," this was the most touching declaration of love I had ever heard in my life. Donald always knew what I needed most.

Nobody knew just how much courage it had taken me to overcome this sudden incident. I had to admit that my parents had not loved me as much as I had imagined!

The music continued, and as Donald and I walked over the carpet strewn with flowers, applause and cheers slowly rose around us. The journey wasn’t long, but I felt incredibly satisfied and fulfilled.

So this was what it felt like to walk side by side with Donald.

Licia and Prince Henry, as well as the various ministers of the Royal City were sitting at the very front of the left side of the entire hall. When Donald led me up the steps at the center of the chapel, all eyes converged on us, including those of Licia and Henry.

The music faded, and what followed was the priest’s deep, solemn voice:

"Thanks to the gracious Moon goddess, in this holy and solemn moment, we are gathered here to witness the grand and romantic wedding of this couple."

"My most noble and incomparable Lycan King, do you take this woman before you—Margaret, to be your wife, to live together in the sacred bond of marriage, to love her, cherish her, comfort her, protect her, and to stay devoted to her for the rest of your life, through sickness and health, prosperity and adversity?"

As the priest chanted these matrimonial vows, his gaze was fixed on Donald. I was extremely nervous and excited, yet I inadvertently glanced at the priest, and it was this casual glimpse that helped me divinely save Donald in the following seconds!

One moment the priest was devoutly reciting the matrimonial vows for Donald and me, and the next, his eyes held a sudden change with a bone-chilling murderous intent!

"I do..."

Donald said this tenderly to me, and at that same moment, the priest quickly pulled a handgun from his wide sleeve and aimed straight at Donald’s head! There was also me, who with a loud shout, swiftly stepped forward and with an incredibly forceful karate chop knocked the priest’s gun-wielding wrist aside.

"Bang!"

The gunshot shattered all the romance and sanctity of the scene, signaling the end of the wedding ceremony.

"Ahhhh!"

"It’s an attack! Help!"

"Someone protect His Majesty and the Lycan Queen, we’re under attack!"

chapters.title: Chapter 306 Assassinations
chapters.content:
Chapter 306: 306 Assassinations

[Margaret’s Perspective]

In the midst of the surrounding gasps and cries for help, I swiftly knocked the gun out of the priest’s hand, and Donald, quickly regaining his composure, subdued the would-be assassin with a sudden and precise elbow strike.

I kicked the fallen handgun away and looked sideways at Donald and this ’priest’.

With a menacing look, Donald coldly interrogated the attacker, "Who sent you? Speak now, or I will kill you this instant!"

This person, who’d dared to brazenly attempt to assassinate the Lycan King, was naturally not intimidated by Donald’s threat. With a look of deep resentment, he glanced at Donald and scoffed with irony, "If I feared death, I wouldn’t be here today! Donald, even if you kill me right now, the outcome of this day will not change. ’MASTER’ will replace you and lead us towards a new future!"

"Is that so? Then die first!"

With a crisp ’snap’, Donald broke the man’s neck without any hesitation, not bothering to conceal the anger in his eyes. I looked on worriedly and said, "Calm down, Donald, don’t let them disrupt your judgement."

Upon hearing this, Donald stood up and looked at me with a hint of a smile, but his words were ice-cold, "Don’t worry, baby, I’m very calm. I just don’t like others ruining our wedding. These people all deserve to die!"

As he spoke, a burst of intense gunfire suddenly came from outside the chapel and we both turned our heads in that direction almost simultaneously.

Meanwhile, from the VIP seats on the left side of the chapel, came a familiar yet utterly loathsome voice.

"Oh? Why has the wedding suddenly stopped? Are we no longer continuing? Is it time for the priest to ask if there’s anyone present who objects to this marriage? Hahaha!"

Lillian burst into deranged laughter, her eyes filled with rage and hate as she looked at me and Donald. In her hand, she held a lady’s pistol, aimed straight at the temple of Licia!

Several Lycans who were protecting Licia had been killed by Lillian at some unknown time. Now, the only ones left around the two of them were some ministers with no power to fight, and the paralyzed Prince Henry, who likely couldn’t even defend himself.

I stared at Lillian with my brows deeply furrowed, "You actually managed to escape from the surveillance of so many Lycans, I’ve underestimated you! Do you realize who you’re aiming your gun at? The Empress Dowager was the only person who personally protected you after Austin’s death. Is this how you treat your benefactor?"

"Lillian! I advise you to let go of my mother right now, otherwise, I can’t guarantee that my next bullet won’t blow your brains out!" Donald, unlike me, was not patient, his concern for Licia was clearly evident.

I glanced at Donald out of the corner of my eye, my fingers clenched tightly around the hem of my dress, which concealed a gun as I frantically assessed the situation.

It was truly strange for Lillian to show up here all of a sudden, I mean, without anyone’s help from behind!

As I thought this, my gaze quietly fell on Licia.

In the face of a gun pressed against her temple, my mother didn’t seem panicked at all; I even had a strange intuition that Licia had everything under control.

Why was Licia so intent on protecting Lillian, even at the cost of sacrificing so many Lycans? What was she thinking, and what was her ultimate goal?

I couldn’t fathom why Licia was doing this, but I dared not doubt her love for Donald. I didn’t want Donald to be betrayed by his own kin as I had been—betrayed!

"Fine! Try it then, and we’ll see whether you kill me first or if I kill our most esteemed Empress Dowager here first!" Lillian’s response to Donald was filled with excitement and madness. Watching her, I was suddenly reminded of something very familiar.

That’s right, hadn’t there been a similar situation with Angel before?

I reached out and took hold of the hem of Donald’s coat, speaking in a calm and low voice, "Don’t take such risks with mother. Let’s ask her what she wants first."

Donald obviously heard my suggestion, and after clenching his molars and exchanging a look with Licia, he finally spoke, "I can give you a chance to negotiate with me, I hope you’re smart."

"Be clear, if you dare harm a single hair on my mother’s head, it’s highly unlikely that you will walk out of this chapel alive today," I added, reinforcing the implied threat in Donald’s unfinished statement.

Lillian glared at me and Donald with fury, her chest heaving wildly several times before she managed to suppress her overwhelming emotions. She sneered and replied, "My demand is simple, return my brother’s body to me, and then let us leave the Royal City safely."

As I had suspected, Lillian’s obsession was still with Austin. She had indeed been profoundly devoted to her brother.

chapters.title: Chapter 307 transaction
chapters.content:
Chapter 307: 307 transaction

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Lillian had proposed her exchange condition, which Donald and I naturally heard loud and clear. I silently watched this deranged woman, while Donald also refrained from responding to her immediately.

A deathly silence spread between us, and just as Lillian was about to say something more, Henry, who had been silent and almost inconspicuous among us, suddenly spoke up:

"To exchange the Empress Dowager for a mere corpse, Your Majesty, what’s there to hesitate about? This woman has gone mad. Are you really willing to gamble with the Empress Dowager’s safety?"

Henry’s pale face was filled with worry and incomprehension as he spoke. He genuinely seemed very concerned that Lillian might suddenly go berserk and hurt Licia, but I found it odd that he would interject at this moment!

I hadn’t heard from Donald that Henry and Licia were particularly close...

While I was pondering these distractions, the situation on the field changed abruptly. Lillian, from seemingly nowhere, drew a sharp dagger and quickly slashed at Licia’s throat!

"Ah!" Licia let out a faint cry of pain as bright red blood immediately flowed down her neck, staining her collar, creating a sight that was truly alarming!

Shocked, I widened my eyes and shouted, "Stop, Lillian!"

"Stop! Don’t move, Lillian!" Donald’s reaction was much stronger than mine. He quickly drew his handgun and aimed it at Lillian with a face full of deadly ice.

I grabbed Donald’s sleeve and then stepped forward with hands raised, gesturing to Lillian to calm down, "Let’s calm down, Lillian. We haven’t said we won’t agree to your demand! Don’t hurt Mother now; whatever you ask, we’ll agree to it!"

Donald’s heavy breathing sounded like thunder in my ears. I knew he detested the feeling of being threatened, but given the current situation, we didn’t seem to have a better option.

My grip tightened on his sleeve, conveying an unspoken message. "Fine! I agree to your terms; you can take Austin away," Donald said with a heavy voice, conceding.

I barely released a sigh of relief and coldly watched Lillian from across the way.

"Heh! Look, isn’t everyone happy now? I don’t understand why you were so stubborn before! Go on, bring my brother out from the Holy Medical Pavilion, and I’ll wait here for you," Lillian instructed with a flicker of triumph on her face, arrogantly directing me and Donald.

"You seem to know quite a lot, even where Austin’s body is kept at the Holy Medical Pavilion!" Donald finished speaking and narrowed his eyes dangerously, and I knew exactly what he was thinking at that moment.

Donald had previously suspected the authenticity of Austin’s suicide by poisoning and had secretly handed over the body to Dean Andrew of the Holy Medical Pavilion early on. This affair was almost entirely confidential, but just now, without a second thought, Lillian blurted out this secret. How could this not arouse suspicions that someone was secretly helping Lillian?

Lillian seemed to realize something too, with streaks of discomfort crossing her face. She looked up defiantly at Donald and shrilly said, "Enough talk, get moving and do as I say! Also, I want a helicopter to leave this place, I want it in 5 minutes, along with my brother. You better deliver them both into my hands, you got that?"

Upon hearing this, Donald continued to mockingly chuckle but still turned his head to glance at Clark beside him, "Do as she says," Donald ordered.

Lillian was very pleased with Donald’s arrangements, the smugness on her face growing ever more apparent, matching the madness in the depths of her eyes.

She looked toward the guest seats on the other side of the hall and mockingly asked, "Margaret, feeling pretty good about yourself today? Though a marriage without the blessings of your own parents can’t be all that perfect, can it? Hahaha!"

"That spectacular argument you had with your father earlier, I didn’t miss a thing! Tsk tsk tsk, it’s actually quite pitiful..."

Though I felt indifferent to the enemy’s schadenfreude, I’ve never liked others mocking me. I snickered back, "Instead of worrying about me, you’d better think about how you’re going to get out of here in one piece."

Seemingly annoyed by my indifference to her verbal assault, Lillian became noticeably infuriated, but she quickly suppressed her emotions and continued, "You won’t always be so lucky. I’ll be waiting to see the pathetic sight of you falling from grace."

At that moment, I didn’t quite understand what Lillian meant by that, but I watched as her brows slowly furrowed.

Meanwhile, during our negotiation and entanglement with Lillian, the sound of gunfire from outside the hall had not ceased.

Suddenly, there was a rush of footsteps from outside the great doors of the hall, and out of the corner of my eye, I saw that it was Captain David, who we had spoken with on the phone earlier.

chapters.title: Chapter 308: People Behind the Scenes of 308
chapters.content:
Chapter 308: People Behind the Scenes of 308

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I found it strange that the person who came in was not Mark, because when the gunshot first sounded outside, it was Lycan Mark who had rushed out to check on the situation.

David’s footsteps were erratic, his face marked with a solemn and urgent expression, and his clothes were even stained with blood and mud!

He looked towards Donald on the stage, swallowed before he finally spoke, reporting, "Your Majesty, the patrol team was attacked by the Rebels on their way back. Half of the Lycan have been sacrificed, and we only made it back desperately with the cover of Captain Christian!"

"The Rebels broke through the west gate of the Royal City five minutes ago, and a large group of rogue looters suddenly surged from the suburbs. They must be in league with the Rebels outside the city. Their aim is to coordinate inside and out with the Rebels today, to divert the attention and forces of the Royal City! I am incompetent, for I failed to protect the Royal City and Your Majesty’s safety, please punish me!"

David said as he bowed his head, seemingly utterly disappointed in himself.

I initially wanted to ask David about Anthony’s situation, but seeing him like this, and considering the situation we were now facing, the words in my throat were completely choked off.

"Rogues appeared in the suburbs? When did this happen, and why didn’t Donald and I know anything about it?" I quickly regained my senses, caught the key information in David’s words, and asked in surprise.

At this point, David naturally dared not conceal anything and answered, "It was half an hour ago, I received a report from the Lycan who went ahead for disaster relief. Did they not report to you and His Majesty immediately?"

David also realized something was wrong with this situation. He subconsciously looked at Henry next to Lillian, thought for a moment, and then said loudly, "Isn’t Prince Henry the main official in charge of the suburb’s disaster relief? Your Highness, how could you not know about this?"

The things happening today were getting stranger by the minute. Donald and I almost simultaneously turned our heads towards Henry.

"Do you know about this, Henry?" Donald said, suddenly grasping my wrist and slowly shielding me behind him. His beautiful eyes were filled with scrutiny as he gazed at Henry across from him.

I looked up at Donald, suddenly feeling utterly helpless—a strange intuition told me that what was about to happen would not be something Donald could easily accept!

"Ha! What do you mean by that, Your Majesty? Are you suspecting me?" Henry retorted as if he’d heard some funny joke, then replied with a sneer.

Donald slowly narrowed his dangerous eyes, staring sharply, continued, "Should I not suspect you? Didn’t you tell me just 20 minutes ago that everything was going smoothly with the werewolf pack’s disaster relief in the suburbs? What is this situation now, with rogues suddenly appearing? Do you really not know what happened there?"

Faced with Donald’s relentless questioning, a crack suddenly appeared on Henry’s previously calm and composed face. He maneuvered his wheelchair forward a few steps, slowly lifted his face to look at Donald, a strange mocking look emerging in his eyes, "If I say I indeed do not know what this is all about, would you choose to believe me, Donald?"

In response to Henry’s counter-question, Donald showed no reaction on his face, but the hand holding mine unconsciously tightened. My wrist ached from his grip, yet I had no intention of pulling away. At that moment, my mate seemed to need a fulcrum for support.

An eerie silence fell over the scene. Neither Donald nor Henry spoke further. I glanced at Donald worriedly, then spoke out to Henry, asking, "Why?"

"Why what?" Henry asked with a laugh.

"Why help Lillian? What benefit is there for you in this? Aren’t you the prince of the entire Wolf royal family? Why would you stand with those rebels? Is Austin really more important to you than Donald?" Ignoring the bizarre smile on Henry’s face, I continued to inquire.

Seemingly surprised that I would ask such a question, the smile on Henry’s lips paused. He squinted at me and after several seconds, raised his handsome brows and replied with a smile, "You are much smarter and more perceptive than I imagined."

I sneered, frowning as I met Henry’s gaze, "Should I thank you for your ’compliment’?"

Upon hearing this, Henry suddenly burst into laughter. He spread his hands, his expression extremely pleased, as if he found my reaction very amusing.

"Why didn’t I realize you were so interesting before, Margaret? Oh Donald, my dear brother, you really have found yourself a perfect mate!" Henry then turned to look at Donald standing in front of me, his eyes brimming with deep and complex emotions, not as friendly as his words suggested.

chapters.title: Chapter 309 Coup
chapters.content:
Chapter 309: 309 Coup

[Margaret’s perspective]

Lillian seemed quite dissatisfied with Henry’s remarks at the time, snorting with sarcasm as she added, "Hmph! Her? Margaret, perfect? Henry, you really are funny!"

As Lillian spoke, she loosened her grip on the pistol, her eyes and brows full of impatience.

I watched her interaction with Henry, and all my suspicions were powerfully confirmed—they had indeed been in contact before!

And today’s events were very likely orchestrated by Henry behind the scenes for Lillian, as to the ultimate goal of Prince Henry, I still couldn’t guess at the moment.

If I could figure out and understand these things, Donald naturally wouldn’t have missed them. He had been silent since earlier, and I knew he needed time to accept the situation at hand, his cousin—Henry, was not as friendly and guileless as he had previously seemed!

"Shut your mouth, idiot!"

A harsh rebuke snapped me back from my wandering thoughts, and I focused my gaze on Henry and Lillian across from me.

Henry looked at Lillian with a cold expression, his eyes full of disdain and speechlessness, "Do you think acting this way makes you seem smarter? If it weren’t for Licia valuing you, do you think I would stoop to collaborate with you, an utterly foolish woman? Lured into revealing your ’aces’ just by a few words, can’t you be a bit smarter?"

After scolding Lillian, Henry slowly turned back to look at David, who was still kneeling on the ground, chuckled coldly, and continued, "I really didn’t expect you could survive and come back alive, let alone expose the elaborate scheme I have been designing for so long in front of Donald and Margaret!"

"But no matter, I’m a very generous person by nature. As for some people who are about to die, I can afford to be a bit kinder, don’t you think so, my dear brother!"

Faced with Henry’s continuous provocations and tests, Donald finally couldn’t hold back any longer, his gun’s muzzle ice-cold as he aimed at the other, and asked through clenched teeth, "Why are you like this too? I never thought you would betray me..."

"Hahaha! Is that strange, my dear brother? You’re just used to staying in the honey pot, unaware of how cruel the world truly is!"

Henry didn’t let Donald finish his words, interrupted abruptly, and burst into crazy laughter, like a true madman, "I’m fed up with the humiliation of being oppressed by you for so many years, you wet-behind-the-ears kid, why should you take over this vast kingdom right away? My father and I should be the masters of this country, Alex is just a ’thief’, he’s the one who stole everything!"

"Shut up! I won’t allow you to insult my father like that!"

Donald roared and was about to shoot Henry, and I felt a surge of immense danger in my heart, my body reacting almost faster than my brain, as I grabbed Donald by the neck and narrowly dodged the oncoming bullet!

’Bang bang bang!’ These were the sounds of bullets being fired in rapid succession.

"No! No, don’t!" This was Licia’s cry of alarm and attempt to stop it.

"Yes! That’s it, kill him! Whoever kills Donald, I will appoint as my chief minister, hahaha!" This was Henry’s maniacal roar.

After dodging the bullets, I didn’t hesitate, pulling Donald behind another pillar for cover. David and Louis almost simultaneously returned fire, and bullets poured down in the hall like raindrops, with sounds of bloodshed and cries of pain rising.

I quickly peeked out and saw that our side was suffering heavy casualties, while Henry was safely pushed back by the Rebels who descended like deities!

Looking at the situation at the moment, Donald and I could well understand that all this was a rebellion and assassination meticulously planned by Henry.

’He has been biding his time by Donald’s side for so many years, all for the sake of this moment, I suppose!’

I thought to myself, casually pulling out the pistol I had hidden in my dress earlier, tearing the overly bulky and heavy hem in two or three pulls, turning back to look at Donald’s face, I softly said, "Although that damn priest didn’t get to ask me, I still want to say, I do too."

Willing to spend this romantic yet perilous life together!

Seemingly not expecting me to say that, Donald’s expression stalled for a moment, then he smiled incredibly tenderly, "You are just too adorable, my dear, thank you!"

I smiled and averted my gaze, tightening my grip on the gun, "Don’t be scared, baby, I will protect you and always be with you!"

In the backdrop of a barrage of gunfire, Donald leaned down to kiss my eyes, love spilling from his stunningly beautiful eyes, seemingly about to submerge me completely.

"I will definitely give you a complete and romantic wedding, trust me. But first, we need to drive away this bunch of treacherous Rebels." Donald said.

I nodded, signaling that I understood, "What should we do next? The number of Henry’s Rebels is unclear, and relying solely on Royal City’s Lycan defenses seems insufficient to counter them, and we don’t know the whereabouts of Christian and Mark at the moment, it seems we can only rely on ourselves now!"

chapters.title: Chapter 310 guessed it
chapters.content:
Chapter 310: 310 guessed it

[Margaret’s Perspective]

After listening to my analysis, Donald squeezed my face in what seemed to be admiration, his lips curling into a slight smile as he said, "Darling, you’ve made great progress in your war analysis abilities, truly worthy of being my queen, Donald. However, you’ve overlooked one thing. This is the Werewolf Royal City, the most fiercely guarded of all. How could it possibly be easily breached by a ragtag bunch of Rebels?"

"Even if every Lycan by my side is stretched thin right now, don’t we still have the commanders of the Lycan army stationed at the edge of the Royal City?"

No sooner had Donald mentioned the Lycan army than I heard uniform footsteps outside the hall, followed promptly by a blanket of gunfire and the sounds of battle.

I glanced sideways at Donald and asked with a light laugh, "Even if you call them back on emergency orders, that would take at least 2 hours, wouldn’t it? Don’t tell me you have the power of foresight?"

I hadn’t expected Donald to have such a contingency in place, and this surely wasn’t good news for the Rebels led by Henry!

Holding his silver gun in one hand, Donald touched my face with the other and explained gently, "Your wedding is the most important event of my life. How could I not take precautions? Thankfully, I made these preparations in advance, otherwise we might have been in even more of a predicament today."

"I anticipated some difficulties, but I never imagined it would come to this. Henry... he really hid his intentions well! And Licia, I truly didn’t expect her to collaborate with Lillian."

Startled by Donald’s direct revelation about Licia, my eyes widened as I looked at him. I opened my mouth to say something, but his barely concealed sorrow stopped me, "How... how did you guess?"

At my words, Donald looked down at me with a wry smile, "Do I need to guess? Lillian was previously imprisoned by Licia. Without her indication, who else would have had the chance to approach Lillian? I think Licia was just too concerned about her relationship with Licia. She wanted to help Lillian get Austin’s body and allow them to leave the Royal City safely and never step into my domain again."

"Sometimes Licia is quite naive, which is why she was used by Henry and Lillian. However, considering her help for them, I don’t think Henry will actually harm her. That at least is a relief,"

"Why?" I almost instinctively retorted to Donald, "You’ve already guessed it all, haven’t you? Licia’s collusion with them is already exposed, and you can still be sure they won’t betray her and harm her afterwards?"

While I did not really agree with Licia’s methods this time, out of respect for her being Donald’s mother, I didn’t want her to get hurt and add to Donald’s grief or concerns.

Donald then looked deeply into my eyes before continuing to explain, "It’s my fault for not clarifying these secrets to you earlier. Besides being my mother, Licia is also Henry’s biological aunt."

"Licia is Henry’s aunt?" I repeated Donald’s words subconsciously, feeling my thoughts turn to mush in an instant.

"Henry’s mother is called Martha, Licia’s only biological sister. Of course, like Dan, Martha was also killed many years ago during the rebel warfare, so Henry is not just my cousin..."

"He is also your cousin on your mother’s side, the biological nephew of your mother?" I picked up where Donald left off, my surprise beyond words.

As I quietly digested this information, I slowly began to understand why Donald had said that Henry would definitely not hurt Licia.

Licia and Henry’s mother Martha were real sisters married to Alex and Dan, brothers of the Royal Family... This was an incredibly tragic fate!

I couldn’t imagine the extent of Licia’s pain following that war, the struggle for royal power was indeed cruel, but the betrayal of family beneath it was even more unbearable.

Just like the brotherly strife that seemed to be repeating history before us; Dan and Alex, Henry and Donald engaged in what appeared to be an endless fight to the death.

"I know what you’re thinking, dear, but now is not the time for hesitation. To show even a shred of mercy to our enemies is cruelty to ourselves and irresponsibility toward those we care about. Do you understand what I mean?" Donald’s words were spoken tenderly, as if he could see right through my soul, knowing exactly what I was thinking.

I certainly understood his thoughts and predicament; he had to do this. The burden he shouldered was not only as Donald but also as the monarch of the entire Wolf royal family. His position left no room for hesitation or mercy.

After making eye contact with Donald for a few seconds, I nodded to indicate my understanding. It was not the time to sit down for a meaningful conversation and peacefully sort everything out; we had to be ruthless!

Seeing that I had come back to my senses, Donald smiled gently and instructed softly, "Wait here and keep yourself safe. I will be back soon."

Looking at him, concern in my eyes but also slowly letting go of his hand, I said, "Understood, be careful yourself."

Turning my head towards David standing beside me, I ordered sternly, "Make sure to protect His Majesty."

chapters.title: Chapter 311 ’Rebirth
chapters.content:
Chapter 311: 311 ’Rebirth

[Donald’s Perspective]

I found that the cruel truth that Henry is the MASTER wasn’t as unacceptable to me as I had imagined.

Perhaps it’s because I’ve experienced too much of this lately, I’ve developed some sort of bizarre immunity to it.

In just half a year, I’ve undergone four unforgettable betrayals, beginning with my former capable assistant and excellent member of the personal guard force, Angel, followed by my childhood friend Austin, and now...

Now my own mother and my only brother have joined forces to rebel against me! In fact, at the moment I confirmed Henry was the MASTER, I doubted myself for a second. I wondered why everyone around me keeps betraying me one after another. Could it be my own fault?

Maybe, as Henry said before, I’ve just been in the honey pot for too long and forgotten how cruel the world really is?

But soon, all these questions and self-doubts in my heart were promptly stopped by Margaret, who is mostly very lucid and intelligent and can steadfastly offer me strength and confidence.

At this moment, I’m once again extremely grateful to have met her, my fated mate!

The battle between the Lycan Army and the Rebels outside the hall is extremely fierce, and the inside is no less intense. Of course, I’m not going to let Henry stay smug, the victory in this fight will ultimately belong to Margaret and me.

The first thing I did when I peeked out from cover was to complete my shift. My large and sturdy wolf form was a prominent presence in the entire hall, and that was exactly my aim. I wanted to attract everyone’s attention and quickly take down Henry!

It’s been a while since I last shifted, and Wolf is extremely excited at this moment, shouting that he wants to rush forward and bite the traitor, "Donald, let’s go, today I must take down that despicable traitor Henry! He deserves to die!"

I chuckled indifferently, only the murderous intent in my eyes was startling.

"Donald is over there! Kill him! Go!"

Henry’s kill order sounded ridiculously funny to me; I almost instantly spotted him amidst the crowd of Rebels. His figure seated in a wheelchair was very noticeable.

I moved swiftly towards him, turning into a silver-white streak of light, almost impossible to catch with the naked eye!

"Stop him! Kill him!" Henry knew very well what I wanted to do at this moment; his eyes narrowed dangerously, and he commanded the people around him.

With David and Louis powerfully covering my back, I was practically invincible in front of Henry’s Rebels. The Rebels, fierce and vicious though they might be, were a bit of a handful, but still not enough in face of absolute fighting power.

Like now, I casually swept aside those who stood in front of Henry with my sharp claws, my cold wolf eyes staring straight at Henry, "You’re not escaping today."

Henry seemed to not have expected my ferocity, and looked at me with some surprise, "This is impossible! Your fighting ability couldn’t have grown so fast, you before..."

He paused for a second, his gaze shifting past me to a huge pillar not far away. I slowly narrowed my eyes, a dangerous look on my face.

Henry was looking in the direction where Margaret was hidden!

"Margaret, so that’s the real reason behind your increase in strength, you’re really quite lucky!" Henry finished his previously unfinished words with a deep voice, and even a hint of envy appeared in his gaze as he met mine.

I didn’t really understand the reason for his emotion; I huffed coldly and moved forward another step, "There’s a lot you haven’t figured out. Don’t worry, Henry, this farce today will soon be over, and your ridiculous and boring declarations of rebellion will be a huge joke."

"Donald! You are truly arrogant..."

"So what? I have the power and grounds to be arrogant, unlike you, just a bug that forever lives in gloom and hatred!" Without giving Henry much chance to respond, I quickly cut him off, my lips curling into a mocking smile, continuing in a cold voice.

After appreciating the ’wonderful’ expression on Henry’s face, I continued, "Do you think I don’t know about the shameful tactics you’ve been playing behind my back? Your inferior Potions are like you, perverse and useless!"

As though he could no longer tolerate my continuous mocking and attacks, Henry’s red-rimmed eyes glinted fiercely as he snapped, "Shut up! You can’t possibly understand the grand significance of the ’Rebirth’, you shortsighted trash! The ’Rebirth’ is my greatest Potion innovation; it has given countless disfigured werewolves new hope, and people like you can’t comprehend it!"

So MASTER had named his damn perverse Potion ’Rebirth’; that truly was an extremely laughable joke.

Thinking this, I sneered even more in my eyes, "If such a Potion could actually heal disfigured werewolves, why don’t you use it on yourself first?"

chapters.title: Chapter 312: Choices of 312
chapters.content:
Chapter 312: Choices of 312

[Donald’s Perspective]

I laughed out loud without paying any attention to Henry’s livid expression, nor did I care about the never-ending sound of gunfire. "Are you really the kind of person who sacrifices for others? In the end, it’s because you’re also unsure of the potion’s stability and its ultimate effects, yet you can’t bear to give up the chance that it could help you rise again!"

"Greedy for power, you’ve recruited so many people to die for you—oh no! Saying they’re dying for you doesn’t seem quite accurate, more like they’re your cute ’lab rats’ for drug testing! Are they aware of your despicable and selfish thoughts? Do you need me to spread the word for you?" I suggested this seemingly kindly, while my eyes kept assessing the Guards still surrounding Henry.

These soldiers could hear my entire conversation with Henry. If they had a sliver of sober reason left, now would be the time for them to reconsider whether their respected MASTER was a petty man who had been using them from the start to the end!

However, I clearly underestimated the brainwashing Henry had done to them. A long while passed, and these people showed no reaction, seemingly determined to be Henry’s ’lab rats’.

I laughed with amusement, almost admiring Henry’s ability. "These ’lab rats’ are really quite obedient. Your talent in beguiling hearts is as strong as ever! The Rebels outside the door probably won’t be able to resist much longer. Do you have any other tricks up your sleeve? If not, then today you truly won’t be able to leave this place!"

Henry, contrary to his usual self, did not retort. His gaze towards me was full of resentment and depth, and he fell silent, apparently deep in thought.

Silence spread between us, and I watched the enemy in front of me patiently, without continuing to strike.

"Donald! You’ve already won this war, you..."

Upon hearing this familiar and dear voice, I tensed up a little, but I didn’t immediately pay her any attention.

Seeing my lack of reaction, Licia slowly emerged from the ranks of Lillian and the Rebels, her face full of guilt and reluctance. "Can you let Henry and Lillian go just this once? I know this is an unreasonable request, but consider it as a plea from your mother. Let Lillian take Austin and leave; I guarantee she will never appear before you and Margaret again, she will not affect your lives anymore!"

"As for Henry, he’s our only remaining family in this world. Can you spare him once more? For the sake of your mother and your Aunt Martha, can you spare his life?"

Licia’s wound at her neck had stopped bleeding. Although Lillian was a fool, she hadn’t really intended to hurt Licia before.

They were all acting, taking me and Margaret for fools.

I transformed into human form, slowly turned my head to look at Licia, while the gun in my hand remained aimed at Henry’s head. "Even though it’s a bit too late to ask now, I’m still curious to know, between me and Henry, who is actually your biological son?"

As if my question had pierced the softest part of her heart, Licia broke down in tears, shaking her head unconsciously and sobbing, "Ever since your father and your Aunt Martha died, you and Henry have been my only hope in this world. I can’t stand the pain of losing a loved one again. You understand me, don’t you Donald?"

"You used to like your Aunt Martha and your cousin too, right? For the sake of past kinship and warmth, can you give your cousin a chance?"

In my heart, I actually understood Licia’s difficulty, but that’s not how things should be settled.

This was no trivial family spat. Henry was the final push behind betraying the entire country, and disrupting the natural order of the werewolf world. If such a crime could be forgiven, how could the Wolf royal family ever demand other werewolf packs to submit?

Licia, now ensnared by what she called kinship, was completely blind to these obvious matters, but I couldn’t act recklessly without regard.

"Loyalty and kindness are the most basic qualities of a werewolf. Without even these two basic things, how can Henry claim to be a Prince of this country? You consider him a close family member in your heart, but how much has he considered you? Mother, I don’t want you to feel troubled or pained caught between us, but some things inevitably require a choice."

I slowly withdrew my gaze from Licia and continued in an uncompromising tone, "Now you can make a choice between him and me."

"Today, one of us between Henry and me will die. You don’t have a second option that will make everyone happy."

After hearing my words, Licia collapsed to the ground in a breakdown, crying with her hands covering her face.

Henry seemed to take a deep breath before bursting into laughter, looking at me with eyes red as if filled with tears, "The Lycan King of the Wolf royal family really is heartless through and through. Such disgusting traits make stand staying here any longer unnecessary."

chapters.title: Chapter 313 ’Newlywed Gift
chapters.content:
Chapter 313: 313 ’Newlywed Gift

[Donald’s Perspective]

Henry even looked back at Licia as he spoke, with a hint of fondness on his crazed face, "If my Martha were still alive... sorry, my dear aunt, I’m a bit preoccupied with my own troubles, so I can’t take you with me. I think we’ll be meeting again very soon."

No sooner had Henry finished speaking than Licia and I simultaneously sensed that something was amiss. My gaze darkened, and without any hesitation, I opened fire, aiming to take Henry’s life!

But evidently, Henry was faster than anyone else present. He crushed a vial of potion he somehow produced, and the moment the red liquid touched his skin, Henry let out a spine-chilling roar.

My bullet did hit Henry, but it caused him no injury, because, in that instant, he had completed his shift from human to wolf form!

Due to injuries, I had hardly ever seen Henry shift, and I didn’t even know he could do that.

Henry’s wolf form was huge and burly, black as ink—a beast far larger than any normal wolf!

It looked incredibly intimidating, as though it could kill a well-tested Lycan with a single swipe of its paw.

Looking into its eyes, I had a premonition: it was about to escape!

"Stop, Henry!" I bellowed, completing my shift in an instant, ready to grapple with him.

But just then, the sound of Margaret’s cry of pain made me stop in my tracks. I instinctively looked for my mate, while Henry seized the moment of my distraction and grabbed Lillian’s arm, bolting out of the hall in a rampage!

"My ’wedding gift’ for you—I hope you and Margaret will like it. Don’t thank me!" That was the last thing Henry said as he fled.

I furrowed my brow, but my body didn’t hesitate as I rushed towards Margaret. Faced with the choice between killing Henry and protecting Margaret, I decisively chose the latter.

Margaret, I don’t know when, had left her cover and joined the battle in the hall. It was she whom the gaunt Rebel with a fierce face had sent flying with a swipe, and now she lay curled up on the ground covered in rubble, writhing in pain.

Seeing her like this cut me to the core. I immediately swept a group of Rebels aside with my powerful tail and anxiously asked Margaret on the floor, "Are you hurt, Margaret? Where does it hurt?"

Upon hearing my voice, Margaret looked up at me with her pale face and explained in a panicked tone, "I’m all right, Donald. Go save Elizabeth and Armstrong! They’ve been tied to the base of the helicopter, which is about to take off. Please save my sister!"

After hearing Margaret’s words, my heart tightened, and I immediately understood what Henry meant by ’wedding gift.’ That despicable, scheming bastard had planned all along to use Elizabeth and Armstrong to threaten me and Margaret!

"I’ve got it. Try to stand up; I’ll take you to save Elizabeth right now."

My calm voice seemed to give Margaret some strength. She looked at me, gritted her teeth, and slowly stood up from the ground, completing her shift in an instant.

A werewolf in wolf form can indeed conserve much more energy, and Margaret, always clever, certainly knew this.

Watching her stagger slightly, I glanced at her with concern but also knew she wouldn’t easily change her mind. I turned and led the way, running towards the location of the helicopter with her.

Following Henry’s escape, his Rebels began to flee in disregard of consequences. Their only fate was to be hunted down by the elite Lycans of the Royal City, from the hall and spreading outwards, a complete rout!

Because of this, the path for Margaret and me was clear as we chased after them, though we still had one last trouble to deal with.

"It seems just as MASTER said, the Lycan Queen does care a great deal about this she-wolf named Elizabeth!"

The one speaking was that gaunt Rebel leader. His eyes were fixed on Margaret as he spoke, which greatly displeased me.

I shifted my stance, placing Margaret behind me and said coldly, "If you’re smart enough, you should know that fleeing is what you should be doing right now. Henry has already abandoned you and fled for his own life. Are you sure you want to persist in this futile effort?"

At my warning, the leader’s hand, reaching to open the helicopter door, paused for a moment. He turned to look at me, hesitation flickering in his eyes.

I knew he was weighing his options. Henry’s unannounced flight had undoubtedly dealt a devastating blow to their morale, and he understood that tangling with me any longer was pointless.

"If I release these two wolves, will you let me leave peacefully?"

chapters.title: Chapter 314 Surrender Trick
chapters.content:
Chapter 314: 314 Surrender Trick

[Donald’s Perspective]

I chuckled softly upon hearing the words, and after a few seconds of silence, I finally spoke, "You are not in any position to negotiate terms with me. Either you release them now, or I will have to kill you and rescue them myself."

While I was negotiating with the individual, Margaret was surreptitiously assessing Elizabeth and Armstrong below the helicopter!

They were tightly bound together, their heads drooping lifelessly as if they had lost all signs of life. Yet, I still acutely heard their hearts beating, confirming they were indeed alive.

With this realization, I gave Margaret a reassuring glance to suggest that she keep calm.

Of course, Margaret and I were in perfect accord. She quickly controlled her breathing and calmly looked at the werewolf standing by the helicopter, surrounded by several of the Rebels.

"You few have no chance of escaping the Royal City smoothly. You’d better realize that," Margaret said, her gaze emitting a fierce light, "Surrender now. At least that way, Donald can be somewhat more lenient during your trial. You are completely surrounded, there’s no possibility of escape."

I suppose it’s because Margaret is always at my side, the way she speaks and handles matters is becoming quite similar to mine. She is increasingly displaying the presence of a Lycan Queen.

The opponent seemed to be intimidated by her commanding presence and, after a moment of stupor, they voluntarily dropped their weapons and obediently knelt on the ground, seemingly losing all will to resist.

I slightly furrowed my brow, sensing that this was uncharacteristic of the Rebels!

For someone as deranged and sadistic as Henry, could his subordinates have been so compliant, surrendering at just a few words?

I harbored such doubts, yet I had not immediately noticed David moving closer at that moment.

He grasped a highly lethal submachine gun, aimed it at the leader, and coldly commanded, "Kneel down, hands on your head!"

"No! No, don’t get too close to them!" I widened my eyes, loudly alerting all nearby Lycans.

With a loud ’bang,’ flames mixed with massive energy charged toward Margaret and me. I only had time to shield Margaret by my side; I couldn’t clearly see what the leader had done.

After the explosion subsided, I shook my head slightly, dizzy from the impact, and immediately looked toward where the explosion had occurred!

"Captain David? My God!"

"Someone help! Quickly, someone save our captain, his hands and legs... his arms and feet are torn off!"

"Quick! Somebody put out the fire, hurry and save him!"

Margaret then slowly sat up, her face pale as she looked at me, "What happened, Donald? How could there be an explosion all of a sudden? Are you okay? What about Elizabeth and Armstrong? I heard someone calling out David’s name; what happened to him?"

Looking over at David lying in a pool of blood, I struggled to find my voice to respond to Margaret and merely shook my head in response.

It took me several seconds before I could speak, soothingly patting Margaret’s hand before I hoarsely explained, "The enemy had explosives strapped to them and detonated themselves. I’m okay, Elizabeth and Armstrong were rescued by the Lycans, but David..."

I paused, then began walking toward the center of the flames, "Stay here, don’t move; I’m going to check on David."

Margaret, still somewhat dazed, unconsciously nodded, trusting me implicitly.

As I approached, the Lycans around David naturally dispersed. They looked at me, desperately calling out for help!

I knelt beside David, my brow furrowed deeply, and I ordered sternly, "Get Benjamin here quickly, his arms and legs can still be reattached! Hurry!"

"No...no need, Your Majesty, I’m done for!" David, supported by a Lycan, replied weakly after hearing my words.

Looking at David covered in blood, a profound gloom enveloped my face, "You’ll be fine, trust me."

David smiled upon hearing this, his mouth stained with dark red blood, his internal organs blasted to shreds, "I’m not afraid to die, Your Majesty. It’s just that I feel terribly guilty for not fulfilling the task you assigned me today! Anthony... I hid him in a safe place, he’s also still alive; you can tell the Queen!"

"I’m sorry, Your Majesty, I was too careless just now, I completely failed to see the enemy’s feigned surrender; they never planned to leave the Royal City alive," he coughed violently.

Unable to bear seeing David blame himself, I gently chuckled and softly said, "I know, none of this is your fault; you don’t have to feel guilty! You are one of my finest Lycans; I’ve always thought so."

As if receiving the highest of commendations, a joyful smile appeared on David’s face. His eyes slowly lost their color, yet stubbornly he still mouthed the oath unique to the Lycan soldiers, "I dedicate my life and soul to the Werewolf Royal Family and Your Majesty."

chapters.title: Chapter 315 sacrifice
chapters.content:
Chapter 315: 315 sacrifice

[Donald’s Perspective]

When the daylight over the Royal City had finally faded, the bizarre and swift rebellion war had also come to an end, concluding with MASTER leading the remnant of the Rebels fleeing westward. We could actually claim a complete victory!

What I mean is, if there hadn’t been so many Lycan sacrifices.

"Your Majesty! MASTER led the remaining Rebels southwest, and after entering the Mol Lake region, they disappeared completely. Mark and Louis closely followed them all the way and finally lost track of MASTER near there. I have ordered them to continue the search, and we should hear news soon."

"Moreover, in today’s battle, we lost a total of 120 Lycans, including 2 from the assault team, 4 from the personal guard force, and 47 warriors from the Royal City Garrison. The Royal City Patrol Team... The Royal City Patrol Team lost a total of 67 Lycans, including their captain—David."

Christian’s voice grew heavier as he reported this last part, his eyes reddening especially when mentioning the Royal City Patrol Team, which suffered the heaviest losses.

He clenched the document in his hand, veins bulging, and a silence slowly fell upon the grand hall.

The loss of each comrade weighed heavily on all of us. Although everyone was an elite Lycan who had survived countless battles and near-death experiences, it seemed we could never quite get used to this pain.

Recalling the last words David said before he closed his eyes, I felt a warmth in my eyes, but I eventually stifled the sadness, took a deep breath, and said solemnly, "The compensation for the sacrificed Lycans must be issued immediately, and the families of the warriors must be well taken care of."

"Furthermore, bury all the Lycans who perished in this war in the royal family’s Heroes’ Tomb. I will communicate this with the elders myself, so you need not worry."

Clark, Christian, and Benjamin, who had just come in and had yet to report, all stood aside. Knowing what they likely wanted to say, I spoke ahead of them, addressing all their concerns.

The Heroes’ Tomb of the Wolf royal family is not a resting place for just anyone; it requires specific qualifications and conditions. For example, only Lycan warriors and scientists who had made exceptional contributions to the Wolf royal family, or royal kin, are eligible to be buried in this sacred place.

Seeing that I had no intention of changing my decision, Clark wisely kept his mouth shut, silently acquiescing to my somewhat presumptuous decision.

Christian took a deep breath, his eyes still visibly moist, yet his expression was clearly one of relief. Being an exceptional Lycan himself, he was naturally reassured that his comrades would be honored so well in death.

Benjamin had been silent since entering, and I knew he was here to report on the condition of the injured, so I gathered myself and looked at him, "How are things at the infirmary camp?"

"Your Majesty, all the injured soldiers and officers have been settled. The severely injured warriors are being treated by the Holy Medical Pavilion and the medical school. Here is the compiled list of injuries for your review," Benjamin said as he stepped forward and handed me the document. I took it and looked it over, then nodded and responded, "Good, I feel relieved having you handle these matters. Continue to follow up on these issues, and remember to report back to me promptly."

Upon hearing this, Benjamin bowed his head in acknowledgment. His dark circles looked frighteningly deep. He had been engrossed in his lab studying MASTER’s elixirs and dealing with Angel’s issues all day. After the outbreak of the war, he had been rushing around at the front lines. Having just managed to sort out all the injured, he could finally breathe a little easier.

I looked around at everyone in the hall, thought for a moment, and then spoke loudly, "Everyone has worked hard during this period, but the matters related to MASTER cannot afford any slack from us. Please continue to be vigilant, thank you for your efforts!"

Hearing this, the ministers and Lycans in the grand hall instantly became solemn, placed a fist over their hearts, and responded in unison, "Thank you for your compassion, Your Majesty. We are willing to offer our hearts and souls for you and the Wolf royal family."

I nodded to signify that everyone could disperse, keeping only Benjamin and Christian behind.

With just the three of us left in the grand hall, it quieted down a bit, I rubbed my tired eyes and asked Benjamin, "How are they doing?"

Benjamin knew I was referring to Elizabeth and Armstrong, and the critically wounded Anthony.

"Lord Armstrong and Miss Elizabeth’s injuries are not particularly severe. They should fully recover in about two months with careful treatment from the Holy Medical Pavilion. Anthony..." Benjamin paused, seemingly choosing his words carefully.

I opened my eyes and looked at him, speaking softly, "Why has everyone become so long-winded? Just say what you need to say. Do I need to emphasize this?"

Since meeting Margaret, I had rarely been so stern with my subordinates, but today’s situation was particularly special, and I admit that I was slightly out of sorts.

chapters.title: Chapter 316 last chance
chapters.content:
Chapter 316: 316 last chance

[Donald’s Perspective]

"I apologize, Your Majesty, it’s not that I intend to dilly-dally, it’s just that Anthony’s condition is critical and dangerous right now, and I’m not sure if I should take a risk for him."

Before I could open my mouth to lighten the mood, Benjamin replied urgently, as if he were concerned that I might have misunderstood him.

I looked at Benjamin, softened my serious expression, and said, "Speak plainly."

Upon hearing this, Benjamin didn’t delay any further and expressed all his thoughts to me straightforwardly. Anthony’s escape from the enemy’s tight and perverse torture wasn’t due to luck nor the enemy’s underestimation and carelessness!

He was indeed confirmed dead by the enemy and discarded as a corpse; only to then regain a trace of consciousness by his incredibly strong willpower, hanging onto his last breath to get the message to the Royal City. And now, to use Benjamin’s words, it was just half a breath that was still being sustained.

Anthony’s injuries are too severe, and now conventional medical treatments are meaningless to him—his severe injuries leading to death are just the final problem.

The reason Benjamin was hesitating earlier about whether to take a risk for Anthony was not without cause.

Benjamin and a group of researchers had not wasted their efforts during this period; they made use of the previously collected information and data, and with Angel as a living example, they actually managed to decipher the composition of the twisted drug concoction by MASTER, as well as its entire production process!

"Are you saying you’ve successfully modified ’Rebirth’? Its side effects have been significantly reduced compared to before, and after observation and testing for some time, it can be fully implemented for practical use?" I was amazed at the news Benjamin brought and incredulously asked.

Perhaps the surprise in my eyes was too apparent, as Benjamin too showed a smile, nodding and saying, "Yes, Your Majesty, we have already passed the first round of animal testing. The second round is about to be conducted on actual humans. My colleagues and I are still looking for suitable candidates. Today, I wanted to report to you first. But what did you say just now? The drug is called ’Rebirth’?"

Seeing Benjamin mulling over the word ’Rebirth,’ a hint of irony slowly emerged in my expression. Of course, I was not mocking Benjamin, who is an upright and intelligent genius medic, and an excellent Lycan with outstanding contributions to the Wolf royal family.

The one I was really mocking was MASTER, who contradicted natural laws and ethics for his own twisted desires! The name ’Rebirth’ used for his concoctions is nothing but a joke laden with irony. A physician like Benjamin, who strives to save lives, truly represents the benevolent researchers who can bestow rebirth upon people.

"That’s not important; don’t dwell on it too much."

I stood up, walking over to Benjamin and patted his shoulder, continuing with a light laugh, "I understand what you mean, using this successfully modified new drug is the last chance to save Anthony! But before that, I need to discuss it with Margaret first, as this drug is still experimental, to see if they agree to let Anthony take such a risk."

While I understood this could indeed be the last opportunity, I couldn’t decide on behalf of everyone. Anthony was a friend of Margaret’s from childhood, and I needed to ask her thoughts first. Of course, the opinions of the Silver Moon Pack Alpha and Luna mattered too.

"Time is of the essence, so let’s do this: come with me to see them now; they should all be at the Holy Medical Pavilion!"

I said this and strode out of the great hall, gesturing for Benjamin and Christian to follow.

On hearing this, Benjamin’s eyes lit up, and knowing the urgency of the situation, he nodded without a word and followed directly.

In the intensive care unit of the Holy Medical Pavilion.

As expected, Margaret and Elizabeth, who had awakened, had already come to watch over Anthony and Armstrong, who was still in a coma.

When Margaret saw me and Benjamin walk in, she stood up from the chair outside the ward, and beside her was a pale Elizabeth sitting in a wheelchair, her eyes filled with sorrow.

"Donald? Why have you come now? Have all matters been dealt with? You’re alright, aren’t you?" Margaret looked at me, her voice full of concern.

I stepped forward, bent down to kiss her between the eyebrows, and said softly, "It’s all taken care of, everything on that end is sorted. How are you and Elizabeth doing?"

Upon hearing this, Margaret shook her head, her clear, beautiful eyes a bit red, as if she had cried, and she looked down at Elizabeth and replied in a low voice, "I’m fine, but Elizabeth has really been frightened these past two days. She just told me about yesterday’s ambush, and how Anthony and Armstrong desperately protected her and the other pack members..."

As Margaret spoke, her eyes reddened again, and she looked up at me, her voice carrying a hint of pleading, "I know Anthony’s in critical condition, but can’t you think of a way to save him?"

chapters.title: Chapter 317 Growth
chapters.content:
Chapter 317: 317 Growth

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Half an hour ago, the head of the Holy Medical Pavilion, Andrew, had already explained Anthony’s condition to Elizabeth and me in detail. Now, saving Anthony is really, really difficult!
