I also know that making such a request to Donald now is very unreasonable, but I really can’t accept a friend just leaving me like this, especially someone as good as Anthony. And another most important reason is because of my twin sister Elizabeth.

Her feelings for Anthony are so complicated and profound; I don’t want her to live the latter half of her life in constant guilt and regret over this.

At this time, Elizabeth also looked up at Donald, her beautiful eyes red and swollen from crying, and the wounds on her face and body made her look utterly pitiful and disheveled.

Perhaps it’s because of the twin thing, but I could almost empathize with her pain. With my eyes red, I looked toward Donald, hoping that my God-like mate could help my poor sister once more.

Donald raised his hand to caress my face, looking at me with a hint of heartache in his eyes. His palm was warm and dry; the touch made me feel slightly better.

"We do have one more method to try, but it involves some risks, a kind of ’all-or-nothing’ gamble! If we win, we could still save Anthony’s life. If we lose, then there’s truly no hope left for him," Donald said softly.

Surprised by his words, I subconsciously blurted out, "What method? What do we need to do?"

Only then did I notice another gaze coming from beside Donald. It was from Benjamin.

Donald didn’t answer my question immediately, but, like me, shifted his gaze to Benjamin before continuing, "Benjamin and the scholars at the research institute have successfully modified the MASTER’s potion. It’s the same one that healed Angel’s physical injuries so quickly. We’ve carefully considered the viability of this method and think it’s worth a try, although there are still some risks involved. You’ll have to make the final decision for Anthony."

The method Donald mentioned, I had guessed the gist of it just a second earlier, but hearing it still surprised me slightly. My emotions at that time were a bit complex; I didn’t know whether to feel proud of Benjamin or happy for Anthony.

But then I thought back to some moments of Angel in the prison, and after pausing, I asked Benjamin, "If the treatment with the potion fails, will Anthony suffer a lot afterward?"

This was also what Elizabeth wanted to know. She might not fully understand the origins and consequences of the potion’s development, but seeing the grave and serious expressions on all of our faces, she guessed the gist of the situation.

Her tears, unbeknownst to anyone, had fallen once more as she looked at Benjamin, her eyes full of pleading.

"The side effects of the potion have been reduced by us, but to say there will be no pain at all is impossible. Your Highness, I know you care a lot about Anthony, but this really is the last method I can think of. Just like His Majesty said, it’s a ’gamble,’ and what we’re betting is Anthony’s life," Benjamin said earnestly, his face solemn.

I trusted Benjamin’s medical skills a lot; if he said it was worth a try, then the chances of success were at least 60%! I quickly made up my mind, took a deep breath, and turned to look at Elizabeth. But before I could even say anything, Elizabeth had already made a decision that surprised us all!

With her head lowered and sobbing softly, she murmured, "Go ahead and use it."

"What?"

Benjamin almost instinctively questioned, while I instantly understood what Elizabeth intended to do.

She looked up again at Benjamin, her eyes frightfully swollen but her gaze was incredibly steadfast and bright, "Use that potion on Anthony, save him at this very moment! Whatever happens after, let’s at least get through tonight. If he were conscious now, he would probably choose the same, after all, he is the Silver Moon Pack’s brave and resilient Beta."

I looked at Elizabeth somewhat surprised; at this moment, she hardly seemed like her usual self. It was as if imperceptibly, she had been growing up, becoming braver and more responsible.

Although the present scene was hardly suitable for it, I couldn’t help but smile slightly, celebrating my sister’s hard-won growth.

I stepped forward and gently enfolded Elizabeth’s small head in my embrace, then spoke warmly to Benjamin behind me, "Let’s go with her decision, you can start now."

At this moment, Donald seemed to let out a quiet sigh, giving Benjamin a nod when he turned to look his way.

Upon receiving the command, Benjamin quickly pulled out his phone, spoke rapidly and gave a few instructions on the other line, then pushed open the door to the intensive care unit and went in.

chapters.title: Chapter 318 ’I want to stay with him
chapters.content:
Chapter 318: 318 ’I want to stay with him

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Elizabeth’s tears soaked my clothes, and I gently stroked her hair with a pang of heartache, silently praying to the Moon Goddess.

A few minutes later, a Lycan carrying a refrigerated briefcase hurried over. He first nodded to Donald and me and then pushed open the door and went straight in. I knew he was Benjamin’s lead assistant in the lab.

"It’s going to be alright, the Moon Goddess will bless Anthony," I murmured softly, as if to comfort Elizabeth, and also to bolster my own confidence.

Time quickly passed, and 10 minutes later, Benjamin still hadn’t emerged from the room, but we were joined by a few others.

Seeing Frantz push Armstrong slowly toward us, Elizabeth and I unconsciously frowned. I had no desire to speak with Frantz or Nora, so I pretended not to see them and edged closer to Donald.

"You’re finally awake, Armstrong. How are you feeling now? The doctors here say you must rest in bed. You should go back and lie down!" Elizabeth said to Armstrong, who was also sitting in a wheelchair, her tone anxious.

While speaking, she looked at Frantz and Nora following behind Armstrong, sighed helplessly, and continued, "He doesn’t know the situation here, do you, Dad and Mom? Didn’t I tell you to go rest? Even if you’re worried about this place, you shouldn’t just let Armstrong act recklessly. I’m watching over Anthony; that’s enough. Please take Armstrong back with you!"

Elizabeth spoke in a tone that was far from polite. Donald and I had no intention of intervening, and the atmosphere suddenly became awkward, especially for Frantz, who thought he had a shield by accompanying Armstrong. His unnatural expression was all too evident.

"I’m okay, how is Anthony doing right now?"

In the end, Armstrong took it all upon himself. His voice was weak, but the concern in his eyes when he looked at Elizabeth was much more genuine than Frantz’s.

I wasn’t intentionally discrediting Frantz. I just saw too clearly the selfishness and indifference in his bones. The clearer I saw, the more I felt it unnecessary to waste my energy on him.

Elizabeth repeated Anthony’s situation to Armstrong. Nora, standing nearby, listened with moist eyes. She had, of course, watched Anthony grow up from a child.

Once Armstrong was also informed of the situation here, Donald finally spoke up, "This issue here won’t resolve momentarily. There’s no need to have so many people lingering. Armstrong, your injuries are the most severe; you should go back to your room and rest. I’ll have someone inform you immediately if there’s any news."

Donald said this and turned to look at Frantz and Nora. His expression was unchanged, but his tone was noticeably colder, "You’ve both had a hard day too. You should go rest as well."

Armstrong, always sharp, first glanced at Mr. and Mrs. Frantz, then turned to Donald, responding respectfully, "Thank you, Your Majesty, for your concern. I’ll go back to my room now. If there is any new information about Anthony, I hope Your Majesty can inform me promptly."

Donald nodded, indicating he understood.

Seeing this, Armstrong respectfully nodded and then turned to Elizabeth, "You’re also hurt; come back with me. The Lycan Queen and His Majesty are here."

There was no way Elizabeth would go with Armstrong at that moment. She shook her head and explained, "I need to stay here with Anthony. You go back first, let Mom and Dad go with you."

Upon hearing this, Armstrong momentarily froze, looking at Elizabeth with a complex expression, silently pondering something.

And Frantz, whose gaze was flitting uncertainly, finally found an opening to interject, "With so many doctors and nurses here, why stay? Armstrong is right, you’re also not lightly injured. Go back to the room and rest with him. Don’t make me and your mother worry, okay?"

Nora, at this moment, was also looking at Elizabeth, and suddenly, as if remembering she had another daughter, turned to look at me with an awkward purse of her lips, then said softly, "Margaret, I heard you were also injured today in the hall, weren’t you? How do you feel now? I have some medication for external injuries here, let me take a look for you?"

Hearing Nora’s words of concern, I felt a bit heartbroken. Although Nora was always weak and incompetent, she would still speak up gently and caringly for me, which I found immensely precious.

"My injury is minor; Donald had a doctor attend to it earlier," I answered Nora gently, looking at her concerned face.

Seemingly not expecting my refusal, a look of disappointment appeared on Nora’s face.

I turned my head pretending not to see, and at that moment, Donald gripped my hand tightly as if to lend me some strength. Invisibly, he quickly soothed my emotions. It seemed we had become each other’s indispensable ’Stabilizer’.

In the end, Armstrong couldn’t persuade the stubborn Elizabeth, let alone Frantz and Nora, who always saw Elizabeth as their prized jewel. They stood no chance against the willful Elizabeth.

So, seeing them return ineffectually, their silhouettes cloaked in dejection, I felt a bit amused. This was, in its own way, a case of ’the biter bit’.

chapters.title: Chapter 319 Elizabeth’s decision
chapters.content:
Chapter 319: 319 Elizabeth’s decision

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I think the kind Moon goddess must have heard my prayers before, because after nearly an hour of waiting, we finally received news from the ICU that Benjamin was not too bad!

After they injected Anthony with that drug, his vital signs began to improve at an extremely slow pace, but after half an hour of close observation, the wounds on Anthony’s body began to heal at a rate visible to the naked eye.

It was like a miracle.

Later, Donald and I watched the complete record of the treatment process, and I was pleasantly surprised by our initial success. This time, Anthony was literally snatched back from the clutches of death by the collective power of the entire Werewolf Royal Family, including Donald and me.

Because of the unparalleled specialty of the drug, after Benjamin emerged from the ward, he seriously suggested to Donald that they should immediately move Anthony to their laboratory for further observation.

At the same time, to avoid any other unforeseen changes, they needed to intervene and treat Anthony in the lab as soon as possible.

Donald and I naturally wouldn’t oppose such a suggestion, knowing it was all for Anthony’s quick recovery.

As for Elizabeth, the other person concerned on the scene, she certainly wouldn’t refuse. In her words, she would do anything as long as it would allow Anthony to continue living in this world.

Anthony was quickly taken away by Benjamin’s arrangements, and looking at Elizabeth, who couldn’t snap back to reality at the entrance of the elevator, I thought for a while and then spoke in a gentle voice, "The current situation is a blessing in disguise. With Benjamin and the many doctors of the Holy Medical Pavilion taking care of him, Anthony will definitely be alright. Don’t worry."

"Besides, you yourself have not yet healed from your injuries. You need to listen to the doctors and nurses, heal your wounds first, because even if Anthony wakes up, you won’t be in condition to really take care of him. Do you think I’m right, Elizabeth?"

Upon hearing this, Elizabeth came back to her senses and looked at me, her eyes shining with determination as she nodded and responded firmly, "You’re right, Margaret. I’ll get better as soon as I can!"

As she spoke, it seemed like she remembered something, and a look of concern slowly appeared on her face. She then added, "But will it be convenient for outsiders to enter and leave Benjamin’s research institute? I mean, when it’s time for me to go in and care for Anthony, will it interfere with their work?"

Seeing the passionate and sincere emotions in the depths of Elizabeth’s eyes, how could I not understand her dedication to Anthony?

I smiled knowingly and assured her, "Don’t worry, I’ll go in with you when the time comes. They surely won’t keep me out, will they?"

With a satisfying answer, a smile finally appeared on Elizabeth’s face. I chuckled and gestured to the nurse behind us to take Elizabeth back to her room; she needed care and rest just as much at this point.

Only after everyone in the hallway had left did Donald wrap an arm around my waist, lean down, and ask in a low voice, "What were you laughing about just now?"

"Doesn’t Elizabeth seem a bit too concerned about Anthony? Even when Armstrong left earlier, she didn’t seem so worried. If I didn’t know for sure she was the Luna of the Silver Moon Pack, I’d nearly believe she and Anthony were the intimately close lovers."

On hearing this, I looked up at my mate, and after several seconds, I softly and tenderly explained, "Then you might not know that before becoming Armstrong’s Luna, Elizabeth and Anthony were considered by everyone in our pack to be a pair."

I said this and raised my hand to hook around Donald’s neck, drawing myself even closer to him, and sighed before continuing, "Maybe it was fate playing tricks. Later, during our coming-of-age ceremony, Armstrong and Elizabeth suddenly found out they were predestined mates for each other, and she and Anthony..."

"Slowly returned to the relationship they had as children. To put it in human terms, then Anthony is Elizabeth’s most loyal and dependable Knight. When it comes to concern for Elizabeth, Anthony might even surpass Armstrong. The recent surprise attack by the Rebels on the Silver Moon Pack shows that even when Anthony faced death, he wouldn’t want Elizabeth to be harmed."

"Remember, Donald, he once turned down your offer because a big part of him couldn’t bear to leave Elizabeth. Even if he couldn’t become my sister’s lifelong mate, he still preferred to guard her for his whole life."

As I recount these past events, Donald has already embraced me and slowly started walking away from the Holy Medical Pavilion.

He had a focused expression on his face; he was listening to my words attentively.

I let out a slight sigh and buried my face in his chest, then added, "I’m not saying Armstrong isn’t good to Elizabeth, but I just feel they could both have better options. Are we always right to choose to be with our predestined mates?"

I knew my statement might sound absurd and amusing, but what I had observed in Elizabeth and Anthony made me question the instincts we had always followed.

chapters.title: Chapter 320 new messages
chapters.content:
Chapter 320: 320 new messages

[Margaret’s perspective]

The compassionate Moon goddess pities all her children, but could there be some drawbacks to this seemingly arbitrary and authoritarian way of determining mates?

After all, we are living werewolves, not just driven by instincts rooted in our blood and bones. We also have our own emotions and dependencies.

"Elizabeth wasn’t as happy as before after she got together with Armstrong," I murmured the final conclusion, feeling the warmth in Donald’s embrace and looking up to see the expression on his face, "Do you find it strange that I think about these things? After all, you and I were also chosen by the Moon goddess to be mates."

As if seeing the uncertainty and doubt in my heart, Donald lowered his head to kiss me and chuckled in response, "I think your point makes some sense, I’ve never heard such a unique theory before."

He pondered it seriously for a while before continuing, "Judging by Elizabeth’s reaction just now, she must have made a decision, right? Anthony is a great choice. After this incident, Elizabeth finally clarified her own feelings, and that’s why you laughed just now, am I right?"

Donald really is the most astute Lycan King, guessing everything incredibly accurately.

I smiled and kissed his lips back, playfully blinking my eyes and softly said, "Spot on, smart, this is your reward."

"My ’reward’? That’s not quite enough, I want more!"

Donald’s gaze slowly deepened, filled with startling desire, but he seemed to remember something and sighed helplessly, "Considering you were injured today, you can owe me. You stayed up all night with me yesterday, are you tired now? I’ll take you back to the Eastern Shine Palace to rest, and we’ll have dinner together later."

Before I could refuse, Donald quickly arranged everything. I couldn’t help but smile lightly, not opposing his arrangements.

Today really was eventful, and Donald still had a lot of work to attend to himself. He tucked me in before he got up and left, and I closed my eyes peacefully and soon fell asleep.

I really was exhausted these past two days.

When I opened my eyes again, it was already 2 a.m. Unfortunately, this was a tricky time as I noticed the empty bed next to me and realized belatedly that Donald hadn’t come back to the bedroom to rest.

I frowned slightly and immediately used mind-link to contact Donald: [Sweetie, where are you?]

[Awake? I’m in the study. There is a message here; you can come and listen to it. Take your time; I’ll wait for you here.]

Luckily, Donald responded quickly. I put on my slippers and walked out of the bedroom, heading straight for the small kitchen across the hallway.

Donald was alone in the study, sitting behind the desk with a slight frown between his handsome brows, holding his phone and seemingly lost in thought.

"What’s wrong?" I approached him, setting down the hot milk I was carrying next to him, and asked warmly.

Donald looked up at me, a smile appearing on his face, "Thanks, sweetie. Come here."

At his words, I parted my legs and naturally settled into Donald’s embrace, tilting my face to look into his stunningly beautiful eyes and continued, "What message requires me to come over to hear? Is there something I can help you with?"

Donald wrapped an arm around my waist, he touched my cheek with his other hand, and after a brief two-second silence, he began, "Eric’s scouting team disappeared at Lilo Sea; Christian hasn’t been able to contact them for several days now. I find this very strange."

"And the Lycans I sent to track Henry and the Rebels all came back empty-handed. They lost them near Mol Lake to the southwest. I suspect Henry had arranged for someone to meet them there. Moreover, they successfully evaded all the checkpoints and searches along the way with the other party’s help. Heading further south from Mol Lake, it won’t take many days to reach into the Lilo Sea territory..."

Hearing this, I had already roughly understood Donald’s point—Henry and his group ultimately escaped successfully, bringing along the burdensome Lillian, as well as the body of the deceased Austin!

Thinking about this, more confusion arose in my mind. I couldn’t understand what terms Lillian and Austin, these siblings, offered to make Henry remember to bring them along during his escape, even though it caused a lot of trouble for Henry.

Donald didn’t know much more than I did, and he couldn’t truly answer these odd queries.

I looked at Donald’s face, temporarily pressing down the doubts in my heart, and then took up Donald’s question and continued, "So, do you think these two incidents are connected?"

chapters.title: Chapter 321 Lilo Sea trip
chapters.content:
Chapter 321: 321 Lilo Sea trip

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"Eric and his team wouldn’t have disappeared for no reason, they must have encountered some other trouble," I said, my brows slowly knitting together as I pondered how to deal with the situation at hand.

Indeed, many things had happened recently. Donald’s capable subordinates were either injured or tied up with tasks they could not abandon, and at this moment, there truly was no suitable candidate to take over this matter.

The previously decided upon Anthony could also no longer accept this special mission due to severe injuries, which indeed was a concerning situation.

"I plan to head south to Lilo Sea with a team in two days; on one hand, to search for Eric and his group, and on the other, because I cannot truly allow Henry to escape and grow stronger. His ambition is too great and he acts with no regard, the Wolf royal family can’t give him another chance to rise again," Donald’s voice was especially deep as he spoke, the wolfen eyes flickering with profound darkness, "Additionally, I need to confirm the political stance of the Spirit Dragon Pack at Lilo Sea. Are they colluding with Henry just like Austin?"

I saw no issue with Donald’s sudden decision, it was only some concerns that I had.

Donald had mentioned the Spirit Dragon Pack, and their Alpha — Levi, seemed to have a myriad of connections with Licia! Compared to the unknown dangers that could occur after heading south to Lilo Sea, what worried me more was what stance Licia would take?

If she really had to choose between Donald and Henry, Levi, who would she pick? Was her only son more important, or was the remaining bloodline of her birth sister more crucial?

"Have you seen Licia tonight? Did she tell you anything?" I decided to ask directly after some thought; there were no topics that Donald and I couldn’t discuss.

At my words, Donald slowly turned his head, his chest rising more with emotion, he exhaled and replied, "Licia hasn’t told me anything about Henry and Levi, she just kept crying, begging me to forgive her... and forgive Henry and Aunt Martha."

The expression in the depths of Donald’s eyes was one of both distress and concealment, which I found somewhat heartbreaking.

I cupped his cheeks with my hands, making him turn to look at me, "I’ll accompany you to Lilo Sea, the situation there is unclear, and I worry for you going alone. Also, if you need it, I can talk to Licia."

"I believe Licia isn’t such a foolish person, there must be reasons for her actions. Licia values blood ties and family so deeply, and you are her only son, I think she wouldn’t truly do anything that endangers your life. I may not know others well, but I can try to communicate with her, so don’t worry too much about this issue," I said whilst lifting my neck to kiss the corner of Donald’s lips, my eyes filled with a soothing light.

At that moment, Donald raised his hand to hold mine, leaning down to exchange a deep kiss with me, as if giving a silent assent.

I knew he agreed with my two suggestions. Of course, he didn’t want to part with me, nor was he willing to actually press Licia for answers.

Now that all matters had been planned with solutions, Donald was finally able to ease some of his burdens and breathe a sigh of relief. After returning to the bedroom together, he quickly fell asleep in bed, he was truly exhausted!

Leaning against the pillow, I lay on my side watching him, completely devoid of sleep. Even in slumber, Donald’s brows were slightly furrowed. I sighed ever so slightly, smoothing out the wrinkles with my hand, "So being the Lycan King is this hard. Did you endure this all alone before?"

"But that’s okay now, no matter what you do, you have me by your side, supporting you. I love you, Donald." Perhaps those tender words truly had an effect, as Donald’s tense muscles began to relax, and he fell into a deep and sweet sleep.

I quietly chuckled to myself, still gazing at Donald, but my mind began to wander to other matters.

Early the next morning, Donald and I received a report from Benjamin on the latest situation regarding Anthony.

I turned off the tablet’s screen in my hand and looked up at Donald, who looked much better after a good night’s sleep. It seemed he had reverted to that unique, astute monarch once more.

"I’ll take Elizabeth over to have a look, you go ahead with your tasks. Let’s have lunch together at noon. I’ve asked Ruth to make some excellent steak and delicious soup!"

Donald gave a noncommittal nod, bending down to share a kiss with me before striding into the Eastern Shine Palace’s council hall, where several ministers were already waiting for him.

I glanced briefly at the people in the hall before withdrawing my gaze and leaving. Tomorrow we would set off for Lilo Sea, and before we departed, Donald had to make necessary explanations to the council of elders and ministers, as well as ensure fail-safe preparations.

Of course, I had matters to address and prepare in advance as well.

At 9:30 in the morning, outside Benjamin’s laboratory.

chapters.title: Chapter 322: Specimen 322 No. 1
chapters.content:
Chapter 322: Specimen 322 No. 1

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Elizabeth’s condition had improved a lot compared to yesterday, at least her little face didn’t look as pale and frightening as it did last night.

I walked forward and took the wheelchair from the nurse, saying gently, "Others are not allowed to come and go inside, you all may leave first, I will make sure Elizabeth is safely returned later."

The doctor and nurses who came with Elizabeth, of course, wouldn’t question my command. They nodded respectfully, answered with a ’yes,’ and then left, leaving only Elizabeth, my maid Ruth, and me outside the door.

"Let’s go inside," I said to Elizabeth in a warm tone.

Elizabeth nodded her head, staring straight at the door that had been opened from inside.

The air conditioning in the research facility was a bit low, and I glanced at the clothes on Elizabeth, asking, "Are you cold? Should I get you a shawl?"

Elizabeth, seemingly not expecting me to suddenly speak, shook her head and said, "It’s okay, I’m not cold, Margaret."

Seeing her so stiff in her reaction, I chuckled softly to comfort her, "Don’t be so nervous, Benjamin already reported to me and Donald today. Anthony’s condition is stable now, there is nothing to worry about."

"Hm? Oh, good!" The expression on Elizabeth’s face was tense, her eyes somewhat evasive.

I noticed her mind was elsewhere, and as I continued to push her forward, I softly said, "Just speak up if you have something on your mind, don’t beat around the bush."

Busted for her distraction, Elizabeth fortunately didn’t continue to hide her thoughts. She turned to look at me, "My mom told me all about the commotion my dad caused at your wedding yesterday, and I know you’re angry with them, but..."

She paused, as if still contemplating how to apologize in a way that would be easier for me to accept.

"They were just really worried about me, and that’s why they lost control like that. Please don’t be angry, Margaret. I apologize on their behalf. I’ve learned how to cook recently; let me make you a meal, would you forgive them this time?"

If this were in the past, Elizabeth would never have lowered her head to me like this; even if she was truly at fault, but now she had grown up, becoming softer and gentler. Even when she wasn’t at fault, she was willing to come and apologize to me and ask for forgiveness.

I slowly stopped walking, looking down at her gently, "I’m not angry about that incident, and you don’t have to apologize for them. Let’s not talk about this now, shall we? Let’s go in and see Anthony!"

Saying this in a tone that brooked no refusal, I continued to push her forward. Benjamin, with his team, came to meet us from the opposite direction, holding some papers in his hand, looking very busy.

I didn’t ask Benjamin to personally attend to us, just had his assistant lead us to Anthony’s ward.

Benjamin indeed hadn’t lied; Anthony’s condition was very stable now, just for some reason he hadn’t awoken yet. The assistant stood by explaining this issue patiently to Elizabeth, while I was slightly lost in thought about some other matters.

"What just happened? Benjamin seemed rather urgent," I said, putting down the cup in my hand and looking up at Benjamin’s assistant.

He seemed taken aback by my sudden question, hesitating for a few seconds before coming back to his senses and responding respectfully, "Lycan Queen, you are truly perceptive. A mere glance, and you guessed that something else happened in the lab. It’s our Subject No. 1 that has displayed abnormal conditions, and Teacher Benjamin is rushing over to handle the situation."

Ignoring the assistant’s compliments, I twirled the gemstone ring on my finger and continued to ask in a warm voice, "What is Subject No. 1? What happened there, do we need to go over and help?"

The assistant shook his head upon hearing my words, replying, "Thank you for your concern, Lycan Queen, but you needn’t worry. We have dealt with emergencies with Subject No. 1 many times before, and such incidents happen frequently in the lab."

During his explanation, I listened patiently without interrupting.

Realizing that he hadn’t answered my question directly, his expression tensed up markedly, he nodded quickly and said, "I apologize, Lycan Queen. The Subject No. 1 you asked about is actually the prisoner of war, Angel. This is her second emergency resuscitation today!"

"Are you saying she’s close to death?"

"What? Subject No. 1 is Angel? That traitor, Angel?"

Mine and Elizabeth’s voices echoed one after another in the air, stirring into a mix as the assistant looked at Elizabeth, who had suddenly spoken up, and then looked back at me, "Indeed, Lycan Queen. Yes, Miss," he replied.

I nodded slightly, stood up, and said to Elizabeth, "Stay here and don’t wander off. I’ll be back soon. Ruth, you stay with the second miss here; I’ll return shortly."

The latter part I directed at Ruth.

After saying this, I looked at the assistant standing beside me and spoke in a calm voice, "Take me there now, I want to see her."

chapters.title: Chapter 323: She is going to die
chapters.content:
Chapter 323: She is going to die

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Benjamin’s laboratory had a very thorough protection system for the experimental subjects. I glanced through the glass window at the researchers in sterilized suits moving back and forth, before finally resting my gaze on the she-wolf lying on the bed.

I had imagined what she would look like now, but when I actually saw her again, I suddenly felt that I had been too merciful before. Her current state was her best possible retribution, as the Moon goddess had not spared her.

Most of Angel’s skin had deteriorated away, with the only flesh still visible on her face and neck. But even that was just a piece of lifeless, worn-out hide!

There used to be a very elderly grandmother in the Silver Moon Pack, the oldest she-wolf I had ever seen, but now I had changed my mind.

"She is kept alive solely by nutrient fluids nowadays, all her organs are at the end stage of failure. My teacher said she could only make it through today at most," a researcher in a white lab coat softly explained to me from behind, slightly bowing.

I nodded lightly in response, signaling that I understood. I looked at Angel, who seemed already dead with her eyes closed, and after a moment of silence I asked, "If you’ve already given her the final diagnosis, then why still try to save her? Isn’t that contradictory?"

Truthfully, after seeing Angel in her current state, I didn’t feel thrilled, nor did I find her pitiable. I thought it was all a making of her own.

I had thoroughly hated her before, even wishing she would just die! But now, that sentiment had shifted slightly; the person who turned her into what she was now should be more detestable, even more deserving of death!

Yes, I’m talking about the MASTER who created "Rebirth" and brought disaster to the werewolves.

"In medical terms, this would be considered final humanistic care. Even the deadliest war criminal in prison has the basic rights to medical care and to be rescued," came the voice of Benjamin, who had just emerged from the resuscitation room.

He took off his medical mask, gave me a slight bow, and continued to explain, "Of course, our relentless efforts to save her are not without other reasons. Aside from her having a lot of research value, there are also people who do not wish for her to die like this."

Upon hearing this, I turned back to look at Benjamin and asked quietly, "So these people you are talking about, do they include yourself?"

Upon hearing my question, Benjamin’s expression paused for a moment, the meaning clear without words; he still cared about the camaraderie of former team members, as Benjamin was a Lycan who valued relationships deeply.

As I turned back with a light smile and a nod, I continued, "I understand, she was after all your teammate and comrade in arms. It’s normal for you to not want her to die in such a tragic way."

"I won’t pursue these matters. I just want to know, besides you assault team and personal guard force veterans, who else cares about whether she lives or dies? Is it the family influence behind her and Elliot? Are they still not giving up on her?"

As if he had anticipated my reaction, Benjamin let out a slight sigh of relief, then replied, "Yes Your Highness, aside from us veterans, Angel’s mother Judy has been very concerned about her condition. The lab occasionally receives various valuable items sent by her. Of course, we haven’t taken anything from her, nor have we disclosed any information about Angel’s condition, His Majesty is aware of this."

I had no intention of judging Benjamin, on the contrary, I trusted him and his character very much. I shook my head with a chuckle and glanced at him, "I’m not accusing you of corruption or bribery, so why so serious and tense? Okay, I get it, relax a bit."

I said, taking a deep breath and looked at Benjamin, "You don’t have to worry about Judy’s side. If there are any problems, have her come directly to me and Donald, we’ll deal with it. Additionally, I wanted to ask about Elliot’s status. I didn’t see him last time at the Holy Medical Pavilion. I heard from Dean Andrew that he was discharged early?"

"Elliot’s injuries have fully healed, and after being discharged he probably went home. If Your Highness needs to contact him, I can relay the message," replied Benjamin.

At this, I raised an eyebrow and laughed softly, "It’s not that I need to see him, but Donald has another mission for him. Of course, that includes you as well."

Seeing the puzzled look on Benjamin’s face, my smile grew, "I’ll let you in on it then. Donald and I are planning a trip to Lilo Sea, and you and Elliot are on the list of candidates to accompany us. This is all confidential for now, remember to keep it secret!"

chapters.title: Chapter 324 ’It depends on her luck
chapters.content:
Chapter 324: 324 ’It depends on her luck

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"Are we heading to the Lilo Sea? When do we leave, Your Highness? Anthony is still lying in the lab. Are you and the Emperor really at ease leaving him behind?"

The tone of Benjamin’s voice shifted from excitement to concern, his eyes full of inquiry as he looked at me.

I didn’t quite understand what had excited Benjamin, so I asked curiously, "Why do you seem so thrilled? Is there something about the Lilo Sea that surprises you so much?"

At my words, Benjamin somewhat sheepishly touched the back of his head and replied, "I apologize, Your Highness. I did get a bit too excited. It’s just that when I think about the precious medicinal herbs that grow in the Lilo Sea, I can’t help but lose control. I’ve always wanted to go, but never found the right opportunity. This time it’s really quite coincidental."

With that explanation, it made sense, as Benjamin was indeed a medical enthusiast, and it was natural for him to be happy about a chance to visit the Lilo Sea.

I nodded in understanding, and started to walk away, continuing as I went, "We leave tomorrow night. You can arrange any unfinished work you have. As for Anthony’s situation, I’ll communicate and confirm with Dean Andrew again. Don’t worry."

Benjamin followed behind me as I spoke and had no objections to my plans. They all understood that, most of the time, my words represented Donald’s wishes.

As I reached the doorway of the sickroom, I slowed my pace and without turning back said to Benjamin, "Do you have any of that medicine you used on Anthony on hand now?"

Benjamin also stopped, his face puzzled but respectful as he answered, "We made three doses initially. After using one on Anthony, there are two left, all in the lab."

I stared at the patterns on the door of the sickroom, silent with a neutral expression for a long while. Just as Benjamin thought I hadn’t heard and was about to repeat himself, I spoke.

"Use one on her as well. Whether she lives or dies after this will be up to her own luck," I said.

After speaking, I left without bothering about the conflicted expression twisting on Benjamin’s face!

I knew exactly what I was doing. I was not some saintly white lotus, nor had I any intention of forgiving and sparing Angel. I simply felt that we couldn’t let this she-wolf die off so easily, despite it being a fate she brought upon herself.

Angel should be alive, or rather, in a clear state of mind, to face the final judgment of the Wolf royal family, not die silently in Benjamin’s lab. That would be especially unfair to those Lycans who died due to her betrayal. She deserved to be judged!

I didn’t explain to Benjamin why I had to do this; I trusted that with his loyalty and noble character, he would come to understand my reasons in time.

When Elizabeth and I left the lab, it was nearly 12:00 noon. Elizabeth didn’t question me about Angel, focusing most of her attention on Anthony.

"I’ve been talking to Anthony all this time, I don’t know if he heard me! Margaret, when will Anthony wake up? I’ve never seen him lie in bed this long. Also, Dean Andrew just visited and said Anthony’s condition is very stable, better than they anticipated. Does this mean Anthony is really alright?"

Elizabeth, pushed by Ruth, followed behind me, her chatter unceasing since we’d left the lab!

I was helpless, yet didn’t have the heart to interrupt her, so I simply smiled and remained silent. However, our young lady clearly didn’t understand the helplessness of others as she kept on chattering.

"Did you hear me speak, Margaret? Why aren’t you answering me?"

"Alright, dear, I know you’re delighted to see Anthony, but could you be quiet for a moment? You’ve been repeating the same thing three times now. Anthony’s condition is very stable and gradually improving, you don’t need to be so anxious," I finally spoke softly, unable to withstand the relentless interrogation of the young lady.

Elizabeth pouted, seemingly dissatisfied with my response, but she quieted down obediently.

I looked at her beautiful face, amused, and said, "You care about Anthony so much? What about Armstrong? His injuries weren’t light either. How are things between you now, still fighting often?"

Elizabeth first shook her head, then took a deep breath, and looked at me saying, "We haven’t been fighting anymore. He has been sleeping in the study when at the pack. Honestly, I was quite taken aback by how fiercely he protected me this time!"

"I always felt like he didn’t care about me, but after this incident, I..."

chapters.title: Chapter 325 Divorce
chapters.content:
Chapter 325: 325 Divorce

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Elizabeth paused while speaking, her beautiful brows furrowing as if she encountered something extremely troubling.

I patiently listened to her speak. This dilemma did not trouble her for long; it seemed she had made a huge decision in that instant.

"I’ve made up my mind, I want to divorce Armstrong! The day Anthony collapsed in front of me, I felt like my heart was shattering. Yesterday at the Holy Medical Pavilion, I thought I was truly going to lose him, but luckily, with you and my brother-in-law there, we snatched him back from the hands of death!"

Elizabeth said with a laugh, her eyes filled with warmth and determination. She looked directly into my eyes, without any hesitation, and continued, "The moment I heard there was still hope for him, I felt like I was slowly coming back to life too! Margaret... no! Sister, the person I truly like has always been Anthony, and I only realized this yesterday."

I was not surprised at all by Elizabeth’s decision, on the contrary, I was glad she could make a choice so quickly. In matters of the heart, it’s not good for any of the three involved to keep dragging things out.

The longer time passes, the deeper the hurt and torment, and there’s no such thing as a personal resolution. This is true for Elizabeth, and for Anthony as well!

As for Armstrong, I can only hope that he can find someone more suitable in the future, as Elizabeth is not his destined mate.

"You only call me sister in times like these, I see right through you now."

Seeing that I didn’t respond directly to her initially but started joking around instead, Elizabeth slowly narrowed her eyes, looking somewhat ’dangerously’ at me.

"I’m talking about something serious here, Margaret, be serious," she reminded me.

I nodded, my gaze playful as I looked at her, "Isn’t what I’m saying serious too? You’re no longer a child. Shouldn’t you be a bit more sensible now? Calling me sister is only right, didn’t you call Donald brother-in-law before?"

"You... you’ve changed, Margaret! You’re not the sweet and gentle Margaret you used to be," Elizabeth concluded.

The disgruntled expression on Elizabeth’s face brought me joy, and my smile grew wider. I reached out and touched her puffed-up cheek before cooing, "Alright, alright, I’ll stop teasing you. As long as you’re happy, I’ll support whatever decision you make. You don’t have to worry about anything else, I’m here for you."

Upon hearing my words, it took a few seconds for Elizabeth to slowly smile. Her eyes were reddened, looking both pitiable and adorable.

I think she didn’t fully understand the full meaning of my last words, but it doesn’t matter. As long as she knows I’m here, she can freely do whatever she wants, and that’s enough. She is my only sister, and I’m willing to create a sky of freedom for her, even if it brings much misunderstanding and criticism from others.

That evening, I had people prepare a lavish dinner in the Eastern Shine Palace, inviting only the Frantz couple and the still-recovering Armstrong, even Donald wasn’t present.

Armstrong’s reaction wasn’t as intense as Frantz’s and Nora’s to the direct issue I brought up; it seemed he even felt a sense of relief and resignation that this moment had finally arrived.

Frantz stood up from his chair, banging on the table and shouting at me, but I was unfazed, watching him with utter calm and detachment.

"This is a decision Elizabeth thought over for a long time and finally made. She hopes for your approval and understanding. Of course, now that I’m spearheading this, you don’t have any other choice, you have to agree, even if you don’t want to."

"Margaret! Do you know what you’re saying? Are you threatening me and your mother with your status as the Lycan Queen? What if we simply do not agree with this matter? Are you going to kill us?" Frantz was so infuriated he turned red, as if I were his enemy.

"Is what I’m saying hard to understand? Or are you so old that you can’t hear what others are saying? Even if I am using my position as the Lycan Queen to pressure you, what can you possibly do about it? Not to mention this matter hardly concerns you, even if you disagree to your last breath, who would really care? As long as I am here, you have to agree whether you like it or not, do you understand?"

I said and threw aside the napkin I was holding, indifferently shifting my gaze away from him, turning to the other party involved.

"The ones who have the most say in this matter are not you two elders, I hope you can recognize your place and attitude. This is Elizabeth and Armstrong’s own matter, they are adults capable of managing how they’ll spend the rest of their lives, don’t you think I’m right, Alpha?"

Although Frantz was initially upset by my remarks, the mention of Alpha Armstrong helped him somewhat calm his emotions.

chapters.title: Chapter 326 Respect the most sincere self
chapters.content:
Chapter 326: 326 Respect the most sincere self

[Margaret’s Perspective]

All eyes in the room fell on Armstrong, whose complexion was still somewhat pale. Sitting in a wheelchair, he slowly lowered his head, remaining silent and seemingly lost in thought.

Seeing Armstrong silent, Nora interjected somewhat anxiously, "Armstrong, you need to think this through. This isn’t child’s play. If you really divorce Elizabeth, how will you explain it to the pack members when you return? You and Elizabeth are fated mates; we can’t defy the Moon goddess’s will..."

"This isn’t Silver Moon Pack’s public business. Armstrong only needs to be responsible for himself and doesn’t need to explain to anyone." I added to Nora’s words, not giving her the chance to say anything superfluous.

Nora turned to look at me with a face full of sadness and incomprehension, saying, "Margaret! Do you really need to completely tear this family apart to be happy? I know you’re dissatisfied with your father and me, but this is a lifetime matter for Elizabeth. What right do you have to make decisions for her?"

"Of course, I have no right to decide for her; no one does, and that includes you and Frantz. Understand, Mother?" I retorted coldly, my gaze ice-cold and expression resolute. "I can repeat it once more; I said from the beginning that this was Elizabeth’s own decision after much consideration. Is that clear enough?"

With a hint of sarcasm, I curled my lips into a smile and continued looking at Nora, "Our family was never that solid or harmonious to begin with, no tearing down needed from me. I’ve shown enough respect and tolerance towards you and Father already. I hope you recognize that and don’t keep putting me in difficult positions."

Nora looked at me as if I were a complete stranger. I didn’t care and kept silent after speaking.

For a moment, the atmosphere around the dining table became incredibly somber, like stagnant water.

Armstrong seemed to take a very, very deep breath. He slowly raised his eyes to look at me and softly said, "If that’s truly what Elizabeth wants, then I agree to this matter."

At his words, I laughed, saying directly to Armstrong, "I knew you would be the first one to agree. I wasn’t wrong about you."

I picked up my glass and raised it to him, "Here’s to your happiness in the future, and a toast to freedom and the most sincere self."

Upon hearing this, Armstrong’s tight face cracked for a moment, and a flicker of indefinable emotion crossed his eyes before he gave a slight, rueful smile, lifting his glass to look at me in return, "Thank you! You’re right, this glass is to freedom and the most honest self."

Armstrong and I tacitly finished our drinks, putting a final period on the matter, despite the other two people at the table still wearing faces of anger and misunderstanding. But no one cared.

Life is our own; we only need to be responsible for ourselves and not live in the hopes and assumptions of others, even if those people are our closest kin!

Elizabeth found out about this matter the next day, crying and laughing at the same time as she mocked my ’battle’ against the three powerhouses alone the day before. Her teary, red eyes brimmed with happiness and resolve.

She hugged my waist and wept quietly for quite a while, then raised her hand promising that she would definitely be good to Anthony, not letting down the ’great exploits’ I had done for her.

She was, of course, aware of how difficult it was to get past Flender and Nora, which is why she was so stirred and thankful towards me. I was also glad to have resolved this annoying matter for her, even if it meant resorting to intimidation and using the status of Lycan Queen, playing the villain. But I had no regrets about it.

In Donald’s words, I possessed the aura and dignity befitting a queen, which made dealing with selfish and shortsighted people like Frantz effortless.

At 6 p.m. that day, I secretly left the Royal City with Donald and an elite force, accompanied by the personal guard captain Christian, the new assault team captain Elliot, and the genius medic Benjamin.

Of course, there was also the Empress Dowager Licia, who held a knife to her own throat, using her life as leverage to force Donald to take her along.

The sound of the helicopter’s normal flight filled my ears. Sitting to my left was the silent and cold Donald. I withdrew my gaze from looking at the back of the cabin and reached out to hold Donald’s hand, silently comforting the emotionally turbulent Lycan King.

"Since things have developed this way, we’ll just solve each issue step by step, so don’t be angry. We have so many people watching her; she can’t do anything else," I said.

After hearing my words, Donald exhaled a breath through his nostrils and grasped my hand tightly, the restlessness in his brow dissipating slightly.

"Anyhow, I am already on the helicopter. If you think of leaving me behind to act covertly, then I’ll injure myself for you all to see! I mean what I say." Licia was still holding the dagger, with the fresh wound Lillian had inflicted on her neck just a few days prior now joined by another cut.

Donald and I naturally heard her, but neither of us responded. Instead, Elliot, sitting beside her, replied in a cool, indifferent tone, "The helicopter has already taken off, Your Highness Empress Dowager. Even if you wanted to jump out now, no one would agree, so you might as well put down the dagger. Isn’t it tiring to keep holding it like that?"

chapters.title: Chapter 327 Donald and Licia’s Game
chapters.content:
Chapter 327: 327 Donald and Licia’s Game

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"You..."

Seeming not to have expected such a retort, Licia’s expression froze on her face. She glanced sideways at Elliot who had spoken, then turned back to see Donald’s reaction.

Donald simply closed his eyes in silence, showing no intention to speak.

In my heart, I recalled the ’spectacular performance’ Licia had put on all the way here, and at this moment, I had no desire to respond to her either. Today, Licia had indeed gone too far!

However, cured and returning as sharp and direct as ever, Elliot’s retort to Licia, albeit somewhat presumptuous, indirectly allowed everyone to breathe a sigh of relief, preventing the journey from being utterly depressing.

And that was the reason no one had spoken up against him just now.

I withdrew the corner of my gaze from Licia and silently pondered another matter.

Our secret mission to head south to Lilo Sea was supposed to be clandestine. Licia had been kept segregated in her own residence by Donald because of an incident a few days ago; hence, unless someone deliberately leaked the information to her, it was unlikely she could have known!

Now, what puzzled and troubled me, was who this person secretly informing Licia might be, and what their motive could be.

Licia’s vehement reaction to our journey to Lilo Sea surely indicated there were matters Donald and I were unaware of. As I thought this, I stealthily raised my eyes to glance at Donald beside me.

True to being mother and son, his temperament mirrored Licia’s—both equally stubborn and persistent, which indeed gave me and the rest of our party a headache.

Licia had attempted to force Donald’s hand with the threat of her own death, aiming to halt him from continuing southward. But Donald was hardly the type to be coerced. Thus, the two of them stood at loggerheads, until, just 10 minutes prior, Licia seemingly had a change of heart and stepped back, stating she would allow passage, but on the condition that she accompany us for the entirety of the operation.

As departure time had been greatly delayed, Donald eventually had no choice but to accept Licia’s demand. Although he didn’t show it on his face, or voice it, I knew he still cared deeply about Licia.

I sighed softly, turning to glance at Benjamin, who stood holding a bandage, wanting to treat the wound on Licia’s neck but unable to begin due to his highly noble but thoroughly uncooperative patient.

"Empress Dowager! Your wound is still bleeding. Could you please put down the dagger so I can treat it? If you carry on, it will get infected," Benjamin said with a hint of helplessness.

The only response to Benjamin was silence.

Licia had, of course, heard Benjamin, but she was just too indolent or unwilling to respond.

Perceiving the pleading look Benjamin cast my way, I took a sharp breath and began in a calming voice, "Benjamin is right, Licia. If the wound isn’t treated, the infection could be troublesome! You still want to carry out the mission with us, right? If you fall ill, how can we proceed together?"

"Don’t worry, since you’re already on board the helicopter, we won’t just abandon you. Donald and I wouldn’t do such a thing..." Before I could finish, Licia cut me off with an interruption.

"What do you mean by that? Are you accusing or mocking me, Margaret?" she said.

I looked back at her with a touch of exasperation, thought for a moment, and countered, "Which word in my sentence was mocking you? It’s your own conscience and emotions accusing you, not me."

With that, I pulled my gaze away, eyes landing on Donald’s hand, which was gripping mine tightly, "You should also know that even if you force Donald to take you on the journey to Lilo Sea, it’s unlikely you’ll change anything he wants to do. You’re merely wasting your effort."

"Henry is still going to be pursued and captured, and Levi is going to be surveilled. You can’t change anything."

Perhaps struck by the stark truth in my words, Licia’s facial expression, which had been fraught with emotion, gradually steadied. When she looked at me, her eyes were filled with scrutiny and darkness, as if she was plotting something else.

I wasn’t overly concerned about what Licia was thinking, for even if she had many ideas, under the watchful eyes of so many of us, she would hardly be able to act on them.

Licia finally quieted down, and Benjamin and Christian glanced my way, their looks one of gratitude. I felt a mix of helplessness and a deep sympathy for Donald.

According to the plan we had established earlier, we would fly from Royal City by helicopter to the borders of Mol Lake, and then split into two groups, switching to speedboats and a cruise ship to rush southward!

Elliot would lead the assault team ahead to investigate the situation concerning Eric and his missing party, while the main force would follow close behind, conducting secretive inquiries into the Spirit Dragon Pack and preparing to provide any necessary support to Elliot and Eric.

chapters.title: Chapter 328 Shared Lunch
chapters.content:
Chapter 328: 328 Shared Lunch

[Margaret’s Perspective]

While we were changing our mode of transportation by the shores of Mol Lake, Licia sharply realized that Elliot had disappeared. She stood on the other side of me and Donald, her expression changing unpredictably, seemingly lost in thoughts.

Feeling a warm, large hand grasping mine, I came back to my senses and looked towards Donald, who gave a light smile before speaking directly, "It’s not a solution to keep being dragged by Licia like this. We need to think of a way to send her back to the Royal City."

"You’re right, the journey to Lilo Sea could be dangerous, and having her with us would only distract me and slow all of us down." Donald gripped my hand, nodded, and earnestly agreed with me.

Blinking at him, I suppressed my voice and asked, "You’ve been quiet and uninquiring all this way, I thought you didn’t care much about Licia. Though her methods aren’t exactly straightforward, when you think about it, they are understandable. Licia, caring so much about Martha’s only bloodline, probably won’t let you actually execute Henry."

Donald gave a somewhat indignant snort upon hearing this, glancing over at Licia who was behind us, and responded quietly, "We would need to actually catch Henry for that. I truly had no idea her relationship with Henry was so profoundly mixed; it really shocked me."

Looking at Donald’s face, bearing a restrained yet very helpless expression, I found it slightly amusing yet also somewhat bitter. What a mess this all was!

I took Donald by the hand in return and led him towards the cruise ship, "Okay, don’t think too much now. Later, I will invite Licia, and the three of us can have lunch together tomorrow noon! No matter the cause of concern, we must still eat."

Upon hearing this, Donald seemed to have thought of something. He looked down at me with a flash of insight in his eyes. I smiled softly without saying more, highly trusting in the tacit understanding between us.

The next day at noon, on the open-air deck of the cruise ship.

"Once you have set up the dining tables, you may leave. If I need anything, I will call for you." I said, fluttering the beautiful feather fan in my hand slightly, frowning a bit, and gesturing for the maids and chefs to leave.

I didn’t know what was going on, but since we boarded the cruise ship the day before, I had been feeling subtly uncomfortable, and I hadn’t slept well last night either.

The sea breeze, mingled with sunlight from where the umbrella couldn’t shield, crept in and brushed across my face, making me feel a bit better. Thinking that Donald and Licia would still need a while to arrive, I walked towards the nearby bow of the ship, wanting to enjoy the sea breeze.

The Silver Moon Pack is rooted deep in the forest and, although the several lakes there are not small, they certainly can’t compare to the real ocean. So in the strictest sense, this was my first time seeing the real ocean!

No wonder humans enjoy traveling, appreciating different landscapes and cultures. Who could truly resist such a beautiful sea?

"It’s really beautiful." I took a deep breath, leaned on the railing looking out at the sea, and murmured with delight.

"You’ve been down all morning, what’s wrong? Are you seasick?"

As I was enveloped in a warm and broad embrace, Donald’s gentle and mellow voice echoed in my ear.

I nestled closer into his embrace, and without turning my head, I softly replied, "It’s nothing serious, probably just a bit of seasickness. Why have you come here alone, where is Licia?"

"She’s behind, seems a bit unwell too. I just had Benjamin check on her. She should be here soon."

Upon hearing this, I turned to look at Donald and quietly shared my immediate plan, "Cele Island and the Lilo Sea gateway isn’t far from here. Once Licia falls asleep, we can have a speedboat take her across, and from the gateway, a helicopter can go directly to the Royal City. Christian has planned everything, don’t worry."

Donald looked into my eyes, kissed my lips lightly, and responded, "I’ve always been confident in your decisions."

I laughed upon hearing this, blinking as I teased, "Really? I hope you don’t think I’m trying to ’sell’ Licia."

Donald laughed at this moment too, as if he found my way of speaking very amusing, "Licia is not as simple as you think. I’ll cooperate with you later."

I certainly wouldn’t think of the Empress Dowager of the Wolf royal family as a simple and foolish person. Licia had never been one to be manipulated easily.

Lunch finally started after a delay of 15 minutes. Licia, looking across at Donald and me, lifted her hand only to set it back down on the water glass beside her without immediately drinking.

"Doesn’t this contain something untoward?" Licia asked.

I was a bit surprised by her directness today, paused, and then looked up at her before replying after a moment, "If I say no, would you believe me?"

At this, Licia slightly narrowed her eyes, her face darkening a bit as she gently pushed away the glass filled with water.

chapters.title: Chapter 329 Simple and Unadorned Strategy
chapters.content:
Chapter 329: 329 Simple and Unadorned Strategy

[Margaret’s perspective]

I found myself at a loss, but managed a smile and gestured for Ruth to fetch her glass of water. I took a sip before I continued, "It’s really just dinner; there’s no need for you to be so tense and guarded. You know Donald wouldn’t harm a hair on your head, let alone resort to something as despicable as poisoning you!"

After saying this, I signaled Ruth to return the glass. Licia’s wariness was so acute that she wouldn’t touch anything we hadn’t used ourselves. I estimated she hadn’t even drunk a sip of water since boarding the ship yesterday.

My conjecture proved correct. Licia looked at the water glass before her, her parched throat involuntarily swallowing. She thought her action was so subtle that no one noticed, but Donald and I saw everything quite clearly.

The chef began serving the dishes one by one—all very fresh and delicious seafood meals. My appetite had improved somewhat by then, and I motioned for Ruth to serve Donald and me. But before we could start, Licia across from us spoke up again!

She first set down her glass, its rim still wet with drops, and looking straight at Donald’s plate, she demanded, "I want the portion in front of you."

Donald, fork in hand, paused and looked up at Licia, clearly irritated. He spoke in a stern voice, "How much longer do you plan to keep this up, Margaret has already made it clear enough, haven’t she? Do you think we are like you, unscrupulous in achieving our goals? If you think everyone is out to get you, then don’t eat—just starve yourself!"

Donald had been quite harsh, yet Licia merely kept silent, staring at him with inscrutable eyes, giving away no hint of her thoughts.

I took a deep breath for show, held Donald back, and said in a gentle voice, "Don’t lose your temper with Licia, it’s a minor issue. Our meals are all identical. Let her have it, it’s okay."

With that, I gestured for Ruth to exchange the meals, entirely missing the suspicion flashing across Licia’s eyes.

Donald glanced at me with a look of utter resignation.

At that moment, Licia, now faced with the two meals in front of her, fell silent for a brief instant before saying, "Never mind, I’ll eat the one in front of me."

She then grabbed the cutlery without hesitation and began her lunch.

Watching her actions, my own hands froze in place. It took a while before I motioned Ruth to take the meal back, and Donald did not touch his meal any further either.

As if she felt she had gained a small victory, Licia glanced at me with a light in her eyes that seemed to say: Do you two really think you can outsmart me with such petty tricks? You’re thinking too simply.

Licia and I locked eyes briefly. I remained unfazed internally, but feigned frustration and anger on my face, as if we had indeed been outplayed by Licia this time.

However, even as lunch ended and Licia returned to the resting room for a nap, lying drowsily on the bed, she hadn’t realized she had been ’caught in the act’.

Donald and I stood at the cabin door, watching as two tall handmaidens escorted Licia into the speedboat. Her face was serene, clearly in a deep slumber.

Donald signaled to Christian with a gesture and said solemnly, "Ensure the Lycan Queen’s safe return to the Royal City. Clark knows what to do."

Upon hearing this, Christian looked at Donald with a hint of concern creasing his stoic brow, "Your Majesty, if I take the Empress Dowager back to the Royal City, you and the Queen will be without protection here. Isn’t that a bit improper?"

Donald clapped Christian on the shoulder, his gaze deepening towards the speedboat, "Don’t worry about Margaret and me. Just make a quick trip and we’ll regroup at the first ferry dock in Lilo Sea."

Seeing that Donald had made up his mind, Christian stopped dawdling, gave a slight bow, and prepared to board the boat for departure. Just then, I called out to him, "Wait a moment!"

As I spoke, I tossed him an item I had been holding and continued, "Spray this nasal spray every two hours for Licia, and she will sleep soundly until you reach the Royal City."

Christian, holding the spray, nodded respectfully to me and then boarded the ship. He was accompanied by dozens of impressively strong Lycans, partly to prevent Licia from waking up and escaping on the way, and partly to ensure her safety.

The waves created by the speedboat soon settled back into calmness. Donald turned and wrapped his arms around my waist, sighing softly as he inquired, "Is that the contraption you and Benjamin concocted? It’s the first time I’ve seen you use it."

I, feeling somewhat weary, closed my eyes and leaned on Donald’s broad and firm chest, laughing lightly as I explained, "You could use it for insomnia later, too. Everything in it is completely harmless, don’t worry."

"I wasn’t doubting the spray," he replied, "I just think you could totally make Benjamin your medical tutor now. You’ve learned a lot from him."

chapters.title: Chapter 330 Unusual Physical Symptoms
chapters.content:
Chapter 330: 330 Unusual Physical Symptoms

[Margaret’s perspective]

I felt myself about to fall asleep any second, so I slowly raised my hand to wrap it around Donald’s neck and whispered, "Ever since our time at the Silver Moon Pack, Benjamin has already been my teacher. The sleeping pill is taking effect, and I’m so sleepy, Donald. Take me back to sleep, will you?"

Donald had long realized something was off with me, his strong arms hooking around the crook of my knees, lifting me up securely.

He looked down at me with concern in his voice, "So the sleeping pills you slipped into Licia’s drink were actually in that seemingly safe glass of water, is that it? How could you be so careless, drinking the water you spiked for someone else!"

"But how else was I to catch Licia off guard if I didn’t drink it? I’m okay, darling, I’m just really sleepy. I didn’t sleep well last night either, so keep me company while I catch up on sleep, all right?"

By this time, my voice was completely slurred, coming off as unabashed coquetry to Donald’s ears.

Donald’s eyes were full of helplessness and affection which I couldn’t see, but I felt something warm touch the corner of my eye and then snuggled into Donald’s embrace, drifting into a deep, sweet sleep.

The little gadget Benjamin and I had collaborated on was indeed effective, colorless, and tasteless, catching one completely off guard. Even if Licia had extraordinary vigilance, what good would it do her, thoroughly conquered by advanced technology!

In fact, Donald and I were both aware that conventional methods were unlikely to outsmart Licia, who was a very clever person herself!

However, sometimes even the smartest people can be misled by their assumed correct judgments. My gambit of ’sacrifice one to kill a thousand,’ as clumsy as it seemed, was effective and multifaceted. Looking at the final outcome, it didn’t seem so foolish.

After all, who would have thought that the one laying out the plan would step into the trap first? It must be said this move was simple but unexpected!

Luckily, I had only taken a slight sip from Licia’s glass, and by evening, I had naturally woken up.

Resting my head on the pillow, I felt a bit stuffy, and faintly nauseous!

Ruth, who was waiting outside our bedroom door, was the first to notice my condition. She stepped in quickly, her voice gentle with concern, "You’re awake, Your Highness the Lycan Queen, would you like some water?"

I nodded, and Ruth handed me the glass she’d filled swiftly.

The clear, sweet water moistened my throat, and only then did I feel revitalized. I handed the glass back to Ruth, glanced at the quiet doorway of the bedroom, and asked, "What about everyone else?"

The one I truly wanted to inquire about was Donald. Ruth, having been with me for so long, understood implicitly. Her face broke into a smile as she replied warmly, "His Majesty and the others are discussing matters in the next room. His Majesty said to inform him as soon as you woke up. I’ll go and let His Majesty know right away."

She was about to rise to leave when I quickly raised my hand to stop her and, after a moment’s thought, continued, "Before you call Donald, could you ask the kitchen to prepare something for me to eat? I’m a bit hungry."

Of course, Ruth wouldn’t refuse my request and with a nod, she left to carry out the order.

I then lifted the soft quilt and tried to get up to freshen up in the bathroom, but barely had I steadied myself than a wave of intense dizziness hit me. I grabbed onto the lamp beside me in a fright!

’Crash!’

The lamp, unstable, fell to the ground, and I stumbled a few steps before I managed to regain my balance. My vision cleared at that moment, and a cold sweat broke out on my arm!

I subconsciously thought that this happened because I had slept too much today and had gotten up too quickly from bed.

Pressing the unnaturally throbbing in my chest, I slowly walked into the bathroom. Looking in the mirror at my pale complexion, I raised my eyebrows and began to freshen up, completely oblivious to the fact that my current condition was not normal.

Ruth quickly returned with some delicious food. I sat in the seating area outside the bedroom, eyes closed, enjoying the gentle touch of the evening sea breeze.

Hearing Ruth’s footsteps, I opened my eyes and slowly said, "The scented candles in the bedroom don’t smell very good. Throw them away later, will you?"

Ruth paused slightly, setting down the food before replying, "All right, Your Highness. But those were the ones you personally chose before and you said they smelled particularly good, so you bought a lot of them at once!"

"Is that so? If you like them, take them for yourself, but I don’t want to smell it anymore. It makes me feel nauseous." I murmured nonchalantly, recalling and began to dine with the utensils.

Ruth watched me silently and gave a small smile, finding my current behavior somewhat endearing, "Then, what scent would you and His Majesty prefer for your bedroom? I’ll go search the storeroom later to see if I can find a suitable one."

Hearing this, I immediately shook my head and, frowning, refused, "No need, let’s not use any scented candles at all. Just make sure to ventilate the bedroom and keep it clean."

"Oh, okay." Seeing how adamant my refusal was, as if I truly detested the scent of all the scented candles, Ruth nodded and compliantly agreed.

chapters.title: Chapter 331 missing Licia
chapters.content:
Chapter 331: 331 missing Licia

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Ruth told me that Donald would come over after he finished handling his affairs, but even after I finished the food on my plate and sat in the chair, letting the sea breeze blow over me for a long, long time, he never appeared in my sight.

I lightly shook the feather fan in my hand, thought for a moment, then stood up, "Has something else happened over there? Let’s go have a look."

After finishing my sentence, I picked up my skirt and walked out, with Ruth closely following behind me.

The meeting room was not far from mine and Donald’s bedroom. Ruth stepped forward to knock on the door for me, and when I entered, all eyes in the room fell on me.

I swept my gaze over everyone present with some confusion, then finally rested my eyes on Donald who was on the sofa, "What’s happened? Why does everyone look so upset? Has something happened?"

Standing closest to Donald were Louis and Mark. They looked down at me with serious and grave expressions on their faces, and I had a sense of foreboding as I looked at Donald’s face and came to a halt.

"Christian just contacted me, saying they were attacked by an unknown force on Cele Island, and their mission failed!"

Under my scrutiny, Donald didn’t stay silent for long. He let out a light sigh and spoke with a tone of impatience and frustration.

I widened my eyes in surprise and quickly closed my fan, asking directly, "Is Licia hurt? How badly? Where exactly are they now? How is Christian dealing with this?"

After hearing my questions, Donald furrowed his brows, lowered his head slowly, and rubbed his tense face, speaking in a hoarse voice, "Licia... Mother is missing! Christian said that the main goal of the attackers was Mother, their plan to abduct her was meticulous and well-executed, bringing people who were all exceptional in combat and armed with formidable weapons. Only three Lycans that Christian took with him are left."

"He has already urgently ordered the werewolf packs on the island to help search, but so far, there has been no response."

Donald briefly recapped the situation reported by Christian, his handsome face relaxed slightly after the rubbing, he looked up at me, softened his voice, and asked slowly, "How about you? Are you feeling uncomfortable anywhere?"

Hearing his words, I simply shook my head, not even remembering the slight discomfort I had felt earlier, "Sending Licia back to the Royal City was a sudden decision made by the two of us; there shouldn’t be any issue of leaking information, right? The helicopter from Cele Island that Christian contacted is also one of our own. So where did these sudden attackers get their information from?"

"Are they aware of Licia’s identity, and what is their purpose in directly abducting Licia? Is it to threaten us? Do these people belong to Henry, or someone else?"

As I spoke, I paced slowly to where Donald was, looking at him with a face full of doubts and concern.

Donald didn’t answer my questions immediately, and the room fell into a moment of complete silence.

After some thought, I sighed with some dismay and raised my hand to gently pat Donald’s shoulder, saying, "Perhaps I was really wrong, I shouldn’t have thought to send Licia back like that. The current situation is chaotic and dangerous; it’s safer for her to stay close to us, at least you wouldn’t always have to worry about her like this."

When Donald looked up at me after hearing what I said, his eyes were full of disagreement. He stood up, touching the back of my head with his large hand, and said warmly, "This matter isn’t your fault, don’t worry needlessly. Those people clearly came prepared, and they definitely won’t stop until they reach their goal. There is no problem with our plan, so there is no need for self-blame."

"Besides, given the current situation where dangers lurk everywhere, there is no absolutely safe place. If they can attack our small squad for the first time, they’d dare to boldly attack us the next time; Licia following us doesn’t bring any benefits."

Speaking of which, Donald seemed to think of something, a trace of sarcasm flashed in his eyes, and he continued, "Perhaps Licia’s current situation is even safer than ours, since she has always been protecting Henry, and Henry is not ungrateful enough to be a monster."

I held Donald’s hand, feeling a bit distressed. After thinking for a while, I spoke up softly with a furrowed brow, trying to comfort him, "If that’s the case, at least Licia isn’t in any immediate danger at this moment. Don’t worry too much, let Christian continue the search."

Donald looked down at me, his eyes showing a tender look, he let out a small laugh before continuing, "I’m glad to have you always by my side, otherwise I really don’t know what I would do. Too many things have been happening lately."

"It’s alright, everything will get better! You are our omnipotent Lycan King, there is nothing that can stump you, Donald. And besides, you have me and so many teammates with you, we will definitely overcome all difficulties and complete our mission."

chapters.title: Chapter 332 Arrives at the Lilo Sea
chapters.content:
Chapter 332: 332 Arrives at the Lilo Sea

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Perhaps the comfort I offered Donald had an incredibly peculiar effect, for he quickly regained his composure and spirit, and with the other few Lycan leaders in the conference hall, continued to discuss the matters at hand.

The sudden disappearance of Licia indeed had many oddities, and Donald and I, in tacit agreement, decided not to let this unexpected event affect us, our undercover investigation and inquiry must still go on, and of course, the search for Licia’s whereabouts must not be forgotten.

We traveled down the coastal route, and finally, on the evening of the second day, we reached the No. 1 ferry terminal of the Lilo Sea, which was the meeting place previously agreed upon with Christian and Elliot.

The climate in Lilo Sea was hotter than both the Silver Moon Pack and Royal City, and the ferry terminal, as the hub of business and trade in the region, was bustling, with plenty of passing ferries and travelers.

I was half-embraced by Donald as we stepped out of the cabin, and the next moment, I was seated in an all-black, armored stretch luxury vehicle, fully equipped with bulletproof features.

From the rear window, I saw several other SUVs traveling with us and, without turning my head, said to Donald, "Won’t this attract too much attention? Such a big target makes it hard to truly hide our identities."

Donald turned my face back to him, kissed my lips, and smirked somewhat slyly, "Who says we are still trying to hide? I want to strut into Lilo Sea just like this. I’m curious to see what sort of name my ’cousin’ has made for himself in these waters!"

I looked at Donald with some confusion as our convoy started moving. Donald’s smile widened a bit, and looking at me with feigned profundity, he continued, "You’ll see in a bit, just remember to coordinate with me, oh most distinguished ’Diana’ lady!"

I felt a little dizzy being led on by Donald and chuckled at him before going with the flow and accepting the new identity he arranged for me, the mysterious and beautiful power woman — Diana, heiress from across the ocean, possessing countless wealth.

"You want me to pretend to be the famous heiress Diana, known all over the world’s rich list! Why? Do we have something to do tonight? And what about you, Donald, will you come with me?"

Surrounded by several maids, I was quickly and methodically dressed in much more splendid and luxurious clothes, lifting my hand to look back at Donald, who sat calmly drinking tea, full of questions as I asked.

Donald’s gaze on me slowly deepened, radiating emotions that I was very familiar with — he desired me!

I looked at him somewhat speechlessly, raised my voice, and continued to press, "Did Your Majesty hear what I said? You want me to cooperate with you, but you need to tell me what you’re planning to do, right?"

It was then that Donald slowly came back to his senses, finishing the water in his teacup as though he was extremely thirsty.

He stood up from his chair and approached me, his arm locking around my perfectly sculpted waist, hugging the corseted dress and he whispered deeply, "I really want to rip off all the clothes on you, and then take you fiercely."

I: "..."

Aware of the curious and amused looks coming from the maids beside me, I glanced at Donald with a blend of speechlessness and shyness.

Fortunately, Donald hadn’t really lost his senses. After I glared at him, he playfully withdrew his sturdy arm and smirked, "I’ll spare you for now. We’ll see after the business is done later."
