Half an hour later, at the top floor of the Lilo Sea Sant Angelo Hotel, the wealthy wine tasting party, as well as the auction event shining with gold and indulgence in wealth, was about to begin.

I looked through the black lace mask in front of me, clinked my glass with Donald’s, and slowly withdrew my gaze from the scene, "Is this wine tasting hosted by Levi? Where is he?"

Donald took a sip of the red wine in his glass, raised an eyebrow as if the wine was quite good, and replied in a low voice, "The wine tasting is just a smoke screen for this event; the auction that follows is the main act. He’ll definitely show up later."

"Moreover, according to the information Elliot found, we might even see other ’surprise guests’ today! Levi’s underground business and methods are all dirty, and today we have the opportunity to investigate. Be careful during the event later, and remember not to stray too far from me."

It turned out that Donald had already contacted Elliot and had a preliminary plan for rescue, and tonight promised not to be boring!

Donald and I tacitly played out our roles well; without him saying too much, I was able to quickly grasp what he wanted to do and obediently cooperate with him, helping him to accomplish what was next on the agenda.

Time always flies during banquets, but only after three rounds of drinks among the dignitaries and wealthy gentlemen did today’s event officially kick off.

Donald and I were escorted by two waiters into the venue for the auction — another secret and secure place on this floor, the famous auction arena of the Sant Angelo Hotel!

chapters.title: Chapter 333 Outrageous Auction
chapters.content:
Chapter 333: 333 Outrageous Auction

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"Miss Diana, this is a personal supreme VIP auction position specially prepared for you by our boss. Please have a seat. If you need anything later, just call me, and I’ll be right outside the door!"

Before the attendant standing by our side could finish speaking, I gracefully walked into this lavishly decorated room, with Donald following quietly behind me. Today, he was playing the role of the silent, handsome companion brought by ’Diana.’"

I took the main seat in the room and, without turning my head, said, "Have your people stand back further; I can’t stand the smell of strangers around me."

These people clearly couldn’t quite grasp my fastidiousness. After exchanging glances, they respectfully uttered their assent and silently withdrew.

The door of the room was closed from the outside. I slightly turned my face towards Donald, patted the spot next to me, and gestured for him to come and sit.

[How come I never knew you were so good at acting? You just wait, you’ll see what’s coming to you after this is over.] Donald communicated to me via mind-link, then very ’obediently’ came and sat down slowly.

I looked at Donald and slightly cocked my lip, pulling at his tie and planting a teasing kiss on his lips, [Of course, we have to perform a full act, otherwise how would they believe our identities? But their cameras are quite brazen, aren’t they? Anyone being slightly attentive could spot them.]

I said this and then looked directly at the camera, my gaze as cold as if it could freeze someone to death.

[They do it on purpose. This is also an unwritten rule of underground auctions. The presence of cameras is to monitor safety within the venue, and the footage is only for venue use and won’t be leaked outside.] Donald spoke and sat up straight, shielding me entirely with his body.

I casually stroked his jaw and felt somewhat speechless about this rule, [Doesn’t this amount to a bit of ’bullying by the venue’? Do we have to keep pretending that we haven’t noticed them?]

While Donald and I were covertly communicating, music suddenly started playing outside, followed by the auctioneer warming up the audience.

[Let’s wait and see.]Donald responded while turning to look downstairs, his face mostly hidden by a mask and showing no emotions.

At that moment, I also slowly turned my head towards the host and suddenly felt that the voice sounded familiar.

"Distinguished guests, dear ladies and gentlemen, the much-anticipated auction is about to begin. But first, let us welcome with applause the initiator of this auction, our esteemed Alpha of the Spirit Dragon Pack—Lord Levi!"

As the host’s words fell, a rustle of applause broke out, interspersed with silence from a considerably large portion of the crowd, causing the atmosphere to slightly stagnate.

Something occurred to me, and I raised my eyebrows, intrigued, telling Donald, [It seems today’s auction will be quite interesting. It looks like not everyone is pleased with this Alpha!]

Donald didn’t respond to my comment, just watched as the werewolf slowly made his way to the stage, his aura gradually turning colder.

Levi’s face was uncovered as he stood in front of the microphone at the auction stand, his lips curled in a casual smile. He glanced around at everyone below him before slowly beginning, "Welcome everyone for taking time out of your busy schedules to attend my auction. I won’t say more; I believe the items today will satisfy you all!"

"Oh, and another thing, during the auction, please do not leave or whisper to each other. If I find anyone violating these rules, I will assume you are disrespecting me, Levi, and you really wouldn’t want to offend me!"

After Levi finished, the smile on his face broadened, but his eyes were full of provocation.

I didn’t quite understand what Levi was trying to do, my brows furrowing behind my mask as a somewhat unpleasant feeling arose.

"Humph!"

At this moment, Donald scoffed lightly. I glanced at him sideways, feeling as if the disdain in the eyes of the Lycan King was about to spill out!

[He sure knows how to throw his weight around, I wonder who gave him so much courage.]

"Oh? Since when did these two rules get added to the underground auction? Why didn’t we know about them before? Levi, I advise you not to go too far. Everyone is here for what they need, Lilo Sea isn’t ruled by you alone... it is still under the jurisdiction of the Wolf royal family, you better remember that!"

"I heard that your backer in Royal City, Prince Henry, has already fallen from power? The streets are filled with warrants all bearing his name. What are you being arrogant about here? Are you wishing for a shorter life?"

Two dissenting voices quickly arose from below, revealing that not only Donald and I found Levi’s methods inappropriate, but there were also reasonable and sane people among the VIPs in the venue.

chapters.title: Chapter 334 The first lot
chapters.content:
Chapter 334: 334 The first lot

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"Is that so?" Having heard the previous two conversations, Levi responded without any expression.

He looked down and leisurely touched the watch on his wrist, as if signaling something!

’Bang!’

"Ahhh!"

The sound of a gunshot and the screams from the crowd rose simultaneously. I stiffened completely, thinking of getting up immediately, but Donald quickly pressed down on me to stop, [Stay calm! Don’t move.]

At his words, I gasped, relaxed my body, and leaned back into the sofa, [What exactly does he want to do?]

[Kill one to warn a hundred, just some unspeakable tricks!]

"I didn’t want to shed blood so soon, but some of you really pushed me to this point. Now, I have no choice! It’s best if everyone stays quiet because I really hate people who talk too much. For instance, this gentleman who supports the monarchy, he’s precisely the type I loathe the most."

The voices of Donald and Levi sounded one after the other. I looked down at the people below, silently replying in my mind, [That doesn’t justify senseless killing, right? Is this an auction or a trap?]

[Doesn’t it seem more like a rebel demonstration?] Donald commented caustically.

After finishing his speech, Levi walked directly away from behind the microphone.

He stepped onto the person who was already lying in a pool of blood, quickly leaving a bloody footprint on the victim’s face, a scene that seemed to please Levi.

The corners of his mouth slowly curved up, displaying a cruel smile, "Now why don’t you open your eyes and see whose life isn’t long enough! So what if I am arrogant, what can you do about it?"

The atmosphere in the room was as still as dead water for a moment. Levi straightened up and coldly surveyed his surroundings, his gaze full of defiance.

"Madman! You’re just a freak, aren’t you? By using the guise of an auction, what do you intend to do after misleading us here? Do you have any regard for the law?"

"I don’t want to partake in this damn auction anymore, open the door! Let me out, Levi!"

"You.. you... let us out! Do you know who I am?"

"Everyone here today is either rich or powerful. I don’t think you’d dare kill us all! Just say what you want to do!"

Downstairs, a few gentlemen were still unsatisfied with Levi’s reckless actions and began verbally denouncing him. However, Levi didn’t seem to care.

He chuckled and continued, "Who said I’m going to kill you all? My items are still waiting for your bids! Besides, the bigwigs upstairs haven’t said a thing. Why are you guys in such a hurry? The real show has just begun. Sit down nicely!"

Hearing Levi say this, the guests below seemed to remember something. The expressions on their faces changed, and eventually, they really sat down quietly.

At this point, Donald and I realized things were not going to be simple. The preparations we had made before entering were about to come in handy!

The wine tasting outside continued, and under the radar, Elliot and Benjamin had already begun to move with their team.

At that moment, Donald’s gaze cleared, I knew he had been coordinating the operation just a second ago. I took a deep breath and then raised my hand to hold his.

"We now proudly present the first auction item, a defense map of Royal City provided by Prince Henry and Mr. Levi. The starting bid is 100,000. The first auction starts now!"

"..."

Hearing what the auction item was, the room again fell into silence.

I slightly widened my eyes, uncontrollably stood up, walked two steps forward, and leaned my hands on the railing, saying coldly, "These people are insane!"

Donald immediately stood up following me, he stepped forward to my side and wrapped his arms around me, as if to calm me, [Don’t act rashly. Let’s wait for Henry to appear.]

[Okay, I understand.] I clenched the fan in my hand and responded to Donald.

Noticing the movements of me and Donald, the host downstairs looked up with a slight smile, somewhat excitedly saying, "It seems that the distinguished Ms. Diana is very interested in this defense map. Are you starting to bid? First come, first served!"

At his words, I exhaled slowly, suppressed the anger in my heart, and coldly observed the crowd below.

I was about to say something when suddenly a voice from the room next door said, "Everyone knows that Henry was defeated at Royal City, right? He himself had to flee from there in disgrace. Can I assume this defense map is fake? What use would it be to us?"

"Also, you set the starting bid at 100,000. Are you trying to show how audacious you are, or are you looking for praise? Mr. Levi, is this always your style?"

I almost instinctively looked over, but the wall effectively blocked any view. Donald and I could only hear the voice of the other party.

"Mr. Gino, what do you mean by that? Are you questioning the auctioneers?" The host’s volume increased, his face turning red as if he had been hit on a sore spot.

Utilizing the dim lights below, I saw Levi’s expression growing darker.

chapters.title: Chapter 335 Werewolf Gang
chapters.content:
Chapter 335: 335 Werewolf Gang

[Margaret’s Perspective]

The man known as Gino seemed to sneer disdainfully as he coldly continued, "Hmph! As a buyer, can’t I question the product a bit, or do you all find this absurd and ridiculous, this goddamn excuse of an auction?"

"You..."

"Rather, let’s call this a gathering of pathetic losers; that would be more apt!" Gino did not give the host any chance to retort, his words sharp and incredibly direct.

I quickly searched my mind for any significant figure named Gino and, finding none, turned to whisper to Donald beside me: [Who is this Gino? He seems to have a grudge against Henry and Levi.]

At this moment, a hint of amusement flickered in Donald’s eyes. He quietly lowered his head to look at me and replied, [An interesting person, I’ll introduce you after this is over.]

Seeing that Donald wasn’t surprised by this man’s appearance, I felt somewhat relieved and continued to watch the scene unfold below.

"Do you think that by locking down Sant Angelo, no one will disturb your ridiculous assembly? Then you are indeed naïve. The Lilo Sea is, after all, the Wolf Royal Family’s Lilo Sea, not something for a despicable coward like Henry and you to mess with! At least I, Gino, will not agree, nor will the Werewolf Gang of Lilo Sea."

"So, Mr. Gino knows he is just a small-time gang leader. I thought you also had armed forces, bold enough to take on me and Henry! Ha ha ha!"

Levi said as he rose from the sofa and, tilting his head, looked towards the second floor, "Cleaning up your Werewolf Gang is a bit troublesome, but not entirely impossible. As far as I know, you have no dealings with Donald; your protection of him surprises me, or perhaps, do you also want to be the Lycan King?"

"That’s none of your business. Like you lot, who thrive on the Royal Family’s benefits yet foolishly dream of usurping the throne, you naturally can’t understand the integrity of our Werewolf Gang. The affairs of the Royal City are for the Lycan King to worry about, not me, but right now you are in the Lilo Sea. As long as I, Gino, have a say here, I won’t allow you to mess around on this turf, got it?"

After listening to Gino’s statements, I suddenly realized what Donald meant by this person being interesting.

Though he is just a leader of the Lilo Sea Gang, he possesses an integrity and courage akin to that of a soldier, which is indeed valuable!

[He really does speak interestingly. If I were Henry, I would be furious by now.] I communicated with Donald via mind-link while slowly waving the fan in my hand.

Right now, there’s no need for Donald and me to take action, as there are already others causing trouble for Levi and Henry, which is indeed a delightful turn of events.

"It seems you are determined to oppose us, is that correct? I actually wouldn’t mind using your blood to clear the path for my and Henry’s revenge." Levi’s eyes flashed dangerously, as if he might surge forward and consume Gino’s flesh the next moment.

"Oh? I can understand Henry having a generational feud with the Lycan King, but you, Levi... what deep-seated vendetta do you have against Donald? Look at you, no hurry to draw your gun; I am genuinely curious about this!"

Seeing Levi angrily aiming his gun at him, Gino remained defiant, his face even filled with a provocative expression.

And just as Levi drew his gun, red-faced and ready to kill Gino on the spot, many armed people suddenly stood up around the hall!

They were all dressed in black suits, and if you looked closely, the patterns on their clothes were very similar to those on Gino’s, indicating they all belonged to the Lilo Sea Gang.

At this point, the servers under Levi’s command also stopped concealing their intentions, drawing their guns almost simultaneously and preparing for battle. Suddenly, the whole space seemed to be filled with the cold and lethal scent of firearms and ammunition.

From where I and Donald stood, the numbers of Gino’s and Levi’s men were nearly equal. If they were to clash here, neither side would gain any advantage!

Levi had long planned this auction, and the guests attending held significant influence. If a fight broke out carelessly causing injuries, dealing with the aftermath would be extremely complicated, which was not Levi and Henry’s ultimate goal.

For these reasons, the two opposing factions fell into a stalemate, the atmosphere chilling to an extreme.

Donald silently observed the situation unfolding before him, his thoughts inscrutable. I glanced at him and used the mind-link to ask: [This place is too narrow and crowded, hardly suitable for a large-scale fight. If these two start clashing directly, casualties among innocent bystanders will likely be inevitable. What are you planning to do, Donald?]

Donald naturally heard my question. He opened his arms to envelop me tightly in his embrace before replying, [If I were Henry, hiding all this time, I would think now is the crucial moment to intervene and control the situation, but why hasn’t he shown up yet?]

chapters.title: Chapter 336 trade
chapters.content:
Chapter 336: 336 trade

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I furrowed my brows slowly upon hearing his words, just as I was about to say something more to Donald, the situation on the field suddenly changed again!

"Levi, stop! Mr. Gino is an indispensable partner of ours, how can you treat him like this? Have your men put their guns away now!"

Donald and I were all too familiar with this abrupt voice, which belonged to the very person we had long been trying to capture, the leader of the Rebels who had escaped the Royal City not long ago and the traitor of the Wolf royal family — Henry!

This unforgivable traitor had finally appeared. A flash of light crossed my eyes, and I instinctively glanced at Donald beside me, "Shall we make our move now? He doesn’t seem to have brought many people with him!"

Donald’s gaze was icy and sharp, like poison darts fixed on Henry, and beyond that, he was far steadier than me, "He hasn’t noticed us here, let’s wait and see. I want to know what other plans he has up his sleeve."

I admitted Donald was better at keeping his composure than I was, and I understood why he chose to do so. Struggling to suppress the rage in my chest, I continued to observe Henry and Levi downstairs.

In the rebellion in the Royal City, we lost many of our outstanding Lycans, including Donald’s patrol captain, David. And all these blood debts were to be accounted for on Henry’s head; he couldn’t escape!

"Gino, don’t be angry, hasn’t Levi always been like this? I know all about the small fights you two have around the Lilo Sea. Today, we have something big to do. Can you two be a little calmer?" Henry said as he leisurely stood up from his wheelchair.

He looked around with a smile on his lips, seemingly very pleased with the surprised gazes directed at him.

"How, how is this possible? Wasn’t he just a crippled and powerless prince? Why is he able to stand up again now?"

"So the news that’s been spreading around is all true! MASTER has invented a Divine Medicine that can restore disabled werewolves to normal, and he himself has used it to create an army of unparalleled bravery. All these are true!"

"Good heavens! I heard about it before but didn’t dare to believe it was true. The ’Rebirth’ actually exists!"

"If Henry would sell me the formula for this potion, wouldn’t I make a fortune? This is worth so much more than regular pharmaceuticals; it’s a miracle!"

I always knew that merchants and officials were profit-driven, but I hadn’t expected Henry to cause such a sensation and evoke such greed by merely showcasing a bit of his power in front of them!

’Rebirth’ wasn’t any miraculous medicine at all; it was more like an evil key that could unlock the great gate of greed in people’s hearts — exactly the scenario Donald and I least wanted to see.

With his stance solidified, Henry paced back and forth under the scrutinizing eyes of the crowd, his mouth hanging with an apparently gentle and friendly smile, "Since everyone is dissatisfied with the first auction item, let’s move directly to the last and most treasured piece, shall we, ladies and gentlemen?"

A slight restlessness began in the room, and Donald and I exchanged a tacit glance, knowing the situation was dire.

"I am a person who very much enjoys making friends, something Levi can testify to. All the distinguished guests here today can be my, Henry’s, good friends. If you are willing to stand with me now, to rebel against this corrupt and deceitful dynasty, then I am willing to offer ’Rebirth’ unconditionally, to benefit and transform all werewolves who have suffered injustice and adversity!"

"Of course, those here have the right to choose first. If you are now willing to willingly call me king, then this miraculous medicine can be yours!"

Henry was always skilled at stirring people’s hearts; he raised his hands as if he was already that supreme monarch.

He gestured for Levi to put down the firearms, then turned with a smile and looked up to Gino on the second floor, declaring loudly, "Mr. Gino, I know your gang includes many brothers who are disabled due to injuries. If you wish, I can offer you fifty doses of Divine Medicine free of charge to help your brothers regain their healthy bodies! It’s a most worthwhile deal, take some time to consider it."

Gino, addressed by Henry, fell silent for several seconds. Just when Donald and I were sensing an ominous turn, he began to ask in a deep voice, "How can I be sure you’re not just bluffing? If your concoctions are really that miraculous, then why did you lose to Donald in the Royal City?"

"I heard that besides ’Rebirth,’ you also have a special Recovery Potion, that anyone who still has a breath can be pulled back from death’s clutches. Is that true?"

Seeming to find Gino’s question amusing, Henry’s expression took on a playful look. He let his hands fall and responded with feigned helplessness, "I didn’t expect that the news, which was strictly blockaded by the Royal City, would spread so quickly in the Lilo Sea. I have indeed underestimated everyone present here."

chapters.title: Chapter 337 ’Eternal Life
chapters.content:
Chapter 337: 337 ’Eternal Life

[Margaret’s perspective]

With my understanding of Henry the traitor, after he finished speaking just now, there must have been some other malicious ideas sprouting in his mind.

This paranoid and terrifying man was too adept at observing and controlling people’s hearts. Among the guests present, it seemed no one could match him, except for Donald.

"Yes, that’s right, I did indeed develop the potion you mentioned before, but it currently has some minor flaws... Of course, if Mr. Gino, you need it, I can offer it to you unconditionally, provided that you agree to help me with three things!"

As Henry finished speaking, the smile on his face became much more sincere. Had I not already witnessed his superb acting skills, I would have been deceived by him now. He was really good at concealing his true intentions, determined to achieve his goals at any cost!

"’Minor flaws’—what do you mean by that? This vague talk won’t do, as it doesn’t allow us to really trust you," Gino slowly lowered the gun in his hand, his previously confrontational attitude had eased somewhat, though he remained guarded.

I thought to myself that this Gino still retained a bit of sense, not to be lured in by Henry’s smooth talk. The mysterious potion they mentioned was the same one that nearly caused organ failure throughout Angel’s body, bringing her a step away from direct death—a lethal ’poison’.

Henry called these terrifying side effects and sequelae of the poison ’minor flaws,’ which truly was a huge joke. His underlying motives were incredibly malicious and insane. Donald and I could not continue to stand by idly, for Gino seemed genuinely interested in making such a trade!

After putting down his gun, Gino walked down the steps, approaching Henry in the center of the first floor, silently waiting for an affirmative answer from him.

"Alright, let me be honest with you. I’m not yet able to fully control the side effects of ’Eternal Life,’ but there are other ways to use it safely! I mean you can utilize it with my assistance, and should any situation arise, I can also handle it immediately. How does my suggestion sound to you, Mr. Gino?"

Seemingly unable to resist Gino’s gaze, Henry put on a helpless expression and spoke in an extremely gentle tone.

Seeing the wariness in Gino’s face relax, I had a very bad premonition. After thinking it over, I tapped my folding fan against the windowsill.

The transaction between Henry and Gino was underway, and the scene was very quiet, so the noise I made was like a thunderclap out of the blue, quickly attracting the attention of many people, including Levi, who had been watching Gino with a cold face.

"Ms. Diana? Do you want to say something? Please, speak up!"

Levi spoke with a gleam in his eyes, as if I were some sort of glowing mobile vault, highly captivating his attention.

I unfolded my fan and curled my lips into a smile, though my eyes behind the mask held no warmth, "After hearing so much, I’ve become interested in Mr. Henry’s goods. One can never have too many business partners, and I think we might sit down and have a detailed talk. I will show you my sincerity."

Levi had not seen me before. I was confident he would have no suspicion of me, but as for Henry, I wasn’t sure if my altered voice would fool him, since we had spent some time together before.

"Oh? So Ms. Diana is also interested in our goods, that’s wonderful. We are in great need of powerful partners like you! A detailed talk is certainly possible, but it will have to wait a moment..."

"Ambition is indeed necessary in business, but more often, what you need is to learn how to assess the situation and control the scene. The stakes at Lilo Sea are still too small; we can talk bigger! You two had better think it through. I am giving you an opportunity right now, and I hope you don’t disappoint me."

I interrupted Levi, gently swaying the fan and leaning on the windowsill. Donald, standing motionless, half-embraced my waist. The lights on the second floor were somewhat dim, making it not very clear to see from downstairs, and his elaborate mask also concealed him very well.

My ideas and plans were certainly compelling enough for Levi. His struggle with Gino made it clear to me that he was not the true ruler of Lilo Sea, at least not the sole authority here! Therefore, an external and very capable partner was crucial; he would not reject the wealthy and powerful ’Diana.’ He needed ’Diana’s’ support.

What puzzled me somewhat was Henry’s continued silence. He shouldn’t have noticed anything amiss, but his gaze in our direction was unreadable.

"You remind me of a friend I know; talking about her makes me miss her already."

After being silent for a while, Henry suddenly spoke, his comment—seemingly out of nowhere—must have sounded quite baffling to the others.

"A ’friend’? Who might that be?" Levi furrowed his brow and asked almost reflexively.

His expression seemed to question, how could you have a friend I don’t know?

chapters.title: Chapter 338 Recognized it
chapters.content:
Chapter 338: 338 Recognized it

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"She is a very smart and beautiful person, but her judgment of character isn’t great; she happened to fall for a haughty, hypocritical, and despicable werewolf."

After Henry finished speaking, his face even showed some regret, as if he was recalling something unpleasant.

[Could he be talking about us?]

I didn’t actually want to make this connection, but it was quite obvious that Henry meant us; I stealthily mind-linked with Donald while my speechlessness was nearly breaking through the heavens.

At that moment, Donald took a deep breath and asked with a cold smirk, "He says you’re his friend, did you know about this?"

Unexpectedly, Donald suddenly spoke; I turned to look at him with a somewhat puzzled gaze.

"There’s no need to pretend anymore, Margaret. He’s clearly recognized us," Donald said with a snicker, raising his hand to take off his mask, his gaze towards the downstairs filled with icy coldness and murderous intent.

I widened my eyes in surprise, feeling Donald’s arm wrap tightly around my waist, his whole body radiating an aura of cold deterrence!

Donald seemed to be angry.

I suppressed my surprise at the bottom of my heart and slowly withdrew my gaze from Donald, speaking in a calm voice, "It seems I was careless and exposed us; I didn’t expect him to be so sharp. Are Elliot and Benjamin prepared on their end?"

"Just waiting for my command."

Upon hearing this, I nodded slightly, my eyes landing on Henry downstairs; I spoke while conversing with Donald, "So what do we do now? The wanted criminal won’t just stand there waiting for us."

Just as Donald said, Henry seemed to have seen through our identities early on. Even when Donald took off his mask, there wasn’t the slightest sign of surprise or fear on Henry’s face, as if he really just met an old friend.

I really didn’t like the look on his face at the moment; I frowned and said coldly, "You might not be as lucky as you were in Royal City. Our people are outside both doors; you won’t get away, Henry!"

"Hahaha! Who says I’m leaving? This is Lilo Sea, Margaret. What makes you think those few Lycans under you can control the situation? Levi, the tyrant of Lilo Sea, is not a vegetarian!"

Henry suddenly burst into crazed laughter, turning to look at Donald with a gaze that grew dark and venomous.

Of course, Donald wasn’t afraid of a lunatic like this. His other hand, which had been hanging down, somehow grabbed a gun. As he raised the gun, he said, "Let’s try then, and see who wins."

"Bang!"

The gunshot rang out, and the next second, the guests on the first floor scattered like birds and beasts, occasionally punctuated by a scream or two!

As if anticipating that Donald wouldn’t hold back, the moment Henry saw Donald starting to raise his hand, he quickly pulled the host standing beside him to shield himself.

The bullet pierced the host’s chest and finally lodged into Henry’s shoulder. With a muffled grunt, he let go of the human shield and took several agile steps to hide behind a few pillars on the left side of the first floor.

His escape route was crystal clear to me; I raised my voice urgently, "Henry is behind the pillars on the left; he’s trying to get away!"

Donald, gripping me, quickly retreated backward, skillfully dodging the bullets sprayed by the enemies on the first floor. His voice low, he said, "Eliot is guarding the left door; he won’t get away today. Stay here and take care of yourself; I’m going to check out the situation."

I nodded in agreement, knowing that in such a confined area, I might not necessarily be able to help Donald, "Got it, I have a gun to protect myself; be careful."

Donald kissed my forehead, then swiftly made his way down the stairs, his body bent. His marksmanship was excellent—a spitting image of efficiency as he cleared the way of enemies blocking his path.

I crouched at the top of the second-floor stairs, my gun aimed directly at a black-clothed waiter, ending his life with a single shot. The next second, Donald stepped over his body.

[Well done.] Donald said.

I slightly lifted the corners of my mouth without responding, only covering Donald’s attack movements with even more caution and care.

Suddenly, I saw Gino hidden behind the podium, holding a gun and looking at Donald’s advancing back with a grave expression.

I retracted my gaze upon a thought, leaning against the wall to dodge several passing bullets, and then loudly said, "Mr. Gino, don’t be truly deceived by Henry! Not a word he says can be trusted. That ’Eternal Life’ potion is no Divine Medicine; it’s all lies to deceive you."

Backed against the wall, I didn’t see the change in Gino’s expression. He glanced sidelong at my position, then after a silence of two seconds, asked, "If you and Donald sneaked in early, why didn’t you stop them from the start? How many people did you bring into Lilo Sea this time? These two lunatics aren’t going to let you off lightly!"

I laughed at the remark and replied, "Don’t worry, Donald has it all planned out."

chapters.title: Chapter 339 The battle begins
chapters.content:
Chapter 339: 339 The battle begins

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"Besides, I can tell you that the Wolf royal family can give you what you want. You don’t need to make any deals with those vermin! Donald just said you are a very interesting person. I would really like to get to know you."

Shouting so loudly had made my throat a bit dry, I swallowed some saliva, waiting for Gino to make a decision at this moment.

If he was really fooled by Henry’s words, then the situation inside would be a bit tricky—Donald’s fighting skills were good, but not enough to withstand several rounds of attacks from their numbers!

Elliot and Benjamin were probably being held up at the door by Levi’s men and couldn’t break through to help anytime soon. So, Gino’s stance, with several of his men, was crucial.

I didn’t want Donald to get hurt again, so securing Gino’s support was critical. Seeing no response from the other side, I opened my mouth to say more, but suddenly, gunfire erupted like a torrential rain, bombarding the wooden corridor behind me.

’They have already discovered my location,’ I thought, quickly ducking down and crawling on the ground, my ear pressed to the floor, trying to gauge the number of enemies pouring in from the stairwell.

Mixed with the sound of gunfire were chaotic footsteps. I realized there were too many of them, and this was no longer a safe place to hide, so I quickly stood up and ran into the second floor.

In the corridor, a few people occasionally poked their heads out to carefully check around. I quickly passed by and, lowering my voice, warned, "Stay hidden, don’t come out, and don’t open the door again. Go inside!"

Seemingly surprised that I would speak out to warn them, a few people hesitated before seemingly making up their minds. One young girl then asked tremulously, "Do you need help? Do you want to come in and hide?"

"There are many officials and big shots on the second floor. Levi wouldn’t dare to mess around. You can come in and hide a bit!"

Taking a brief moment to think, I listened to the footsteps behind me getting closer and quickly made a decision.

"Thank you!" I said, quickly sidestepping into the room. As my skirt brushed against the door frame, I narrowly dodged a bullet and hurried over to the window. The rooms on the second floor had large, ornate windows designed to offer a good view of the items downstairs.

I glanced at the main door on the first floor to the right, which Levi’s men had blocked, and the air outside was filled with gunshots and the stench of blood!

I felt a bit nauseous and frowned, but the children in the room thought I was worried and scared.

"My name is Brand, the director of the Marine Supervision Bureau of Lilo Sea is my father. I really hope I can help you, Your Highness the Lycan Queen!" the boy who called himself Brand said excitatively, his head crowned with striking red hair.

Surprised, I looked at him and after two seconds of silence, I asked, "How do you know who I am? Aren’t you afraid of mistaking me for someone else?"

"You said your father is the director of the Marine Supervision Bureau. Do you realize the problem with being seen here? Where is your father?"

Brand was a very smart boy. He quickly understood the deeper meaning in my words and hurriedly waved his hands to explain, "Please don’t misunderstand, Your Highness, my father definitely doesn’t know about today’s events. My sister and I sneaked out to have fun, and our family doesn’t know about it.

"Of course, we have no connection with the rebels downstairs. We were tricked into coming here today, and most of the people here were, too!"

I didn’t want to suspect a child, but his words were full of oddities and discrepancies that forced me to be on guard.

My silent expression must have seemed oppressive to them. Brand opened his mouth to say more, but I raised my hand to stop him, "I know about this. I will jump out of the window in a moment. Just don’t open the door for those people. Don’t peek out anymore, and be careful."

With that, I steadied myself on the window ledge and, in the moment of leaping out, I completed the shift into my wolf form, which allowed me to land steadily on the ground floor.

"Wait, I..."

"Ah! Be careful!"

"Whoa, that’s pretty incredible!"

The exclamations from the three kids hiding by the window were outright ignored by me, as I was busy dodging enemy bullets and trying to find a way to open the right-side main door, enabling the Lycans at the door to come in and join us.

Betty hadn’t been out in a long time, and now she was quite literally guiding me in a "dance" amidst a barrage of bullets. ["These people are really dumb; even the least skilled Lycan of the Royal City could do better."]

Listening to Betty’s complaints, I cracked a faint smile, then suddenly a weird premonition hit me, [Yes, Levi’s men aren’t strong fighters, so why can’t Elliot break through? Something must have happened!]

With this conclusion, a sense of unease welled up inside me. Betty, hearing this, moved even faster, as she, like me, cared a lot about Donald and his group’s situation.

chapters.title: Chapter 340 days of rooftop struggle
chapters.content:
Chapter 340: 340 days of rooftop struggle

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I swatted away one of the men in black with a swipe of my claw, the wolf eyes shining with a bone-chilling cold light, "Get in my way again and I’ll kill you all! Get out of my way!"

With my body, I barged through the tightly shut doors. Just then, Betty suddenly shrieked a warning: "Move out of the way, Margaret, they’ve thrown a bomb in!"

"What?" I instinctively voiced out in question, but my body was already making evasive maneuvers. In the instant before the bomb exploded, Betty and I leaped backwards with almost supernatural speed!

’Bang!’

The massive sound caused the entire floor to tremble slightly. I crashed into the seats behind me, smashing through several chairs and sofas, nearly getting buried under them.

In such dangerous circumstances, I couldn’t stay put. Shaking my dizzy head, my wolf eyes turned toward the thoroughly ravaged doorway!

If inside was the battlefield, then outside must be hell, right? This question flickered through my mind as I stepped outside.

There were no longer any living people standing beyond the door, only blood and shards of glass on the floor, and of course, various corpses strewn haphazardly!

I was relieved not to see Benjamin or any of the others among them, but the scene still sent shivers down my spine and chilled my heart.

"Donald? Benjamin? Where are you guys now?"

No one responded to my question, so I frowned, turned, and left, heading toward another set of doors where Donald had been chasing Henry.

Because I had just created an exit, those who were still alive began to flee towards it, turning the scene into utter chaos. However, I had no energy to care about that now—my mind was consumed with concern for Donald and his group.

The men who pursued me on the second floor were merely a small part of Levi’s forces, while Donald and Elliot faced everyone he and Henry could muster in outright rebellion!

"Donald just chased them to the rooftop..."

This was Gino’s voice; I had been talking to him upstairs earlier.

I glanced sideways at him. Gino also looked in pretty bad shape, having apparently fought ferociously. He was now leaning against a wall, catching his breath!

"Thank you." I said, then headed out without further discussion.

Gino seemed taken aback by my urgency. He raised his hand, wanting to stop me but failed, "The rooftop is too dangerous, Donald told me to wait for you here...Hey, wait, don’t go!"

His voice of restraint was left far behind as I rushed forward, only wanting to ensure Donald’s safety.

Sant Angelo truly deserved its reputation as the most luxurious hotel in Lilo Sea. Its sprawling grounds were part of the reason why we were split into two battlefields.

The night breeze from the sea carried the stench of blood, and when Gino and I led the rest of the group to the rooftop, the battle was nearing its end.

I surveyed all those on the rooftop, returned to human form, and, holding a gun, slowly approached. The first to notice my arrival was Benjamin slumped on the ground, his face covered in bloodstains and dust. Even he seemed surprised to see me, "Lycan Queen, how did you come here? Didn’t Gino protect you well?"

I ignored Benjamin’s surprise and questions, directly asking, "Where’s Donald? Why did you suddenly change the battlefield, and why did no one inform me?"

At this, Benjamin stared at me in a daze. I furrowed my brows and continued, "That’s not important, where is he?"

The disheveled Medical Practitioner obviously knew whom I was asking about. He pointed to a spot not far off, surrounded by a crowd, and briefly answered, "Henry is like a madman, completely out of his mind. His Majesty is engaging with him trying to capture him personally! His subordinates are desperate felons, Your Highness, be careful if you go over there!"

I nodded at Benjamin, signaling that I understood, "You be careful too. Look after the other wounded here; we’ll be back soon."

As my gaze stayed fixed in that direction, a silver-white figure suddenly appeared in my peripheral vision—it was Wolf!

With a ’snap,’ the silver wolf was brutally slammed to the ground, and the dark wolf immediately lunged forward, its jaws wide open, aiming for Wolf’s neck.

I was stunned and yelled hoarsely, "Be careful, Donald!"

But all that answered me were the clouds of dust on the ground and the agonizing cry from Donald that pierced my heart!

I rushed forward, my hand holding the gun trembling nonstop. I wanted to shoot to cover Donald, but as I watched the two wolves, rapidly entangled and biting in the midst of the fray, my reaction quickly became the same as that of Elliot and those nearby.

As it turned out, the Lycans surrounding the edge of the battle were not unwilling to help; in such a brawl, nobody could lend a hand!

Their entwined bodies were too close; I dared not fire, afraid of hitting Donald by mistake, and so did Elliot.

He glanced at me, distractedly asking, "Are you alright, Your Highness? We were too late to fetch you and had to entrust Mr. Gino to keep you safe on the first floor. Are you okay?"

I shook my head, my nerves stretched tight, forcing myself to quickly calm down, "I’m fine. Tell me what’s happened so far!"

chapters.title: Chapter 341 ’Completely Beastified
chapters.content:
Chapter 341: 341 ’Completely Beastified

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Upon hearing my question, Elliot nodded and swallowed, as if trying to calm his overly tense nerves.

"Benjamin and I split into two teams under His Majesty’s arrangements, and initially, everything went smoothly. Levi’s men were not strict with their guard outside, as if they were not worried about any mishaps at all! In fact, for an event of today’s scale, their security was very inadequate. I reported this to His Majesty right from the start—it was very strange."

Elliot spoke while furrowing his brows tightly, his gaze fixed on Donald and Henry in the center of the room; their struggle continued, and both Elliot and I were incredibly tense.

I listened to his report in silence, but a cold light slowly emerged in my eyes.

Earlier inside, Levi acted recklessly without any concern, which did not seem like a man unprepared or without backup. Today’s auction was attended by the rich and powerful; how could he and Henry determine that no one would object or make a scene? For instance, Gino, who disrupted the event right at the start!

Levi could not have been unaware of Gino’s attendance, and knowing his archenemy was present, why didn’t he prepare more manpower just in case? This was truly puzzling.

Elliot was right; today’s events, every single aspect, reeked of strangeness!

My silence did not deter Elliot. I took a deep breath, looked at him, and signaled him to continue.

Seeing my gesture, Elliot continued without hesitation: "Since entering the inner area required a special qualification, Benjamin and I kept looking for a chance to sneak in, but without success. By then, we realized the auction’s defense was lax on the outside but tight on the inside. If we wanted to break in and assist you and His Majesty, there was no other way but to confront them directly."

As Elliot spoke, he clenched his pistol tightly and pulled the trigger, ’Bang!’

The sudden gunshot drew everyone’s attention. I instinctively looked towards where Elliot’s pistol was pointed, and there, a black-clad werewolf collapsed, a large hole blown open in his forehead, blood pouring out.

"His eyes turned red, and that’s the precursor to their full bestial transformation," Elliot explained.

"’Full bestial transformation’? What do you mean?" I instinctively asked back.

Elliot, holding his pistol, swiftly changed the magazine, his eyes coldly fixed on the few enemies standing in the corner of the rooftop. They were all wounded but showed no sign of surrender, their expressions cold and indifferent.

"You must have seen all the battle conditions on your way here," he said, his lips curling into a cold smile, a glint of killing intent flashing in his eyes, "All these are the handiwork of these beasts. When we engaged them in battle, these maniacs began to attack indiscriminately, slaying both our Lycans and other innocent guests present."

"They are a group of beasts who have lost their nature, with only slaughter and destruction in their eyes! We had to protect ourselves from attacks and also try our best to protect others, so our movements were somewhat restricted in the fight. Following this, His Majesty issued an order to break through. Benjamin and I split up to attack from two different points, but suddenly, a group of assassins emerged, their combat style much more swift and fierce, like professional killers!"

"Benjamin and I were both held up, and by that time, the fight involving you and His Majesty must have also begun inside. In a desperate situation, Benjamin and I had no choice but to focus on breaking through the inner defenses! However, unexpectedly, His Majesty soon broke through their blockade from the inside, chasing the wounded Master all the way up to the rooftop."

By this point, I had already grasped the full situation. Watching Henry pinned down on the ground by Donald, I remained silent for two breaths before speaking, "So Benjamin also followed you guys up here, and is that why there’s no one guarding the right side door?"

Out of the corner of my eye, I saw Elliot nod, indicating that this was indeed the case.

Understanding this, I glanced at the few remaining goons across from us and, frowning, asked, "Henry is here, but where is Levi?"

Upon hearing this, Elliot’s expression paused, and he turned to look at me, "Wasn’t Levi always downstairs? Did you not see him?"

I slowly narrowed my eyes, quickly recalling the events, and then replied affirmatively, "No, all those chasing me were his men. I didn’t see him in person."

As I spoke, a flicker of unease crossed my mind. I turned to the person beside me, "Mr. Gino, did you see Levi? I’m talking about after the battle inside started."

Seeing Gino shaking his head next to me, Elliot and I almost simultaneously confirmed something...

Just then, the sound of a helicopter rising from the back of the Sant Angelo Building approached from afar. The helicopter’s large rotors sliced through the sky, and the strong winds they kicked up caused the clothes of those near the edge of the rooftop to flutter wildly!

chapters.title: Chapter 342 Fierce Battle
chapters.content:
Chapter 342: 342 Fierce Battle

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I lifted my arm to shield my face, barely blocking the dust stirred up by the helicopter wings, while Donald and Henry slightly separated from their entanglement at that moment.

Since Donald and I weren’t far apart, I could even hear his heavy breathing and the smell of blood brought by the wind!

"Donald," I lowered my hand and called out his name against the strong wind, "Are you alright? Don’t get too close to them, come here a bit!"

Just as the words behind me fell, the people inside the helicopter had already opened the cabin door and set up a machine gun!

’Bang bang bang!’

Bullets rained down on Donald like droplets; he agilely stepped back a few steps, narrowly avoiding the enemy’s gunfire. When he turned back, his strong forelimbs slightly lowered, his gaze fiercely fixed on Henry who was still paralyzed on the spot.

Henry ultimately couldn’t withstand Donald, gaining no advantage in their brawl-like fight.

His shoulder seemed as if it had been torn, fresh blood dripped and soaked the ground as he panted heavily through his wolf mouth, appearing severely injured.

However, even so, he slowly got up from the ground with the cover of firepower from the helicopter, lifting his dark wolf head to look at Donald and sneered, "That’s all for today’s play. We’ll meet again soon."

’Bang!’

The response to Henry was a few unceremonious bullets; I gripped my gun tightly, almost feeling the startling heat produced by the bullets rapidly grazing the gun’s muzzle.

"Ah! Margaret!" Henry staggered to the side as he was hit by the bullets, coldly calling out my name.

Elliot and the assault team’s Lycan finally had something to do; they provided cover fire for me while quickly figuring out how to deal with the guy on the helicopter.

"Shut up! It’s the same lines every time, aren’t you tired of them? We all find it tedious!" I said, swiftly moving towards Donald’s direction, my gun’s muzzle tightly following Henry’s evasive figure.

While I was trading barbs with Henry, the gunfire from the helicopter suddenly stopped, followed by Levi appearing.

"Stop it all! This bloody battle is pointless. Henry is definitely coming with me today, no one can stop me!" After shouting this, Levi pulled a blood-soaked person from behind him.

He struck the man’s head hard with the butt of the gun, then stepped on him, his gun muzzle rubbing against the man’s temple, without pausing, he continued, "Do you still want this man’s life? I guess part of the reason you’re here is for these few people, right?"

"It’s Eric and the others!" Elliot’s eyes widened in shock; he froze, holding his gun. Soon, as if realizing something unbelievable, he continued, "That’s not right, how could Eric be in Levi’s hands? Our previous information showed they were attacked by an unknown group of assassins and then disappeared from the dock!"

At this, I glanced at Elliot and said thoughtfully, "It seems not only are we adept at stealth assaults, but the enemy is as well."

Levi, clearly hearing my conversation with Elliot, stomped on the person beneath his foot; a fierce gleam shone in his eyes as he coldly said, "So you’re called Eric, huh? You didn’t say anything even when tortured so brutally before, and now I still found out, didn’t I?"

"To be honest, I admire your loyalty to your master, but there’s really no choice. You might really have to die today! Who made your master Donald? And I need to exchange your little lives for Henry!"

Levi said this and looked up at Donald, who was on the edge of the rooftop, his cold gaze felt like being targeted by a venomous snake, "What do you think of my proposal, your Majesty? Exchanging these loyal subordinates of yours for the worthless life of Henry, what do you think?"

The Eric under Levi’s foot seemed unconscious, his half-body hanging outside the helicopter being tossed around by the strong wind, and even dark red liquid could be seen dripping down his hair!

"Levi! You are an utter madman."

This was Gino’s summary of Levi’s crazed actions, which I found quite fitting.

I had no doubt about the authenticity in Levi’s words, I thought if Donald refused to trade with him, he would really kick the man off the helicopter, like discarding a rotten watermelon!

"Do you know what the consequences of your actions are? What the outcome of collaborating with Henry is, do I need to spell it out for you again? How Angel and Austin died, you must remember that, right?"

Donald spoke, transformed from his wolf form to human form, looked at Levi with an unyielding gaze, his presence equally intimidating.

"To be honest, I really want to know what Henry promised you to make you betray the entire dynasty and me like this. For your Aunt Licia’s sake, can you tell me why?"

Donald’s voice wasn’t very loud, but it was clear enough for everyone present to hear; I turned back to look at him, quickly understanding what he truly intended to do.

chapters.title: Chapter 343 Extreme
chapters.content:
Chapter 343: 343 Extreme

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Just like me, Elliot shared an unspoken understanding with Donald. Utilizing the chance when Donald distracted the attention on the field, he slowly approached the helicopter with a few Lycans.

Donald, of course, would not abandon Eric and the others, and at the same time, he didn’t want Henry to escape from him again. That would be too unfair to those who had died in this rebellion war!

Levi didn’t respond to Donald’s question immediately. Holding his gun, he looked at Henry, who had already collapsed to the ground, and after a long pause finally continued, "You wouldn’t understand these things. You have stood high above for too long. It’s time for you to fall and see!"

"Does your aunt know about your trip south? Did she cry and make a fuss to stop you? Speaking of which, I really haven’t seen her for a long time. I should have attended your and Margaret’s wedding to ’congratulate’ you, but who knew about Martha..."

"Enough, Levi! If you keep babbling, we really won’t be able to leave today!"

The one who interrupted Levi was Henry; he swayed as he stood up, seeming to want to sprint towards the helicopter.

Realizing his intention, Donald and I almost simultaneously took action. I fired two shots at the ground tiles near his feet, while Donald quickly completed his shift and pounced directly at Henry.

Everything happened too quickly, too quickly for the helicopter’s gunner to react. Wolf, carrying Henry, smashed through the roof’s railing and almost fell off!

This was the 22nd floor of the building; even with the robust physical qualities of a werewolf, falling from such a height unscathed was impossible! More likely, they would smash into a pulp, beyond any hope of recovery.

This was something I absolutely couldn’t accept. I screamed Donald’s name hoarsely, completed my shift in half a second, and transformed into a streak of light, shooting out!

As Donald was about to be pulled off by Henry, I bit onto his hind leg with my mouth. Although this method was clumsily hilarious, the rescue effect was astonishing, slowing their descent considerably.

Soon, more Lycans rushed up to help pull them up. My stress dropped drastically, I spit out the silver fur from my mouth, and looked eye to eye with Henry hanging in the mid-air. "Levi can’t save you!" I declared.

A sneer flickered through Henry’s icy wolf eyes, as if he was talking to himself, "Is that so?" he said.

The next moment, Henry did something that made everyone’s jaw drop. He swatted away Donald’s wolf head, opened his mouth, and bit off his own hind leg that was being held, falling directly in the posture of hanging upside down!

"Don’t you think of escaping!" This was Donald’s roaring voice.

I tensed up every nerve in my brain and shouted urgently, "Donald! Get back here!"

My voice acted like a powerful seal; Donald immediately crouched in place, his wolf eyes regaining some lucidity. He was about to jump down to kill Henry!

"Quick! Pull Donald back." I said to the Lycans beside me and quickly stretched my neck to look over the edge of the roof.

Henry’s figure continued to fall. I couldn’t understand why he chose such a sure path to death. Did he think that smashing into pieces was a better fate than falling into my and Donald’s hands?

This curious thought was soon answered. How could someone as unkillable and sneaky as Henry easily decide to face death willingly? He still had a backup plan!

As we and Henry reignited the battle alert, Levi had already made a certain decision. Without hesitation, he kicked Eric out of the cabin and directed the helicopter to sweep backward and downward, clearly aiming to rendezvous with Henry.

He took a black crossbow from a subordinate’s hand, squinting as he focused on Henry falling.

Then, without hesitation, he pulled the trigger. The specially made crossbow bolt, with a steel cable attached, swiftly shot downward, the next second piercing through Henry’s shoulder. The bolt head locked onto Henry’s bone, the cable instantly taut, miraculously holding onto Henry’s falling body!

I widened my eyes at this scene, swallowed hard, and incredulously turned back to look at Levi in the cabin. He just waved his hand at the pilot behind him, smiled at me with a light smirk, and said a word before turning his head and disappearing into the cabin.

The noise of the helicopter blades was loud, but it didn’t carry his words to my ears. Still, by clearly reading his lips, I knew, he said: "See you next time!"

I took a deep breath, feeling like I was about to explode on the spot from anger! Henry, Levi, each one of them was a fanatic extremist, a madman to the core. Ordinary people could never truly understand what was going through their minds.

Henry’s bitten-off hind leg was thrown aside by Donald, who then walked over to me, gasping heavily. In a hoarse voice, he asked, "Are you alright, Margaret? Are you hurt?"

I didn’t turn back to look at Donald, just stared at the helicopter disappearing into the night sky and muttered dejectedly, "The enemy is really too terrifying. I’m beginning to question what is really going on with this world. I..."

chapters.title: Chapter 344 Crossbow
chapters.content:
Chapter 344: 344 Crossbow

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Donald noticed something was wrong with me and immediately reached out his arms to embrace me, when suddenly everything went black before my eyes, and I lost all consciousness, collapsing directly into Donald’s arms!

As I closed my eyes and shut out all disturbances from the outside world, a soft, gentle voice suddenly appeared in my mind.

At that moment, my will was blurry, and I couldn’t identify where the voice came from or who it belonged to; I just felt very tired and in desperate need of a good sleep.

What I didn’t know was that my fainting had nearly scared Donald out of his wits.

[Donald’s Perspective]

The sound of the wing slicing through the air gradually faded, and the maniac Henry was finally taken away by Levi.

The wind blew against my face, and as the Lycans expressed their concern, I regained all my senses. As I unconsciously searched for that figure in the crowd, Benjamin spoke up to remind me.

"Your Majesty, the Lycan Queen is on your left hand."

I glanced sideways, slowly got up, and walked toward Margaret.

She stood alone at the edge of the rooftop, her gaze somewhat dazed as she looked into the void. Her face was extremely pale, the usually plump lips now colorless, and she looked incredibly weak, almost as if the wind might carry her away at any moment.

I softly asked her how she was, and she started mumbling to herself, but before she could finish her sentence, she suddenly went limp and collapsed in my arms.

"Margaret! What’s wrong, darling?" I exclaimed, my voice shaking with fear as I crouched down to hold Margaret in my arms.

Her body was abnormally cold. I turned my head to call Benjamin, "Margaret has fainted suddenly, come quickly and check on her!"

Benjamin immediately ran over and quickly checked Margaret’s condition.

It only took him a few seconds to ascertain, and he looked up at me somewhat anxiously, "The Lycan Queen’s body temperature is too low. We need to move her immediately, Your Majesty; I need some equipment to properly assess her condition."

I immediately agreed to Benjamin’s suggestion as the environment was indeed poor, not suitable for a prolonged stay. "Have Elliot bring Eric and the others, we need to relocate immediately!"

As I spoke, I held Margaret and stood up, feeling a sharp pain in my leg, bit down hard to suppress it, and led everyone towards the lower floors.

I certainly wouldn’t be without a place to stay in the Lilo Sea; in fact, I was well-prepared for this scenario.

The conflict with Henry and Levi might not go smoothly. To call it a protracted battle wouldn’t be an exaggeration. After all, the Lilo Sea is a region both the Wolf royal family and I find challenging!

The Lilo Sea, White Rose Manor.

I was isolated outside the door by Benjamin and a group of medical staff, standing somewhat helplessly in the corridor.

When Elliot briskly approached with a few Lycans, I finally regained my senses and heard Elliot report in a grave tone, "Your Majesty, Eric has severe injuries; we need Benjamin to check on him immediately! Also, there’s a large crowd outside the manor gates, all requesting to see you."

I wasn’t concerned about who had come at that moment; after all, these people had ulterior motives, and I could afford to keep them waiting before dealing with them slowly one by one.

I turned to look at Elliot and softly replied, "Understood, the situation inside should end soon. Eric really owes you this time; had you not acted decisively to pull him back from such a height, he might not have survived today."

I had learned about Elliot’s rooftop rescue of Eric during the journey here from Benjamin; today, indeed, everyone had a hair-raising experience.

At this, Elliot bowed his head, his expression a bit forlorn, "We definitely had to save Eric, but it’s a pity we couldn’t capture Henry and Levi today."

"Speaking of which, those two lunatics are really ruthless. Who would have thought Henry would go to the extent of biting off his own leg to escape? And Levi, wasn’t he afraid that an arrow might kill Henry outright?"

I quietly inhaled at this moment, my gaze shifting to the glass windows in the corridor, memories from my childhood flashing through my mind, "Henry was always such a person, willing to resort to anything to achieve his goals. As for Levi, I truly underestimated him this time. Deep down, he is the same as Henry. Remember that crossbow he used at the end? I don’t believe that was from the Lilo Sea."

"Your Majesty, are you suggesting..." Elliot struggled to keep up with my thought process, his brow furrowing deeply.

I nodded and affirmed his guess, "That crossbow likely came from the Windstorm Pack, it was made by Austin."

I said, breaking into a laugh, but my eyes filled with sarcasm and gloom, I continued with a sigh, "Austin has always enjoyed tinkering with such things. The power of that crossbow differs vastly from ordinary bows. Of all the people I know, only he would have the skill to make such a device."

chapters.title: Chapter 345 fantastic news
chapters.content:
Chapter 345: 345 fantastic news

[Donald’s Perspective]

I hadn’t expected Austin and Levi’s collusion with Henry to organize the Rebels to have a breakthrough so soon.

There wasn’t even a need to wait for Eric to regain consciousness and report to me all the clues and evidence he had gathered at Lilo Sea, for if I thought about it carefully, it wasn’t hard to trace the subtle connections between these events.

The fact that Levi and Henry had colluded with the Rebels was indisputable, and the peculiar crossbow that had just appeared in his hands confirmed all our suspicions and investigations. Today’s fierce battle wasn’t in vain.

"Your Majesty, are you saying that the crossbow is from the batch of smuggled weapons that Eric had been investigating, and that it was personally made by Austin?" Elliot had an epiphany, though he was still asking, his heart had already accepted my conjecture.

I nodded slightly, thought for a moment, and then continued, "When Henry escaped from the Royal City last time, didn’t he take Austin’s body with him? Did you notice anything else unusual when you went to secretly investigate Levi today?"

At my words, Elliot bowed his head, deep in thought for a moment before shaking his head and answering, "No, we weren’t even sure when Henry would come. Besides Aiden, his lieutenant, there was no one else I recognized."

What Elliot meant was that he hadn’t seen neither Austin nor Lillian. It was as if the siblings had vanished directly into the Lilo Sea.

"Alright, I understand. You all have worked hard these past few days. Get some good rest tonight; I’ll arrange for others to take over the investigation," I said, waving my hand to indicate that he and his companions could leave. When I turned back to look at the door, Benjamin had already opened it and stepped out.

The moment he saw me, his eyes lit up and he hurriedly took off his mask, speaking to me with an excitement that was hard to contain, "Your Majesty! The Lycan Queen hasn’t been injured. It’s just that she is weak and had been under intense stress all day, which is why she fainted from exhaustion. You don’t need to worry too much."

Hearing Benjamin explain that Margaret was not seriously harmed was a huge relief for me.

I let out a deep breath and nodded, saying, "Good, now I know. Eric’s condition isn’t looking good, you..."

"Also, there is another piece of excellent news I need to report to you—Her Majesty the Queen is pregnant! She is already four weeks along!"

Benjamin and I spoke almost simultaneously, our voices colliding in the corridor and rendering me speechless!

I had heard every word Benjamin had said, but it seemed incomprehensible. My mouth hung open as I looked at him, and after a moment, I repeated, "’Her Majesty the Queen is pregnant’?"

"You mean Margaret is pregnant! She is carrying my child. I am going to be a father?" I suddenly asked Benjamin loudly, an air of silliness enveloping me.

Benjamin seemed startled by my reaction. He paused before continuing with a smile, "Yes, Your Majesty, you are going to be a father! There will be a little prince or princess in the Wolf royal family soon!"

I felt as if my heart had been injected with a rush of warmth, my brain releasing dopamine at breakneck speeds, enveloping me in foolish happiness.

"Then... Margaret is alright? What do we need to do now? Should I go in and stay with her?" I asked excitedly, bursting with energy.

Benjamin’s face showed a kind smile as he calmly assured me, "I’ve already administered a nutrient injection to Her Majesty the Queen. Right now, all she needs is a good night’s sleep. With a bit of caution, ensuring that Her Majesty doesn’t get hurt or overly stressed, there shouldn’t be any major issues."

"Of course, you can go in and stay with Her Majesty now. I need to go examine Eric’s wounds first, but I will come back later to check on Her Majesty’s condition. Don’t worry," he said.

Benjamin bowed respectfully and walked away, a smile still on his face.

I paid no mind to the teasing glint in his eye, waved my hand to hurry him along, and then pushed open the door and entered.

My external injuries weren’t severe, and had already been urgently treated by a doctor while I waited in the corridor. However, the scuffle with Henry had left me looking quite disheveled, as if I hadn’t bathed in days.

At that moment, though, I didn’t care about such things. I was eager to see my Margaret.

I reckon I’m quite a careless father, completely unaware of Margaret’s recent changes, and my child silently crept into our lives.

Of course, Margaret was just as thoughtless a mother!

"Your Majesty, the Queen has already fallen asleep! Would you like your injuries attended to further?" inquired a duty nurse in the room, looking at me respectfully with a suggestion.

Taking a deep breath, I replied, "I will be staying here today. Have someone bring in a change of clothes; I need to freshen up."

Naturally, the nurse didn’t object, nodded her head, and withdrew.

chapters.title: Chapter 346 Mysterious Island
chapters.content:
Chapter 346: 346 Mysterious Island

[Donald’s Perspective]

I walked up and stood by Margaret’s bedside, looking at her still somewhat pale face with heartache, and said in a gentle voice, "It’s alright now, I’ll always be with you."

I didn’t know if Margaret heard my voice, but the frown on her brow relaxed, and her breathing through her nostrils became longer and more even.

I breathed a sigh of relief, watched Margaret for a long while, then turned and went to the bathroom in the room to wash up and re-treat the wound on my leg.

I had originally planned to stay with Margaret, but the actual situation proved that plans can never catch up with changes. At 3 o’clock midnight, I received the latest news from my Lycan subordinates: they had traced the whereabouts of Levi and his group at the Third Crossing of the Lilo Sea, in the northern waters of the Lilo Sea.

And after several days of investigation, Christian finally found some clues about the group who kidnapped Licia.

I looked at the dust-covered Christian and his group, pointed to the couch beside me, and said in a grave voice, "Speak directly."

Christian sat down on the couch and reported with a nod, "Your Majesty, our investigation over the past few days has made a significant discovery! There is an extremely mysterious island to the north of the Third Crossing of the Lilo Sea. We’ve been following the kidnappers’ trail and found that they and the Empress Dowager all disappeared in that area!"

"That sea area is shrouded in fog all year round and is full of hidden reefs. Even the most experienced fishermen can’t really get close. Our best-swimming Lycan also said there is no other way, so I led the team back to ask for your instructions, please command us!"

I patiently listened to Christian’s report, rubbing a ring with my finger, and remained silent for quite a while before asking, "Besides this, did you find out the identity or origins of the kidnappers? Are they locals from the Lilo Sea, or are they from elsewhere?"

"Before I came back, I met with officials from the Marine Supervision Bureau here. They said they had noticed these mysterious werewolves from outside long ago, and Alpha Levi of the Spirit Dragon Pack had some interactions with them! That official said they previously thought these people were brought in by Levi for a high price to deal specifically with the local Werewolf Gang, but it seems that is not the case now. This group doesn’t seem to follow Levi’s orders directly."

Christian’s analysis was logical. From the combat we had today with Levi and Henry, it was clear that both of them seemed unaware that Licia had been kidnapped while traveling south with us.

If these kidnappers were really in an employment relationship with Levi, why didn’t they inform him about the abduction of Licia? Or is there a third party behind all this? Besides Henry, who was severely injured today and whose life and death are uncertain, who else could have such terrifying power?

All the events seemed to come full circle, covered with a veil that made them mysterious and eerie everywhere!

I pondered these issues in my mind, frowning deeper and deeper, "We can’t keep making blind guesses without a basis. We need to continue to look for clues and evidence. You said you met with that official before you came back, who is he?"

Hearing this, Christian looked up at me and replied, "Your Majesty, this official is the Director of the Lilo Sea Maritime Supervision Bureau, named Berton."

"Berton?" I repeated the name softly, unable to find a corresponding figure in my memory. However, Elliot, who was beside me, stepped forward.

He looked up at me and said respectfully, "Your Majesty, I seem to have seen this man before. Just now, he was leading a group of officials at the manor’s entrance, eagerly wishing to have an audience with Your Majesty."

After hearing this, I glanced at the corner clock on the wall, pondering the oddity of these matters, took a breath, and said, "Where is he now? Is he still waiting at the entrance? Have someone call him in."

A Lycan near the door left to carry out the order after hearing my command, and I withdrew my gaze, turning instead to Christian, "When did you meet with him? It took you a day to rush back from Crossing Number Three, and this Director of the Marine Bureau made the journey in nearly the same time; that’s quite impressive indeed!"

Understanding the doubt in my words, Christian also realized the peculiarity. After thinking for a moment, he said, "We met with Berton two days ago. At that time, he seemed to be dealing with the issue of stolen ships at the Third Crossing. He rushed over to meet you. Was it about the theft of the ships or was it because of the kidnappers?"

"Another thing, I never mentioned your whereabouts during our meeting. Where did he get the information that you had arrived at the Lilo Sea? This Mr. Berton seems very suspicious, Your Majesty!"

Christian has always been meticulous. After being slightly reminded by me, he also immediately sorted out his thoughts and quickly pointed out several strange aspects.

I listened and slowly nodded my head, lifted the teacup from the desk, drank a sip, and replied, "He has already come to us, hasn’t he? Let’s wait and see what he really wants to do."

chapters.title: Chapter 347: Marine Officer Berton 347
chapters.content:
Chapter 347: Marine Officer Berton 347

[Donald’s Perspective]

Just a few minutes after my conversation with Christian ended, Berton was brought in by someone.

Berton was an elderly man with all white hair and a very kind and approachable appearance. The moment he saw me, he snapped to his knees with a thud!

In this temporary council chamber, there were only a few Lycans closest to me. Seeing Berton give such a grand salute, everyone’s expression had a hint of surprise and curiosity, while I took the chance to scrutinize this Marine Officer without showing any emotion.

"Your Majesty, I have three very urgent matters to report to you! I know you still harbor resentment about the traitor auction at the Sant Angelo Hotel. The elite of the Lilo Sea shouldn’t have attended the banquet, but could you please listen to my explanation for these events first?"

As Berton spoke, his aged and swollen body began to tremble slightly, appearing deeply sad and remorseful.

I looked at Berton kneeling on the ground and gestured to Christian at my side, signaling him to help the man up from the ground. We, a group of strong Lycans, would seem a bit heartless if we indifferently let an old man kneel there like that.

Berton seemed surprised by my reaction. After a moment of astonishment, he quickly stood up with the help of Christian’s hand, wiping away the sweat and tears on his face while repeatedly expressing his gratitude. "Thank you, Your Majesty, for your understanding of an old man. I truly have no way to repay this kindness, and for that, I am ashamed!"

"Just say what you have to, and skip the nonsense. I will investigate the Sant Angelo Hotel banquet in due course. If not all the officials of the Lilo Sea have perished, the duty of oversight isn’t entirely on the shoulders of one Marine Officer," I said and took another sip from the cup of black tea, hoping to clear my head a bit more.

Upon hearing this, Berton stood frozen, his body stiffening. Christian brought over a chair for him to sit, while I began to silently observe him.

The continuous travel and battle had left me somewhat exhausted, so my expression when facing possibly problematic subordinate officials was not pleasant or friendly. My whole persona was more daunting than usual, which was likely the reason for Berton’s evident fear.

Of course, he also had many issues of his own to account for, which might have contributed to his nervousness.

"By your command, Your Majesty, I will get to the point! My son and daughter attended the Sant Angelo auction yesterday, but that does not reflect my stance or that of the Lilo Sea authority. They were deceived! The children had no idea about the significance of the banquet beforehand... they thought it was just a fun event and had no dealings with the traitor MASTER or Levi!"

"Brand and his sister even saved the Lycan Queen once at the auction, which the Lycan Queen can fully attest to! Moreover, Levi has close ties with the pirates at sea, which is something we have always kept an eye on."

"Over the past two years, the Spirit Dragon Pack has become increasingly defiant of the Lilo Sea authority. They’ve been clashing with the Werewolf Gang over territory and maritime business, and the small conflicts have never ceased, which have been a constant headache for us!"

Berton spoke at length, but only a few key points caught my attention. "Explain in detail about the pirates," I asked with a furrowed brow, my fingers unconsciously tapping on the desk.

Swallowing nervously and with great seriousness, Berton replied, "Yes, Your Majesty! These pirates are foreign werewolves marauding in the waters of the Lilo Sea, robbing ships and murdering their crews. Their usual hideout is on Poison Snake Island to the north. Speaking of which, the disappearance of the Empress Dowager, Licia, is closely linked to them as well. I believe Mr. Christian must be well aware of this and has reported it to you, hasn’t he?"

The information in Berton’s words was substantial. Hearing this, I let out a slight sigh without answering his question but continued to inquire, "So you mean to say, the group that abducted the Empress Dowager are assassins as well as pirates at sea? They have connections with the Spirit Dragon Pack, right?"

Berton nodded frantically, as if fearful that any delay might lead me to devour him.

I raised an eyebrow, choosing not to dwell on this matter further, and asked aloud, "And the third matter?"

Seemingly unable to keep up with my train of thought, Berton opened his mouth with a confused look in his eyes.

"His Majesty is asking you about the third matter you wanted to report," Christian said with a furrowed brow, his gaze fixed on Berton, full of scrutiny.

As if struck by a revelation, Berton wiped the sweat from his forehead and hurriedly explained, "Yes, Your Majesty. To make amends, I now offer you a strategy for how to proceed to Poison Snake Island to rescue the Empress Dowager. Additionally, I hope to borrow an assault team from your men to help us defeat these lawless pirates and restore peace to the maritimes of Lilo Sea."

After Berton finished speaking, there was silence in the council chamber, with no one responding to his suggestion, and the atmosphere became somewhat tense and still.

chapters.title: Chapter 348 ’I had a dream
chapters.content:
Chapter 348: 348 ’I had a dream

[Donald’s Perspective]

"Your Majesty... What do you think of my suggestion? If you have any thoughts, please tell me directly. I’m getting old and my mind doesn’t work as fast, so I ask for your and the other lords’ patience!"

Perhaps feeling the atmosphere in the room had become too stiff and awkward, Berton stumbled over his words and continued speaking.

I looked at the elderly werewolf, who spoke carefully and acted smoothly. After a while, I smiled helplessly, stood up while leaning on the table, and said with a sneer, "It’s not that you’re old and slow, but rather, you have too deep of schemes, and you’re trying to foist inappropriate ideas onto me!"
