Berton immediately fell to his knees after I finished speaking, continuously claiming he didn’t dare do such.

The smile at the corner of my mouth disappeared, leaving only coldness in my eyes, "Stop using those tricks you play in the official circles on me. I hate being calculated and deceived the most. If you truly want to atone for your crimes, tell me directly how to get to Poison Snake Island instead of trying to gain advantage from me when you think you have the upper hand."

As I spoke, I gestured to the Lycan at my side to pull him up, and continued in a calm voice, "Perhaps we don’t meet often and you’re unaccustomed to my ways and methods. Then, please inform the others when you go back that I, Donald, am determined to cleanse the Lilo Sea."

"Whether it’s the Rebels of MSTER, the Spirit Dragon Pack of Levi, or the masterminds behind Poison Snake Island, they will not affect the Wolf royal family’s ultimate rule. Was I clear enough, Director Berton?"

Even though Lycan was supporting Berton, his body still shook slightly. Knowing I had achieved my goal for today, I gave him a cold look and, without turning back, snapped my fingers at Christian and walked out of the council hall.

My intention was very clear. Berton had to unconditionally disclose today how to enter Poison Snake Island, and Christian would be responsible for the specifics of the task.

No matter which power had abducted Licia, their ultimate goal would fall on either me or Margaret. Licia was an unstable bomb; I had to find her and bring her under my control as soon as possible.

As for the murky political environment of Lilo Sea, I would find an opportunity to slowly cleanse it; there was no need to rush.

This council meeting took nearly two hours. By the time I returned to Margaret’s hospital room, dawn had begun to break. A new day was about to start, and Margaret, alongside another new life about to arrive, gave me inexhaustible strength.

Once again, I felt my heart stuffed full with something, as if no problem or challenge was too great. There was no need for tension or worry.

Benjamin, who had watched over Eric all night, was sent by me to rest. My subordinates were the finest Lycans in the entire Werewolf Royal City, but I could not truly treat them like cold machines. Margaret taught me that every Lycan is a vibrant and lively being, deserving of care and affection.

In fact, I just couldn’t stand seeing the haunting dark circles under Benjamin’s eyes. I felt that looking at them too much would give me nightmares!

The vegetation at White Rose Manor was very lush, and if we hadn’t come here on serious business, it would have been an excellent place for a vacation.

This season, the roses were in full bloom everywhere, something I thought Margaret would love—if only she could open her eyes right now.

I held Margaret’s hand tightly to my face and said with a smile and a gentle voice, "The situation in Lilo Sea is a bit complicated, but the local scenery and culture are still beautiful. How about we consider this our honeymoon? There has been too much happening recently, I know you’re tired. I will protect you and the baby, do you believe me?"

"How can you sleep for so long? Could it be that Benjamin is a quack? He said you would wake up this morning."

The warm light in the room illuminated Margaret’s face. I watched her long lashes quiver slightly; then she slowly opened her beautiful eyes.

"Are you awake, my love? How are you feeling?" I asked urgently, sitting up straight to wait for Margaret’s reply.

"Hmm... Donald? What are you doing here, and where are we? What’s happened to me? I feel so weak and hungry!" Margaret spoke softly, her eyes squinting, her voice low and sticky, as if she was acting coquettishly.

I kissed her forehead gently and responded tenderly, "This is White Rose Manor. You suddenly fainted yesterday, and I carried you back. You’ve just been too tired these past few days. Rest well and eat well, and you’ll be fine. What would you like for breakfast? I’ll have Ruth make it for you."

Margaret slowly closed her eyes as she listened, and just when I thought she was going to fall asleep again, she suddenly murmured, "I had a dream, Donald. I dreamed of a little one calling out to me, saying her stomach hurt, telling me to be happy every day. Isn’t that strange?"

chapters.title: Chapter 349 Careless?
chapters.content:
Chapter 349: 349 Careless?

[Margaret’s perspective]

The hospital room was very quiet, Donald didn’t answer my question aloud.

My head felt a little dizzy, my body extremely weak, and my stomach so hungry I could eat a whole cow. I opened my eyes somewhat discontentedly and looked at Donald, my gaze questioning him, "Why did you suddenly stop talking?"

But when I saw the smile on Donald’s handsome face clearly, the dissatisfaction in my eyes dissipated instantly, and curiosity and wonder began to fill my heart. "Did something happen? Why do you look so happy?"

As I spoke, I reached out and touched the corners of Donald’s upward-curving lips, then added, "Did you catch Henry and Levi? Or is there a breakthrough in Licia’s disappearance? What are you smiling about? You look so silly."

Donald’s happiness was contagious. With a mixture of helplessness and a smile, I looked at him, my heart soft and warm.

The young and handsome king wrapped my hand with his large one, his smile broadening. He kissed the palm of my hand and gazed at me tenderly, "Baby, I have some great news for you..."

"What great news?" I asked with a smile.

Donald’s beautiful eyes shone like the eternal stars in the night, "You’re pregnant, you’re going to be a mom! And I’m finally going to be a dad," he said.

I stared at him, not quite comprehending the meaning of his words for a moment.

Seeing me stunned, Donald leaned down and kissed my brow, and only after his hot breath landed on my face did I come to my senses slightly, "What? I’m pregnant?"

Donald simply nodded with a smile, the love and joy in his eyes almost drowning me.

I blinked and instinctively looked down at my belly, "You mean I have a baby? It’s ours? When? How come I don’t feel anything? She..."

My eyes suddenly welled up as I spoke, and my nose turned red in an instant. Turning to look at Donald, my voice trembled as I spoke, "Who told you? Was it Benjamin? When did he tell you?"

"Weren’t you the one who fainted on the rooftop yesterday? I was almost scared to death! After we returned to White Rose Manor, Benjamin checked your physical condition!"

Donald explained in a gentle voice, lifted his hand to wipe away the tears from the corner of my eye, kissed my eye and continued, "Don’t cry, baby. This is a wonderful thing; your tears are melting my heart. The baby is already 4 weeks along. You’ve been lacking energy recently; I thought you were just tired, but now it seems that was just a normal pregnancy reaction."

"We’ve both been so careless to only now discover the baby’s arrival. Do you think she might blame me?" Donald said, his brow slightly furrowed as if he truly worried that the embryo in my belly would blame him.

I burst into laughter through my tears, my eyes red yet smiling at him, I said warmly, "She’s still so small, how could she blame you? Stop making me laugh; I’m running out of strength."

Warm tears slid down from my eyes as I looked at Donald and said with a smile, "I’m hungry, Donald. I want a bowl of hot seafood porridge!"

Donald leaned down to kiss away the tears on my face and nodded, "Okay, just a moment, I’ll have Ruth bring it in! To prevent you from being hungry when you woke up, she has been keeping the pot of seafood porridge warm."

The feeling of being cared for is truly wonderful, especially when the person is your lifelong love.

I smiled contentedly and my other hand instinctively touched my belly, my gaze falling on the soft blanket. It was as if I could see my baby through everything, my heart beating wildly, suddenly filled with a new sense of purpose.

"By the way, why ’she’ and not ’he’? Do you hope it’s a girl?" Donald asked tenderly after putting down the phone.

At his words, I shook my head, tilted my face up to meet Donald’s, my expression somewhat dazed, "I don’t know, it just instinctively felt like ’she’."

I laughed, my smile waxing, and continued, "I just referred to her as ’she’, right? In my dream last night, it was she who was calling me, telling me to be happier! So that’s it; she seems to be a very considerate baby."

Content with the conclusion I’d reached, the smile on my face deepened; I felt like the happiest person in the world at that moment!

"I think so too; she will be as smart and beautiful as you, a cute and lively little girl," Donald said, agreeing with me as if he would pluck the stars from the sky without a second thought if I desired them.

I thought this Lycan king seemed a bit overwhelmed by passion; smiling helplessly, I reached out and took his hand, "It’s only been 4 weeks, and we’re already discussing it so animatedly. Isn’t that a bit silly? But Elizabeth would surely be ecstatic to hear the news, wouldn’t she?"

I didn’t know which of my words sparked Donald; his smile faded slightly, and after a long pause, he finally made a suggestion.

chapters.title: Chapter 350 Hide information
chapters.content:
Chapter 350: 350 Hide information

[Margaret’s perspective]

"The situation is quite unstable right now, so just to be safe, I want to keep the news of your pregnancy under wraps before we return to the Royal City. Aside from a few people close to us, we’ll prevent anyone from finding out. That way, we can ensure your and the baby’s safety better. What do you think?"

I seemed to have no reason to refuse Donald’s suggestion.

His considerations were right, the apparent calmness and stability of the Lilo Sea were merely superficial. Underneath, chaos reigned! Capturing Henry and Levi, quelling the rebels—these were the things we must do now, and this baby seemed to have come at an inconvenient time.

While I was lost in these scattered thoughts, I found myself slowly nodding in agreement with Donald.

Several seconds later, I finally spoke up, "With us in light and the enemy in the dark, it is necessary for you to think one step ahead. I agree."

"But the baby’s arrival is a wonderful thing. I don’t want us to become a burden to you at this time. Of course, I will also protect myself and our child well after this."

As I finished speaking, Donald bent down and hugged me tightly as if I were his entire world. "How could you and the baby ever be a burden to me? You both are the source of my strength, and I will protect you both. Don’t worry," he said with a deep, tender voice.

Donald’s shoulders were always so broad and warm. I pressed my face into his neck and replied softly, "Okay."

These warm and romantic moments always seemed to pass too quickly. A few knocks on the door interrupted us, and I peeked out from Donald’s embrace. "It must be Ruth, right? She brought me some food!" I guessed.

Donald chuckled, let me go, and pinched my face as he said, "No wonder your appetite has been so good lately. The real reason is right here! This little one is pretty amazing. You’ll both need to eat more from now on!"

Perhaps it was the overly gentle look on Donald’s face that caused me to space out. By the time I returned to reality, Ruth had already walked in with a serving tray.

"Your Highness, you’re finally awake. You must be starved. This is the meal I prepared for you early this morning. Eat it while it’s hot, don’t starve yourself!" Ruth said with a delighted look in her eyes.

Upon hearing this, I nodded as the aroma of seafood porridge made my stomach rumble.

Donald and Ruth watched me gently as I put down my third bowl of porridge, and I looked up at them puzzled, "Why are you both looking at me like that? Won’t you eat some? This porridge is really good."

"This feeling... it’s also very nice," Donald said with raised eyebrows and a teasing look at me.

I wiped my mouth with a napkin and instinctively asked, "What feeling is very nice?"

Donald replied with a smile, "It’s like raising a little pig."

Me: "..."

Ruth stood by, covering her mouth to stifle a laugh, while I looked speechlessly at the suddenly joking Lycan king. We bantered, "Then should the ’keeper’ lay down to rest a bit? The bloodshot in your eyes is somewhat frightening."

At that, Donald nodded without arguing with the new nickname I gave him, "Then lie down with me for a while, I have things to take care of in the afternoon."

Thinking of the events that occurred yesterday, I patted the space next to me, gesturing for Donald to lie down.

Ruth, quite perceptively, took the tray and left, carefully closing the door behind her.

Donald took off his jacket and lay down in bed, closing his eyes. With a long arm, he pulled me into his embrace while saying, "Christian returned early this morning. He brought back some important news, and I have to confirm it with them later."

"At the auction yesterday, you met Berton’s son and daughter, didn’t you? He said those two kids saved you once. Is that true?"

Donald suddenly broached a topic out of the blue. I tried to look up at him but only managed to see his perfectly chiseled jawline. "Who are you referring to as Berton? The person who saved me at the auction yesterday... Are you talking about the Brand siblings?"

I recalled the events of yesterday, and the faces of the Brand siblings flashed through my mind. I thought for a moment and continued, "So Berton is the head of the Marine Supervision Bureau? I did hear something about that from Brand yesterday. Have you met him already?"

With closed eyes, Donald rubbed my head with his chin and slowly began to tell me about what happened after I had passed out.

I hadn’t expected so much to have happened after I lost consciousness, and for a moment, my thoughts were in disarray.

Donald was just habitually telling me about work, not really needing me to provide any immediate constructive advice. He lightly patted the back of my head with his voice sounding somewhat hoarse, as if he was extremely tired, "Don’t worry about it too much, my dear. I didn’t tell you this to make you worry. You just need to stay with me for now."

After these last words, Donald fell asleep, his breathing became even and prolonged through his nostrils.

I knew he hadn’t had a proper night’s sleep for several days, so I pushed down my confusion and curiosity, nestled into his shoulder, and closed my eyes.

The world was noisy and complex; only we were each other’s true and steadfast reliance.

chapters.title: Chapter 351: Caught off guard
chapters.content:
Chapter 351: Caught off guard

[Levi’s Perspective]

The roaring sound of the helicopter made me incredibly irritable, I looked down at the bloodied Henry on the ground, and the anxiety and anger in my heart felt like they were going to engulf me completely.

"Levi, you have to figure out a way to save His Highness—he’s... he’s about to die! To hell with all your damn theories, if it wasn’t for you, His Highness wouldn’t have attended that sham of an auction and wouldn’t have run into Donald and his crew there!"

The one loudly chastising me in front of the entire cabin was Henry’s long-time capable assistant Aiden, his eyes red as if they were about to drip blood the next moment!

I lifted my eyes to look at him, coldly curled the corner of my mouth, but didn’t respond to him.

Even Henry wouldn’t yell at me so rudely; Aiden, a mere assistant, dared to speak to me like this—I think the failure today has driven this group of people mad.

"Why aren’t you speaking? I told you to think of a way to save our prince. If anything untoward happens to His Highness this time, you should know what the MASTER’s capable of, and she won’t let you off either..."

"Shut your goddamn mouth!"

I exploded, sternly interrupting Aiden’s ’threat’, looked at him coldly with the pistol in my hand pointed directly at his throat, "How I deal with it is my business. Who the hell are you to talk to me like that?"

Perhaps my argument with Aiden was too intense, for a moment nobody in the cabin dared to speak up, or to make any superfluous movements, and the cabin was filled only with the sounds of propeller rotation and the engine’s roar!

Seeing Aiden bite down on his molars and say no more, I sneered, put away the gun, and ignoring the anger in his eyes, I scornfully continued, "He’s not dead yet, is he? If you keep wasting my time with your words, he really will die. That we were able to rescue him from Donald and Gino today, that’s because we were quick to react, so spare me all that crap!"

"Besides, doesn’t he love tinkering with those strange gadgets? Just give him a shot or something, it’s not as if he’s short of one or two."

Having said that, I let out a heavy breath, the sarcastic smile on my face growing larger, and without any pause, I looked at Aiden and continued, "I’ve been meaning to say, you don’t still think this is the Werewolf Royal City, do you? Repeatedly calling him ’His Highness’, how can you bear to say that? Donald is probably itching for him to die, doesn’t that make Henry’s title as Prince a joke? I advise you and your men to stop pressing that Prince authority on me—I, Levi, don’t buy it!"

"Don’t say I didn’t warn you, in the Lilo Sea, you still need my help. As for Poison Snake Island, she wouldn’t completely disregard my face either, you’d best remember that."

These Lycan under Henry are too arrogant, if you don’t do something to suppress their spirit, I always feel that they will eventually walk all over me!

Of course, I don’t actually want Henry to die—my subsequent series of plans all require his assistance; this was something we had agreed upon earlier.

I have to admit, Henry really does have an exceptionally smart brain, at least in the research of potions, no one can compare to him.

And of course, utterly heartless as well.

Remembering the scenes I had previously seen in his underground laboratory, goosebumps appeared on the skin under my suit, and a wave of nausea surged within me.

I suddenly became curious as to whether his group of crazed followers would feel a conflict within if they saw his ’masterpieces’, and whether the image of his holiness and purity would still remain convincing.

"Alpha, have you thought of something?" asked a werewolf sitting opposite me, timidly, his face showing a mix of fear and trepidation.

I looked at him with a smile, raising my eyebrows in good humor, "Nothing much, just remembered some interesting matters. How much longer till we fly over this sea area, and is there a boat from Poison Snake Island coming to meet us? If we delay any further, I’m afraid our MASTER really won’t last much longer!"

The latter part of my statement was directed at the pilot in the cockpit; he wasn’t one of mine or Henry’s men, but someone specially sent from Poison Snake Island to meet us.

I don’t know if that woman has the ability to foresee the future, but we owe our narrow escape from Donald’s clutches today to her prudence.

Donald’s action in hunting down Henry from the south was really swift; I had prepared for the Sant Angelo event for so long, so secretly, yet he managed to expose it at the last moment, the thought of it itches my teeth with hatred.

Thinking of this, I exhaled, if it weren’t for Henry’s keen perception, noticing their disguise in advance, I really don’t know if we could have made it back alive tonight...

"It won’t be long, Mr. Levi, about another 40 minutes or so. Someone from the island will come directly to meet us, please be assured!"

The werewolf piloting the helicopter responded solemnly, turning to glance at Henry lying on the ground, he thought for a moment and then added, "I think it would be wise to tend to Mr. Henry’s wounds first. If the MASTER sees him like this, she is likely to be quite displeased. You wouldn’t want to see that scene, would you?"

chapters.title: Chapter 352 ’Resurrected from the Dead
chapters.content:
Chapter 352: 352 ’Resurrected from the Dead

[Levi’s Perspective]

I listened to the werewolf’s words and understood the hidden meaning behind his tone.

Although I was annoyed by their commanding attitude, I was still willing to resolve some avoidable conflicts beforehand.

"Just to clarify, you can’t blame me entirely for today’s incident. I was also caught off guard by Donald’s move! The arrow I shot at Henry was solely to save him. Don’t go stirring things up in front of her and cause me trouble," I said.

After speaking, I nodded to the werewolf beside me, signaling him to use some of the rare medicinal herbs I kept on me for Henry, to ensure he wouldn’t die before we reached Poison Snake Island.

It seemed like he accepted my explanation. The werewolf in the cockpit withdrew his gaze from Henry, nodded, and stopped talking.

One hour later, we encountered a ship coming to pick us up on the sea near Poison Snake Island. Their ship looked ordinary at first glance, but if you observed carefully, you could see the signs of reinforced fortification.

The waters near Poison Snake Island were filled with countless hidden reefs and the weather was extremely unpredictable. Without this kind of specially made ship and an experienced sailor, it would be absolutely impossible to enter Poison Snake Island!

And this was exactly why the owner of the island was so arrogant.

Thinking about her effortless businesses over the surface of the Lilo Sea, I couldn’t help but be tempted, though the authorities had recently been monitoring too closely, and Gino was always an obstacle, leaving me truly lacking the energy to partner with her in this venture.

Although I didn’t make any money, at least I kept every member of our pack safe, which wasn’t a fruitless return. My uncle was right; only those who are truly cautious can survive to the end.

What I didn’t expect was that Henry woke up before we landed on the island. I attributed this to my precious medicine.

"They will find out about Poison Snake Island sooner or later, but before then, I want you to help me with one more thing," Henry said weakly as he lay on the simple wooden boat.

I sat down opposite him and looked him in the eye, "After today’s events, Donald won’t let any of my pack members go. I hope you remember all that you promised me earlier, instead of constantly asking me to do this and that!"

Silence spread between me and Henry and I frowned at him, not planning to compromise right away.

At that moment, Henry just looked at me without speaking. After a while, he gestured to Aiden, who seemed to understand and came forward, kneeling in front of Henry.

"Bring me the green potion tube from my briefcase," Henry whispered to Aiden.

At his words, I raised an eyebrow at Henry, ideas flickering through my mind.

As if he read my thoughts, Henry, after taking the syringe from Aiden’s hand, continued weakly, "I know what you’re thinking. I will fulfill my promises. I have always been generous with my allies, as you can see from what you’ve learned from Austin. You must have received his reply letter by now?"

As he talked, he injected himself with the medication, his actions expertly executed, obviously well-practiced.

I saw the eerily green medication disappear into his veins, and inexplicably, a chill rose in my heart, "Sometimes, I truly think you’re a monster."

"Oh? Is that a compliment for me?" Henry seemed to be in a good mood and responded lightly.

I looked into Henry’s eyes and suddenly chuckled, continuing, "To even think of such tactics as faking death to escape, of course, I’m complimenting you. Donald and his queen are not fools, and the Medical Practitioner under him seems very capable too. How did you make them believe Austin was dead?"

Curious about how Henry managed it, I asked him earnestly, "Aren’t you afraid they’ll find out Austin isn’t dead and then discover your secret?"

After the injection, Henry’s vitality visibly improved, and his wounds healed at a rate visible to the naked eye. He truly appeared to be using some kind of magic!

"Every magical medication has its drawbacks, every perfect plan has flaws. Of course, Austin wasn’t really dead at that time. It wasn’t that Benjamin and the other Medical Practitioners weren’t smart enough. On the contrary, they were too smart, which is why they fell into my trap," Henry explained as he turned to look toward the upper part of the ship, a smug expression on his face. "You wouldn’t understand all this medical talk, but what you need to understand is that I can achieve anything I set my mind to, including helping you achieve your dreams."

I admit I am not as smart as Henry, but I also know he’s good at bluffing. After a sneer, I didn’t pursue the matter further and said bluntly, "Tell me, and I’ll consider whether or not to help you."

"You will certainly help me because what I’m asking you to do involves Margaret, and you seem quite fond of her," Henry said confidently, and I narrowed my eyes at him, a dangerous look flashing through them.

chapters.title: Chapter 353 Pregnancy Precautions
chapters.content:
Chapter 353: 353 Pregnancy Precautions

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Stealing moments of leisure amid busyness had become a norm in Donald’s daily work. After having lunch with me, he took Elliot and Christian into the council chamber.

I was curious about his plans for the rest of the day, but I also knew very well that what I needed to do the most right now was to take good care of my health.

Reining in my wandering thoughts, I turned my attention back to the Medical Practitioner who had been standing by my bed, chattering away.

"Since it’s your first pregnancy, Your Majesty, there are certain things you must be careful about. For instance, I hope you won’t encounter what happened yesterday again! Of course, I will list the special precautions and report them to His Majesty, who will then supervise you," he said.

When Benjamin mentioned yesterday’s incident, there was a noticeable seriousness in his face. Both Ruth and I felt his aura was intimidating, and we subconsciously didn’t dare to say much.

It seems that Medical Practitioners around the world act the same when faced with patients who don’t listen—both troubled and helpless.

I was half a Medical Practitioner myself, so I could understand Benjamin’s feelings. Clutching the cup in my hand, I remained silent as the hospital room turned quiet for a moment.

Benjamin seemed pleased with my patient attitude, nodding before continuing, "I have specifically tailored this for Your Highness—a diet and exercise plan for the first three months of pregnancy. You can keep it as a reference. Also, I heard from Ruth that you had dizziness and nausea a few days ago. These symptoms are normal pregnancy reactions, and you needn’t worry."

"Moreover, Your Highness, I know your mother was an excellent midwife before, and when we complete our tasks and return to the Royal City, you can have her take care of you personally. I’ve read in books that a pregnant she-wolf greatly needs the companionship of close relatives to alleviate the anxiety and unease during pregnancy. Of course, His Majesty’s company is indispensable too," he added.

Benjamin, at that moment, was like a nagging old man. Knowing he cared for me genuinely as a friend and as a Medical Practitioner, I didn’t blame him for being unaware of my strained relations with Nora.

Although his suggestion felt like salt on an open wound for me.

I curled the corners of my mouth and said with a light laugh while glancing sideways at Benjamin, "If it’s about the company of someone close, wouldn’t Elizabeth be a better option? She is always lively and lovely; staying with her should make things even happier."

Benjamin, not understanding the strained relationships I had at home but keen and intelligent, seemed to sense something. After pausing for two seconds, he nodded and said, "Of course, whatever you think is best, Your Highness."

"Alright, Benjamin, I know this is your first time taking care of a pregnant woman, but you don’t have to be so anxious. Since I’m a Medical Practitioner myself, I can take good care of myself. Besides, I have Donald and Ruth by my side; they will take good care of me," I reassured him.

After finishing, I handed the cup back to Ruth, stretched lazily, and turned my head toward the window. "The weather is lovely today. Could I ask Ruth to go out with me for a stroll? It’s so stuffy in here."

Benjamin naturally wouldn’t refuse my suggestion to go outside; he even encouraged me to breathe in some fresh air, saying it would be beneficial for my physical and mental health.

Although I wanted to reject the wheelchair, being watched closely by both of them, my simple desire to walk on my own was thwarted.

Ruth pushed me downstairs and into the estate’s garden.

The climate of Lilo Sea was perfect for roses to flourish, and this season was their prime blooming period. Seeing the many roses in full bloom throughout the garden made my mood exceptionally delightful!

Beautiful things have always been more likely to win people’s affection—it’s an eternal truth.

Leaning against the wheelchair, I squinted my eyes and enjoyed the sun’s caress, while Ruth spoke to me in a gentle voice nearby. This feeling was truly comfortable; it would have been better if nobody appeared to disturb this peace.

"Your Majesty the Lycan Queen? Do you remember me? I’m Brand! Let me go, I just want to talk to the Lycan Queen for a bit and then I’ll leave, I promise I won’t bother you again!"

From across a few winding and elegantly designed walkways, I straightened up slightly and looked toward the source of the voice.

The first thing I saw was Elliot standing to the side, instructing a Lycan to kick the boy out of the estate. Upon seeing me noticing them, he stopped what he was doing and walked straight toward me.

"My apologies, Your Majesty, I’ll throw that noisy brat out right away," Elliot said, nodding respectfully to me, his voice carrying a hint of apology.

I recognized the boy Elliot referred to as Brand and waved my hand, beckoning gently, "It’s okay, bring him over."

Although Elliot didn’t quite understand why I wanted to see the boy, he nodded and did as I asked. I looked up at him, signaling that he could leave.

chapters.title: Chapter 354’Protons’ Delivered Right to Your Door
chapters.content:
Chapter 354: 354’Protons’ Delivered Right to Your Door

[Margaret’s Perspective]

The manor had now been turned into a fortress by Donald’s orders, with an extremely high safety index. Not to mention a little boy, even tough guys like Henry and Levi wouldn’t dream of attacking from the outside.

Elliot still didn’t feel at ease leaving Brand with me and waved off the other Lycans accompanying him, opting instead to stand guard nearby himself.

I felt that Donald and his group of Lycans seemed to be a bit on edge. After taking a couple deep breaths, I turned back to Brand and asked softly, "Why are you here? What do you want to do?"

Perhaps Elliot’s gaze was too sharp, as Brand seemed frightened and uneasy, but he relaxed slightly after hearing me speak, "I came to explain the situation to His Majesty. He seems to have misunderstood my father because of what happened yesterday! My father didn’t sleep all night and is still locked in his study!"

"Lycan Queen, I know you and His Majesty are good people, and of course, so is my father. So, could you please talk to His Majesty and ask him not to make things difficult for my father..."

"Shut up! Do you know what you’re talking about?" Ruth immediately interrupted Brand’s speech, speaking with a cold tone and a stern expression on her face.

Brand, seemingly not expecting Ruth to suddenly stop him, froze before looking at me with some concern.

I had been patiently listening to him talk and was sure that the boy had no ill intentions. I sighed slightly and asked directly, "You say the Lycan king is making things difficult for your father, but what exactly does that entail?"

Upon hearing this, Brand opened his mouth as if to say something, but ultimately couldn’t cite a specific incident, knowing full well that Berton’s busyness was due to work matters.

"You clearly understand what is going on, so why do you slander the Lycan king like this? Mr. Berton is indeed busy with work these days, and this has nothing to do with Donald. Do you understand?" I said, shielding my eyes from the sun and looking at Brand with a hint of amusement.

The child’s downcast look was somewhat cute, his profile even resembling Elizabeth’s. "Alright, I think I know what you want to do. But Mr. Berton indeed has work to do, and you should be understanding of that. If you don’t mind, you can also come to chat with me tomorrow, and you can bring your sister, too."

I said this while patting the young boy’s shoulder, my face full of smiles.

Upon hearing this, Brand looked up at me, his eyes filled with delight and astonishment, "You... Your Highness, you’re not upset about my barging in today? I was even rude to the Lycan king, aren’t you angry? Can I really come here to play with you tomorrow? Is that true?"

It was always easy to understand the minds of the young. Watching his bright eyes, I nodded and smiled, "Don’t do something this dangerous again; your loved ones and friends will worry about you. Of course, you can come and see me because I’m currently recovering from an injury and really need someone to talk to."

"That’s great, Your Highness! You really are a beautiful and gentle person; I like you," Brand said candidly.

His frankness made me laugh, thinking it was good that Donald wasn’t here at the moment, or else he would have gotten jealous.

"I’ll ask that brother over there to take you back. Don’t go running around anymore, okay?" I indicated to a standing Lycan not far away, signaling to Brand that Elliot would take him home.

Brand seemed a little reluctant at this, but the thought of coming over to chat with me tomorrow made this minor ’difficulty’ seem acceptable.

He nodded in agreement, waved goodbye to me, and then happily trotted off.

Elliot, suddenly assigned this task, looked somewhat puzzled. I gave him a glance and a slight nod, telling him to follow my orders.

The group of Lycans always executed my commands without refusal.

Elliot led Brand away, and I watched their figures disappear at the end of the corridor, sighing softly to myself.

"Your Highness, why are you so kind to that boy? Isn’t he the Marine Officer’s son? Aren’t you worried he has ulterior motives for approaching you?" Ruth seemed puzzled by my earlier behavior and couldn’t help but ask.

I maneuvered my wheelchair in a different direction, getting even closer to the rose field, "Brand may be somewhat cunning, but he’s not bad by nature. I probably know what he wants to do, and why should I push away a hostage that came to me on his own? We are engaging in what can be described as mutual benefit. Don’t talk about any ’ulterior motives’ or the like."

Ruth appeared even more puzzled upon hearing this, looking at me with eyes full of confusion.

Seeing her reaction, I smiled without intending to share my full thoughts. Some things aren’t as benevolent and innocent when being carried out, and it’s better not to corrupt someone so endearing!

chapters.title: Chapter 355 Yangmou
chapters.content:
Chapter 355: 355 Yangmou

[Margaret’s Perspective]

By the time Ruth and I returned to the ward from the garden, Donald had already come by without my knowing. He was sitting on the sofa against the wall, holding a tablet in his hand, his handsome brows slightly furrowed as if he encountered some kind of difficulty.

"You’re back?"

"When did you get here?"

Donald and I almost opened our mouths at the same time, our voices colliding in the air. I laughed and looked up at him, continuing to ask, "Is there some trouble again? Seeing you, your brows haven’t relaxed these past few days. Is there anything I can help with?"

On hearing this, Donald stood up and walked over. He rested one hand on the armrest of the wheelchair and gently touched my face with the other, responding warmly, "It’s all just minor things, I can handle them. Did you just go sunbathing in the garden? Your cheeks are red and flushed, looking like fresh apples."

"So, is this your special way of giving compliments, comparing someone’s cheeks to apples?" Donald’s palm was dry and comfortable, and without thinking, I rubbed my face against it, looking at him with a smile.

At this moment, Donald also smiled, his expression unspeakably tender.

Sometimes I think that Donald really has changed a lot. At least, when I first met him at Silver Moon Pack, his face wouldn’t show such a gentle and vivid expression. He has become much kinder now.

"You look much better now; you almost scared me to death yesterday. Are you hungry? Want to eat something?" Donald didn’t answer my question; instead, he changed the subject to talk about afternoon tea.

I wasn’t very hungry originally, but hearing Donald mention food suddenly made me feel a bit peckish.

At this point, the smile on Donald’s face deepened. He leaned down to kiss my lips and continued with a laugh, "I see now, you and the baby are hungry again. It so happens that a pastry chef came to the mansion today; let’s try his skill later together."

Such a proposal was, of course, as good as it gets. I nodded my head in agreement and added, "By the way, I ran into Brand in the garden just now. He should be coming over tomorrow as well."

Donald gave me a puzzled look. I rose from the wheelchair, aided by him, and walked back to the sofa area. After sitting down, I continued, "Brand is a rather clever boy. Due to Berton’s busy work, he hasn’t given enough attention and care to him. He must really admire his father, so now that Berton has stumbled in front of you, he wants to do something to help his father."

"So why did you let him get close to you?" Donald asked.

I was aware that Donald already knew this information, and he might even know it in more detail. He didn’t know what I wanted to do, nor did he oppose or stop me immediately, but instead, he patiently asked for my reasoning.

I looked at Donald and chuckled, snapping my fingers, "Brand is Berton’s eldest son and he values him highly. By allowing Brand to show me favor, I am sending a signal to Berton that the Wolf royal family can recognize him as a Marine Officer, but only if he is obedient and accepts all directives from Lcyan king."

"Your cooperation with Berton is imminent; we can’t let our guard down against him. Brand could serve as a bridge for cooperation between you two, or be the key for you to directly control Berton."

I continued, a flicker of thought crossing my eyes as I looked into Donald’s, "I’ve already had Elliot send Brand back just now. Berton isn’t a fool; surely he can understand our intentions."

Donald listened to me in silence, stroking his chin thoughtfully. After several seconds, he finally responded, "So what you mean is, Brand can be the medium through which we control Berton, or to put it in layman’s terms, he is a pawn sent by Berton. And Berton’s aim is to win my trust?"

Donald had thoroughly grasped the concept of the plan I proposed. I nodded my head, my eyes shining with approval, "Actually, it’s a mutual cooperation, beneficial to both parties, as long as Berton does not deviate halfway through."

"I understand. Your method is definitely more sound and reliable; it can even be called a ’masterstroke’."

Actually, this was just an idea I had come up with on the spur of the moment. With Donald praising me so generously, I was almost feeling a little embarrassed.

I picked up the water glass from the table and took a sip, glancing at Donald with a light laugh, "As long as you don’t mind, my doing this shouldn’t disturb your other plans, should it?"

Donald shook his head, replying softly, "No, your idea is very good. I was just worried about not being able to catch Berton’s fox tail, but now it’s perfect!"

At this moment, I didn’t know that this sudden idea would turn out to be such a big help to Donald later on. Brand was a fine young man; it was just that Berton, as a father, was too greedy.

And in this world, those who are harmed by their own insatiable greed are certainly not few!

chapters.title: Chapter 356 siblings
chapters.content:
Chapter 356: 356 siblings

[Margaret’s Perspective]

During this time, I was completely locked in the manor by Donald, whose protective desire for me had reached an unprecedented level. Although he claimed to others that I was still recovering from injuries, anyone who was attentive enough could see some signs.

Take, for instance, the Brand siblings who had been sticking to me these past few days.

Brand’s sister is a very cute little girl named Lisa. I really hope that the baby in my belly will be as adorable and lively as her.

"I heard from the aunt at home that Dad and the others are going to fight pirates at sea, just like in the TV shows! Aunt Margaret, have you ever seen those pirates? My brother said your husband is a very capable man; can he defeat those pirates?" Lisa looked up at me, her tone filled with innocent romance.

She is five years younger than Brand, at an age full of infinite curiosity and desire to explore the world.

After listening to her, I raised my hand to touch her little face, smiling gently as I replied, "I haven’t seen those pirates, but they rob cargo ships and fishing boats on the sea, and they are definitely very bad people. Bad people will ultimately be caught and punished. Do you understand, little Lisa?"

The little girl tried hard to grasp the meaning of my words, her face entirely serious.

I was amused by her adorable expression and smiled as I touched her cheek, feeling pleased.

At that moment, Brand, not wanting to be neglected, pouted and spoke up, "Lisa, how many times have I told you not to call the Lycan Queen ’aunt’? Look how young she is; she could only be our sister, understand?"

I have to admit, both siblings are very sweet-tongued, their actions and words always bring joy, truly my daily dose of happiness!

Especially the smart and cheerful Brand, whose skills in making girls happy I think Donald should learn from.

Of course, such thoughts should not be expressed in front of Donald. Because of Brand lingering around me these days, our Lycan King was nearly turning into a jealous pot!

I gathered my somewhat scattered thoughts, watching Brand and little Lisa bickering and interrupted with a light laugh, "Alright, alright, stop arguing. I am very willing to be little Lisa’s aunt; who could resist her cuteness!"

Upon hearing this, Lisa’s lips curled into a proud smile, looking triumphantly at her brother as if she had already won the battle.

Brand grimaced, now deeming it beneath him to continue the struggle with his sister. He looked at me, lowering his voice as he slowly asked, "Margaret, is there a little baby in your belly?"

His words caught me off guard, my expression remained unchanged, but my heart tightened. I let go of Lisa’s cheek and asked, "Why do you ask that?"

Brand’s look towards me shimmered with subtle changes; when he averted his gaze as if hiding it, I even noticed the moistness in his eyes, "It’s nothing, I just felt that the way you talked to Lisa... it reminded me of someone."

He paused, a nostalgic smile spreading across his face as he continued looking at Lisa, "You remind me a lot of our mother! She has been gone for many years now, and just now, I seemed to see her in you. I miss her. You probably don’t know that the first time we met at Sant Angelo, Lisa told me she liked you, saying you resembled her a lot."

As Brand spoke, he raised his hand to wipe away a tear, the vibrant red on top of his head seemingly becoming more subdued.

I looked at the defiant yet broken young man before me without saying a word. After several breaths, I pulled out a handkerchief and handed it to him.

This silent, not excessive comfort considerably relaxed the young man. He took the handkerchief and clumsily wiped his face, his smile returning quickly, "You’ve been staring at your belly and zoning out these days, your face always bearing a happy and contented smile! I saw the same expression on my mom when Lisa was still in her belly."

Hearing this, I sighed lightly, looking at the somewhat pitiable siblings and smiling before speaking, "Then, after the baby in my belly is born, why don’t you both play with her too? I think she will really like Lisa as a big sister."

"Really? Am I going to be a big sister?"

Lisa, who had been anxiously watching Brand at first, suddenly lit up when she heard me mention that key word. Her round, clear eyes gleamed, her whole being like a shining little star.

I looked at her and then at Brand beside her, speaking gently, "Yes, it’s true. And I am also a big sister; I have a very beautiful sister named Elizabeth."

"Then where is she now? Why aren’t you together? Don’t you miss her?"

Speaking of Elizabeth, I indeed missed her a bit.

It’s been almost a month since we traveled south to Lilo Sea, and she only called me twice from start to finish, that forgetful creature. I wonder how Anthony’s injuries are healing?

Perhaps my expression was too obvious, as Brand quickly noticed something was amiss and sat beside me, asking with concern, "What’s wrong? You look a little sad."

chapters.title: Chapter 357 Abnormal
chapters.content:
Chapter 357: 357 Abnormal

[Margaret’s Perspective]

The concern on the young man’s face was somewhat touching, and I glanced at him, responding with a slight smile, "Of course, I’m thinking of my sister. We haven’t seen each other for a long time."

As I spoke, I sighed, feeling somewhat listless, and added, "I really wish I could see her now. She makes several kinds of snacks, all of which are very good."

Lisa was still too young, and the mention of delicious treats made her mouth water involuntarily. She licked her lips, took my hand, and asked eagerly, "So when can you meet her? I want to try her snacks too, can I, Angel Margaret!"

At her words, I smiled helplessly and looked down at Lisa as I replied, "So I become an angel just by mentioning food? You really are charmingly blunt."

Lisa was tickled by my teasing, her eyes curved into crescents, and she looked like a little sweet fruit!

Maybe it was because of the pregnancy, but I really enjoyed talking and playing with children now. Their simple and pure happiness was quite contagious.

But happy times are always brief. After dining together, night had fallen, and I knew they had to leave for home.

"Alright, that’s enough fun for today, you need to behave on the way home with big brother, okay? We’ll see each other tomorrow," I said, petting Lisa’s cheek and looking at Brand with a gentle voice.

The person responsible for transporting Brand and his siblings was Lcyan Louis. He was always meticulous, indeed better suited for the task than Elliot.

Louis stretched out his hand to open the car door and stood up straight, bowing slightly to me.

I nodded at him, and said softly, "Be careful on the way, and make sure to hand them over to the butler."

"Yes, Your Highness the Lycan Queen," Louis responded, indicating he understood, and he closed the car door for Brand after Brand got in.

Brand rolled down the window and looked at me as if he had something to say. I turned to look at him and asked with my eyes, What’s the matter?

He pressed his lips together and shook his head, not saying much else.

I didn’t pay much attention to this interlude and waved goodbye to the siblings, signaling Louis that it was time to leave.

It wasn’t until a half hour later, when the news of their being attacked came from an accompanying bodyguard, that I suddenly realized Brand’s earlier peculiarity.

"Where’s Donald? Is there no one left in the manor now?" I asked while pulling on a soft, long coat, questioning Ruth.

Ruth, crouching to help me with my shoes, responded, "Yes, Your Highness. His Majesty and the others left 10 minutes ago. Since you were resting at the time, His Majesty did not wish to disturb you."

Listenting, I nodded, remembering Donald had indeed mentioned this to me during the day, "Alright, I know. Call Christian immediately and tell him to inform Donald that I must go and see the place where Louis and the others were attacked!"

I paused, then turned to look at Ruth and continued, "Eric is still recovering from his injuries in the manor, isn’t he? Go tell him..."

Sensing that I had stopped mid-sentence, Ruth looked at me with a puzzled face and asked, "What is it that you want to say to Lord Eric, Your Highness?"

I took a deep breath, suppressing the unease and anxiety within me and said calmly, "Never mind, perhaps I’m just overthinking. Just do as I’ve instructed for now!"

With that, I walked out of the room. Outside, several members of the personal guard force were standing duty, with the tall and muscular Mark at the forefront. He and Louis were mainly responsible for my security.

"Are you all ready?" I asked without turning back, not halting my steps.

Mark followed behind me and responded solemnly, "The stretch of road where Louis and the others lost contact isn’t far from the manor, it’s only a 15-minute drive away."

After hearing this, I frowned slightly and, wrapping my coat around myself, continued, "Then why did it take half an hour before we received the news? Was there deliberate interference?"

"The satellite signal in that area was suddenly cut off, and the cameras stopped working, too. If it weren’t for the alertness of the patrolling team, it might have been another half hour before anyone discovered the night attack! His Majesty and the others are on a mission tonight, all personnel are outbound. I have a bad feeling about this," Mark said with an uneasy look toward me.

He thought for two seconds before speaking, "I disagree with Your Highness going out at this time. It might well be a trap set by the enemy to lure us out. For your safety, I recommend that I lead the team to check it out while you stay at the manor."

Of course, Mark’s concerns had crossed my mind, and I stayed silent for a while before raising my eyes to the inky night sky and speaking firmly, "I’ll go with you. There’s no one else of significance in the manor tonight besides me. How do you know the enemy isn’t using a diversionary tactic? Gather all the other members of the personal guard force. Let’s go."

Seeing my firm stance, Mark no longer argued. As I got into the back seat of the car, he bent over to hand me a black box, saying respectfully, "Just in case, it’s best for you to hold onto this box. It contains items prepared for you by His Majesty."

I glanced at Mark, took the box, and said softly, "Let’s go, quickly and decisively."

chapters.title: Chapter 358 Night Raid
chapters.content:
Chapter 358: 358 Night Raid

[Margaret’s Perspective]

The night seemed devoid of both moon and starlight, leaving the street visible only by the dim street lights.

I gave Mark a vigilant look, gestured forward with the gun in my hand, and the Lycans surrounding me were highly tense, missing nothing.

The overturned black vehicle ahead was Louis’s, the majority of its body completely wrecked, with scattered debris and harsh brake marks on the ground!

We approached cautiously and found no bodies or bloodstains.

This breathed a slight sigh of relief, perhaps proving that none of Louis Brand’s party were injured. The attackers had come and gone without a trace, even capable of blocking satellite surveillance—they were clearly no amateurs.

"Spread out and search, report back to me immediately if you find any clues," I ordered.

The Lycans nodded, spreading out a bit with their guns, while Mark and a few others stayed close by my side.

I had no objections to this, aware of my own importance at the moment.

"Can you tell what kind of vehicle the attackers used? Their speedy attack and retreat don’t seem like a sudden assault."

Upon hearing my question, Mark crouched down and began a silent observation. I diverted my gaze from him and looked towards the Lycans searching the bushes nearby.

They carried night-vision equipment, making the dark woods fully visible, and just then, a flashlight from one of the Lycans flashed across a tree branch, and I caught a glimpse of a torn red scarf hanging there!

Of course, such an obvious item couldn’t escape the personal guard force’s sight, and the piece of fabric soon made its way to me.

"This is the fabric from the clothes Lisa was wearing today. It seems they made a stop here after the attack, keep looking carefully for more clues!"

I said, clenching the scarf in my hand, glanced over to that area again with furrowed brows, filled with perplexity.

The situation gradually became stranger. After about 15 minutes of searching, apart from the torn scarf, we found nothing more, and the case hit a deadlock.

After a thorough examination of the site, Mark returned to me and reported seriously, "Your Highness, we found some fragments of leather in the dented areas of the vehicle, possibly from the attackers’ vehicle. I’ve had it analyzed by those skilled in such comparisons; they said the vehicle series is likely an official one specifically from the Lilo Sea..."

As Mark intently reported the details, he suddenly paused, his eyes fixating for a few seconds.

I knew someone was establishing a mind-link with Mark, and a sense of foreboding washed over me as I stood straight to look at him.

Mark’s expression quickly cleared, and he spoke rapidly, "It’s Eric; he says the manor was suddenly attacked by a group in black. Fearing danger here, he confirmed our location and is now on his way to join us!"

"Your Highness, what do you think we should do now? Should we contact His Majesty and join them? We’re always especially worried when you are out like this!"

Mark said, his expression becoming anxious as he looked at me with concern.

I knew what he feared. After thinking for a moment, I responded gravely, "Eric’s injuries haven’t fully healed, we should withdraw and regroup with him first. Also, since the situation at the manor is unclear, we shouldn’t return rashly. There aren’t many Lycans left on watch, don’t let them confront the enemies directly; have them evacuate too..."

I paused for a second, then after another two seconds of silence, continued, "Let’s meet outside the manor at Angel Bridge, tell them to move quickly and discreetly. Go!"

Mark nodded and turned to make contact the moment I finished speaking. I headed back to the vehicle we had arrived in, my heart racing a bit too fast, a very bad feeling surfacing, but I couldn’t pinpoint what it was.

This night was destined not to be peaceful, both for White Rose Manor and myself.

Mark and I quickly reached the rendezvous point, approaching midnight by then. The cool night breeze made me close the car window slightly, feeling somewhat queasy.

The timing for such discomfort was highly inopportune. I rubbed my belly over my clothes, took a deep breath, and whispered softly, "Baby, you need to behave, now is not the time to fuss. Mommy has important things to do; don’t scare me, okay!"

Of course, the baby couldn’t respond to my words, but it seemed as if sensing my sentiments, the discomfort in my belly eased after a few minutes, providing some relief.

"Where’s the Lycan Queen? Why are you all waiting directly in the vehicle? It’s too easy for hidden enemies to spot you this way!"

Eric and Mark’s voices came from outside the window; I pressed the window down and spoke in a low voice, "Get everyone in the vehicle, let’s leave here first."

chapters.title: Chapter 359 The Spy Within
chapters.content:
Chapter 359: 359 The Spy Within

[Margaret’s perspective]

In the dim light, I saw Eric’s face drenched in cold sweat; his lips were too pale. I paused and then continued, "Eric, you and Mark get in the car with me. Everyone else hurry up and get in, we’re leaving."

Hearing my voice seemed to relax Eric. He crouched down next to me, wincing as he moved, as if pulling at a wound that had not yet healed, his teeth clenched with sweat pouring out!

Our convoy started moving. I bent down to open the medical kit at my feet, took out a small box, and handed it to Eric, "This was sudden, so you’ll have to make do with some painkillers for now. Once we’re at a safe place, I’ll have someone treat you."

Startled by my words, Eric quickly raised his hands to take it and said hoarsely, "Thank you, Your Highness. I’m sorry for the trouble."

I chuckled softly, retracted my gaze, and closed the medicine box, replying, "Stop the nonsense. As long as you’re a Lycan that Donald values, I will do everything in my power to protect you. It’s part of my duty as the Queen."

After this severe injury, Eric seemed much steadier. His eyes flickered, and he bowed his head, saying solemnly, "I would give my life for the Lycan Queen and the Wolf royal family!"

Mark, sitting in the front passenger seat, chuckled and asked, "Your Highness, Mr. Gino has replied. He says everything is ready, just waiting for us to come over!"

"Are we going to Gino’s place now? Your Highness, do you really trust that gang leader?" Eric, after taking the medicine, felt a bit better and looked at Mark then at me.

He paused, worry filling his voice as he continued, "Does His Majesty know about our situation right now? Gino is, after all, a criminal operating between law and the underworld. Shouldn’t we discuss what’s to come a bit more?"

I let out a soft breath and explained gently, "Donald has known Gino for a long time. He is trustworthy. Don’t worry. Moreover, Donald told me that he’d meet with Gino to discuss some matters after today’s operation. We’re heading that way to join them."

"Exactly, Eric. Your injuries haven’t healed yet, so don’t think too much. Her Highness knows more than you do; we just need to follow her orders now," Mark chimed in, his voice full of concern for his teammate.

After hearing this, Eric took a deep breath, nodded, and didn’t say more. I too fell silent, ready to close my eyes and rest.

’Bang!’

Suddenly, a gunshot rang out from outside the window. I instinctively bowed my head to protect myself, while disbelieving thoughts flashed through my mind, and I exclaimed, "This is impossible! How could they know we’d take this route? And how could they keep up?"

"It’s an attack! They damn caught up!"

"There are too many of them; we can’t just hold our ground! The vehicles in front speed up to get out of here; those at the rear will cover. Just beyond the next two bends is Gino’s territory, rush there first!"

Mark and Eric, from front and back, communicated through their radios, issuing orders to the other vehicles immediately.

I had by then regained my composure, listening to the gunshots outside with a stern expression.

Unsure of what I was thinking, Eric, holding a gun, looked at me. I slowly raised my head and met his gaze, speaking sternly, "We have spies among us! Otherwise, they couldn’t have tracked us down so quickly. Both you and Mark need to be very careful."

I said this feeling somewhat ironic, then scoffed and added, "Of course, I need to be the most careful, as their ultimate target— is me."

"Your Highness..."

Eric’s brow furrowed at my words, about to say something more when, in the next breath, our vehicle suddenly tipped over, rolling once along the guardrails at the roadside, scraping out nearly 20 meters!

I only managed to crouch down and curl into a ball. The next second, I slammed violently against the car window. As the grating sound of scraping blared, I glanced through the corner of my eye at the opposite window.

That one look made my heart clench tight; the next second, my vision went dark, and I passed out...

What I didn’t know was that in the first minute of our command vehicle being suddenly attacked and rolling over, a large, furry hand pulled me from the wreckage. It didn’t care about the other people in the car. After confirming I was still breathing, it let out a loud howl at the sky!

Then it disappeared into the woods with me, completely ignoring the personal guard force’s frantic gunfire and pursuit behind it!

"The Queen! It took the Queen! Hurry! Let’s chase it down!"

"Quick, someone help! The team leader and Eric are still in the car, and the gas tank is ruptured; it could explode at any moment!"

"Damn it! They’re retreating already. They were just covering for that creature!"

chapters.title: Chapter 360 Powerless
chapters.content:
Chapter 360: 360 Powerless

[Eric’s Perspective]

Actually, my consciousness was extremely clear when the vehicle tilted and rolled over. I didn’t hear any sound of shells exploding, so the real reason our vehicle was struck and sent flying wasn’t this!

After the painful scraping noise outside the car stopped, I even subconsciously turned back to check on the Lycan Queen’s condition. A gash had opened on her forehead, and she was already bleeding. I opened my mouth to say something, but the severe pain in my body didn’t support my doing so.

Then, I heard an odd sequence of footsteps, or were they footsteps? I had some doubts.

The sound moved slowly from behind me, stopped at the opposite car window, and then the deformed car door was violently yanked open from the outside!

Such an act was not something a werewolf in its normal form could achieve, at least I couldn’t.

And the image I saw next, I think I will never forget it for the rest of my life!

Because of the tilt of the vehicle, I was bound in place by the seatbelt, looking downward from a higher-than-ground-level perspective. This monster’s eyeballs were enormous, surrounded with crimson blood vessels, seemingly devoid of any sense, only showing the ferocity and brutality of a wild beast.

It sniffed the air inside the car, as if confirming something.

When it reached out and grabbed half of the Lycan Queen’s shoulder, I held my breath, widened my eyes, and roared, "Let her go! Stop it, you bastard!"

Of course, it didn’t respond to me at all; its eyes seemed fixed only on the unconscious Lycan Queen. I clenched my teeth, drew my gun wanting to shoot it, but its movements were obviously much faster!

When it dragged the Lycan Queen out, her seatbelt was also torn off. I tightly gripped the gun and fired a shot, only hitting the ground near the monster’s feet. Then, a wolf howl pierced the sky, and the next moment, it leapt over the roadside barrier and dashed into the depths of the woods.

"Ah!" I roared, trying to free myself from the things pressing down on me, and urgently shouted out the car window to the other Lycans, "Chase quickly! The Lycan Queen is still in its grasp. Don’t let it escape!"

"Team Leader Eric! This car’s fuel tank has ruptured; it could explode any moment. Let me first get you and Team Leader Mark out, just hang on!"

One of the Lycan team members reached to pull me first, but I slapped his hand away directly. I pointed in the direction where the monster had disappeared, shouting angrily, "I told you to chase that monster first! If it takes the Lycan Queen away, how are we supposed to explain to His Majesty? Are you an idiot? Hurry after it!"

My roar awakened the stunned Lycan. He gritted his teeth, completed his shift, and sprang up in pursuit.

For a while, the forest was filled with the chasing figures of Lycans. I took several deep breaths, hoping within that the monster was not as fast.

Mark in the front and the driver had been silent from the start to the end. My heart tightened slightly, and I spoke out loudly, "You two, wake up! Mark? Can you breathe? Can you respond if you can speak?"

What returned to me was silence. Smelling the blood scent in the car, I once again felt I was utterly useless! The last time I felt this way, it was in Levi’s hands.

Knowing my mindset was problematic, I forcefully suppressed it and, enduring the severe pain, dragged my leg out from under the deformed car door.

When I finally managed to crawl out of the car with great effort, my nose quickly filled with the smell of gasoline; I knew I had to be quicker. Before the car exploded, I had to rescue Mark and the driver from the car!

"You look like you could use some help!"

Hearing someone speaking, I looked up and saw that the person was none other than Mr. Gino, the mob boss we were supposed to meet.

With my voice hoarse, I urgently said, "Please help my teammates. This car is about to explode, and keep your men back as well!"

"You do look a bit familiar, do you know me?" Gino asked directly with some confusion, after speaking, he didn’t forget to signal his subordinates with a gesture to rescue people.

Seeing this, I slightly relaxed, turned my gaze towards the dense forest, and weakly said, "I’ve seen your photo before. My name is Eric, and I am the Lycan escorting the Lycan Queen to meet with you."

"If possible, I ask that you notify His Majesty as soon as possible, say that the Lycan Queen has been captured by the enemy! Thank you, Mr. Gino."

Hearing this, Gino immediately became anxious, his face tensed up.

He surveyed the surroundings and said in a deep voice, "This place is still too dangerous. Let me take you away from here first. Have your other teammates caught up yet?"

I nodded at his words, supporting the last vestiges of my clear consciousness.

"Your personal guard force Lycans are not familiar with this terrain; it seems they won’t catch up with them. Let’s go back and meet up with Donald. We’ll have to leave Margaret’s situation for Donald to figure out!"

With no better solution, Gino said and then bent down to carry me on his back and quickly moved away from the overturned vehicle.

chapters.title: Chapter 361 Setup
chapters.content:
Chapter 361: 361 Setup

[Eric’s Perspective]

’Boom!’

The enormous sound of a vehicle explosion erupted behind us, momentarily lighting up half the sky above the highway bend with a blazing inferno!

As I was placed onto the stretcher, I looked back and said huskily to Gino who was standing to the side, "It’s a good thing you arrived just in time, otherwise Mark and I would have been done for! Thank you, Mr. Gino."

Gino, upon hearing me, took a light breath, looked down at me, and said, "All Lycans from the Royal City have one terribly bad habit, and that’s always being overly polite and reserved."

"It’s not just because you’re one of Donald’s men. Even if you were just a random werewolf passing by, I couldn’t stand by and watch you have an accident without helping. I, Gino, can’t stomach doing nothing in the face of death! My only regret is that I arrived a bit too late. If I had arrived just 10 minutes earlier, Margaret might not have been abducted by those people!"

Gino turned his head, his gaze drifting off into the deep woods that seemed capable of swallowing a man whole.

No one can predict the unforeseen. The enemies were dead set on abducting the Lycan Queen, and no matter how meticulously we planned our defenses, we couldn’t deter them from their goal. Thus, these ’accidents’ were ultimately unavoidable, and no one among us can be blamed!

Of course, that excludes the traitor who was secretly sending messages to the enemy. He truly deserves to die.

I thought about these things silently, slowly turning my head to look at Mark who was still unconscious, as well as the driver. Although the car crash was extremely dangerous, thankfully, no one in our midst had died; only the driver had sustained more serious injuries!

As for me, it’s just adding new injuries to old ones. My mind is the only thing that’s relatively clear. Lying there on the stretcher, I can’t move at all, let alone do anything about the situation.

Another half hour passed, and one by one, the Lycans who had gone out to pursue returned, heads bowed in defeat. The disarray of their appearances was proof of their effort; they truly gave it their all, but to no avail.

I took a sharp breath and said hoarsely, "Let’s retreat in formation. We’ll go meet with His Majesty first. As for everything else, we’ll discuss it after we see His Majesty."

After finishing, I looked at Gino again and continued, "Please have your people lead the way."

Gino nodded upon hearing this, giving a hand signal to his subordinate driver, indicating that we could leave this place.

I hung my head, my expression forlorn, as the carriage slowly sank into silence.

[Donald’s Perspective]

Nearing 2 o’clock in the middle of the night, as I led the assault team of Lycans back from a mission at sea, I received some unpleasant news from Christian’s mouth!

Oh no, not just one piece of bad news.

"How long have you been out of contact with the Lycans guarding the manor? Did Margaret leave me any message? What about Mark? And Eric? And the others?" I suppressed the rage in my chest, my eyes interrogating and perplexed as I looked at Christian.

With his head bowed and one knee on the ground, Christian’s face was filled with guilt, his clenched fists taut with tension, "I’m sorry, Your Majesty! It’s indeed my negligence today, it’s me who failed to arrange the manor’s defenses properly, disturbing Her Highness!"

I narrowed my eyes slightly at Christian, fell silent for 2 seconds, then continued, "You not only mishandled this task, you seem a bit unclear about the seriousness and urgency of certain matters!"

"The Brand siblings mysteriously disappeared on their way home in the evening, Margaret got the message and went to investigate, then White Rose Manor suddenly came under attack, even Eric who was healing joined the fight to command. You’re telling me you just failed to arrange the manor’s defenses?"

My gaze darkened, fixating dangerously on Christian’s head as I spoke.

Christian stiffened upon hearing this, and when he lifted his gaze to mine, his face was full of confusion and obliviousness, "Who reported this news to you? No... no, Your Majesty, nobody informed me about these matters! I only knew that the manor was attacked by a group of men in black, nothing else!"

Christian’s surprise and confusion didn’t seem feigned. I watched him for a long while before finally looking away and exhaling, "It’s normal that you’re unaware; the message wasn’t passed on to you."

I said as I settled into the car, tapping the steering wheel, and continued, "First, let’s meet up with Gino. Eric and Mark are with him; they’ll explain everything that happened to me!"

Christian seemed not to understand the implication of my previous statement, but he didn’t ask or argue. To him, absolute obedience to my orders is more critical than his own life!

My questions were not meant as blame or interrogation. I was merely angry and disappointed that even the captain of my personal guard could have such a lapse in his duties, to be played for a fool.

And it seems I, Donald, have been played the fool as well.

chapters.title: Chapter 362 Convergence
chapters.content:
Chapter 362: 362 Convergence

[Donald’s perspective]

We had just left White Rose Manor, and within half an hour, numerous attacks had taken place, yet there was not a single capable person by my dear Margaret, the Lycan Queen, to protect her.

I had snapped back to reality just minutes ago, realizing that tonight there were no prominent Lycans left guarding the White Rose Manor, only Eric, who was still recovering from severe injuries.

Thinking of this, I furrowed my brows in annoyance, my fingers pressing against my temple, silently digesting the news of Margaret being kidnapped!

Yes, in the minute before I met with Christian, I had received a satellite phone call from Gino. The environment deep in the Lilo Sea was special, making the mind-link among werewolves unstable. I suppose this was why Margaret hadn’t contacted me immediately after the incident at the manor.

At times, emerging technological forces are indeed more reliable than the werewolves’ own abilities.

Upon arriving at Black Sand Bay Pier in the Lilo Sea, I stepped onto the Werewolf Gang’s territory. Gino’s headquarters was hidden not far from the pier in a secluded villa, and though it was my second time here, my mood today was drastically different from the first!

Before the motorcade had come to a complete stop, werewolves standing on both sides of the road came forward to open my door, but I pulled the door open myself and stepped out.

Gino rushed out of the main gate, looking flustered at the sight of me as if seeing a savior, "Donald, you’re finally back. Eric and Mark are almost tearing the place apart inside. Go in and calm them down!" he said urgently.

Without saying a word in response, I just nodded to my old friend and strode past him into the building.

Christian followed behind me in silence, his expression extremely grim, but nevertheless, he took on the responsibility of handling the aftermath, such as arranging care for a few Lycans who were injured in the recent mission!

"We can’t just keep waiting like this! Her Majesty has been taken by those bastards, and we still don’t know what’s happening. Why are you keeping me and my team here? I want to go out, I want to chase those bastards down!"

"I’ve already told you many times. That area has been completely searched by us. Mr. Gino even sent helicopters to scout the area several times just now, and no trace of that creature was found. It’s already fled with the Queen, understand?"

"Didn’t some of your team members also just come back? Even if you don’t trust me, you should trust your own team members, Mark!"

"How is this about not trusting you? I’m saying we can’t just keep waiting. We need to do something, understand? We..."

Mark was a forthright and direct werewolf who spoke with a voice so loud it was astonishing. I had barely reached the door of the room when I heard the unending argument between him and Eric.

And when the werewolf guarding the door bowed to open it for me, their argument abruptly ceased, especially the shouting Lycan, Mark.

"Your Majesty, you’re back. I and Eric..."

"Your Majesty! This time, we failed in our duties. Please punish us!"

I paid no attention to what they had just said or their guilt-ridden, stiff expressions. After taking a seat on the sofa, I casually pulled out the handgun tucked at my waist and flung it onto the coffee table with a ’bang’.

The sound caused the room to fall into silence. I then raised my hand to press my slightly aching forehead, suppressing the overwhelming anxiety and irritation since my mind-link with Margaret wasn’t working anymore!

At that moment, Gino moved forward, seated himself on the sofa next to me, and handed me a cup of water, trying to soothe me, "Donald, don’t be upset right now. Let Eric report the situation to you first. Today’s events really weren’t their fault. Who could have anticipated the enemy’s cunning tactics!"

Upon hearing this, I put down my hand, glanced at the glass of water Gino pushed towards me, and said indifferently, "I’m not upset, and I won’t genuinely blame anyone. Don’t worry, Gino."

After saying that, I took a deep breath in, raising my eyes to look at Eric and Mark before me.

They both had many wounds, especially Eric, who was half-leaning on a wheelchair, almost entirely wrapped up in gauze.

"Go on, Eric, recount exactly what happened," I said, looking at Eric while glancing sideways at the whiskey bottle on the table.

At that moment, I didn’t care whom it had been prepared for. I grabbed the bottle, poured myself a glass, and drank it down in one gulp, sighing softly in relief—the entire process completed very swiftly.

Gino, seeing this, opened his mouth as if to speak but eventually said nothing, only pouring me another glass in silence.

"Yes, Your Majesty! Around 11 last night, Ruth suddenly came with an order from the Queen, finding me. She said Louis had been attacked on the road while taking the Brand siblings home. The situation wasn’t clear, and Her Majesty, concerned, had already gone to check. With no one else in the manor capable of helping, she asked me to find a way to help the Queen!"

Eric spoke, slowly furrowing his brows as if struggling to remember something, and then his eyes suddenly brightened. He continued somewhat eagerly, "Just minutes after she finished speaking, the manor was suddenly attacked by a group of mysterious people. She was very urgent, wanting me to find Her Majesty, as if she had known that something unexpected was about to happen."

chapters.title: Chapter 363 Ruth’s Betrayal
chapters.content:
Chapter 363: 363 Ruth’s Betrayal

[Donald’s Perspective]

"Ruth? Indeed, if I remember correctly, Your Highness had indeed asked her to contact Christian and you when she left the manor, but that was around 10 o’clock, wasn’t it? Are you sure you didn’t get the time wrong?" Mark seemed to think that the time Eric mentioned was not quite right and asked directly.

However, I noticed an even more important point, I sat up straight and looked at Mark, saying, "Are you stating that Margaret had already tried to contact me immediately? Then why hasn’t Christian received any news at all? Where is Ruth now?"

"What did you say? The captain didn’t receive the call from Ruth? That’s impossible, I distinctly heard the Lycan Queen instruct her!" Upon hearing this, Mark asked back loudly, his eyes full of confusion about the matter.

I didn’t immediately answer his question but instead, Christian, who had been standing by, defended himself, "I didn’t receive any message from Ruth, even the sudden attack on the manor was reported to me by the manor’s guard, Lycan!"

He then turned his head towards me, thought for a moment, and continued, "Your Majesty, if neither Mark nor Eric are lying, then there’s only one possibility left."

I knew what Christian meant by ’possibility,’ but I somewhat didn’t want to believe it, and I remained silent for a while without speaking.

Eric then directly looked at me, his hands gripping tightly on the arms of his wheelchair, veins bulging, he said coldly, "It’s Ruth, she’s the one meddling in the middle!"

Suddenly hearing Eric’s conclusion, everyone in the room turned their heads towards him, their expressions changing, as if they had heard something astonishing.

"Isn’t this Ruth just Margaret’s maid? How could she... how could she ruthlessly betray Margaret like this?" Gino spoke with some confusion, his speech slowing down as if he found this conclusion somewhat cruel.

"I didn’t misremember the time, and Mark didn’t mishear the Lycan Queen’s orders back then! It’s Ruth, she’s the traitor who leaked our whereabouts to the enemy." Eric snorted coldly, and a crimson red flashed in his eyes.

He widened his eyes slightly as he looked at me, without any pause, he continued, "Your Highness had already foreseen these issues before the accident occurred. She mentioned back then that there was a mole among us, and unfortunately, I didn’t realize at first that she was referring to Ruth! Your Majesty, it was my oversight, please punish me!"

After listening to Eric’s analysis, I didn’t show any surprise; I had already guessed as much beforehand.

I just looked at him somewhat helplessly, pressed my temples, and said in a deep voice, "Is this the time for you all to apologize and ask for punishment? I just want to know one thing, where is Ruth now? Has anyone been paying attention to her whereabouts?"

As I said the last sentence, I almost gritted my teeth, spitting out every word, at this moment, I no longer cared about a mere maid’s betrayal, I just wanted to know where this traitor was.
