Ruth had come out of the Silver Moon Pack with Margaret and saying that she was one of Margaret’s most trusted people wasn’t an overstatement. Margaret treated her so well, I don’t understand why she would betray Margaret!

Just like I previously couldn’t understand why Austin would betray me.

I glanced at the expressions on my Lycan’s faces, and finally, my gaze returned to Eric, if I were correct in my guess, he was the last person to have seen Ruth.

Seemingly intimidated by my gaze, Eric swallowed and then replied, "Your Majesty, Ruth had retreated together with the other Lycans from the manor, she should be here now."

"Hmm, so where is she? Go bring her to me!" I said, roaring out loud, the coldness in my eyes as if it could freeze someone to death.

Upon hearing my command, a Lycan at the door dashed out, with Christian following closely behind.

At this moment, Gino stood up and looked at me, saying outright, "Only now reacting to capture someone, could Ruth have already run away?"

The answer to Gino was silence because I didn’t know the answer to this question either; the room was extremely quiet for a time, as if even a dropping pin could be heard.

To Gino’s surprise, Ruth had not run away in advance; she even showed no surprise or astonishment on her face when several Lycans escorted her in, not even fear or dread!

I narrowed my eyes dangerously at her, remaining silent for quite some time before speaking, "If you still have a shred of conscience, then tell me who set up tonight’s scheme, or maybe say who was behind that incredibly strong monster?"

"Margaret can’t withstand any strain or exertion now, you’re well aware of that. And this is the only chance I’m giving you, I hope you appreciate it. Don’t waste my time and effort."

Ruth had no reaction after I finished speaking the first part, standing there like a wooden stake, hollow and unfeeling.

But as I mentioned Margaret, slight fluctuations appeared on her face, and then she knelt down, covering her face and crying bitterly!

chapters.title: Chapter 364 out of control
chapters.content:
Chapter 364: 364 out of control

[Donald’s Perspective]

Ruth was a frail she-wolf, her somewhat dry and sallow face covered in freckles. Apart from her meticulous and thoughtful nature in her work, there was nothing particularly striking about her.

Back when she was in the Silver Moon Pack, it was Margaret who pitied her and rescued her from her hellish family! Ruth had five or six siblings and was the most ordinary and easiest to bully among them, and of course, least loved by her parents.

One could say that if it weren’t for Margaret’s kindness in helping her, she would have never been able to leave the Silver Moon Pack or escape that loveless hellish family.

And it was because I knew all this that I couldn’t understand her act of betraying Margaret!

Ruth lay prostrate on the floor, covering her face and crying in agony. She looked disheveled and pitiful, but I merely watched her coldly, feeling no stir of emotion within me.

I even found her reaction somewhat ridiculous and ironic. I bent down and looked at her, scoffing coldly, "What are you crying for here? The one who should be crying is Margaret, isn’t it right? What has she ever done to wrong you that you would stab her in the back like this?"

Ruth clearly understood my sarcasm. Her whole body trembled slightly as she cried, as if she was enduring some unbearable inner pain.

My patience finally ran out, and I snatched up the handgun that I had thrown on the desk earlier. I stood up abruptly from the sofa, the cold barrel pointing directly at her forehead, "Shut your mouth, I don’t want to hear your howling! Either tell me where they are taking her, or I will kill you right now, and you won’t have to struggle with explaining yourself! Make your choice, now!"

The gun barrel was pressed against Ruth’s forehead, and she, terrified, forgot to cry. Her expression froze, and my finger was already on the trigger, the muzzle so low that she had to lean her neck back!

The Lycans surrounding me were already too scared to speak. The room was so quiet except for my heavy breathing, it seemed like even their heartbeats were suppressed.

"I...Woo woo! It was a werewolf named Levi. He threatened me with my mother’s life. If I didn’t do as he commanded this time, he would kill her! I didn’t want to do it, the Lycan Queen has been too good to me, but I had no choice!"

"I know I was wrong, Your Majesty! You can kill me now, but I beg you to rescue my mother from those people. She is just a foolish and ignorant old woman who knows nothing!"

As Ruth spoke, tears welled up in her eyes like a dam had broken; she was nearly collapsing from crying!

But I had already collapsed. The corners of my mouth curled up in a bloodthirsty arc, and my tone was like millennia-old ice, "Without Margaret, your worthless life means nothing to me! Killing you would be a waste of a bullet."

"I don’t want to listen to your pathetic excuses anymore. Even if they really threatened you with your mother’s life, you shouldn’t have betrayed Margaret!" I said, unable to control my rage any longer, my gun-wielding hand wavered by a few centimeters, and I pulled the trigger!

’Bang!’

"Ahhhh!"

The sound of the gunshot mingled with my roar shattered Ruth’s sanity; she screamed, terrified by the bullet that grazed her cheek.

"Your Majesty!"

"Your Majesty!"

"Donald! Calm down!"

Several tense voices cried out simultaneously; even the usually very calm Gino called out. They seemed terrified that in my rage, I might shoot and kill this disgraceful traitor!

"In Color-pennant Town to the south of Black Sand Bay! The Queen must have been taken to Color-pennant Town..." Ruth’s voice broke down as she spoke, tears mingling with fresh blood streaming down her face, making it look like an ugly palette.

She collapsed to the ground, continuing to tremble as she spoke, "When I talked to my mother, she mentioned this town, and said that after it was all over, those people would let her leave directly by boat! I really didn’t want to harm the Lycan Queen, but I had no other choice; if I didn’t, they would kill my mother!"

"I didn’t truly intend to endanger the Lycan Queen’s life, otherwise, I wouldn’t have notified Eric! The Queen initially didn’t mean to bother Eric. She just asked me to find a way to contact Captain Christian, but I followed Levi’s orders and hid this message of her seeking help! I was wrong, I deserve to die, please hurry and save the Queen, Your Majesty!"

Ruth, crying and kneeling down before me, looked up with an expression of emotional collapse.

I withdrew my cold gaze from her face, gestured to Christian standing aside to get ready to move, and spoke in a harsh tone, "You do deserve to die, but it will wait until Margaret is safely back. You better pray your information is accurate, because even dying a hundred times won’t be enough to atone for a fraction of your sins!"

chapters.title: Chapter 365 Color-pennant Town
chapters.content:
Chapter 365: 365 Color-pennant Town

[Donald’s Perspective]

After finishing speaking, I no longer cared about Ruth, who was prostrated on the ground, and walked out the door with my gun in hand.

Unbeknownst to me, Gino signaled his men to watch Ruth closely and then followed me out with large strides, saying as he walked, "I know I can’t dissuade you now, but Levi and Henry are not easy to deal with! Going there now might not necessarily rescue Margaret, and it’s very likely to expose your own position and put you in danger, so I suggest..."

I stopped by the car door and turned back to glance at him, saying indifferently, "Since you know you can’t dissuade me, what’s the point of saying so much? I suggest you don’t give me suggestions, just stay here and wait for my further instructions. Our plan to attack Poison Snake Island remains unchanged!"

Just as I was about to crouch into the passenger seat, Gino grabbed my arm.

I glanced at my arm, held by him, and looked sideways at Gino, my eyes filled with iciness saying: Let go!

Gino sighed, released my arm, and looked at me somewhat speechlessly, saying directly, "You always act impulsively without considering anything else; that hasn’t changed at all. Can you hear me out before leaving, Your Majesty? My suggestion is that I lead the way, and we take a shortcut by flying there to save them!"

Flying was certainly the best option, but the only drawback was that the main force couldn’t keep up; we could only take a few elites from the assault team ahead, while the rest of the troops would follow quickly by vehicle.

"This is the first time I’ve seen you lose control like this; it seems Margaret really is the love of your life!"

Gino sat next to me, holding a mini submachine gun and said without turning his head.

On hearing this, I gathered my somewhat distracted thoughts and slightly frowned, retorting, "Less talk, be careful when we rescue her later. Don’t slow down the assault team with your age."

Seemingly dissatisfied that I mentioned his age, Gino gave me a light glare and quirked his mouth speechlessly, "Still as unlovable as ever, haven’t changed a bit! How did a gentle and beautiful girl like Margaret fall for you? Tell me honestly, did you use your Lycan king identity to force her?"

"So you remember I am the Lycan king, huh! Since we last met, you haven’t addressed me properly even once, right? Considering you made such an effort today, I won’t hold this ’grave disrespect’ against you; no need to thank me!"

After finishing speaking, I faintly exhaled, feeling some of the irritation inside me dissipate.

Gino feigned a snicker and gave me a somewhat speechless look, but I naturally didn’t take it too seriously because I knew he was deliberately joking to lighten my agitation and anger.

If we really calculate, Gino and I have known each other for nearly ten years. This guy has always been this carefree, even before I inherited the Wolf royal family. He says I haven’t changed at all, but hasn’t he remained the same himself?

This guy has always been a troublesome friend to me; whenever we meet, we inevitably tease or mock each other, and that’s just our way of getting along.

Actually, this trip to Lilo Sea saw Gino putting in quite an effort, both openly and behind the scenes. I ought to thank him, even though he is a ’jerk’ hovering between the lawful and the lawless sides.

From Black Sand Bay Pier to this so-called Color-pennant Town, it took us less than half an hour. Because helicopters are too conspicuous, we found a place to land as we approached the town.

The night sea breeze was cold, chilling even, when it blew on my face.

I adjusted my overcoat, disrupted by the helipad’s breeze, and my wolfish eyes gleamed in the darkness, "People are still loading and unloading goods at the pier at this hour; the villagers really live ’diligently,’ don’t they!"

As I spoke, I took out my gun and crouched low, moving closer; Gino and Christian quickly followed behind me.

[Spread out and close in, I want to see what’s inside their cargo holds!] I ordered all the Lycans through the mind-link.

As their figures merged into the night, I turned back to look at Gino and asked in a low voice, "Can you tell which shipping company that boat belongs to?"

Upon hearing that, Gino slightly straightened up and began observing, and after a while, he replied in a low voice, "The ones who use red and black stripes as their flag are only Berton’s private ships and the ’Far Voyage’ logistics company from the north. But judging by the vessel’s construction, I tend more towards it being ’Far Voyage’."

"’Far Voyage,’ huh? Seems we can’t avoid them no matter how much we turn around," I repeated the name of the logistics company, my eyes dark and dangerous.

This was the clue that Eric had tried so hard to find previously, and now it emerged blatantly before me—I was not about to be polite!

Thinking this, I raised my hand signaling Christian to act, paying no mind to Gino’s somewhat puzzled look toward me.

"You know about ’Far Voyage’ logistics? Then what were you asking me for just now?" Gino followed closely behind me, his voice low.

Only then did I turn to look at him, the corners of my mouth curving up slightly, and I whispered, "Eric came here under my orders for them. Do you think I should know?"

chapters.title: Chapter 366 ’Far Voyage’ Logistics
chapters.content:
Chapter 366: 366 ’Far Voyage’ Logistics

[Donald’s Perspective]

Gino burst into laughter upon hearing that. He glanced over and, without missing a beat, teased me, "So you’ve stumbled into this by pure luck and ended up doubling your achievements! Not bad at all, even if we can’t rescue the sister today, it’s not like we’re going back empty-handed!"

Me: "..."

"Oops! Sorry, that was out of line, I sometimes speak without thinking. Don’t mind me, ha!"

What responded to Gino was a dangerous look from me. Giving him a glance, I got down to business upon receiving the mind-link from the assault team’s Lycans, immediately bending over and taking long strides toward the ship.

With the assault team’s Lycans clearing the "obstacles" ahead, my boarding of the ship was eerily quiet. Gino followed behind me all the way, daring not to babble anymore!

Five minutes later, I was squatting in the cargo hold, patiently listening to two crew members grumble nearby.

"Damn our luck, we’ve been loading the ship all night and it’s still not done. I really don’t know what the captain is thinking. Are we going to wait out until daylight to set sail?"

"Idiot! It’s not that the cargo isn’t loaded. It’s obviously because the ’goods’ that were requested haven’t arrived yet, and the captain doesn’t dare move!"

"What goods? Don’t we just transport some regular supplies over there? Did the captain take on some other task this time?"

"Let me tell you, I just heard the captain talking to those guys on deck! They said something about the cargo being uncooperative, almost died after receiving a beatdown, and the doctor’s busy trying to save them!"

"My God! What have they captured? Is it a person?"

"Keep your voice down, idiot! Do you want others to hear too, or is your life not precious to you?"

Hearing this, the crew members lowered their voices even more. As my patience ran thin, I glanced at Christian, gesturing for him to go and ’invite’ them over!

Thanks to the ’warm-hearted’ aid of these two crew members, we quickly located the place where the ’goods’ were detained. Tapping one of the crew members on the head with my gun, I spoke softly, "Your buddy is indeed an idiot, but you’re not much better! Far Voyage must be paying you handsomely, otherwise you wouldn’t be doing this kind of life-threatening job."

The crew member, with broken teeth from Christian’s blow, of course couldn’t answer my question, nor was I actually interested in knowing.

I signaled to take these two down first, then crouched low to approach the room where the people were detained. Before the guard at the door could spot me, I snapped his neck first.

My movements were too swift; he lost his life without a chance to cry out. I felt for the pocket on his chest and pulled out a key.

Staring at the tightly shut door two meters away, I felt my nerves tense up slowly. I started praying to see Margaret inside, yet dreaded the possibility of finding her covered in wounds.

Just then, Gino seemed to sense what I was thinking, took the keys from me, and stepped forward to open the door, "Don’t worry too much. The sister is a great person; surely nothing would’ve happened to her!"

Taking a deep breath, I followed him in, only to find the person I wished to see wasn’t there.

The room was pathetically small. After Gino and I entered, there was no more space for anyone else. Christian, compliant as ever, stood guard at the door, warily watching the corridor ahead.

"It’s not Margaret, which is good. At least it proves those two idiots weren’t talking about her as the ’goods’, and that she probably isn’t hurt." Gino said as he squatted down, carefully inspecting the little figure on the floor.

Looking at the blood-covered girl, Lisa, on the ground, my brow furrowed with anger, "If she’s here, it at least confirms we’re on the right track. Those who kidnapped Brand and Lisa are the same ones who abducted Margaret!"

Hearing my conclusion, Gino stood up, facing me with a puzzled expression, "So you know this little girl? Her name’s Lisa, isn’t it? Whose child is she?"

After finishing my piece, I turned and left the room without answering Gino’s question immediately, "Send two people down to take Lisa off the ship immediately. Benjamin should have returned to Black Sand Bay by now. Bring her to him for a look."

Christian nodded at my words, quickly went back into the room, and carried her out.

"You haven’t answered my question, Donald!" Gino stood beside me, watching Christian’s retreating figure disappear around the corner, pressing on with a hushed voice.

I turned to give him a glance, stepping forward to keep up, and didn’t forget to reply, "She’s Berton’s little daughter. Margaret was attacked and taken away because of those two siblings! She must know something; we need to pull back and get out of here for now."

"Huh? We’re not continuing to search for Margaret? It seems a bit unprofitable to retreat now. Can’t you still contact Margaret?"

Gino seemed taken aback by my sudden suggestion to retreat and asked, his last question being the real issue at hand.

Without breaking my stride, I paused for two seconds before responding softly, "They must have used the same method they did before, using a special agent to sever the mind-link between Margaret and me. And the moment I saw Lisa just now, I had an intuition that Margaret was no longer on this ship!"

"’Intuition’? Can we really rely on that?" Gino asked with a hint of skepticism.

I suppressed the agitation and anxiety in my heart and countered, "Do you have a better idea?"

chapters.title: Chapter 367: Track 367
chapters.content:
Chapter 367: Track 367

[Donald’s Perspective]

What was somewhat unexpected was that there weren’t too many werewolves guarding the cargo ship of the Far Voyage, as from the moment we boarded the ship until now, having saved the hostages and left, we encountered no particularly thorny guards along the way, and Christian didn’t even get a chance to act.

Taking advantage of the darkness before dawn, I led the assault team’s Lycans in a smooth withdrawal, while Gino held his miniature submachine gun, looking back regretfully with every step, as if he was not ready to leave and wanted more from this rescue operation.

"Are we really just retreating like this? The way Ruth looked when she broke down didn’t seem like she was lying, maybe Margaret really is in Color-pennant Town, we just don’t know where exactly!"

Gino muttered to himself and, after speaking, turned his gaze away from the ship’s hull towards Christian in front, who was holding Lisa, "It would be great if this little one could wake up right now, maybe just asking her would give us something!"

In fact, that was also what I was thinking, so once we had all safely withdrawn from the dock’s detection range, I had Christian stop.

"Your Majesty, what are you planning to do?" Christian asked.

I signaled for him to crouch down so Lisa could lie more comfortably in his arms, "Who has the medicinal kit that Benjamin prepared? Bring it over." I spoke gravely, my gaze toward Lisa fraught with a gambler’s intent.

A Lycan standing to my left quickly took off the backpack he was carrying, unzipped it, and handed me a small medical kit embedded with a constant temperature system that could preserve certain precious agents, like the Recovery Potion which had passed various tests,

"Your Majesty, are you intending to use this kind of potion on her? She is still so young, shouldn’t we ask Benjamin for the correct dosage?"

Christian, seeing what I intended to do, voiced his concerns.

I took out a potion, looked at the pale green liquid inside, and without any hesitation, said directly, "I just want to wake her up to ask about Margaret’s whereabouts. Injecting her with half a dose should be fine; there won’t be any problems."

As I said this, I rolled up Lisa’s sleeve, pressed the release button on the syringe, and the needle sprung out automatically, injecting the pale green Recovery Potion into Lisa’s body.

After securing the remaining potion, I took a deep breath and began to wait for the drug to take effect in Lisa.

Gino had been silently watching by my side, and seeing me administer the injection to Lisa with proficient movements, he discreetly let out a sigh of relief and said earnestly, "That must be the same potion Margaret mentioned before; after the fight that day, you all withdrew so hastily, I didn’t even get a chance to ask her properly about it..."

When Gino suddenly brought up Margaret, my attention was instantly diverted, I turned to look at him and asked, "What do you mean? When did she mention this to you?"

"It was that day at Sant Angelo Hotel, she promised to give me that potion! You can’t just weasel out of the deal," Gino replied anxiously as I questioned him.

I quickly recalled the event and understood what he was referring to, withdrawing my gaze and calmly responding, "It’s a small matter, I’ll discuss it with Benjamin."

Gino, hearing this, smiled again and turned his head towards Lisa in Christian’s arms, "So, after using this potion, how long does it take to take effect? How long do we need to wait here?"

Just as Gino’s words fell, I saw Lisa’s eyelashes flutter slightly. I exhaled a breath and answered Gino, "Not long now."

Lisa opened her eyes at the same moment I finished speaking, she firstly groaned and then, as the dawn’s early light became clearer, recognized her situation, "You are my sister’s husband, I have seen you before at White Rose Manor."

The little girl’s voice was very weak, but her round eyes were incredibly lively. They soon became moist, and she lifted her hand as if attempting to grab the hem of my clothes.

I looked at her and spoke softly, "Yes, can you tell me if you have seen Margaret recently? Also, what happened last night, why were you alone on that ship, and the others who were with you, where are they?"

Lisa obediently listened to me finish speaking, tears streamed down her dirty face. She coughed harshly, it sounded as if her lungs were damaged, "My brother and Margaret have been taken away by those people! They went out over the sea, said they were going to Poison Snake Island, cough cough cough! Please hurry and save them, my brother is very, very hurt."

As Lisa spoke, her injuries began to bleed again, I raised my hand to stop her attempt to get up, and hurriedly said in a soothing voice, "I will go save them, please don’t worry, just be good, okay? Regulate your breathing, don’t rush."

"Do you remember the approximate time they were taken? Other than Margaret and Brand, what about the other people, the brothers who sent you home last night, how are they doing?"

chapters.title: Chapter 368: Preparing for Battle
chapters.content:
Chapter 368: Preparing for Battle

[Donald’s Perspective]

Little Lisa tried hard to regulate her breathing, clutching the hem of my clothes, her fingers tightened as she sobbed in a hoarse voice, "I’m sorry, I don’t know what time it was! I only remember hearing a few strokes of the bell..."

"The others... everyone except the leader, Brother Louis, is dead! After that group attacked us, they quickly brought us to this place. They didn’t ask any questions, they just shot the other Lycan brothers, wuu wuu wuu! They’re so terrifying, they’re like a bunch of demons!"

"Cough cough cough! You... you must avenge everyone, you must catch all those bad guys..." Lisa spoke slowly then closed her eyes, her little face smeared with tears and grime, looking very wretched.

"She she she, is she okay? Did she fall asleep?" Gino was startled and stuttered out the question.

I took a deep breath and looking at Lisa’s face, I said, "She’s alright, just exhausted and asleep."

After saying that, I stood up and faced the direction of the dock, silently pondering.

The information Lisa brought us was very useful, the most important being the confirmation of Margaret’s situation, my Margaret, who at least was not in any mortal danger!

Levi and Henry had gone to great lengths to abduct her and would certainly not miss this excellent opportunity to threaten me, so until I received their demands, Margaret wouldn’t be in any real danger.

That was, provided they didn’t know Margaret was pregnant now. If they found out, the child’s situation could be even more dangerous than Margaret’s!

Would a rebel alliance agree to leave the progeny of the monarch they rebelled against? It’s unthinkable.

So rescuing Margaret can’t be delayed at all, attacking Poison Snake Island is imperative!

The morning sea breeze was cool, but I didn’t feel cold at all, I just felt more awake and rational than ever.

"What are you thinking about, Donald? What are we going to do next?"

Perhaps I had been silent too long, Gino walked over to stand alongside me, looking at me with some concern as he asked.

"This time they kidnapped Margaret and Berton’s children, and killed so many of my Lycans, it seems like they are declaring all-out war on me! Berton’s previous attitude was a bit ambiguous, but after tonight, I could give him a little push."

I said and let out a cold laugh, tapping the handle of the handgun with my fingertip, and continued speaking, "Lisa is so badly injured, and Brand’s situation is unclear after being captured, even if Berton was hesitant before, he can’t stand it anymore, right? They’ve gone too far this time."

"You’re not wrong in your analysis, just a bit disgusting in your wording," Gino commented quietly, his eyes turning colder as he went on, "Let’s go back first; we’re just waiting for Elliot to return with the news."

I nodded and turned towards the vehicles hidden behind the trees, Gino also took a deep breath and followed, taking long strides.

Lilo Sea has always just been calm on the surface, while treacherous tides churned beneath with various powers fishing in murky waters. I hadn’t been able to clean up this ’thorn in my side’ before, but now it was time for it to recognize who its real master was.

Henry and Levi both wanted to take advantage of the special situation here to grow and expand, and I absolutely couldn’t tolerate it — I don’t stand for sand in my eyes!

Them kidnapping Margaret was a provocation in my eyes, and those who dare to provoke me usually don’t end well.

After returning to Gino’s Black Sand Bay, I instructed Benjamin to treat Lisa’s injuries immediately. Since we had already used the Recovery Potion on her, Benjamin simply checked her wounds thoroughly and bandaged some that hadn’t yet healed well.

By the time he had finished, daylight was upon us, and a new day had officially begun.

"Your Majesty, the troops you previously ordered have all been fully equipped, and the heavy machinery camp led by Charlie can also arrive by this afternoon. In addition, Lord Simpson and Dean Andrew will also be coming," Benjamin reported to me respectfully, bowing his head formally.

Upon hearing this, I stopped what I was doing, and without lifting my head, I asked, "Dean Andrew is coming to be in charge of backup and rescue operations, but what’s Simpson doing in Lilo Sea? Is the Elders’ Council up to some new trick?"

Benjamin of course knew what I was asking about. He took a folder from his subordinate and explained as he handed it to me, "The news of the Empress Dowager’s disappearance somehow leaked. Lord Simpson is coming because of that. I’ve already sent a letter in advance to explain to him, but he didn’t accept it, saying he wants to come and see the situation for himself and help you with the search for the Empress Dowager."

I glanced at the paper reply on the desk and snorted wordlessly, "These old fellows always have some plan in mind, claiming to lighten my burden, but in reality, they are coming to put pressure on me."

chapters.title: Chapter 369 suppresses them
chapters.content:
Chapter 369: 369 suppresses them

[Donald’s Perspective]

Seeing me complaining about that bunch of old farts in the Council of Elders, Benjamin didn’t dare to say much; he just lowered his head, not knowing what he was thinking.

I lifted my hand to close the papers, let out a sigh, and said, "Is there anything else? Any news from Elliot’s side?"

Benjamin looked up at me and replied, "Half an hour ago, Elliot said they were already on their way back, and if all goes well, they should be able to return in two hours. Moreover, Miss Elizabeth and Anthony will arrive at the Lilo Sea with Dean Andrew. I haven’t told the second young miss about the Lycan Queen’s kidnapping yet, Your Majesty..."

"I know, you are to take care of bringing her and Anthony here; leave the rest to me," I said, taking the troublesome matter off his hands.

Benjamin breathed a sigh of relief and was about to leave but was stopped by me calling out.

"Gino needs some Recovery Potion; when you go down, prepare five doses for him immediately. The battle environment for this expedition is special; your medical team must also be well-prepared. I will have Dean Andrew fully support you from the rear."

"Yes, Your Majesty! We’ve given the Recovery Potion a new name, ’New Birth’."

Benjamin continued with a smile, "This was inspired by Her Highness. She said before, MASTER has ’Rebirth’ and ’Eternal Life’, those are like things of demons, and our ’New Birth’ can suppress them both in practical and spiritual sense! Sounds good, doesn’t it?"

"That’s a good name, really nice," I chuckled, already picturing the scene when Margaret said these words to Benjamin.

Her eyes must have been sparkling, her pretty face full of conviction and tenacity, her whole being as if imbued with a boundless vitality.

"She always brings new strength and courage, like sunflowers in the summer."

Benjamin wholly agreed with my words, nodding deeply as he inhaled sharply, "Her Highness is the most wonderful, warmest woman in the world. You will certainly lead us to successfully bring her back from there! And anyone trying to harm her deserves to die under our sharp claws."

As Benjamin spoke, the light in his eyes was resolute and clear.

Indeed, someone as adorable and gentle as Margaret was almost impossible not to like.

Of course, that excludes some who are inherently abnormal, those who are born hating the world, who, upon seeing beautiful and gentle people and things, can only think of destroying and erasing them!

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I was awoken by the ice-cold seawater, the stench of the fishy water incredibly unpleasant. I involuntarily furrowed my brow, and even this movement pulled at the wound on my forehead!

Agonizing pain stimulated my nerves, and I couldn’t help but groan out loud, becoming fully alert in an instant, with the surroundings slowly coming into view.

The waves crashed against the beach less than ten meters away from me, the sea breeze was cool on my skin, and my soaked clothes clung coldly to my skin. I heard a somewhat familiar voice!

"I told you we would meet again soon, and look, here we are," Levi’s voice was smug and frivolous as he sat ungracefully on a rock on the surface of the beach, seeming in quite a good mood.

I tried to lift my neck to say something, but before I could speak, I began to cough violently, bending over in a sorry state.

"I know what you want to say; no outsider can enter this deserted island! And even if Donald wants to save you, he has no other means. This time he is destined to lose, hahaha!" Levi laughed loudly, as if he could already foresee their imminent victory.

Listening to him, I slowly curled up the corners of my mouth, my voice hoarse as I spoke, "Only those whose hearts are not firm enough keep on stressing their strength. In my eyes, you are such a person right now."

"Cough, cough, cough! Despicable and vile methods might give you a momentary victory, but the ultimate win will belong to Donald in the end. You won’t be able to be arrogant for long," I said, coughing violently, as if my lungs were on fire.

Levi first watched me silently, then after a while motioned with his finger for a subordinate to step forward and check on me.

I bore the discomfort in my body, and with a cold gaze, I deterred the subordinate, shouting, "Keep away from me! Get lost!"

"You want to play the part of defiant unto death, then at least make it look convincing! Right now you seem like a pitiful fish out of water, gasping for breath, completely unthreatening," Levi said, standing up and walking over to me.

With his chin tilted upward, his gaze mocking, he finally added, "Lock her up with that boy, give them some water and food later. Just don’t let them die!"

chapters.title: Chapter 370 Trapped
chapters.content:
Chapter 370: 370 Trapped

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Levi’s words were not directed at me. The werewolf behind him came up, yanked me up from the ground with such brutality that it seemed as though he wanted to strangle me alive!

"Do you intend to kill her just by yanking? Look at her, fragile as a reed, about to lose her life at any moment. Can you lighten up a bit? She is the Lycan Queen, after all. If you kill her now, aren’t you afraid that Donald will go insane and crush you to death?"

As Levi bellowed, the grip of the werewolf holding me loosened slightly. He cast me a wary glance and snorted coldly before continuing to drag me forward.

I could feel Levi’s gaze on my back, making me shiver involuntarily. I stumbled, and they tossed me into a low shack!

I was relieved that there was a layer of straw on the floor of the shack, so I wasn’t thrown directly onto the ground. My child was still too fragile to withstand any more turmoil.

"Margaret? Is that you?"

Due to the poor lighting in the shack, I hadn’t noticed another person lying there. His sudden voice gave me quite the scare.

"Brand? How come you’re here too? Are you hurt?" I asked somewhat uncertainly, the smell of blood tainting the air of the shack tightening my voice with tension.

Brand seemed to shift his body, his head and upper body emerging into a dimly lit area. After managing a few muffled noises, he responded, "I’m alright. How are you? I vaguely heard those people talking. Were you harmed?"

Automatically, I shook my head, then realizing Brand couldn’t see me, I spoke out, "No, I’m not injured. What about the others? Where are Lisa and Louis? Why aren’t they with you?"

Brand went silent after my question, the tension making my muscles clench in anticipation.

"Where are they?" I closed my eyes and asked again, more sternly.

Brand took a deep breath, pressed his head against the wall, and slowly sat up straight. His expression was obscured by the dim light, but his voice gradually continued, "Louis was tortured by them for two hours and then thrown into the sea right before we arrived at the island! As for Lisa..."

My heart ached sharply, and the pain made it difficult to speak, but luckily Brand did not need my further prompting. After a few seconds of pause, he continued.

"Lisa was slapped around by them as we left that small town. She hit her head on the corner of a wall and got a big gash... She passed out right there. Those people, fearing she wouldn’t wake up and would anger the people on the island, left her behind. I have no idea how she is now."

Brand spoke slowly, his voice saturated with sorrow and guilt, as if he was on the verge of breaking apart.

I opened my mouth to say something, but before I could speak, I heard Brand’s muffled sobbing, which made my heart tremble, and a faint pain surged in my abdomen.

"It’ll be alright, Brand. Little Lisa is so adorable, the Moon goddess wouldn’t bear to see her suffer! Maybe she has already been rescued by now. Not being brought to the island might be a blessing. Donald will surely find a way to save us all once he gets the news."

"Louis... perhaps I shouldn’t have let him lead the group to take you all home. It’s my fault he and the other Lycans died."

Tears slid uncontrollably down my cheeks as I lay trembling slightly on the ground.

Brand stifled his crying and gave me a kinship-in-suffering wry smile, "We are in a real mess now, not knowing when the Lycan King or my father will come to save us. Margaret, do you think we can make it off this island alive?"

Despite my deep grief for the loss of my friends, I never doubted the answer to Brand’s question. Donald would never give up on me. He must be racking his brains to track my whereabouts. He was so smart and astute that conquering the difficulty of reaching the island was merely a matter of time for him.

I moved my uninjured shoulder, leaned against the wall to sit up, breathed out a heavy sigh, and looked towards Brand, saying softly, "We can, we will definitely make it back alive. I have a lot of faith in Donald."

At that, Brand also met my gaze, a glint of light in his eyes, and nodded before taking a sharp breath, "Are you alright? I see you’re soaking wet."

I knew what Brand was hinting at, and my face grew stern as I lowered my voice, "I’m fine. From now on, let’s not talk about my situation nor what they might do to me next. Don’t let them catch on, alright? By not saying anything, you’re helping to protect her. Do you understand?"

Brand’s face turned serious at my words, and he nodded solemnly, "I understand. Don’t worry, I’ll keep you both safe."

I let out a soft chuckle, twisting my bound hands.

"Does it make you uncomfortable being tied up like that? Wait for me, okay? I’m about to break the rope on my hands. Once I’m free, I’ll help you loosen yours!"

At those words, I was taken aback. Then I asked, "What are you using to wear down the ropes? Don’t let them find out."

chapters.title: Chapter 371: Premonition of 371 Brand?
chapters.content:
Chapter 371: Premonition of 371 Brand?

[Margaret’s perspective]

While Brand was silently working on his own, he looked at me after hearing my question and whispered, "No, their ropes are so rough that I almost managed to break them!"

Seeing this, I fell silent and lent an ear to help Brand listen for any sounds outside the room.

Fortunately, no one came during that time, and Brand quickly untied the ropes. He knelt on the hay and slowly crawled toward me, and only then did I fully see all the wounds on his face.

"Oh my God, Brand, how could they beat you like this? Does it hurt? How could these beasts be so cruel to both you and Lisa!" I said with furrowed brows and a pained expression, looking at Brand with compassion.

Brand was helping me untie the ropes around my wrists. After hearing me, he gave a light laugh as if to soothe my emotions.

He nonchalantly said, "It only looks worse than it is. I’m not in pain at all. There, I’ve untied your ropes. Remember to hide when they come in later; don’t let them see you!"

As Brand spoke, he looked at me as if he had just noticed the wound on my forehead. "Margaret, you’re hurt too! Your forehead is still bleeding. Doesn’t it hurt? You can’t go on like this, we need to find a way to get them to treat your wound, or you’ll lose too much blood."

Only when Brand mentioned my wound did I start to feel the pain again. I bit my lip and stopped Brand from calling out for help, hurrying to say, "Don’t bring them here; I can handle this wound myself."

While speaking, I took off my soaked coat and tore a strip of cloth from the hem of my skirt. Lacking anything to disinfect or stop the bleeding, I folded it and pressed it directly onto my forehead to prevent further exposure and somewhat stem the bleeding.

Fortunately, the room was not drafty, and it was still possible to keep warm.

After settling down, I turned back to look at the silent Brand. Recalling some past events, I then asked, "When we left White Rose Manor yesterday, did you have something you wanted to tell me? You were hesitating; did you have a premonition of the attack that happened later?"

Brand stiffened for a moment upon hearing this, then shook his head and responded, "No, I just suddenly remembered what my dad had said."

The young man turned his head to look at me and continued, "Yesterday, when my sister and I were going out, he reminded us to be careful. He said that Lilo Sea has been quite unrestful recently and didn’t want us running around everywhere. I just wanted to tell you about that, not that I had a premonition about the attack."

I listened and nodded, deciding not to ask further.

Too much had happened since last night, and after Brand quieted down, I felt rather tired myself, utterly exhausted and hungry.

I looked up at the window where the light was coming in, trying to discern the current time, just as footsteps approached the house!

Sensing someone coming, I quickly said to Brand, "Someone’s coming, hide the ropes behind you."

As soon as these words fell, the door was flung open from the outside, light flooding in. I turned my head and shut my eyes, taking a moment to adapt before I looked up.

It was a werewolf I did not recognize. He glanced at Brand lying on the floor and then shifted his gaze to me, "Get this woman out, the miss wants to see her first."

I didn’t know who he was referring to as the miss, but from his commanding demeanor toward the other werewolves, it wasn’t hard to tell that the miss was of noble status.

"Where are you taking her? Let her go, come at me instead if you need something." Brand couldn’t help but speak up to stop them, but these people clearly weren’t going to listen to him!

I looked at Brand and shook my head, signaling him not to provoke these people, but by then it was already too late to remind him.

The werewolf who had spoken saw Brand speak up, snorted coldly, and then looked at him, "You’re pretty caring for such a young age, eh? Hasn’t anyone ever told you not to meddle in things that don’t concern you?"

With that, the werewolf stepped forward and stomped on Brand, a mocking sneer on his face, "Take this kid too. The miss might be more pleased seeing how protective he is of that woman!"

After hearing the werewolf’s comment, Brand stopped struggling. Seeing him at that moment, I knew he was worried about me being taken away alone; he deliberately spoke up to provoke these people, hoping to be taken with me.

I took a gentle breath, withdrawing my gaze from Brand, feeling partly reassured yet helpless.

After we were led out of the cabin, Brand and I were blindfolded and thrown into a car, which rattled along, seemingly over a rocky path.

About half an hour later, the car finally stopped, and I was dragged into a building by several strong hands onto a smooth, wooden floor with a pleasant fruit aroma in the air.

chapters.title: Chapter 372 ’Miss
chapters.content:
Chapter 372: 372 ’Miss

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I sat sideways on the ground, and although I couldn’t see anything, my ears became exceptionally sensitive. Suddenly, the door to the room was flung open and the sound of high heels clacking on the floor began: could this be the "Miss" those werewolves were talking about?

With that haphazard guess in mind, I swallowed and asked, "What exactly do you want? Release me and Brand."

The sound of footsteps eventually stopped in front of me, as her gaze fell unabashedly upon me. She squatted down, apparently wanting to look me in the eye, "So, you’re Margaret? Quite pretty and gentle indeed – no wonder Henry is so fond of you!"

I had never heard this woman’s voice before, she must be someone I didn’t know.

But she casually mentioned my name and even brought up Henry, speaking his name with a hint of affection and longing as if she knew him well.

"Who are you? Skulking about without the courage to show your face, what exactly are you feeling guilty about?" I retorted, tilting my chin up to face her as best as I could.

Though my eyes were blindfolded at the moment, I refused to lose the confrontational aura against my enemy.

As if finding my question particularly amusing, she stood up with a chuckle, her sharp finger hooking under my chin, "You’re brave but not very smart. I had them blindfold you actually to protect you, because most people who have seen me end up dead."

"Of course, a precious hostage like you – I wouldn’t let you die easily. You could fetch a very nice price."

As she spoke, she even stroked my face with her hand. I didn’t find the gesture endearing in the least; it felt more like a cold, venomous snake slithering across my face!

I jerked my head away and sneered in response, "Such mystification! If you were really that powerful, would you still be lingering on this small island? By the way you speak, you seem quite concerned about MASTER. Is he still alive? After I shot him so many times, and yet he can still live, what a monster!"

’Smack!’

A crisp slap suddenly echoed through the room. As I turned my head, feeling the sharp sting on my face, I began to laugh again after a moment, "What? Seeking revenge for Henry, eh? This little hurt is nothing; if you’ve got the guts, just kill me. Threats and intimidation like this won’t scare me. If Henry is any sort of man, he shouldn’t be hiding behind your skirts."

After I delivered those lines, the other person suddenly regained her composure, as if she hadn’t been the one losing control and hitting someone just a moment ago.

For several breaths, I could feel her gaze as if it wanted to tear me to shreds, but in an eerie twist, her voice became somewhat gentle, "I misspoke earlier, you’re not less smart, but too smart! Do you think you can anger me with such words? You’re underestimating me."

It seemed she turned and walked away a few steps, her voice continuing in a gentle manner, "I won’t let you die easily. On the contrary, I’m going to take good care of you – at least until I can make sure Donald dies before your eyes!"

Upon hearing this, I took a deep breath, ignoring her provocation. Meanwhile, Brand, who had been silent beside me, finally spoke.

He straightened up and spoke sarcastically, "Since you’re so confident, why not let us see your face? You slapped Margaret right away – is that what you call taking good care of someone? Your actions speak the opposite of your words; is this how people on your island behave? Pfft!"

"So eager to defend her, could it be you fancy her too? Hahaha, I really didn’t see that Margaret has such charm; why do all men have such a favorable impression of her?"

"Shut up! I won’t allow you to insult Margaret; she is the most esteemed woman of this kingdom, the Lycan Queen!" Brand retorted furiously, seemingly driven to madness.

I listened to their exchange coolly, taking a faint breath and then speaking out to caution, "Brand! Don’t let her provoke you with words; she’s trying to make you angry on purpose. Don’t talk to her!"

Hearing my voice, Brand gradually calmed down, but the woman across from us couldn’t contain herself. She scoffed and said, "You really are quite something. I couldn’t compare to you when I was younger, and now even a 14- or 15-year-old boy is utterly ensnared by you!"

"Heh! Only someone as vile and filthy as you would look at a man and assume he must fancy you. Brand is just my brother; you better watch your mouth." I scoffed with derision, snapping back.

While the woman and I were engaged in this verbal sparring, someone else approached from behind me and Brand!

I lowered my head slightly and listened carefully, but the newcomer seemed hesitant and remained silent.

After a long pause, the woman I had been bantering with suddenly sighed, stood up, and in a conceding tone said, "Enough, for your sakes I’ll let her go for today. I’m leaving, enjoy your ’playtime’ with her, Lillian!"

chapters.title: Chapter 373 long time no see
chapters.content:
Chapter 373: 373 long time no see

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Upon hearing this familiar name, I lifted my head and spoke with some surprise, "Lillian? Is that you?"

The next moment, I heard her step forward and yank off the eye mask on my face.

I squinted my eyes and took a moment to adjust, then I looked up at her.

"It’s been a long time, Your Highness, the Lycan Queen!" Lillian said with a hint of sarcasm, her familiar face brimming with schadenfreude.

Faced with Lillian’s sudden appearance, the surprise in my heart gradually dissolved. After pondering for a moment, I felt a sense of helplessness and amusement.

I slowly curved my lips while maintaining eye contact with Lillian, but there wasn’t a trace of a smile in my eyes, "Licia went to great lengths to get you out of the Royal City, and this is how you repay her efforts? Austin and Henry made their choice to collaborate, but you didn’t have to get involved. After all, being called a traitor isn’t flattering!"

Lillian smiled upon hearing this and leaned in, looking straight at me to say, "How do you know that isn’t what Licia truly wants? Perhaps she has already betrayed you, and now she’s on Henry’s side, along with us."

With an unmistakably proud look on her face, I began to doubt the truth behind her words! If Licia had indeed abandoned Donald in favor of Henry, that would be exceedingly cruel.

Thinking of this, my heart ached for Donald, but I didn’t show any fear on my face.

Seeing that I remained silent, Lillian straightened up and slowly made way for the door, "I forgot to tell you, my brother doesn’t need your concern about what he wants to do; he can manage it himself."

"Although many have said this to you before, I still want to say, long time no see, Margaret!"

My eyes widened as I looked at the werewolf behind Lillian. If it weren’t for the searing pain on my face, I would have started to doubt everything as a terrible nightmare!

"Have you forgotten me so soon? I thought I had left a rather deep impression on you!" Austin said with a smile.

Goosebumps crawled up my arms, and the sudden ’resurrection’ of Austin frightened me so much that I unconsciously tugged at the corner of my mouth, speaking in an evasive tone, "Because of your death, Donald nearly collapsed several times, and now you’re telling me you’re still alive? Ha! You’re really a bunch of terrifying and twisted maniacs!"

I said, and then burst into an abrupt laugh, feeling as if I too was going mad.

"Do you have any more ’surprises’ to tell me? Perhaps now is the time! Is Licia here too? Why doesn’t she come out and see me?"

Seemingly satisfied with my current state of disarray, Lillian took Austin’s arm with a raised chin and a mocking smile, "What’s the rush? Aunt Licia is busy with something right now, she’ll come to see you later."

My suspicions were confirmed by her words, and I looked down, laughing bitterly without saying more.

Brand was the first to notice that something was wrong with me. He looked at me with great concern and, after some thought, spoke directly to the man standing last in the crowd, who had been silent all along, "Haven’t you noticed that Margaret’s condition is off? If you truly want her dead, you might as well leave her unattended! Even feeble, foolish humans have rules against mistreating captives, is that all the scope you have, Rebels?"

Lillian laughed at Brand’s words and looked up and down at him, amused.

"Your intentions are too obvious. The fact that we haven’t killed Margaret is testament to our kindness. What makes you think we would treat her wounds? Kids are just too naive." Lillian spoke, and even scoffed disdainfully, but the next moment, her facial expression froze in place.

Henry gestured toward Aiden beside him with a casual tone, "Clean up the wounds on her face, and try not to kill her too easily."

"Have you lost your mind, Henry? Why would you treat her wounds? Have you forgotten who put those bullets in you?" Lillian asked, perplexed, looking at Henry as if he were a madman.

Henry didn’t even spare her a glance, just lifted the corners of his lips, and coldly said, "Do I need to explain my actions to you? You seem to be losing sight of your place. By what authority are you questioning me?"

Lillian closed her mouth upon hearing this, seemingly a bit wary of Henry at that moment, and she gripped Austin’s hand tighter.

Austin seemed to have changed a lot. He no longer protected Lillian with meticulous care as he did before, and he appeared accustomed to Lillian’s unrestrained and blunt attitude.

Aiden walked over with a medical kit and began to kneel down to clean the wound on my forehead.

The alcohol-soaked gauze was pressed mercilessly onto my wound, and the sharp pain made me grunt softly, as my face flushed red quickly!

I glared coldly at Aiden, with no doubt in my mind that he was doing it on purpose.

"My apologies, Your Highness, I am not a professional Medical Practitioner, so my treatment might be a bit crude. Please bear with it for a moment!"

Aiden’s eyes were full of provocation and mockery. As he spoke, he pressed the object against me again. I shivered and laughed coldly, "Then you are indeed very unprofessional. I wonder how someone deemed a ’genius with potions’ like MASTER ever saw potential in you, to have you as his subordinate."

chapters.title: Chapter 374 Standoffs
chapters.content:
Chapter 374: 374 Standoffs

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Aiden’s face turned red with anger as I rebuked him with a single sentence. He looked at me and shouted, "You’re ungrateful! Do you think you are still the high and mighty Lycan Queen? If it weren’t for the master’s instructions to treat you well, do you think you could still be sitting here comfortably?"

"You don’t even look at the wounds on that boy; did the master spare him a glance? If not for..."

"Shut up, Aiden!"

The fervent Aiden was suddenly interrupted by Henry. His face flushed with indignation, but he still obediently closed his mouth as Henry commanded.

I coldly glanced at Aiden in front of me and then turned my head aside, coldly saying, "How ridiculous. According to your logic, should I also be grateful to Henry? Do I need his absurd ’kind treatment’?"

After I finished speaking, I looked up at Henry, who was sitting opposite me, sneered, and continued, "Stop playing these utterly hypocritical games. I’m not buying it!"

"Margaret!"

Brand suddenly spoke up at this point, cutting off my words. He looked at me and shook his head slightly, his eyes filled with worry and disapproval.

I knew what Brand was thinking. He was worried about my injuries and the child in my womb.

But I couldn’t accept Henry’s hypocritical favors; feeling his extra concern only made me feel nauseated.

I remained silent, my face clearly showing my refusal. Even Lillian, who was usually oblivious to other people’s expressions, could notice my disgust toward Henry at this moment.

She smirked in schadenfreude, but the next second, Austin warned her with a look.

The room became extremely quiet for a while, like a pool of stagnant water.

Ultimately, the one who broke this silence was Henry himself. He gestured for Aiden to step back and slowly stood up, looking at me.

As he approached, I didn’t move but began to ponder other things, like who was the woman who had been probing me earlier? Why did she leave as soon as Lillian and her group entered? Why didn’t she let me and Brand see her face?

This woman held a very high status and had an ambiguous and intimate attitude toward Henry, yet her actions were secretive. If I wasn’t mistaken, she was likely the mastermind behind Poison Snake Island that Donald had mentioned before.

"Maybe I shouldn’t have let her see you; that was my oversight," Henry suddenly said to himself while standing in front of me.

Instinctively, I looked up, quickly realizing who Henry was referring to. My gaze toward Henry sharpened. After a moment’s thought, I decided to skip over that topic, "The earlier abduction of Licia was also done by your people, right? I hadn’t seen it before, but you seem to have a knack for this kind of job!"

I said lightly, my tone filled with some confusion, "How did you know about our whereabouts? Could it be that you still have spies in the Royal City?"

Henry seemed quite pleased that I had started talking. A smile spread across his face as he squatted down to my level. Being so close was already beyond normal interaction, but I knew I couldn’t back down—a step back would signify fear and cowardice.

"Who said Licia was ’abducted’? Couldn’t she have gone with someone willingly?" Henry’s gaze drifted across my face, making me feel as if ants were crawling all over me.

We had not ruled out the real reason behind Licia’s disappearance, and one possibility was precisely what Henry had just mentioned—Licia went with them voluntarily!

So, the issue reverted to the original question, what made Licia make such a choice? If it was only because Henry was her nephew, then Donald, her own son, would have been more significant. Any reasonable person would know how to choose between the two, right?

I was genuinely curious about how Licia thought. My mother-in-law seemed to possess some unconventional ways of thinking.

"I despise the Werewolf Royal City deeply; how could I have spies there?"

As Henry spoke, a hint of ruthlessness flashed in his eyes, but then he suddenly laughed and continued, "I really don’t like engaging in such schemes of turning people against each other. As for your maid, Ruth, that was a necessary evil."

Hearing this, I glanced at Austin beside me, my lips curling into a sarcastic smile.

Henry was a lunatic full of lies. If he disliked inciting others to betray friends and relatives, then what about Angel, Austin, and even Licia?

I really didn’t want to listen to his nonsense anymore. The expression in my eyes was utterly dismissive, "What did you offer Ruth to bewitch her? She doesn’t seem like someone who would easily betray me."

"Was that monster that could knock over a car your new ’creation’? Sometimes I really wonder if the Moon goddess is sleeping too soundly, forgetting to punish you, this devilish beast!"

chapters.title: Chapter 375 Strange Henry
chapters.content:
Chapter 375: 375 Strange Henry

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"Turning your maid against you and getting her to cooperate with us indeed took quite a bit of effort, but everyone has weaknesses... Fortunately, hers were quite obvious, and that old foolish she-wolf was easy to control."

Henry had hinted so much, how could I still not understand? I glared at him with wide eyes and scornfully rebuked, "You despicable scoundrel, you really would stoop to anything! What have you done to Ruth’s mother?"

Upon hearing my rebuke and insults, Henry didn’t get angry but laughed instead, as if he found my current expression very amusing, "It seems I won the bet with Levi!"

This jerk had no intention of answering my question. I took a deep breath and looked at him with icy eyes.

"Don’t look at me with such resentment. It was just a harmless bet. I bet Levi that when you learned about this, your first reaction would definitely be to ask about that she-wolf first, and sure enough, I won."

He said, his smile deepening as he continued in a softer tone, "Levi said you wouldn’t be so naive, but as it turns out, my judgment of you was right. You yourself are now a prisoner, yet you still think of worrying about others. I really don’t know if you’re overly naive or just plain stupid."

I looked at Henry without speaking, not showing any signs of fear. After a while, I retorted with a cold laugh, "So what? What does it matter to you what kind of person I am? But for someone with a naturally rebellious personality like yours, it’s quite sad that you will never understand the pure and kind heart of a normal person."

After hearing what I said, Henry’s expression changed. He dropped his pretense of gentleness and said coldly, "I really hope you stay this defiant because it’s going to make the upcoming war with Donald much more interesting."

I was slightly stunned by his words, realizing I couldn’t quite grasp his meaning.

However, I didn’t pay much attention to this interlude, for delving into the thoughts of a madman can be rather disturbing.

Henry quickly left with a group of people, as if he was busy with some matter. During our conversation, a werewolf entered, holding several letters, seemingly urgent to hand them to Henry.

Although the werewolf didn’t dare to interrupt the conversation people in the room, I still sensitively noticed him.

I glanced at their disappearing figures from the corner of my eye and began to ponder silently.

Brand and I were not thrown back into the original wooden hut but were taken into a basement in this house!

This basement was very hidden, and the decoration could be considered comfortable and warm, definitely not like a normal place for imprisoning criminals.

A female Medical Practitioner treated the wound on my forehead again, and ice was applied to the swollen area of my face. Brand was pressed down on the sofa by the Medical Practitioner to clean his wounds, which looked very strange!

Did Henry suddenly come to his senses after being scolded by Brand, no longer planning to torture prisoners? I shook my head to dismiss this somewhat absurd thought, leaning on the sofa, feeling somewhat sleepy.

I don’t know how long it had been when the basement finally quieted down, Brand lay on the sofa and spoke in a steady voice, "Prince Henry is really very strange, don’t you think, Margaret?"

I was somewhat roused by this question, opened my eyes, and gently corrected, "He is no longer a prince of the Wolf royal family, don’t call him that, he doesn’t deserve it."

As I spoke, I didn’t plan to dwell on that issue. After thinking for a moment, I continued, "Who was that woman who talked to us first? She definitely has some kind of relationship with Henry."

Brand first nodded in agreement with my correction, then looked at me and asked, "Have you thought of something again? But I also feel that her tone towards Henry was very intimate, like a lover."

Upon hearing this, I shook my head in denial, "I don’t think she’s a lover, she seems more like a close elder. But besides Aunt Licia, does Henry have any female elders alive in this world?"

I mused to myself, a name suddenly flashed through my mind, but I quickly dismissed it. That woman had died many years ago in a rebellion, the information Donald gave me couldn’t be wrong, it couldn’t be her.

My thoughts were quickly disrupted by the sound of the door opening, a maid walked in carrying a tray of food. She placed the food on the table without saying a word, like a wooden doll stripped of emotions.

"Is there a toilet here?" I asked her, scrutinizing her closely.

The maid still did not speak, but she lifted her hand and pointed to a side wall of the basement, where there was a concealed door; I had noticed it when I first came in.

I pretended to be confused and looked in that direction, then continued to ask, "I don’t know how to open that door, can you help me? Also, if you don’t unlock our handcuffs, how are we supposed to eat?"

The maid seemed to be stumped by my questions, her expression faltered for a moment, then she got up and walked over to the secret door, pressing a concealed switch on the wall, revealing the bathroom inside.

chapters.title: Chapter 376 brings disgrace on oneself
chapters.content:
Chapter 376: 376 brings disgrace on oneself

[Margaret’s Perspective]

The maid rejected my request to unlock the handcuffs, and she didn’t move at all from outside the door even when I went to the bathroom.

Sitting on the toilet, I carefully observed the layout of the restroom and, finding no chance to escape, decisively chose to give up. It seemed that the only opportunity for me and Brand would be during outings.

After finishing in the bathroom, I turned the water at the sink to its maximum, using the noise to cover the conversation between the maid and me, "This isn’t the usual place where you detain captives, is it? Is the second floor where your master lives? Will she be back tonight? Why do some of you refer to her as ’Miss’ and others as ’Master’?"

While speaking, I turned to look at her with a smile on my face, "Don’t be so tense; I was just asking casually. If you think there’s something you can answer, then tell me. It’s fine if you don’t speak about the rest."

At that moment, the maid looked up at me. Still, she didn’t speak, but her eyes relaxed a bit defensively, "You must be talking about Miss Lillian. We are not accustomed to calling the master ’Miss’. If you’re finished here, please come out. The young master will come after you’ve had your meal."

I turned off the water at her words, but my eyes remained on her, "The young master you mentioned, isn’t it Henry? What is his relation to your master?"

My question went unanswered, and seeing this, I slowly nodded, understanding I would not get anything out of her.

Being handcuffed was quite inconvenient, so the meal with Brand took a long time, but the maid did not urge us at all. If it hadn’t been for the clarity of the situation just then, I almost would have felt like a guest invited by them, not at all like a captive.

After the meal, Brand and I did not meet MASTER, but unexpectedly, an uninvited guest showed up.

During the day today, I had felt that Lillian wouldn’t let me off easily, and sure enough, now she came to provoke me again!

"Henry really took great care of you. Look at the décor of this basement; if outsiders didn’t know, they might think you’re a VIP on Poison Snake Island!"

Upon entering, Lillian first looked around the basement, then said in a somewhat sarcastic tone.

Brand and I had no intention of responding to her, and suddenly the basement was incredibly silent.

Seeing this, Lillian’s expression darkened, apparently displeased by my disregard, "I forgot to ask you earlier, when did you and Henry become so close? I always feel he treats you quite unusually." Lillian’s tone became ambiguous as she looked down at me condescendingly, her gaze filled with disdain and provocation.

I looked up and met her eyes coldly, yet still did not speak.

"Thinking about it, you haven’t even been in Royal City for two months, right? When did you and Henry get to know each other so well? I didn’t think you were anything special before, but now..."

Lillian said, bending down, coming close and mockingly at me, she continued, "I think you have some ’methods’. Otherwise, you wouldn’t be manipulating Donald while flirting ambiguously with Henry, not even sparing such a teenager!"

The words from this woman’s mouth became increasingly unpleasant, so I straightened up slowly and stood, raising my eyebrow and speaking coldly, "No wonder you fit in with this crowd. It turns out you all have the same dirty tricks! Seeing any man, you probably subconsciously think he should like you, right?"

I said, stepping forward, noting the surprise on Lillian’s face, I scoffed and continued, "I don’t know why you and that woman harbor such strange ideas. I’ll just say this once, I’m not at all close to MASTER, nor do I have anything to do with what you’ve mentioned."

"Actually, I shouldn’t have to explain anything to you, but since you keep bringing it up again and again, it annoys me and makes me feel foolish, understood? I roughly know why you bring these things up in front of me; you’re still thinking about Donald and believe that sowing discord between him and me gives you an opportunity, right?"

Maybe my laugh was too ironic and provocative, causing Lillian’s face to start turning red, like someone had stepped on her tail.

I didn’t care about her mood at the moment and thought for a moment before continuing, "I can guess all the little schemes you have in mind, so don’t try to be clever. You can go now since you cannot win the argument with me; staying here any longer would just be humiliating yourself!"

"Margaret! You... as a lowly captive, what right do you have to speak to me like this? No one here can save you now. Do you think I would hesitate to kill you directly?"

Lillian continued, becoming somewhat uncontrollably emotional, with a reddish gleam in her eyes, and her demeanor became somewhat terrifying.

I did not respond to her bluff, just quietly maintained my gaze on her, warning her with my eyes: If you dared to do something, it wouldn’t have taken you this long.

chapters.title: Chapter 377, are you scared?
chapters.content:
Chapter 377: 377, are you scared?

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Because of the current tinderbox situation, that woman and Henry didn’t really make a move against me. How could she dare to touch me, being nothing more than a powerless figurehead?

Lillian was merely trying to show off her authority in front of me and then use her words to embarrass me. But with her poor articulation, she never had a chance to gain any advantage over me! She wasn’t even a match for Elizabeth when it came to verbal sparring and irritating others.

Brand had been nervous ever since Lillian entered the room, but seeing the current situation, he gradually relaxed, sensing that Lillian wouldn’t pose any threat to me!

Indeed, it had proven true. Lillian’s thoughts were still stuck on competing with me out of jealousy, but the reality of the situation wasn’t that simple. Donald never had eyes for her, and I never considered her a rival in love.

This ludicrous love triangle probably only existed in her naive imagination.

Having failed to find fault, Lillian was nearly enraged to death, but luckily, the next person to push the door open provided her an out, saving her the embarrassment of not being able to exit gracefully.

"Why are you here too? Who let you in? If you keep failing to control yourself, I wouldn’t mind having Austin lock you up directly!"

Following Henry was Alpha Levi of the Spirit Dragon Pack; these two coming to see me together probably wasn’t good news.

Thinking this silently, I no longer gave Lillian any attention and turned to resume my seat.

With her biggest support not present, Lillian didn’t dare confront Henry directly; she could only force herself to control her emotions, shooting me a fierce glare before leaving.

Henry glanced at her departing figure with the corner of his eye, then withdrew his gaze after a long moment, "What was her purpose in coming here?" Henry asked directly.

Facing the constant appearance of these people, I was somewhat speechless. I looked down at the silver handcuffs on my wrist and didn’t intend to talk with Henry.

But this madman couldn’t be avoided just by staying silent. He took a seat across from me, looked at me, and continued, "It’s okay if you don’t want to talk to me. Would you like to know the news about Donald, though?"

At his words, I paused my movements and looked up coldly at him, "What exactly do you want, get to the point and don’t waste time!"

Henry smiled and spoke, "As expected, only matters concerning Donald can catch your attention. I win again, do you admit defeat, Levi?"

His words were clearly not directed at me. I took a breath, my cool gaze flowing between him and Levi.

Levi seemed unwilling to speak with Henry either; hearing this, he only hummed with a laugh and looked down at me, speaking directly, "You must know about Donald’s plan to attack Poison Snake Island. What is his exact strategy? What secrets is he plotting with Gino? You all have been close with the Marine Supervision Bureau, certainly, you have other plans. You better tell me everything you know, or else..."

Levi paused, his held gun shifting to point directly at my head!

"Or else what? If you’re going to threaten someone, you might as well finish your line," I said, helping him complete it, though he narrowed his eyes coldly at me. I hooked a corner of my mouth, attempting to explain, "You ask things I’m not privy to. Donald rarely talks to me about his work, so I really don’t know."

"If you tone down that sarcasm in your eyes a bit, then maybe I’d find that believable, but now, do you think I would believe you?"

If the situation weren’t so unsuitable, I truly wanted to roll my eyes at Levi. He, like Henry, loved to talk nonsense.

Me: "..."

My silence made Levi a bit embarrassed; he took a deep breath and continued, "You fear nothing, but would you fear for this young man’s life because of you?"

As he spoke, he moved the gun to point at Brand nearby.

I stood up, looking at him with an increasingly frosty expression, but didn’t speak.

Brand, with the gun pointed at him, tensed up, his eyes darting briefly towards me, showing no plea for help but a flicker of resignation.

At that moment, I wasn’t catching onto Brand’s emotions; I was just staring at Levi, my mind racing for a solution.

"Dealing with Donald must be headache enough for you and Henry, right? Brand’s father is the director of the Marine Supervision Bureau, Berton. Are you sure you want to kill him now and directly provoke the entire Lilo Sea?" I said harshly, slowly moving to stand in front of Brand.

Levi wasn’t as tall as Donald, but I still had to tilt my head up to meet his cold, narrow eyes.

His gun was pressed against my chest, and though I was scared, I dared not gamble with Brand’s life! Levi, like Henry, was mad and obsessive.

The scene became a tense standoff. Neither I nor Levi backed down, while Henry watched leisurely from the sidelines.

"Margaret!"

Brand’s hoarse, tense voice rose behind me, dispersing some of the tension at the scene.

chapters.title: Chapter 378 Abdominal pain
chapters.content:
Chapter 378: 378 Abdominal pain

[Margaret’s Perspective]

At that moment, Henry put down the teacup he was holding and stood up to press down Levi’s gun-wielding hand. He spoke lazily, "I said this woman won’t budge, hard or soft. Now do you believe me?"

As he talked, he slightly squinted his eyes and glanced at Brand behind me, then laughed lightly and continued, "This boy can’t die now. Put away your gun, Levi!"

I didn’t know what was going through Henry’s mind, but I felt relieved when I heard him say those words.

Levi indeed feared Donald and Burton but I wasn’t sure he wouldn’t kill Brand in a fit of impulse, or shoot me!

Either of these two situations, should they occur, were something I couldn’t accept at this moment.

Levi’s hand slowly lowered, while my back broke into a cold sweat, and Brand even ungracefully collapsed on the sofa behind.

"Neither Margaret nor Brand can be touched; what is this, taking two hostages or welcoming two mighty deities? I can leave those two alone, but you need to come up with a way to deal with Donald, otherwise, we’re all doomed!" Levi, somewhat irritable, paced back and forth in the basement, seemingly helpless against Henry’s actions.

My clenched fist slowly relaxed, but just then, I felt a wave of pain in my abdomen. Suppressing my increasingly heavy breathing, I leaned on the back of the nearby sofa.

Brand was the first to notice my movement. He sat up straight and moved closer, his shoulder supporting my slightly tilted body. Worry and panic filled his downturned eyes.

"I didn’t ally with you just to watch you bring about your own doom. You’d better think of a solution to these problems fast, or don’t blame me for turning my back on you!"

As if wary of me being nearby, Levi didn’t disclose all the difficulties they were facing at the moment. Although nearly desperate, his mind was still crystal clear.

Henry remained silent as if he couldn’t feel Levi’s urgency.

Meanwhile, the pain in my abdomen became more pronounced. A cold sweat formed on my forehead. Taking a deep breath, I slowly sat down with the support of Brand’s shoulder, trying to appear natural.

But Henry was a man with incredibly keen senses. Almost instantly, he noticed something was amiss with me. Lifting his eyes, he looked straight at me, "What’s wrong with you? Why are you breathing so heavily?"

I turned my head, daring not to meet Henry’s gaze. Even forcing a cold laugh, I responded, "Nothing’s wrong. Can’t I just sit down and enjoy the show you two are putting on?"

"Really? But the expression on your face tells a different story," Henry leaned in, peering at my expression, his eyes darkening.

I somewhat hated that Levi didn’t continue speaking to distract Henry’s attention. Lifting my gaze, I breathed heavily, my face filled with defiance as I continued, "What does it have to do with you how I am feeling!"

Henry seemed momentarily speechless at my words, and then got up and came closer, his large hand seizing my chin, forcing me to look directly into his unshielded eyes.

"Let her go! She just ate too much and her stomach is unwell, she needs to rest! Let go of her!" Brand stepped forward, grasping Henry’s hand, and hurriedly offered an explanation.

I grabbed Henry’s wrist, my eyes revealing a bone-chilling coldness and a dangerous warning.

Perplexed by the scene, but seeing Brand dare to physically challenge Henry, Levi immediately stepped forward, using a few deft maneuvers to subdue the young man to the ground!

"Boy, you’ve got quite the nerve, even daring to touch the hands of this genius Medical Practitioner. Do you have a death wish?" Levi looked down at the youth he pinned to the ground, his mouth curling into a mocking smile.

At this point, I could not care about protecting Brand anymore. Pain wracked my belly so severely I nearly lost all my strength. I bit down on my teeth and glared at Henry in front of me, unwilling to plead with him but also too weak to struggle!

Henry’s gaze was complex as he looked at me. For a few moments, it was as if Donald was watching me, but, in brutal reality, Donald would never treat me so roughly; he was always a gentle and considerate husband.

Enduring the pain didn’t reduce it one bit; in fact, it seemed to be intensifying. A moment came when I couldn’t suppress a pained grunt, not very loud, but enough to spark surprise and astonishment in Henry’s eyes.

He seemed to act on instinct, easing his grip on me, and at that moment, I lost all support and collapsed onto the ground like a puddle of water!

I lowered my head, unwilling to let these people see me in my current state of weakness, my hands instinctively protecting my abdomen as I started to moan in pain quietly.

"What’s wrong with her? Did she really get sick from something she ate?"

"Damn it! Let me go! Margaret? You..."

Levi and Brand’s voices drifted in and out of my hearing. Biting down on my pale lips, a fierce sense of unease and fear surged through me!

Out of nowhere, Henry’s voice arose, his gaze heavy upon me, "This doesn’t look like any ordinary stomach ailment. Never mind the kid for now, go out and call the Medical Practitioner in for me!"

chapters.title: Chapter 379 Common Stomach Disease?
chapters.content:
Chapter 379: 379 Common Stomach Disease?

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"But this Brand guy..."

Levi, with one hand pressing Brand, who struggled aimlessly on the ground, started to say something, but the next second he was abruptly interrupted by Henry’s harsh voice!

"I said, leave him alone! Go quickly!"

Hearing this, Levi looked up at Henry. Despite his deep dissatisfaction with Henry ordering him around, seeing me in severe pain, sweating profusely, and curled up, he held back his anger and stood up to head toward the door.

With no one holding him back, Brand quickly got up, approached me with a worried tone, and asked, "How are you, Margaret? You... "

"Get lost! Stay away from her!" Henry glanced sideways at Brand and commanded in a grim voice.
