I lacked the energy to respond to Brand; all my strength was consumed by enduring the severe pain in my lower abdomen.

I bit my lip to keep from crying out in pain, but I didn’t realize that such an act was useless, for anyone could see how much pain I was in from the cold sweat on my face and my pained expression!

At this point, Brand dared not say much more, as he could see I urgently needed the Medical Practitioner. As for whether the Medical Practitioner would discover my secret, he could not worry about that now.

It had only been a few minutes, but it felt like several centuries to me.

The Medical Practitioner, brought in by Levi, nodded slightly to Henry and then knelt down to carefully analyze my condition.

After a moment, the Medical Practitioner looked up at Henry and bluntly said, "It looks like an acute stomach ailment. I recommend taking the patient to the hospital for treatment, otherwise she will continue to suffer like this!"

"Can’t you treat her here? Does it have to be the hospital?" Henry asked the Medical Practitioner with narrowed eyes, his tone oppressively inquisitive.

The Medical Practitioner bowed his head affirmatively, "Yes, young master, this kind of ailment can be minimally or highly dangerous; it’s urgent. I suggest you make a decision immediately!"

After the Medical Practitioner had spoken, Brand cast a deep glance at him from the corner of his eye before finally turning back to look at the silent Henry.

"You must have known Margaret for a long time? You should know what kind of person she is. If she wasn’t truly unable to bear it, she wouldn’t show weakness like this! Please, help her, I beg you!"

Brand spoke earnestly, his gaze filled with pleading as he looked at Henry.

Henry, who had been ignoring Brand from the start, snorted softly in response and then immediately bent down to lift me from the ground, his voice cold as he told the Medical Practitioner beside him, "You’d better pray that your judgment is correct, or else you know what comes next."

The Medical Practitioner, hearing this, quickly nodded in respect and hurried to match Henry’s swift stride as they left.

Seeing this, Brand’s spirits lifted. He was just about to get up and follow but was stopped by Levi, who was beside him, "Do you think this is your backyard where you can come and go as you like? Stay put and don’t move, or I’ll kill you!"

After speaking, Levi also turned and left, leaving Brand alone in the basement, standing there seemingly lost in thought.

Later, the hospital’s Medical Practitioner told me that when Henry carried me out of the basement, I had passed out from the pain!

At that time, I just listened quietly without saying much, thinking disdainfully in my heart: If I had been conscious, I wouldn’t have let Henry come even a little close to me!

The hospital on Poison Snake Island is not very big; it’s rather like a well-secured courtyard than a hospital.

When Henry got out of the car, he held me in his arms again, with Levi following quietly behind until the Medical Practitioner brought out a crash cart, then Henry finally let go and placed me on it.

"She is a very important captive; watch her closely! If she escapes, everyone in this yard will be buried with her," Henry stated, looking at the Medical Practitioner who had made the suggestion, his eyes cold and frightening.

The Medical Practitioner swallowed hard at these words and nodded, uttering a ’yes’, before he and the nurse quickly wheeled me away!

"What in the world is going on here? Didn’t your people just take her away? How did it come to this in such a short time?"

Levi’s voice, filled with confusion, sounded as he stood by Henry, his gaze towards the hospital doors.

Henry slightly exhaled and, after a pause of 2 seconds, turned to face Levi and explained, "It wasn’t my people who brought her here; I guess it was Lillian, though she must have been used by someone else too."

Henry’s eyes dimmed as he spoke, and after pondering, he continued, "Did Margaret get hurt when she came over from the sea? Don’t you think this stomach disease struck very suddenly?"

"Didn’t you just say it didn’t seem like an ordinary stomach problem? I thought you already had a diagnosis; didn’t you?"

chapters.title: Chapter 380 Medical Practitioner Simo
chapters.content:
Chapter 380: 380 Medical Practitioner Simo

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Levi retorted somewhat oddly as he looked at Henry and frowned, then added, "If you don’t trust the Medical Practitioner, I can go get her help. The healer in her hands is definitely better than ours."

Upon hearing this, Henry slowly shook his head and, with a grave voice, turned to Levi, saying, "No! Have you forgotten whose territory was the scene of Margaret’s incident? When we entered, the maid must have just provided them with food."

"What do you mean by that? Do you suspect her? Why?" Levi’s confusion deepened as he looked at Henry as if he were some kind of monster.

Answering Levi was only silence; Henry did not offer any further explanation.

Seeing this, Levi let out a somewhat sarcastic laugh, then crossed his arms and said with a smile, "So now, besides yourself, do you trust no one else? Then why did you accept her invitation and come to this island? What is there on this broken island that could possibly attract you?"

Sometimes Levi really was a bit crazy, but Henry seemed to have gotten used to it. He didn’t take it as sarcasm and was silent, seemingly lost in thought.

"I really don’t get you two. So what do you say we should do now? If we keep being indifferent, Donald will definitely hit us where it hurts!"

On hearing the name Donald, Henry seemed to snap back to reality. He looked up at Levi and said in a low voice, "Bring Brand to the hall, it’s time to make use of him."

At these words, Levi looked at Henry for a long while before he sighed and nodded in agreement.

And all this secret conversation between the two of them was something I, in my comatose state, knew nothing about. By the time I opened my eyes again, it was already midnight.

Although this room wasn’t as comfortable and warm as the basement, it still made me let out a sigh of relief. I tried to sit up, supporting my body with my hands, but in the next moment, the needle on the back of my hand reminded me of its existence!

"Ah!" I groaned in pain, instinctively glancing over, as blood had already begun to flow there.

I withdrew my gaze without much care and began to look around this new environment. Brand wasn’t by my side, and I had no idea whether he was still in that basement.

My mind started to wander aimlessly, but in the next second, I realized a far more important problem!

I lifted the quilt to glance at my lower abdomen and felt that, aside from a bit of fatigue and weakness, there was nothing else unusual. The severe pain that had afflicted me in the basement felt like a bad dream!

’Beep beep beep!’

The life monitor on the bedside table suddenly started to sound. I was startled, my body tensing as I turned to look at it. Realizing that it was my sudden movement that had caused an abnormal contact and set off the alarm, I let out a sigh of relief, tore off the items clamped on my hand, and pulled out the bloody needle.

Barefooted, I stepped on the floor, cautiously moving towards the door of the room.

There was no sound outside the door. After thinking for a moment, I reached for the doorknob, just about to walk out, when suddenly, some footsteps approached the door. My heart skipped a beat, and I quickly let go of the handle and hid behind the door!

"Dr. Simo, why hasn’t this woman woken up yet? We have to stay here in the hospital till the small hours because of her, and I haven’t slept for 2 days! If it weren’t for her, I’d be lying in my own bed resting beautifully now!"

"I can watch over her by myself for the rest of the night. You go back to sleep with the others! Look at those dark circles under your eyes; they are almost covering your whole face!"

"Ah? Really? Well then... I’ll go back first. Be careful by yourself! Notify us immediately if anything happens!"

"All right, off you go!"

The conversation outside the door came to an abrupt halt. I heard the sound of the nurse turning and walking away, and the next moment, the doorknob was turned open directly!

I held my breath, attempting to perform the shift to summon Betty, but bizarrely it did not succeed. I had no time to ponder why, as the moment the doctor came into the room and turned to close the door, I stepped forward swiftly, my arm wrapping around the person’s neck, my fingers locking directly onto their throat!

"If you don’t want to die, stay quiet! I can crush your throat in the blink of an eye; you’d best not try to provoke me!" I said coldly, my gaze fixed intently on the person in front of me.

Taken aback by my sudden awakening, the Medical Practitioner named Simo was startled at first, then stiffly replied in a hoarse voice, "Perhaps you should put on some shoes first. You’re in the early stages of pregnancy, and the condition of the child is not very stable. You really shouldn’t get sick at this stage!"

At his words, a chill flashed in my eyes as I tightened my grip on Simo’s neck and asked sharply, "So Henry and the rest know my situation, is that it? If that’s the case, don’t blame me!"

The situation I’m facing is the last thing I want to see. If Henry and Levi know about my pregnancy, then the predicament for me and my baby becomes even more dangerous, while Donald also becomes more passive!

chapters.title: Chapter 381 Bring her to see me
chapters.content:
Chapter 381: 381 Bring her to see me

[Margaret’s perspective]

There shouldn’t be more than three guards outside the door. After killing this doctor and facing them, I would not be completely without a chance of winning.

The thought flashed through my mind, and just as I was about to forcefully snap Simo’s neck, he suddenly spoke up to stop me, "No, no, no! Wait a second! I haven’t told them about your pregnancy, they don’t know! Can you wait and listen to me first? I’m not one of Henry and Levi’s men, I’m here to help you, Lycan Queen!"

Upon hearing this, I stopped what I was doing and took a shallow breath, looking at Simo’s profile and saying coolly, "Just because you say you’re not, I should believe you? How do I know you aren’t just trying to save your own skin by lying to me?"

"It’s true! I’m not deceiving you. I told them you were just having an acute onset of a stomach issue and needed to stay in the hospital for quiet recuperation, that’s all. I didn’t mention your pregnancy! You see, you’re still safe and sound, doesn’t that prove I’m not lying! Can you let me go back to the bed first, and then I will explain slowly."

Simo babbled on, but I kept still, controlling him, my aura frighteningly cold.

Seeing that I remained silent and did not seem inclined to release him, Simo bit his teeth and said directly, "Actually, I’m one of the Empress Dowager’s men. Just now, by a fluke, Levi called me down to the basement. I had seen your photo before, so I recognize you. I won’t tell anyone else about your pregnancy, you can rest assured!"

Simo claimed to be Licia’s man, which surprised me!

After thinking about it, I let go of him. When he turned his face to look at me, I stared at him icily, "You are Licia’s man? Why would her people be on Poison Snake Island? Where is she now? Have you already told her about my situation?"

With his neck already turning blue and purple, Simo looked at me somewhat shrunkenly and explained, "Yes, Your Highness. As for why I am here... that’s a bit complicated. I’ll explain it to you after we get out of here, is that okay?"

I continued to look at him silently, and after pondering for a moment he added, "The Empress Dowager is staying in the western residence. I have reported your situation to her. She just instructed me to keep it secret and to take good care of you."

"Anything else? Didn’t she say anything else?" I asked in return, not really planning to trust this Simo.

I didn’t even trust Licia, let alone this Medical Practitioner of unclear origins.

Caught off guard, Simo seemed not to understand what I wanted to know, and he hesitantly lowered his head and stammered, "Empress Dowager... What exactly do you wish to know, Lycan Queen?"

Seeing this, I gave him a look before turning back to the bedside to put on my shoes, "Figure out a way to get me out of here. I want to see Licia in person; I have some matters I want to ask her myself!"

Simo stopped rubbing his neck, his eyes lowered as if he was pondering something, and after a long pause, he replied, "Henry’s men have turned this hospital into an impregnable fortress. If you want to get out of here, you need his permission, so it’s absolutely impossible to take you out in secret!"

He took a deep breath and continued, "I can relay your words to the Empress Dowager, and if she’s willing, I can bring her in to see you! But before that, you cannot leave here on your own, and you need to cooperate with the ’treatment’ I’m providing."

Throughout Simo’s speech, I just watched him coldly and said nothing in response.

Seeing my reaction, Simo became a bit anxious. He lowered his voice and continued, "I know you are reluctant to trust me now, but by no later than noon tomorrow, I can bring back the Empress Dowager’s response to you! They are watching you too closely; this is the only way I have!"

I slowly sat back on the hospital bed, emotionless, but inside, I began to plot rapidly!

I couldn’t verify the truthfulness of Simo’s words right now, but I also couldn’t proceed with my earlier plan—kill him and then break out directly.

If everything Simo said was true, and Licia ordered him to stay here to cover up the fact of my pregnancy and to take care of me, it would mean that Licia and I had consistent intentions regarding the protection of the child!

Licia’s status on the island was certainly higher than mine. I might be able to use her to escape or to pass some message to Donald.

Maybe my contemplation was taking too long because Simo’s worry and impatience were almost spilling out. I looked up at him indifferently and stated flatly, "If she does not come herself, then don’t bother to bring me any response. Whether I live or die has nothing to do with her; let her pray that Henry is defeated later rather than sooner so she has more time to think about how to face Donald!"

"Convey my words precisely to Licia. I’ll wait for her until 10 a.m. tomorrow. If she doesn’t come, I’ll take it as her truly giving up on Donald this time!"

After saying this, I waved Simo away, indicating he could leave if he wanted.

Seeing my resolute attitude, Simo dared not say more; he put down the utensils in his hands and turned to leave.

chapters.title: Chapter 382 ’Accident
chapters.content:
Chapter 382: 382 ’Accident

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I glanced at the medicine on the plate, and as expected, they were all stomach-nourishing and pain-relieving drugs. Of course, the one with no label must be the real medication he meant to use for my recuperation!

I quickly withdrew my gaze without reaching for them. I don’t trust Simo, so naturally, I wouldn’t use the medicines he brought.

The lights in the sickroom were very bright. I pulled the covers back over myself and listened intently to the conversation between Simo and the guard at the door.

Touching my belly, I murmured in my heart, "We will be fine. The Moon goddess will surely protect us!"

[Henry’s Perspective]

After the Medical Practitioner reported Margaret’s condition to me, I felt a slight sense of relief!

I even felt some gratitude that things hadn’t turned out as I had imagined, perhaps Margaret and Donald were never meant to be together.

Yesterday, I had already met Aunt Licia on the island. She looked haggard, no doubt suffering greatly for betraying Donald for Martha and me. However, it was inevitable. A battle between me and Donald was bound to happen, just like Dan and Alex many years ago!

I didn’t actually want to become the so-called Lycan king, but if Donald were to sit on that throne, that would be unacceptable.

The battle in the Royal City was something I had prepared for many years. In fact, if everything had gone smoothly, Donald should never have returned to the Royal City. He was meant to die in the attack by the Red Sun and Starry Sky pack, or in the subsequent battle at the Misty Forest!

Time and again he’d turned danger into safety, even slowly tracing the plots back to me, which forced me to admit that he’s a very clever man. I had no choice but to fabricate the incident of Austin’s poisoned suicide to divert his and the other Lycans’ attention.

As it turned out, I was actually successful. If it weren’t for Margaret, an ’accident’ I hadn’t anticipated!

The first time I met her, I was surprised at how devotedly she protected Donald, this woman was indeed different. She was sensitive and smart. Even when Donald hadn’t begun to suspect me, she always looked at me with a certain judgmental and defensive gaze, as if she had foreseen that I would do something to betray Donald.

Of course, I have never done anything to betray Donald. On the contrary, it’s he and the entire Wolf royal family who have wronged me!

I should have had a happy complete family, a healthy body, and a perfect life. But because of those hypocritical and greedy people, everything was destroyed! They should compensate me, with the lives of all of them.

What I didn’t expect was that Donald’s training expedition would allow him to grow so quickly and even unexpectedly find love, securing a gentle woman like Margaret.

On the road fleeing from the defeated Royal City, I began to notice my concern for Margaret. It was a very strange feeling, unheard of in my previous life!

They say this special concern is called liking. I’m a bit confused. I think it’s because of Donald—I want to take everything he has, to make him experience the agony of losing everything, just like his father did to me before.

That’s what I thought before, but this idea changed just now.

Margaret collapsed in front of me, vulnerable and helpless, and I was the one who could completely control her. Her life and soul seemed to belong to me!

This feeling was very nice. My heart was telling me that it would be even better if she could always stay by my side, just like how she obediently, motionlessly remained in my arms just now.

Thinking this way, my lips slowly curved into a smile, to the point where Aiden next to me found it strange.

"What’s the good idea that’s got you so happy?" he asked.

In response, I toned down the warmth on my face and looked at him with a counter-question, "How is the hospital doing? Haven’t there been any news about Margaret waking up?"

At this, Aiden bowed his head and respectfully responded, "No, Master, no news has come through!"

I nodded to show I understood, tossed aside the document in my hand, leaned back on the sofa, and continued to inquire, "Any new developments outside the island? Donald and Berton must be growing impatient. Tell everyone on the island to stay alert. Inform me at the first sign of any disturbances."

"And those mercenaries on the east side of the island, pass down my death order. Whoever dares to cause trouble during this time—don’t blame me for being unkind!"

Aiden seemed startled by the way I spoke and hesitated before hurriedly replying, "Yes, Master! But those mercenaries only follow the lady’s orders. Should I..."

Aiden stopped, his eyes searching mine with inquiry and caution.

I knew what he was thinking and sat up straight to look at him, my tone growing colder, "You are my man. You don’t want to follow my orders, then whose do you wish to follow?"

No sooner had my words fallen than Aiden kneeled on the ground with a slap, his head bowed in the attitude of one who had committed a grave error, "I have erred, Master! Please punish me."

chapters.title: Chapter 383 Attacked
chapters.content:
Chapter 383: 383 Attacked

[Henry’s Perspective]

Aiden was terrified and knelt on the ground, begging for my forgiveness.

I looked at him, feeling annoyed, and suddenly kicked the coffee table in front of me away, rubbing the throbbing pain at my brow as I said coldly, "Get out!"

Hearing this, Aiden hastily responded with several ’yeses’ before scurrying out.

I found myself missing Angel, who had great rapport with me. She was a smart and powerful Lycan. If only she hadn’t been so fond of Donald, acting impulsively and falling into the trap set by Donald and Margaret, the one sitting in Levi’s place wouldn’t be Levi!

Levi was not someone I could control absolutely. His stubbornness and madness sometimes even resembled mine. Martha said this might be something inherent in the Wolf royal family’s bloodline. I didn’t quite agree with this, as I and Donald were essentially different, and as for the Wolf royal family...

I felt even more disdainful inside. After all, it was nothing more than a despicable and hypocritical family, with no real claim to stand high and mighty!

I reached for the wine glass on the coffee table, took a small sip, and gathered my scattered thoughts. Margaret had been ’missing’ for nearly two days now, and Donald must be frantic. It was about time for me to formally negotiate with him.

As I thought this, a strange feeling suddenly surged within me!

Trading Margaret away to get what I’d always wanted... was that really what I wanted to do now?

This question took root deep within my heart, and I found myself hesitating for a moment! This was so unlike the usual me.

I threw back the rest of the wine in my glass, stood up, and walked out. The midnight on Poison Snake Island was not quiet. Beyond a wall, I heard the calls of birds and animals.

At first, I didn’t notice anything amiss, but as I put down my glass and stood by the window, a sense of crisis suddenly rose within me!

[Master Henry! The patrol team on the west side of the island has been attacked, and we are on our way to the scene with reinforcements. Do you want to come over and take command?]

It was the leader of the Guard Army, reporting this sudden situation to me through mind-link.

My expression turned cold, and a dangerous glint appeared in my eyes.

[I’ll be there right away, make sure to search thoroughly.] After giving this order, I turned and picked up the firearms on my desk, opened the door, and left briskly with my guards.

There were no ferries on the west side of Poison Snake Island to get ashore, so these sudden attackers must have crossed over by relying on their extraordinary physical strength and swimming skills!

After getting this information from the head of the Guard Team, my heart sank, and the night partially hid the fierce light in my eyes. "Does nobody know when they landed on the island? What is the Guard Team there for, just decoration?"

My two rhetorical questions received no answer, as the leader had already been frightened into a stunned silence by me.

Knowing I wouldn’t get any more useful information from him, I turned away from the cliff and walked straight to a residence halfway up the hill not far away.

Licia, with a cloak draped over her, walked out of the bedroom, clearly annoyed at being disrupted from her sleep.

She adjusted her cloak and looked at me with surprise in her eyes. "Henry? What brings you here so late? Is there an emergency?"

As Licia spoke, she glanced unconsciously at the clock in the corner, and after a moment, her expression changed as she continued, "Is it something with Margaret? Has she had an accident?"

I stared at Licia in front of me for several seconds before shaking my head and saying, "She’s fine, just hasn’t woken up yet."

Licia seemed to breathe a sigh of relief upon hearing this and walked over to the sofa area.

She gestured for me to sit as well and said softly, "That’s good to hear. But you wouldn’t come here so late for no reason, would you? Do you need my help?"

"Auntie, you seem very concerned about Margaret, huh?" I asked in return, not directly answering her, and took a seat opposite Licia.

I wasn’t in a hurry to search the house anymore; Licia’s reaction was obviously more interesting to me.

"Why do you suddenly ask that?" Licia seemed puzzled, looking at me and asking.

Her eyes were clear and honest as if she had no guard against me at all. I chuckled at that and explained, "Just curious why you’re so concerned about her, that’s all. No other reason."

Licia nodded, apparently believing my explanation. "It’s not concern, just a passing question. You wouldn’t have come this late just to have a heart-to-heart with me, would you? So, what’s going on?"

As she spoke, she gestured for a maid nearby, who stepped forward and poured water for both of us.

I looked at the water glass pushed towards me by Licia, replied with a smile, "Nothing major, just that the patrol team by the precipice has been killed, and since you’re close by, I thought I’d check on your safety. Did you hear anything strange earlier?"

I said this while glancing sideways at the few guards behind Licia, my lips carrying a smile, but my eyes filled with scrutiny and inquiry.

"What? You’re saying someone sneaked onto the island? Who could it be? Martha won’t be in any danger, will she? She hasn’t been with me since this afternoon."

Licia stood up in shock, saying this as she directed the maid to get the phone, seemingly about to call to ensure Martha’s safety at that moment.

chapters.title: Chapter 384 Search
chapters.content:
Chapter 384: 384 Search

[Henry’s Perspective]

I stood up to stop her movement and spoke in a calm voice to explain, "It happened 15 minutes ago. After killing those on patrol, the attacker hid. Martha is on the east side of the island today, with many mercenaries around her. She’ll be all right!"

Upon hearing this, Licia sighed in relief, looked up at me, and said softly, "As long as your mom is fine, I’m relieved. Speaking of which, since you came to the island I haven’t heard you call Martha ’mom.’ You..."

She suddenly paused, seemingly hesitant about what she was about to say next.

She pursed her lips, her warm palm supporting my arm, and continued, "Haven’t you had a proper talk with her about the past? When she left you behind, it was out of desperation. You must understand her, right?"

"Your mother... she was running around with your father’s body, and she nearly died in the Demon Domain Forest several times! She mentioned those experiences to me a few days ago, and I wished I could have borne them for her! Of course, the events of those days are too complex to blame anyone for. I don’t want to see her dwelling in a river of hatred, and neither do I want to see you step by step down that path of no return!"

"Henry, do you understand what your aunt is saying? The situation is not beyond redemption. If you want, I can..."

"Enough, Aunt Licia! Please stop with this meaningless talk, okay?" My patience, seldom exhausted, finally wore thin as I coldly interrupted Licia’s lengthy speech.

I narrowed my eyes at Licia and continued in an icy tone, "You’re not me, nor are you Martha, so you can’t understand the depth of this hatred. Stop pretending to enlighten everyone from on high, alright? Even the Moon goddess doesn’t demand her people to be ’generous’ and kind!"

I finished speaking and took a slight breath, shifting my gaze toward the bedroom behind Licia.

"I know Aunt Licia has made many sacrifices for me and Martha, and that being caught between us and Donald must have been incredibly painful. But stabbing Donald in the back was your choice, and nobody but you can pay that price."

"Martha and I can accept you living here because we are rational and kind enough not to conflate you with Donald! So, please don’t get any greedier, okay?"

I signaled the subordinates behind me with a gesture, indicating they should start searching the entire house.

During my speech, Licia had been hanging her head, her expression filled with sorrow and pain. Watching the soldiers rush into her bedroom, she slowly lifted her eyes to me and asked hoarsely, "What are you doing?"

I did not respond to the disbelief and sadness in her eyes, and instead said firmly, "Since Aunt has declared there is nothing abnormal here, you shouldn’t oppose my routine search, should you?"

"Henry! You..."

I glanced sideways at her, my gaze not particularly soft, "I’m also doing this for Aunt’s safety. Please don’t overthink it."

Tears welled up in Licia’s eyes as she looked at me and managed a bitter smile, releasing the hand that held mine, and said faintly, "Sometimes I too find myself naive, to foolishly dream that I could single-handedly resolve the deadly feud between Alex and you and your mother!"

"Do you know, Henry, how happy I was when I found out Martha was still alive? She is my only sister, and you are my beloved nephew! You and I... we are family..."

"So what? Blood relatives separated by a distance can never compare to one’s own flesh and blood!" I said to Licia with cruel bluntness.

Something shattered within Licia at that moment, and her tears started to fall.

After a moment, she let out a bitter laugh and said, "In my eyes, you have always been as important as Donald. Because of my debt to Martha, many times I cared for you even more than Donald..."

"So you’ve always thought of me that way. When you saw me secretly helping you against Donald, did you think I was like a poor actor? Stupid and ridiculous?"

By the time Licia finished speaking, she was already weeping terribly, and if you added her self-mocking smile, she looked utterly pitiful and laughable.

Seeing this, I suddenly let out a sneer, feeling an unexplainable pang of pity, but my words spared her no mercy, "You’re just now realizing this? Although it’s a bit late, I still congratulate you. It’s much better to wake up now than to continue deluding yourself. Since we’ve talked so much today, I might as well say a few more words."

I checked my sneer and turned away, adding, "Donald and I are in an irreconcilable struggle, and you should have been aware of this all along. So stop trying to change anything. I can arrange for someone to take you off the island if you want to leave before my final battle with Donald, but there won’t be such a chance later. As your ’nephew,’ that’s the last bit of courtesy I’m willing to extend to you."

After saying this, I didn’t wait for Licia’s reaction and walked away from the house.

A few minutes later, the soldiers returned from their search and reported to me respectfully, "No suspicious areas found, Sir. The attacker hasn’t been here!"

After their report, I slowly furrowed my brow, somewhat surprised by this outcome.

chapters.title: Chapter 385 Difficult
chapters.content:
Chapter 385: 385 Difficult

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I didn’t sleep at all for the rest of the night, my mind kept going over the conversation I had with Simo earlier.

To be honest, I’m not sure if Simo would convey my message to Licia, or if Licia would come to see me after hearing my words. My current situation is just too passive!

Henry’s people really have surrounded this hospital like a fortress; that’s what I discovered when I attempted to go out in the morning but was directly stopped by the guards.

The Medical Practitioner who came in to check on me just now wasn’t Simo. Of course, I wasn’t foolish enough to ask this Medical Practitioner about Simo’s whereabouts. I just silently watched him, and when he asked me to take the medicine, I waved his tray away with a flourish of my hand.

"You..." It seemed he hadn’t expected me to physically react. The expression on the Medical Practitioner’s face turned cold, and he opened his mouth as if to scold me, but then he suddenly remembered something, and the curse that was in his throat got stuck.

I raised my eyes and looked at him coldly, speaking in a deep voice, "Get out!"

The commotion in the room quickly attracted the guards at the door. They pushed open the door, stuck their heads in, glanced first at the mess on the ground, then raised their heads to look at me.

"She must take her medicine today. Someone come in and hold her down so that I can work better!" the Medical Practitioner said through gritted teeth.

I looked around at them with a cold gaze and scoffed, "I’d like to see who dares to touch me."

The guards at the door glanced at the Medical Practitioner, who was grinding his teeth, and then suggested in a stern voice, "The young master has ordered us to take good care of this prisoner; we can’t lay hands on her. I suggest you be more courteous toward her too!"

"You weren’t on duty yesterday, so you might not know about this, but she was personally brought here by the young master and Mr. Levi. Think about it for yourself—it’s best not to offend the young master over some trivial matter!"

The guard seemed to know the Medical Practitioner and gave a couple of well-intentioned reminders before closing the door of the ward.

I frowned and listened to their conversation, filled with disdain and impatience.

After hearing what was said, the Medical Practitioner seemed to calm down a bit. When he turned his head to look at me again, his tone was much more temperate. "I’m just doing my routine duty. Your diagnosis and treatment plan were all decided by Simo. If you have any complaints, you should take it up with him instead of making things difficult for me like this!"

"Fine, where is this Simo?" I asked with an apparent go-with-the-flow attitude, my face full of feigned arrogance.

He took a deep breath, then explained, "I’m on duty this morning; Simo won’t come until noon. I don’t know who you are, and I’m not interested in learning about your special relationship with the young master. All I know is that here, you are a patient, and I am the doctor. Please respect my job."

"Many people die every day on Poison Snake Island. If you don’t cherish your own life, you might as well continue to court death. After all, it’s your own body!"

The Medical Practitioner said this and bent down to pick up the items on the floor. Of course, I wasn’t deliberately making things difficult for him; it was just that things in the middle weren’t easy to explain to others, nor could they be clarified.

However, my earlier ’acting’ wasn’t without gains; at the very least, I now knew that Simo would come in the afternoon and also picked up other hidden messages in the Medical Practitioner’s words.

Such as "Many people die every day on Poison Snake Island!"

"Ha! I thought Poison Snake Island was something remarkable, but it turns out to be nothing special! With such natural advantages in terrain, it’s probably being eroded into a sieve by outsiders, right? I’m really curious how many people died here last night; after all, the commotion next door didn’t stop at all!"

As I spoke, I sneered, my gaze towards the Medical Practitioner was full of provocation, while the deeper probing was perfectly concealed.

"How do you know there was an attack on the island last night? Did Simo tell you?"

As he spoke, it was as if he had realized something. He laughed and then said with understanding, "Right, Simo used to be transferred from the lady on the east side of the island, so it’s not surprising he knows about this! But don’t think those people came to save you; the young master has already issued a Level 10 alert across the island. The perimeter of the hospital is even more secure; there’s a blockade line every 10 meters, not even a small bird can get in, let alone an invader!"

I certainly wanted to get some new information, but seeing the annoyingly smug look on the Medical Practitioner’s face, I was indeed quite speechless and irritated.

I silently leaned back onto the hospital bed, my mocking smile the only response I gave him.

Seeing that I had finally quieted down, the Medical Practitioner thought his words had scared me. He sneered and left with the medicine tray.

I watched his retreating figure and kicked off the covers, jumping out of bed and tiptoeing to peek out the window.

Just as the Medical Practitioner had said, in addition to the guards I had seen in the open before, there were now a lot more patrolling soldiers and watchtowers outside.

It seemed that Poison Snake Island had indeed been restless last night. Did he mean Licia when he mentioned the lady on the east side of the island? Who could the people coming to the island be? If they were Donald’s men, what should I do to make them notice where I am?

A myriad of questions flashed through my mind in an instant, but no definite answers were forthcoming. I crinkled my brow in frustration, forcing myself to calm down!

chapters.title: Chapter 386 "Please step into the urn
chapters.content:
Chapter 386: 386 "Please step into the urn

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Thankfully, the sudden knocking at the door didn’t let my restlessness linger too long. I turned to look and to my surprise, it was Levi who had come.

He was alone, with no Henry following behind him.

I raised my eyebrows slightly, watching him step by step as he walked in, "Is everyone on your island this idle? The first thing in the morning is to come and watch the captive?"

Levi seemed indifferent to my sarcasm. He picked a spot to sit down on his own, and only when he looked up at me again did he speak, "I didn’t sleep all night because Licia’s yard was breached!"

I didn’t expect Levi to be so direct in telling me the news, so I looked at him silently, not joining the conversation.

Seeing my lack of reaction, Levi sighed and continued, "Though the patrol team discovered the enemy’s attack immediately, and Henry ordered a thorough search of the island right away, they just couldn’t find where the person was hiding! They can’t turn into plants or trees, nor can they vanish into thin air, so why can’t they be found?"

I didn’t think Levi had come to complain to me. Given he’d said so much to himself, there must be something waiting for me next.

Thinking this, I still looked at him with cold eyes, "What exactly are you trying to say?"

Levi’s eyes flashed as I spoke to him, then he continued, more cheerfully, "Henry is afraid they’ll discover your location, so he’s had the hospital surrounded inside and out. I’m curious when they’ll expose themselves to rescue you, so I came over early to keep watch!"

I was utterly speechless at his words, huffing back at him, "What a nice ’invitation into the urn’ you’ve set up. It seems you can’t wait for Donald to realize I’m here, and then ambush him with elite troops and well-designed traps, waiting for him to fall into the net!"

Levi raised an eyebrow at me and remained noncommittal.

I took a deep breath and continued, "Donald isn’t as foolish as you think. He won’t miss such an obvious trap. You won’t succeed!"

"That’s not necessarily true," Levi immediately countered, his eyes filled with cynicism and mockery, "Henry said Donald would rather die in your place, if he knows you’re here, how can he bear not to rescue you? The battle between us is inevitable; it can’t be avoided."

After murmuring that last sentence, I looked at him, feeling somewhat baffled, "You seem pretty confident that you can defeat Donald. I’m rather curious where such confidence comes from. Is it all because of the elusive Poison Snake Island Master?"

"Do you even realize what kind of army you’re up against? Donald would mobilize the entire strength of the Wolf Royal Family to eradicate Henry. Ordinary irregular soldiers or mercenaries stand no chance against a large-scale assault. It’s only a matter of time before this island is flattened. If you’re thinking of using me to negotiate any terms with Donald, I’d advise you to abandon that hope. I would rather die than be a burden to Donald!"

The half-truths I was trying to use to brainwash Levi—even if they couldn’t undermine his resolve to face Donald head-on, I still hoped to plant a seed of doubt and insecurity in his mind.

Levi was quiet as I finished speaking, his eyes intensely focused on me.

After a moment, he scoffed in mockery, as if he found my expression quite amusing, "Has Henry ever mentioned that there’s a special kind of cuteness about you? It’s an innocence that no one has the heart to shatter. Are you trying to convince me to turn over a new leaf? Hahaha!"

As Levi spoke, he suddenly stopped laughing and said coldly to me, "It’s too late now, there’s no going back. Donald won’t forgive those who have betrayed him, and neither can I."

Neither Donald nor I had delved into the fundamental reason for Levi’s alliance with Henry. He was like a particularly prickly conundrum; anyone who even thought of getting a closer look would find themselves viciously pricked until bleeding from the face!

At this point, I couldn’t comprehend the significance of Levi’s words. I frowned at him, finding this man’s madness somewhat terrifying, his thoughts incomprehensible to a normal person.

"The duty doctor told me you smashed all the medicine given to you. Why? Don’t you remember how you were rolling in pain on the ground yesterday?"

Levi’s change in topic surprised me. I frowned and locked eyes with him, not responding.

But given his bizarre way of thinking, it seemed he didn’t need me to speak anyway, "Henry was genuinely distraught over you. I had never seen him look so worried before—it was really something! If he had said you were his lover, I might have believed it at the time..."

"Shut your mouth!" I couldn’t stand his madman-like babbling any longer and sharply interrupted him.

Levi mockingly closed his mouth and looked at me with amusement in his eyes.

"Get out!" I continued.

Levi casually crossed his legs with a provocative posture, looking at me, motionless.

After taking several deep breaths, unable to contain my urge, I turned my head away, fearing I’d lose control and strike him the next second!

chapters.title: Chapter 387 Injured
chapters.content:
Chapter 387: 387 Injured

[Margaret’s Perspective]

"Why are you so angry? Is it really that hard to accept that Henry has feelings for you? You shot him so many times at Sant Angelo and he didn’t hold it against you. After coming to the island, he couldn’t bear to see you suffer the slightest hardship. I heard that when Lillian’s people took you away, he rushed to rescue you without a second thought. Yesterday, he was so anxious he almost killed all the Medical Practitioners in the entire hospital! If that’s not liking someone, then what is?"

"Henry is such a vengeful person, yet he’s done all this for you, and it’s not just liking anymore, he..."

’Bang!’

The only glass vase in the hospital room shattered against the wall behind Levi, who turned sideways with an amused look as he flicked the water off himself and turned back to look at me.

I lowered my hand that had thrown the vase, and my gaze was chilling as I watched him—if looks could kill, he would have died a hundred times over!

"Talking to you is getting more and more interesting. If we weren’t short on time, I’d really love to spend a few more days with you, smashing things or hitting people—I wouldn’t mind."

I really did want to hit someone, especially the lunatic in front of me.

My stomach started to ache faintly again, and I took a deep breath, pretended to press my hand against my abdomen, and said coldly, "Scram!"

Seeing this, Levi’s mocking look in his eyes somewhat faded as he smirked, "Are you hurting again? It couldn’t be that my words upset you, right? The Medical Practitioner didn’t mention anything about getting stomach aches from being upset, did he?"

I closed my eyes, utterly speechless, and realized that Levi was quite annoying—the more you react to his words, the more excited he gets! Ignoring him seemed like a better option.

I turned my head away and stopped talking, but the knocking at the door caught my attention.

"Master Levi! Are you alright? I heard the sound of something breaking in the room. Do you need someone to come in and clean it up? Glass shards are bad news for everyone on the island!"

This voice sounded somewhat familiar. I stood still, my ears perked up.

Levi glanced behind him as if he thought of something and stood up.

"You can come in and clean this up! I’m worried that a certain lady might lose her temper and harm herself or others," he joked, as if he and I were old friends rather than enemies.

I sneered coldly and ignored his teasing.

Simo entered from outside with a nurse fully covered in protective gear, including a face mask. I glanced at them out of the corner of my eye, a glint flashing in my eyes.

Simo and the nurse started cleaning up the mess in the sofa area. I walked over, a pair of scissors glowing with a silver sheen sliding out from the long sleeves of my hospital gown, and in one swift movement, I lunged at the slightly distracted Levi!

Levi was highly skilled and reacted quickly. He first dodged the sharp scissors then tried to grab my wrist to disarm my ’weapon.’ I withdrew my hand and drove my knee toward him; the movement was clean and nimble, but Levi’s combat experience was clearly richer. He deflected the impact from my knee with his other hand and said coldly, "So fierce? You really want to kill me, huh?"

I ignored his nonsense and stabbed with the scissors again, this time aiming for his stomach!

Levi frowned and snorted, a fight igniting in his eyes. His elbow suddenly shot out, striking my hand holding the scissors. My entire arm went numb, and pain shot through my palm—a sensation of flesh and blood being forcibly sliced open!

I let out a muffled grunt and evaded Levi’s next elbow strike. The injured hand trembled slightly, and bright, vivid blood dripped onto the floor like strings of red pearls, strikingly conspicuous!

"Ah! She’s hurt!" Simo was the first to notice and exclaimed in alarm.

Levi snapped back to reality, his fighting spirit fading as he looked coldly at the palm of my hand hanging by my side. His expression softened slightly, and he blurted out, "You... are you an idiot? Hurting yourself while fighting? Come here and let me have a look!"

I looked at him coldly and ignored his reproach. The pain and blood loss from the wound in my palm made my face turn pale.

Levi seemed extremely exasperated, took a deep breath, and said sternly, "Do you even want to keep your hand? Let the doctor treat it!"

"Your body is already in poor condition, you can’t afford to lose more blood! Stop being angry, let’s go to the pharmacy and stitch it up," Simo timely added, his tone filled with fearful trepidation but his concern was genuine.

In the tense minutes following Levi’s reaction, Simo’s accompanying nurse had already stepped forward to support me, leading me straight to the pharmacy at the other end of the hospital ward!

chapters.title: Chapter 388 ’female nurse
chapters.content:
Chapter 388: 388 ’female nurse

[Margaret’s Perspective]

The floor of the corridor was covered with my blood as Levi followed closely behind us, silently pondering over something unknown.

Just as Simo pushed open the pharmacy door anxiously waiting for me to enter, I suddenly stopped, glanced sideways at Levi with a cold look, and said in a tone devoid of any warmth, "Stay away from me. Don’t let me see you again."

After I spoke, I entered, leaving Levi and Simo frozen in place.

"Master Levi, the patient’s mood is very stable now. To prevent the recurrence of what just happened, I suggest you wait at the door for a while, alright?" Simo tentatively suggested, his gaze filled with sincerity as he looked at Levi.

Levi was silent for a few seconds and didn’t verbally respond to Simo’s suggestion. He just turned and stood rigidly in place, neither leaving nor advancing further.

Seeing this, Simo heaved a sigh of relief, bowed to Levi, and then turned to walk into the room.

At that moment, a hint of regret flashed across Levi’s face, but since no one in the corridor dared to look at him, this curious sight went unnoticed.

Inside the pharmacy, the "female nurse" gripped my wrist tightly while Simo deftly disinfected and attempted to stitch up the wound.

"Why are you so impulsive? What would you do if you really pierced your hand? Don’t you want this hand anymore?"

Listening to the "female nurse" lower her voice to ask me, I bore the pain and glanced at her, then after a moment of silence, I bluntly said, "Your willingness to come see me at the hospital, does it mean there’s a shift in your loyalties again? Are you planning to help Donald or help Henry? If you don’t clarify these things, it’s difficult for me to trust you again!"

At my words, she pulled off the mask on her face, revealing the familiar face I knew.

Licia was overwhelmed, seemingly unable to bear watching Simo stitch the center of my palm, she turned her head, lowered her voice, and replied, "I know my trustworthiness is very low among you, but what other choice do you have now except to cooperate with me? Henry won’t let you off easily; escaping from here is harder than reaching the heavens!"

"I know that myself, no need to dampen my spirit," I said as I shivered, the hidden pain in my abdomen and the piercing pain in the palm threatening to break me down completely.

Simo pressed down on my hand and said sternly, "Don’t move! If the stitching goes crooked, it’s really the end!"

Battling the cold sweat on my forehead, I looked at him and sarcastically said, "Your master hasn’t made his stance clear yet, and here you are working so diligently!"

"Margaret!"

Licia called out my name softly, I closed my mouth and shifted my gaze back to her, silently waiting for her to continue.

Seeing this, she sighed, her eyes slightly moist as she looked into mine, "Donald and his group arrived on the island yesterday, but because it’ll take some time for the main forces to follow, they didn’t initiate the battle immediately!"

"I informed him that you were here. He wanted to come directly and take you away, but I knew Henry had set numerous traps here. With just him and those few men with him, it would have been impossible to break through! That’s why I came to act as the vanguard for him."

The critical information in Licia’s words somewhat dazed me. I took a deep breath to compose myself and hoarsely asked, "What do you need me to do to coordinate with you? How many people does he have with him now? Levi said Henry searched all night and couldn’t find them; where are they hiding? I hope this time you’re truly trying to help Donald and not planning to betray him again!"

Licia’s eyes turned sad upon hearing this, after a moment she counterquestioned rather than answered, "How is the baby in your stomach? Simo told me yesterday the situation was unstable, you need to return to a hospital on land for recuperation."

"You can’t even be firm in your love for Donald, do you really care about this unborn grandchild? Please answer my question directly, time is pressing, and I don’t want to waste words with you!"

As I spoke, I glanced sideways toward the door. The blood loss was making my head feel dizzy, but I was struggling to stay alert. Licia’s attitude was crucial at this moment, concerning me, the child in my abdomen, and all of Donald’s future plans.

My concern proved necessary. Licia didn’t seem to want to abandon protecting Henry and his group. She hesitated for a few seconds before explaining, "I can get you out of here first, but I cannot stay uninvolved in the conflict between Donald and Martha Henry. I don’t want to see either side lose."

Licia’s indecisiveness on this matter was consistently stable. I was not even surprised, but another name she mentioned caught me off guard.

I slightly widened my eyes and countered, "You just mentioned Martha? It can’t be the Martha I’m thinking of, can it? She isn’t dead?"

Under my astonished gaze, Licia frowned and bluntly said, "Haven’t you met her once before? It was the day you were first brought to Poison Snake Island!"

chapters.title: Chapter 389 Princess Lington
chapters.content:
Chapter 389: 389 Princess Lington

[Margaret’s Perspective]

There was such certainty in Licia’s retort that I was taken aback for a moment, followed by a sensation as though something had cleared a path straight through my thoughts!

"Do you mean to say that the woman who first spoke to me that day was Martha, Henry’s mother—Princess Lington? And she is also the true mastermind behind Poison Snake Island?"

I asked incredulously, my eyes filled with amazement and surprise.

I shifted my gaze away from Licia while nodding and attempting to sort out my thoughts. So, the underlying reason behind all those seemingly inexplicable puzzles that racked our brains lay here!

The reason Licia did not hesitate to betray Donald to help Henry during the Royal City battle wasn’t solely because she valued and cherished her nephew, Henry. The major reason behind her actions was mostly because of Princess Lington, her ’resurrected’ biological sister.

After Henry’s rebellion failed in the Royal City and he fled southward, first forming an alliance with Levi at the Lilo Sea, then being chased by Donald and forced to seek refuge on the island with Martha—it was all traceable. Henry had an escape plan from the beginning, and Poison Snake Island must have been their final trump card, right?

My brain was whirring wildly as information flooded in, and I became so overwhelmed that I was frozen in place for a long time.

Seeing me muttering a few questions to myself and then falling silent, Licia turned to me and asked softly, "You didn’t know? I thought you were aware of all this after coming to the island."

Licia’s voice snapped me back to reality, and I turned to look at her, continuing my inquiry with a touch of puzzlement, "Why didn’t Martha let me see her face? Throughout our conversation that day, someone kept my eyes covered."

I thought it was just a peculiar detail, but seeing a hint of doubt and complexity wash over Licia’s face, I suddenly realized there must be other reasons behind it I was not aware of.

Simo had finished stitching up the wound on my palm, and I took a breath and temporarily shelved my question to ask Licia, "You still haven’t answered my question. Where are Donald and the others?"

Upon hearing this, Licia pursed her lips and, after a long while, reluctantly responded, "They are in the forest not far from the hospital. Since the area is surrounded by Henry’s men, they could not approach rashly to check on you, so they sent me in first to see how you were."

"What? They are that close?" I asked with some surprise.

Realizing that I was getting agitated, I took a deep breath to lower my voice and added, "Do you know when they plan to make their move? This time, Henry and Levi have laid a net of heaven and earth, waiting for Donald to walk into it. You must tell him to be cautious in his actions! I’m stable here for the time being, so tell him not to worry about me."

Licia furrowed her brows as she listened to me speak, shaking her head before answering my first question, "He didn’t tell me the details of the battle plan, so I can’t relay any useful information to you. He will certainly find a way to break through and rescue you. All you can do is wait patiently."

I wasn’t surprised by Licia’s answer. Donald probably doubted Licia’s allegiance just as I did, so not telling her any specific battle plans was actually quite reasonable.

I glanced at Licia and did not mock her further. Her situation was likely very awkward. Henry and Martha would surely not fully trust her, and her only son had been deeply hurt by her past actions. It was already very kind and generous of her to come in and relay a message to me.

Simo remained silent as Licia and I exchanged information, his hands never ceasing their work.

Layer upon layer of white gauze was wrapped around my palm. I glanced sideways and suddenly heard someone approaching outside. "Someone’s coming in!" I whispered as a warning.

At the sound, Licia swiftly put on her mask and took two steps back, positioning herself to appear as nothing more than a quiet, harmless nurse.

Simo paused for a moment, then forced himself to calm down quickly, striving to appear as nonchalant as possible.

"Has it not been cleaned up yet? Is the cut so severe that other medical practitioners need to be involved?" The tense and slightly unnatural voice of Levi came from the doorway.

With the gauze now secure, Simo looked up at me and nodded before responding, "Fortunately, the tendons and bones are untouched, and I’m nearly finished. Just a moment more, Young Master Levi!"

Levi seemed reassured by Simo’s reply and fell into silence, but he remained immobile at the doorway.

We had already been inside for nearly 10 minutes. Staying any longer might raise Levi’s suspicions. I frowned and stood up from the chair, swaying slightly and almost tumbling over!

"Be careful!" Startled, Simo quickly extended a hand to steady me.

With a loud ’bang’, the door was suddenly kicked open from the outside. Levi strode in, his face still showing unmasked concern, "What happened? Is something wrong?"

chapters.title: Chapter 390 A step behind
chapters.content:
Chapter 390: 390 A step behind

[Margaret’s Perspective]

With Simo’s arm supporting me, I struggled to stand and looked at him coldly. My voice was hoarse as I said, "Who let you in? Get out!"

Seeing that everything in the room was normal, Levi froze in place and frowned as he said, "Don’t overstep your bounds! Understand your situation; you’re our captive!"

Upon hearing this, I took a deep breath and decided not to pursue the issue with Levi any further. I shifted my gaze to my right hand and realized that my condition was not good.

"It’s just due to losing too much blood, causing weakness. Don’t worry about it!" Simo interjected timely to explain and then helped me onto the wheelchair pushed over by Licia.

He let go of my hand and turned to Levi, "Let her go back to rest now. It’s time for her medicine."

As soon as Simo finished speaking, he signaled Licia with a dismissive gesture to leave, which looked entirely natural. Levi did not seem to suspect anything.

"Wait!"

Suddenly, Levi spoke up, which tensed everyone instantly. My face was pale, and there was a lot of cold sweat on my forehead.

He walked toward Simo and me but didn’t notice Licia, who had already neared the door. Licia paused, then slowly walked out.

It was undeniable that at that moment, Licia’s psychological resilience was very strong. She didn’t panic but instead left the room as naturally as possible.

Simo held his breath for several seconds until this moment, and then he resumed his normal breathing. I also sighed in relief, although my pale, composed face showed no emotion.

"What is it, Mr. Levi?" Simo asked with a smile.

Levi ignored him and stared at me, demanding, "I haven’t asked you yet. Where did you get those scissors from earlier?"

Upon hearing this, I looked up at him with a cold expression. "What? You want to interrogate me? It’s a pity I didn’t kill you. You’d better be careful when you talk to me in the future. I can’t guarantee I won’t lash out at you!"

Of course, I wouldn’t tell Levi the source of the scissors. If I just obediently listened to him, would I still be myself?

Moreover, I had already used the Medical Practitioner to accomplish many things. It wouldn’t be good to burden him further because of the scissors.

After speaking, I withdrew my icy gaze and said faintly to Simo, "Push me back."

Levi was almost infuriated by my words but didn’t say anything to stop me from leaving. His gaze remained fixed on me, deep in thought.

What I didn’t know was that in Levi’s eyes at that moment, I was just a poor creature with a bad temper and a stubborn nature, completely oblivious to my own predicament!

My face was extremely pale due to blood loss, and there were cold sweats all over my forehead, which was covered in adhesive bandages from the pain. My right hand and wrist were wrapped up like a ’bun’, and there was a faint smell of blood on my body as I huddled small and pitifully in the wheelchair.

Besides the stubbornness deep in my bones, unwilling to give in, I looked immensely sorrowful and fragile.

Levi was silent for 2 seconds, then he scoffed sarcastically and curled his lips as if he found me exceedingly absurd. On closer inspection, one could even detect a hint of helplessness deep in his eyes.

When I returned to the hospital room, Licia had already found an excuse to leave the hospital, and the recent mess in the room had been cleaned up.

I looked around the room and noticed that Levi had many items removed without saying a word, keeping a cold face, I lay down on the hospital bed with the help of Simo’s hand.

After swallowing the medication Simo had prepared in advance, I felt a bit of relief in my abdomen and thanked Simo with a glance.

"Install them in these two spots, quickly!"

Levi told the Werewolves who just entered, lazily pointing at the wall opposite the bed and the corner above the door.

I turned my head to see, noticing that the Werewolves had brought in cameras!

I scoffed coldly, thinking to myself: This bastard has really started monitoring me as if I were a true captive.

Seeing this, Simo looked at me somewhat anxiously, silent and apparently deep in thought.

Thinking about the information Simo and Licia had successfully passed on to me, Levi was a step slow in his actions. I sighed softly, leaning against the headrest and didn’t spare Levi another glance.

After all the events earlier, it was now 12 noon. After installing the cameras, Levi left, leaving me alone in the room.

I hadn’t slept the previous night and had lost blood from being injured, feeling extremely weary and weak, but I dared not close my eyes to sleep. I wondered when Donald would make his move.

Since they arrived at the island, nearly 10 hours had passed. Their main forces should have been ready by now, but it was strange that there had been no movement.

The room was too quiet. Despite my strong will, my physical strength was reaching its limits. As I thought about it, I drifted into an uncontrollable sleep.

chapters.title: Chapter 391 going to sea
chapters.content:
Chapter 391: 391 going to sea

[Donald’s perspective]

The midday sea breeze that brushed against my face was cool, yet it did nothing to ease the suppressed restlessness and frustration within my heart!

I glanced at the watch on my wrist, and my eyes grew a bit colder.

"The agreed time has come, Berton is late," Elliot said from behind me, his voice heavy with discontent. "This old guy isn’t pulling some shenanigans again, is he? We just did him a huge favor by rescuing his daughter, if he’s still hesitating and delaying, then it’s really becoming intentional."

Of course, I knew what Elliot was implying with his words. Eradicating the pirates was initially the duty of the Marine Supervision Bureau, and Berton’s constant shirking and stalling were utterly despicable and nauseating!

The assault team had already discreetly confirmed the pirates’ location at sea; all that was left was for Berton to bring over the naval main force, but that man had the audacity to be late.

My silence made everyone on the ship tense up. Christian looked at me with some concern and slowly gripped his firearm tighter.

Benjamin stood on the nearby deck, commanding the Lycans around him as they moved supplies. He was the first to notice the people coming up from the stairs and immediately reported, "Your Majesty, Dean Andrew and Miss Elizabeth have arrived."

At these words, I tore my gaze from the sea and turned to look at the newcomers.

"Your Majesty, we have arrived!"

"Brother-in-law, we’re here!"

The voices of Dean Andrew and Elizabeth rose at the same time. Hearing that title, the light in my eyes softened a bit, and I took a few steps towards them. "You’ve had a hard journey, come sit down and rest for a while."

As I spoke, I gestured for the three of them to take a seat and also nodded to Anthony, who hadn’t had the chance to speak yet. He seemed to have fully recovered, had a healthy glow, and stood respectfully beside Elizabeth, bowing slightly to me.

"Here is the summary of the combat medical supplies brought by the Holy Medical Pavilion this time, please have a look, Your Majesty," Andrew said as he handed me a booklet with both hands. His elderly face was etched with exhaustion, clearly having endured many sleepless nights before coming.

I took the booklet and looked at Andrew with grave sincerity. "I’ve always trusted your work, Dean. I’ll have Benjamin coordinate with you. The army’s rear guard is in your hands!"

"Thank you for your trust, Your Majesty. I, your servant, shall fulfill my duty without fail," Andrew solemnly assured me with a serious expression.

I nodded, then turned to Elizabeth but hesitated for a second before speaking.

"Why haven’t I seen Margaret? Where is she? It’s already late; she can’t still be sleeping, can she?" Elizabeth, seemingly oblivious to my change in demeanor, asked in a relaxed and cheerful tone.

Anthony glanced at me then slowly looked around at everyone else, as if sensing something, he respectfully asked, "Isn’t the Lycan Queen here? Where are we heading, Your Majesty?"

Elizabeth, sensitive to changes in Anthony’s mood, toned down her smile and looked at me with eyes full of inquiry.

Knowing I could no longer hide the truth, I took a shallow breath, lowered my gaze, and explained in a deep voice, "Margaret was abducted by the enemy last night, and we’re about to set out to rescue her!"

"What? Margaret was kidnapped? What happened?" Elizabeth’s reaction was the quickest, her direct questioning voice filled with concern.

Just then, my satellite phone suddenly rang. I glanced at it, and a dangerous light flickered in my wolfish eyes, "We’re pressed for time right now, I’ll explain the details of this situation to you later."

With that, I looked up at Anthony and after a moment’s thought, I cautioned, "You keep Elizabeth safe. Stay on the ship and don’t act rashly. I’ll inform you as soon as there’s news, and later, we might need your help with something!"

Upon hearing this, Anthony stood up respectfully, bowed his head to me, and pledged earnestly, "My life was given to me by you and the Lycan Queen, Your Majesty. Please don’t speak like that. Whatever I can do to help, just command me. Elizabeth is very safe with me, please be at ease for yourself and the Lycan Queen."

I appreciate and trust Anthony, so after hearing his reply, I nodded. I got up, patted his shoulder, and walked away.

As I descended the deck, I glanced at Benjamin and instructed in a calm voice, "Escort Dean Andrew safely to the ship at the rear. We’ll rendezvous on the outskirts of Poison Snake Island. Be quick!"

Naturally, Benjamin obeyed my royal command, bowed, and turned to walk away without another word, while Elliot and Christian stayed close at my heels.

I answered Berton’s incoming satellite call and spoke in an icy voice, "I thought Mr. Berton’s ship was stuck at sea, I was about to send someone to ’invite’ you."

"It’s my fault, Your Majesty! Assembling the navy does indeed take some time; I apologize for the delay! I’m not far from your fleet now; do you still need to come over and address the troops?" Berton’s voice came through the phone.

I narrowed my eyes, the intent to kill gleaming within them, "The navy is the main force in the blockade against the pirates. I’m not just going to address the soldiers; I’m also taking over your military command. Understand?"

chapters.title: Chapter 392 Suppress Bandits
chapters.content:
Chapter 392: 392 Suppress Bandits

[Donald’s Perspective]

I said with a sneer, glanced through the window at the naval fleet on the sea, and continued, "I’m giving you 15 minutes to come here; I’ll be waiting on my ship."

There was silence on the other side for a while before they started to speak, seemingly trying to change my plan of attack.

"Your Majesty, you can certainly command in person, but if you do not advance and retreat with the navy, I fear some uncontrollable accidents might occur."

"You have so many formidable Lycans on your ship; you really don’t need to worry about the safety of your fleet. The navy fleet is what truly needs your presence!" Berton’s tone was heartfelt, as if he truly had my and the navy fleet’s best interests at heart.

I silently smiled, not accepting the suggestion from the head of the Marine Supervision Bureau, and coldly stated, "Cut the chatter. I have just one question for you: what is the ultimate duty of a soldier?"

Berton seemed choked by my question and, after a long pause, finally replied, "To follow orders, Your Majesty!"

"I thought you might not be clear on that," I said calmly. Before hanging up, I very ’kindly’ reminded him, "You have 12 more minutes. I dislike officials who are always late, do as you must!"

After finishing, I handed the phone to Christian next to me and gestured to Elliot, signaling that both the bandit suppression and the reconnaissance actions should proceed simultaneously!

Elliot nodded to show he understood and turned around, followed by a dozen highly skilled Lycans. The person being pushed forward step by step with a pistol to the back of his head was the ’old fisherman’ familiar with the waters around Poison Snake Island, sent by Berton.

"Our Majesty is far more generous than your superior, I hope you keep a clear head going forward, don’t make a mess," Elliot said coldly, though he had already put away his gun, a cruel smirk hanging at the corner of his mouth.

The assault team heading to Poison Snake Island had already set off, and the next step was to ambush the sea bandits trying to return to the island. They had robbed two cruise ships at sea that day and were probably feeling joyful about their hefty bounty, completely unaware of their impending doom!

minutes later, Berton, sweating profusely, boarded my ship. I sat motionless in a chair watching him approach, took the naval orders and communication device from his hand, slowly stood up from the chair and said, "If not for Brand really resembling you, I would almost doubt he’s your biological son."

Berton wiped the sweat off his face and looked at me, seemingly puzzled why I would suddenly bring up this topic.

Playing with the military orders in my palm, I smiled curiously and asked, "The methods of those bandits are so brutal, Brand has been out of contact for so long, aren’t you worried as a father? Or do you already have the confidence that they won’t really harm Brand, which is why you seem so fearless?"

"Mr. Berton, you must have been very busy from last night until now, right? Have the bandits relayed to you any needs for a hostage exchange?"

Berton’s eyes became a bit panicky, and after a few seconds, he knelt down on the ground and burst into tears, "Your Majesty, you are well-informed and matchlessly wise, please save poor Brand!"

I did not respond, merely watching him with a pressing gaze.

Seeing my reaction, Berton realized he could no longer hide anything from me. While still crying, he continued, "Half an hour ago, I received a mysterious phone call, the caller claimed they had Brand! They said that if I wanted that child to live, I had to cooperate with them. I..."

"So you agreed?" I interrupted Berton’s complaints in a neutral tone.

"No, Your Majesty! I would never betray you and the Wolf royal family, you can be assured of that. But Brand... please, you must find a way to save him. Poor Lisa has been severely injured and is still unconscious in the hospital; I don’t want Brand to end up like that,"

Berton’s words were somewhat incoherent, but I did not care at all. I crouched down and looked at him, my dangerous eyes faintly flashing as I directly asked, "Can I trust you?"

Taken aback at first, Berton soon seemed to realize something, looking earnestly back at me and replied, "My loyalty to Your Majesty can be seen by heaven and earth, and if I ever betray it, may I die from a thousand arrows piercing my heart!"

A slight movement crossed my face after hearing these words. Moments later, I reached out to help Berton off the ground, chuckling as I responded, "Of course, I am willing to trust you. Get up now, don’t always be so formal; it makes things awkward."

As I indicated for Berton to take a seat, I held the communicator in my hand and issued the first command!

We had already reached the waters frequented by the sea bandits three minutes ago. The weather conditions in this maritime area were poor, the fog heavily limiting visibility. I could only order the navy to use radar to scan.

A few seconds later, a naval officer responded, "Reporting to Your Majesty, the radar scan has completed, we haven’t found any suspicious targets!"

chapters.title: Chapter 393 Can’t fit anymore?
chapters.content:
Chapter 393: 393 Can’t fit anymore?

[Donald’s Perspective]

Upon hearing this, my expression became stern, and the aura around me turned intimidating. "Scan again, and do not miss any corner! Direct the Vanguard in, shoot to kill if a signal fluctuation is detected, and do not report back to me."

"Yes, Your Majesty!"

Hearing the reply, I casually put down the communicator, took a couple of deep breaths, and then turned to look at Berton.

Berton also looked shocked and confused, clearly unable to understand why the enemy had suddenly vanished. Just half an hour ago, we had received definite news that this group of sea pirates’ vessels would definitely pass through this sea area!

"That’s impossible, Your Majesty! The information we received couldn’t have been wrong. The channel of this information is absolutely reliable," he exclaimed.

"Is that so? Then how would you explain the current situation?" I asked calmly.

Berton stood up from his chair again, looking at me with a worried expression. After a moment, he seemed to have organized his thoughts and spoke, "If that’s the case, then it can only mean one thing. We have a mole among us! He must have informed the enemy of our military plans in advance."

"Your analysis makes some sense. So, what should we do next?" I inquired further.

Berton looked at me, his eyes filled with sorrow.

He spoke softly, "Do you suspect me, Your Majesty? I could never be the mole. I have not responded to the messages they have sent us. How can I prove my loyalty to you?"

I smiled upon hearing this, turned back to gaze at the navy ships advancing into the sea, and replied, "I said I am willing to believe you, but whether or not you are trustworthy is a question you can only answer yourself."

’Bang, Bang, Bang!’

The thunderous noise of the cannon fire erupted along with my calm voice. Berton froze in shock, startled by the source of the loud sounds.

"What’s going on? Why are they firing, weren’t you just saying there were no suspicious ships in this area? Your Highness!"

Upon hearing this, I remained silent, waving my hand at Christian to give a sign.

Berton naturally didn’t understand the tacit understanding between me and my subordinate. I was telling Christian to turn on the giant screen television mounted on the cabin wall.

The live footage of the naval bombardment was clearly transmitted back. I returned to my seat, pleasantly watching, "My soldiers only said there were no suspicious ships on the surface, but they didn’t say there weren’t any beneath the sea! They thought by submerging deeper than the range of the ship’s radar, they’d be safe, which is utterly ridiculous. The navy’s ships need updating, and so do these pirates’ ships!"

Berton, stunned by the images on the giant screen, froze completely. It took him a few seconds to accept the reality before he asked in a hoarse voice, "You just said ’my soldiers.’ What does that mean? Isn’t the navy under the Lilo Sea regime? How could they not obey my orders and only follow yours?"

Berton’s rationality was nearly shattered, and he didn’t realize how presumptuous his words were.

I scoffed in response, "Between the Lycan king and a mere head of the Marine Supervision Bureau, whose orders do you think soldiers should follow? Isn’t the Lilo Sea regime still under the Werewolf royal family? You must be frightened out of your wits, Berton, to speak so inappropriately!"

After hearing my words, Berton’s expression cracked, his facade no longer maintainable, and he suddenly burst out laughing as he charged at me!

I quickly pulled out my gun and aimed it at his head, taunting him, "Can’t keep up the act any longer? I haven’t even had enough fun, and you’re already ’chickening out’?"

"Donald, you are despicable!" Berton’s movements halted as he glared at me and gritted his teeth.

I was taken aback, looking at him with cold eyes, "You’re wrong! This isn’t about being despicable, it’s about having a backup and taking precautions early. Otherwise, do you think being Lycan king is easy? If I was a bit less sharp, people like you would have left nothing of me!"

"I was wondering why that cargo ship was stationary at Color-pennant Town until I gradually figured it out. You orchestrated a huge drama all by yourself! The purpose was to conceal your collusion with the pirates. I wonder, if Lisa and Brand knew about the cruel acts you committed, would they still acknowledge you as their father?"

Berton’s wrinkled face turned bright red, as if I had hit a nerve.

I didn’t really care about the turmoil within this old man’s heart. I sneered and continued, "To get close to Margaret and to accomplish the mission she gave you, you really would abandon anything! Trading your children for the luxuries of your later life, your ’deal’ is truly baffling. Don’t you think you’ve made a huge loss?"

"Shut up! Don’t talk anymore!"

"Oh! What? You can do these deeds, but fear others speaking of them? Does being openly criticized sting? Do you need me to help you experience it again?"

chapters.title: Chapter 394 ’Going to War with Justification
chapters.content:
Chapter 394: 394 ’Going to War with Justification

[Donald’s perspective]

Berton was somewhat hysterical as he shouted. I squinted dangerously at him and didn’t think he had any chance of turning defeat into victory!

’Bang!’

As I spoke, I fired a shot at his right shoulder without hesitation, "Consider this shot a compensation for little Lisa. You crazy old man don’t deserve to be her father."

Berton, as if he hadn’t expected me to actually shoot, let out a muffled grunt as he took the bullet, and blood quickly pooled from near his right shoulder that was close to the ground.

This time he truly cried out in pain, much more genuine than his earlier acting.

"You... Even if you have seen through me, you won’t really be able to successfully land on the island! Without internal personnel to guide you, it’s impossible to pass through the waters of Poison Snake Island. Killing me directly is also useless; it seems you are doomed not to rescue your beloved Margaret," Berton said with a voice intermittent, yet he continued to provoke me by bringing up Margaret.

I took a step forward and forcefully stomped on his right shoulder with my combat boot!

I ignored his suddenly piercing scream and said coldly, "That’s my own business, no need for your concern! You can choose to keep your mouth shut, or continue provoking me, but I can’t assure that my next shot will be this ’off target’!"

When I talked about being ’off target,’ I didn’t mean actually missing, but rather that the next shot would aim to take his life.
