"This band of sea pirates, I finished them all today. Do you think that mysterious island master will still trust you, think you are a reliable ally? You actually have no choice anymore, understand, Berton?" I ground my heel into the torn flesh under my boot, my expression still calm.

I admit I’m no match for Henry when it comes to manipulating people’s hearts, but it’s not something I’m incompetent at. I am quite adept at battlefield negotiations, which are very straightforward, generally boiling down to just threat or temptation!

Berton isn’t afraid of losing relatives or friends, but he values his own life unexpectedly highly; a kind of extreme selfishness.

Under my pressure, he remained silent for a long time until he realized that he was losing too much blood and his body temperature and life signs were dropping rapidly, then he reluctantly spoke in a hoarse voice, "There are three factions of rebels on the island: Henry’s Rebels, Levi’s Spirit Dragon Pack, and the followers of the Poison Snake Island Master!"

This sentence seemed somewhat redundant. I slightly frowned, my patience quickly fading.

Berton noticed my mood and, gritting his teeth, continued, "Underneath the eastern part of Poison Snake Island, it’s said there’s a submarine passage that leads directly to Demon Domain Forest! So I actually began to suspect the identity of the island master quite early, until the rebel Prince Henry fled south and finally ended up in the Lilo Sea, arriving at this Poison Snake Island!"

I realized the importance of this information. My gaze at Berton turned colder, but inside I was tumultuously turning things over.

Magic Forest! Poison Snake Island! Henry!

When these keywords were added together, they uncontrollably sent a chill up my spine. A speculation arose in my heart, yet my face remained as calm as ever while I continued to stare down Berton.

"It is well-known to everyone that King Lington died in the Demon Domain Forest. However, no one has ever delved into the circumstances of another person, that is, the wife of King Lington - Princess Lington! She is the biological sister of the current Empress Dowager. Although some say she died to accompany King Lington in death, it’s merely a rumor after all, and no one knows what the real situation was."

"I came to know of the existence of that passage in a very coincidental situation. And from that, I deduced the current conclusion: the master of Poison Snake Island is Princess Lington and also the biological mother of Henry!"

"Thus, it makes perfect sense for Henry to have traveled such great distances to escape here. And the ’sea pirates’ committing plunder on the Lilo Sea weren’t just any robbers or any damned mercenaries; I suspect they are the descendants of the remains of King Lington’s forces!"

Overwhelmed by the load of information in Berton’s statement, he closed his eyes, as if he could barely hold on.

I took my foot off him, slowly stood up, and my brain quickly processed all this information.

So initially, the raw ingredients for those strange potions created by Henry truly came from the Demon Domain Forest, and then were transported out of Poison Snake Island through Martha’s hands, until they finally reached Henry, who was in the Royal City.

Furthermore, Austin and Levi’s betrayal of the Wolf Royal Family was also because of this, as Henry, backed by the ’revived’ Princess Lington and in command of King Lington’s remnants, was well-placed to directly confront the current Royal Family.

They could completely stand under the banner of avenging King Lington, coupled with the forces of rebels gathered from various sources, to ’righteously’ declare war against me and the Wolf Royal Family!

With everything starting to clear up, the unease in my heart surged even more. Margaret’s situation on the island seemed increasingly difficult.

chapters.title: Chapter 395 Another Space
chapters.content:
Chapter 395: 395 Another Space

[Donald’s Perspective]

I had long known that Berton was in cahoots with the people on Poison Snake Island, but I had not realized he knew so much. The news that Princess Lington was not dead was crucial, yet I had only come to truly understand this now. How dull I had been!

It seemed that the reason Licia was desperately protecting Henry lay here too. How could she let her ’resurrected’ sister suffer the pain of losing loved ones again?

Feeling somewhat sad about this, I tugged at the corner of my mouth and cast my eyes down to Berton, who was gasping for breath on the ground.

"Don’t blame me for not giving you a chance. If you come clean now about what you’ve done, I might consider sparing your life, otherwise..."

Upon hearing this, Berton slowly opened his eyes. His completely pale lips trembled as if he was actually considering my proposition.

I narrowed my eyes at Berton, and a bloodthirsty warning flashed in my pupils, "otherwise, you will just have to die. The Werewolf Royal Family despises traitors the most."

"Your Majesty! I’ll confess everything. Please have mercy and spare my life!" Berton pleaded weakly from the ground as he strained to look up at me.

I raised an eyebrow without responding, the implication clear.

"Confess quickly, before you’ve bled out completely." Christian added aptly from the side, his gun aimed straight at Berton’s heart.

At this point, of course, Berton, the old man, had no other choice. He bowed his head and slowly began to confess other matters!

For instance, how he orchestrated the ’attack’ on Brand and Lisa, then their disappearance, and how he had his subordinates attack White Rose Manor, forcing Margaret to move her camp in the dead of night.

He said the people from Poison Snake Island only asked him to lure Margaret out of White Rose Manor. He wasn’t very clear about the details, as it was the other party’s people who took over the next step of the plan!

Of course, Brand and Lisa did indeed disappear later.

Henry did not fully trust Berton. The incident where we rescued Lisa in Color-pennant Town was half accidental, and I felt the other half was deliberate by the other party! Their reasons for taking Brand might be similar to those for taking Margaret, both for leverage and threat.

However, Henry saw Berton too simply. This old man was far greedier and more cunning than he usually appeared! Outwardly, he was placating me while hiding his disloyalty to the Werewolf Royal Family, and behind the scenes, he even wanted a share of Martha’s ’business’ at sea. He had even collaborated with Henry—an outright triple agent!

As for why I divided Henry and Martha into two factions here, it’s because Henry had always been far away in Royal City and was not likely to personally control King Lington’s old subordinates, ruling the waters of the Lilo Sea and engaging in acts of robbery. So, this act could only be Martha, Princess Lington.

I utterly despised scum like Berton who would sacrifice everything for his goals, even betraying his own soul, and using his own son as a bargaining chip!

By the end, Berton was barely clinging to life. I glanced sideways, signaling Christian that he could take him away. Holding the navy squad’s communicator in my hand, I continued to issue orders to attack, demanding the annihilation of all enemy yachts.

Gradually, some remnants of yachts surfaced on the sea. The enemy revealed itself, and I watched unblinkingly as the sea area reddened with blood, then diluted back to normal by the seawater.

"Clean up the battlefield, leave none behind," I ordered sternly, my tone chilling.

Christian obeyed and personally led the navy’s charge into the center of the sea.

Half an hour later, he returned, covered in bloodstains, holding the head of a ’sea pirate’ leader in his hand—it was quite a catch.

I did not interfere with the interrogation of this Werewolf, instead ordering the ship to swiftly turn and head to the location previously agreed upon with Elliot.

This warship was specially made, very fast compared to ordinary ships. It was perfectly suitable as a mobile command ship for naval warfare.

Eliminating this gang of ’sea pirates’ took up my morning, but the only slightly uplifting part was that the gains from this operation were substantial.

Thanks to Berton’s dying confessions and the guidance of an old fisherman, we smoothly approached the vortex waters around Poison Snake Island, just one step away from landing on the island!

The weather overhead was incredibly strange, with a heavy layer of clouds stacked together. Rain hit the ship, exuding a sour, rotten smell. A thick fog isolated the command ship’s route forward. The radar system, which had been very useful until now, had collapsed. The compass spun wildly on the dashboard, and we seemed to have entered another primal space.

"Your Majesty! It looks like there’s really no way forward; this fog is nearly solidifying into a wall," Elliot patted his compass and looked up at me with furrowed brows.

chapters.title: Chapter 396 Bone Flute
chapters.content:
Chapter 396: 396 Bone Flute

[Donald’s Perspective]

Through the bulletproof specialty glass window, I stared at the mist in front of me and fell into deep thought before slowly responding, "That old fisherman was nearly scared to death by Benjamin, it’s unlikely that he lied. This proves that our general direction is correct, and this should be the last checkpoint. Once we get through here, we’ll be able to see the ’gate’ to Poisonous Snake Island."

Upon hearing this, Elliot nodded but his brow remained furrowed, "So, what do we do next? The fisherman said this is the deepest he’s ever gone, he doesn’t know the way beyond this point."

I turned my head toward Christian, who was standing to one side, and said in a soft voice, "Bring that guy over, we’ll have to rely on him to lead the way from here."

Elliott, unaware of our recent skirmish with the ’pirates,’ turned his head as well toward the silent Christian.

The personal guard captain nodded and turned to leave.

Seeing this, I let out a sigh and addressed Benjamin below, "You didn’t kill the man just now, did you? He should be able to lead the way, shouldn’t he?"

Upon hearing this, Benjamin immediately stood up from his chair and replied respectfully, "Your Majesty, I didn’t! Although that person’s mouth was somewhat difficult to ’pry open,’ our Medical Practitioners always have their ways. He’ll lead us well, rest assured."

I was certain I saw excitement in Benjamin’s eyes, and thinking of his maniacal obsession with the werewolf’s physique, I slightly curled the corner of my mouth and diverted my gaze, thinking to myself: Falling into Benjamin’s hands is just your bad luck, but you should feel fortunate to make your own ’contribution’ to the genius Medical Practitioner’s medical endeavors!

A few minutes later, Elliott saw the ’pirate’ leader who had collapsed on the ground, yet was exceptionally lucid.

"He’s in this state, can he still lead us?" Elliott asked somewhat skeptically as he looked at him.

Benjamin crouched down, making eye contact with the ’pirate’ leader, and patted his face while replying to Elliott’s question, "I gave him an injection of Truth Potion, something often used during military interrogations as well, it works quite well, don’t worry."

As he spoke, he turned the man’s face toward him to look him in the eyes, "How does one get through the misty area of Poisonous Snake Island?"

"Bone Flute. Use the Bone Flute to summon schools of fish to lead the way," the man replied.

"What Bone Flute? Where is it?" Benjamin pressed.

The man’s expression was vacant, his eyes filled with red veins, and blood seeped from the corner of his mouth, as if bitten by his own wolfish teeth!

Seeing the man’s resilience, Benjamin furrowed his brow and quipped forcefully, "Where is the Bone Flute? Speak!"

"In... in the pocket on the chest of my underwear! Ah!" He cried out as though experiencing extreme agony, arching his body and screaming.

Benjamin, unblinking, reached deftly into the man’s clothes and retrieved the so-called Bone Flute, then turned and handed it to me with both hands.

No one paid attention to the enemy lying on the ground in agony and despair; we all knew this was the retribution he deserved.

I raised my hand and took the Bone Flitte, its body dark and glossy, its bone structure tight and firm. It wasn’t clear which animal’s bones it was made of. But since the man claimed it could summon a school of fish to lead the way, it shouldn’t be anything ordinary.

Seeing that I was about to blow the Bone Flute, Elliott and Christian almost simultaneously suggested, "Let me do it, Your Majesty!"

Realizing their thoughts had coincided, Christian was the first to recover, stepping forward respectfully, "After we land on the island, Your Majesty will have to command the battle personally. This object doesn’t look simple, dark and not known from which animal’s bone it’s made of; just in case, let me blow it."

Seeing this, Elliott’s raised hand slowly fell, and his gaze toward the Bone Flute tightened.

Benjamin glanced at Elliott, then at Christian, before finally settling his gaze on my hand, "Just now, after revealing the Bone Flute, the man showed signs of severe pain, a direct effect of the Truth Potion. The more pain he’s in, the more important the secret is to him—it’s unlikely he’s deceiving us!"

Benjamin continued, taking the Bone Flute back and after looking at me, added, "I’ll do it, Your Majesty. I am more familiar with these things."

I did not speak, just watching as he slowly furrowed his brow.

Strangely enough, when Benjamin puffed his cheeks and blew into the Bone Flute, there was no sound to be heard in the cabin, silent except for our breathing and heartbeats.

After blowing for about 2 minutes, I signaled for Benjamin to stop, frowning in silence.

"What’s going on? There’s no sound at all? Did that guy break it, can it be fixed?" Elliott expressed his confusion with a puzzled look, his eyes seeking answers from Benjamin.

Benjamin glanced at him, then turned back to me.

"Could it be a matter of special sound frequencies?" I asked, though I was already sure of the conjecture.

"Possible!" Benjamin nodded, and after thinking a moment, explained to Elliott, "Just because we can’t hear doesn’t mean there is no sound, it might be that the frequency is outside our range of hearing, it’s not meant for us to hear."

chapters.title: Chapter 397 Fish Leading the Way
chapters.content:
Chapter 397: 397 Fish Leading the Way

[From Donald’s perspective]

"Who was that for?" Elliot asked, puzzled.

I glanced at him helplessly and said bluntly, "Were you distracted just now? Didn’t that guy just say that the Bone Flute is used to summon schools of fish!"

Upon hearing this, Elliot’s face turned red, and he looked somewhat embarrassed.

He bowed slightly toward me and said apologetically, "I’m sorry, Your Majesty! I indeed got distracted just now and missed that information!"

I nodded lightly, which was as much as accepting Elliot’s explanation. His workload had been intense over the past few days; he probably hadn’t had a good sleep, so it was normal for his mind to be a bit slow.

"Why don’t you take a seat over there in the chair? You’re not needed here for now," I said with a light sigh, my tone gentler.

Elliot clearly understood what I meant, nodded, and bowed before sitting down in the designated spot.

I raised my hand, signaling Benjamin to hand me the Bone Flute, "The flute has been played, so why is there still no response from outside?"

Before I finished speaking this perplexing question, a Lycan hurried into the cabin doorway, bowing respectfully and reporting, "Your Majesty! We have spotted a massive school of fish surging beneath the ship; they keep swimming behind us; it looks very strange indeed."

The newly arrived Lycan, unaware of the recent events in the cabin, reported with a face full of confusion and surprise as if he too was startled by such a scene!

At this news, my eyes brightened suddenly. I stood up and took a few steps toward the window, not forgetting to ask for more details, "When did you notice this school of fish? Do you know where they came from?"

The Lycan shook his head upon hearing this, indicating that they were also unsure of when or from where the fish had come.

Outside, the weather was still foggy with extremely low visibility; I looked at the swirling water and the faintly visible school of fish around the ship, thought for a moment, and then quickly instructed Christian, "Turn the ship around, follow the fish!"

Christian reacted swiftly, immediately leaving to carry out the order.

Actually, in such extreme weather, we could not fully discern our direction. Our only belief was that we had been advancing inward along the correct course. But at some point, we had unknowingly become lost in the dense fog!

Luckily, we still had an ace up our sleeve. We had captured a minor leader of the enemy, who had revealed information about the Bone Flute, so we were not completely lost in this sea area.

The rumors about Poison Snake Island were largely true. Without an inside guide or someone to meet us, it was absolutely impossible to enter such a place. Even if one did accidentally stumble upon it, they would be trapped in the Mist.

We followed the fish for about 40 minutes before finally escaping the fog-laden sea area. It was nearing evening, but the sea was pitch dark, stirring up fear of the unknown.

"My watch shows it’s 5 PM now, but it’s already completely dark here; is there a time difference from the outside world?" Elliot stood behind me, looking out at the darkened sky, his voice filled with doubt.

Because my position was further forward, I was the first to clearly see the faintly visible cliffs ahead. I frowned slightly and motioned to the Lycan piloting the ship to halt our progress.

Almost at the same moment, the radar, which had been inactive until now, suddenly came to life. Its screen slowly displayed our ultimate destination, the mysterious and elusive Poison Snake Island!

"Your Majesty! We’ve arrived!"

"My goodness! This island was so well hidden. Who on earth was the first to discover its existence? They must be incredibly clever!"

Christian and Elliot spoke, one after the other, their tones filled with excitement and thrill.

Of course, I too breathed a sigh of relief but could not feel happy at all, because just reaching Poison Snake Island had already cost us so much time and effort. The crises and challenges we would face on the island, though still unknown, were sure not to be smooth sailing.

After all, my enemies were no ordinary foes.

I nodded slightly in response to their comments, my furrowed brow slowly relaxing as I let out a cold chuckle and ordered, "Have the command ship stay put for now. Christian, send down a few small boats—we’ll go first and check things out."

At this, Christian sprang into action, and the Lycans in the cabin all perked up, their faces stern and serious as they gazed toward the island we were about to conquer.

After thinking a bit more, I continued, "All of the personal guard force Lycans stay on the command ship, the assault team prepare yourselves. Don’t carry too much heavy gear; let’s go in lightly armed to scout the situation up there first."

At this, Elliot became excited. He always enjoyed such thrilling and perilous missions.

chapters.title: Chapter 398 Landing on the Island
chapters.content:
Chapter 398: 398 Landing on the Island

[Donald’s Perspective]

minutes later, I led Elliot and the elite members of the assault team toward the cliff, leveraging the faint twilight before us. I raised my hand and gestured for action to Elliot, prompting the Lycans on the boat to start moving immediately.

They had climbing gear around their waists, and each person was agile, climbing up the rock face in no time.

I followed closely behind, moving even faster to catch up. As soon as we landed on the island, I heard footsteps not far away!

I gestured for everyone to be on alert and crouched low, hiding behind the lush trees, "It must be the island’s patrol team; try not to alert them."

While squatting, I moved in another direction, but once we left the woods, we no longer had natural barriers, and the patrol team’s surveying intervals seemed even more frequent!

"Your Majesty! There’s a patrol team of three approaching us on the right; they’ll see us once they turn at that tree. What do we do now, Your Majesty? Shall we engage?" Elliot asked in a lowered voice.

I narrowed my eyes upon hearing his words; a bloodthirsty danger flashed in my eyes, and without much hesitation, I continued, "Let them come a bit closer, then take them down cleanly!"

"Yes, Your Majesty!" Elliot responded.

I took a deep breath, leaning against the thick trunk, and looked up toward the light-emanating residence mid-way up the hillside as my brain quickly pondered the situation.

Our landing spot should have been highly concealed since no one would expect anyone to climb up from below the cliff. From what we’ve seen so far, there aren’t many man-made houses in this area, but there are numerous patrolling teams who interact closely, giving me the sense that they are protecting something.

Such arrangements are rather conspicuous; anyone not too foolish would notice that something was amiss.

I couldn’t quite grasp why Henry did this. After pondering without results, I decided to put it aside, thinking to go up and check on it myself soon!

Dealing with the Werewolf Squad on patrol was straightforward for the assault team; I didn’t even distract myself to glance again. After Elliot reported the area clear, I led them toward that residence.

Although the night reduced our visual range, it was also the best means to hide our tracks.

When I single-handedly pushed open a loosely closed window on the first floor of the building, the strangeness I sensed became even more apparent.

The house was very quiet, brightly lit but hardly anyone was moving, a stark contrast to the frequent patrols outside!

If there were no important personnel residing here, why were there so many patrol teams outside? If there were important people inside, why were there no bodyguards in the house? These two contrasting scenarios were downright contradicting.

While pondering these issues, I had already climbed through the window and entered, with Elliot right behind me, and the other Lycans tense, scanning the surroundings.

A Lycan stayed outside the window to keep watch, a tacit understanding among the assault team members.

"...I have reported everything to you just now, what do you think we should do at the moment? How should I deal with that woman?"

A Werewolf’s voice echoed from within the house, and I slowly moved closer, pressing against the wall, lowering my breath to better hear the other person’s voice.

The person opposite was silent for a while, pacing back and forth in the room as if pondering something, before finally speaking, "Are you sure she is almost 2 months pregnant? Could there have been a mistake?"

As this voice sounded, my expression stiffened, and the frown on my brow deepened, the air around me turning icy cold while my muscles tensed!

A suspicion arose in my heart, but I couldn’t fully confirm it yet.

[Is that the Empress Dowager’s voice? Why would she be here? Shall we go up and save the Empress Dowager, Your Majesty?]

Elliot used the mind-link from behind me. I turned to look at him and explained: [Wait a moment, I want to understand who the person they are talking about is!]

[Yes, Your Majesty!]

The Lycans of the assault team silently stood by my side, waiting for my command to rush in and seize the enemies inside and rescue Licia.

I was initially startled by Licia’s sudden mention, but I quickly calmed down.

I thought to myself, of course, Licia’s previous disappearance was not really due to ’pirates’; I guessed she had been invited by her own sister to visit this island.

I snorted softly under my breath, catching every word of Licia and that Werewolf’s conversation without missing a beat.

"I may not be a professional midwife, but I can still tell if a she-wolf is pregnant or not! I am absolutely certain about this, Your Highness, please rest assured!"

Upon hearing this, Licia hummed softly, seemingly accepting the explanation, while my heart hung suspended— the she-wolf they were discussing might very well be Margaret, whom I was searching for!

chapters.title: Chapter 399 Compromise
chapters.content:
Chapter 399: 399 Compromise

[Donald’s perspective]

"The most urgent thing now is her physical condition! She has just been hospitalized because of abdominal pain, and I’ve conducted an instrumental examination on her without telling anyone else, confirming my initial judgment!"

The other party paused suddenly, and I almost lost control, wanting to rush forward and grab his throat, demanding he tell me everything about Margaret!

But the last shred of my sanity woke me up, I took a deep breath and forced myself to stay calm, thankfully Licia was not completely indifferent to Margaret.

She looked toward the Medical Practitioner opposite and asked, "What judgment?"

"It’s her first pregnancy, and the timing is critical, which means her condition is extremely unstable! If this continues, there’s a high likelihood that she will miscarry!"

"What? Miscarry?" Licia seemed surprised, her voice becoming sharp.

I couldn’t control myself anymore, gripping my gun and stepping out from behind the wall, looking at the Medical Practitioner with cold murderous intent, "What’s the location of the hospital? Take me there now, or I’ll kill you!"

As I said this, I approached him, not even sparing a glance at Licia.

When she saw me and the assault team behind me, she was completely stunned, her eyes wide with disbelief, "Donald? You... how are you here? Didn’t anyone notice you coming to the island? Don’t be impulsive, this place is full of Martha and Henry’s people!"

I didn’t respond to Licia’s surprise, only looking at the Medical Practitioner with a face bleached white with fear, I moved closer, dangerously repeating, "Where is she? I’m asking you a question!"

"Lycan King! No, Your Majesty! Who... who are you looking for?" The Medical Practitioner, sweating profusely with fear, stammered to me.

"Playing dumb? Isn’t she the one you just mentioned in your talk? I’m asking you one last time, my Lycan Queen—Margaret, where is she now?" As I finished speaking, my finger on the trigger of the gun moved slightly, my eyes filled with the intent to kill.

"Donald! Let go of Simo, he’s the only one who can approach Margaret smoothly right now! Henry and Levi are guarding Margaret very strictly, and if we want to rescue her safely from the hospital, we cannot alert the people on the island!"

Licia took two steps closer to me as she spoke, her eyes imploring as she looked at me urgently.

At her words, I slowly quelled the rage welling up in my chest, glancing coldly at Licia, and said lightly, "Would you be so kind? After all, she’s Margaret, my only weakness! It’s already a surprise that you aren’t torturing her alongside Henry, and now you’re offering to help her—it’s hard to believe indeed!"

I continued without paying further attention to Licia, turning back to the Medical Practitioner and said coldly, "Take me to her now, or you won’t see tomorrow’s sun."

"I... Your Majesty! Please, calm down first and consider the Empress Dowager’s opinion! That hospital is located on the southwest side of Poison Snake Island, not far from here, haha!"

He swallowed, not daring to delay any further, "When I left the hospital just now, I noticed that the sentries and patrols nearby were especially numerous! It’s like they’ve cast a net around the Lycan Queen, with just you and the Lycans here, it’s almost impossible to break through! Even if you fight your way in with your excellent skills, the Lycan Queen cannot withstand any shaking or stimulation! For her safety and the little Highness’s sake, both I and the Empress Dowager advise against it!"

"My life was given by the Empress Dowager, and if she wishes to rescue the Lycan Queen, then I could never do anything to harm her! If you don’t trust my word, I can swear an oath to the Moon goddess!"

Licia’s eyes started to redden as she looked at me, seeing the change in my expression, she took over from Simo’s words, "Yes, Donald! Think about it, if I really wanted to harm Margaret, I would not have helped her conceal her pregnancy just now, nor would I have let Simo treat her!"

As she spoke, she stepped forward and took hold of my wrist, a tear slipping from her eyes.

I raised my hand to brush hers away, looking down at her inquiringly, with a touch of compromise in my voice, "I really don’t understand you now! Didn’t you choose to stand with Henry and Martha? Why are you extending a hand to help me now? Or is there a bigger trap behind this, waiting for me to fall into?"

"That’s right! I forgot to congratulate you when we first met..."

I paused, looking into Licia’s eyes which slowly started to show complex emotions, masking my own complexity with a sneer, "Congratulations, Mother, on reuniting with your beloved kin, Aunt Martha must be delighted to see you, right?"

I called her Aunt Martha, but felt a very strange sensation inside. I think I shouldn’t have been so sarcastic with Licia; it seemed a bit immature, like a child’s vindictiveness.

The pain and disappointment on Licia’s face flashed by, tears streaming down as if they cost nothing, "I know you hate me for my lack of firm stance, but I really can’t stand to watch you kill each other!"

chapters.title: Chapter 400 Obtain intelligence
chapters.content:
Chapter 400: 400 Obtain intelligence

[Donald’s Perspective]

"Donald, Martha is the dearest person to me in this world besides you. Do you really want me to watch her become irredeemable and relive the tragedy from back then?"

"Didn’t you also like your Uncle Dan when you were little? Have you forgotten? I often think if you could sit down and talk it out properly, maybe there could have been a different outcome..."

"Stop being naive, Licia! Your assumption can’t be put into practice. My father and I are fated to be at loggerheads with their family to the death!"

I frowned and cut off Licia’s long speech, ruthlessly tearing apart her peace fantasies, and then continued with a bitter laugh, "This matter has long since ceased to be the internal strife within the family you thought it was. It concerns the entire order and rules of the Wolf royal family and the werewolf world, a war that could lead to rivers of blood if one is not careful, and not something that can be settled by simply sitting down!"

"Besides, Henry and Martha probably won’t accept your ’peace argument’, will they? It’s not the first time I’ve asked you to see the situation for what it is, but you’re always so naive; it really makes things difficult for me! I do appreciate you having this Medical Practitioner save Margaret, but as for the rest—stop dreaming!"

After speaking, I started fiddling with the gun in my hand and then looked up at Licia again after a moment of thought.

Her eyes were red from crying, and she was much thinner than when she was in the Royal City, which made it clear that Martha and Henry hadn’t taken good care of her.

"If you want to help me, then go to the hospital to check on Margaret’s condition and tell her I’ve already landed on the island, so she can be at peace! Also, I need you and this doctor to protect her at all costs—can you do that for me?" I said gravely, examining and waiting for Licia’s response in her eyes.

Licia was silent for a few seconds before she nodded, agreeing to my request.

At this point, the Medical Practitioner named Simo also sighed in relief and looked at me and Licia, adding, "The Lycan Queen was thinking the same before. She really needs the Empress Dowager to go and talk to her personally!"

I raised my brow slightly, glanced at Simo, and said, "That doesn’t sound exactly like something Margaret would say, does it? Given Licia’s current attitude, it’s unlikely to be that mild."

Upon hearing this, Simo’s expression stiffened as if he choked on my words, his demeanor turning very unnatural.

Noticing this, I chuckled, realizing that I might have guessed correctly, and withdrew my gaze.

Licia tugged at my clothes, still wanting to ask about our next military plan, but I couldn’t fully trust her, so I remained silent, my expression indifferent, listening to Elliot continue to question Simo about the other situations on the island.

Because Licia and I were temporarily at a truce, Simo no longer hemmed and hawed, beginning to detail the situation and military deployment on Poison Snake Island!

"The land area of Poison Snake Island is quite large. The terrain in the east, west, south, and north of the island is relatively flat. Our current location is on the west side, hence the entrance here is a natural cliff pass, which is why the stationed force here is the least, making it relatively the safest place."

"The east side of the island is where the Madam usually engages in activities and work. That area is home to the bulk of the hired soldiers and is the most heavily guarded defense line. It is also the most convenient and quickest foothold for entry and exit of Poison Snake Island!"

"The situation in the north is not much different from the west, but I don’t know the specifics there, as the Madam has designated it a forbidden area and doesn’t allow personnel to roam there. The south side has been particularly lively recently; Henry’s subordinates are stationed there, that area could be considered Henry’s domain. Madam still very much cherishes her son despite their long separation!"

Simo seemed to be very familiar with the conditions on the island. After briefly finishing the introduction, he then closed his mouth and said no more.

I glanced at him without a word but a sense of suspicion arose in my heart.

"You just said you’re a Medical Practitioner, but I notice you have a high sensitivity to military defenses; you’re not as simple as you claim to be, are you?" Elliot has always been direct; after putting away his map, he stared straight at Simo and asked.

Simo, who had been frightened several times already tonight, had seemingly gotten used to it. Looking at Elliot, he replied, "This is Poison Snake Island off the coast of Lilo Sea. If I didn’t have some other skills, I would have died many times over by now! These are the very basic skills possessed by people on the island, nothing to be curious about!"

As he spoke, he looked away, his head hung as if muttering to himself, "If it weren’t for the Empress Dowager’s sudden arrival on the island, I would have found a way to leave this damned place long ago. While it’s easy to make money here, staying alive to spend it outside requires real talent."

"What are you mumbling about? Can you speak up?" Elliot, not having heard him clearly, asked with some dissatisfaction.

I glanced at my watch, thinking about the patrol team outside, and reminded in a stern voice, "It’s getting late; we should withdraw after we finish with the intelligence. I have a feeling that it’s too quiet out there—it doesn’t bode well."

chapters.title: Chapter 401 Secret Passage
chapters.content:
Chapter 401: 401 Secret Passage

[Donald’s Perspective]

Upon hearing this, Elliot repressed the excess emotions on his face, stood up, and then began to reorganize the Lycan troops.

We had achieved a substantial gain from today’s landing, so we could return to the command ship to rest briefly and regroup before continuing operations tomorrow morning!

Before leaving Licia’s residence, I stood outside the window, turned my head, and spoke softly to Licia, "I will find a way to contact you before noon tomorrow, and I hope by then that you’ll be waiting for me with news of Margaret."

After finishing, I paused for a moment, fell silent for 2 seconds, then continued, "I am not sure about Martha’s feelings for you, but Henry definitely does not truly wish to protect you! In his eyes, you might merely be the mother of his enemy, so I advise you one last time, don’t foolishly tell him everything. He is not a good man."

After saying these words, I casually closed the window by my hand, turned, and disappeared into the night.

What Elliot and I did not know was that not long after we had retreated from behind the cliff, Henry soon discovered that his patrol had been attacked, which led to a thorough search of Licia’s residence and questioning of all the personnel.

Henry arrived a step too late and didn’t obtain any useful information from Licia. He could only turn Poison Snake Island upside down in his rage, and, ultimately fruitless, was forced to enhance the island’s defenses even further.

He couldn’t have imagined that we had ascended from beneath the cliff. All his efforts were in vain.

After returning to the command ship, I leaned on the railing of the deck, my gaze deep as I observed Poison Snake Island not far away. It lay in the vast night like a cold, venomous snake lurking in waiting.

"Your Majesty, I have just received a report from the commander of the rear army. They have completed forming ranks and are only awaiting Your Majesty’s order to set off directly from the Lilo Sea crossing!" Christian stood two steps behind me, head bowed, and respectfully reported.

Upon hearing this, I reined in my wandering thoughts and, after thinking for a moment, said, "Sending the entire army at once targets too large and makes it difficult to avoid the enemy’s watch. Send word back to General Lucas leading the army to disperse the troops into three groups for transportation. The navy in the Lilo Sea has been active recently, and he can use this opportunity to accomplish the mission."

Christian nodded, noting my command. I turned to look at him, pondered for a moment, and then said, "Charlie’s Heavy Mechanical Camp needs to keep their movements hidden. They are our main force for attacking the eastern side of Poison Snake Island, and before the war truly breaks out, we cannot let the enemy know of this ace up our sleeve! Also, there is another very important matter. Before we launch a full-scale attack on Poison Snake Island, I hope to find the secret passage connecting from the Demon Domain Forest to the island that Berton mentioned."

"But of the passage’s location, we have absolutely no clues, Your Majesty! Even Berton himself said that he only heard of it and has never actually seen it. He isn’t even sure where the passage might be," Christian said, furrowing his brow with evident confusion in his eyes.

I knew what he was worried about. We were to launch a general assault on Poison Snake Island the next afternoon, and finding the location of this underwater passage before then was extremely urgent and also immensely challenging.

I raised my hand to pat the shoulder of my personal guard captain, chuckled slightly, and asked, "Do you remember the words Simo mentioned, that are recorded in Elliot’s manual?"

Naturally, Christian had seen Elliot’s preparedness manual. Upon hearing my question, he silently nodded, but his expression was still puzzled, as if he didn’t understand why I had suddenly brought this up.

I lowered my hand, no longer keeping him in suspense, and guided further, "If the information Simo provided is accurate, then the restricted area he mentioned on the northern side of the island, isn’t it incredibly intriguing?"

As I spoke, I saw a flash of insight in Christian’s eyes. He snapped his fingers, without pausing, continued, "Martha is a complex woman. The enemies, so cautious as to restrict ordinary people from moving through there, could very likely be hiding some secret! Since we are looking for the underwater passage, naturally we cannot overlook the north."

Upon hearing this, Christian nodded and, as if a thought struck him, asked, "Who do you plan to send, Your Majesty? Eric is seriously injured and is recuperating at Mr. Gino’s dock; Mark’s injuries are light, but his condition is clearly not optimal; Elliot carries the burden of both coordinating assaults and linking various camps and cannot be everywhere; previously solely responsible for the Queen’s safety, Louis is still missing; and as the Captain of Your Majesty’s personal guard force, my primary task is to ensure your safety, so I cannot leave your side."

As he spoke, his brow slowly furrowed, seeming truly at a loss for this issue.

Indeed, many Lycans had been sacrificed in our campaign to the south, and the platoon leaders around me were either injured or had already fallen in battle. The loss of personnel was quite direct, and it was precisely for this reason that we found ourselves unable to fully utilize our forces.

But I wasn’t worried about this at all, for I already had a suitable candidate in mind for the task, and he was perfectly suited for it!

chapters.title: Chapter 402 Landing on the Island Again
chapters.content:
Chapter 402: 402 Landing on the Island Again

[Donald’s Perspective]

"Anthony? Are you suggesting to assign Anthony to investigate the underwater passage? But he’s still outside now; wouldn’t it be a bit late to rush him here?"

When I mentioned Anthony’s name, Christian seemed a bit surprised but he quickly came to accept it. Recalling our previous meeting with Anthony outside, he asked me with confusion.

I took a light breath and explained, "I did have another assignment for him before, but the circumstances have changed, and investigating the passage is clearly more important now. Anthony is an excellent Beta; he’s meticulous, patient, brave, and strategic—it’s perfect to entrust him with this task!"

As I spoke, I looked back at Christian and hooked the corner of my mouth as I continued, "As for your concern that he’s outside and might not make it in time for the task... Don’t worry, I reckon you’ll see him in about 5 minutes!"

It didn’t actually take 5 minutes; not long after my words had ended, Anthony was brought up on deck by Elliot, and for safety reasons, Elizabeth didn’t come with him this time.

"Your Majesty! The Lycan troops assigned to Anthony are assembled and ready for your command,"

Elliot, who had been on duty since his return without rest, reported. I glanced at him, nodded to acknowledge I heard.

I took two steps closer to Anthony, speaking solemnly and with gravity, "Elliot has told you all the information. This mission is urgent and fraught with danger, but I trust in your abilities and am certain you will face no issues! Set off, and be cautious."

Upon hearing this, Anthony immediately knelt on one knee, with his fist against his heart, and said in a deep voice, "I offer my life and soul for Your Majesty and the Wolf royal family."

I laughed at that, clapped him on the shoulder in jest and said, "I always feel that phrase is a bit ominous now. You’d better return unharmed, or neither Elizabeth nor Margaret will let me off the hook! Go now, and may all go well."

"Yes," Anthony replied with a smile, his eyes tender like he was thinking of something, and with an agile response, he turned and walked away briskly.

Christian and I watched his retreating figure for several seconds before turning our gazes away.

"Your Majesty, you should go rest for a bit too. You’ll need to direct the entire battle later," Christian suggested timely.

I knew he was right, but how could I possibly sleep? My mind was constantly preoccupied with Margaret’s condition.

"I won’t be able to sleep lying down; I need to re-evaluate the battle plan for later. You’ve also been worn out these past few days, go take a rest yourself," I replied.

Christian shook his head upon hearing this and respectfully said, "I’m not tired. I’ll stay with you."

Seeing his response, I didn’t push further, hummed acknowledgment and with hands in pockets, returned inside the cabin. This battle would be the biggest and most difficult one I have ever commanded in my life. It would be a lie to say I wasn’t nervous at all, for no one is unafraid of failure. But a life-or-death showdown between Martha and myself was inevitable.

As day broke and Poison Snake Island’s silhouette lost its mysterious and treacherous appearance, what revealed itself before us was its most common aspect. This time we didn’t anchor the command ship, but boldly stopped right under the cliffs. Due to the darkness last night, we hadn’t noticed a significant protrusion of rock at the cliff’s base!

It was about a dozen meters above the sea level, perfect for mooring the command ship.

"Is the Moon goddess aiding us? Our battle today will surely be a triumphant victory!" Elliot, touching the military dagger at his waist, said spiritedly.

I moved my somewhat stiff neck, raised my eyebrows without saying a word, and gave the order to all to disembark onto the island!

Thanks to yesterday’s reconnaissance, our landing on the island was much smoother, even though the enemy’s patrols and defenses had clearly increased.

We cleared out many werewolves on our way but became cautious as we neared the hospital Simo referred to! He was right; Henry’s men had fortified it into an impenetrable defense.

I crouched low in the bushes, observing the enemy’s defense line through binoculars.

"The hospital is backed by a mountain range, and the enemy has checkpoints all along the way in. We have no choice but to break through by force," Elliot reported, his brow furrowed in cool analysis.

I put down my binoculars, glanced at my watch, and said, "A frontal assault definitely won’t work. By the time we break in, Henry might have moved Margaret. Or he might use Margaret to threaten me, and that would put me at a disadvantage."

I swallowed the rest of that thought, turned to Christian, and asked, "Has there been any response from the squads assigned to assist the main army?"

chapters.title: Chapter 403: Forbidden stimulus
chapters.content:
Chapter 403: Forbidden stimulus

[Donald’s Perspective]

Upon hearing this, Christian responded, "We’ve already reported back to Your Majesty! They said it would take at most half an hour to sail out of the Mist Sea Region, and a full frontal assault on the island can certainly go ahead according to the battle plan."

After hearing this, I nodded slightly and said in a deep voice, "That’s good. When the entire island’s attention is focused on the eastern side, that’s when we will catch them fully unprepared!"

This was the safest plan I could come up with. When the main force launched a frontal assault on the island, Henry would definitely be distracted. Even if he was truly meticulous and prepared for defense, breaching the defenses of this small hospital would still be very simple with the Lycans from my assault team and personal guard force at hand.

The only challenge for me was how to do all this without hurting Margaret in the slightest.

But what I could not have anticipated was that at the very moment I was trying all means to avoid this situation, Margaret had already been forced to resort to "self-harm" as a means to achieve her goal of making contact with Simo.

This half-hour truly made me feel as if seconds were dragging by like years. I would look at my wristwatch every minute, also waiting for the notification of the attack on the island the moment it began!

"Your Majesty! Don’t you find the female nurse who just came out a bit odd?" Christian, whose mind was always sharp, seemed to have noticed something and promptly said to me while squeezing the binoculars.

At his words, I lifted my gaze and picked up the binoculars in my hand.

Soon, I understood why Christian thought she was strange. What kind of hospital nurse leaves work without changing out of her clothes or taking off her mask? At least the two nurses a short distance behind her weren’t doing that.

After a careful look, I suddenly recognized the figure as being familiar. Narrowing my eyes slightly, I immediately said, "It’s Licia! Elliot, take two Lycans and follow her right away, bring her to me. Remember, don’t alarm anyone else."

Elliot promptly left to carry out the order, and I slowly furrowed my eyebrows, a bad premonition rising in my heart.

Sure enough, before Elliot had gone long, a new situation emerged. "Your Majesty! It looks like there’s some footage being displayed on the monitor above the hospital entrance... it seems a bit like surveillance footage!"

My heart gave a fierce jolt, and a very strange feeling washed over me. I immediately turned to look!

The moment I saw the very familiar figure on the screen, I was so shocked I almost stood up from the bushes, but thankfully Christian’s level voice was very timely in waking me.

"Is that the Lycan Queen? Why are they suddenly broadcasting the Lycan Queen’s surveillance footage? What are they trying to do?" Christian asked, his eyes full of confusion.

I took a few deep breaths to barely suppress the urge to rush over and rescue her, my eyes glued to the surveillance screen showing Margaret.

Suddenly, Christian confirmed that it was indeed a live surveillance feed, but as for why the enemy would make such a strange move, we had no way of knowing at the moment!

With the aid of the binoculars, I could see the hospital’s front entrance very clearly, even though our position was extremely far from the hospital.

The monitor’s footage wasn’t particularly high-definition, but I still noticed that Margaret’s face was pale. The white thing on her forehead had to be a bandage, and such bandaging only indicated one thing—she was hurt!

I forced myself to suppress the anger and killing intent in my chest, staring at the surveillance screen with an icy demeanor, as Christian, somewhat worried, looked at me. I, however, gripped the binoculars without moving an inch, like a cold statue.

My dear Margaret, curled up on a simple hospital bed, looked incredibly thin and fragile. After Henry had people take her away, he had not treated her well at all!

Yes! How could I have such a naive idea? How could Henry, that madman, treat Margaret well? He probably couldn’t wait to torment Margaret to death, satisfying his vindictive, narrow-minded, and insane psychopathy!

"Margaret..." I muttered her name, my heart wrenching as I looked at her now blurry profile.

Noticing the change in my emotions, Christian leaned in a little and calmly reminded me, "The enemy might be doing this to provoke us. We can’t fall for it, Your Majesty! There are only 10 minutes left before the attack on the island begins. Can you hold on a little longer? At least now we can be certain of the Lycan Queen’s safety. That’s not entirely bad news!"

I didn’t respond to Christian’s reminder immediately; I just stared at the display screen, my other fist clenching tighter and tighter.

"Your Majesty! It’s Levi from the Spirit Dragon Pack! He’s appeared on the screen too."

Before the nearby Lycan alerted me, I had already seen Levi’s face through the binoculars.

It seemed he had interrupted the surveillance footage around Margaret and appeared on the screen with an incredibly clear image. I could even see the reflected footage in his eyes through his gaze.

Levi was sitting in front of a computer, his expression mocking as he said something to someone nearby, "Has the footage been aired? Can I start talking now? Alright then."

As he spoke, he stared straight at me, curling his lips and then continued, "Can you see me, Donald? I know you’re out there. Don’t you want to come in and see Margaret? These past two days have been exceptionally tough for her..."

chapters.title: Chapter 404 Provocation
chapters.content:
Chapter 404: 404 Provocation

[Donald’s Perspective]

Levi, of course, didn’t actually see me. He was just neurotically certain that I could see this footage. His face, unmasked in its mocking and provocative expression, stared into the camera with a dangerously provocative air!

I gripped the binoculars tightly, squinting as I suppressed the violence within me.

"Speaking of which, if I hadn’t discovered it in time, this foolish woman would have played herself to death! Did you get a clear look at those injuries on her? The ones on her forehead, inside her right palm?"

As Levi spoke, the smile on his face widened, as if he had discovered something delightfully joyful, but a closer look would reveal the complex darkness in his eyes.

"She nearly died last night! I mean, if Henry and I weren’t so kind as to find a Medical Practitioner to treat her. Henry said you care about this woman more than your own life, which I hardly believe. Still, now you have a perfect chance to prove it to me! Come out, I know you’re out there."

At that moment, Levi seemed mad—his chilling eyes were fixed on the camera, as if trying to murder me directly through it!

Christian looked at me anxiously, worried that I might lose my mind and recklessly charge forward to kill Levi, who kept provoking me, but I didn’t.

I scoffed, a mocking smile curling at my lips, "You certainly think too highly of yourself. Why should I prove how I feel about Margaret to you? Henry is wiser than you are. He wouldn’t engage in such pointless, meaningless actions."

I quietly ridiculed Levi’s clownish antics. Yet, deep down, I wasn’t as calm as I appeared. I knew that Levi and Henry were capable of anything, and his brazen provocations now could mean he had other plans in store!

So, I must rescue Margaret from his clutches soon. Starting a conflict might divert their attention, but it wasn’t a perfect solution.

I slowly formed other ideas in my mind and stopped focusing on the screen through the binoculars.

"Your Majesty!" Christian said at this moment. "Elliot has arrived with the Empress Dowager. Let’s listen to what the Empress has to say about the situation inside? I believe Levi won’t do anything to the Lycan Queen unless you show up."

Upon hearing this, I turned to look at Elliot and his entourage nearby, then sat up straight and spoke softly to Christian, "Take a few Lycans around to the hillside behind the hospital, see if you can find a way down from above. I’ll cover you from the front. Once inside, act according to the situation and locate Margaret immediately, understand?"

Appearing surprised by my sudden command, Christian hesitated momentarily before nodding. He then took four agile Lycans and departed immediately. In such a critical time, he certainly wouldn’t disobey any of my orders.

Elliot came back to my side just as Christian and his group had left. He glanced briefly before leading Licia straight to me and reported, "Your Majesty! I have brought the Empress Dowager back as you asked. She says she has something important to tell you!"

Hearing this, I looked at Licia with a tense demeanor behind him and spoke, "Cut to the chase. You saw Margaret inside, didn’t you? Is she injured, how bad? Which room is she in?"

Licia lifted her eyes to meet mine, her expression slightly relaxed as she replied, "I saw her! But..."

My heart jumped at Licia’s ’but’, my gaze sharpening with urgency, silently urging her to continue.

Licia lowered her eyes as if contemplating how to explain Margaret’s situation. Unable to contain my anxiety, I was about to prompt her directly when Licia took a deep breath and said solemnly, "Levi is keeping a very close watch on Margaret, not taking his eyes off her for a moment! After I slipped in with Simo, Margaret recognized me at a glance."

"But Levi was in the room at the time, so it wasn’t possible to communicate. Thinking quickly, she feigned an attempt to stab Levi and then pretended to be overpowered, injuring her own palm, which Simo seized as a chance to take to the pharmacy to treat the injury... I’ve told her all about your situation, so don’t worry too much!"

After Licia finished, I felt as if my heart had been gripped tightly, throbbing with pain while a fierce anger blazed within me, "’Stabbing Levi,’ ’Injuring her palm’? So you didn’t think of any strategies once inside and relied on Margaret’s self-harm to exchange information successfully?"

My eyes reddening uncontrollably, I turned my head and said coldly, "We’re short on time, so I’ll spare the harsh words. Just tell me, which room is Margaret confined in?"

chapters.title: Chapter 405 Corridor Combat
chapters.content:
Chapter 405: 405 Corridor Combat

[Donald’s Perspective]

"After entering the hospital, take the longest corridor on your left, keep going, and it’s the second-to-last ward," Licia replied without hesitation this time.

As she spoke, she looked up at me, her eyes red, and tugged at the corner of my cloak. "I won’t stop you from saving Margaret, but Levi has turned the place into a dragon’s lair. It’s full of dangers once you’re inside, so be very careful! I know I can’t dissuade you or change anyone else’s mind, but Margaret is the most innocent and pitiful person in this grudge; she shouldn’t suffer all this. It’s right for you to rescue her."

Upon hearing this, I lowered my head to look at Licia, without giving a response. I was somewhat baffled by Licia now, as she actually seemed to care for Margaret!

As if guessing my surprise and confusion, Licia gave a self-mocking smile and continued, "When I was first pregnant with you, I was as unstable as Margaret. She can’t get proper care here. For the sake of my beloved grandson and both of your children, I will help you complete this task this time!"

I silently looked at Licia for a while, then stood up from the ground, made a departure gesture to Elliot, and lightly said to Licia, "No need! Just take good care of yourself and stay out of the conflict between us. I would be very grateful. I will have some Lycans escort you off the island immediately. Please leave this place of contention early, consider this my final request, okay?"

Upon hearing this, Licia cried, her tears uncontrollable. She nodded slightly, as if agreeing to my suggestion.

I quietly breathed a sigh of relief and gestured to the Lycans nearby to take her to a safe place.

Talking to Licia didn’t take much of my time. Five minutes after Christian had left, I also began to forcefully breach the hospital’s defense line with the Lycans at my side!

They were well-equipped, immediately raising their guns to guard upon spotting us, and notified all the werewolf soldiers behind them at once.

Elliot’s assault team, the fiercest and most formidable among all the Lycans of Royal City, even my personal guard force’s Lycans were slightly inferior to them, let alone these ordinary werewolf soldiers. Facing the combined forces of the assault team and personal guard force, they were like children who had just learned to walk!

With the full protection of many Lycans, Wolf’s silvery-white figure among these werewolf soldiers looked as if he was walking on flat ground.

["These guys’ combat strength is really lacking. Why did Licia say it was so dangerous here? I don’t feel it at all! Although it’s only been a few days since I’ve seen Margaret, it feels like centuries have passed. If I come across the one who hurt Margaret later, I’ll definitely kill them with one swipe of my paw!"] Wolf muttered to himself, his shadow darting through the enemies like a silvery-white blade.

I didn’t respond to Wolf’s words, just sharpened my gaze towards the hospital doors! Wolf is my wolf, so naturally, his feelings are the truest reflection of my own inner thoughts.

Elliot remained vigilant, guarding my surroundings; everywhere he passed was filled with enemy blood and corpses.

"Your Majesty! Let me go in first to check the situation inside," Elliot suggested. "Christian just notified me via mind-link that they have entered the hospital, but Levi has discovered them, and they are currently fighting!"

At his words, I paused momentarily, taking a sharp breath and quickly replied, "Just storm in and join them. Your primary task later won’t be to protect me but to find Margaret’s ward and guard it until I arrive."

Elliot seemed about to say something else but I stopped him with a glance, "These people can’t get close to me; I don’t need that many people to guard my safety. Finding Margaret is what’s most important, understood?"

After hearing my command, Elliot’s expression turned solemn. He nodded ’yes’ and charged ahead to lead the way!

The two heavy machine gunners at the hospital entrance were swiftly taken out by my Lycans’ bullets, allowing us to easily enter the hospital corridor. With Elliot leading the assault team, my path became remarkably smooth—if only we could ignore the occasional monsters that popped out to launch sneak attacks!

["What on earth are these monsters? Why are they so disgusting? My claws couldn’t even slash through its neck; can it not be killed?"] Wolf asked, puzzled and slightly irate.

I looked around at the corridor environment and spoke directly, ["Poison Snake Island is essentially Henry’s lair. It’s not strange that such creatures are here. Haven’t you seen them before? The heart and brain are their only weak spots. Just attack these parts directly!"]

As I was speaking, a sudden figure flashed in the corner of my eyes, and I urgently warned, ["To the left!"]

Wolf reacted swiftly, slightly sidestepping to dodge the attack and, as the creature was about to land, flashed his claws and fiercely swatted it away!

chapters.title: Chapter 406 Tilted Balance
chapters.content:
Chapter 406: 406 Tilted Balance

[Donald’s Perspective]

With a loud bang, the werewolf that Wolf had knocked away smashed through the right side of the infirmary door, and quickly, dozens of dark gun muzzles peeked out. At this moment, doors on both sides of the corridor also opened, and werewolf soldiers swarmed out, their bloodthirsty wolf eyes and gun barrels aimed directly at me.

Elliot also noticed this situation, and just as he turned to help me, he seemed to remember my strict order from earlier, clenched his teeth, and ignoring the enemy’s pincher attack from both sides, led the assault team through a trail of blood and broke out!

I took a deep breath and remained motionless, watching the dense crowd of enemies in front of me.

"I thought that the mighty Lycan King could keep his cool? It seems you’re just like the rest, Donald."

Without a doubt, the one who would start a conversation in such a sardonic tone could only be the ever-boastful and insane Levi!

Henry is a bit more composed than this guy. Henry is both hypocritical and brutal, highly unstable emotionally, but Levi is just foolish and insane.

Seeing me silent, a crack appeared in Levi’s arrogant expression, and a chilling intent to kill surfaced in his wolf eyes, "The first Lycan who rushed in was the captain of your personal guard force, right? I’ve heard about him from Henry. He is quite skilled and clever enough, truly fitting to be your subordinate!"

"But do you think by sending them in there you can meet Margaret? Then you’re too naive! How could I possibly let you rescue her so easily..."

Not really wanting to hear Levi’s villainous cliché speech, I coldly interrupted him, "You didn’t want me entering the hospital, but I’m in! Your ’impossibles’ seem like a piece of tissue paper that could be easily punctured, and are not worth my time to listen to."

I said, lifting my wolf head high, looking down on Levi with scorn, and scoffed, continuing, "Is this all you have to stop me? Honestly, it doesn’t look like much."

I threw Levi’s words back at him, watching his face change expressions, as if admiring it, his eyes becoming harsh, and a crazy murderous intent slowly building up!

Suddenly, a werewolf approached Levi from beside him, the stranger’s expression frantic as he whispered something to him, and then I saw shock and disbelief flash across Levi’s face.

’The east side of the island was suddenly attacked by a large army, the Lady’s soldiers sustained heavy casualties, Henry has already rushed to support, but our people report he can’t hold out much longer! We need to make plans for ourselves now, Alpha!’

I am somewhat proficient in reading lips, and this was the content of the werewolf’s words to Levi.

It seems Lucas has successfully landed on the island, and the prologue to assaulting Poison Snake Island had officially begun! Martha and Henry on the east end of the island were preoccupied, leaving only Levi to stop me from rescuing Margaret.

With such thoughts running through my mind, my gaze toward Levi grew deeper.

"I will take Margaret with me, and you can’t stop me! Henry is now overwhelmed by a direct battle and certainly cannot spare any effort to support you. In other words, guarding Margaret now is pointless for you, and the downfall of Poison Snake Island is today. Henry is done for, Martha too, but you still have one last bit of hope!"

I said, pausing for a moment, then shifting back to human form, sneeringly looking across at Levi.

Levi repressed the emotions leaking out of his face, looked up at me, and was silent, pondering something.

The atmosphere in the corridor tensed up for a moment, and I, leading a dozen Lycans behind me, stood our ground, confronting Levi’s faction head-on.

I wasn’t sure what Levi was thinking at that moment, but we needed to buy more time for Christian and Elliot, and whether we could successfully rescue Margaret would depend on today’s attempt!

Two minutes later, the sound of cannon fire exploded outside the hospital, gunfire dense like falling rain, and the normal werewolves opposite me started to grow restless; most of them were Levi’s underlings, werewolves of the Spirit Dragon pack.

Simo mentioned that Poison Snake Island had been roughly divided into three factions; not to digress on the complex connection between Martha and Henry, Levi was unlikely to be truly aligned with them. He represented the Spirit Dragon pack, naturally different from those madmen who only wanted vengeance!

As the situation shifted and the scales of battle began to tilt in my favor, he should seriously consider whether to ’live and die’ with Henry.

"Overthrowing Poison Snake Island, capturing the rebel Princess Lington, and killing the traitor Henry, are the main objectives for initiating this war! Now that the situation has changed, Margaret is of no use to you anymore, and continuing to guard her is pointless. Give up, let me through now, and I will not annihilate your Spirit Dragon pack!"

I looked into Levi’s eyes, offering a proposition that he and the werewolves behind him could hardly refuse. As the sounds of battle outside grew louder, the inner turmoil of the werewolves before me became more evident.

chapters.title: Chapter 407 Damn it!
chapters.content:
Chapter 407: 407 Damn it!

[Donald’s Perspective]

Their gazes were no longer firm, their occasional glances towards Levi made this Alpha all the more passive.

"Such a golden opportunity comes but once, and if you don’t make a stand now, don’t blame me for being inhospitable," I said aloud, raising the gun in my hand, pointing it straight at Levi’s heart.

Levi remained silent, his eyes dark and full of resentment as he looked at me. I sensed something in the pit of my stomach, a bad premonition quickly rising. "You wouldn’t be foolish enough to cooperate with Henry and move Margaret’s location, would you? Do you know what the consequences of angering me are?"

I tested him with my question. Levi slowly curved his lips into a grin, suddenly replying, "You’re not as stupid as I thought. I didn’t think you’d notice our stalling tactic!"

My facial expression swiftly turned cold at his words, my eyes filling with murderous intent as I locked gaze with Levi.

[My King! Can you hear me? The Lycan Queen isn’t in this hospital room, we’ve been deceived by Levi’s illusion, they’ve moved her somewhere else and we don’t know where!]

[My King! We’ve spotted suspicious footprints on the exterior wall of the hospital room, they must have moved her out through the window! I’m already hot on their trail with my men, get out of there quickly!]

The unstable mind-link that had plagued me ever since we landed on the island suddenly returned to normal, and I heard the reports from Elliot and Christian, one after the other.

Christian had indeed arrived one step ahead of Elliot and, being more meticulous, it wasn’t surprising he discovered more clues.

[Chase them down! At any cost, don’t let any leads slip through, bring Margaret back safely.] I ordered while without hesitation, I fired at Levi.

’Bang bang bang!’

This despicable and cunning werewolf deserved to die, daring to mock me in such a way, I was going to show him what the true wrath of the Lycan King was like, to see if he could endure it!

"Kill them all, leave none alive," I commanded the Lycans behind me, leading the charge with a new type of submachine gun that was terrifyingly deadly.

My target was the hastily retreating Levi. He and his subordinates had to die in this hospital today.

When rage reached its peak, I became absolutely cold and rational. Flanked by two trusted aide Lycans, I quickly caught up with Levi at the end of the corridor.

His subordinates tried to cover for him to escape through the window, but were suddenly killed by bullets flying in from outside, their blood and brains splattering on Levi’s cheek. Frozen in place by the scalding heat, Levi turned around with a maniacal laugh, glaring back at me!

The roaring of helicopters came from beyond the wall, and I could even hear Charlie shouting through a loudspeaker.

The Heavy Mechanical Camp had breached the eastern defenses and was coming to back me up, sooner than I expected.

"Margaret once asked me a similar question, but I never answered directly. I just said it was too late, that I could no longer turn back... Donald! You are truly lucky, the Moon goddess’s special favor towards you makes me envious. If I had met someone like Margaret earlier, I might not have been so greedy to work with Henry, stepping deeper into his dream filled with sin and blood!"

While speaking, Levi put a hand over his bloodshot eyes, his arm trembling slightly as he grasped the gun, his words and actions seemingly out of control.

I watched him impassively, my face cold and unmoved by his words.

Suddenly, Levi flung the hand from his eyes and made a shot at me with a twisted head, but before his finger could pull the trigger, a sniper from the helicopter outside shot through his wrist. Overwhelmed by the acute pain, Levi let go of the gun, his face twisted in agony as he groaned.

Seizing the perfect opportunity, I charged forward and mercilessly struck his head with the butt of my gun, following with a series of fierce punches to his face and nose. Only when I felt Levi’s nose break and my fists were covered in blood did I stop, panting heavily, grabbing his neck to force him to look at me and shouted fiercely, "Where did Henry take her? Speak!"

Levi’s consciousness was no longer clear, his eyes closed, his face covered in blood, yet he still managed to give me a defiant, mocking smile.

Seeing this, I let go, ready to give him a taste of a bullet from my gun, but a Lycan standing behind me stopped my impulsive action!

"My King! Keeping him alive allows us to interrogate about Henry’s whereabouts; it’s more valuable than killing him outright! Calm down, Squad Leader Benjamin will surely pry his mouth open!"

I admit I wanted to kill this madman directly, but my subordinate’s suggestion was most logical. I took a moment to catch my breath, my hand gripping the pistol remained motionless.

After a few seconds, I kicked Levi on the ground viciously and shot both his thighs, demanding coldly, "Notify Benjamin to come here, I want to know where Levi helped Henry take Margaret as soon as possible!"

chapters.title: Chapter 408: Irreversible damage
chapters.content:
Chapter 408: Irreversible damage

[Donald’s Perspective]

Because the plan I had made for the assault on the hospital was so sudden, Benjamin, who was a Medical Practitioner, did not join the battle with the team. I had left him outside the hospital, ready to respond to my call at any moment.

The Lycan who had just spoken out to stop me had already rushed out with my orders. I slowly put away the gun in my hand and coldly looked at Levi on the ground. I turned to the Lycans still on alert behind me and said in a deep voice, "Tie him up, I want to interrogate him personally later. Kill all the werewolf prisoners from the Spirit Dragon Pack on the island directly. The battlefield is bloody and cruel; they should pay with their lives for their foolish leader and their own desecration of the Royal Family!"

Levi’s blood stained the floor of the corridor red, and I frowned in disgust, turning my head to look at Charlie on the helicopter outside the window.

The noisy sound of the rotors came through the wind was blowing through my blonde hair. I gestured at Charlie to come inside the hospital to meet up.

The Lycans quickly cleared out a ward. Levi, looking like a bloodied gourd, was tied to a chair, his blood staining the floor. Benjamin administered a life-sustaining drug to him, and after using the Truth Potion, he finally began to speak coherently!

"No... don’t hurt Margaret! Let her go, uh!"

"Henry! You shouldn’t have lied to me, you bastard! The Spirit Dragon pack... my people are innocent, you can’t treat them like this!"

"Ahh! I’m going to kill you! Kill you!"

Levi mumbled some incoherent words, his broken face still showing traces of immense pain. Benjamin crouched in front of him with a cold expression and sternly asked, "Who took Margaret from the hospital? Where did Henry flee to after his defeat on the east side of the island? Princess Linton, Martha, has not shown herself throughout the battle; where is she now?"

The disheveled Levi did not immediately respond to Benjamin’s questions, even under the influence of the Truth Potion. Seeing this, I slightly narrowed my eyes, the dangerous killing intent churning within me.

"It seems his willpower is indeed very strong," I said, standing upright. I beckoned to another Medical Practitioner beside Benjamin and continued coldly, "Give him another dose, I don’t believe his bones are that tough."

Benjamin did not oppose my order, even though he knew that an overdose of the Truth Potion could potentially cause irreversible damage to the werewolf’s body.

Throughout this entire process, Charlie, the head of the Royal City’s military mechanized camp, remained composed. Apart from reporting the course of the battle to me, he didn’t speak any unnecessary words.

At this moment, I did not realize how intimidating my own presence was. Other than Benjamin, who always followed by my side, no one else in the room dared to speak much, even their breathing seemed too afraid to be too loud!

"Eh ah!"

That was Levi’s pained scream. The drug was attacking his nerves, and Benjamin did not stop questioning him for a second, looking just like a relentless demon!

Of course, so was I.

"It was... it was Austin who took Margaret! I put a little sleeping medicine in the water she drank, and after she fell asleep, Austin took her out from outside."

"Henry? Henry won’t let Margaret go easily; if he is leaving, he will definitely take Margaret with him. North... the north side, that’s the last escape route Martha left for herself. She will take Henry with her! They must have gone there!"

"Damn Henry! I shouldn’t have trusted him; he said Donald was not to be feared... but the defensive line in the east was so easily broken, he lied to me! He deserves to die, he is a devil!"

Levi started spouting illogical nonsense, tortured by the medication to the point of nearly going mad. One moment he blamed Henry, and the next it seemed like he hoped Henry would come back to rescue him. His whole demeanor was caught in a state of self-attack and contradiction!

Benjamin quietly watched him for a long while, then finally straightened up and turned to look at me, furrowing his brow and reporting in a grave tone, "He’s done for. His brain nerves have been damaged by the medication, and he’ll either be a madman or a fool from now on!"

I did not care about Levi’s fate. I just looked at Benjamin and asked, "So, can we still trust the information he just provided? I mean about Austin taking Margaret and them leaving through that ’escape route’?"

Benjamin, of course, knew what I wanted to ask. He nodded and directly said, "You can believe it, Your Majesty! Although the medication has attacked his brain nerves, causing irreparable nerve damage, everything he just said was his most genuine inner thoughts; it was all the truth."

Hearing this, I glanced sideways at Levi, barely clinging to life in the chair, and after a few seconds of silence, I spoke, "Order Luke’s forces to perform a thorough search and arrest of the rebel forces on the island. Execute those who seek to resist stubbornly on the spot and initially detain those who surrender. I’ll deal with them slowly after they are brought out."

chapters.title: Chapter 409 Quirky
chapters.content:
Chapter 409: 409 Quirky

[Donald’s Perspective]

"Charlie, you bring the heavy machinery troops along, I’m going down that passage to snatch Margaret back! Benjamin, you’ll follow my lead as well."

As I spoke, I turned and walked out of the ward, with Charlie and Benjamin respectfully answering "Yes" behind me before hastily catching up.

As I stepped out of the hospital door, I took the communicator handed over by my trusted aide, Lycan.

"Captain Christian has sent back a message; they’ve been tracking the enemy and discovered that they are retreating towards the northern area of Poison Snake Island!" Lycan reported succinctly.

Upon hearing this, I nodded, clutched the communicator silently for a moment, then from the corner of my eye, saw Elliot with the assault team emerging from the hospital and I ordered in a deep voice, "The entire army, assemble and depart for the submarine passage on the north side of the island."

After giving the order, I boarded the helicopter, with Benjamin, Charlie, and the last to arrive, Elliot, sitting behind me.

There were a total of 12 helicopters for the heavy machinery troops; aside from the one carrying Charlie, the rest had been circling above the hospital. Once they saw the commander’s helicopter take off, they, along with the Lycans and warriors on the ground, quickly followed suit.

I had not overlooked contacting Anthony directly, but he is an extremely cautious and meticulous warrior. If he hadn’t volunteered any updates on the submarine passage mission to me, it meant the time wasn’t ripe, or perhaps he was too busy to report to me at the moment!

Considering the latter possibility, my nerves tensed slightly as my gaze out the window grew dark and profound.

After today’s warfare, Poison Snake Island wasn’t some enigmatic realm to me anymore; it was just a broken isle about to be completely conquered by my forces!

Perhaps the atmosphere in the cabin was too severe and silent, for Charlie couldn’t help but speak out loud, "Your Majesty, if I may say so, I find today’s entire operational process rather peculiar."

I certainly heard Charlie’s words and turned my head with a serene expression, gesturing for him to continue.

Swallowing hard, he spoke in a grave tone, "General Lucas hasn’t fought against the Werewolf army modified by Henry before, so he doesn’t quite understand what it’s all about, but I noticed that the ones we encountered today weren’t as strong as before!
