If the strength of the monsters transformed by Henry were classified on a scale of ten, those we met before were at level eight or even nine, but the strongest ones we encountered today were only around level six!"

"I’m unsure if there may be other reasons, but the battle for Poison Snake Island is a matter of life and death, and it’s also his and Martha’s last combat choke point. Isn’t it too abnormal for him to ’wrap up’ like this? To be honest, Your Majesty, I joined the battle today prepared to sacrifice the entire mechanical battalion if necessary to take down Poison Snake Island, but I didn’t expect it to be so..."

Charlie paused, searching for the right words, then took a deep breath and continued looking at me, "I don’t know if it’s appropriate to say this, but I feel that our operation this time went a bit too smoothly, almost like child’s play!"

I patiently listened to Charlie give his extended remarks.

As he finished his last few sentences, he carefully observed my expression to see if I became angry or had any other reaction; seeing none, he quietly heaved a sigh of relief.

I leaned back in my chair, took a deep breath, and responded gravely, "Your feelings are justified. Although we seem to have emerged victorious on the surface, Henry managed to escape during the battle, and Martha didn’t show her face from beginning to end. I went to great lengths to organize the raid on the hospital, only to be thoroughly mocked by them in the end!"

"They abandoned an ally who had lost his usefulness but preserved most of their own strength. How is that not another kind of victory?"

As I spoke, a mocking smile curled at the corners of my mouth, and I opened my eyes to look at him and Elliot beside him, "Get ready, the real battle is coming next. They must want to use the submarine passage to safely evacuate from Poison Snake Island, but with so many troops and monsters that don’t completely obey commands, it won’t be an easy task for them to execute a rapid retreat."

Since boarding the helicopter, Elliot’s mood had been down, as if he was quite dissatisfied with his performance in the recent rescue mission. He, like me, detested being toyed with by others and felt a mix of anger and self-reproach, wondering why he hadn’t detected the enemy’s plot sooner.

Having listened to my analysis of the current situation, he turned his body to face me, his eyes blazing with a battle-ready spirit, and boldly said, "What do we need to do next? Just give your orders, Your Majesty! That despicable villain Henry dares to toy with us like this, I must kill him!"

At his words, I raised an eyebrow and curled my mouth slightly, calmly replying, "What’s the rush now? Just follow my command later."

chapters.title: Chapter 410 Forest Backland
chapters.content:
Chapter 410: 410 Forest Backland

[Donald’s Perspective]

"The reason you hate Henry so much, beyond his committing so many evils, must also have to do with his previous exploitation of Angel, right?"

As if not expecting me to suddenly bring up another person, a pause flashed across Elliot’s face.

He quickly reined in his emotions and looked at me with clear eyes, explaining earnestly, "I know Angel has committed a serious crime and doesn’t deserve Your Majesty’s forgiveness. But she is still my sister, a friend and comrade who grew up with me since we were children!"

"It’s only natural that she has become what she is now and deserves her fate, but behind all this, can Henry truly remain invisible? In my view, he is the root of all evil, and he deserves death."

I didn’t oppose Elliot’s speech but gazed at him with a calm expression.

Seeing this, he took a sharp breath and continued, "Perhaps you are unaware, Your Highness, that the Lycan Queen previously used ’New Birth’ to save Angel’s life! I guess at that time, you wanted Angel to come to her senses and accept the punishment she deserved. Everyone should be responsible for their actions – Angel, Henry, and Austin alike, and all the traitors who dare to challenge the authority of the Wolf royal family, the same goes for them!"

Clearly agitated by the end of his speech, Elliot saw me remain composed and I let out a soft sigh after a long pause, saying, "I understand all that you’re saying; I never said Henry didn’t deserve to die."

Pausing, I raised my eyebrows and chuckled lightly, "Who said I didn’t know about the thing Margaret did? I knew it right away and wasn’t the least bit surprised. Do you know why?"

Not expecting an answer from anyone, I answered my own question, "Because that’s just the kind of exceptional person she is, filled with a sense of justice and at the same time extremely kind and innocent!"

On this point, Benjamin and Elliot deeply agreed, silently nodding their heads as the atmosphere in the cabin fell somewhat silent for a moment.

Sitting up straight, I looked out the window, frowning and commanding in a deep voice, "We’ve arrived at the ’Forbidden Area of Poison Snake Island’; everyone be on guard, the fight is about to begin!"

As my words landed, the helicopter steadied itself above the dense forest, and while waiting for the Lycans beside me to rappel down one by one, I surveyed the situation below with my eyes.

[Do you sense Margaret’s presence?] I asked Wolf.

Wolf seemed to let out a deep sigh, complaining, [Ever since we arrived at this damned place, not to mention the werewolf mind-link, even regular communication signals are gone! Truly a weird place where not even birds would defecate.]

Hearing this, I fell silent, not surprised at all. If the enemy could silently move Margaret from right under my watch and cut off mind-link communication, these are old tactics that Henry has used before!

I hadn’t noticed anything strange about this forest from the plane, but after setting foot on this land, I could sense its differences.

"Have everyone put on their gas masks; this miasma is poisonous," I commanded, hooking the mask from around my neck and again appreciating Benjamin’s attention to detail.

He’d mentioned before about the dense vegetation and abundant rainfall on Poison Snake Island, suggesting that poisonous miasma might fester in the woods, and had the person in charge of battle supplies prepare these in advance, even for Anthony’s troop, who had left early this morning.

The helicopter continued to hover overhead, with its military-grade infrared sensors scouring for other life forms in the forest as we conducted a meticulous sweep through the woods in a straight line.

Suddenly, my communicator on my waist vibrated violently, and my heart tightened, knowing that only Anthony or Christian could be calling via satellite phone now.

I smoothly extended the communicator’s signal receiving wire and replied in a deep voice, "This is Donald, speak!"

"Your Majesty! It’s Christian, I can hear the roar of your helicopter, I am in the basin 800 meters northwest of you, you can come directly with the helicopter, we need your help here!"

Christian’s report was clear and concise. After I acknowledged, I hung up the satellite phone and turned to Elliot and Charlie nearby, directing, "Objective is an 800-meter forest basin, move quickly!"

After speaking, I completed a swift transformation and, as the werewolf, led everyone as well as the overhead helicopter swiftly through the woods.

It didn’t take long before I arrived at the basin Christian had mentioned.

Once I saw the scene before me, I was so shocked that I froze in place, taking a moment before I moved my paws and sprinted towards him.

Christian was covered in fresh blood, standing in front of a massive black cave that looked like hell itself, his face full of icy chill, the gun in his hand still dripping blood. He stood motionless, confronting the ’enemies’ at the cave’s entrance, as if he had been in a standoff for a long time!

Arriving beside Christian, I transformed back into my human form and asked directly, "Have you seen Austin or the others? Levi said it was Austin who took Margaret from the hospital!"

"What’s going on here? Have all of Henry and Martha’s people retreated inside? What’s the meaning of leaving behind these disgusting monsters; do they intend for this horde of creatures to cover their retreat?" As I spoke to Christian, I stared coldly at the monsters before us.

chapters.title: Chapter 411 ’dog species
chapters.content:
Chapter 411: 411 ’dog species

[Donald’s Perspective]

A mass of monsters was piled up, blocking the entrance to the cave. Their wolfish eyes revealed nothing but the unsettling whites, devoid of any rational thought. Drool, disgusting enough to induce nausea, hung from their gaping maws, making them look like dogs guarding the ’gate’ for Henry!

I slowly narrowed my eyes, a dangerous signal rising within them.

Elliot and Benjamin quickly got behind me, positioning themselves protectively on either side. Christian seemed to come to his senses only then, stepping back with his gun and bending over as he spoke to me in a grave tone, "Please forgive us, Your Majesty. We lost track of the enemy around the outskirts of this forest!"

As he spoke, a flicker of guilt briefly crossed his face. I raised my hand and patted his shoulder, signaling him to continue.

Christian straightened his back and continued, "I led the two remaining Lycans into the woods and we were lured here by two creatures! We clashed with the creatures, but strangely, besides those two, the group blocking the cave entrance didn’t make a move."

"They seemed mindless and unaffected by the agonized howls of their kind during the fight. I’m not sure if it counts as good news, but fortunately they didn’t react; otherwise, the three of us couldn’t have held out until you arrived, Your Majesty!"

The two Lycans previously led by Christian were lost, which certainly wasn’t good news. Yet luckily, he and the other two were alive and had discovered this bizarre cave.

I withdrew my gaze from the cave entrance and looked at Christian, who had finished his report. "Did you see any trace of others?"

Christian shook his head, his brows quickly furrowing.

He clearly realized how irregular the situation was. Austin had taken an unconscious Margaret, and Martha’s forces included much of King Lington’s battered remnants. They couldn’t possibly have completed their retreat so quickly; there must be something we didn’t know behind all this!

What Christian had considered, of course, I hadn’t overlooked either. I turned sideways to look at Charlie next to me, thought for a moment, then spoke in a subdued voice, "Have the Lycans on the helicopter expand the search area. I suspect they’re hiding in this forest. Move quickly!"

With a slight nod, Charlie gripped his command communicator and began issuing orders.

"Roar!"

Suddenly, one of the monsters lying in front of us cried out. It struggled as if trying to stand again, but Christian and the other two Lycans had inflicted heavy wounds on it. Its slender hind legs couldn’t support its excessively strong front body, and after stumbling a bit, it fell to the ground again!

I coldly watched this scene, a frigid murderous intent rising in my eyes.

I glanced at the helicopter hovering overhead, unworried by the wind tousling my hair. I made a gesture to the Lycan inside the cabin to use the heavy machine gun to clear out the monster and that disgusting group at the cave entrance!

I took the lead and stepped back a few paces, not bothering to lift my head as I fiddled with the gun in my hand, completely ignoring the wall of monsters behind me being pulverized into a bloody mess by the formidable firepower.

Perhaps the scent of their comrades’ blood had ignited their will because the creatures at the back of the cave entrance suddenly started to move. They howled, attempting to surge out from inside, with their sights set on us, not far from the cave entrance.

I glimpsed this scene from the corner of my eye, sneered, and didn’t retreat even half a step. With such formidable firepower overhead, these monsters couldn’t get anywhere near me.

No matter how frightfully they howled, they were ultimately shredded by the helicopter’s gunfire.

The scene was somewhat revolting. I shifted my gaze to Charlie, who also looked up at me at that moment and shook his head without saying a word.

I furrowed my brows, my irritation and anger bubbling once again. Despicable and cunning Henry—what kind of sinister schemes has he concocted this time? It was truly distressing and aggravating to have Margaret under his control like this.

Monstrous beings in wolf form continued to emerge incessantly from the cave. I watched coldly, eager to know just how many werewolves Henry had subjected to such inhumane ’transformations’?

Calling it ’transformation’ was actually just turning them into mindless, pain-unaware killing machines. Levi was right to call Henry a mad devil. The madman was indeed terrifying.

I was reflecting on this when I suddenly noticed a sharp glint of cold light approaching from the corner of my eye. I pushed Benjamin next to me aside and barely sidestepped in time!

"Your Majesty, take care!"

"Your Majesty!"

"It’s an ambush! They’re rushing over from the forest on our left! Everyone, on guard, protect the king!"

I narrowly avoided the sharp claw, and when I looked back, my pupils couldn’t help but dilate a bit!

I decided to correct my previous moniker for that heap of flesh, for what appeared before our eyes just a second ago was the true monster!

chapters.title: Chapter 412 Monster Wolf
chapters.content:
Chapter 412: 412 Monster Wolf

[Donald’s Perspective]

Its size was especially massive, almost indistinguishable from a mature male lion, but what was most terrifying was not its size, but the deadly speed that seemed not at all impeded by its bulk.

"Oh my God! What kind of monster is this? Is it a wolf? A wolf the size of a male lion?"

Charlie rushed to me at the first sign of the attack, holding his submachine gun, and muttering to himself in disbelief.

He watched the Monster Wolf, which was crouching and ready to pounce again, with a vigilant expression, and without turning his head, he reported to me, "I had just received a warning from the Lycans a second ago, and the next second, this monster appeared in front of us! It’s incredibly fast, and it’s targeting you, Your Majesty! You’re standing right behind us, but I’ve already got the Lycans in the helicopter aiming a pursuit missile at it, it won’t get away!"

My hands were sweating as I held the gun, and I didn’t respond to Charlie’s words. It wasn’t because I was afraid of the giant Monster Wolf in front of me; it was the sight of other things slowly emerging from the forest that had my nerves on edge in an instant!

"It’s not difficult to kill this beast, the difficulty is dealing with the hundreds of modified rogues behind it!"

I replied calmly, glancing sideways at Elliot and Christian, then continued, "Be careful not to get scratched by the creature’s claws; I’m worried they might be poisoned. Charlie, you command the heavy machinery troops to take on the main assault against the rogue army, and the rest of the Lycans will follow me. Let’s lure this Monster Wolf into the forest!"

As I issued these commands, I had already taken the lead in completing the shift and agilely led the Monster Wolf into the woods.

Elliot and Christian followed closely, shooting at the Monster Wolf to cover me, while tensely moving deeper into the forest!

At this moment, Benjamin also got up from the ground. The situation had been urgent, and all I could do was push him out from under the Monster Wolf’s gaping jaws.

He quickly collected himself, picked up the backpack that had fallen to the ground, and ran towards us!

With Charlie resisting the rogue army’s attack behind me, the pressure of leading the assault team and the personal guard force against the Monster Wolf lessened significantly, even though it didn’t seem to have any apparent weaknesses.

"How do we fight this? Its hide isn’t like ours; bullets won’t penetrate!" Elliot said, feeling powerless after firing more than a dozen shots at the Monster Wolf, which still moved fiercely and nimbly.

I crouched low and stared into the Monster Wolf’s red eyes, responding gravely, "Its eyes and mouth are its only two weaknesses; aim for its eyes! And it’s not immune to bullets; they hurt it even though they don’t go through!"

As I spoke, the Monster Wolf suddenly lunged at me, its huge mouth gaping as if it intended to bite off my neck. I narrowed my wolf eyes and, with a speed imperceptible to the naked eye, I dodged its open mouth and massive frame, slashing its rushing face with my claws!

"Roar!"

It seemed not to have expected me to fight back, enduring the fresh intense pain on its face, it opened its mouth wide and roared angrily at me!

Seeing this, I sneered, glancing at the blood on my claws, and offered a cold observation, "You seem smarter than those beasts. Wouldn’t it be even better if you could talk? I’m quite curious about what you looked like before Henry modified you, because now... just one more glance feels like an assault on the eyes!"

What I hadn’t anticipated was that the Monster Wolf actually understood me; it tried to pounce again the moment my words ended. However, the Lycans behind me kept it at bay with gunfire, preventing it from advancing. It roared uncontrollably, while glaring at me with its bleeding head.

’Bang bang bang!’

Christian’s shooting was always good, and several bullets grazed the Monster Wolf’s eyes. It howled in pain, instinctively closing its eyes and pouncing wildly in this direction!

I called out a stern command to fall back and suddenly lowered my body to absorb the impact of its fierce attack. I felt my forelimbs crack under the immense force as I clenched my back teeth and snarled, "Now, fire!"

Responding to me, the Lycans let loose a barrage of bullets, which burrowed into the Monster Wolf’s tough fur, causing its forelimbs to gradually weaken from pain.

I seized this excellent opportunity, exerting all my strength to fight back, baring my sharp wolf teeth and biting fiercely at its exposed neck.

As Wolf’s teeth pierced its skin, I felt a surge of elation, a fierce light flashing in my wolf eyes. I shook my head violently, my sharp teeth slicing its hide, and it was thrown to the ground beside me.

"Roar! Roar roar roar!"

It began to whine and howl in fear, but with its throat severed by me, only hoarse and staccato yelps came out, grating to the ear!

chapters.title: Chapter 413 Joint Kill
chapters.content:
Chapter 413: 413 Joint Kill

[Donald’s perspective]

I didn’t give it a chance to recover. I got up and pounced towards it, my claws about to pierce its already shattered throat.

The Monster Wolf seemed to realize the urgency of the moment and tried to flee with wide steps!

"Elliot!" I roared, pinning the Monster Wolf down with my body, my claws pressed against its neck.

Elliot silently rushed up, followed closely by Christian. Two dark barrels were almost simultaneously thrust into the Monster Wolf’s wide-open mouth, and a full magazine of bullets was poured directly into its throat!

Perhaps because the situation was so urgent and dangerous, Elliot roared as he twisted the gun barrel inside the Monster Wolf’s mouth. The bullets couldn’t penetrate the Monster Wolf’s outer fur, but its relatively soft throat and mouth couldn’t withstand such an attack!

I quickly jumped off the Monster Wolf, glanced at Elliot and Christian, their eyes blood-red, and breathed a heavy sigh of relief while the sound of helicopters bombing the rogue army re-entered my ears.

Only then did I realize how tense I had been. This Monster Wolf was really tough to handle!

But no matter how fierce and strong the monster was, it was now taken down by me and my Lycans. The Monster Wolf’s massive head was shot into a sieve from the inside out, its throat emitting wisps of blue smoke from the jammed bullet casings, with more bullets continuously fired inside, causing the blue smoke from the friction between the casings.

Christian let out a heavy breath and stepped forward to confirm the Monster Wolf was dead. "It’s dead! Stop it," he said.

His words, of course, were directed toward Elliot.

Hearing his teammate’s reminder, Elliot slowly stopped his finger movement, took a moment to look up at Christian, "Damn! If it’s not dead after this, I really don’t have any other way. This monster should be the only one, right? Please, no more, I can’t take it anymore!"

Christian, hearing this, chuckled and looked down at the Monster Wolf on the ground just as tiredly. "If a few more of these came, it would really be terrifying! This must be Henry’s most unexpected trump card. The one who wrested the Lycan Queen from Eric must have been this creature!"

While Elliot and Christian were talking, I had already shifted back to human form, silently staring at the scratch on my arm.

This was, of course, caused by the Monster Wolf’s claws when I was forcibly confronting it head-on, injuring me. I endured the intense pain and slowly leaned against a nearby tree trunk, my eyes ceaselessly scanning the battlefield behind.

Benjamin crouched next to me with a backpack, efficiently tearing open my shredded sleeve, pulled out some antiseptic serum for injection, and then tried to administer some anesthesia to facilitate immediate wound cleaning and stitching.

I knew what he was thinking and immediately interrupted him, saying, "Anesthesia might affect my ability to fight later, don’t use it now. Just stitch up the wound carelessly, make it quick!"

After speaking, I grimaced at Elliot and Christian nearby and continued, "Check our remaining ammunition. Henry has played his trump card, and now we need to push forward tenaciously." Margaret was still waiting for me to rescue her!

The last sentence I didn’t say out loud, I just prayed silently to the Moon Goddess to protect my mate, to let her suffer less torment and hardship at Henry’s hands.

Elliot and Christian, following my order, began to check the ammunition in the Lycans’ hands.

Benjamin took only minutes to clean and stitch my wound. I looked at the white bandage wrapped around my arm, slowly stood up, and frowned toward Charlie not far away, who was deeply troubled.

I walked over to him, surveyed the battlefield and flatly said, "It’s unlikely that we’ll be able to completely clear out this group of rogues with helicopters alone. You’ve done well up to now. I will lead the remaining Lycans directly into the fray, and you are responsible for blocking them outside the cave. Understand?"

Charlie looked at me upon hearing this, thought for a moment, and replied, "Your Majesty, isn’t this a bit too risky? We have no idea what’s inside the cave, and if you rush in like this, being ambushed by the enemy would be too passive! I suggest we clear out these rogues first, then have the Lycans on the helicopter with heavy machine guns clear the way. That would be safer and more reliable!"

I knew Charlie’s suggestion was more reasonable, but I couldn’t wait that long! Who knows if Henry might have already escaped through the underwater passage with Margaret while we are stuck outside the cave dealing with these monsters.

I couldn’t bear the lack of news about Margaret any longer. Today I had to snatch Margaret back from Henry’s hands!

"Just follow the military order, I don’t have time to delay any longer outside," I said, glancing at Charlie. I gestured to Elliot and Christian on either side, and to Benjamin, who had been silently working, signaling them to advance towards the cave!

chapters.title: Chapter 414: Cave 414 Stone Gate
chapters.content:
Chapter 414: Cave 414 Stone Gate

[Donald’s Perspective]

Charlie seemed to want to say something else, but after seeing the undeniable expression on my face, he took a deep breath and responded with a ’yes’.

I followed behind my Beta, heading towards the mouth of the cave.

The closer we got to this huge black cave, the thicker the corpses and shattered flesh on the road became; the blood submerged the soles of my combat boots. I raised my hand to put on the mask around my neck and continued forward with a frown!

With Lycan warriors clearing the path, we moved quickly, but as we went further inside, the temperature clinging to our bodies dropped lower and lower. About half an hour later, I noticed that the gauze on my arm was soaked with fresh blood, the pain stabbing at my brain nerves, making me all the more alert.

The serum that Benjamin had just injected me with seemed unable to completely cleanse the toxins in my body. I began to feel an abnormal fever in my body, but it was still within my tolerable range.

Because we needed to move cautiously, we didn’t use flashlights, relying solely on the werewolves’ innate night vision to navigate in the pitch dark.

I held my handgun, keenly listening to the movements ahead.

Suddenly, the Lycan leading us reported in a deep voice, "Your Majesty! There’s a huge stone gate here, we are blocked outside!"

Upon hearing this, I quickly walked over, using my superior night vision to examine the stone gate. It appeared very abruptly and seemed out of place in this underground passage.

"Could it be the actual undersea passage behind this? I think I can smell the salty scent of seawater!" Christian said, following closely behind me with some confusion.

He was always meticulous and good at spotting anomalies in our environment.

I gently sniffed and indeed caught the fishy damp smell Christian mentioned, but this odor was subtly strange, similar to the bloody smell we had smelled at the entrance of the cave!

"This doesn’t seem right, this doesn’t smell like seawater! And the geography here is off too. If there was all seawater behind this, the pressure that this stone gate would have to withstand would be immense."

"The connection between this stone gate and the cave isn’t tight, seawater could completely seep through these cracks, yet the ground beneath our feet is dry, so it’s unlikely that there’s sea behind this." I thought through slowly and came to this conclusion, touching the stone wall before glancing at Elliot.

"Can you check how thick this stone gate is? With explosives, could we blast through it?" I asked Elliot.

Upon my words, my Beta immediately placed his ear against the gate, lightly tapping the stone wall. After a while, he shook his head and answered, "This stone gate is too thick, ordinary explosives can’t blast through! Also, the cave structure we’re in can’t withstand such a strong impact. If we try, the gate might not open and instead the cave could collapse on us!"

Elliot’s response aligned closely with my prediction. I sighed lightly, then turned to examine the situation on both sides of the stone gate.

"Is there really no other way? Your Majesty and us have come through so much difficulty to get here, and we’re so close to catching up with Henry and his group. How can we let a stone gate stop us now?"

The one who spoke, always silent on the journey, was Benjamin. He stepped forward and punched the stone gate, his expression urgent and agitated.

After thinking, he looked at me and continued, "Your Majesty! Since Henry and his group passed through here before us, at least it proves that this route is passable. There must be some mechanism on this gate that can’t be destroyed simply by human force or modern technology."

I looked at him sideways upon hearing this, my mind swiftly flashing through some thoughts. I turned to the Lycans next to me and instructed straightforwardly, "Bend down and search inch by inch. I refuse to believe there isn’t a switch here to open this stone gate! I must capture the traitors Henry and Martha today!"

"Yes, Your Majesty!"

My Lycan warriors responded in unison, and then knelt on the ground to start searching bit by bit. This method was indeed a bit foolish, but without any clues, that was all we could do for now!

"Your Majesty! Shall we turn on a couple of flashlights? It might make it easier to observe." Christian squatted next to me and suggested.

Upon hearing this, I took a deep breath and nodded, agreeing to his suggestion.

Previously, we avoided using flashlights for fear of startling the enemy, but now with this heavy stone gate blocking, it was unlikely that Henry would discover us.

With the light, Benjamin seemed to breathe a little easier. Unlike Elliot and Christian, who needed to frequently complete various missions outdoors and were used to environments like this darkness, the unknown often induces more fear, such as darkness!

I endured the discomfort in my body and carefully examined the stone walls beside me.

Suddenly, Benjamin, squatting on the right side of the wall, let out a strange questioning sound, "Huh? What’s this piece of torn cloth doing under this stone?"

chapters.title: Chapter 415 Engagement
chapters.content:
Chapter 415: 415 Engagement

[Donald’s perspective]

As soon as I heard, I perked up and gestured for Benjamin to hand over the item for me to examine.

"Although it’s only a small piece, it’s still recognizable as a silk handkerchief or something of the sort used by a woman," I said after carefully inspecting it.

Upon hearing this, Benjamin’s face brightened with excitement as he eagerly pressed on, "Is this something the Lycan Queen would use? What was she wearing the day she was captured by Henry? Did she have this handkerchief with her?"

I shook my head, directly dismissing Benjamin’s guess, "If it were truly Margaret’s, how could I not recognize it? It’s probably just something the enemy left behind by accident. Let’s keep looking!"

My words were like a bucket of cold water poured directly over Benjamin’s head. He sighed, squatted down, and continued to search the ground, while I looked up somewhat painfully, feeling the anxiety in my heart threatening to engulf me.

After nearly 5 more minutes, we had touched every inch of ground in front of the Stone Gate, but still hadn’t found the mechanism to open it!

I knew that I absolutely could not show any sign of discouragement or restlessness at this time, and I continued to suppress the agitation in my heart as I observed the patterns on the Stone Gate.

Suddenly, I noticed that the Stone Gate seemed to tremble. Holding my breath, I turned to Christian beside me and asked, "Did you see the Stone Gate move?"

Christian swallowed hard and nodded, confirming my suspicion.

I immediately ordered the flashlight turned off and for everyone to retract 5 meters, to stay alert, and to shoot to kill at the first sign of the enemy!

Surprisingly, the Stone Gate began to open from the right side. I was taken aback, wondering about the mechanism behind it and what kind of force could move such a heavy stone?

It seemed that since arriving at the Lilo Sea, many things happening around me couldn’t be explained by science, like the sudden opening of the Stone Gate or the formation of this massive natural cavern.

But soon, the sound of intense gunfire from behind the Stone Gate snapped my drifting attention back to the present.

Looking intently ahead, the first thing I saw was Anthony with a few Lycans, bloodied, taking cover at the entrance of the Stone Gate, with Austin, his face grim, wielding a submachine gun and relentlessly chasing after them.

"Provide covering fire! Get Anthony back here!" I barked without hesitation, my gun aimed directly at Austin’s heart.

I fired three shots at Austin in one breath. Two bullets hit him!

Austin, wounded, yelped in pain and quickly retreated into the Stone Gate, shouting something loudly, and the gunfire stopped abruptly after half a minute.

Elliot quickly dragged Anthony and the few Lycans back from the entrance of the Stone Gate, while Benjamin rushed over with the medical kit. After confirming that Austin and his men had ceased firing, I stepped forward to assess Anthony’s condition.

He had been shot in the left leg, the bullet lodged in the bone and temporarily inextractable. The upper half of his body was covered in someone else’s blood, his face so pale it was frightening, "How did you encounter Austin inside? Did you see anyone else after you entered? Is Margaret there?"

As I spoke, I realized I was asking too much and raised my hand to grasp Anthony’s shoulder, urgently reassuring him, "Don’t worry! We’ve got Benjamin and the team; you and the other Lycans will be okay! Just take a moment to collect yourself, then report back what happened from this morning until just now."

With my steady comfort, Anthony gradually calmed down, panting heavily and speaking hoarsely, "Margaret is inside! She’s about to be taken by Henry on a yacht through an underwater passage. Your Majesty, you must rush in and stop Henry!"

"Cough cough cough! I’m fine, Your Majesty, it’s just a minor wound! The soldiers under Austin’s command aren’t many, and they’re definitely no match for the assault team Lycans. You can charge in now and save her!" Anthony said, coughing up a mouthful of fresh blood, which contradicted his claim of being unharmed.

Seeing his condition, I looked at him with concern, and Benjamin, who was busy treating his wound, urgently reported as well, "Anthony’s gunshot wound to the leg isn’t fatal, Your Majesty, you can rest assured!"

Relieved, I nodded slightly, then looked at Elliot and Christian beside me, "Benjamin, take care of Anthony and the injured Lycan team members! Assault team to take the lead! Personal guard force to cover me in rescuing the Lycan Queen, everyone, move out!"

I took the lead and charged out after giving the command, while Austin, sensing something was amiss, had already fled with his men through the Stone Gate.

I narrowed my eyes intently, watching his figure disappear around the corner of the stone tunnel, quickening my pace, and fired two shots in that direction, roaring, "Austin! Stop! You know there’s no way you’re getting out of this today, and you’re going to be the next ’Levi’!"

"You’re just Henry’s expendable flag! Since you’re going to die sooner or later anyway, you might as well surrender now. If you do, I might consider our past friendship and let you keep an intact body!"

chapters.title: Chapter 416 ’I’m late
chapters.content:
Chapter 416: 416 ’I’m late

[Donald’s Perspective]

Austin certainly didn’t respond to my "friendly" suggestion. In fact, his speed of fleeing on foot increased significantly upon hearing my voice!

His figure quickly disappeared into the darkness ahead, and my heart sank as I quietly and swiftly commanded Elliot, "Catch him! Just keep him breathing."

Upon hearing the order, Elliot nodded slightly and took the lead to complete the shift, turning into a streak of blue light and sprinting out!

He was followed by a dozen Lycan warriors from the assault team. I had no worries about them, for even if there were thousands of troops waiting to ambush them ahead, I firmly believed they had the ability to come back unscathed.

The command to "just keep him breathing" for Austin meant that no matter what combat tactics they used, as long as Austin didn’t die on the spot, everything else was of no concern to me!

In comparison to my urgent desire to rescue Margaret and the wish to kill Henry, the "rebirth" of the traitor Austin seemed to have lost its importance to me this time around.

As I chased after him, my mind was full of various thoughts, and the rapidly dropping temperature around me soon captured my attention, prompting me to start observing the scene inside the cave carefully!

Unlike the situation at the Stone Gate, the air here was very moist, the walls of the cave were covered with various kinds of moss, and the ground was wet and full of mud, with clusters of withered and limp sea grass occasionally visible.

I almost instantly realized that the massive Stone Gate inside the cave pathway certainly didn’t appear without reason—it probably functioned like a barrier to prevent the extreme temperature differences between the two ends of the cave, and more importantly, to block the large amount of seawater from the Lilo Sea during tidal changes from backflowing!

It was there to stop the rising seawater from flooding the cave; simply put, a watertight switch placed within this huge cave!

And yet, these days were the special period of tidal changes in the Lilo Sea. The fact that Henry chose this time to urgently withdraw his troops from Poison Snake Island made me wonder if there was some compelling reason for him to do so?

Of course, this question in my mind naturally didn’t receive any answer. With a thorough madman like Henry, who could really understand his inner thoughts?

Noticing my pace slowing down, Christian glanced at me with some confusion and asked in a subdued voice, "What’s wrong, Your Majesty? Did you think of something?"

His call snapped me back from my wandering thoughts. I shook my head and replied somberly, "It’s nothing. Let’s hurry and catch up."

After entering deeper into the cave, the terrain under our feet gradually became more complicated, with not only the challenge of the muddy ground but also many forked passages appearing!

At this point, Christian stopped to discern which of the four paths in front of us was the correct route, while I zeroed in on the leftmost passage and commanded directly, "This way, everyone follow me!"

I then charged into the passage with my gun at the ready, and although Christian didn’t know why I had chosen this route, he didn’t hesitate to follow my lead. All the Lycan warriors behind me did the same; it seemed as if they would obey my next command to face death without question if that were what I asked!

This was the innate nature of soldiers to obey orders!

Of course, I absolutely cherished every Lycan under my command. Without their loyalty and absolute trust, my position as Lycan King would certainly not be secure.

After chasing through the forked caves for nearly 10 minutes, we finally saw a huge space illuminated by light not far away. Austin, and Margaret—the one who haunted my dreams—all of them were still there!

Elliot took the Lycan warriors and moved to Austin’s side. I glanced at Henry and his group, now surrounded by soldiers in the middle, and my eyes flashed with a fierce and savage glint.

"Donald!"

When that long-missed call came from not too far away, my hardened heart felt as if it was injected with a stream of fresh, boiling blood. I was slightly stunned, and then my gaze involuntarily fell back on Margaret!

"I’m sorry, baby, I came too late!" My eyes moistened with untold heartache and guilt as I looked at the fragile and thin Margaret.

To my words, Margaret smiled. Her small face was deathly pale with no hint of color, and her forehead was covered with a bloodstained bandage. She looked at me with endless tenderness and replied, "Not late! Not at all late, I knew you would come to take me home, Donald!"

As she spoke, her expression became resolute, her eyes red and filled with dependence and yearning for me.

I suppressed the urge to rush over and hold her in my arms, chuckling gently to comfort her, "Of course, I’ll take you home. Can you wait just a moment longer for me?"

After hearing this, Margaret promptly cried, her face adorned with glistening tears, firmly nodding at me.

I gave her a reassuring look, then turned my gaze to another woman next to her. My eyes turned cold, and I looked at the woman with a mocking laugh, "What a long time no see, Your Highness, Princess Lington!"

chapters.title: Chapter 417 Assassination Mechanism
chapters.content:
Chapter 417: 417 Assassination Mechanism

[Donald’s Perspective]

It seemed like a long time since someone had addressed him in that manner; Martha’s facial muscles visibly twitched, then morphed into a sneer.

She looked at me and scoffed, "Don’t you find yourself ridiculous at this moment, Donald? Aren’t you, who deprived Dan of his title, the epitome of hypocrisy? Oh, I forgot, the members of the Wolf royal family always pretend to be something they’re not, of course you won’t admit to the disgusting and sinful acts you’ve committed!"

Martha’s sneer intensified as she spoke; she arched an eyebrow and a sharp dagger slid from her wide sleeve! She adeptly grasped the hilt, turned her body slightly, and pressed the blade of the dagger against the side of Margaret’s neck.

"What do you think, if I kill her now, will you feel even a fraction of the pain I felt back then?"

There was a hint of negotiation in her tone, but I slowly narrowed my eyes, filled with a dangerous warning.

Just as I was about to verbally warn Martha to release Margaret, Henry, who stood beside them, suddenly spoke out!

He looked down and said in a deep voice, "Put down your dagger."

I almost instinctively glanced at Henry out of the corner of my eye, feeling puzzled by the situation, while Martha didn’t seem surprised at Henry’s protective stance toward Margaret.

She glanced sideways at Henry who had spoken, the defiance in her eyes softened somewhat, and her tone became complex as she spoke, "This woman won’t easily come with us, you must understand this from what just happened! Her heart is entirely with that bastard, you guys—"

"Enough! Stop talking."

Martha’s words were cut off abruptly by Henry.

It was then that he finally looked up at me, his eyes filled with indecipherable emotions.

I frowned, no longer wanting to waste words with this mother and son, and said coldly, "You can’t take Margaret! And none of you are leaving here alive today! I won’t give you another chance to flee, Henry."

After speaking, I signaled Christian for an attack, shifted in an instant, and charged at the soldiers surrounding them without hesitation!

While I was conversing with Henry, Elliot had silently taken down Austin and his group! Just as I had said before, in the face of substantial danger, Henry would not hesitate to abandon all his allies, even someone as close as Austin.

Austin, clutching his bleeding chest, leaned against the wall and coldly watched our battle unfold, "I really didn’t expect you to break in so quickly! I had laid so many Transformed Werewolves in ambush in the forest; even the ’Demon King’ was stationed there, how did you bypass them to get here?"

His voice wasn’t very loud, yet I heard him clearly.

Disregarding him was all the response I gave; he let out a self-deprecating laugh, watching me weave through the enemy ranks, and continued without pause, "It was that werewolf named Anthony who told you, wasn’t it? He must be quite capable to sneak in so successfully with those Lycans, but we disrupted all satellite signals with jammers as soon as we discovered him, how did you receive the message he sent?"

Elliot and Christian were covering me from threats, and amidst the intense noises of battle, no one responded to him.

Of course, I had no interest in addressing his confusion; my focus was solely on Margaret in the crowd.

Earlier, standing on the outskirts, I hadn’t seen what was behind Margaret, but now that I was closer, I realized they were standing on a huge stone platform that hid a dark and mysterious deep pool!

A large submarine floated in the water, its hatch open, but no one was inside; it seemed Henry had used all his forces for their own protection.

I sensed trouble; Henry’s forces weren’t just this few. Most of his soldiers must have already evacuated, but why hadn’t he and Martha, both leaders of their forces, left first?

Questions flooded my mind as I looked at Henry with a puzzled expression.

"Donald! Be careful!" Margaret suddenly disregarded the soldiers restraining her and screamed out to me.

I instantly snapped back to reality as the sound of numerous arrows slicing through the air reached my ears; I sank into my werewolf instincts and swiftly dodged!

Elliot and Christian’s protection came at a crucial time; they quickly gathered around me, clearing the path of werewolf soldiers and shooting at the swarm of incoming arrows.

I glanced at two segmented arrow shafts on the ground, then turned my icy gaze back to Austin, guarded by two Lycans, "You really go out of your way for Henry, even wasting such expensive arrows so recklessly? It must have taken you a lot of time to set up this mechanism; you truly spared no effort."

Having assessed Austin’s ’masterpiece,’ I redirected my gaze to Margaret on the platform, silently sending her a reassuring look.

chapters.title: Chapter 418 ’Demon King
chapters.content:
Chapter 418: 418 ’Demon King

Donald’s Perspective

Seeing the mechanism I had painstakingly set up being breached, Austin’s composed facade cracked, his gaze toward me filled with venom and hatred. He roared hysterically, "Impossible! How could your bullets possibly sever my arrows? These arrows are made of..."

"Are you trying to say they’re made from special materials? I knew that much already." I interrupted Austin without hesitation, smirking contemptuously at him.

"You... how do you know they’re made of special materials? Impossible, that’s my secret," Austin stuttered, his question faltering.

Shifting my gaze away from him, I turned to Henry, "You should be thanking Henry and Levi for that! If they hadn’t put on such a splendid show with your bow and arrows earlier, I might have fallen for your little tricks."

"Having greater lethality than regular bullets does make your contraptions pretty good, but that’s all they are, just pretty good! They still don’t amount to much compared to the new weapons we have at our disposal." I said with a light sneer, catching a glimpse of Austin’s stunned expression in the corner of my eye.

Henry, seemingly caught off guard by this as well, narrowed his eyes, bristling with murderous intent as he locked his gaze with mine.

"Did you really only bring these few dozen Lycans with you today? You might want to take back all the big talk you just spouted, because who lives and who dies today is still up in the air!" Henry finished, lifting his wrist, his gun trained directly on my heart.

He didn’t shoot me right away, but the gesture acted like some kind of signal as the soldiers surrounding us suddenly raised their arms in unison, tearing off the long, strip-shaped pendants around their necks.

This scene was not unfamiliar to me; I had witnessed the exact same scenario in previous battles. I ducked down and barked out a warning, "Everyone on guard! These soldiers are about to transform with the help of a serum injection, handle them with care!"

Before I could even finish roaring out my warning, those soldiers didn’t even blink as they plunged the needle into their major arteries!

Instantly, the space was filled with the bizarre and agonized howling of the mutating Werewolves; their wolf forms started to grow wildly, especially the upper limbs. Any semblance of reason in their wolf eyes vanished in an instant, leaving only terrifying whites of the eyes swimming with blood.

"Roar!"

The unending roars thundered, Christian swallowed hard, speaking somewhat nervously, "Your Majesty! Do you think their combat power will be as strong as that Monster Wolf outside?"

I too wished to know the answer to this question. After a 2-second silence, I replied, "Didn’t Austin just mention that the Monster Wolf is called ’Demon King’? I figure something they would name as their trump card isn’t likely to be mass-produced."

Indeed, my speculation was correct. The creatures that had successfully mutated thanks to the soldiers’ injections were nowhere near as enormous as the ’Demon King’, nor as dexterous and formidable in combat, but they were far from your typical rogue-modified monsters!

But what was even more frightening was their sheer numbers, which were several times our own, and their bodies’ higher pain threshold. They were veritable, fearless war machines.

I swatted a monster that was too close for comfort, my grey-green eyes gleaming with a bloodthirsty will to fight, "We can’t outlast them in a battle of endurance, we need to make this quick!"

Saying this, I glanced sideways at Christian and a few other members of the personal guard force Lycans, lowering my voice to issue a command, "You’re responsible for covering me and Elliot. I’m going to charge in and rescue Margaret! Elliot, you’ll have to keep Henry busy for a while, don’t let him get in my way. Do you all understand?"

Following my urgent order, the Lycans immediately nodded in understanding and started to engage with the surrounding creatures after completing their shift!

Seeing this, I quickly moved closer to the platform, with Elliot and Christian right behind me. I looked at Margaret not far away, thinking to myself: The last 10 meters!

Smart as Henry and Martha were, they quickly discerned my real intentions.

They retreated while harshly directing the surrounding creatures to leap at me, attempting to obstruct my path.

I clenched my teeth, my eyes bloodshot, staring down the mother and son as I bellowed, "Give Margaret back to me, Henry!"

Margaret had been struggling all along to break free from Martha and Henry’s hold, but she was far too weak to stop them from forcefully taking her onto the yacht!

I dodged a creature’s attack with grace, pushed hard off the ground with my hind legs, and leapt onto the high platform without pause, hurling myself towards Margaret.

Seeing my diving figure, Henry’s eyes widened slightly. He completed his shift in an instant, howling and attempting to engage me, but Elliot, who was following close behind, didn’t give him the chance!

"Go to hell, Henry!"

chapters.title: Chapter 419: April 19, Feeling Heartache
chapters.content:
Chapter 419: April 19, Feeling Heartache

[Donald’s Perspective]

Elliot’s yell stole Henry’s attention, and in order to avoid having Elliot bite his neck, Henry was forced to move away from Margaret’s side a bit!

I seized the chance, casting a dangerous glance toward Martha on the other side.

Martha’s obsession with Margaret apparently wasn’t as strong as Henry’s. She decisively let go of Margaret’s hand and, frowning coldly at Henry said, "The submarine is fixed! Let’s leave here first. With only his Lycan underlings, he definitely won’t be able to escape alive from this cave today. These experimental werewolves can easily drag them to their deaths!"

While she shot her gun in self-defense, she urgently added, "Margaret is destined not to like you, and your persistence is useless! Since she wants to die alongside that bastard, then we can only ’grant’ her that wish. Do you understand what I mean, Henry!"

"Shut up! It’s none of your business!" dodging Elliot’s sharp claws, Henry snapped back, grinding his teeth.

The moment Martha let go, I shifted into human form, stretched my left arm to wrap around Margaret’s waist and began to swiftly retreat.

"Don’t get entangled in the fight! Everyone prepare to retreat!"

I roared at my Lycan warriors, then glanced down at Margaret with immense affection and whispered tenderly to console her, "Don’t be afraid! I’ll get you out of here."

Margaret’s eyes moistened, her hands tightly embracing my neck, burying her frail self into my chest.

My heart, suspended in anxiety for days and nights, was now pressed back into its original place by this gesture, a rush of emotions named reassurance and satisfaction flooding my nerves!

Suppressing the urge to crush Margaret into pieces and stuff her into every crevice of my body, I didn’t hesitate to kick aside Aiden who tried to lunge at us. My kick was forceful, seemingly snapping his ribs.

God knows where that guy popped up from, thinking he could try his sneaky tricks on me!

I watched coldly as Aiden, thrown onto the countertop, raised my right hand and without mercy shot him three times. Each bullet accurately pierced his heart.

He fell to the ground without even a chance to feel the pain, head to the side, his eyes wide, staring straight at Henry not far away.

"Scum that can’t show its face, you deserve to die." I spoke softly, then raised my eyes to look at Henry a few steps away.

Elliot was breathing heavily, standing between Henry and me, looking somewhat off.

Knowing he was no match for Henry, my command to him was merely to hold Henry off, and he had done a great job.

Christian had bitten off a monster’s neck and was also slowly moving toward my position.

I looked down at the werewolves that packed the space below the stone platform, my brows knitted tightly in concern!

"These werewolves, hastily transformed by injections, are incredibly unstable. If we drag this out, there might be a chance!" Margaret, while embracing my neck, reminded me with a hoarse voice.

Naturally, I wouldn’t doubt Margaret’s judgment. Grasping her body tightly with my left hand, I ordered Elliot and Christian, "Have all the Lycans converge here, don’t fight these monsters head-on!"

Both of them nodded at my word, began directing the Lycan warriors to close in, slowly forming a protective ring centered on me and Margaret. To break through this line, the first step was to kill all of us!

Henry was blocked at the other end of the stone platform, his eyes blood-red as he stared at me and Margaret.

I was indifferent to these hostile gazes. After looking around the protective circle, I slightly bent down to set Margaret down from my arms.

I bent down and kissed her hair soothingly, whispering warmly, "Charlie should be able to fight his way in and join us soon. Even if we can’t break through, it’s okay. As long as we hold out until reinforcements arrive. How’s your injury? Can you hold on?"

Margaret leaned her head against my chest, seemingly unable to stand firmly. She wrapped her arms around my waist, her voice weak, "I’m okay, I can hold on. How’s your hand? When did you get hurt?"

I was reminded then of the bandage tied around my arm.

Knowing I couldn’t hide it from her, I raised my injured hand to touch her cheek, cajoling in a soft voice, "It doesn’t hurt anymore. It just looks a bit scary, that’s all."

"It doesn’t hurt? But your blood has soaked through the bandage, can you smell the heavy stench of blood on you?" Margaret pressed my hand, her pale face rubbing against my palm, her eyes still clear and beautiful, filled with distress and worry.

I opened my mouth to explain more, but the surging concern in her eyes stopped the words. My mate, even in such a life-or-death emergency, still didn’t forget to care and worry for me!

A smile curled on my lips, and I couldn’t resist giving her a kiss, laughing, "Compared to not seeing you, these injuries are nothing!"

As I spoke, I wrapped my arms tighter around her, raising my eyes to Henry, who was still watching us.

chapters.title: Chapter 420 Coveting
chapters.content:
Chapter 420: 420 Coveting

[Donald’s Perspective]

"Come on, Henry! Now is not the time to be foolish. Once their reinforcements from outside arrive, we won’t be able to escape!"

With the Lycans ceasing their assault, Martha faced much less pressure in combat. Her mind was clearly much sharper than Henry’s, urgency and helplessness apparent on her face.

Henry stood motionless, as if he hadn’t heard Martha’s warning at all.

I narrowed my eyes slightly, a murderous intent flashing within.

"Their submarine just broke down, so they couldn’t achieve their goal of transporting the Lycan Queen. It seems the Moon goddess is on our side! It is destined, Your Highness, you and the queen will definitely be able to leave here safely today!" Elliot, having reverted to his human form, said so nonchalantly while holding his submachine gun.

There were traces of blood on his back, wounds incurred during his struggle with Henry.

I snapped back to reality and glanced at him, my brow slowly furrowing, thinking I should have brought Benjamin along too, leaving just a Medical Practitioner at Anthony’s would have been enough.

Many Lycans got injured in the recent battle, and a few Lycan warriors couldn’t withstand the relentless attacks by the beasts and perished here!

I felt heavy-hearted, knowing this would lead to more casualties, but I had no better plan to lead everyone out of the encirclement to escape. Staying in position and waiting for help was the best tactic we could employ to minimize casualties.

After resting for two minutes by my side, Margaret seemed to have regained some strength. She looked up at Elliot’s departing figure and explained in a soft voice, "The submarine didn’t break down for no reason. Anthony and I together sabotaged its energy storage unit, delaying their second departure time, and that’s how we managed to wait for your rescue."

I looked down at Margaret, slightly curved my mouth, and praised her, "You’re really smart, my dear. If it weren’t for your and Anthony’s teamwork, we might have been too late to stop them! And Anthony, if he hadn’t opened the Stone Gate from inside in time, we wouldn’t have had the chance to come in and save you both."

Margaret chuckled lightly and said warmly to me, "That’s the understanding between us! If you hadn’t had Anthony sneak in ahead of time, I couldn’t have stopped them from leaving by myself. What Elliot said was right—the Moon goddess is indeed on our side."

"And those who commit many evils, full of sins, are destined not to receive the Moon goddess’s blessings." Margaret’s last words were clearly not intended for me as she looked sideways at Henry across from us with undisguised disgust and hatred!

At that moment, I also looked over and saw Henry’s face, which had been cold and indifferent, slowly crumble after hearing Margaret’s words.

His eyes twitched sickly for a moment, and he slightly tilted his chin up, stubbornly asking, "So, this is how you see me? ’Committing many evils,’ ’full of sins’? Am I any worse than this bastard? Just because you met him first, I didn’t have a chance..."

He suddenly laughed out loud, his gaze towards Margaret turning crazed, I even saw a sort of possessive affection in his eyes!

My expression instantly turned icy, and as I looked at Henry, it was as if I was looking at a dead man. I interrupted his soliloquy, "You seem to love spouting nonsense now. Is this part of a villain’s usual script? I used to think you were smart, but it seems you are not."

Henry’s gaze turned malevolent as if he wished to devour me alive.

I was unafraid of such a gaze and held Margaret, looking at him coldly as I continued, "Because smart people don’t dare covet everything that belongs to the Lycan King! You are not only foolish but also mad beyond basic sense. Perhaps you should listen to Martha and give up some unrealistic fantasies early and accept the outcome of dying by my hands!"

After saying that, I let go of Margaret and slowly stood up from the ground. I sensed what Margaret feared I would do, and she quickly grabbed the hem of my clothes.

"Hold him back, that will be enough. No need to engage directly! He has several potions on him that can heal injuries. Don’t clash directly with him; stay calm!" Margaret urgently said, looking into my eyes with worry and disapproval.

I knew Margaret was worried about me, but I couldn’t bear Henry’s excessive ’probing’. He even dared to covet my Margaret, as if he thought his life was too long and was eager to die!

"Some boundaries are not meant to be tested by others, especially since he has always wanted to replace me, Henry!" I patted Margaret’s hand, signaling her to be at ease and not hold me back этапных.

Margaret looked at me, her expression slightly stunned as she seemed unable to comprehend the meaning in my words.

I didn’t blame Margaret for her momentary slow response, for certain traits encoded in the male species’s genes are completely incomprehensible to adorable females like her.

I wouldn’t allow anyone to covet my mate unless he was completely dead!

So, Henry must die here today.

chapters.title: Chapter 421 Duel
chapters.content:
Chapter 421: 421 Duel

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Donald let go of my arm and stood up from the ground.

He took off his coat and draped it over my legs, his large hand gently caressing my ear and cheek, silently comforting my worried emotions.

I looked up at him, not quite understanding why he had to engage in a death battle with Henry right now, but I knew I couldn’t stop him, nor should I!

A battle between Donald and Henry seemed inevitable, an unchangeable matter for anyone.

I swallowed the words I wanted to use to persuade him further and softly said, "Be careful."

Donald looked at me and chuckled softly, responding gently, "You should trust your husband. I am the finest successor in the history of the Werewolf Royal Family. Killing a traitor who betrayed his own country, isn’t that a simple matter?"

Since our wedding in the Werewolf Royal City, Donald had rarely emphasized to others that I belonged to him. Henry’s behavior today seemed to have stimulated his highly sensitive nerve, proclaiming his ’sovereignty’—Donald is Margaret’s husband!

After speaking, he removed the pistol from his armed belt, handed it to me, and explained softly, "This gun is custom-made; it can easily penetrate those monsters’ skulls. Keep it on you for protection."

Upon hearing this, I took the gun, my brow slightly furrowed as I looked at Donald, who straightened up, turned around, and shifted. Naturally, his target was Henry, who had already lost some of his sanity across from us!

"Fine! Come and try! I want to see who is destined to die here today!" Henry began with a taunting jeer.

He lowered his lupine form menacingly, his red eyes swirling with murderous intent, as if they were about to spill out!

The Lycans blocking Donald’s path stepped aside one by one, allowing their most revered king to slowly approach the forefront of the formation.

Donald’s aura was chilling, his tall, strong figure stood at the front of the camp, a silver-white werewolf, the most noble and powerful in the werewolf world. He was like a peerless blade, sharp from battle, just his presence commanding respect and admiration.

Of course, Henry and Martha clearly did not belong to the vast majority.

"All show! I will kill you!" Henry, apparently very displeased with Donald’s posture, yelled fiercely and charged at Donald, swinging his claws as if he wanted to slash directly through Donald’s throat!

My heart raced, gripping the gun, slowly standing up from the ground.

Donald growled low in his throat, showing no signs of retreating or dodging. He slightly dipped his body and pounced a second before Henry reached him. The two gigantic wolves collided, engaging in a terrifying tangle and frantic biting and slashing in mere moments!

"Your Highness! Your body is too weak; you should sit down!"

I ignored Christian’s concern, stood straight, and watched the battle in the center of the arena intently.

I didn’t know if it was an illusion, but I felt that the color of Henry’s lupine form had become redder than before. As he entangled with Donald, that redness painfully stung my eyes!

A loud ’bang!’ sounded, and I saw their figures finally separate a bit. Donald stood in place, his voice a low rumble, and Henry was violently smashed against the wall on the side, cracking the dark, black stonewall.

The first round was undoubtedly won by Donald.

As he had said before, he was the finest successor in the Werewolf Royal Family, his lineage and combat abilities far surpassed Henry’s, let alone considering Henry’s current body and combat power had been acquired through improper means. How could he truly outmatch a lineage specially blessed and favored by the Moon goddess?

I should believe he could win this duel smoothly, but a restless unease fluctuated in my heart, as if something bad was about to happen.

I took a deep breath, watching intently as Donald and Henry tangled together again.

Henry’s left leg had two slashes from Donald’s claws, causing his step to tremble slightly as he retreated. This trembling was very brief and would surely go unnoticed without careful observation.

"His left hind leg is injured; don’t give him a chance to escape!" I shouted at Donald, gripping the gun with one hand, my expression tense as I watched the arena.

Donald naturally knew Henry’s injury better than I did. His gray-green wolf eyes full of a bloodthirsty murderous intent, he launched another attack on Henry, his body movements even more agile and fierce.

I had seen many battles between him and Henry before, but today was really different. Each attack they delivered used all their might, leaving no room for the other, truly desiring to bite through the opponent’s throat and see them dead by their own hands.

chapters.title: Chapter 422 shooting unexpectedly
chapters.content:
Chapter 422: 422 shooting unexpectedly

[Margaret’s Perspective]

’Boom! Boom!’

Two bullets skimmed past Donald’s lower back, and I saw him pause mid-swing, growling from his throat, but he didn’t stop his attack on Henry.

I glanced towards the source of the bullets and noticed Martha holding a gun, her gaze cold as she watched Donald’s figure. No doubt, she was the one who had fired those shots!

"Has no one ever taught Princess Lington that outsiders aren’t supposed to interfere when two Lycans are dueling? Shooting from behind someone’s back is truly a disgraceful act!" I said, lifting my gun towards Martha, my voice icy.

Martha, of course, heard my words. She sneered, continuing to pull the trigger while mocking, "If someone else had said that, I might have listened, but coming from you... doesn’t it sound ironic?"

Almost simultaneously, Martha fired another shot at Donald who was in the fray, and I also pulled my pistol’s trigger. The bullet tore through the air and hit her right hand that was holding the gun!

"Ah!" She grunted, dropping the gun to the ground and turned her head to glare furiously at me.

I barely breathed a sigh of relief, meeting her icy gaze with mine, my expression frosty, "What? Are you angry? I learned this from you, didn’t I? Aren’t you fond of such despicable and shameless acts?"

"You cheeky little wretch! I underestimated your cunning and skills. I’ve no idea what love potion you’ve given Henry to make him unwilling to give up on you! But don’t get too cocky, he’ll soon see through you, a treacherous little backstabber!"

Watching Martha’s enraged expression, I sarcastically curled my lips, "Forget the nonsense you just spouted! Do you think he’ll make it out of here alive today? Donald won’t give him another chance to flee and he himself..."

I paused, glancing briefly at the red figure in the arena, then continued, "...seems to have no intention of leaving, at least for this moment."

"It’s because of you, you little wretch, meddling over and over again! If it weren’t for you and that werewolf who suddenly appeared, ruining my submarine, we would have left this cursed place long ago! This is all your fault! I shouldn’t have agreed to Henry’s proposal to keep you around. I should have killed you the moment you set foot on the island!"

Martha spoke harshly, her eyes, much like Henry’s, turning blood-red!

In this moment, mother and son looked strikingly similar, hysterical and insane in the same manner.

A Medical Practitioner came up to her, swiftly bandaging her right hand’s through-and-through wound, whispering something anxiously to her. The person spoke too softly for me to guess their conversation, as I couldn’t read lips like Donald.

My brow furrowed slowly watching Martha’s expression gradually calm down, a bad premonition rising in me!

I turned to look at Elliot, who was fending off werewolf creatures, and said gravely, "Martha is probably planning to escape. We need to find a way to hold her back!"

Upon hearing this, Elliot let go of his current foe, glanced at me, and asked, "If the King has completely occupied Henry, and she still wants to run, does she not care about her son anymore?"

I didn’t answer Elliot’s question because I felt it was impossible to predict this mother and son’s thoughts using common sense. For Martha, abandoning a stubborn and obstinate son seemed to not be a difficult decision!

For people like them, it seemed that the only thing they truly couldn’t give up was themselves.

"Your Highness, what do you want to do? We’re too short on manpower right now to launch an effective attack, and she’s too far from us to charge!"

Seeing that I remained silent, Elliot spoke again.

I sighed softly, not disputing Elliot’s analysis of the battle, and somewhat helplessly said, "You’re right; we really can’t manage that much! Donald just mentioned that Charlie is just outside the cave. When can they come in to support us? You just rescued Anthony, right? How is his injury; can he still fight?"

"The heavy machinery troops led by Charlie are mainly responsible for killing the monster army outside the cave. Considering the time since the King led us here, the fight outside has been going on for nearly two hours. I guess Charlie might be on his way here! Anthony was injured in the leg, Benjamin has treated him urgently; he should be able to protect himself now, but it’s doubtful he can fight!"

I nodded slowly, indicating that I understood.

I looked up at Donald’s imposing figure in the arena, moved my hand on the gun, and added, "You take responsibility for covering Donald. Keep an eye on Martha and her group, don’t let them disrupt Donald again!"

"Yes, Your Highness!" Elliot replied, shifting back to human form, holding his gun and sneaking closer to the center of the arena.

chapters.title: Chapter 423 Give Up
chapters.content:
Chapter 423: 423 Give Up

[Margaret’s Perspective]

I glanced at Elliot’s back when suddenly, everything before my eyes went dark. I immediately took a step back and supported myself against the stone wall, slightly shook my head, and clarity returned!

Knowing my situation was very dire, I endured the excruciating pain in the palm of my hand and tried to stay conscious.

"Your Highness, what’s wrong? Are you feeling unwell?"

The speaking Lycan seemed somewhat familiar, but I could not recall his name. I only knew he must be a member of Christian’s personal guard force. "Do you have any of those potions for recovering injuries? Give me one," I said hoarsely.

He nodded at my words, handed me his backpack, then turned and fired two shots at the creatures emerging from beneath the stone slab. Without looking back, he explained, "Captain Benjamin equipped each of us with three ’New Births’. I have exactly one left; you use it. There’s also some dressing medication in the bag, change it yourself first!"

I found the ’New Birth’ from his combat backpack, gave a light chuckle of thanks, then popped off the syringe cap to inject myself!

"Your Highness, wait a moment!"

Christian, who had transformed back into his human form, came over, reeking of blood, "Benjamin said ’New Birth’ is currently in the third experimental phase, it’s not clear whether it might have side effects on pregnant women and fetuses, you..."

He paused, looked at me with eyes full of concern and worry, swallowed, and then continued, "If you want to use ’New Birth’, please consider it carefully!"

I looked up at him, then without hesitation, plunged the syringe directly into my vein. "I know that, but my condition is too severe; I can’t wait that long!"

I said as I finished injecting the medication in the syringe. My eyes moistened as I looked toward Donald, not far away, gave a slight chuckle and continued, "Between Donald and this baby, who isn’t fully formed yet, Donald is more important. I can’t continue being a burden to you all! Let all the Lycans be alert; we are preparing to break out from the right side of the cave."

Christian, seemingly puzzled by my sudden decision to break out, looked at me about to speak but was immediately interrupted by me, "Henry’s Transformation Potion is unstable in effect, and the transformed werewolves will rapidly enter a state of decline after half an hour. Time is running out; we can’t afford to keep dueling with them here, breaking out is the more rational choice!"

"Merely waiting passively for rescue won’t work; I’m worried you guys can’t hold out. How many ’New Births’ do you have left? Are they out?"

As I spoke those words, the medication started to take effect within me; new, soft flesh formed over the wound in my palm, and the continuous cramping in my lower abdomen ceased!

My eyes began to moisten, and my heart suddenly twitched.

In fact, the moment I was afraid to open my eyes again in this cave, I had a feeling I might lose this baby. Simo said I couldn’t endure another moment of torment, which certainly wasn’t an exaggeration; a Medical Practitioner cannot deceive their patient.

The bloodstain between my thighs under my long skirt had started to dry. I didn’t know whether my baby was still alive; his arrival by my side was terribly untimely.

After hearing my explanation, Christian hesitated no more and turned to organize all the Lycans around him. The personal guard force and the assault team’s Lycans had already suffered significant losses; breaking out was imperative!

I stood up and looked at the two figures in the arena, energy madly accumulating in my body.

Ultimately, Henry was no match for Donald. After being thrown against the stone wall who knows how many times, he stood up, transformed into his human form, and swiftly tore off the injectable on his neck as if to inject himself!

Of course, Donald wasn’t going to stand idly by and wait for him to recover; he roared and charged quickly, his massive form completely overshadowed Henry beneath him, his raised claws about to smash the opponent’s head when Martha, who had been observing their condition, once again tried to intervene with gunfire.

’Bang bang!’

’Bang!’

These gunshots almost sounded simultaneously. Donald’s forelimb took a bullet, and Martha’s chest on her clothing was also dyed a splendid flower with blood!

Elliot’s marksmanship was quite good; a shot hit Martha directly in the chest. He sidestepped the werewolf creature’s attack and out of the corner of his eye, saw Martha being dragged by two subordinates directly into the submarine behind them.

Then, the submarine door closed directly from the inside!

The sound of the submarine’s engine starting was loud, attracting the attention of all of us involuntarily, including Henry, who had just completed his injection, and Donald, whose breathing became heavier after taking a shot.

"Did they forget there’s still one person not aboard, what do you think, Henry?" Donald spoke with a sarcastic laugh, seemingly unconcerned by the slow motion of the submarine sliding into the pool.

Henry calmly stared at the large ripples on the water surface, then after a while turned to look at Donald, answered coldly after having shifted, "Seems you too have quite a bit to say, aren’t you going to make a move?"

He lowered his red wolf head, menacingly-eyeing the opposite Donald, seemingly not worried at all about lacking a way to retreat.

chapters.title: Chapter 424 ’Everyone deserts him and his friends turn against him
chapters.content:
Chapter 424: 424 ’Everyone deserts him and his friends turn against him

[Margaret’s Perspective]

And the most important point, he seemed to have been abandoned by his biological mother once again!

Just like during that year’s rebellion war, Martha almost unhesitatingly made her choice between him and his father. Back then, she left the young Henry behind and retreated to the Demon Domain Forest with the defeated King Lington, never once inquiring about her only "Lington Crown Prince" from the beginning to the end.

To call him the Crown Prince, he was, in fact, merely a hostage despised by outsiders and loathed by members of the Royal Family.

After King Lington’s death, Alex and Licia stated they would not pursue the fact of him being the traitor’s lone son, allowing him to inherit another royal throne and offering him all their love and trust, even treating him on equal terms with Donald!

But the fact proved that a child rooted in greed and sin cannot be nurtured, and Alex’s final act of kindness turned out to be a colossal joke! Henry was always Dan’s son, and leaving him behind would only make him a new vessel of hatred.

I did not know how desperate and complicated Henry’s feelings were at the moment. The mother, who should have been closest to him, still did not choose him when faced with this decision a second time; it seemed he had never been chosen by those around him from the very beginning.

"What a pity."

Donald voiced the very thought in my mind. For a moment, Henry truly seemed pitiable.

"Abandoned by all? It seems the only ones left to ’support’ you are these foolish, ugly monsters. If I were you, I would pull out a gun right now and shoot myself in the heart! Only in that way could you ’console’ your laughable and pitiful life!"

"Shut up! Even if I die today, I will drag you and Margaret down with me!" Henry’s werewolf eyes were full of dark red blood vessels, and the entire wolf was in a very unusual state, with an extreme sense of exhilaration and a murderous intent!

Provoked by Donald’s words, he lost his last shred of reason and lunged at Donald like a madman!

At that moment, Donald was in no hurry to kill him, and nimbly stepped aside to avoid the attack, retreated a few steps to the side, and continued with a sneer, "The only one dying here today will be you alone. I will take Margaret safely out of this cursed place, and what awaits her and me is a future filled with happiness! And you, Henry, you have no future."

"I will kill you! Shut up!"

"Hahaha! This flustered and exasperated look of yours, it’s ’adorable’ more than ever before! I actually really want to continue admiring it, but like I said, you’re out of time!"

As Donald spoke, he feigned appreciation with a few glances at Henry, dodging the opponent’s completely disordered fierce attack. He turned back with some surprise to look at me.

I understood the message in Donald’s eyes. He was saying: Why did you come over? It’s dangerous, go back!

As I raised my gun, I smiled at Donald and ran several quick steps towards him. Keeping an eye on Henry, I explained, "Didn’t you hear those footsteps coming from outside? Our reinforcement has arrived. Let’s pull out now, there’s no need to keep tangling with him!"

My words were followed by a signaling gesture for Donald to listen and an icy gaze fixed on Henry, "Because of you, how many people betrayed their faith and country, how many lost their lives for nothing. Do you really think those things you firmly believe in are correct? Donald is not entirely right, you are actually not pitiable at all, you deserve this desolate fate."

Watching Henry standing there, dumbfounded, I gave a mocking laugh and couldn’t help but continue, "Do you know why you ended up like this? Because you’re selfish, venomous, and mad, you don’t consider life and the natural order at all, attempting to challenge the entire world with your own strength, whether it’s the reality of the werewolf world or the laws of the natural existence of life!"

"Do you really think you’ve changed the fate of the deficient werewolves, and those rogues? Ridiculous! Stupid! Everything in the world must adhere to natural order, life and death, gaining and losing are normal, and anything obtained by unjust means is called theft. Moon goddess and reality will punish those who are greedy and selfish. And now, your punishment has come!"

My eyes were moist with heat when I finished speaking. It wasn’t for Henry; he wasn’t worth any tears from anyone. It was a lament for all the precious lives lost in the war he instigated!
