Henry, behaving very unusually, listened to all the accusations I hurled at him. After the last sentence I spoke fell, he slowly transformed back into human form, his body covered in new flesh wounds and black-red blood pooling under his feet, but his eyes unwaveringly fixed on me!

At this time, Donald also transformed back into human form and silently stood in front of me, cutting off Henry’s direct gaze.

I turned to look at everyone who had rushed in from the exit, gave a light chuckle towards Elizabeth standing behind Charlie, and sighed with relief slowly. Even the panting elderly Dean Andrew was by her side – I had no idea when the Dean had arrived at Poison Snake Island, but this journey must have been very tough, right?

chapters.title: Chapter 425 Perish Together
chapters.content:
Chapter 425: 425 Perish Together

[Margaret’s Perspective]

Actually, it was Elliot who first noticed the arrival of reinforcements. He stood at the forefront of the Lycan camp, keenly hearing the roaring footsteps coming from the exit!

At that moment, his expression turned to one of surprise, and he reported to me loudly, "Your Highness, our reinforcements have arrived! Listening to these footsteps, it must be General Lucas and Charlie’s soldiers who have entered together!"

I did not know who this General Lucas was, but Charlie, who ranked behind him, was an old acquaintance of mine.

minutes later, I saw the army appearing at the cave’s entrance! Elliot was right; among those who came to help were Charlie’s heavy machinery troops and General Lucas leading the army!

Suppressing my own immense excitement, I held the gun in my hand and instructed Elliot while walking, "Cancel the breakout operation! Coordinate with the reinforcements to complete the cleanup; I’m going to tell Donald the good news."

While I was talking, I glanced at Christian by my side and immediately continued, "Christian, follow me. I need the Lycan of the personal guard force to lend a hand."

Naturally, both of them obeyed my command.

Christian acknowledged with a ’yes,’ transformed into human form, and followed closely behind me, with several other Lycans from the personal guard trailing behind us.

The thing I wanted to do was to capture Henry alive!

After recounting the arrival of the reinforcements, unable to contain my bursting sorrow and rage, I aimed the gun at Henry’s heart and furiously cursed the sin-laden man.

Donald impassively blocked Henry’s gaze towards me; I barely managed to calm some of my turbulent emotions when I suddenly noticed a familiar gaze fixated on me from afar.

Turning my head, I saw Elizabeth and instinctively curved my lips slightly. My eyes were slightly moist when I just noticed in the corner of my eye that Dean Andrew had arrived. The next second, Donald abruptly embraced me and lunged toward the edge of the stone platform!

’Bang!’

Something had suddenly exploded. Intense shock waves and blinding light leaked out from behind Donald. I only felt as if my organs had shifted. The next second, I smelled the stench of blood emanating from Donald...

"Donald? Don’t scare me..."

"Margaret!"

"There was an explosion? Your Majesty! Lycan Queen!"

"It was Henry who used a bomb to blow himself up! Hurry and save someone! Someone save Your Majesty and the Lycan Queen!"

The sudden calamity stunned me. After falling to the ground with Donald, it took half a moment for me, driven solely by reflex, to struggle out of his embrace. I lifted my hands, cradling Donald’s face, desiring him to open his eyes and look at me!

I didn’t know whether the blood on my hands was Donald’s or my own. I sobbed hysterically, tears streaming down as I shakily embraced Donald’s neck.

My throat felt as if it was choked, unable to make any sound until Benjamin and Elizabeth rushed over to check on Donald and me. Only then did I suddenly find my voice again, shrieking, "Save Donald! He can’t die. Quick, save him!"

"Okay! We know. Can you let go of your brother-in-law first? You’re also injured, sis!"

"I know, Your Highness. Please let go of His Majesty! I’m Benjamin. With me here, I won’t let anything happen to you or His Majesty!"

Actually, all I could hear at the moment was a massive buzzing in my ears. I only saw Elizabeth and Benjamin’s lips moving differently, but I couldn’t make out a word they said.

I watched the tears and panic on Elizabeth’s face slowly dissipate, my senses gradually returning as I reluctantly let go of Donald sprawled in my arms, allowing Benjamin to frantically press his cape against Donald’s back!

Because the wounds on Donald’s back were too extensive, it wasn’t possible to find a large enough bandage to stop the bleeding, so Benjamin had no choice but to do this.

I mostly understood what Benjamin was trying to do. Although I couldn’t hear anything, my slowly recovering rationality told me Benjamin was doing the right thing.

Elizabeth held up both hands, her face overwhelmed with helplessness and panic; she opened her mouth as if to say something, but I still couldn’t hear her.

Suddenly realizing something, I turned and looked towards the center of the stone platform, the location where the bomb had just exploded, where Henry had been standing motionlessly!

The pitted ground was covered in blood from who knew who, spreading across half of the stone platform. Henry’s body no longer had any smooth, intact skin; he lay there, looking like a pile of half-cooked rotten flesh.

"Cough, cough, cough!" He barely coughed a few times, blood flowing from the corners of his mouth and nostrils, making him seem as if he would crumble with a single touch.

He slowly opened his eyes, his gaze falling on Donald and me.

I stared blankly at him, my body trembling slightly, tears uncontrollably sliding down my face, rage and hatred furiously gathering in the depths of my eyes!

chapters.title: Chapter 426 ’What a pity?
chapters.content:
Chapter 426: 426 ’What a pity?

[Margaret’s perspective]

I propped myself up with my hands and slowly sat up, looking toward Henry, whose eyes had turned blood red.

Henry was truly a madman, watching our reinforcements arrive, with no chance of escape left for himself, he actually thought of a way to take Donald and me down with him.

It turned out that when he said, "Even if I die today, I will take you and Margaret down with me," he wasn’t just threatening Donald; he really meant it.

"It’s such a pity that none of us three died? Cough cough! If only one of you could have accompanied me to hell, I would have felt it was worth it! Such a pity, cough cough!"

If possible, I hoped I hadn’t guessed what Henry had said; as he spoke, he actually curved the corner of his mouth into a smile, seemingly truly regretting not having killed any one of us, Donald or me!

I glanced at the gun that had fallen not far off on the ground, bent down, and slowly crawled toward it.

Elizabeth watched me with worry in her eyes; understanding my intention, she immediately got up, ran over, picked up the gun, and ran back, crying as she handed it to me with both hands.

"Sister, please don’t move around anymore, okay? Your injuries are too severe. What do you need this gun for? I’m giving it to you; won’t you sit down and let Dean Andrew check you? Even if you don’t think your body is in trouble, what about the baby in your belly? Are you going to give up on him too?"

Elizabeth’s voice faintly reached my ears.

I looked up at her, took the object she handed over, managed to curve my mouth slightly, and with my other hand gently pushed her away as I raised the gun toward Henry, who was still lying on the ground, looking this way!

"I’m going to kill him," I said softly, my face pale, filled with a lethal chill.

Elizabeth was stunned for a moment, her eyes blankly watching the movement of my hand.

’Bang bang bang!’

I fired three shots at Henry on the ground; due to my wrist lacking strength, two of the shots missed!

"Margaret? Sister..." Elizabeth collected her thoughts and said in a hoarse voice.

Tears flowed down again as the hand holding the gun began to tremble violently; I regretfully knelt on the ground with my other hand also gripping the gun handle, forcing my hand not to shake like this.

Henry laughed at this moment, as if he found my current appearance amusing.

I grit my teeth and said fiercely to him, "You deserve to die! I will kill you for sure."

"Sure! Dying by your hand seems like a pretty good ending. You’d better come closer, otherwise, you might miss, hahaha!"

"Madman! Pervert!"

I roared as I pulled the trigger, firing bullets recklessly at Henry, who was still talking!

’Bang bang bang!’

When the magazine was emptied, the gun in my hand fell silent, losing all its menace, and Elizabeth, trembling, crawled beside me, hugged me, and wailed, "He’s dead! He’s already dead, Margaret! Can you calm down? Henry is dead!"

At that moment, I finally heard what Elizabeth said. My wrist went limp, dropping the handgun, and I collapsed into her arms.

As for what happened afterward, I really had no idea. But I thought, with Benjamin and Dean Andrew there, Donald should be safe.

However, what I didn’t expect was that the simultaneous severe injuries Donald and I sustained nearly scared everyone present to death!

Elizabeth almost instantly realized that I had lost consciousness, her body stiffened, and she started calling for help with all her might.

Dean Andrew, not minding his own aged and unsteady legs, left Benjamin dealing with Donald and rushed over to me with his cumbersome body!

He first signaled for Elizabeth to put me down, then swiftly began checking my body with his hands.

When he found no particularly obvious or severe wounds on me, Dean Andrew breathed a small sigh of relief. Just as he was about to say something, Christian, who had been circling around Donald and me, suddenly spoke up!

[Elizabeth’s perspective]

"The Lycan Queen’s pregnancy is almost two months in! She has been in the enemy’s hands these past few days, and to my knowledge, her pregnancy is very unstable... We don’t know what her current physical condition is like, so please, Dean, you must find a way to stabilize the life of the Queen and the little prince, please!"

I had never seen Christian so panicked and disorganized before; his face was covered in a thick sweat, his nerves seeming to tense to the limit.

The last time I saw him was after Margaret and Donald’s wedding ceremony; he was the chief of the Royal Guards, the most stable and responsible of all Lycans!

But even he was panicking and fearing at this moment, the last bit of luck in my heart began to dissipate.

He nervously swallowed and without pause continued, "There’s another situation I need to tell you about. Due to the critical battle situation just now, her highness used ’New Birth’ to heal her injuries temporarily! So now you won’t see any external injuries on her."

chapters.title: Chapter 427 serious injuries
chapters.content:
Chapter 427: 427 serious injuries

[Elizabeth’s Perspective]

"What did you say? His Highness used ’New Birth?’"

I heard a surprised voice coming from behind, and both I and Dean Andrew instinctively turned to look.

It was Lord Benjamin, the Medical Practitioner of the Lycan King, squatting next to Margaret and me, his forehead drenched in cold sweat!

Christian nodded at him, his eyes holding some gravity and obscurity that I couldn’t comprehend.

My gaze shifted back and forth between the two of them, and seeing the atmosphere slightly stagnate, I stuttered, "You...both of you, are you talking about the same ’New Birth’ that was used on Anthony before? Are you saying Margaret also used that concoction just now?"

"I heard from Dean Andrew that you found no side effects on Anthony, so why did both of you look so horrified just now? Is there something I do not know?"

As I spoke, I slowly came to my senses and widened my eyes at Benjamin, urgently asking, "’New Birth’ has side effects on pregnant she-wolves? Is that what you are worried about?"

Benjamin and Christian were silent for a second or two before they looked at me and nodded.

I slumped to the ground, my stiff neck turning to look at Margaret beside me, my heart plummeting into an abyss!

"Is there any way to do it? I mean, to terminate the effects of ’New Birth’ prematurely, or perhaps to pump her stomach?" I spoke up again after thinking of something.

Benjamin just quietly watched Andrew busy with Margaret, then finally turned to look at me and said softly, "There’s no other way, Miss. ’New Birth’ is administered through muscle or vascular injection; stomach pumping wouldn’t do any good!"

He said this and turned to Christian, continuing, "You clearly knew ’New Birth’ was still in its third experimental phase, why didn’t you stop the Lycan Queen? You should think about how you’re going to explain this to His Majesty!"

"OK! I know this isn’t the right time for such discussions. Go let General Lucas deploy two squads here; we must get His Majesty and the Lycan Queen out immediately! His Majesty’s external injuries are too severe, and even with ’New Birth’, he still needs much more advanced medical equipment for treatment! And the situation with the Lycan Queen...is even more complicated, they can’t afford any delay at all!"

Perhaps this is just the natural rational thinking of a medical practitioner. As Christian was mired in his emotions, it was he who took charge of the situation.

At that moment, Dean Andrew also raised his head to look at us, his expression grave. Yet when he spoke, he brought us some really good news, "I detected a very weak vital sign inside the Lycan Queen! The young prince should be alright for now, but we need to leave quickly, for the Lycan Queen requires a thorough examination and treatment!"

I also gathered my senses then, quickly getting up from the ground, "Then let’s get out of here quickly! We’ll leave the battlefield cleanup to General Lucas, as for..."

As I spoke, I remembered Margaret earlier, desperately wanting to kill Henry, and glanced toward the figure on the ground not far away.

I thought for a moment then asked, "How are you going to deal with that body? I think when Margaret wakes up, she might want to personally verify his death."

"I understand, Miss! We’ll take Henry’s body back, along with capturing Austin, rest assured." It was Christian who responded, then turned and left, summoning a few soldiers who started to deal with Henry’s body.

Actually, none of us had fully recovered yet, nor did we find it strange that Henry, the rebel leader, had died by Margaret’s hand; we were too stunned by the simultaneous severe injuries of the Lycan King and the Lycan Queen and had no energy left to think about anything else.

When we had planned to quickly transport Margaret and Donald out, I noticed a man standing next to Donald’s stretcher whom I didn’t recognize.

The man seemed very concerned about Donald’s condition and when he noticed something unusual here, he immediately broke away from the enemies around him and ran over with his gun! From Elliot’s words, I learned that this was a good friend of Donald’s from the Lilo Sea, and he also helped Margaret and Donald a lot in this battle.

I heard Elliot call him Mr. Gino, but his way of speaking and handling things didn’t seem like those of someone from the political circles of the Lilo Sea.

It wasn’t until later, when Margaret had regained consciousness and I was chatting with her, that I found out this Mr. Gino was actually the head of the Werewolf Gang of the Lilo Sea, a legendary figure indeed!

After another hectic hour, we finally reached the departure dock of Poison Snake Island.

I had thought earlier that we might be leaving the mysterious island directly by helicopter.

But Anthony shook his head and looked at me, gently explaining, "The surrounding Mist Sea Region of Poison Snake Island is very peculiar; a helicopter could get lost due to the radar malfunctioning."

chapters.title: Chapter 428 jealous
chapters.content:
Chapter 428: 428 jealous

[Elizabeth’s Perspective]

"For safety’s sake, let’s leave the way we came in; that will be more secure!"

Anthony said while lifting his hand to arrange my stray hair blown about by the sea breeze. He sighed slightly and continued, "You wouldn’t want them to have any accidents again, would you? Were you terrified by today’s events?"

I gasped upon hearing this, crouching in front of Anthony’s wheelchair, looking up at him, "Such a perilously dense experience — I dared not even think of it before! I can’t fathom how Margaret endured these past few days; she is much calmer and smarter than me."

"I can’t imagine if I were in her shoes if I could be half as sensible — it’s truly terrifying! You didn’t see when Henry detonated the bomb, how the Lycan King decisively threw himself onto Margaret to shield her with his body!"

As I spoke, I involuntarily got goosebumps on my arms, my eyes turning red as I looked toward Anthony, "That scene was like those decisive moments life and death in movies! Now I understand why Margaret could abandon everything for Donald; such a man is simply irresistible."

I raved on and on about Donald’s bravery, my tone filled with admiration for Margaret and Donald’s love, not even noticing the change in Anthony’s facial expression.

"The Lycan King is truly one in a million, and the bond between him and the Lycan Queen is truly enviable and desirable! But everyone has their unique life experiences, we don’t always have to be envious of others’ emotions and lives, ours are also wonderful."

The night fell early on Poison Snake Island. I turned towards Anthony in the tender evening light. After a brief silence, I chuckled softly, suddenly stood up, and wrapped my arms around Anthony’s neck, "You’re becoming braver now, daring to be jealous of His Majesty! Their love is so profoundly depicted, stirring and powerful, am I not allowed to sing praises?"

"He is our brother-in-law, what on earth are you thinking?"

As if I had uncovered his hidden thoughts, Anthony’s face flushed with a tinge of red. He held me tight and explained softly, "I understand what you’re saying, but I still can’t tolerate your praise for another man, even if he is the Lycan King! It doesn’t really matter who it is; I’m just jealous of such actions."

After the recent events, Anthony seemed much more direct than before; he had never spoken to me like this.

I felt as if my heart was tenderly squeezed, instantly turning into a puddle of warm water, my lips uncontrollably curled up, and I felt as though I was swirling in a whirlpool of happiness!

"When I saw you in the cave earlier, I was nearly scared to death. Promise me you won’t get hurt on your missions anymore, okay?"

I said, slowly pouting, knowing that my request could be rather challenging. For someone as brave and intelligent as Anthony, a Beta, his moments of shining were truly on battlefields and missions!

But thankfully, Anthony was happy to indulge me, he nodded without hesitation and agreed, "Alright! I promise I’ll try not to get hurt so easily and to return to Elizabeth safely every time."

The smile in my eyes grew wider, feeling it was the most moving declaration of love I had ever heard. To have someone, no matter where he might go or what he might face in the future, he would strive to return safely to my side every time; I would always be his dearly beloved!

Unable to resist, I leaned closer to Anthony and pressed my lips fiercely onto his pale lips, recklessly committing my whole heart to him, for he was my closest, most needed lover.

Anthony was taken aback by my sudden action, frozen for a few seconds before catching his senses. His arms around my waist tightened, and he leaned down, kissing me back irresistibly.

I heard his sexy, thick breathing through his nostrils, my heart racing as I gently responded to his kiss. Then I pushed his shoulders forcefully, catching my breath as I explained, "Now is not the time for this; we need to board the ship and leave soon, do you want others to see us in such a reckless make-out session? Margaret and Donald’s situation isn’t stable yet!"

I realized that my behavior had been somewhat impulsive, and it wasn’t just Anthony’s fault.

Anthony first looked at me dumbly, then seemed to think my words made sense. He nodded and said, "You’re right, that wasn’t appropriate; I was impulsive."

He looked over to the Lycan soldiers approaching us and whispered, "Next time, you won’t have the chance to stop me."

Me: "..."

This was the first time Anthony revealed such a domineering side in front of me. Suppressing my overly active heartbeat, I pushed him towards the boat, only I knew how hot my face felt, like the summer sun!
